155: int de_delete(struct dentry *de);
156: struct dentry *de_check_cache(struct dentry *parent, char *name);
157: /**
158:  * If de is a moint point, return the mounted root.
159:  */
160: static inline struct dentry *de_mnt_in(struct dentry *de)
161: {
162: 	while (de->mount != NULL)
163: 		de = de->mount->root;
164: 	return de;
165: }

5: 
6: // which hart (core) is this?
7: static inline uint64
8: r_mhartid()
9: {
10: 	uint64 x;
11: 	asm volatile("csrr %0, mhartid" : "=r" (x) );
12: 	return x;
13: }
14:

16:

37: // machine exception program counter, holds the
38: // instruction address to which a return from
39: // exception will go.
40: static inline void 
41: w_mepc(uint64 x)
42: {
43: 	asm volatile("csrw mepc, %0" : : "r" (x));
44: }
45: 
46: // Supervisor Status Register, sstatus
47: 
48: #define SSTATUS_SPP (1L << 8)  // Previous mode, 1=Supervisor, 0=User

233: w_sscratch(uint64 x)
234: {

238: static inline void 
239: w_mscratch(uint64 x)
240: {
241: 	asm volatile("csrw mscratch, %0" : : "r" (x));
242: }

359: 
360: // flush the TLB.
361: static inline void
362: sfence_vma()
363: {
364: 	// the zero, zero means flush all TLB entries.
365: 	// asm volatile("sfence.vma zero, zero");
366: 	#ifdef QEMU
367: 	asm volatile("sfence.vma");
368: 	#else
369: 	// asm volatile("fence");
370: 	// asm volatile("fence.i");
371: 	asm volatile(".word 0x10400073");
372: 	// asm volatile("fence");
373: 	asm volatile("fence.i");
374: 	#endif
375: }
376: 
377: 
378: #define PGSIZE 4096 // bytes per page
379: #define PGSHIFT 12  // bits of offset within a page
380:

366: 	#ifdef QEMU
367: 	asm volatile("sfence.vma");

445: 
446: // init floating-point unit
447: static inline void floatinithart()
448: {
449: 	// If sstatus.fs is off, floating-point instructions
450: 	// will be treated as illegal ones.
451: 	w_sstatus_fs(SSTATUS_FS_INIT);
452: 	w_frm(FRM_RNE);
453: 	w_sstatus_fs(SSTATUS_FS_CLEAN);
454: }
455: 
456: 
457: #endif

446: // init floating-point unit
447: static inline void floatinithart()
448: {
449: 	// If sstatus.fs is off, floating-point instructions
450: 	// will be treated as illegal ones.
451: 	w_sstatus_fs(SSTATUS_FS_INIT);
452: 	w_frm(FRM_RNE);
453: 	w_sstatus_fs(SSTATUS_FS_CLEAN);
454: }
455: 
456: 
457: #endif

156: extern struct rb_node *rb_last(const struct rb_root *);
157: 
158: /* Fast replacement of a single node without remove/rebalance/add/rebalance */
159: extern void rb_replace_node(struct rb_node *victim, struct rb_node *new, struct rb_root *root);
160: 
161: static inline void rb_link_node(struct rb_node * node, struct rb_node * parent, struct rb_node ** rb_link)
162: {
163: 	node->rb_parent_color = (unsigned long )parent;
164: 	node->rb_left = node->rb_right = NULL;
165: 
166: 	*rb_link = node;
167: }
168: 
169: #endif	/* _LINUX_RBTREE_H */

39: #define C(x)  ((x)-'@')  // Control-x

41: void consputc(int c) {
42: 	if(c == BACKSPACE){
43: 		// if the user typed backspace, overwrite with a space.
44: 		sbi_console_putchar('\b');
45: 		sbi_console_putchar(' ');
46: 		sbi_console_putchar('\b');

61: } cons;
62: 
63: //
64: // user write()s to the console go here.
65: //
66: int
67: consolewrite(int user_src, uint64 src, int n)
68: {
69: 	int m, tot;
70: 	char outbuf[INPUT_BUF];
71: 
72: 	// acquire(&cons.lock);	// use inode's lock instead
73: 	for (tot = 0; tot < n; tot += m, src += m) {
74: 		m = n - tot;  // left count
75: 		if (m > INPUT_BUF) {
76: 			m = INPUT_BUF;
77: 		}
78: 		if (either_copyin_nocheck(outbuf, user_src, src, m) == -1)
79: 			break;
80: 		for (int i = 0; i < m; i++) {
81: 			sbi_console_putchar(outbuf[i]);
82: 		}
83: 	}
84: 	// release(&cons.lock);
85: 
86: 	return tot;
87: }
88:

90: {
91: 	int tot = 0;
92: 	char outbuf[INPUT_BUF];

89: int consolewritev(struct inode *ip, struct iovec *iovecs, int count, uint off)
90: {
91: 	int tot = 0;
92: 	char outbuf[INPUT_BUF];
93: 
94: 	// acquire(&cons.lock);	// use inode's lock instead
95: 	for (int i = 0; i < count; i++) {
96: 		uint64 addr = (uint64)iovecs[i].iov_base;
97: 		uint64 n = iovecs[i].iov_len;
98: 		if (!rangeinseg(addr, addr + n))
99: 			return -EFAULT;
100: 
101: 		int m, j;
102: 		for (j = 0; j < n; j += m, tot += m) {
103: 			m = n - j;  // left count
104: 			if (m > INPUT_BUF)
105: 				m = INPUT_BUF;
106: 			if (copyin_nocheck(outbuf, addr + j, m) < 0)
107: 				break;
108: 			for (int c = 0; c < m; c++) {
109: 				sbi_console_putchar(outbuf[c]);
110: 			}
111: 		}
112: 		if (j < n)
113: 			break;
114: 	}
115: 	// release(&cons.lock);
116: 	return tot;
117: }
118: 
119: //
120: // user read()s from the console go here.
121: // copy (up to) a whole input line to dst.
122: // user_dist indicates whether dst is a user

96: 		uint64 addr = (uint64)iovecs[i].iov_base;

108: 			for (int c = 0; c < m; c++) {
109: 				sbi_console_putchar(outbuf[c]);

111: 		}

113: 			break;
114: 	}

xv6-k210/kernel/console.c::consoleread
匹配分数 1.000 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": true, "exact_token_fp": true, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 1.0}

oskernel2023-zmz@recover 源码

120: // user read()s from the console go here.
121: // copy (up to) a whole input line to dst.
122: // user_dist indicates whether dst is a user
123: // or kernel address.
124: //
125: int
126: consoleread(int user_dst, uint64 dst, int n)
127: {
128: 	int c = 0;
129: 	char inbuf[INPUT_BUF];
130: 	uint tot = 0;
131: 
132: 	while (n > 0) {
133: 		// wait until interrupt handler has put some
134: 		// input into cons.buffer.
135: 		acquire(&cons.lock);
136: 		while (cons.r == cons.w) {
137: 			if (myproc()->killed == SIGTERM) {
138: 				release(&cons.lock);
139: 				return -1;
140: 			}
141: 			sleep(&cons.r, &cons.lock);
142: 		}
143: 
144: 		int m = n < INPUT_BUF ? n : INPUT_BUF;
145: 		int j;
146: 		for (j = 0; j < m && cons.r < cons.w; ) {
147: 			c = inbuf[j++] = cons.buf[cons.r++ % INPUT_BUF];
148: 			if (c == '\n')
149: 				break;
150: 			if (c == C('D')) {
151: 				if (tot > 0) {	// Save ^D for the next time, 
152: 					cons.r--;	// to make sure caller gets a 0-byte result.
153: 				}
154: 				j--;
155: 				break;
156: 			}
157: 		}
158: 		release(&cons.lock);
159: 		
160: 		// copy the input byte to the user-space buffer.
161: 		if (either_copyout_nocheck(user_dst, dst, inbuf, j) < 0)
162: 			break;
163: 		dst += j;
164: 		n -= j;
165: 		tot += j;
166: 		if (c == '\n' || c == C('D'))
167: 			break;
168: 	}
169: 
170: 	return tot;
171: }
172: 
173: int consolereadv(struct inode *ip, struct iovec *iovecs, int count, uint off)
174: {
175: 	uint tot = 0;
176: 	int c = 0;

173: int consolereadv(struct inode *ip, struct iovec *iovecs, int count, uint off)
174: {
175: 	uint tot = 0;
176: 	int c = 0;
177: 	char inbuf[INPUT_BUF];
178: 
179: 	for (int i = 0; i < count; i++) {
180: 		uint64 dst = (uint64)iovecs[i].iov_base;
181: 		uint64 n = iovecs[i].iov_len;
182: 		if (!rangeinseg(dst, dst + n))
183: 			return -EFAULT;
184: 
185: 		while (n > 0) {
186: 			// wait until interrupt handler has put some
187: 			// input into cons.buffer.
188: 			acquire(&cons.lock);
189: 			while (cons.r == cons.w) {
190: 				if (myproc()->killed == SIGTERM) {
191: 					release(&cons.lock);
192: 					return -1;
193: 				}
194: 				sleep(&cons.r, &cons.lock);
195: 			}
196: 
197: 			int m = n < INPUT_BUF ? n : INPUT_BUF;
198: 			int j;
199: 			for (j = 0; j < m && cons.r < cons.w; ) {
200: 				c = inbuf[j++] = cons.buf[cons.r++ % INPUT_BUF];
201: 				if (c == '\n')
202: 					break;
203: 				if (c == C('D')) {
204: 					if (tot > 0) {	// Save ^D for the next time, 
205: 						cons.r--;	// to make sure caller gets a 0-byte result.
206: 					}
207: 					j--;
208: 					break;
209: 				}
210: 			}
211: 			release(&cons.lock);
212: 
213: 			// copy the input byte to the user-space buffer.
214: 			if (copyout_nocheck(dst, inbuf, j) < 0) {
215: 				count = 0; // break the out-level loop
216: 				break;
217: 			}
218: 			dst += j;
219: 			n -= j;
220: 			tot += j;
221: 			if (c == '\n' || c == C('D')) {
222: 				count = 0;
223: 				break;
224: 			}
225: 		}
226: 	}
227: 	return tot;
228: }
229: 
230: 
231: //
232: // the console input interrupt handler.
233: // uartintr() calls this for input character.

consoleintr修改候选
oskernel2023-zmz@recover 第 237 行 · 主要确定性来源：xv6-k210/kernel/console.c::consoleintr

候选来源与匹配信号

xv6-k210/kernel/console.c::consoleintr
匹配分数 0.834 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": true, "exact_token_fp": false, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 0.445}

oskernel2023-zmz@recover 源码

232: // the console input interrupt handler.
233: // uartintr() calls this for input character.
234: // do erase/kill processing, append to cons.buf,
235: // wake up consoleread() if a whole line has arrived.
236: //
237: void
238: consoleintr(int c)
239: {
240: 	acquire(&cons.lock);
241: 
242: 	switch(c){
243: 	case C('P'):  // Print process list.
244: 		procdump();
245: 		break;
246: 	case C('E'):  // Print file-entry list.
247: 		rootfs_print();
248: 		break;
249: 	case C('K'):  // Print mem-alloc list.
250: 		kmview();
251: 		break;
252: 	case C('B'):  // Print mem-alloc list.
253: 		bprint();
254: 		break;
255: 	case C('Q'):  // Print physical memory status
256: 		pm_dump();
257: 		break;
258: 	case C('U'):  // Kill line.
259: 		while(cons.e != cons.w &&
260: 					cons.buf[(cons.e-1) % INPUT_BUF] != '\n'){
261: 			cons.e--;
262: 			consputc(BACKSPACE);
263: 		}
264: 		break;
265: 	case C('H'): // Backspace
266: 	case '\x7f':
267: 		if(cons.e != cons.w){
268: 			cons.e--;
269: 			consputc(BACKSPACE);
270: 		}
271: 		break;
272: 	default:
273: 		if(c != 0 && cons.e-cons.r < INPUT_BUF){
274: 			#ifndef QEMU
275: 			if (c == '\r') break;     // on k210, "enter" will input \n and \r
276: 			#else
277: 			c = (c == '\r') ? '\n' : c;
278: 			#endif
279: 			// echo back to the user.
280: 			consputc(c);
281: 
282: 			// store for consumption by consoleread().
283: 			cons.buf[cons.e++ % INPUT_BUF] = c;
284: 
285: 			if(c == '\n' || c == C('D') || cons.e == cons.r+INPUT_BUF){
286: 				// wake up consoleread() if a whole line (or end-of-file)
287: 				// has arrived.
288: 				cons.w = cons.e;
289: 				wakeup(&cons.r);
290: 			}
291: 		}
292: 		break;
293: 	}
294: 	
295: 	release(&cons.lock);
296: }
297: 
298: void
299: consoleinit(void)
300: {
301: 	initlock(&cons.lock, "cons");

xv6-k210@final / 来源候选源码

232: // the console input interrupt handler.
233: // uartintr() calls this for input character.
234: // do erase/kill processing, append to cons.buf,
235: // wake up consoleread() if a whole line has arrived.
236: //
237: void
238: consoleintr(int c)
239: {
240: 	acquire(&cons.lock);
241: 
242: 	switch(c){
243: 	case C('P'):  // Print process list.
244: 		procdump();
245: 		break;
246: 	case C('E'):  // Print file-entry list.
247: 		rootfs_print();
248: 		break;
249: 	case C('K'):  // Print mem-alloc list.
250: 		kmview();
251: 		break;
252: 	case C('B'):  // Print mem-alloc list.
253: 		bprint();
254: 		break;
255: 	case C('Q'):  // Print physical memory status
256: 		pm_dump();
257: 		break;
258: 	case C('U'):  // Kill line.
259: 		while(cons.e != cons.w &&
260: 					cons.buf[(cons.e-1) % INPUT_BUF] != '\n'){
261: 			cons.e--;
262: 			consputc(BACKSPACE);
263: 		}
264: 		break;
265: 	case C('H'): // Backspace
266: 	case '\x7f':
267: 		if(cons.e != cons.w){
268: 			cons.e--;
269: 			consputc(BACKSPACE);
270: 		}
271: 		break;
272: 	default:
273: 		if(c != 0 && cons.e-cons.r < INPUT_BUF){
274: 			#ifndef QEMU
275: 			if (c == '\r') break;     // on k210, "enter" will input \n and \r
276: 			#else
277: 			c = (c == '\r') ? '\n' : c;
278: 			#endif
279: 			// echo back to the user.
280: 			consputc(c);
281: 
282: 			// store for consumption by consoleread().
283: 			cons.buf[cons.e++ % INPUT_BUF] = c;
284: 
285: 			if(c == '\n' || c == C('D') || cons.e == cons.r+INPUT_BUF){
286: 				// wake up consoleread() if a whole line (or end-of-file)
287: 				// has arrived.
288: 				cons.w = cons.e;
289: 				wakeup(&cons.r);
290: 			}
291: 		}
292: 		break;
293: 	}
294: 	
295: 	release(&cons.lock);
296: }
297: 
298: void
299: consoleinit(void)
300: {
301: 	initlock(&cons.lock, "cons");
consoleinit完全一致

56: // push arg or env strings into user stack, return strings count
57: static int pushstack(pagetable_t pt, uint64 table[], char **utable, int maxc, uint64 *spp)
58: {
59: 	//printf("NNN: enter pushstack!!!\n");
60: 	uint64 sp = *spp;
61: 	uint64 stackbase = PGROUNDDOWN(sp);
62: 	uint64 argc, argp;
63: 	char *buf;
64: 
65: 	if ((buf = allocpage()) == NULL) {
66: 		//printf("NNN: allocpage failed!\n");
67: 		return -ENOMEM;
68: 	}
69: 	for (argc = 0; utable; argc++) {
70: 		if (argc >= maxc || fetchaddr((uint64)(utable + argc), &argp) < 0){
71: 			//printf("NNN: get arg[%d] failed!\n",argc);
72: 			goto bad;
73: 		}
74: 		__debug_info("pushstack", "argp=%p\n", argp);
75: 		if (argp == 0){
76: 			// 正常应该来到这里
77: 			break;
78: 		}
79: 		// IMP: in fetchstr using copyinstr2
80: 		int arglen = fetchstr(argp, buf, PGSIZE);   // '\0' included in PGSIZE, but not in envlen
81: 		__debug_info("pushstack", "arglen = %d\n", arglen);
82: 		//printf("NNN: arg[%d] arhlen = %d!\n",argc,arglen);
83: 		if (arglen++ < 0) {                               // including '\0'
84: 			__debug_warn("pushstack", "didn't get null\n");
85: 			//printf("NNN: didn't get null!\n");
86: 			goto bad;
87: 		}
88: 		sp -= arglen;
89: 		// 把用户空间的字符串拷贝到栈上
90: 		if (sp < stackbase || copyout(pt, sp, buf, arglen) < 0){
91: 			//printf("NNN: from buf to stack failed!\n");
92: 			goto bad;
93: 		}
94: 		table[argc] = sp; // 新的字符串首地址(转移到用户栈上)
95: 	}
96: 	table[argc] = 0;
97: 	*spp = sp;
98: 	freepage(buf);
99: 	return argc;
100: 
101: bad:
102: 	if (buf)
103: 		freepage(buf);
104: 	return -EFAULT;
105: }
106: 
107: void show_vm_load(pagetable_t pagetable, uint64 where_addr)
108: {
109: 	struct proc *p = myproc();
110: 	w_satp(MAKE_SATP(pagetable));

xv6-k210@final / 来源候选源码

55: 
56: // push arg or env strings into user stack, return strings count
57: static int pushstack(pagetable_t pt, uint64 table[], char **utable, int maxc, uint64 *spp)
58: {
59: 	uint64 sp = *spp;
60: 	uint64 stackbase = PGROUNDDOWN(sp);
61: 	uint64 argc, argp;
62: 	char *buf;
63: 
64: 	if ((buf = allocpage()) == NULL) {
65: 		return -ENOMEM;
66: 	}
67: 	for (argc = 0; utable; argc++) {
68: 		if (argc >= maxc || fetchaddr((uint64)(utable + argc), &argp) < 0)
69: 			goto bad;
70: 		__debug_info("pushstack", "argp=%p\n", argp);
71: 		if (argp == 0)
72: 			break;
73: 		int arglen = fetchstr(argp, buf, PGSIZE);   // '\0' included in PGSIZE, but not in envlen
74: 		if (strncmp(buf, "lat_pagefault", arglen) == 0) {
75: 			goto bad;
76: 		}
77: 		if (strncmp(buf, "lat_mmap", arglen) == 0) {
78: 			goto bad;
79: 		}
80: 		__debug_info("pushstack", "arglen = %d\n", arglen);
81: 		if (arglen++ < 0) {                               // including '\0'
82: 			__debug_warn("pushstack", "didn't get null\n");
83: 			goto bad;
84: 		}
85: 		sp -= arglen;
86: 		if (sp < stackbase || copyout(pt, sp, buf, arglen) < 0)
87: 			goto bad;
88: 		table[argc] = sp;
89: 	}
90: 	table[argc] = 0;
91: 	*spp = sp;
92: 	freepage(buf);
93: 	return argc;
94: 
95: bad:
96: 	if (buf)
97: 		freepage(buf);
98: 	return -EFAULT;
99: }
100:

107: void show_vm_load(pagetable_t pagetable, uint64 where_addr)
108: {
109: 	struct proc *p = myproc();
110: 	w_satp(MAKE_SATP(pagetable));
111: 	sfence_vma();
112: 	permit_usr_mem();
113: 	pageDump((char *)(where_addr));
114: 	protect_usr_mem();
115: 	w_satp(MAKE_SATP(p->pagetable));
116: 	sfence_vma();
117: }
118:

122: 		show_vm_load(pagetable,start);

126: void
127: show_stack(pagetable_t pagetable,uint64 sp,uint64 sz)
128: {
129:   for(uint64 i = sp;i<sz;i+=8){
130:     uint64 *pa = (void*)walkaddr(pagetable,i) + i - PGROUNDDOWN(i);
131:     if(pa)printf("addr %p phaddr:%p value %p\n",i, pa, *pa);
132:     else printf("addr %p value (nil)\n",i);
133:   }
134: }

136: #define min(x, y) ((x)<(y)? (x):(y))
137: #define max(x, y) ((x)>(y)? (x):(y))

137: #define max(x, y) ((x)>(y)? (x):(y))

139: uint64
140: size4mapping(struct elfhdr* interp_elfhdr, struct inode* interpreter){
141:   uint64 low_bound = -1, upper_bound = 0;
142:   int valid = 0;
143:   struct proghdr ph;
144:   // 先理解好interpreter这个文件的意义
145:   //printf("NNN: phoff: %d, phnum: %d\n",interp_elfhdr->phoff,interp_elfhdr->phnum);
146:   for(uint i = 0, off = interp_elfhdr->phoff; i < interp_elfhdr->phnum; i++, off += sizeof(struct proghdr)){
147:     if(interpreter->fop->read(interpreter, 0, (uint64)&ph, off, sizeof(struct proghdr)) != sizeof(struct proghdr)){
148:       printf("[get_total_mapping_size] read failed\n");
149:       return -1;
150:     }
151:     if(ph.type == ELF_PROG_LOAD){
152:       low_bound = min(low_bound, PGROUNDDOWN(ph.vaddr)); //读取虚拟地址大小
153:       upper_bound = max(upper_bound, ph.vaddr + ph.memsz);
154:       valid = 1;
155:     }
156:   }
157:   return valid ? (upper_bound - low_bound) : 0;
158: }
159:

156:   }
157:   return valid ? (upper_bound - low_bound) : 0;
158: }

160: uint64
161: load_elf_interp(pagetable_t pagetable, struct seg* seghead, struct elfhdr* interp_elfhdr, struct inode* interpreter){
162:   //NNN1printf("In load interpreter\n");
163:   //uint64 whole = 0;
164:   uint64 start_addr = 0;
165:   struct proghdr ph;
166:   struct seg *seg;//*newseg;
167:   // 计算需要装载的总大小
168:   //whole = size4mapping(interp_elfhdr, interpreter);
169:   //printf("whole: %d\n",whole);
170:   /*
171:   if(whole == 0){
172:     printf("[load_elf_interp]get_total_mapping_size failed\n");
173:     return -1;
174:   }*/
175:   // 使用mmap来完成对这一部分的装载
176:   // start_addr = mmap(0, whole, PROT_READ | PROT_WRITE | PROT_EXEC, MAP_PRIVATE | MAP_ANONYMOUS, -1, 0);
177:   // consider the permission, and the constants defined.
178:   // 注意映射的类型
179:   uint64 flags =  PROT_READ | PROT_WRITE | PROT_EXEC;
180:   // start_addr = do_mmap(0,whole,PROT_READ | PROT_WRITE | PROT_EXEC, MAP_ANONYMOUS | MAP_PRIVATE, 0, 0);
181:   // start_addr = do_mmap(0,whole,PROT_READ | PROT_WRITE | PROT_EXEC, MAP_ANONYMOUS | MAP_SHARED, 0, 0);
182:   // start_addr = do_mmap(0,whole,PROT_READ | PROT_WRITE | PROT_EXEC, MAP_ANONYMOUS | MAP_SHARED, 0, 0);
183:   // 请一定注意mmap是当前进程执行的哦!!!
184:   // printf("mmap start addr: %p\n",start_addr);
185:   start_addr = VUMMAP;
186: 
187:   //printf("NNNAfter mmap: show segs:\n");
188:   //showsegs(seghead);
189:   // start_addr = do_mmap(0,whole,PROT_READ | PROT_WRITE | PROT_EXEC, MAP_ANONYMOUS | MAP_PRIVATE, -1, 0);
190:   if(start_addr == -1){
191:     printf("[load_elf_interp]do_mmap failed\n");
192:     return -1;
193:   }
194:   // 开始加载
195:   //printf("NNN load interpreter start!!!\n");
196:   for(uint i = 0, off = interp_elfhdr->phoff; i < interp_elfhdr->phnum; i++, off += sizeof(struct proghdr)){
197:     if(interpreter->fop->read(interpreter, 0, (uint64)&ph, off, sizeof(struct proghdr)) != sizeof(struct proghdr)){
198:       printf("[load_elf_interp]eread failed\n");
199:       return -1;
200:     }
201:     if(ph.type == ELF_PROG_LOAD){
202:       if(ph.memsz < ph.filesz){
203:         printf("[load_elf_interp]ph.memsz < ph.filesz\n");
204:         return -1;
205:       }
206:       if(ph.vaddr + ph.memsz < ph.vaddr){
207:         printf("[load_elf_interp]ph.vaddr + ph.memsz < ph.vaddr\n");
208:         return -1;
209:       }
210: 	  // printf("NNN locateseg!!!\n");
211: 	  //seg = locateseg(seghead, ph.vaddr);
212: 	  // seg = locateseg(seghead,start_addr + ph.vaddr);
213: 	  //printf("NNN loc_seg: %p!!!\n",seg);
214: 	  //printf("NNN f_off: %d\tf_sz: %d!!!\n",ph.off,ph.filesz);
215: 	  // seg = newseg(pagetable, seghead, MMAP, start_addr + ph.vaddr, ph.filesz, flags);
216: 	  
217: 	  seg = newseg(pagetable, seghead, LOAD, start_addr + ph.vaddr, ph.filesz, flags);
218: 	  seg = locateseg(seghead,start_addr + ph.vaddr);
219: 	  /*
220: 	  int sz_tmp = ph.filesz;
221: 	  struct file fp_tmp;
222: 	  fp_tmp.inode = interpreter;
223: 	  do_mmap(0,sz_tmp,PROT_READ | PROT_WRITE | PROT_EXEC, MAP_ANONYMOUS | MAP_PRIVATE, &fp_tmp, 0);*/
224: 	  seg->f_off = ph.off;
225: 	  seg->f_sz = ph.filesz;
226: 	  //printf("NNN Hello here!!!\n");
227: 	  // 注意loadseg操作一次小于一个页面
228: 	  
229:       if(loadseg(pagetable, start_addr + ph.vaddr, seg, interpreter) < 0){
230: 	  // if(loadseg_ext(pagetable, start_addr + ph.vaddr, seg, interpreter) < 0){
231: 	  //if(loadseg(pagetable,  ph.vaddr, seg, interpreter) < 0){
232: 	  // if(loadseg_ext(pagetable, start_addr + ph.vaddr, interpreter, ph.off, ph.filesz) < 0){
233:         // 转交给子函数load
234:         printf("[load_elf_interp]loadseg failed\n");
235:         return -1;
236:       }
237: 	  uint64 tmp = start_addr + ph.vaddr;
238: 	  //NNN1
239: 	  //show_vm_load(pagetable,tmp);
240: 	  //NNN1
241: 	  //many_vm_load(pagetable,tmp,tmp+ph.filesz);
242: 	  //printf("NNN locateseg 2!!!\n");
243: 	  //showsegs(seghead);
244:     }
245:   }
246:   return start_addr;
247: }

341: uint64
342: load_elf_interp_3(pagetable_t pagetable, struct seg* seghead, struct elfhdr* interp_elfhdr, struct inode* interpreter,uint64 brk){
343:   uint64 whole = 0;
344:   uint64 start_addr = PGROUNDUP(brk);
345:   start_addr = VUMMAP;
346:   struct proghdr ph;
347:   struct seg *seg, *fisrt;//*newseg;
348:   // 计算需要装载的总大小
349:   uint64 flags =  PROT_READ | PROT_WRITE | PROT_EXEC;
350:   whole = size4mapping(interp_elfhdr, interpreter);
351:   fisrt = newseg_get(pagetable, seghead, LOAD, start_addr, whole ,flags,&seg);// first是头节点，seg是新分配的段
352:   seg->f_off = 0;
353:   seg->f_sz = whole;
354:   //printf("whole: %d\n",whole);
355:   /*
356:   if(whole == 0){
357:     printf("[load_elf_interp]get_total_mapping_size failed\n");
358:     return -1;
359:   }*/
360:   // 使用mmap来完成对这一部分的装载
361:   // start_addr = mmap(0, whole, PROT_READ | PROT_WRITE | PROT_EXEC, MAP_PRIVATE | MAP_ANONYMOUS, -1, 0);
362:   // consider the permission, and the constants defined.
363:   // 注意映射的类型
364:   // start_addr = do_mmap(0,whole,PROT_READ | PROT_WRITE | PROT_EXEC, MAP_ANONYMOUS | MAP_PRIVATE, 0, 0);

366:   // start_addr = do_mmap(0,whole,PROT_READ | PROT_WRITE | PROT_EXEC, MAP_ANONYMOUS | MAP_SHARED, 0, 0);
367:   // 请一定注意mmap是当前进程执行的哦!!!
368:   // printf("mmap start addr: %p\n",start_addr);
369:   //start_addr = PGROUNDUP(brk);
370:   //printf("NNNAfter mmap: show segs:\n");
371:   //showsegs(seghead);
372:   // start_addr = do_mmap(0,whole,PROT_READ | PROT_WRITE | PROT_EXEC, MAP_ANONYMOUS | MAP_PRIVATE, -1, 0);
373:   /*
374:   if(start_addr == -1){
375:     printf("[load_elf_interp]do_mmap failed\n");
376:     return -1;
377:   }*/
378:   // 开始加载
379:   //printf("NNN load interpreter start!!!\n");
380:   for(uint i = 0, off = interp_elfhdr->phoff; i < interp_elfhdr->phnum; i++, off += sizeof(struct proghdr)){
381:     if(interpreter->fop->read(interpreter, 0, (uint64)&ph, off, sizeof(struct proghdr)) != sizeof(struct proghdr)){
382:       printf("[load_elf_interp]eread failed\n");
383:       return -1;
384:     }
385:     if(ph.type == ELF_PROG_LOAD){
386:       if(ph.memsz < ph.filesz){
387:         printf("[load_elf_interp]ph.memsz < ph.filesz\n");
388:         return -1;
389:       }
390:       if(ph.vaddr + ph.memsz < ph.vaddr){
391:         printf("[load_elf_interp]ph.vaddr + ph.memsz < ph.vaddr\n");
392:         return -1;
393:       }
394: 	  // printf("NNN locateseg!!!\n");
395: 	  //seg = locateseg(seghead, ph.vaddr);
396: 	   //seg = locateseg(seghead,start_addr + ph.vaddr);
397: 	  //printf("NNN loc_seg: %p!!!\n",seg);
398: 	  //printf("NNN f_off: %d\tf_sz: %d\tvaddr:%d!!!\n",ph.off,ph.filesz,ph.vaddr);
399: 	  // seg = newseg(pagetable, seghead, MMAP, start_addr + ph.vaddr, ph.filesz, flags);
400: 	  
401: 	  //seg = newseg(pagetable, seghead, LOAD, start_addr + ph.vaddr, ph.filesz, flags);
402: 	  //seg = newseg(pagetable, seghead, LOAD, start_addr + ph.vaddr, ph.filesz, flags);
403: 	  //seg = locateseg(seghead,start_addr + ph.vaddr);

405: 	  int sz_tmp = ph.filesz;
406: 	  struct file fp_tmp;
407: 	  fp_tmp.inode = interpreter;
408: 	  do_mmap(0,sz_tmp,PROT_READ | PROT_WRITE | PROT_EXEC, MAP_ANONYMOUS | MAP_PRIVATE, &fp_tmp, 0);*/
409: 	  seg->f_off = ph.off;
410: 	  seg->f_sz = ph.filesz;
411: 	  //printf("NNN Hello here!!!\n",);

413: 	   uint64 tmp = start_addr + ph.vaddr;
414: 	  //printf("Need mapped: %d\n",seg->f_sz);
415:       //if(loadseg(pagetable, start_addr + ph.vaddr, seg, interpreter) < 0){

440: // All argvs are pointers came from user space, and should be checked by sys_caller
441: int execve(char *path, char **argv, char **envp)
442: {
443: 	__debug_info("exeve", "path = %s\n", path);

xv6-k210/kernel/exec.c::execve
匹配分数 0.527 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 0.922, "exact_ast_shape": false, "exact_token_fp": false, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 0.117}

436:   }

439: 
440: // All argvs are pointers came from user space, and should be checked by sys_caller
441: int execve(char *path, char **argv, char **envp)
442: {
443: 	__debug_info("exeve", "path = %s\n", path);
444: 	//printf("NNN:In execve, path = %s\n", path);
445: 	int ret;
446: 	struct inode *ip = NULL;
447: 	pagetable_t pagetable = NULL;
448: 	struct seg *seghead = NULL, *seg;
449: 	struct proc *p = myproc();
450: 	// 
451: 	int dynamic_need = 0;
452: 	struct proghdr ph_store;
453: 	ph_store.vaddr = 0; // to avoid warning
454: 	int already_store = 0;
455: 
456: 	__debug_info("execve", "in\n");
457: 	//printf("NNN In execve start parsing!\n");
458: 	if ((ip = namei(path)) == NULL) {
459: 		__debug_warn("execve", "can't open %s\n", path);
460: 		//printf("NNN execve can't open %s\n", path);
461: 		ret = -ENOENT;
462: 		goto bad;
463: 	}
464: 
465: 	// Make a copy of p->pagetable without old user space, 
466: 	// but with the same kstack we are using now, which can't be changed.
467: 	if ((pagetable = (pagetable_t)allocpage()) == NULL) {
468: 		// 在这里为新进程分配页表
469: 		ret = -ENOMEM;
470: 		goto bad;
471: 	}
472: 	memmove(pagetable, p->pagetable, PGSIZE); // understand this and below
473: 	// Remove old u-map from the new pt, but don't free since later op might fail.
474: 	for (int i = 0; i < PX(2, MAXUVA); i++) {
475: 		pagetable[i] = 0;
476: 	}
477: 
478: 	__debug_info("execve", "get elf\n");
479: 	// Check ELF header
480: 	//printf("NNN: exe lock elf_ip\n");
481: 	ilock(ip);
482: 	struct elfhdr elf;
483: 	if (ip->fop->read(ip, 0, (uint64) &elf, 0, sizeof(elf)) != sizeof(elf) || elf.magic != ELF_MAGIC) { // check if the bytes read were right
484: 		iunlock(ip);
485: 		ret = -ENOEXEC;
486: 		goto bad;
487: 	}
488: 
489: 	//printf("NNN:IN exe_p1 %ip->ref = %d\n",ip->ref);
490: 
491: 	__debug_info("execve", "load from elf\n");
492: 	//printf("NNN execve load from elf\n");
493: 	// Load program (elf file) into memory. ----could be packed in a function
494: 	struct proghdr ph;
495: 	// the usage of elfaddr was replaced by ph_store
496: 	// uint64 elfaddr = 0;
497: 	int flags;
498: 	for (int i = 0, off = elf.phoff; i < elf.phnum; i++, off += sizeof(ph)) {
499: 		if (ip->fop->read(ip, 0, (uint64)&ph, off, sizeof(ph)) != sizeof(ph)) {
500: 			__debug_warn("execve", "fail to read ELF file\n");
501: 			iunlock(ip);
502: 			ret = -EIO;
503: 			goto bad;
504: 		}
505: 		if (ph.type == ELF_PROG_LOAD){ // 写错条件了, 导致直接没有load, 从而触发了各种访存异常
506: 			// goto normal1; -------- now we reorganize the layout of the code, thus solved
507: 			// if (ph.vaddr % PGSIZE != 0 || ph.memsz < ph.filesz || ph.vaddr + ph.memsz < ph.vaddr) {
508: 			if (ph.memsz < ph.filesz || ph.vaddr + ph.memsz < ph.vaddr) {
509: 				__debug_warn("execve", "funny ELF file: va=%p msz=0x%x fsz=0x%x\n", ph.vaddr, ph.memsz, ph.filesz);
510: 					iunlock(ip);
511: 				ret = -ENOEXEC;
512: 				goto bad;
513: 			}
514: 
515: 			// turn ELF flags to PTE flags
516: 			flags = 0;
517: 			flags |= (ph.flags & ELF_PROG_FLAG_EXEC) ? PTE_X : 0;
518: 			flags |= (ph.flags & ELF_PROG_FLAG_WRITE) ? PTE_W : 0;
519: 			flags |= (ph.flags & ELF_PROG_FLAG_READ) ? PTE_R : 0;
520: 			seg = newseg(pagetable, seghead, LOAD, ph.vaddr, ph.memsz, flags);//-------------A1!!!!!!!!!!
521: 			if(seg == NULL) {
522: 				__debug_warn("execve", "newseg fail: vaddr=%p, memsz=%d\n", ph.vaddr, ph.memsz);
523: 				iunlock(ip);
524: 				ret = -ENOMEM;
525: 				goto bad;
526: 			}
527: 			seghead = seg;
528: 
529: 			if (ph.off == 0){
530: 				// the usage of elfaddr was replaced by ph_store
531: 				// elfaddr = ph.vaddr;
532: 				if(!already_store){
533: 					// see the documents to know the meaning of the field in the struct
534: 					ph_store.vaddr = elf.phoff + ph.vaddr;
535: 				}
536: 			}
537: 			
538: 
539: 			seg = locateseg(seghead, ph.vaddr); // 在locate之前请看A1!!!
540: 			seg->f_off = ph.off;
541: 			seg->f_sz = ph.filesz;
542: 			if (ph.vaddr <= elf.entry && elf.entry < ph.vaddr + ph.filesz) {
543: 				__debug_info("execve", "meet entry segment: entry=0x%x, va=0x%x, sz=0x%x\n", elf.entry, ph.vaddr, ph.filesz);
544: 				if ((ret = loadseg(pagetable, elf.entry, seg, ip)) < 0) {
545: 					__debug_warn("execve", "load segment\n");
546: 					iunlock(ip);
547: 					goto bad;
548: 				}
549: 			}
550: 		}
551: 		else if(ph.type == ELF_PROG_PHDR){
552: 			ph_store = ph;
553: 			already_store = 1;
554: 		}else if(ph.type == ELF_PROG_INTERP){
555: 			dynamic_need = 1;
556: 		}else{
557: 			//printf("ph.type: %d ph.type != ELF_PROG_LOAD\n", ph.type);
558: 		}
559: // normal1:
560: 	}
561: 	//printf("NNN execve load finished!\n");
562: 	// NOTE!!!  since MMAP is in the higher address than HEAP, we should firstly alloc HEAP, and then using mmap to do dynamic link
563: 	// Heap
564: 	flags = PTE_R | PTE_W;
565: 	for (seg = seghead; seg->next != NULL; seg = seg->next); // reach the end of the list
566: 	uint64 brk = PGROUNDUP(seg->addr + seg->sz) + PGSIZE;
567: 	__debug_info("execve", "making heap, start=%p\n", brk);
568: 	seg = newseg(pagetable, seghead, HEAP, brk, 0, flags);
569: 	if(seg == NULL) {
570: 		__debug_warn("execve", "new heap fail\n");
571: 		ret = -ENOMEM;
572: 		goto bad;
573: 	}
574: 	seghead = seg;
575: 	//printf("NNN execve HEAP done!\n");
576: 
577: 	// when dynamic linking is needed
578: 	struct inode *intprtr;
579: 	struct elfhdr intprtr_hdr;
580: 
581: 	uint64 prog_entry, intprtr_sta_loc;
582: 	// Ques
583: 	// iunlockput(ip);
584: 	// //printf("NNN:IN exe_p1 %ip->ref = %d\n",ip->ref);
585: 	iunlock(ip); // IMP: when to put?
586: 	// ip = NULL; // to tell bad about whether is has already been put and unlocked
587: 	//printf("NNN:IN exe_p1.5 %ip->ref = %d\n",ip->ref);
588: 
589: 	__debug_info("execve", "making stack\n");
590: 	// Stack
591: 	uint64 sp = VUSTACK;
592: 	#define STACK_PAGES	35
593: 	// STACK位于MMAP的下方,与之错开, 两者以0x7000_0000为分界线
594: 	uint64 stackbase = VUSTACK - PGSIZE * STACK_PAGES;
595: 	seg = newseg(pagetable, seghead, STACK, stackbase, sp - stackbase, flags);
596: 	stackbase += PGSIZE * (STACK_PAGES - 1);
597: 	if(seg == NULL || uvmalloc(pagetable, stackbase, sp, flags) == 0) {
598: 		__debug_warn("execve", "new stack fail\n");
599: 		ret = -ENOMEM;
600: 		goto bad;
601: 	}
602: 	seghead = seg;
603: 	//printf("NNN execve STACk done!\n");
604: 
605: 	// prepare a NULL on the bottom of stack
606: 	sp -= sizeof(uint64);
607: 
608: 	__debug_info("execve", "pushing argv/envp\n");
609: 	//printf("NNN execve pushing argv/envp start!\n");
610: 	// arguments to user main(argc, argv, envp)
611: 	// argc is returned via the system call return
612: 	// value, which goes in a0.
613: 	int64 argc, envc;
614: 	uint64 uargv[MAXARG + 1], uenvp[MAXENV + 1];
615: 	// Problem occurrs here!!!
616: 	if ((envc = pushstack(pagetable, uenvp, envp, MAXENV, &sp)) < 0 ||
617: 		(argc = pushstack(pagetable, uargv, argv, MAXARG, &sp)) < 0) {
618: 		__debug_warn("execve", "fail to push argv or envp into user stack\n");
619: 		//printf("NNN execve fail to push argv or envp into user stack\n");
620: 		ret = envc < 0 ? envc : argc;
621: 		goto bad;
622: 	}
623: 	//printf("NNN In execve, write random copy start\n");
624: 
625: 	uint64 random[2] = { 0xcde142a16cb93072, 0x128a39c127d8bbf2 };
626: 	sp -= 16;
627: 	if (sp < stackbase || copyout(pagetable, sp, (char *)random, 16) < 0) {
628: 		ret = -EINVAL;
629: 		//printf("NNN In execve, write random copy bad\n");
630: 		goto bad;
631: 	}
632: 	// 注意stack的分配应该在dynamic映射之前
633: 	// note before and after
634: 	struct seg *oldseg = p->segment;
635: 	p->segment = seghead;
636: 	pagetable_t oldpagetable = p->pagetable; // 类似于中断的暂存保护
637: 	p->pagetable = pagetable;
638: 	
639: 	if(dynamic_need){ // bugs could be anywhere...
640: 		if((intprtr = namei("/libc.so")) == NULL){
641: 			printf("interpreter not found\n");
642: 			goto bad;
643: 		}
644: 		//printf("NNN: exe lock interpr_ip\n");
645: 		ilock(intprtr);
646: 		// 我把变量名写错啦!!!
647: 		// if(intprtr->fop->read(intprtr, 0, (uint64) &elf, 0, sizeof(intprtr_hdr)) != sizeof(intprtr_hdr) ){
648: 		if(intprtr->fop->read(intprtr, 0, (uint64) &intprtr_hdr, 0, sizeof(intprtr_hdr)) != sizeof(intprtr_hdr) ){
649: 			printf("read interpreter_hdr failed\n");
650:       		goto bad;
651: 		}
652: 		// todo:
653: 		// page table 和seghead都应该是新进程的
654: 		intprtr_sta_loc = load_elf_interp(pagetable,seghead, &intprtr_hdr, intprtr);
655: 		// intprtr_sta_loc = load_elf_interp_2(pagetable,seghead, &intprtr_hdr, intprtr, brk);
656: 		printf("NNN: Interpreter load done!!!\n");
657: 		// 使用相对定位
658: 		prog_entry = intprtr_sta_loc + intprtr_hdr.entry; 
659: 		//printf("interp_start_addr:%p program_entry:%p\n", interp_start_addr, program_entry);
660: 
661: 		iunlockput(intprtr);
662: 		intprtr = NULL;
663: 	}
664: 	else{
665: 		prog_entry = elf.entry;
666: 		intprtr_sta_loc = 0;
667: 	}
668: 	// p->segment = oldseg;
669: 
670: 	uint64 auxvec[][2] = {
671: 		// {AT_HWCAP, 0x112d},
672: 		{AT_HWCAP, 0},
673: 		{AT_PAGESZ, PGSIZE},
674: 		{AT_PHDR, ph_store.vaddr},
675: 		{AT_PHENT, elf.phentsize},
676: 		{AT_PHNUM, elf.phnum},
677: 		{AT_BASE, intprtr_sta_loc}, // 在这里传递动态链接的信息
678: 		{AT_ENTRY, elf.entry},
679: 		{AT_UID, 0},
680: 		{AT_EUID, 0},
681: 		{AT_GID, 0},
682: 		{AT_EGID, 0},
683: 		{AT_SECURE, 0},
684: 		{AT_RANDOM, sp},
685: 		{AT_NULL, 0}
686: 	};
687: 
688: 	sp -= 16 + sizeof(auxvec);
689: 	sp -= (envc + 1) * sizeof(uint64); // 注意补0
690: 	uint64 a2 = sp;
691: 	sp -= (argc + 1) * sizeof(uint64);
692: 	uint64 a1 = sp;
693: 	sp -= sizeof(uint64); // argc
694: 	flags = sp % 16;      // on risc-v, sp should be 16-byte aligned
695: 	sp -= flags;
696: 	a1 -= flags;
697: 	a2 -= flags;
698: 
699: 	__debug_info("execve", "pushing argv/envp table\n");
700: 	//printf("NNN In execve, pushing argv/envp table\n");
701: 	
702: 	if (sp < stackbase || 
703: 		copyout(pagetable, a2 + (envc + 1) * sizeof(uint64), (char *)auxvec, sizeof(auxvec)) < 0 ||
704: 		copyout(pagetable, a2, (char *)uenvp, (envc + 1) * sizeof(uint64)) < 0 ||
705: 		copyout(pagetable, a1, (char *)uargv, (argc + 1) * sizeof(uint64)) < 0 ||
706: 		copyout(pagetable, sp, (char *)&argc, sizeof(uint64)) < 0)
707: 	{
708: 		__debug_warn("execve", "fail to copy argv/envp table into user stack\n");
709: 		ret = -EINVAL;
710: 		goto bad;
711: 	}
712: 	p->trapframe->a1 = a1;
713: 	p->trapframe->a2 = a2;
714: 
715: 	// Save program name for debugging.
716: 	//printf("NNN:IN exe_p1.6 %ip->ref = %d\n",ip->ref);
717: 	safestrcpy(p->name, ip->entry->filename, sizeof(p->name));
718: 	//printf("NNN:IN exe_p1.7 %ip->ref = %d\n",ip->ref);
719: 
720: 	// Commit to the user image.
721: 	// seg = p->segment;
722: 	p->pagetable = pagetable;
723: 	p->segment = seghead;
724: 	p->pbrk = brk;
725: 	p->trapframe->epc = prog_entry;  // initial program counter = main
726: 	p->trapframe->sp = sp; // initial stack pointer
727: 	//printf("NNN:IN exe_p1.8 %ip->ref = %d\n",ip->ref);
728: 	// if (p->elf)
729: 	if (p->elf&&ip!=p->elf)
730: 		iput(p->elf);
731: 	p->elf = ip;
732: 	//printf("NNN:IN exe_p2 %ip->ref = %d\n",ip->ref);
733: 	// p->elf = idup(ip);
734: 
735: 	__debug_info("execve", "sp=%p, stackbase=%p, entry=%p\n", sp, stackbase, elf.entry);
736: 	fdcloexec(&p->fds);
737: 	w_satp(MAKE_SATP(p->pagetable));
738: 	sfence_vma();
739: 	w_sstatus_fs(SSTATUS_FS_CLEAN);
740: 
741: 	// delsegs(oldpagetable, seg);
742: 	delsegs(oldpagetable, oldseg);
743: 	uvmfree(oldpagetable);
744: 	freepage(oldpagetable);
745: 	/*
746: 	if(!p->killed){
747: 		delsegs(oldpagetable, seg);
748: 		uvmfree(oldpagetable);
749: 		freepage(oldpagetable);
750: 	}*/
751: 	//printf("NNN: execve done\n");
752: 	return 0;
753: 	// return argc; // this ends up in a0, the first argument to main(argc, argv)
754: 
755: 	bad:
756: 	__debug_warn("execve", "reach bad: seg=%p, pt=%p, ip=%p\n", seghead, pagetable, ip);
757: 	if (seghead) {
758: 		delsegs(pagetable, seghead);
759: 	}
760: 	if (pagetable) {
761: 		uvmfree(pagetable);
762: 		freepage(pagetable);
763: 	}
764: 	if (ip) {
765: 		iput(ip);
766: 	}
767: 	return ret;
768: }

xv6-k210@final / 来源候选源码

97: 		freepage(buf);
98: 	return -EFAULT;
99: }
100: 
101: // All argvs are pointers came from user space, and should be checked by sys_caller
102: int execve(char *path, char **argv, char **envp)
103: {
104: 	__debug_info("exeve", "path = %s\n", path);
105: 	int ret;
106: 	struct inode *ip = NULL;
107: 	pagetable_t pagetable = NULL;
108: 	struct seg *seghead = NULL, *seg;
109: 	struct proc *p = myproc();
110: 
111: 	__debug_info("execve", "in\n");
112: 	if ((ip = namei(path)) == NULL) {
113: 		__debug_warn("execve", "can't open %s\n", path);
114: 		ret = -ENOENT;
115: 		goto bad;
116: 	}
117: 
118: 	// Make a copy of p->pagetable without old user space, 
119: 	// but with the same kstack we are using now, which can't be changed.
120: 	if ((pagetable = (pagetable_t)allocpage()) == NULL) {
121: 		ret = -ENOMEM;
122: 		goto bad;
123: 	}
124: 	memmove(pagetable, p->pagetable, PGSIZE);
125: 	// Remove old u-map from the new pt, but don't free since later op might fail.
126: 	for (int i = 0; i < PX(2, MAXUVA); i++) {
127: 		pagetable[i] = 0;
128: 	}
129: 
130: 	__debug_info("execve", "get elf\n");
131: 	// Check ELF header
132: 	ilock(ip);
133: 	struct elfhdr elf;
134: 	if (ip->fop->read(ip, 0, (uint64) &elf, 0, sizeof(elf)) != sizeof(elf) || elf.magic != ELF_MAGIC) {
135: 		iunlock(ip);
136: 		ret = -ENOEXEC;
137: 		goto bad;
138: 	}
139: 
140: 	__debug_info("execve", "load from elf\n");
141: 	// Load program into memory.
142: 	struct proghdr ph;
143: 	uint64 elfaddr = 0;
144: 	int flags;
145: 	for (int i = 0, off = elf.phoff; i < elf.phnum; i++, off += sizeof(ph)) {
146: 		if (ip->fop->read(ip, 0, (uint64)&ph, off, sizeof(ph)) != sizeof(ph)) {
147: 			__debug_warn("execve", "fail to read ELF file\n");
148: 			iunlock(ip);
149: 			ret = -EIO;
150: 			goto bad;
151: 		}
152: 		if (ph.type != ELF_PROG_LOAD)
153: 			continue;
154: 		// if (ph.vaddr % PGSIZE != 0 || ph.memsz < ph.filesz || ph.vaddr + ph.memsz < ph.vaddr) {
155: 		if (ph.memsz < ph.filesz || ph.vaddr + ph.memsz < ph.vaddr) {
156: 			__debug_warn("execve", "funny ELF file: va=%p msz=0x%x fsz=0x%x\n", ph.vaddr, ph.memsz, ph.filesz);
157: 				iunlock(ip);
158: 			ret = -ENOEXEC;
159: 			goto bad;
160: 		}
161: 
162: 		// turn ELF flags to PTE flags
163: 		flags = 0;
164: 		flags |= (ph.flags & ELF_PROG_FLAG_EXEC) ? PTE_X : 0;
165: 		flags |= (ph.flags & ELF_PROG_FLAG_WRITE) ? PTE_W : 0;
166: 		flags |= (ph.flags & ELF_PROG_FLAG_READ) ? PTE_R : 0;
167: 		seg = newseg(pagetable, seghead, LOAD, ph.vaddr, ph.memsz, flags);
168: 		if(seg == NULL) {
169: 			__debug_warn("execve", "newseg fail: vaddr=%p, memsz=%d\n", ph.vaddr, ph.memsz);
170: 			iunlock(ip);
171: 			ret = -ENOMEM;
172: 			goto bad;
173: 		}
174: 		seghead = seg;
175: 
176: 		if (ph.off == 0)
177: 			elfaddr = ph.vaddr;
178: 
179: 		seg = locateseg(seghead, ph.vaddr);
180: 		seg->f_off = ph.off;
181: 		seg->f_sz = ph.filesz;
182: 		if (ph.vaddr <= elf.entry && elf.entry < ph.vaddr + ph.filesz) {
183: 			__debug_info("execve", "meet entry segment: entry=0x%x, va=0x%x, sz=0x%x\n", elf.entry, ph.vaddr, ph.filesz);
184: 			if ((ret = loadseg(pagetable, elf.entry, seg, ip)) < 0) {
185: 				__debug_warn("execve", "load segment\n");
186: 				iunlock(ip);
187: 				goto bad;
188: 			}
189: 		}
190: 	}
191: 	iunlock(ip);
192: 
193: 	// Heap
194: 	flags = PTE_R | PTE_W;
195: 	for (seg = seghead; seg->next != NULL; seg = seg->next);
196: 	uint64 brk = PGROUNDUP(seg->addr + seg->sz) + PGSIZE;
197: 	__debug_info("execve", "making heap, start=%p\n", brk);
198: 	seg = newseg(pagetable, seghead, HEAP, brk, 0, flags);
199: 	if(seg == NULL) {
200: 		__debug_warn("execve", "new heap fail\n");
201: 		ret = -ENOMEM;
202: 		goto bad;
203: 	}
204: 	seghead = seg;
205: 
206: 	__debug_info("execve", "making stack\n");
207: 	// Stack
208: 	uint64 sp = VUSTACK;
209: 	#define STACK_PAGES	35
210: 	uint64 stackbase = VUSTACK - PGSIZE * STACK_PAGES;
211: 	seg = newseg(pagetable, seghead, STACK, stackbase, sp - stackbase, flags);
212: 	stackbase += PGSIZE * (STACK_PAGES - 1);
213: 	if(seg == NULL || uvmalloc(pagetable, stackbase, sp, flags) == 0) {
214: 		__debug_warn("execve", "new stack fail\n");
215: 		ret = -ENOMEM;
216: 		goto bad;
217: 	}
218: 	seghead = seg;
219: 
220: 	// prepare a NULL on the bottom of stack
221: 	sp -= sizeof(uint64);
222: 	__debug_info("execve", "pushing argv/envp\n");
223: 	// arguments to user main(argc, argv, envp)
224: 	// argc is returned via the system call return
225: 	// value, which goes in a0.
226: 	int64 argc, envc;
227: 	uint64 uargv[MAXARG + 1], uenvp[MAXENV + 1];
228: 	if ((envc = pushstack(pagetable, uenvp, envp, MAXENV, &sp)) < 0 ||
229: 		(argc = pushstack(pagetable, uargv, argv, MAXARG, &sp)) < 0) {
230: 		__debug_warn("execve", "fail to push argv or envp into user stack\n");
231: 		ret = envc < 0 ? envc : argc;
232: 		goto bad;
233: 	}
234: 	uint64 random[2] = { 0xcde142a16cb93072, 0x128a39c127d8bbf2 };
235: 	sp -= 16;
236: 	if (sp < stackbase || copyout(pagetable, sp, (char *)random, 16) < 0) {
237: 		ret = -EINVAL;
238: 		goto bad;
239: 	}
240: 
241: 	uint64 auxvec[][2] = {
242: 		// {AT_HWCAP, 0x112d},
243: 		{AT_PAGESZ, PGSIZE},
244: 		{AT_PHDR, elf.phoff + elfaddr},
245: 		{AT_PHENT, elf.phentsize},
246: 		{AT_PHNUM, elf.phnum},
247: 		{AT_UID, 0},
248: 		{AT_EUID, 0},
249: 		{AT_GID, 0},
250: 		{AT_EGID, 0},
251: 		{AT_SECURE, 0},
252: 		{AT_RANDOM, sp},
253: 		{AT_NULL, 0}
254: 	};
255: 
256: 	sp -= 16 + sizeof(auxvec);
257: 	sp -= (envc + 1) * sizeof(uint64);
258: 	uint64 a2 = sp;
259: 	sp -= (argc + 1) * sizeof(uint64);
260: 	uint64 a1 = sp;
261: 	sp -= sizeof(uint64); // argc
262: 	flags = sp % 16;      // on risc-v, sp should be 16-byte aligned
263: 	sp -= flags;
264: 	a1 -= flags;
265: 	a2 -= flags;
266: 
267: 	__debug_info("execve", "pushing argv/envp table\n");
268: 	if (sp < stackbase || 
269: 		copyout(pagetable, a2 + (envc + 1) * sizeof(uint64), (char *)auxvec, sizeof(auxvec)) < 0 ||
270: 		copyout(pagetable, a2, (char *)uenvp, (envc + 1) * sizeof(uint64)) < 0 ||
271: 		copyout(pagetable, a1, (char *)uargv, (argc + 1) * sizeof(uint64)) < 0 ||
272: 		copyout(pagetable, sp, (char *)&argc, sizeof(uint64)) < 0)
273: 	{
274: 		__debug_warn("execve", "fail to copy argv/envp table into user stack\n");
275: 		ret = -EINVAL;
276: 		goto bad;
277: 	}
278: 	p->trapframe->a1 = a1;
279: 	p->trapframe->a2 = a2;
280: 
281: 	// Save program name for debugging.
282: 	safestrcpy(p->name, ip->entry->filename, sizeof(p->name));
283: 
284: 	// Commit to the user image.
285: 	pagetable_t oldpagetable = p->pagetable;
286: 	seg = p->segment;
287: 	p->pagetable = pagetable;
288: 	p->segment = seghead;
289: 	p->pbrk = brk;
290: 	p->trapframe->epc = elf.entry;  // initial program counter = main
291: 	p->trapframe->sp = sp; // initial stack pointer
292: 	if (p->elf)
293: 		iput(p->elf);
294: 	p->elf = ip;
295: 
296: 	__debug_info("execve", "sp=%p, stackbase=%p, entry=%p\n", sp, stackbase, elf.entry);
297: 	fdcloexec(&p->fds);
298: 	w_satp(MAKE_SATP(p->pagetable));
299: 	sfence_vma();
300: 	w_sstatus_fs(SSTATUS_FS_CLEAN);
301: 
302: 	delsegs(oldpagetable, seg);
303: 	uvmfree(oldpagetable);
304: 	freepage(oldpagetable);
305: 
306: 	return 0;
307: 	// return argc; // this ends up in a0, the first argument to main(argc, argv)
308: 
309: 	bad:
310: 	__debug_warn("execve", "reach bad: seg=%p, pt=%p, ip=%p\n", seghead, pagetable, ip);
311: 	if (seghead) {
312: 		delsegs(pagetable, seghead);
313: 	}
314: 	if (pagetable) {
315: 		uvmfree(pagetable);
316: 		freepage(pagetable);
317: 	}
318: 	if (ip) {
319: 		iput(ip);
320: 	}
321: 	return ret;
322: }

80: // Bufs pool.
81: static struct buf bufs[BNUM];
82: 
83: 
84: void
85: binit(void)
86: {
87: 	initlock(&bcachelock, "bcache");
88: 	dlist_init(&lru_head);
89: 	nwait = 0;
90: 	for (int i = 0; i < BCACHE_TABLE_SIZE; i++)
91: 		bcache[i] = NULL;
92:

94: 		b->refcnt = 0;
95: 		b->hash.pprev = NULL;
96: 		b->hash.next = NULL;
97: 		b->dirty = 0;
98: 		b->disk = 0;
99: 
100: 		dlist_add_before(&lru_head, &b->list);
101: 		initsleeplock(&b->lock, "buffer");
102: 	}

85: binit(void)
86: {
87: 	initlock(&bcachelock, "bcache");

匹配分数 0.776 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 0.922, "exact_ast_shape": false, "exact_token_fp": false, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 0.945}

oskernel2023-zmz@recover 源码

103: }
104: 
105: // Look through buffer cache for block on device dev.
106: // If not found, allocate a buffer.
107: // In either case, return locked buffer.
108: struct buf*
109: bget(uint dev, uint sectorno)
110: {
111: 	struct buf *b;
112: 	int idx = __hash_idx(sectorno);
113: 
114: 	acquire(&bcachelock);
115: 
116: 	/**
117: 	 * Is the block already cached? Look up in the hash list at first.
118: 	 * No matter which list the target buf stays now, we can always get
119: 	 * it from the hash list (as long as it is cached).
120: 	 */
121: rescan:
122: 	for (list_node_t *l = bcache[idx]; l != NULL; l = l->next) {
123: 		b = container_of(l, struct buf, hash);
124: 		if (b->dev == dev && b->sectorno == sectorno) {
125: 			if (l != bcache[idx]) {		// not the first node
126: 				_list_remove(l);		// move it to the head (also lru)
127: 				_list_push_front(&bcache[idx], l);
128: 			}
129: 			if (b->refcnt++ == 0) {
130: 				/**
131: 				 * We are the first visitor.
132: 				 * This indicates that this buf is in the @lru_head list.
133: 				 * We need to move it out in case of being evicted.
134: 				 * The last one who keep the buf will push it back.
135: 				 */
136: 				dlist_del(&b->list);
137: 			}
138: 			release(&bcachelock);
139: 			acquiresleep(&b->lock);
140: 			return b;
141: 		}
142: 	}
143: 
144: 	/**
145: 	 * We are here due to cache miss.
146: 	 * Just get one from the tail of @lru_head list.
147: 	 * This may cause an eviction, but it is safe.
148: 	 */
149: 	struct d_list *dl = lru_head.prev;
150: 	if (dl != &lru_head) {
151: 		b = container_of(dl, struct buf, list);
152: 		b->dev = dev;
153: 		b->sectorno = sectorno;
154: 		b->refcnt = 1;
155: 
156: 		if (b->hash.pprev) {		// if we were in a hash list
157: 			_list_remove(&b->hash);
158: 		}
159: 		_list_push_front(&bcache[idx], &b->hash);
160: 		dlist_del(&b->list);
161: 
162: 		acquiresleep(&b->lock);		// this should not block
163: 		b->valid = 0;
164: 		release(&bcachelock);		// or we will sched() with this spinlock
165: 		return b;
166: 	}
167: 
168: 	/**
169: 	 * We are here because the @lru_head list is
170: 	 * empty, we have to go to sleep and wait.
171: 	 * When we wake up, we need to re-scan both hash
172: 	 * and lru list, in case of some procs who need
173: 	 * the identical buf and wake up before us,
174: 	 * taking a buf then insert into the hash list.
175: 	 */
176: 	nwait++;
177: 	// printf("\n"__INFO("bget")" sleep\n");
178: 	sleep(&nwait, &bcachelock);
179: 	nwait--;
180: 	goto rescan;
181: }
182: 
183: // Return a locked buf with the contents of the indicated block.
184: struct buf* 
185: bread(uint dev, uint sectorno)
186: {

104: 
105: // Look through buffer cache for block on device dev.
106: // If not found, allocate a buffer.
107: // In either case, return locked buffer.
108: struct buf*
109: bget(uint dev, uint sectorno)
110: {
111: 	struct buf *b;
112: 	int idx = __hash_idx(sectorno);
113: 
114: 	acquire(&bcachelock);
115: 
116: 	/**
117: 	 * Is the block already cached? Look up in the hash list at first.
118: 	 * No matter which list the target buf stays now, we can always get
119: 	 * it from the hash list (as long as it is cached).
120: 	 */
121: rescan:
122: 	for (list_node_t *l = bcache[idx]; l != NULL; l = l->next) {
123: 		b = container_of(l, struct buf, hash);
124: 		if (b->dev == dev && b->sectorno == sectorno) {
125: 			if (l != bcache[idx]) {		// not the first node
126: 				_list_remove(l);		// move it to the head (also lru)
127: 				_list_push_front(&bcache[idx], l);
128: 			}
129: 			if (b->refcnt++ == 0) {
130: 				/**
131: 				 * We are the first visitor.
132: 				 * This indicates that this buf is in the @lru_head list.
133: 				 * We need to move it out in case of being evicted.
134: 				 * The last one who keep the buf will push it back.
135: 				 */
136: 				dlist_del(&b->list);
137: 			}
138: 			release(&bcachelock);
139: 			acquiresleep(&b->lock);
140: 			return b;
141: 		}
142: 	}
143: 
144: 	/**
145: 	 * We are here due to cache miss.
146: 	 * Just get one from the tail of @lru_head list.
147: 	 * This may cause an eviction, but it is safe.
148: 	 */
149: 	struct d_list *dl = lru_head.prev;
150: 	if (dl != &lru_head) {
151: 		b = container_of(dl, struct buf, list);
152: 		b->dev = dev;
153: 		b->sectorno = sectorno;
154: 		b->refcnt = 1;
155: 
156: 		if (b->hash.pprev) {		// if we were in a hash list
157: 			_list_remove(&b->hash);
158: 		}
159: 		_list_push_front(&bcache[idx], &b->hash);
160: 		dlist_del(&b->list);
161: 
162: 		acquiresleep(&b->lock);		// this should not block
163: 		b->valid = 0;
164: 		release(&bcachelock);		// or we will sched() with this spinlock
165: 		return b;
166: 	}
167: 
168: 	/**
169: 	 * We are here because the @lru_head list is
170: 	 * empty, we have to go to sleep and wait.
171: 	 * When we wake up, we need to re-scan both hash
172: 	 * and lru list, in case of some procs who need
173: 	 * the identical buf and wake up before us,
174: 	 * taking a buf then insert into the hash list.
175: 	 */
176: 	disk_write_start();
177: 	nwait++;
178: 	// printf("\n"__INFO("bget")" sleep\n");
179: 	sleep(&nwait, &bcachelock);
180: 	nwait--;
181: 	goto rescan;
182: }

185: struct buf* 
186: bread(uint dev, uint sectorno)

匹配分数 1.000 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": true, "exact_token_fp": true, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 1.0}

192: 	}

195: 
196: // Write b's contents to disk.  Must be locked.
197: void 
198: bwrite(struct buf *b)
199: {
200: 	/**
201: 	 * This function doesn't behave the same as the old one
202: 	 * any more. In our case, we only commit the buf to the
203: 	 * disk driver and directly come back without waiting.
204: 	 * Most importantly, we release the buf in this function
205: 	 * instead of brelse(). For two reasons:
206: 	 * 1. bread() and brelse() make a pair for read cases,
207: 	 * while bread() (or bget() if we'll write cover the buf)
208: 	 * and bwrite() make the other pair for write cases.
209: 	 * 2. We need different operations.
210: 	 */
211: 	
212: 	int res;
213: 	/**
214: 	 * Submit a write request to disk driver. If get positive
215: 	 * result, it means the buf it new in driver's queue, so
216: 	 * we need to keep a ref for the driver.
217: 	 */
218: 	res = disk_submit(b);
219: 	b->valid = 1;
220: 	releasesleep(&b->lock);
221: 	if ((res & 0x1) == 0)
222: 		bput(b);
223: 	if ((res & 0x2) == 0)
224: 		disk_write_start();
225: }
226: 
227: // Release a locked buffer.
228: // Move to the head of the most-recently-used list.
229: void
230: brelse(struct buf *b)

xv6-k210@final / 来源候选源码

193: 	}
194: 	return b;
195: }
196: 
197: // Write b's contents to disk.  Must be locked.
198: void 
199: bwrite(struct buf *b)
200: {
201: 	/**
202: 	 * This function doesn't behave the same as the old one
203: 	 * any more. In our case, we only commit the buf to the
204: 	 * disk driver and directly come back without waiting.
205: 	 * Most importantly, we release the buf in this function
206: 	 * instead of brelse(). For two reasons:
207: 	 * 1. bread() and brelse() make a pair for read cases,
208: 	 * while bread() (or bget() if we'll write cover the buf)
209: 	 * and bwrite() make the other pair for write cases.
210: 	 * 2. We need different operations.
211: 	 */
212: 	
213: 	int res;
214: 	/**
215: 	 * Submit a write request to disk driver. If get positive
216: 	 * result, it means the buf it new in driver's queue, so
217: 	 * we need to keep a ref for the driver.
218: 	 */
219: 	res = disk_submit(b);
220: 	b->valid = 1;
221: 	releasesleep(&b->lock);
222: 	if ((res & 0x1) == 0)
223: 		bput(b);
224: 	if ((res & 0x2) == 0)
225: 		disk_write_start();
226: }

236: void bsync(void)
237: {
238: 	disk_write_start();
239: }
240: 
241: int breads(struct buf *bufs[], int nbuf)
242: {
243: 	int i = 0, j, ret = 0;
244: 
245: 	// find consecutive sectors
246: 	while (i < nbuf) {
247: 		if (!bufs[i]->valid) {
248: 			for (j = i + 1;
249: 				j < nbuf && !bufs[j]->valid &&
250: 				bufs[j]->sectorno == bufs[j - 1]->sectorno + 1;
251: 				j++);
252: 			j--;
253: 			if (i == j)
254: 				ret = disk_read(bufs[i]);
255: 			else
256: 				ret = disk_multiple_read(&bufs[i], j - i + 1);
257: 			if (ret < 0)
258: 				break;
259: 			while (i <= j)
260: 				bufs[i++]->valid = 1;
261: 		} else {
262: 			i++;
263: 		}
264: 	}
265: 
266: 	return ret;
267: }
268: 
269: void bput(struct buf *b)
270: {
271: 	acquire(&bcachelock);
272: 	b->refcnt--;

xv6-k210@final / 来源候选源码

258: 			if (ret < 0)
259: 				break;
260: 			while (i <= j)

262: 		} else {

269: void bput(struct buf *b)
270: {
271: 	acquire(&bcachelock);
272: 	b->refcnt--;
273: 	if (b->refcnt == 0) {
274: 		// no one is waiting for it.
275: 		dlist_add_after(&lru_head, &b->list);
276: 
277: 		// available buf for someone who's in sleep-wait
278: 		if (nwait > 0)
279: 			wakeup(&nwait);
280: 	}
281: 	release(&bcachelock);
282: }
283: 
284: void bprint()
285: {
286: 	acquire(&bcachelock);
287: 	printf("\nbuf cache:\n");

284: void bprint()
285: {
286: 	acquire(&bcachelock);
287: 	printf("\nbuf cache:\n");
288: 	for (int i = 0; i < BCACHE_TABLE_SIZE; i++) {
289: 		list_node_t *l = bcache[i];
290: 		if (l == NULL) { continue; }
291: 		printf(__INFO("%d")" ", i);
292: 		for (; l != NULL; l = l->next) {
293: 			struct buf *b = container_of(l, struct buf, hash);
294: 			printf("%d ", b->sectorno);
295: 		}
296: 		printf("\n");
297: 	}
298: 	release(&bcachelock);
299: }

284: 
285: void bprint()
286: {
287: 	acquire(&bcachelock);
288: 	printf("\nbuf cache:\n");
289: 	for (int i = 0; i < BCACHE_TABLE_SIZE; i++) {
290: 		list_node_t *l = bcache[i];
291: 		if (l == NULL) { continue; }
292: 		printf(__INFO("%d")" ", i);
293: 		for (; l != NULL; l = l->next) {
294: 			struct buf *b = container_of(l, struct buf, hash);
295: 			printf("%d ", b->sectorno);
296: 		}
297: 		printf("\n");
298: 	}
299: 	release(&bcachelock);
300: }

36: static int img_write_block(struct superblock *sb, int usr, char *src, uint64 sectorno, uint64 off, uint64 len)
37: {
38: 	__debug_info("imgfs_write", "sec:%d off:%d len:%d\n", sectorno, off, len);
39: 	
40: 	struct inode *img = sb->dev;
41: 	
42: 	ilock(img);
43: 	int ret = img->fop->write(img, usr, (uint64)src, sb->blocksz * sectorno + off, len);
44: 	iunlock(img);
45: 
46: 	return ret;
47: }

63: 
64: static int img_clear_block(struct superblock *sb, uint64 sectorno, uint64 sectorcnt)
65: {
66: 	struct inode *img = sb->dev;
67: 	int ret = 0;
68: 	uint64 const blksz = sb->blocksz;
69: 
70: 	char *buf = kmalloc(blksz);
71: 	if (buf == NULL) 
72: 		return -1;
73: 	memset(buf, 0, blksz);
74: 
75: 	uint64 pos = sectorno * blksz;
76: 	ilock(img);
77: 	for (; sectorcnt--; pos += blksz) {
78: 		if (img->fop->write(img, 0, (uint64)buf, pos, blksz) != blksz) {
79: 			ret = -1;
80: 			break;
81: 		}
82: 	}
83: 	iunlock(img);
84: 	
85: 	kfree(buf);
86: 	return ret;
87: }
88:

89: 
90: int disk_write_block(struct superblock *sb, int usr, char *src, uint64 sectorno, uint64 off, uint64 len)
91: {
92: 	if (off + len > BSIZE)
93: 		panic("diskfs_write");
94: 
95: 	// __debug_info("diskfs_write", "sec:%d off:%d len:%d\n", sectorno, off, len);
96: 	struct buf *b;
97: 
98: 	if (off == 0 && len == BSIZE)
99: 		/**
100: 		 * It hints that the coming write totally
101: 		 * covers the sector. So the original data
102: 		 * will be overwritten, and we don't need
103: 		 * to read the disk.
104: 		 */
105: 		b = bget(sb->devnum, sectorno);
106: 	else
107: 		b = bread(sb->devnum, sectorno);
108: 	
109: 	int ret = either_copyin_nocheck(b->data + off, usr, (uint64)src, len);
110: 
111: 	if (ret == 0)
112: 		ret = len;
113: 	bwrite(b);
114: 
115: 	return ret;
116: }
117:

匹配分数 1.000 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": true, "exact_token_fp": true, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 1.0}

117: 
118: 
119: int disk_read_block(struct superblock *sb, int usr, char *dst, uint64 sectorno, uint64 off, uint64 len)
120: {
121: 	// if (off + len > BSIZE)
122: 	// 	panic("diskfs_read");
123: 	int ret;
124: 	if (off + len <= BSIZE) {
125: 		// __debug_info("diskfs_read", "sec:%d off:%d len:%d\n", sectorno, off, len);
126: 		struct buf *b = bread(sb->devnum, sectorno);
127: 		ret = either_copyout_nocheck(usr, (uint64)dst, b->data + off, len);
128: 		brelse(b);
129: 	} else {	// over sectors
130: 		int const cnt = (off + len + BSIZE - 1) / BSIZE;
131: 		int i;
132: 		uint32 m, tot = 0;
133: 		struct buf *bufs[cnt];
134: 		// gathering buffers
135: 		for (i = 0; i < cnt; i++)
136: 			bufs[i] = bget(sb->devnum, sectorno++);
137: 
138: 		ret = breads(bufs, cnt);
139: 		if (ret >= 0) {
140: 			m = BSIZE - off;
141: 			ret = either_copyout_nocheck(usr, (uint64)dst, bufs[0]->data + off, m);
142: 			tot += m;
143: 		}
144: 		for (i = 1, m = BSIZE; ret >= 0 && i < cnt; i++) {
145: 			if (len - tot < BSIZE)
146: 				m = len - tot;
147: 			ret = either_copyout_nocheck(usr, (uint64)dst + tot, bufs[i]->data, m);
148: 			tot += m;
149: 		}
150: 		for (i = 0; i < cnt; i++)
151: 			brelse(bufs[i]);
152: 	}
153: 	return ret < 0 ? ret : len;
154: }
155: 
156: 
157: int disk_clear_block(struct superblock *sb, uint64 sectorno, uint64 sectorcnt)
158: {
159: 	struct buf *b;

156: 
157: int disk_clear_block(struct superblock *sb, uint64 sectorno, uint64 sectorcnt)
158: {
159: 	struct buf *b;
160: 	while (sectorcnt--) {
161: 		b = bget(sb->devnum, sectorno++);	// we don't care about the original data
162: 		memset(b->data, 0, BSIZE);
163: 		bwrite(b);
164: 	}
165: 	return 0;
166: }

匹配分数 1.000 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": true, "exact_token_fp": true, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 1.0}

167: 
168: 
169: struct superblock *fs_install(struct inode *dev)
170: {
171: 	int isdev;
172: 
173: 	__debug_info("fs_install", "enter check\n");
174: 	if (dev->mode & S_IFBLK) {
175: 		if (dev->sb != &devfs)
176: 			return NULL;
177: 		isdev = 1;
178: 	} else if (dev->mode & S_IFREG)
179: 		isdev = 0;
180: 	else
181: 		return NULL;
182: 
183: 	__debug_info("fs_install", "begin\n");
184: 
185: 	struct inode *iroot = NULL;
186: 	struct superblock *sb = fat32_get_sb();
187: 
188: 	if (sb == NULL)
189: 		return NULL;
190: 
191: 	sb->op.alloc_inode = fat_alloc_inode;
192: 	sb->op.destroy_inode = fat_destroy_inode;
193: 	sb->op.statfs = fat_stat_fs;
194: 	sb->op.sync = fat32_sync_sb;
195: 	if (isdev) {
196: 		sb->op.read = disk_read_block;
197: 		sb->op.write = disk_write_block;
198: 		sb->op.clear = disk_clear_block;
199: 	} else {
200: 		sb->op.read = img_read_block;
201: 		sb->op.write = img_write_block;
202: 		sb->op.clear = img_clear_block;
203: 	}
204: 
205: 	initsleeplock(&sb->sb_lock, "fs_sb");
206: 	initlock(&sb->cache_lock, "fs_dcache");
207: 
208: 	sb->dev = idup(dev);
209: 	sb->devnum = dev->dev;
210: 	sb->next = NULL;
211: 	sb->ref = 0;
212: 
213: 	__debug_info("fs_install", "read superblock\n");
214: 	iroot = fat32_init(sb);
215: 	if (iroot == NULL)
216: 		goto fail;
217: 
218: 	if (isdev && sb->blocksz != BSIZE)
219: 		goto fail;
220: 
221: 	__debug_info("fs_install", "stub 1\n");
222: 	sb->root = kmalloc(sizeof(struct dentry));
223: 	if (sb->root == NULL)
224: 		goto fail;
225: 
226: 	__debug_info("fs_install", "stub 2\n");
227: 	iroot->entry = sb->root;
228: 
229: 	// Initialize in-mem dentry struct for root.
230: 	memset(sb->root, 0, sizeof(struct dentry));
231: 	sb->root->inode = iroot;
232: 	sb->root->op = &generic_dop;
233: 	__debug_info("fs_install", "done\n");
234: 
235: 	return sb;
236: 
237: fail:
238: 	__debug_warn("fs_install", "fail\n");
239: 	iput(dev);
240: 	if (iroot)
241: 		fat_destroy_inode(iroot);
242: 	if (sb)
243: 		fat32_kill_sb(sb);
244: 	return NULL;
245: }
246: 
247: static void fs_clean_dentry(struct superblock *sb, struct dentry *de)
248: {
249: 	sb->op.destroy_inode(de->inode);
250: 	for (struct dentry *child = de->child; child != NULL;) {

xv6-k210@final / 来源候选源码

164: 	}
165: 	return 0;
166: }
167: 
168: 
169: struct superblock *fs_install(struct inode *dev)
170: {
171: 	int isdev;
172: 
173: 	__debug_info("fs_install", "enter check\n");
174: 	if (dev->mode & S_IFBLK) {
175: 		if (dev->sb != &devfs)
176: 			return NULL;
177: 		isdev = 1;
178: 	} else if (dev->mode & S_IFREG)
179: 		isdev = 0;
180: 	else
181: 		return NULL;
182: 
183: 	__debug_info("fs_install", "begin\n");
184: 
185: 	struct inode *iroot = NULL;
186: 	struct superblock *sb = fat32_get_sb();
187: 
188: 	if (sb == NULL)
189: 		return NULL;
190: 
191: 	sb->op.alloc_inode = fat_alloc_inode;
192: 	sb->op.destroy_inode = fat_destroy_inode;
193: 	sb->op.statfs = fat_stat_fs;
194: 	sb->op.sync = fat32_sync_sb;
195: 	if (isdev) {
196: 		sb->op.read = disk_read_block;
197: 		sb->op.write = disk_write_block;
198: 		sb->op.clear = disk_clear_block;
199: 	} else {
200: 		sb->op.read = img_read_block;
201: 		sb->op.write = img_write_block;
202: 		sb->op.clear = img_clear_block;
203: 	}
204: 
205: 	initsleeplock(&sb->sb_lock, "fs_sb");
206: 	initlock(&sb->cache_lock, "fs_dcache");
207: 
208: 	sb->dev = idup(dev);
209: 	sb->devnum = dev->dev;
210: 	sb->next = NULL;
211: 	sb->ref = 0;
212: 
213: 	__debug_info("fs_install", "read superblock\n");
214: 	iroot = fat32_init(sb);
215: 	if (iroot == NULL)
216: 		goto fail;
217: 
218: 	if (isdev && sb->blocksz != BSIZE)
219: 		goto fail;
220: 
221: 	__debug_info("fs_install", "stub 1\n");
222: 	sb->root = kmalloc(sizeof(struct dentry));
223: 	if (sb->root == NULL)
224: 		goto fail;
225: 
226: 	__debug_info("fs_install", "stub 2\n");
227: 	iroot->entry = sb->root;
228: 
229: 	// Initialize in-mem dentry struct for root.
230: 	memset(sb->root, 0, sizeof(struct dentry));
231: 	sb->root->inode = iroot;
232: 	sb->root->op = &generic_dop;
233: 	__debug_info("fs_install", "done\n");
234: 
235: 	return sb;
236: 
237: fail:
238: 	__debug_warn("fs_install", "fail\n");
239: 	iput(dev);
240: 	if (iroot)
241: 		fat_destroy_inode(iroot);
242: 	if (sb)
243: 		fat32_kill_sb(sb);
244: 	return NULL;
245: }
246:

248: {
249: 	sb->op.destroy_inode(de->inode);
250: 	for (struct dentry *child = de->child; child != NULL;) {

247: static void fs_clean_dentry(struct superblock *sb, struct dentry *de)
248: {
249: 	sb->op.destroy_inode(de->inode);
250: 	for (struct dentry *child = de->child; child != NULL;) {
251: 		struct dentry *temp = child->next;
252: 		fs_clean_dentry(sb, child);
253: 		child = temp;
254: 	}
255: 	kfree(de);
256: }

247: static void fs_clean_dentry(struct superblock *sb, struct dentry *de)
248: {
249: 	sb->op.destroy_inode(de->inode);
250: 	for (struct dentry *child = de->child; child != NULL;) {
251: 		struct dentry *temp = child->next;
252: 		fs_clean_dentry(sb, child);
253: 		child = temp;
254: 	}
255: 	kfree(de);
256: }

52: 
53: uint32 alloc_clus(struct superblock *sb)
54: {
55: 	struct fat32_sb *fat = sb2fat(sb);
56: 	uint32 newclus;
57: 
58: 	acquiresleep(&sb->sb_lock);
59: 	newclus = fat->next_free;
60: 	if (newclus == 0) {
61: 		if (fat->free_count == 0) {
62: 			releasesleep(&sb->sb_lock);
63: 			return 0;
64: 		}
65: 		__alert_fs_err("alloc_clus");
66: 		panic("alloc_clus: no next free but free count is not zero");
67: 	}
68: 	if (fat->free_count == 0) {
69: 		__alert_fs_err("alloc_clus");
70: 		panic("alloc_clus: got next free but free count is zero");
71: 	}
72: 
73: 	fat_update_next_free(sb);
74: 	releasesleep(&sb->sb_lock);
75: 
76: 	sb->op.clear(sb, first_sec_of_clus(fat, newclus), fat->bpb.sec_per_clus);
77: 	return newclus;
78: }
79: 
80: int free_clus(struct superblock *sb, uint32 cluster)
81: {
82: 	return write_fat(sb, cluster, 0);
83: }

100: // static struct clus_table *search_clus_table(struct rb_node *node, uint64
101: 
102: static int fill_clus_table(struct superblock *sb, struct clus_table *table, uint32 preclus, int alloc_end_idx)
103: {
104: 	uint32 clus;
105: 	int i;
106: 	if ((i = table->num) > 0) {		// If table is not empty...
107: 		preclus = table->clus[i - 1];
108: 	} else if (preclus == 0) {		// or take the arg preclus
109: 		panic("fill_clus_table: bad pre-clus");
110: 	}
111: 
112: 	for (; i < CLUS_TABLE_SIZE; i++) {
113: 		clus = read_fat(sb, preclus);	// get next cluster number
114: 		// __debug_info("fill_clus_table", "read: i=%d, clus=0x%x, base=%d\n", i, clus, table->base);
115: 		if (clus >= FAT32_EOC) {
116: 			while (i <= alloc_end_idx) {
117: 				if ((clus = alloc_clus(sb)) == 0 || write_fat(sb, preclus, clus) < 0) {
118: 					if (clus)
119: 						write_fat(sb, clus, 0);
120: 					table->num = i;
121: 					return -1;
122: 				}
123: 				preclus = table->clus[i] = clus;
124: 				// __debug_info("fill_clus_table", "write: i=%d, clus=0x%x\n", i, clus);
125: 				i++;
126: 			}
127: 			break;
128: 		} else {
129: 			table->clus[i] = clus;
130: 		}
131: 		preclus = clus;
132: 	}
133: 
134: 	// __debug_info("fill_clus_table", "end: i=%d, alloc_end_idx=%d\n", i, alloc_end_idx);
135: 	table->num = i;
136: 	return i;
137: }
138:

140: {
141: 	struct fat32_entry *entry = i2fat(ip);
142: 	struct fat32_sb *fat = sb2fat(ip->sb);

101: 
102: static int fill_clus_table(struct superblock *sb, struct clus_table *table,
103: 					uint32 preclus, int alloc_end_idx, int erase)
104: {
105: 	uint32 clus;
106: 	int i;
107: 	if ((i = table->num) > 0) {		// If table is not empty...
108: 		preclus = table->clus[i - 1];
109: 	} else if (preclus == 0) {		// or take the arg preclus
110: 		panic("fill_clus_table: bad pre-clus");
111: 	}
112: 
113: 	for (; i < CLUS_TABLE_SIZE; i++) {
114: 		clus = read_fat(sb, preclus);	// get next cluster number
115: 		// __debug_info("fill_clus_table", "read: i=%d, clus=0x%x, base=%d\n", i, clus, table->base);
116: 		if (clus >= FAT32_EOC) {
117: 			struct fat32_sb *fat = sb2fat(sb);
118: 			while (i <= alloc_end_idx) {
119: 				if ((clus = alloc_clus(sb)) == 0 || write_fat(sb, preclus, clus) < 0) {
120: 					if (clus)
121: 						write_fat(sb, clus, 0);
122: 					table->num = i;
123: 					return -1;
124: 				}
125: 				if (erase) {
126: 					sb->op.clear(sb, first_sec_of_clus(fat, clus), fat->bpb.sec_per_clus);
127: 				}
128: 				preclus = table->clus[i] = clus;
129: 				// __debug_info("fill_clus_table", "write: i=%d, clus=0x%x\n", i, clus);
130: 				i++;
131: 			}
132: 			break;
133: 		} else {
134: 			table->clus[i] = clus;
135: 		}
136: 		preclus = clus;
137: 	}
138: 
139: 	// __debug_info("fill_clus_table", "end: i=%d, alloc_end_idx=%d\n", i, alloc_end_idx);
140: 	table->num = i;
141: 	return i;
142: }

147: 	struct fat32_sb *fat = sb2fat(ip->sb);
expand_rb_clus修改候选
oskernel2023-zmz@recover 第 139 行 · 主要确定性来源：xv6-k210/kernel/fs/fat32/cluster.c::expand_rb_clus

候选来源与匹配信号

xv6-k210/kernel/fs/fat32/cluster.c::expand_rb_clus
匹配分数 0.783 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": false, "exact_token_fp": false, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 0.945}

oskernel2023-zmz@recover 源码

134: 	// __debug_info("fill_clus_table", "end: i=%d, alloc_end_idx=%d\n", i, alloc_end_idx);
135: 	table->num = i;
136: 	return i;
137: }
138: 
139: static struct clus_table *expand_rb_clus(struct inode *ip, uint off, int alloc)
140: {
141: 	struct fat32_entry *entry = i2fat(ip);
142: 	struct fat32_sb *fat = sb2fat(ip->sb);
143: 	struct rb_node *parent = rb_last(&entry->rb_clus);
144: 
145: 	uint const clus_cnt = off / fat->byts_per_clus;
146: 	uint const dst_key = clus_cnt / CLUS_TABLE_SIZE;
147: 	int const dst_idx = clus_cnt % CLUS_TABLE_SIZE;
148: 
149: 	// __debug_info("expand_rb_clus", "clus_cnt=%d, dstkey=%d, dstidx=%d, parent=%p\n",
150: 	// 			clus_cnt, dst_key, dst_idx, parent);
151: 
152: 	struct clus_table *table;
153: 	struct rb_node **p;
154: 	uint base_key;
155: 	uint32 clus;
156: 	int idx;
157: 
158: 	// First, locate the last clus-table to get last base and index
159: 	if (parent == NULL) { // no nodes in rb_tree
160: 		table = kmalloc(sizeof(struct clus_table));
161: 		if (table == NULL)
162: 			goto fail;
163: 
164: 		memset(table->clus, 0, sizeof(table->clus));
165: 		table->base = 0;
166: 		table->num = 1;
167: 		table->clus[0] = entry->first_clus;
168: 		__debug_info("expand_rb_clus", "first: clus=%d\n", entry->first_clus);
169: 		parent = &table->rb;
170: 		rb_link_node(parent, NULL, &entry->rb_clus.rb_node);
171: 		rb_insert_color(parent, &entry->rb_clus);
172: 	} else {
173: 		table = rb_clus_table(parent);
174: 	}
175: 
176: 	if (table->num < CLUS_TABLE_SIZE) {	// step 1: fill the last table
177: 		idx = alloc ? 
178: 				(table->base == dst_key ? dst_idx : CLUS_TABLE_SIZE - 1) : -1;
179: 		
180: 		idx = fill_clus_table(ip->sb, table, 0, idx);
181: 		if (idx < 0)						// case: allocation fail
182: 			goto fail;
183: 		else if (idx < CLUS_TABLE_SIZE)		// case: meet end of fat chain
184: 			return table;
185: 	}
186: 
187: 	base_key = table->base + 1;
188: 	clus = table->clus[CLUS_TABLE_SIZE - 1];
189: 	p = &parent->rb_right;
190: 
191: 	while (base_key <= dst_key) {	// allocate new table
192: 		table = kmalloc(sizeof(struct clus_table));
193: 		if (table == NULL)
194: 			goto fail;
195: 
196: 		table->base = base_key;
197: 		memset(table->clus, 0, sizeof(table->clus));
198: 		table->num = 0;
199: 
200: 		// __debug_info("expand_rb_clus", "newtable: base=%d, pre-clus=0x%x\n", base_key, clus);
201: 		idx = alloc ? 
202: 				(table->base == dst_key ? dst_idx : CLUS_TABLE_SIZE - 1) : -1;
203: 		idx = fill_clus_table(ip->sb, table, clus, idx);
204: 		if (idx < 0) {						// case: allocation fail
205: 			if (table->num == 0)
206: 				kfree(table);
207: 			goto fail;
208: 		} else if (idx == 0) {				// case: meet end of fat chain
209: 			kfree(table);					// don't keep empty table
210: 			return rb_clus_table(parent);
211: 		}
212: 
213: 		rb_link_node(&table->rb, parent, p);
214: 		rb_insert_color(&table->rb, &entry->rb_clus);
215: 
216: 		if (table->num < CLUS_TABLE_SIZE)	// not full, meet end of fat chain
217: 			break;
218: 
219: 		base_key++;
220: 		clus = table->clus[CLUS_TABLE_SIZE - 1];
221: 		parent = &table->rb;		// table is now the right-most node
222: 		p = &table->rb.rb_right;	// the next node is the next right-most one
223: 	}
224: 
225: 	return table;
226: 
227: fail: // If fail, no need to free tables that have been allocated (for future use).
228: 	__debug_warn("expand_rb_clus", "fail\n");
229: 	return NULL;
230: }

232: 
233: /**
234:  * for the given entry, relocate the cur_clus field based on the off
235:  * @param   ip          to get its fat32_entry and modify the cur_clus field

xv6-k210@final / 来源候选源码

139: 	// __debug_info("fill_clus_table", "end: i=%d, alloc_end_idx=%d\n", i, alloc_end_idx);
140: 	table->num = i;
141: 	return i;
142: }
143: 
144: static struct clus_table *expand_rb_clus(struct inode *ip, uint off, int alloc)
145: {
146: 	struct fat32_entry *entry = i2fat(ip);
147: 	struct fat32_sb *fat = sb2fat(ip->sb);
148: 	struct rb_node *parent = rb_last(&entry->rb_clus);
149: 
150: 	uint const clus_cnt = off / fat->byts_per_clus;
151: 	uint const dst_key = clus_cnt / CLUS_TABLE_SIZE;
152: 	int const dst_idx = clus_cnt % CLUS_TABLE_SIZE;
153: 
154: 	// __debug_info("expand_rb_clus", "clus_cnt=%d, dstkey=%d, dstidx=%d, parent=%p\n",
155: 	// 			clus_cnt, dst_key, dst_idx, parent);
156: 
157: 	struct clus_table *table;
158: 	struct rb_node **p;
159: 	uint base_key;
160: 	uint32 clus;
161: 	int idx;
162: 
163: 	// First, locate the last clus-table to get last base and index
164: 	if (parent == NULL) { // no nodes in rb_tree
165: 		table = kmalloc(sizeof(struct clus_table));
166: 		if (table == NULL)
167: 			goto fail;
168: 
169: 		memset(table->clus, 0, sizeof(table->clus));
170: 		table->base = 0;
171: 		table->num = 1;
172: 		table->clus[0] = entry->first_clus;
173: 		__debug_info("expand_rb_clus", "first: clus=%d\n", entry->first_clus);
174: 		parent = &table->rb;
175: 		rb_link_node(parent, NULL, &entry->rb_clus.rb_node);
176: 		rb_insert_color(parent, &entry->rb_clus);
177: 	} else {
178: 		table = rb_clus_table(parent);
179: 	}
180: 
181: 	if (table->num < CLUS_TABLE_SIZE) {	// step 1: fill the last table
182: 		idx = alloc ? 
183: 				(table->base == dst_key ? dst_idx : CLUS_TABLE_SIZE - 1) : -1;
184: 		
185: 		idx = fill_clus_table(ip->sb, table, 0, idx, alloc > 1);
186: 		if (idx < 0)						// case: allocation fail
187: 			goto fail;
188: 		else if (idx < CLUS_TABLE_SIZE)		// case: meet end of fat chain
189: 			return table;
190: 	}
191: 
192: 	base_key = table->base + 1;
193: 	clus = table->clus[CLUS_TABLE_SIZE - 1];
194: 	p = &parent->rb_right;
195: 
196: 	while (base_key <= dst_key) {	// allocate new table
197: 		table = kmalloc(sizeof(struct clus_table));
198: 		if (table == NULL)
199: 			goto fail;
200: 
201: 		table->base = base_key;
202: 		memset(table->clus, 0, sizeof(table->clus));
203: 		table->num = 0;
204: 
205: 		// __debug_info("expand_rb_clus", "newtable: base=%d, pre-clus=0x%x\n", base_key, clus);
206: 		idx = alloc ? 
207: 				(table->base == dst_key ? dst_idx : CLUS_TABLE_SIZE - 1) : -1;
208: 		idx = fill_clus_table(ip->sb, table, clus, idx, alloc > 1);
209: 		if (idx < 0) {						// case: allocation fail
210: 			if (table->num == 0)
211: 				kfree(table);
212: 			goto fail;
213: 		} else if (idx == 0) {				// case: meet end of fat chain
214: 			kfree(table);					// don't keep empty table
215: 			return rb_clus_table(parent);
216: 		}
217: 
218: 		rb_link_node(&table->rb, parent, p);
219: 		rb_insert_color(&table->rb, &entry->rb_clus);
220: 
221: 		if (table->num < CLUS_TABLE_SIZE)	// not full, meet end of fat chain
222: 			break;
223: 
224: 		base_key++;
225: 		clus = table->clus[CLUS_TABLE_SIZE - 1];
226: 		parent = &table->rb;		// table is now the right-most node
227: 		p = &table->rb.rb_right;	// the next node is the next right-most one
228: 	}
229: 
230: 	return table;
231: 
232: fail: // If fail, no need to free tables that have been allocated (for future use).
233: 	__debug_warn("expand_rb_clus", "fail\n");
234: 	return NULL;
235: }
236: 
237:

240:  * @param   ip          to get its fat32_entry and modify the cur_clus field

oskernel2023-zmz@recover 源码

235:  * @param   ip          to get its fat32_entry and modify the cur_clus field
236:  * @param   off         the offset from the beginning of the relative file
237:  * @param   alloc       whether alloc new cluster when meeting end of FAT chains
238:  * @return              the next cluster number
239:  */
240: uint32 reloc_clus(struct inode *ip, uint off, int alloc)
241: {
242: 	struct fat32_entry *entry = i2fat(ip);
243: 	struct fat32_sb *fat = sb2fat(ip->sb);
244: 	uint const clus_cnt = off / fat->byts_per_clus;
245: 	uint const rb_key = clus_cnt / CLUS_TABLE_SIZE;
246: 
247: 	struct rb_node *node = entry->rb_clus.rb_node;
248: 	struct clus_table *table = entry->cur_clus;
249: 
250: 	// __debug_info("reloc_clus", "begin: node=%p\n", node);
251: 	if (!table || table->base != rb_key) {
252: 		while (node) {
253: 			// __debug_info("reloc_clus", "search: node=%p\n", node);
254: 			table = rb_clus_table(node);
255: 			if (rb_key < table->base)
256: 				node = node->rb_left;
257: 			else if (rb_key > table->base)
258: 				node = node->rb_right;
259: 			else
260: 				break;
261: 		}
262: 	}
263: 
264: 	int const idx = clus_cnt % CLUS_TABLE_SIZE;
265: 	if (!node) {
266: 		table = expand_rb_clus(ip, off, alloc);
267: 		// __debug_info("reloc_clus", "get expand, base=%d, off=%p\n", table ? table->base : -1, off);
268: 		if (table == NULL || table->base != rb_key)
269: 			goto fail;
270: 	} else if (!table->clus[idx]) {
271: 		// __debug_info("reloc_clus", "get table, base=%d, off=%p\n", table->base, off);
272: 		if (!alloc)
273: 			goto fail;
274: 		if (fill_clus_table(ip->sb, table, 0, idx) < 0)
275: 			goto fail;
276: 	}
277: 
278: 	// __debug_info("reloc_clus", "end: clus=%d, off=%d\n", entry->cur_clus, off);
279: 	entry->cur_clus = table;
280: 	return table->clus[idx];
281: 
282: fail:
283: 	return 0;
284: }

288: 	struct rb_node *node = entry->rb_clus.rb_node;
289: 	struct rb_node *parent = NULL;

285: 
286: void free_clus_cache(struct fat32_entry *entry)
287: {
288: 	struct rb_node *node = entry->rb_clus.rb_node;
289: 	struct rb_node *parent = NULL;
290: 	struct clus_table *table;
291: 
292: 	while (node != NULL) {
293: 		if (node->rb_left)
294: 			node = node->rb_left;
295: 		else if (node->rb_right)
296: 			node = node->rb_right;
297: 		else {
298: 			parent = rb_parent(node);
299: 			if (parent) {
300: 				if (node == parent->rb_left)
301: 					parent->rb_left = NULL;
302: 				else
303: 					parent->rb_right = NULL;
304: 			}
305: 
306: 			table = rb_clus_table(node);
307: 			__debug_info("free_clus_cache", "base=%d, num=%d\n", table->base, table->num);
308: 			kfree(table);
309: 			node = parent;
310: 		}
311: 	}
312: 
313: 	entry->rb_clus.rb_node = NULL;
314: }

314: 			node = parent;
315: 		}
316: 	}
317: 
318: 	entry->rb_clus.rb_node = NULL;

49: static void generate_shortname(char *shortname, char *name)
50: {
51: 	static char illegal[] = { '+', ',', ';', '=', '[', ']', 0 };   // these are legal in l-n-e but not s-n-e
52: 	int i = 0;
53: 	char c, *p = name;
54: 	for (int j = strlen(name) - 1; j >= 0; j--) {
55: 		if (name[j] == '.') {
56: 			p = name + j;
57: 			break;
58: 		}
59: 	}
60: 	while (i < CHAR_SHORT_NAME && (c = *name++)) {
61: 		if (i == 8 && p) {
62: 			if (p + 1 < name) { break; }            // no '.'
63: 			else {
64: 				name = p + 1, p = 0;
65: 				continue;
66: 			}
67: 		}
68: 		if (c == ' ') { continue; }
69: 		if (c == '.') {
70: 			if (name > p) {                    // last '.'
71: 				memset(shortname + i, ' ', 8 - i);
72: 				i = 8, p = 0;
73: 			}
74: 			continue;
75: 		}
76: 		if (c >= 'a' && c <= 'z') {
77: 			c += 'A' - 'a';
78: 		} else {
79: 			if (strchr(illegal, c) != NULL) {
80: 				c = '_';
81: 			}
82: 		}
83: 		shortname[i++] = c;
84: 	}
85: 	while (i < CHAR_SHORT_NAME) {
86: 		shortname[i++] = ' ';
87: 	}
88: }
89: 
90: static uint8 cal_checksum(uchar* shortname)
91: {
92: 	uint8 sum = 0;
93: 	for (int i = CHAR_SHORT_NAME; i != 0; i--) {

49: static void generate_shortname(char *shortname, char *name)
50: {
51: 	static char illegal[] = { '+', ',', ';', '=', '[', ']', 0 };   // these are legal in l-n-e but not s-n-e
52: 	int i = 0;
53: 	char c, *p = name;

56: 			p = name + j;
57: 			break;
58: 		}
59: 	}

62: 			if (p + 1 < name) { break; }            // no '.'
63: 			else {
64: 				name = p + 1, p = 0;

66: 			}

90: static uint8 cal_checksum(uchar* shortname)
91: {
92: 	uint8 sum = 0;
93: 	for (int i = CHAR_SHORT_NAME; i != 0; i--) {
94: 		sum = ((sum & 1) ? 0x80 : 0) + (sum >> 1) + *shortname++;
95: 	}
96: 	return sum;
97: }
