匹配分数 0.531 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": false, "exact_token_fp": false, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 0.102}

oskernel2023-zmz@recover 源码

101:  * Caller must hold dir->lock unless in fat_alloc_entry.
102:  * @param   dir         the directory inode
103:  * @param   ep          entry to write on disk
104:  * @param   off         offset int the dp, should be calculated via dirlookup before calling this
105:  */
106: int fat_make_entry(struct inode *dir, struct fat32_entry *ep, char *filename, uint off, int islong)
107: {
108: 	struct fat32_entry *dp = i2fat(dir);
109: 	if (!(dp->attribute & ATTR_DIRECTORY))
110: 		panic("fat_make_entry: not dir");
111: 	if (off % sizeof(union fat_disk_entry))
112: 		panic("fat_make_entry: not aligned");
113: 
114: 	uint32 const bpc = sb2fat(dir->sb)->byts_per_clus;
115: 	uint32 clus;
116: 	union fat_disk_entry de;
117: 	memset(&de, 0, sizeof(de));
118: 	if (!islong) {
119: 		strncpy(de.sne.name, filename, sizeof(de.sne.name));
120: 	}
121:     else {
122: 		char shortname[CHAR_SHORT_NAME + 1];
123: 		memset(shortname, 0, sizeof(shortname));
124: 		generate_shortname(shortname, filename);
125: 		de.lne.checksum = cal_checksum((uchar *)shortname);
126: 		de.lne.attr = ATTR_LONG_NAME;
127: 
128: 		for (int i = ep->ent_cnt - 1; i > 0; i--) {
129: 			if ((de.lne.order = i) == ep->ent_cnt - 1) {
130: 				de.lne.order |= LAST_LONG_ENTRY;
131: 			}
132: 			char *p = filename + (i - 1) * CHAR_LONG_NAME;
133: 			uint8 *w = (uint8 *)de.lne.name1;
134: 			int end = 0;
135: 			for (int j = 1; j <= CHAR_LONG_NAME; j++) {
136: 				if (end) {
137: 					*w++ = 0xff;            // on k210, unaligned reading is illegal
138: 					*w++ = 0xff;
139: 				} else { 
140: 					if ((*w++ = *p++) == 0) {
141: 						end = 1;
142: 					}
143: 					*w++ = 0;
144: 				}
145: 				switch (j) {
146: 					case 5:     w = (uint8 *)de.lne.name2; break;
147: 					case 11:    w = (uint8 *)de.lne.name3; break;
148: 				}
149: 			}
150: 			clus = reloc_clus(dir, off, 1);
151: 			// __debug_info("fat_make_entry", "name:%s clus:%d off:%d\n", ep->filename, dp->cur_clus, off2);
152: 			if (!clus || fat_rw_clus(dir->sb, clus, 1, 0, (uint64)&de, off % bpc, sizeof(de)) != sizeof(de))
153: 				return -1;
154: 			off += sizeof(de);
155: 		}
156: 		memset(&de, 0, sizeof(de));
157: 		strncpy(de.sne.name, shortname, sizeof(de.sne.name));
158: 	}
159: 	de.sne.attr = ep->attribute;
160: 	de.sne.fst_clus_hi = (uint16)(ep->first_clus >> 16);      // first clus high 16 bits
161: 	de.sne.fst_clus_lo = (uint16)(ep->first_clus & 0xffff);     // low 16 bits
162: 	de.sne.file_size = ep->file_size;                         // filesize is updated in eupdate()
163: 	clus = reloc_clus(dir, off, 1);
164: 	// __debug_info("fat_make_entry", "name:%s clus:%d off:%d\n", ep->filename, dp->cur_clus, off2);
165: 	if (!clus || fat_rw_clus(dir->sb, clus, 1, 0, (uint64)&de, off % bpc, sizeof(de)) != sizeof(de))
166: 		return -1;
167: 	return 0;
168: }
169: 
170: // trim ' ' in the head and tail, '.' in head, and test legality
171: static char *formatname(char *name)
172: {
173: 	static char illegal[] = { '\"', '*', '/', ':', '<', '>', '?', '\\', '|', 0 };

xv6-k210@final / 来源候选源码

101:  * Caller must hold dir->lock unless in fat_alloc_entry.
102:  * @param   dir         the directory inode
103:  * @param   ep          entry to write on disk
104:  * @param   off         offset int the dp, should be calculated via dirlookup before calling this
105:  */
106: int fat_make_entry(struct inode *dir, struct fat32_entry *ep, char *filename, uint off, int islong)
107: {
108: 	struct fat32_entry *dp = i2fat(dir);
109: 	// if (!(dp->attribute & ATTR_DIRECTORY))
110: 	// 	panic("fat_make_entry: not dir");
111: 	// if (off % sizeof(union fat_disk_entry))
112: 	// 	panic("fat_make_entry: not aligned");
113: 
114: 	uint32 const bpc = sb2fat(dir->sb)->byts_per_clus;
115: 	uint32 clus;
116: 	union fat_disk_entry de;
117: 	memset(&de, 0, sizeof(de));
118: 	if (!islong) {
119: 		strncpy(de.sne.name, filename, sizeof(de.sne.name));
120: 	}
121:     else {
122: 		char shortname[CHAR_SHORT_NAME + 1];
123: 		memset(shortname, 0, sizeof(shortname));
124: 		generate_shortname(shortname, filename);
125: 		de.lne.checksum = cal_checksum((uchar *)shortname);
126: 		de.lne.attr = ATTR_LONG_NAME;
127: 
128: 		for (int i = ep->ent_cnt - 1; i > 0; i--) {
129: 			if ((de.lne.order = i) == ep->ent_cnt - 1) {
130: 				de.lne.order |= LAST_LONG_ENTRY;
131: 			}
132: 			char *p = filename + (i - 1) * CHAR_LONG_NAME;
133: 			uint8 *w = (uint8 *)de.lne.name1;
134: 			int end = 0;
135: 			for (int j = 1; j <= CHAR_LONG_NAME; j++) {
136: 				if (end) {
137: 					*w++ = 0xff;            // on k210, unaligned reading is illegal
138: 					*w++ = 0xff;
139: 				} else { 
140: 					if ((*w++ = *p++) == 0) {
141: 						end = 1;
142: 					}
143: 					*w++ = 0;
144: 				}
145: 				switch (j) {
146: 					case 5:     w = (uint8 *)de.lne.name2; break;
147: 					case 11:    w = (uint8 *)de.lne.name3; break;
148: 				}
149: 			}
150: 			clus = reloc_clus(dir, off, 2);
151: 			// __debug_info("fat_make_entry", "name:%s clus:%d off:%d\n", ep->filename, dp->cur_clus, off2);
152: 			if (!clus || fat_rw_clus(dir->sb, clus, 1, 0, (uint64)&de, off % bpc, sizeof(de)) != sizeof(de))
153: 				return -1;
154: 			off += sizeof(de);
155: 		}
156: 		memset(&de, 0, sizeof(de));
157: 		strncpy(de.sne.name, shortname, sizeof(de.sne.name));
158: 	}
159: 	de.sne.attr = ep->attribute;
160: 	de.sne.fst_clus_hi = (uint16)(ep->first_clus >> 16);      // first clus high 16 bits
161: 	de.sne.fst_clus_lo = (uint16)(ep->first_clus & 0xffff);     // low 16 bits
162: 	de.sne.file_size = ep->file_size;                         // filesize is updated in eupdate()
163: 	clus = reloc_clus(dir, off, 2);
164: 	// __debug_info("fat_make_entry", "name:%s clus:%d off:%d\n", ep->filename, dp->cur_clus, off2);
165: 	if (!clus || fat_rw_clus(dir->sb, clus, 1, 0, (uint64)&de, off % bpc, sizeof(de)) != sizeof(de))
166: 		return -1;
167: 	return 0;
168: }
169:

172: {
173: 	static char illegal[] = { '\"', '*', '/', ':', '<', '>', '?', '\\', '|', 0 };

匹配分数 1.000 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": true, "exact_token_fp": true, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 1.0}

169: 
170: // trim ' ' in the head and tail, '.' in head, and test legality
171: static char *formatname(char *name)
172: {
173: 	static char illegal[] = { '\"', '*', '/', ':', '<', '>', '?', '\\', '|', 0 };
174: 	char *p;
175: 	while (*name == ' ' || *name == '.') { name++; }
176: 	if (*name == '\0')
177: 		return NULL;
178: 	for (p = name; *p; p++) {
179: 		char c = *p;
180: 		if (c < 0x20 || strchr(illegal, c)) {
181: 			return 0;
182: 		}
183: 	}
184: 	while (p-- > name) {
185: 		if (*p != ' ') {
186: 			p[1] = '\0';
187: 			break;
188: 		}
189: 	}
190: 	if (p - name + 1 > FAT32_MAX_FILENAME)
191: 		return NULL;
192: 	return name;
193: }
194:

197:  * Caller must hold dir->lock.
198:  * @param   dir         the directory inode

167: 	return 0;
168: }

170: // trim ' ' in the head and tail, '.' in head, and test legality
171: static char *formatname(char *name)
172: {
173: 	static char illegal[] = { '\"', '*', '/', ':', '<', '>', '?', '\\', '|', 0 };
174: 	char *p;
175: 	while (*name == ' ' || *name == '.') { name++; }
176: 	if (*name == '\0')
177: 		return NULL;
178: 	for (p = name; *p; p++) {
179: 		char c = *p;
180: 		if (c < 0x20 || strchr(illegal, c)) {
181: 			return 0;
182: 		}
183: 	}
184: 	while (p-- > name) {
185: 		if (*p != ' ') {
186: 			p[1] = '\0';
187: 			break;
188: 		}
189: 	}
190: 	if (p - name + 1 > FAT32_MAX_FILENAME)
191: 		return NULL;
192: 	return name;
193: }

195: /**
196:  * Allocate an entry on disk. If the file already exists, just return it.
197:  * Caller must hold dir->lock.

fat_alloc_entry修改候选
oskernel2023-zmz@recover 第 203 行 · 主要确定性来源：xv6-k210/kernel/fs/fat32/dirent.c::fat_alloc_entry

候选来源与匹配信号

xv6-k210/kernel/fs/fat32/dirent.c::fat_alloc_entry
匹配分数 0.568 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": false, "exact_token_fp": false, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 0.227}

oskernel2023-zmz@recover 源码

198:  * @param   dir         the directory inode
199:  * @param   name        the file to allocate on disk
200:  * @param   mode        dir or file (read/write control is not consider yet)
201:  * @return              the inode structure of the new file
202:  */
203: struct inode *fat_alloc_entry(struct inode *dir, char *name, int mode)
204: {
205: 	__debug_info("fat_alloc_entry", "allocating %s\n", name);
206: 	if (dir->state & I_STATE_FREE || !(name = formatname(name))) {        // detect illegal character
207: 		return NULL;
208: 	}
209: 	struct fat32_entry *dp = i2fat(dir);
210: 	if (!(dp->attribute & ATTR_DIRECTORY))
211: 		panic("ealloc not dir");
212: 
213: 	struct superblock *sb = dir->sb;
214: 	struct inode *ip;
215: 	uint off = 0;
216: 	/* If the file exists on disk. */
217: 	if ((ip = fat_lookup_dir(dir, name, &off)) != NULL) {      // entry exists
218: 		return ip;
219: 	}
220: 	if ((ip = fat_alloc_inode(sb)) == NULL) {
221: 		__debug_warn("fat_alloc_entry", "2\n");
222: 		return NULL;
223: 	}
224: 	ip->op = dir->op;
225: 	ip->fop = dir->fop;
226: 	ip->mode = mode | 0x1ff;
227: 
228: 	struct fat32_entry *ep = i2fat(ip);
229: 	ep->attribute = S_ISDIR(mode) ? ATTR_DIRECTORY : 0;
230: 	ep->ent_cnt = (strlen(name) + CHAR_LONG_NAME - 1) / CHAR_LONG_NAME + 1;
231: 
232: 	if (S_ISDIR(mode)) {    // generate "." and ".." for ep
233: 		if ((ep->first_clus = alloc_clus(sb)) == 0 ||
234: 			fat_make_entry(ip, ep, ".          ", 0, 0) < 0 ||
235: 			fat_make_entry(ip, dp, "..         ", 32, 0) < 0)
236: 		{
237: 			if (ep->first_clus > 0)
238: 				free_clus(sb, ep->first_clus);
239: 			goto fail;
240: 		}
241: 	}
242: 	else {  // what is this for?
243: 		ep->attribute |= ATTR_ARCHIVE;
244: 	}
245: 
246: 	if (fat_make_entry(dir, ep, name, off, 1) < 0)
247: 		goto fail;
248: 
249: 	ip->inum = ((uint64)reloc_clus(dir, off, 0) << 32) | (off % sb2fat(sb)->byts_per_clus);
250: 	return ip;
251: 
252: fail:
253: 	__debug_warn("fat_alloc_entry", "fail\n");
254: 	__alert_fs_err("fat_alloc_entry");
255: 	fat_destroy_inode(ip);
256: 	return NULL;
257: }
258:

260:  * Remove the entry in its parent directory.
261:  * The content of the file is removed by 'truncate'.
262:  */

233: 		if ((ep->first_clus = alloc_clus(sb)) == 0 ||

候选来源与匹配信号

xv6-k210/kernel/fs/fat32/dirent.c::fat_remove_entry
匹配分数 1.000 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": true, "exact_token_fp": true, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 1.0}

oskernel2023-zmz@recover 源码

258: 
259: /**
260:  * Remove the entry in its parent directory.
261:  * The content of the file is removed by 'truncate'.
262:  */
263: int fat_remove_entry(struct inode *ip)
264: {
265: 	if (ip->state & I_STATE_FREE)
266: 		return -ENOENT;
267: 	if (ip->nlink == 0)
268: 		return -ENOENT;
269: 	ip->nlink--;
270: 
271: 	struct fat32_sb *fat = sb2fat(ip->sb);
272: 	struct fat32_entry *entry = i2fat(ip);
273: 	uint32 clus = ip->inum >> 32;
274: 	uint32 off = ip->inum & 0xffffffff;
275: 
276: 	__debug_info("fat_remove_entry", "clus:%d off:%d entcnt:%d\n", clus, off, entry->ent_cnt);
277: 	uint8 flag = EMPTY_ENTRY;
278: 	for (int i = 0; i < entry->ent_cnt; i++) {
279: 		__debug_info("fat_remove_entry", "name:%s clus:%d off:%d i:%d\n", ip->entry->filename, clus, off, i);
280: 		if (fat_rw_clus(ip->sb, clus, 1, 0, (uint64)&flag, off, 1) != 1)
281: 			goto fail;
282: 		if ((off += 32) >= fat->byts_per_clus && i + 1 < entry->ent_cnt) {
283: 			if ((clus = read_fat(ip->sb, clus)) == 0 || clus >= FAT32_EOC) {
284: 				__debug_info("fat_remove_entry", "read_fat fail! clus=%d\n", clus);
285: 				goto fail;
286: 			}
287: 			off %= fat->byts_per_clus;
288: 			__debug_info("fat_remove_entry", "entries over clus! new_clus:%d new_off:%d\n", clus, off);
289: 		}
290: 	}
291: 
292: 	return 0;
293: 
294: fail:
295: 	__alert_fs_err("fat_remove_entry");
296: 	return -EIO;
297: }
298: 
299: /**
300:  * Update the inode info to disk. On FAT32, it's actually
301:  * update the directory entry. We only update filesize
302:  * and first cluster in our case.

xv6-k210@final / 来源候选源码

258: 
259: /**
260:  * Remove the entry in its parent directory.
261:  * The content of the file is removed by 'truncate'.
262:  */
263: int fat_remove_entry(struct inode *ip)
264: {
265: 	if (ip->state & I_STATE_FREE)
266: 		return -ENOENT;
267: 	if (ip->nlink == 0)
268: 		return -ENOENT;
269: 	ip->nlink--;
270: 
271: 	struct fat32_sb *fat = sb2fat(ip->sb);
272: 	struct fat32_entry *entry = i2fat(ip);
273: 	uint32 clus = ip->inum >> 32;
274: 	uint32 off = ip->inum & 0xffffffff;
275: 
276: 	__debug_info("fat_remove_entry", "clus:%d off:%d entcnt:%d\n", clus, off, entry->ent_cnt);
277: 	uint8 flag = EMPTY_ENTRY;
278: 	for (int i = 0; i < entry->ent_cnt; i++) {
279: 		__debug_info("fat_remove_entry", "name:%s clus:%d off:%d i:%d\n", ip->entry->filename, clus, off, i);
280: 		if (fat_rw_clus(ip->sb, clus, 1, 0, (uint64)&flag, off, 1) != 1)
281: 			goto fail;
282: 		if ((off += 32) >= fat->byts_per_clus && i + 1 < entry->ent_cnt) {
283: 			if ((clus = read_fat(ip->sb, clus)) == 0 || clus >= FAT32_EOC) {
284: 				__debug_info("fat_remove_entry", "read_fat fail! clus=%d\n", clus);
285: 				goto fail;
286: 			}
287: 			off %= fat->byts_per_clus;
288: 			__debug_info("fat_remove_entry", "entries over clus! new_clus:%d new_off:%d\n", clus, off);
289: 		}
290: 	}
291: 
292: 	return 0;
293: 
294: fail:
295: 	__alert_fs_err("fat_remove_entry");
296: 	return -EIO;
297: }

299: /**
300:  * Update the inode info to disk. On FAT32, it's actually
301:  * update the directory entry. We only update filesize
302:  * and first cluster in our case.
fat_update_entry完全一致
oskernel2023-zmz@recover 第 304 行 · 主要确定性来源：xv6-k210/kernel/fs/fat32/dirent.c::fat_update_entry

候选来源与匹配信号

xv6-k210/kernel/fs/fat32/dirent.c::fat_update_entry
匹配分数 1.000 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": true, "exact_token_fp": true, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 1.0}

oskernel2023-zmz@recover 源码

299: /**
300:  * Update the inode info to disk. On FAT32, it's actually
301:  * update the directory entry. We only update filesize
302:  * and first cluster in our case.
303:  */
304: int fat_update_entry(struct inode *ip)
305: {
306: 	if (!(ip->state & I_STATE_DIRTY))
307: 		return 0;
308: 	if (ip->state & I_STATE_FREE)
309: 		return -1;
310: 	
311: 	struct fat32_sb *fat = sb2fat(ip->sb);
312: 	struct fat32_entry *entry = i2fat(ip);
313: 	uint32 clus = ip->inum >> 32;
314: 	uint32 off = (ip->inum & 0xffffffff) + ((entry->ent_cnt - 1) << 5);
315: 	
316: 	union fat_disk_entry de;
317: 
318: 	// The short entry steps over the cluster.
319: 	if (off >= fat->byts_per_clus) {
320: 		clus = read_fat(ip->sb, clus);
321: 		if (clus == 0) { goto fail; }
322: 		off %= fat->byts_per_clus;
323: 	}
324: 
325: 	// Read from disk.
326: 	if (fat_rw_clus(ip->sb, clus, 0, 0, (uint64)&de, off, sizeof(de)) != sizeof(de))
327: 		goto fail;
328: 	de.sne.fst_clus_hi = (uint16)(entry->first_clus >> 16);
329: 	de.sne.fst_clus_lo = (uint16)(entry->first_clus & 0xffff);
330: 	de.sne.file_size = entry->file_size;
331: 	// Wirte back.
332: 	if (fat_rw_clus(ip->sb, clus, 1, 0, (uint64)&de, off, sizeof(de)) != sizeof(de))
333: 		goto fail;
334: 	ip->state &= ~I_STATE_DIRTY;
335: 	
336: 	return 0;
337: 
338: fail:
339: 	__alert_fs_err("fat_update_entry");
340: 	return -1;
341: }
342: 
343: /**
344:  * Read filename from directory entry.
345:  * @param   buffer      pointer to the array that stores the name
346:  * @param   d           pointer to the raw format entry buffer

xv6-k210@final / 来源候选源码

299: /**
300:  * Update the inode info to disk. On FAT32, it's actually
301:  * update the directory entry. We only update filesize
302:  * and first cluster in our case.
303:  */
304: int fat_update_entry(struct inode *ip)
305: {
306: 	if (!(ip->state & I_STATE_DIRTY))
307: 		return 0;
308: 	if (ip->state & I_STATE_FREE)
309: 		return -1;
310: 	
311: 	struct fat32_sb *fat = sb2fat(ip->sb);
312: 	struct fat32_entry *entry = i2fat(ip);
313: 	uint32 clus = ip->inum >> 32;
314: 	uint32 off = (ip->inum & 0xffffffff) + ((entry->ent_cnt - 1) << 5);
315: 	
316: 	union fat_disk_entry de;
317: 
318: 	// The short entry steps over the cluster.
319: 	if (off >= fat->byts_per_clus) {
320: 		clus = read_fat(ip->sb, clus);
321: 		if (clus == 0) { goto fail; }
322: 		off %= fat->byts_per_clus;
323: 	}
324: 
325: 	// Read from disk.
326: 	if (fat_rw_clus(ip->sb, clus, 0, 0, (uint64)&de, off, sizeof(de)) != sizeof(de))
327: 		goto fail;
328: 	de.sne.fst_clus_hi = (uint16)(entry->first_clus >> 16);
329: 	de.sne.fst_clus_lo = (uint16)(entry->first_clus & 0xffff);
330: 	de.sne.file_size = entry->file_size;
331: 	// Wirte back.
332: 	if (fat_rw_clus(ip->sb, clus, 1, 0, (uint64)&de, off, sizeof(de)) != sizeof(de))
333: 		goto fail;
334: 	ip->state &= ~I_STATE_DIRTY;
335: 	
336: 	return 0;
337: 
338: fail:
339: 	__alert_fs_err("fat_update_entry");
340: 	return -1;
341: }
342: 
343: /**
344:  * Read filename from directory entry.
345:  * @param   buffer      pointer to the array that stores the name
346:  * @param   d           pointer to the raw format entry buffer
read_entry_name完全一致

匹配分数 1.000 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": true, "exact_token_fp": true, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 1.0}

oskernel2023-zmz@recover 源码

343: /**
344:  * Read filename from directory entry.
345:  * @param   buffer      pointer to the array that stores the name
346:  * @param   d           pointer to the raw format entry buffer
347:  */
348: static void read_entry_name(char *buffer, union fat_disk_entry *d)
349: {
350: 	if (d->lne.attr == ATTR_LONG_NAME) {                       // long entry branch
351: 		wchar temp[NELEM(d->lne.name1)];
352: 		memmove(temp, d->lne.name1, sizeof(temp));
353: 		snstr(buffer, temp, NELEM(d->lne.name1));
354: 		buffer += NELEM(d->lne.name1);
355: 		snstr(buffer, d->lne.name2, NELEM(d->lne.name2));
356: 		buffer += NELEM(d->lne.name2);
357: 		snstr(buffer, d->lne.name3, NELEM(d->lne.name3));
358: 	} else {
359: 		// assert: only "." and ".." will enter this branch
360: 		memset(buffer, 0, CHAR_SHORT_NAME + 2); // plus '.' and '\0'
361: 		int i;
362: 		for (i = 0; d->sne.name[i] != ' ' && i < 8; i++) {
363: 			buffer[i] = d->sne.name[i];
364: 		}
365: 		if (d->sne.name[8] != ' ') {
366: 			buffer[i++] = '.';
367: 		}
368: 		for (int j = 8; j < CHAR_SHORT_NAME; j++, i++) {
369: 			if (d->sne.name[j] == ' ') { break; }
370: 			buffer[i] = d->sne.name[j];
371: 		}
372: 	}
373: }
374: 
375: /**
376:  * Read entry_info from directory entry.
377:  * @param   entry       pointer to the structure that stores the entry info
378:  * @param   d           pointer to the raw format entry buffer

375: /**
376:  * Read entry_info from directory entry.
377:  * @param   entry       pointer to the structure that stores the entry info
378:  * @param   d           pointer to the raw format entry buffer
379:  */
380: static void read_entry_info(struct fat32_entry *entry, union fat_disk_entry *d)
381: {
382: 	entry->attribute = d->sne.attr;
383: 	entry->first_clus = ((uint32)d->sne.fst_clus_hi << 16) | d->sne.fst_clus_lo;
384: 
385: 	entry->create_time_tenth = d->sne.crt_time_tenth;
386: 	entry->create_time = d->sne.crt_time;
387: 	entry->create_date = d->sne.crt_date;
388: 	entry->last_access_date = d->sne.lst_acce_date;
389: 	entry->last_write_time = d->sne.lst_wrt_time;
390: 	entry->last_write_date = d->sne.lst_wrt_date;
391: 	
392: 	entry->file_size = d->sne.file_size;
393: }
394:

fat_read_entry修改候选

xv6-k210/kernel/fs/fat32/dirent.c::fat_read_entry
匹配分数 0.556 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": false, "exact_token_fp": false, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 0.188}

oskernel2023-zmz@recover 源码

401:  * @param   count   to write the count of entries
402:  * @return  -1      meet the end of dir
403:  *          0       find empty slots
404:  *          1       find a file with all its entries
405:  */
406: int fat_read_entry(struct inode *dir, struct fat32_entry *ep, char *namebuf, uint off, int *count)
407: {
408: 	if (dir->state & I_STATE_FREE)
409: 		return -1;
410: 
411: 	struct fat32_entry *dp = i2fat(dir);
412: 	if (!(dp->attribute & ATTR_DIRECTORY))
413: 		panic("enext not dir");
414: 	if (off % 32)
415: 		panic("enext not align");
416: 
417: 	union fat_disk_entry de;
418: 	uint32 const bpc = sb2fat(dir->sb)->byts_per_clus;
419: 	int empty = 0, cnt = 0;
420: 	memset(namebuf, 0, FAT32_MAX_FILENAME + 1);
421: 	for (uint32 clus; (clus = reloc_clus(dir, off, 0)) != 0; off += sizeof(de))
422: 	{
423: 		if (fat_rw_clus(dir->sb, clus, 0, 0, (uint64)&de, off % bpc, sizeof(de)) != sizeof(de)
424: 			|| de.lne.order == END_OF_DIR) {
425: 			return -1;
426: 		}
427: 		if (de.lne.order == EMPTY_ENTRY) {
428: 			if (cnt) {      // We have met a l-n-e, but why we come across an empty one?
429: 				goto excep; // Maybe the entry is being removed by its so-call inode.
430: 			}
431: 			empty++;
432: 			continue;
433: 		} else if (empty) {
434: 			*count = empty;
435: 			return 0;
436: 		}
437: 		if (de.lne.attr == ATTR_LONG_NAME) {
438: 			if (de.lne.order & LAST_LONG_ENTRY) {
439: 				cnt = (de.lne.order & ~LAST_LONG_ENTRY) + 1;   // plus the s-n-e;
440: 				*count = cnt;
441: 			} else if (cnt == 0) {    // Should not occur unless some inode is removing this entry
442: 				goto excep;
443: 			}
444: 			read_entry_name(namebuf + (--cnt - 1) * CHAR_LONG_NAME, &de);
445: 		} else {
446: 			if (cnt == 0) {     // Without l-n-e
447: 				*count = 1;
448: 				read_entry_name(namebuf, &de);
449: 			}
450: 			read_entry_info(ep, &de);
451: 			__debug_info("fat_dir_next", "get %s\n", namebuf);
452: 			return 1;
453: 		}
454: 	}
455: 	return -1;
456: 
457: excep:
458: 	*count = 0;     // Caller will re-read
459: 	__debug_warn("fat_dir_next", "??? from %d\n", off);
460: 	return 0;
461: }
462: 
463: int fat_rename_entry(struct inode *ip, struct inode *dp, char *name)
464: {
465: 	if ((name = formatname(name)) == NULL)
466: 		return -EINVAL;

匹配分数 1.000 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": true, "exact_token_fp": true, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 1.0}

462: 
463: int fat_rename_entry(struct inode *ip, struct inode *dp, char *name)
464: {
465: 	if ((name = formatname(name)) == NULL)
466: 		return -EINVAL;
467: 
468: 	uint off;
469: 	struct fat32_entry *dst;
470: 	dst = fat_lookup_dir_ent(dp, name, &off);
471: 	if (dst != NULL) { // file exists
472: 		kfree(dst);
473: 		panic("fat_rename_entry"); // vfs should have checked this, what happened?
474: 		return -EEXIST;
475: 	}
476: 	if (fat_make_entry(dp, i2fat(ip), name, off, 1) < 0) {
477: 		return -ENOSPC;
478: 	}
479: 	int ret = fat_remove_entry(ip);
480: 	if (ret < 0) {
481: 		return ret;
482: 	}
483: 
484: 	safestrcpy(ip->entry->filename, name, FAT32_MAX_FILENAME + 1); // this is with no lock, dangerous
485: 	ip->nlink++; // fat_remove_entry decreased this, add it back
486: 	uint64 clus = reloc_clus(dp, off, 0);
487: 	ip->inum = (clus << 32) | (off % sb2fat(dp->sb)->byts_per_clus);
488: 
489: 	return 0;
490: }

xv6-k210@final / 来源候选源码

458: 	*count = 0;     // Caller will re-read
459: 	__debug_warn("fat_dir_next", "??? from %d\n", off);
460: 	return 0;
461: }
462: 
463: int fat_rename_entry(struct inode *ip, struct inode *dp, char *name)
464: {
465: 	if ((name = formatname(name)) == NULL)
466: 		return -EINVAL;
467: 
468: 	uint off;
469: 	struct fat32_entry *dst;
470: 	dst = fat_lookup_dir_ent(dp, name, &off);
471: 	if (dst != NULL) { // file exists
472: 		kfree(dst);
473: 		panic("fat_rename_entry"); // vfs should have checked this, what happened?
474: 		return -EEXIST;
475: 	}
476: 	if (fat_make_entry(dp, i2fat(ip), name, off, 1) < 0) {
477: 		return -ENOSPC;
478: 	}
479: 	int ret = fat_remove_entry(ip);
480: 	if (ret < 0) {
481: 		return ret;
482: 	}
483: 
484: 	safestrcpy(ip->entry->filename, name, FAT32_MAX_FILENAME + 1); // this is with no lock, dangerous
485: 	ip->nlink++; // fat_remove_entry decreased this, add it back
486: 	uint64 clus = reloc_clus(dp, off, 0);
487: 	ip->inum = (clus << 32) | (off % sb2fat(dp->sb)->byts_per_clus);
488: 
489: 	return 0;
490: }

15:  * @param   fat         fat32 super block
16:  * @param   cluster     number of a data cluster
17:  * @param   fat_num     number of FAT table from 1, shouldn't be larger than bpb::fat_cnt
18:  *                      we only use fat region 1 in xv6-k210, lazy to check legality
19:  */
20: static inline uint32 fat_sec_of_clus(struct fat32_sb *fat, uint32 cluster, uint8 fat_num)
21: {
22: 	return fat->bpb.rsvd_sec_cnt + (cluster << 2) / fat->bpb.byts_per_sec + fat->bpb.fat_sz * (fat_num - 1);
23: }
24:

35: // Init a fat region cache when creating fat32 superblock.
36: int fat_cache_init(struct superblock *sb)
37: {
38: 	struct fat32_sb *fat = sb2fat(sb); 
39: 	if (fat->fatcache.page)
40: 		panic("fat_cache_init: superblock is not clean");
41: 
42: 	if ((fat->fatcache.page = allocpage()) == NULL)
43: 		return -ENOMEM;
44: 	
45: 	char *afsec = fat->fatcache.page;
46: 	uint32 alloc_fat_sec = (fat->next_free >= 2) ? 
47: 							fat_sec_of_clus(fat, fat->next_free, 1) :
48: 							fat->bpb.rsvd_sec_cnt;
49: 	
50: 	for (int i = 0; i < FAT_CACHE_NSEC; i++) {
51: 		fat->fatcache.fatsec[i] = 0;
52: 		fat->fatcache.lrucnt[i] = 0;
53: 		fat->fatcache.dirty[i] = 0;
54: 	}
55: 	fat->fatcache.allocidx = 0;
56: 	fat->fatcache.fatsec[0] = alloc_fat_sec;
57: 	fat->next_free_fat = fat->bpb.rsvd_sec_cnt;
58: 
59: 	acquiresleep(&sb->sb_lock);
60: 	if (sb->op.read(sb, 0, afsec, alloc_fat_sec, 0, SECSZ) != SECSZ) {
61: 		releasesleep(&sb->sb_lock);
62: 		freepage(fat->fatcache.page);
63: 		fat->fatcache.page = NULL;
64: 		return -EIO;
65: 	}
66: 
67: 	// Check whether the 'next free' field lie to us.
68: 	if (*(uint32 *)(afsec + fat_offset_of_clus(fat, fat->next_free)) != 0
69: 		&& fat_update_next_free(sb) == 0)
70: 		panic("fat_cache_init: no next free");
71: 
72: 	releasesleep(&sb->sb_lock);
73: 	return 0;
74: }

35: // Init a fat region cache when creating fat32 superblock.
36: int fat_cache_init(struct superblock *sb)
37: {
38: 	struct fat32_sb *fat = sb2fat(sb); 
39: 	// if (fat->fatcache.page)
40: 	// 	panic("fat_cache_init: superblock is not clean");
41: 
42: 	if ((fat->fatcache.page = allocpage()) == NULL)
43: 		return -ENOMEM;
44: 	
45: 	char *afsec = fat->fatcache.page;
46: 	uint32 alloc_fat_sec = (fat->next_free >= 2) ? 
47: 							fat_sec_of_clus(fat, fat->next_free, 1) :
48: 							fat->bpb.rsvd_sec_cnt;
49: 	
50: 	for (int i = 0; i < FAT_CACHE_NSEC; i++) {
51: 		fat->fatcache.fatsec[i] = 0;
52: 		fat->fatcache.lrucnt[i] = 0;
53: 		fat->fatcache.dirty[i] = 0;
54: 	}
55: 	fat->fatcache.allocidx = 0;
56: 	fat->fatcache.fatsec[0] = alloc_fat_sec;
57: 	fat->next_free_fat = fat->bpb.rsvd_sec_cnt;
58: 
59: 	acquiresleep(&sb->sb_lock);
60: 	if (sb->op.read(sb, 0, afsec, alloc_fat_sec, 0, SECSZ) != SECSZ) {
61: 		releasesleep(&sb->sb_lock);
62: 		freepage(fat->fatcache.page);
63: 		fat->fatcache.page = NULL;
64: 		return -EIO;
65: 	}
66: 
67: 	// Check whether the 'next free' field lie to us.
68: 	// if (*(uint32 *)(afsec + fat_offset_of_clus(fat, fat->next_free)) != 0
69: 	// 	&& fat_update_next_free(sb) == 0)
70: 	// 	panic("fat_cache_init: no next free");
71: 
72: 	releasesleep(&sb->sb_lock);
73: 	return 0;
74: }

79: 	struct fat32_sb *fat = sb2fat(sb);

84: // Caller should hold sb->sb_lock. Sync the fat region and something else.
85: void fat_cache_sync(struct superblock *sb)
86: {
87: 	struct fat32_sb *fat = sb2fat(sb);
88: 
89: 	// sync the fat region
90: 	for (int i = 0; i < FAT_CACHE_NSEC; i++) {
91: 		if (fat->fatcache.dirty[i]) {
92: 			if (sb->op.write(sb, 0, &fat->fatcache.page[i * SECSZ],
93: 							fat->fatcache.fatsec[i], 0, SECSZ) == SECSZ)
94: 			{
95: 				fat->fatcache.dirty[i] = 0;
96: 			}
97: 		}
98: 	}
99: 
100: 	// sync the fs info sec
101: 	sb->op.write(sb, 0, (char *)&fat->free_count, fat->fs_info,
102: 				FAT_FREE_CNT_OFF, sizeof(fat->free_count));
103: 	sb->op.write(sb, 0, (char *)&fat->next_free, fat->fs_info,
104: 				FAT_NEXT_FREE_OFF, sizeof(fat->next_free));
105: }

匹配分数 1.000 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": true, "exact_token_fp": true, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 1.0}

oskernel2023-zmz@recover 源码

109:  * Get the index in the cache page of the given fat sector.
110:  * If cached hit, return 1, the index returns in *pidx.
111:  * Otherwise return 0, the index is the least recently used one
112:  * (or an available one if there is).
113:  */
114: static int get_cache_fat(struct superblock *sb, uint32 fatsec, int *pidx)
115: {
116: 	struct fat32_sb *fat = sb2fat(sb);
117: 	int aidx = fat->fatcache.allocidx;
118: 	int victim = -1;
119: 	uint32 max = 0;
120: 
121: 	for (int i = 0; i < FAT_CACHE_NSEC; i++) {
122: 		uint32 sec = fat->fatcache.fatsec[i];
123: 		if (sec == fatsec) {	// cache hit
124: 			*pidx = i;
125: 			pidx = NULL;
126: 			fat->fatcache.lrucnt[i] = 0;
127: 		}
128: 		else if (sec == 0) {	// find an empty slot
129: 			/**
130: 			 * We fill the cache page in an index-increasing way,
131: 			 * so if we meet an empty slot, the next slots must be
132: 			 * empty, too. We can finish our search.
133: 			 * Since no fat region sector will be invalid, once the
134: 			 * cache page becomes full, it will always be full.
135: 			 * There is no case where there is an empty hole inside it.
136: 			 */
137: 			victim = i;
138: 			break;
139: 		}
140: 		else if (i != aidx) {	// We don't evict the alloc sec.
141: 			uint32 cnt = ++fat->fatcache.lrucnt[i];
142: 			if (cnt > max) {
143: 				max = cnt;
144: 				victim = i;
145: 			}
146: 		}
147: 	}
148: 	if (pidx) {					// cache not hit
149: 		*pidx = victim;
150: 		return 0;
151: 	}
152: 	return 1;
153: }
154:

157: {
158: 	struct fat32_sb *fat = sb2fat(sb);

114: static int get_cache_fat(struct superblock *sb, uint32 fatsec, int *pidx)

116: 	struct fat32_sb *fat = sb2fat(sb);
117: 	int aidx = fat->fatcache.allocidx;
118: 	int victim = -1;
119: 	uint32 max = 0;
120: 
121: 	for (int i = 0; i < FAT_CACHE_NSEC; i++) {
122: 		uint32 sec = fat->fatcache.fatsec[i];
123: 		if (sec == fatsec) {	// cache hit
124: 			*pidx = i;
125: 			pidx = NULL;
126: 			fat->fatcache.lrucnt[i] = 0;
127: 		}
128: 		else if (sec == 0) {	// find an empty slot
129: 			/**
130: 			 * We fill the cache page in an index-increasing way,
131: 			 * so if we meet an empty slot, the next slots must be
132: 			 * empty, too. We can finish our search.
133: 			 * Since no fat region sector will be invalid, once the
134: 			 * cache page becomes full, it will always be full.
135: 			 * There is no case where there is an empty hole inside it.
136: 			 */
137: 			victim = i;
138: 			break;
139: 		}
140: 		else if (i != aidx) {	// We don't evict the alloc sec.

143: 				max = cnt;
144: 				victim = i;
145: 			}
146: 		}
147: 	}
148: 	if (pidx) {					// cache not hit
149: 		*pidx = victim;
150: 		return 0;
151: 	}
152: 	return 1;
153: }

171: 
172: #define FAT_ENTRY_NUM	(SECSZ / sizeof(uint32))
173: 
174: // Caller should hold sb->sb_lock.
175: // Update the next_free field (and fat cache if necessary).
176: uint32 fat_update_next_free(struct superblock *sb)
177: {
178: 	struct fat32_sb *fat = sb2fat(sb);
179: 	int aidx = fat->fatcache.allocidx;
180: 	uint32 *asecdata = (uint32 *)&fat->fatcache.page[aidx * SECSZ];
181: 
182: 	// It's OK if fat->next_free is an illegal value (when initializing).
183: 	uint32 fatoff = fat_offset_of_clus(fat, fat->next_free) / sizeof(uint32);
184: 	uint32 cnt = FAT_ENTRY_NUM;
185: 
186: 	if (asecdata[fatoff] == 0) {
187: 		asecdata[fatoff] = FAT32_EOC + 7;		// mark allocated
188: 		fat->fatcache.dirty[aidx] = 1;
189: 		fat->free_count--;
190: 		cnt--;
191: 		fatoff = (fatoff + 1) % FAT_ENTRY_NUM;
192: 	}
193: 
194: 	// Look up the new next_free
195: 	fat->next_free = 0;
196: 	int idx = aidx;
197: 	for (;;) {
198: 		for (; cnt > 0; cnt--, fatoff = (fatoff + 1) % FAT_ENTRY_NUM) {
199: 			if (asecdata[fatoff] == 0) {		// found
200: 				fat->fatcache.allocidx = aidx;
201: 				fat->next_free = fatoff + FAT_ENTRY_NUM * 
202: 									(fat->fatcache.fatsec[aidx] - fat->bpb.rsvd_sec_cnt);
203: 				return fat->next_free;			// in most case we go out from here
204: 			}
205: 		}
206: 		// Unluckily, the current alloc-sec has no cluster.
207: 		do {
208: 			// Switch to next cached fat sector.
209: 			aidx = (aidx + 1) % FAT_CACHE_NSEC;
210: 			// There is no available cluster in cached fat sec.
211: 			if (aidx == idx)
212: 				goto notfound;
213: 		} while (fat->fatcache.fatsec[aidx] == 0);
214: 
215: 		asecdata = (uint32 *)&fat->fatcache.page[aidx * SECSZ];
216: 		fatoff = 0;
217: 		cnt = FAT_ENTRY_NUM;
218: 	}
219: 
220: notfound:
221: 	// Unfortunately, all the cached fat sectors have no cluster.
222: 	// We have to do more work -- look up to the disk.
223: 	if (fat->free_count == 0)
224: 		return 0;
225: 
226: 	/**
227: 	 * We are going to dig in the disk. But before we start, we
228: 	 * need to do some preliminaries. That is, we must jump over
229: 	 * the sectors that are cached, because we've checked all of
230: 	 * them. More importantly, the identical sectors read from
231: 	 * the disk are not the latest (the cached ones are).
232: 	 */
233: 	uint32 cachesec[FAT_CACHE_NSEC + 1];
234: 	uint32 sec, max = 0;
235: 	cnt = FAT_CACHE_NSEC;
236: 	for (aidx = idx = 0; idx < FAT_CACHE_NSEC; idx++) {
237: 		cachesec[idx] = fat->fatcache.fatsec[idx];
238: 		if (cachesec[idx] == 0) {		// find empty cache slot
239: 			aidx = idx;
240: 			cnt = idx + 1;
241: 			do cachesec[idx++] = 0;
242: 			while (idx < FAT_CACHE_NSEC);
243: 			break;
244: 		}
245: 		sec = fat->fatcache.lrucnt[idx];
246: 		if (sec > max) {				// to determine the eviction victim
247: 			max = sec;
248: 			aidx = idx;
249: 		}
250: 	}
251: 
252: 	for (idx = 0; idx < cnt - 1; idx++)	// small array, swap sort is enough
253: 		for (sec = idx + 1; sec < cnt; sec++)
254: 			if (cachesec[idx] > cachesec[sec]) {
255: 				max = cachesec[idx];
256: 				cachesec[idx] = cachesec[sec];
257: 				cachesec[sec] = max;
258: 			}
259: 
260: 	asecdata = (uint32 *)&fat->fatcache.page[aidx * SECSZ];
261: 	max = fat->bpb.rsvd_sec_cnt + fat->bpb.fat_sz;	// last sector of fat region
262: 	sec = fat->next_free_fat;
263: 	cachesec[FAT_CACHE_NSEC] = fat->bpb.rsvd_sec_cnt;	// place a sentry in tail
264: 
265: try:
266: 	for (idx = 0; cachesec[idx] < sec && idx < cnt; idx++);
267: 	for (; sec < max; sec++) {
268: 		if (sec == cachesec[idx]) {		// meet one in the cache
269: 			idx++;						// since we sorted, move to next value
270: 			continue;
271: 		}
272: 		fat_cache_load(sb, sec, aidx);
273: 		fatoff = (sec == cachesec[FAT_CACHE_NSEC]) ? 2 : 0;	// is the first fat sec?
274: 		for (; fatoff < FAT_ENTRY_NUM; fatoff++) {
275: 			if (asecdata[fatoff] == 0) {			// found
276: 				fat->next_free_fat = sec + 1;
277: 				fat->fatcache.allocidx = aidx;
278: 				fat->next_free = fatoff + FAT_ENTRY_NUM * 
279: 									(sec - fat->bpb.rsvd_sec_cnt);
280: 				return fat->next_free;
281: 			}
282: 		}
283: 	}
284: 	if (max != fat->bpb.rsvd_sec_cnt + fat->bpb.fat_sz)		// the second try fail
285: 		panic("fat_update_next_free: no next but free_count is not zero");
286: 
287: 	// try again from the first fat region sector.
288: 	max = fat->next_free_fat;
289: 	sec = fat->bpb.rsvd_sec_cnt;
290: 	goto try;
291: }
292: 
293: /**
294:  * Read the FAT table content corresponded to the given cluster number.
295:  * @param   sb          super block, we need the functions in it
296:  * @param   cluster     the number of cluster which you want to read its content in FAT table

xv6-k210@final / 来源候选源码

171: 
172: #define FAT_ENTRY_NUM	(SECSZ / sizeof(uint32))
173: 
174: // Caller should hold sb->sb_lock.
175: // Update the next_free field (and fat cache if necessary).
176: uint32 fat_update_next_free(struct superblock *sb)
177: {
178: 	struct fat32_sb *fat = sb2fat(sb);
179: 	int aidx = fat->fatcache.allocidx;
180: 	uint32 *asecdata = (uint32 *)&fat->fatcache.page[aidx * SECSZ];
181: 
182: 	// It's OK if fat->next_free is an illegal value (when initializing).
183: 	uint32 fatoff = fat_offset_of_clus(fat, fat->next_free) / sizeof(uint32);
184: 	uint32 cnt = FAT_ENTRY_NUM;
185: 
186: 	if (asecdata[fatoff] == 0) {
187: 		asecdata[fatoff] = FAT32_EOC + 7;		// mark allocated
188: 		fat->fatcache.dirty[aidx] = 1;
189: 		fat->free_count--;
190: 		cnt--;
191: 		fatoff = (fatoff + 1) % FAT_ENTRY_NUM;
192: 	}
193: 
194: 	// Look up the new next_free
195: 	fat->next_free = 0;
196: 	int idx = aidx;
197: 	for (;;) {
198: 		for (; cnt > 0; cnt--, fatoff = (fatoff + 1) % FAT_ENTRY_NUM) {
199: 			if (asecdata[fatoff] == 0) {		// found
200: 				fat->fatcache.allocidx = aidx;
201: 				fat->next_free = fatoff + FAT_ENTRY_NUM * 
202: 									(fat->fatcache.fatsec[aidx] - fat->bpb.rsvd_sec_cnt);
203: 				return fat->next_free;			// in most case we go out from here
204: 			}
205: 		}
206: 		// Unluckily, the current alloc-sec has no cluster.
207: 		do {
208: 			// Switch to next cached fat sector.
209: 			aidx = (aidx + 1) % FAT_CACHE_NSEC;
210: 			// There is no available cluster in cached fat sec.
211: 			if (aidx == idx)
212: 				goto notfound;
213: 		} while (fat->fatcache.fatsec[aidx] == 0);
214: 
215: 		asecdata = (uint32 *)&fat->fatcache.page[aidx * SECSZ];
216: 		fatoff = 0;
217: 		cnt = FAT_ENTRY_NUM;
218: 	}
219: 
220: notfound:
221: 	// Unfortunately, all the cached fat sectors have no cluster.
222: 	// We have to do more work -- look up to the disk.
223: 	if (fat->free_count == 0)
224: 		return 0;
225: 
226: 	/**
227: 	 * We are going to dig in the disk. But before we start, we
228: 	 * need to do some preliminaries. That is, we must jump over
229: 	 * the sectors that are cached, because we've checked all of
230: 	 * them. More importantly, the identical sectors read from
231: 	 * the disk are not the latest (the cached ones are).
232: 	 */
233: 	uint32 cachesec[FAT_CACHE_NSEC + 1];
234: 	uint32 sec, max = 0;
235: 	cnt = FAT_CACHE_NSEC;
236: 	for (aidx = idx = 0; idx < FAT_CACHE_NSEC; idx++) {
237: 		cachesec[idx] = fat->fatcache.fatsec[idx];
238: 		if (cachesec[idx] == 0) {		// find empty cache slot
239: 			aidx = idx;
240: 			cnt = idx + 1;
241: 			do cachesec[idx++] = 0;
242: 			while (idx < FAT_CACHE_NSEC);
243: 			break;
244: 		}
245: 		sec = fat->fatcache.lrucnt[idx];
246: 		if (sec > max) {				// to determine the eviction victim
247: 			max = sec;
248: 			aidx = idx;
249: 		}
250: 	}
251: 
252: 	for (idx = 0; idx < cnt - 1; idx++)	// small array, swap sort is enough
253: 		for (sec = idx + 1; sec < cnt; sec++)
254: 			if (cachesec[idx] > cachesec[sec]) {
255: 				max = cachesec[idx];
256: 				cachesec[idx] = cachesec[sec];
257: 				cachesec[sec] = max;
258: 			}
259: 
260: 	asecdata = (uint32 *)&fat->fatcache.page[aidx * SECSZ];
261: 	max = fat->bpb.rsvd_sec_cnt + fat->bpb.fat_sz;	// last sector of fat region
262: 	sec = fat->next_free_fat;
263: 	cachesec[FAT_CACHE_NSEC] = fat->bpb.rsvd_sec_cnt;	// place a sentry in tail
264: 
265: try:
266: 	for (idx = 0; cachesec[idx] < sec && idx < cnt; idx++);
267: 	for (; sec < max; sec++) {
268: 		if (sec == cachesec[idx]) {		// meet one in the cache
269: 			idx++;						// since we sorted, move to next value
270: 			continue;
271: 		}
272: 		fat_cache_load(sb, sec, aidx);
273: 		fatoff = (sec == cachesec[FAT_CACHE_NSEC]) ? 2 : 0;	// is the first fat sec?
274: 		for (; fatoff < FAT_ENTRY_NUM; fatoff++) {
275: 			if (asecdata[fatoff] == 0) {			// found
276: 				fat->next_free_fat = sec + 1;
277: 				fat->fatcache.allocidx = aidx;
278: 				fat->next_free = fatoff + FAT_ENTRY_NUM * 
279: 									(sec - fat->bpb.rsvd_sec_cnt);
280: 				return fat->next_free;
281: 			}
282: 		}
283: 	}
284: 	if (max != fat->bpb.rsvd_sec_cnt + fat->bpb.fat_sz)		// the second try fail
285: 		panic("fat_update_next_free: no next but free_count is not zero");
286: 
287: 	// try again from the first fat region sector.
288: 	max = fat->next_free_fat;
289: 	sec = fat->bpb.rsvd_sec_cnt;
290: 	goto try;
291: }

293: /**
294:  * Read the FAT table content corresponded to the given cluster number.
295:  * @param   sb          super block, we need the functions in it
296:  * @param   cluster     the number of cluster which you want to read its content in FAT table
297:  */
298: uint32 read_fat(struct superblock *sb, uint32 cluster)
299: {
300: 	if (cluster >= FAT32_EOC)
301: 		return cluster;
302: 
303: 	struct fat32_sb *fat = sb2fat(sb);
304: 	if (cluster > fat->data_clus_cnt + 1) {     // because cluster number starts at 2, not 0
305: 		__alert_fs_err("read_fat");
306: 		return 0;
307: 	}
308: 
309: 	uint32 fat_sec = fat_sec_of_clus(fat, cluster, 1);
310: 	uint32 sec_off = fat_offset_of_clus(fat, cluster);
311: 	uint32 next;
312: 	int idx;
313: 
314: 	acquiresleep(&sb->sb_lock);
315: 
316: 	if (!get_cache_fat(sb, fat_sec, &idx))
317: 		fat_cache_load(sb, fat_sec, idx);
318: 
319: 	next = *(uint32 *)(fat->fatcache.page + idx * SECSZ + sec_off);
320: 
321: 	releasesleep(&sb->sb_lock);
322: 	return next;
323: 
324: /*
325: 	if (cluster >= FAT32_EOC) {
326: 		return cluster;
327: 	}
328: 	struct fat32_sb *fat = sb2fat(sb);
329: 	if (cluster > fat->data_clus_cnt + 1) {     // because cluster number starts at 2, not 0
330: 		printf("read_fat: cluster=%d exceed the sum=%d!\n", cluster, fat->data_clus_cnt);
331: 		__alert_fs_err("read_fat");
332: 		return 0;
333: 	}
334: 	uint32 fat_sec = fat_sec_of_clus(fat, cluster, 1);
335: 	uint32 sec_off = fat_offset_of_clus(fat, cluster);
336: 	// here should be a cache layer for FAT table, but not implemented yet.
337: 	uint32 next_clus;
338: 	acquiresleep(&sb->sb_lock);
339: 	sb->op.read(sb, 0, (char *)&next_clus, fat_sec, sec_off, sizeof(next_clus));
340: 	releasesleep(&sb->sb_lock);
341: 
342: 	// __debug_info("read_fat", "cur:%d next:%d\n", cluster, next_clus);
343: 	return next_clus;
344: */
345: }

oskernel2023-zmz@recover 源码

348:  * Write the FAT region content corresponded to the given cluster number.
349:  * @param   sb          super block
350:  * @param   cluster     the number of cluster to write its content in FAT table
351:  * @param   content     the content which should be the next cluster number of FAT end of chain flag
352:  */
353: int write_fat(struct superblock *sb, uint32 cluster, uint32 content)
354: {
355: 	if (cluster >= FAT32_EOC)
356: 		return -1;
357: 
358: 	struct fat32_sb *fat = sb2fat(sb);
359: 	if (cluster > fat->data_clus_cnt + 1) {     // because cluster number starts at 2, not 0
360: 		__alert_fs_err("write_fat");
361: 		return -1;
362: 	}
363: 
364: 	uint32 fat_sec = fat_sec_of_clus(fat, cluster, 1);
365: 	uint32 sec_off = fat_offset_of_clus(fat, cluster);
366: 	int idx;
367: 
368: 	acquiresleep(&sb->sb_lock);
369: 
370: 	if (!get_cache_fat(sb, fat_sec, &idx))
371: 		fat_cache_load(sb, fat_sec, idx);
372: 
373: 	*(uint32 *)(fat->fatcache.page + idx * SECSZ + sec_off) = content;
374: 	fat->fatcache.dirty[idx] = 1;
375: 	if (content == 0)
376: 		fat->free_count++;
377: 
378: 	releasesleep(&sb->sb_lock);
379: 	return 0;
380: /*
381: 	struct fat32_sb *fat = sb2fat(sb);
382: 	if (cluster > fat->data_clus_cnt + 1) {
383: 		printf("write_fat: cluster=%d exceed the sum=%d!\n", cluster, fat->data_clus_cnt);
384: 		__alert_fs_err("write_fat");
385: 		return -1;
386: 	}
387: 	uint32 fat_sec = fat_sec_of_clus(fat, cluster, 1);
388: 	uint32 sec_off = fat_offset_of_clus(fat, cluster);
389: 	acquiresleep(&sb->sb_lock);
390: 	sb->op.write(sb, 0, (char *)&content, fat_sec, sec_off, sizeof(content));
391: 	releasesleep(&sb->sb_lock);
392: 	return 0;
393: */
394: }

353: int write_fat(struct superblock *sb, uint32 cluster, uint32 content)
354: {

356: 		return -1;
357: 
358: 	struct fat32_sb *fat = sb2fat(sb);

oskernel2023-zmz@recover 源码

42: /**
43:  * Initialize FAT32 file system
44:  * @param		sb		superblock that get from fat32_get_sb()
45:  * @return				the root inode
46:  */
47: struct inode *fat32_init(struct superblock *sb)
48: {
49: 	int const secsz = 512;
50: 	struct fat32_sb *fat = sb2fat(sb);
51: 	struct inode *iroot = NULL;
52: 	char *buf = kmalloc(secsz);
53: 	if (buf == NULL)
54: 		return NULL;
55: 
56: 	__debug_info("fat32_init", "enter\n");
57: 	
58: 	int ret;
59: 	acquiresleep(&sb->sb_lock);
60: 	ret = sb->op.read(sb, 0, buf, 0, 0, secsz);
61: 	releasesleep(&sb->sb_lock);
62: 	if (ret < 0)
63: 		goto end;
64: 
65: 	if (strncmp((char const*)(buf + 82), "FAT32", 5)) {
66: 		__debug_error("fat32_init", "not FAT32 volume\n");
67: 		goto end;
68: 	}
69: 
70: 	memmove(&fat->bpb.byts_per_sec, buf + 11, 2);            // avoid misaligned load on k210
71: 	fat->bpb.sec_per_clus = *(buf + 13);
72: 	fat->bpb.rsvd_sec_cnt = *(uint16 *)(buf + 14);
73: 	fat->bpb.fat_cnt = *(buf + 16);
74: 	fat->bpb.hidd_sec = *(uint32 *)(buf + 28);
75: 	fat->bpb.tot_sec = *(uint32 *)(buf + 32);
76: 	fat->bpb.fat_sz = *(uint32 *)(buf + 36);
77: 	fat->bpb.root_clus = *(uint32 *)(buf + 44);
78: 	fat->fs_info = *(uint16 *)(buf + 48);
79: 	fat->first_data_sec = fat->bpb.rsvd_sec_cnt + fat->bpb.fat_cnt * fat->bpb.fat_sz;
80: 	fat->data_sec_cnt = fat->bpb.tot_sec - fat->first_data_sec;
81: 	fat->data_clus_cnt = fat->data_sec_cnt / fat->bpb.sec_per_clus;
82: 	fat->byts_per_clus = fat->bpb.sec_per_clus * fat->bpb.byts_per_sec;
83: 
84: 	__debug_info("FAT32 init", "byts_per_sec: %d\n", fat->bpb.byts_per_sec);
85: 	__debug_info("FAT32 init", "root_clus: %d\n", fat->bpb.root_clus);
86: 	__debug_info("FAT32 init", "sec_per_clus: %d\n", fat->bpb.sec_per_clus);
87: 	__debug_info("FAT32 init", "fat_cnt: %d\n", fat->bpb.fat_cnt);
88: 	__debug_info("FAT32 init", "fat_sz: %d\n", fat->bpb.fat_sz);
89: 	__debug_info("FAT32 init", "first_data_sec: %d\n", fat->first_data_sec);
90: 
91: 	__debug_info("FAT32 init", "done\n");
92: 
93: 	// read fs info sector
94: 	acquiresleep(&sb->sb_lock);
95: 	ret = sb->op.read(sb, 0, buf, fat->fs_info, 0, secsz);
96: 	releasesleep(&sb->sb_lock);
97: 	if (ret < 0)
98: 		goto end;
99: 
100: 	if (*(uint32*)buf != 0x41615252 || 
101: 		*(uint32*)(buf + 484) != 0x61417272 ||
102: 		*(uint32*)(buf + 508) != 0xaa550000)
103: 	{
104: 		__debug_warn("fat32_init", "wrong magic\n");
105: 		goto end;
106: 	}
107: 
108: 	fat->free_count = *(uint32*)(buf + FAT_FREE_CNT_OFF);
109: 	fat->next_free = *(uint32*)(buf + FAT_NEXT_FREE_OFF);
110: 	__debug_info("fat32_init", "free_count: %d\n", fat->free_count);
111: 	__debug_info("fat32_init", "next_free: %d\n", fat->next_free);
112: 
113: 	if (fat_cache_init(sb) < 0)
114: 		goto end;
115: 
116: 	struct fat32_entry *root = kmalloc(sizeof(struct fat32_entry));
117: 	if (root == NULL)
118: 		goto end;
119: 
120: 	memset(root, 0, sizeof(struct fat32_entry));
121: 	root->attribute = (ATTR_DIRECTORY | ATTR_SYSTEM);
122: 	root->first_clus = fat->bpb.root_clus;
123: 
124: 	iroot = &root->vfs_inode;
125: 	iroot->mode = S_IFDIR | 0x1ff;
126: 	iroot->state |= I_STATE_VALID;
127: 	iroot->nlink = 1;
128: 	iroot->op = &fat32_inode_op;
129: 	iroot->fop = &fat32_file_op;
130: 	iroot->sb = sb;
131: 
132: 	sb->blocksz = fat->bpb.byts_per_sec;
133: 
134: 	__debug_info("fat32_root_init", "root cluster: %d\n", root->first_clus);
135: 
136: end:
137: 	kfree(buf);
138: 	return iroot;
139: }
140: 
141: int fat_stat_fs(struct superblock *sb, struct statfs *stat)
142: {
143: 	struct fat32_sb *fat = sb2fat(sb);
144:

80: 	fat->data_sec_cnt = fat->bpb.tot_sec - fat->first_data_sec;
81: 	fat->data_clus_cnt = fat->data_sec_cnt / fat->bpb.sec_per_clus;
82: 	fat->byts_per_clus = fat->bpb.sec_per_clus * fat->bpb.byts_per_sec;
83:

85: 	__debug_info("FAT32 init", "root_clus: %d\n", fat->bpb.root_clus);
86: 	__debug_info("FAT32 init", "sec_per_clus: %d\n", fat->bpb.sec_per_clus);
87: 	__debug_info("FAT32 init", "fat_cnt: %d\n", fat->bpb.fat_cnt);
88: 	__debug_info("FAT32 init", "fat_sz: %d\n", fat->bpb.fat_sz);
89: 	__debug_info("FAT32 init", "first_data_sec: %d\n", fat->first_data_sec);
90:

92:

94: 	acquiresleep(&sb->sb_lock);
95: 	ret = sb->op.read(sb, 0, buf, fat->fs_info, 0, secsz);
96: 	releasesleep(&sb->sb_lock);

103: 	{
104: 		__debug_warn("fat32_init", "wrong magic\n");

107:

109: 	fat->next_free = *(uint32*)(buf + FAT_NEXT_FREE_OFF);
110: 	__debug_info("fat32_init", "free_count: %d\n", fat->free_count);
111: 	__debug_info("fat32_init", "next_free: %d\n", fat->next_free);
112: 
113: 	if (fat_cache_init(sb) < 0)
114: 		goto end;
115: 
116: 	struct fat32_entry *root = kmalloc(sizeof(struct fat32_entry));
117: 	if (root == NULL)
118: 		goto end;
119: 
120: 	memset(root, 0, sizeof(struct fat32_entry));
121: 	root->attribute = (ATTR_DIRECTORY | ATTR_SYSTEM);
122: 	root->first_clus = fat->bpb.root_clus;
123: 
124: 	iroot = &root->vfs_inode;
125: 	iroot->mode = S_IFDIR | 0x1ff;
126: 	iroot->state |= I_STATE_VALID;
127: 	iroot->nlink = 1;
128: 	iroot->op = &fat32_inode_op;
129: 	iroot->fop = &fat32_file_op;
130: 	iroot->sb = sb;
131: 
132: 	sb->blocksz = fat->bpb.byts_per_sec;
133: 
134: 	__debug_info("fat32_root_init", "root cluster: %d\n", root->first_clus);
135: 
136: end:
137: 	kfree(buf);
138: 	return iroot;
139: }

141: int fat_stat_fs(struct superblock *sb, struct statfs *stat)
142: {
143: 	struct fat32_sb *fat = sb2fat(sb);
144: 	
145: 	stat->f_type = 0x4d44;	// fat32 magic
146: 	stat->f_bsize = fat->byts_per_clus;
147: 	stat->f_blocks = fat->bpb.tot_sec / fat->bpb.sec_per_clus;
148: 	stat->f_bfree = fat->free_count;
149: 	stat->f_bavail = fat->free_count;
150: 	stat->f_files = 0;
151: 	stat->f_ffree = 0;
152: 	stat->f_fsid[0] = sb->devnum;
153: 	stat->f_fsid[1] = 0;
154: 	stat->f_namelen = FAT32_MAX_FILENAME;
155: 	stat->f_frsize = 0;
156: 	stat->f_flags = 0;
157: 
158: 	return 0;
159: }

190: /**
191:  * Read/Write a cluster, caller must hold relative locks
192:  */
193: uint fat_rw_clus(struct superblock *sb, uint32 cluster, int write, int user, uint64 data, uint off, uint n)
194: {
195: 	struct fat32_sb *fat = sb2fat(sb);
196: 	if (off + n > fat->byts_per_clus)
197: 		panic("fat_rw_clus: offset out of range");
198: 
199: 	uint tot, m;
200: 	uint16 const bps = fat->bpb.byts_per_sec;
201: 	uint sec = first_sec_of_clus(fat, cluster) + off / bps;
202: 
203: 	// __debug_info("fat_rw_clus", "clus:%d off:%d len:%d\n", cluster, off, n);
204: 	// for (tot = 0; tot < n; tot += m, off += m, data += m, sec++) {
205: 	// 	m = bps - off % bps;
206: 	// 	if (n - tot < m) {
207: 	// 		m = n - tot;
208: 	// 	}
209: 	// 	// __debug_info("fat_rw_clus", "sec:%d m:%d\n", sec, m);
210: 	// 	if (write) {
211: 	// 		if (sb->op.write(sb, user, (char*)data, sec, off % bps, m) < 0) {
212: 	// 			break;
213: 	// 		}
214: 	// 	} else if (sb->op.read(sb, user, (char*)data, sec, off % bps, m) < 0) {
215: 	// 		break;
216: 	// 	}
217: 	// }
218: 	if (write) {
219: 		for (tot = 0; tot < n; tot += m, off += m, data += m, sec++) {
220: 			m = bps - off % bps;
221: 			if (n - tot < m)
222: 				m = n - tot;
223: 			// __debug_info("fat_rw_clus", "sec:%d m:%d\n", sec, m);
224: 			if (sb->op.write(sb, user, (char*)data, sec, off % bps, m) < 0)
225: 				break;
226: 		}
227: 	} else {
228: 		tot = sb->op.read(sb, user, (char*)data, sec, off % bps, n);
229: 	}
230: 	// __debug_info("fat_rw_clus", "done: tot:%d\n", tot);
231: 	return tot;
232: }
233:

236: {
237: 	struct fat32_entry *ep = kmalloc(sizeof(struct fat32_entry));

234: // There is no real inode on disk, just return an in-mem structure.
235: struct inode *fat_alloc_inode(struct superblock *sb)
236: {
237: 	struct fat32_entry *ep = kmalloc(sizeof(struct fat32_entry));
238: 	if (ep == NULL) {
239: 		return NULL;
240: 	}
241: 	memset(ep, 0, sizeof(struct fat32_entry));
242: 
243: 	struct inode *ip = &ep->vfs_inode;
244: 	ip->sb = sb;
245: 	ip->nlink = 1;
246: 	initsleeplock(&ip->lock, "inode");
247: 	initlock(&ip->ilock, "inode2");
248: 
249: 	return ip;
250: }

xv6-k210/kernel/fs/fat32/fat32.c::fat_read_file
匹配分数 1.000 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": true, "exact_token_fp": true, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 1.0}

oskernel2023-zmz@recover 源码

268:  * @param   ip          the inode
269:  * @param   off         the offset from the beginning of the relative file
270:  * @param   n           number of bytes to read
271:  * @return              the total bytes that have been read
272:  */
273: int fat_read_file(struct inode *ip, int user_dst, uint64 dst, uint off, uint n)
274: {
275: 	struct fat32_entry *entry = i2fat(ip);
276: 
277: 	if (off > entry->file_size || off + n < off || (entry->attribute & ATTR_DIRECTORY)) {
278: 		return 0;
279: 	}
280: 	if (off + n > entry->file_size) {
281: 		n = entry->file_size - off;
282: 	}
283: 
284: 	// __debug_info("fat_read_file", "file:%s off:%d len:%d\n", ip->entry->filename, off, n);
285: 
286: 	uint tot, m;
287: 	uint32 const bpc = sb2fat(ip->sb)->byts_per_clus;
288: 	uint32 clus;
289: 	for (tot = 0; tot < n; tot += m, off += m, dst += m) {
290: 		if ((clus = reloc_clus(ip, off, 0)) == 0) {
291: 			break;
292: 		}
293: 		m = bpc - off % bpc;
294: 		if (n - tot < m) {
295: 			m = n - tot;
296: 		}
297: 		if (fat_rw_clus(ip->sb, clus, 0, user_dst, dst, off % bpc, m) != m) {
298: 			break;
299: 		}
300: 	}
301: 	// __debug_info("fat_read_file", "done  read:%d\n", tot);
302: 	return tot;
303: }
304: 
305: int fat_read_file_vec(struct inode *ip, struct iovec *iovecs, int count, uint off)
306: {
307: 	uint tot = 0;
308: 	for (int i = 0; i < count; i++) {

xv6-k210@final / 来源候选源码

268:  * @param   ip          the inode
269:  * @param   off         the offset from the beginning of the relative file
270:  * @param   n           number of bytes to read
271:  * @return              the total bytes that have been read
272:  */
273: int fat_read_file(struct inode *ip, int user_dst, uint64 dst, uint off, uint n)
274: {
275: 	struct fat32_entry *entry = i2fat(ip);
276: 
277: 	if (off > entry->file_size || off + n < off || (entry->attribute & ATTR_DIRECTORY)) {
278: 		return 0;
279: 	}
280: 	if (off + n > entry->file_size) {
281: 		n = entry->file_size - off;
282: 	}
283: 
284: 	// __debug_info("fat_read_file", "file:%s off:%d len:%d\n", ip->entry->filename, off, n);
285: 
286: 	uint tot, m;
287: 	uint32 const bpc = sb2fat(ip->sb)->byts_per_clus;
288: 	uint32 clus;
289: 	for (tot = 0; tot < n; tot += m, off += m, dst += m) {
290: 		if ((clus = reloc_clus(ip, off, 0)) == 0) {
291: 			break;
292: 		}
293: 		m = bpc - off % bpc;
294: 		if (n - tot < m) {
295: 			m = n - tot;
296: 		}
297: 		if (fat_rw_clus(ip->sb, clus, 0, user_dst, dst, off % bpc, m) != m) {
298: 			break;
299: 		}
300: 	}
301: 	// __debug_info("fat_read_file", "done  read:%d\n", tot);
302: 	return tot;
303: }

318: 		}
319: 	}
320: 	return tot;
321: }

338:

340: 		|| (entry->attribute & ATTR_READ_ONLY)) {

342: 	}

xv6-k210@final / 来源候选源码

330:  * @param   off         the offset from the beginning of the relative file
331:  * @param   n           number of bytes to write
332:  * @return              the total bytes that have been written
333:  *                      or -1 if completely fail
334:  */
335: int fat_write_file(struct inode *ip, int user_src, uint64 src, uint off, uint n)
336: {
337: 	struct fat32_entry *entry = i2fat(ip);
338: 	static int whatever_flag = 1;
339: 
340: 	if ((uint64)off + n > 0x80000) {
341: 		whatever_flag = 0;
342: 		return -1;
343: 	}
344: 	if (off > entry->file_size || off + n < off || (uint64)off + n > 0xffffffff
345: 		|| (entry->attribute & ATTR_READ_ONLY)) {
346: 		return -1;
347: 	}
348: 	if (entry->first_clus == 0) {   // so file_size if 0 too, which requests off == 0
349: 		if ((entry->first_clus = alloc_clus(ip->sb)) == 0) {
350: 			return -1;
351: 		}
352: 		// entry->clus_cnt = 0;
353: 		ip->state |= I_STATE_DIRTY;
354: 	}
355: 
356: 	uint tot, m;
357: 	struct superblock *sb = ip->sb;
358: 	struct fat32_sb *fat = sb2fat(sb);
359: 	uint32 const bpc = fat->byts_per_clus;
360: 	uint32 const bps = fat->bpb.byts_per_sec;
361: 	uint32 clus;
362: 	for (tot = 0; tot < n; tot += m, off += m, src += m) {
363: 		if ((clus = reloc_clus(ip, off, 1)) == 0) {
364: 			break;
365: 		}
366: 		m = bpc - off % bpc;
367: 		if (n - tot < m) {
368: 			m = n - tot;
369: 		}
370: 
371: 		if (!whatever_flag) {
372: 			if (off >= ip->size && m < bpc) {
373: 				int nsec = m / bps;
374: 				uint32 sec = first_sec_of_clus(sb, clus) + nsec;
375: 				sb->op.clear(sb, sec, fat->bpb.sec_per_clus - nsec);
376: 			}
377: 			if (fat_rw_clus(sb, clus, 1, user_src, src, off % bpc, m) != m) {
378: 				break;
379: 			}
380: 		}
381: 	}
382: 	if (n > 0 && off > entry->file_size) {
383: 		ip->size = entry->file_size = off;
384: 		ip->state |= I_STATE_DIRTY;
385: 	}
386: 	// __debug_info("fat_write_file", "file:%s off:%d len:%d written:%d\n", ip->entry->filename, off, n, tot);
387: 	return tot;
388: }

394: 
395: /**
396:  * Truncate the file content.
397:  * Caller must hold ip->lock.
398:  */
399: int fat_truncate_file(struct inode *ip)
400: {
401: 	struct fat32_entry *entry = i2fat(ip);
402: 	uint32 const bpc = sb2fat(ip->sb)->byts_per_clus;
403: 	uint32 off = 0;
404: 	uint32 clus = reloc_clus(ip, off, 0);
405: 
406: 	while (clus != 0) {
407: 		free_clus(ip->sb, clus);
408: 		off += bpc;
409: 		clus = reloc_clus(ip, off, 0);
410: 	}
411: 	free_clus_cache(entry);
412: 	ip->size = entry->file_size = 0;
413: 	entry->first_clus = 0;
414: 
415: 	ip->state |= I_STATE_DIRTY;
416: 	return 0;
417: }

匹配分数 0.754 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": true, "exact_token_fp": false, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 0.18}

oskernel2023-zmz@recover 源码

448: 
449: /**
450:  * Read a directory from off, and stat the next file. Skip empty entries.
451:  * @return  bytes that the entries take up, or 0 if no entries 
452:  */
453: int fat_read_dir(struct inode *ip, struct dirent *dent, uint off)
454: {
455: 	if (!(i2fat(ip)->attribute & ATTR_DIRECTORY))
456: 		return -1;
457: 
458: 	int count = 0;
459: 	uint off2 = off - off % 32;
460: 
461: 	struct fat32_entry entry;
462: 	int ret;
463: 	while ((ret = fat_read_entry(ip, &entry, dent->name, off2, &count)) == 0) {
464: 		// Skip empty entries.
465: 		off2 += count << 5;
466: 	}
467: 
468: 	// Meet end of dir
469: 	if (ret < 0) {
470: 		return 0;
471: 	}
472: 	off2 += (count << 5);
473: 	ret = off2 - off;
474: 
475: 	// Size of this dent, varies from length of filename.
476: 	int size = sizeof(struct dirent) - sizeof(dent->name) + strlen(dent->name) + 1;
477: 	size += (sizeof(uint64) - (size % sizeof(uint64))) % sizeof(uint64); // Align to 8.
478: 
479: 	dent->ino = entry.first_clus;
480: 	dent->off = off2;
481: 	dent->reclen = size;
482: 	dent->type = (entry.attribute & ATTR_DIRECTORY) ? T_DIR : T_FILE;
483: 
484: 	return ret;
485: }
486: 
487: /**
488:  * Seacher for the entry in a directory and return a structure. Besides, record the offset of
489:  * some continuous empty slots that can fit the length of filename.
490:  *

xv6-k210@final / 来源候选源码

464: 
465: /**
466:  * Read a directory from off, and stat the next file. Skip empty entries.
467:  * @return  bytes that the entries take up, or 0 if no entries 
468:  */
469: int fat_read_dir(struct inode *ip, struct dirent *dent, uint off)
470: {
471: 	if (!(i2fat(ip)->attribute & ATTR_DIRECTORY))
472: 		return -1;
473: 
474: 	int count = 0;
475: 	uint off2 = off - off % 32;
476: 
477: 	struct fat32_entry entry;
478: 	int ret;
479: 	while ((ret = fat_read_entry(ip, &entry, dent->name, off2, &count)) == 0) {
480: 		// Skip empty entries.
481: 		off2 += count << 5;
482: 	}
483: 
484: 	// Meet end of dir
485: 	if (ret < 0) {
486: 		return 0;
487: 	}
488: 	off2 += (count << 5);
489: 	ret = off2 - off;
490: 
491: 	// Size of this dent, varies from length of filename.
492: 	int size = sizeof(struct dirent) - sizeof(dent->name) + strlen(dent->name) + 1;
493: 	size += (sizeof(uint64) - (size % sizeof(uint64))) % sizeof(uint64); // Align to 8.
494: 
495: 	dent->ino = entry.first_clus;
496: 	dent->off = off2;
497: 	dent->reclen = size;
498: 	dent->type = (entry.attribute & ATTR_DIRECTORY) ? T_DIR : T_FILE;
499: 
500: 	return ret;
501: }
502: 
503: /**
504:  * Seacher for the entry in a directory and return a structure. Besides, record the offset of
505:  * some continuous empty slots that can fit the length of filename.
506:  *
fat_lookup_dir_ent修改候选

匹配分数 0.552 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": false, "exact_token_fp": false, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 0.172}

494:  * Caller must hold dir->lock.
495:  * @param   dir         the directory inode
496:  * @param   filename    target filename
497:  * @param   poff        offset of proper empty entry slots from the beginning of the dir
498:  */
499: struct fat32_entry *fat_lookup_dir_ent(struct inode *dir, char *filename, uint *poff)
500: {
501: 	if (!(i2fat(dir)->attribute & ATTR_DIRECTORY))
502: 		panic("dirlookup not DIR");
503: 	if (dir->state & I_STATE_FREE)
504: 		return NULL;
505: 
506: 	// __debug_info("fat_lookup_dir_ent", "in\n");
507: 	struct inode *ip = fat_alloc_inode(dir->sb);
508: 	if (ip == NULL)
509: 		return NULL;
510: 	struct fat32_entry *ep = i2fat(ip);
511: 
512: 	int len = strlen(filename);
513: 	int entcnt = (len + CHAR_LONG_NAME - 1) / CHAR_LONG_NAME + 1;   // count of l-n-entries, rounds up. plus s-n-e
514: 	int count = 0;
515: 	int type;
516: 	uint off = 0;
517: 	char namebuf[FAT32_MAX_FILENAME + 1];
518: 	while ((type = fat_read_entry(dir, ep, namebuf, off, &count) != -1)) {
519: 		if (type == 0) {
520: 			if (poff && count >= entcnt) {
521: 				*poff = off;
522: 				poff = NULL;
523: 			}
524: 		} else if (strncmp(filename, namebuf, FAT32_MAX_FILENAME) == 0) {
525: 			if (poff) {
526: 				*poff = off;
527: 			}
528: 			ep->ent_cnt = count;
529: 			return ep;
530: 		}
531: 		off += count << 5;
532: 	}
533: 	if (poff) {
534: 		*poff = off;
535: 	}
536: 	kfree(ep);
537: 	return NULL;
538: }
539:

匹配分数 1.000 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": true, "exact_token_fp": true, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 1.0}

oskernel2023-zmz@recover 源码

540: /**
541:  * Call to 'fat_lookup_dir_ent', and stuff the fat32_entry into an inode.
542:  * 
543:  * Caller must hold dir->lock.
544:  */
545: struct inode *fat_lookup_dir(struct inode *dir, char *filename, uint *poff)
546: {
547: 	// struct superblock *sb = dir->sb;
548: 	struct inode *ip = NULL;
549: 
550: 	uint off = 0;    // Anyhow, we need this on FAT32
551: 	struct fat32_entry *ep = fat_lookup_dir_ent(dir, filename, &off);
552: 	if (ep == NULL)
553: 		goto end;
554: 
555: 	ip = &ep->vfs_inode;
556: 	ip->op = dir->op;
557: 	ip->fop = dir->fop;
558: 	ip->size = ep->file_size;
559: 	ip->mode = (ep->attribute & ATTR_DIRECTORY) ? S_IFDIR : S_IFREG;
560: 	ip->mode |= 0x1ff;
561: 
562: 	uint64 clus = reloc_clus(dir, off, 0);
563: 	ip->inum = (clus << 32) | (off % sb2fat(dir->sb)->byts_per_clus);
564: 	__debug_info("fat_lookup_dir", "name:%s ipos: clus=%d coff=%d inum=%p sizeof(uint64)=%d\n",
565: 				filename, clus, off, ip->inum, sizeof(uint64));
566: 
567: end:
568: 	if (poff) {
569: 		*poff = off;
570: 	}
571: 	return ip;
572: }

xv6-k210@final / 来源候选源码

556: /**
557:  * Call to 'fat_lookup_dir_ent', and stuff the fat32_entry into an inode.
558:  * 
559:  * Caller must hold dir->lock.
560:  */
561: struct inode *fat_lookup_dir(struct inode *dir, char *filename, uint *poff)
562: {
563: 	// struct superblock *sb = dir->sb;
564: 	struct inode *ip = NULL;
565: 
566: 	uint off = 0;    // Anyhow, we need this on FAT32
567: 	struct fat32_entry *ep = fat_lookup_dir_ent(dir, filename, &off);
568: 	if (ep == NULL)
569: 		goto end;
570: 
571: 	ip = &ep->vfs_inode;
572: 	ip->op = dir->op;
573: 	ip->fop = dir->fop;
574: 	ip->size = ep->file_size;
575: 	ip->mode = (ep->attribute & ATTR_DIRECTORY) ? S_IFDIR : S_IFREG;
576: 	ip->mode |= 0x1ff;
577: 
578: 	uint64 clus = reloc_clus(dir, off, 0);
579: 	ip->inum = (clus << 32) | (off % sb2fat(dir->sb)->byts_per_clus);
580: 	__debug_info("fat_lookup_dir", "name:%s ipos: clus=%d coff=%d inum=%p sizeof(uint64)=%d\n",
581: 				filename, clus, off, ip->inum, sizeof(uint64));
582: 
583: end:
584: 	if (poff) {
585: 		*poff = off;
586: 	}
587: 	return ip;
588: }

117: 
118: /**
119:  * @param   fat         fat32 super block
120:  * @param   cluster     cluster number starts from 2, which means no 0 and 1
121:  */
122: static inline uint32 first_sec_of_clus(struct fat32_sb *fat, uint32 cluster)
123: {
124: 	return ((cluster - 2) * fat->bpb.sec_per_clus) + fat->first_data_sec;
125: }

32: filealloc(void)
33: {
34: 	struct file *f;

37: 		return NULL;

40: 	f->type = FD_NONE;

46: // Increment ref count for file f.
47: struct file*
48: filedup(struct file *f)
49: {
50: 	acquire(&f->lock);
51: 	if(f->ref < 1)
52: 		panic("filedup");
53: 	f->ref++;
54: 	release(&f->lock);
55: 	return f;
56: }
57:

60: fileclose(struct file *f)

89: // Get metadata about file f.
90: // addr is a user virtual address, pointing to a struct stat.
91: int
92: filestat(struct file *f, uint64 addr)
93: {
94: 	// struct proc *p = myproc();
95: 	struct kstat st;
96: 	
97: 	if(f->type == FD_INODE){
98: 		ilock(f->ip);
99: 		f->ip->op->getattr(f->ip, &st);
100: 		iunlock(f->ip);
101: 	} else if (f->type == FD_DEVICE) {
102: 		f->ip->op->getattr(f->ip, &st);
103: 	} else {
104: 		return -1;
105: 	}
106: 	// if(copyout(p->pagetable, addr, (char *)&st, sizeof(st)) < 0)
107: 	if(copyout2(addr, (char *)&st, sizeof(st)) < 0)
108: 		return -1;
109: 	return 0;
110: }

88: // Get metadata about file f.
89: // addr is a user virtual address, pointing to a struct stat.
90: int
91: filestat(struct file *f, uint64 addr)
92: {
93: 	// struct proc *p = myproc();
94: 	struct kstat st;
95: 	
96: 	if(f->type == FD_INODE){
97: 		ilock(f->ip);
98: 		f->ip->op->getattr(f->ip, &st);
99: 		iunlock(f->ip);
100: 	} else if (f->type == FD_DEVICE) {
101: 		f->ip->op->getattr(f->ip, &st);
102: 	} else {
103: 		return -1;
104: 	}
105: 	// if(copyout(p->pagetable, addr, (char *)&st, sizeof(st)) < 0)

107: 		return -1;
108: 	return 0;
109: }

匹配分数 1.000 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": true, "exact_token_fp": true, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 1.0}

109: 	return 0;

111: 
112: // Read from file f.
113: // addr is a user virtual address.
114: int
115: fileread(struct file *f, uint64 addr, int n)
116: {
117: 	int r = 0;
118: 	struct inode *ip = f->ip;
119: 
120: 	if (f->readable == 0)
121: 		return -EPERM;
122: 	if (!rangeinseg(addr, addr + n))
123: 		return -EFAULT;
124: 	if (n == 0)
125: 		return 0;
126: 
127: 	switch (f->type) {
128: 		case FD_PIPE:
129: 				r = piperead(f->pipe, addr, n);
130: 				break;
131: 		case FD_DEVICE:
132: 				// if(f->major < 0 || f->major >= NDEV || !devsw[f->major].read)
133: 				//   return -1;
134: 				// r = devsw[f->major].read(1, addr, n);
135: 				ilock(ip);
136: 				r = ip->fop->read(ip, 1, addr, 0, n);
137: 				iunlock(ip);
138: 				break;
139: 		case FD_INODE:
140: 				ilock(ip);
141: 				r = ip->fop->read(ip, 1, addr, f->off, n);
142: 				if (r > 0) {
143: 					acquire(&f->lock);
144: 					f->off += r;
145: 					release(&f->lock);
146: 				}
147: 				iunlock(ip);
148: 				break;
149: 		default:
150: 			panic("fileread");
151: 	}
152: 
153: 	return r;
154: }

158: 	char buf[PGSIZE];

113: int
114: fileread(struct file *f, uint64 addr, int n)
115: {
116: 	int r = 0;
117: 	struct inode *ip = f->ip;
118: 
119: 	if (f->readable == 0)
120: 		return -EPERM;
121: 	if (!rangeinseg(addr, addr + n))
122: 		return -EFAULT;
123: 	if (n == 0)
124: 		return 0;
125: 
126: 	switch (f->type) {
127: 		case FD_PIPE:
128: 				r = piperead(f->pipe, addr, n);
129: 				break;
130: 		case FD_DEVICE:
131: 				// if(f->major < 0 || f->major >= NDEV || !devsw[f->major].read)
132: 				//   return -1;
133: 				// r = devsw[f->major].read(1, addr, n);

138: 		case FD_INODE:
139: 				ilock(ip);
140: 				r = ip->fop->read(ip, 1, addr, f->off, n);
141: 				if (r > 0) {
142: 					acquire(&f->lock);
143: 					f->off += r;
144: 					release(&f->lock);
145: 				}
146: 				iunlock(ip);
147: 				break;
148: 		default:
149: 			panic("fileread");
150: 	}
151: 
152: 	return r;
153: }

155: 
156: int copy_file_range(struct file *fp_in,int off_in,struct file *fp_out, int off_out, int real_len, int flags)
157: {
158: 	char buf[PGSIZE];
159: 	struct inode *InNode = fp_in->ip;
160: 	struct inode *OutNode = fp_out->ip;
161: 	int cnt = 0, toget = 0;
162: 	for(;cnt<real_len; cnt+= toget){
163: 		toget = real_len - cnt;
164: 		if(toget > PGSIZE){
165: 			toget = PGSIZE;
166: 		}
167: 		InNode->fop->read(InNode,0,buf,off_in,toget);
168: 		OutNode->fop->write(OutNode,0,buf,off_out,toget);
169: 		off_in += toget;
170: 		off_out += toget;
171: 	}
172: 	return cnt;
173: 	//InNode->fop->read(InNode,0,buf,off_in,real_len);
174: }

175: 
176: // Write to file f.
177: // addr is a user virtual address.
178: int
179: filewrite(struct file *f, uint64 addr, int n)
180: {
181: 	int ret = 0;
182: 
183: 	if (f->writable == 0)
184: 		return -EPERM;
185: 	if (!rangeinseg(addr, addr + n))
186: 		return -EFAULT;
187: 
188: 	//if(f->ip == ) //AAA1
189: 
190: 	struct inode *ip = f->ip;
191: 	// __debug_info("filewrite", "addr=%p, n=%d\n", addr, n);
192: 	if (f->type == FD_PIPE) {
193: 		ret = pipewrite(f->pipe, addr, n);
194: 	} else if (f->type == FD_DEVICE) {
195: 		ret = ip->fop->write(ip, 1, addr, 0, n);
196: 	} else if (f->type == FD_INODE) {
197: 		ilock(ip);
198: 		ret = ip->fop->write(ip, 1, addr, f->off, n);
199: 		if (ret == n) {
200: 			acquire(&f->lock);
201: 			f->off += n;
202: 			release(&f->lock);
203: 		} else {
204: 			ret = -1;
205: 		}
206: 		iunlock(ip);
207: 	} else {
208: 		panic("filewrite");
209: 	}
210: 
211: 	// __debug_info("filewrite", "ret=%d\n", ret);
212: 	return ret;
213: }

匹配分数 1.000 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": true, "exact_token_fp": true, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 1.0}

212: 	return ret;

214: 
215: // Read from dir f.
216: // addr is a user virtual address.
217: int
218: filereaddir(struct file *f, uint64 addr, uint64 n)
219: {
220: 	__debug_info("filereaddir", "f->type=%d, f->readable=%d\n", f->type, f->readable);
221: 	if (f->type != FD_INODE || f->readable == 0)
222: 		return -1;
223: 
224: 	struct dirent dent;
225: 	struct inode *ip = f->ip;
226: 
227: 	if (!S_ISDIR(ip->mode))
228: 		return -ENOTDIR;
229: 	if (!rangeinseg(addr, addr + n))
230: 		return -EFAULT;
231: 
232: 	int ret;
233: 	uint off = f->off;
234: 	uint64 old = addr;
235: 	ilock(ip);
236: 	for (;;) {
237: 		ret = ip->fop->readdir(ip, &dent, off);
238: 		if (ret <= 0 || dent.reclen > n) // 0 is end, -1 is err
239: 			break;
240: 
241: 		if (copyout_nocheck(addr, (char *)&dent, dent.reclen) < 0) {
242: 			ret = -EFAULT;
243: 			break;	
244: 		}
245: 
246: 		off += ret;
247: 		addr += dent.reclen;
248: 		n -= dent.reclen;
249: 
250: 	}
251: 	acquire(&f->lock);
252: 	f->off = off;
253: 	release(&f->lock);
254: 	iunlock(ip);
255: 
256: 	if (ret < 0)
257: 		return -ret;
258: 	return addr - old;
259: }
260:

199: 		return -1;
200: 
201: 	struct dirent dent;

204: 	if (!S_ISDIR(ip->mode))

xv6-k210/kernel/fs/file.c::copyfdtable
匹配分数 1.000 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": true, "exact_token_fp": true, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 1.0}

oskernel2023-zmz@recover 源码

331:  * File descriptors operations.
332:  * 
333:  */
334: 
335: // Fork calls this.
336: int copyfdtable(struct fdtable *fdt1, struct fdtable *fdt2)
337: {
338: 	struct fdtable *fd = fdt2;
339: 
340: 	for (;;) {
341: 		fd->basefd = fdt1->basefd;
342: 		fd->nextfd = fdt1->nextfd;
343: 		fd->used = fdt1->used;
344: 		fd->exec_close = fdt1->exec_close;
345: 		for (int i = 0; i < NOFILE; i++) {
346: 			if (fdt1->arr[i]) {
347: 				fd->arr[i] = filedup(fdt1->arr[i]);
348: 			} else {
349: 				fd->arr[i] = NULL;

383: 
384: struct file *fd2file(int fd, int free)
385: {
386: 	if (fd < 0)
387: 		panic("fd2file");
388: 
389: 	struct proc *p = myproc();
390: 	struct fdtable *head = &p->fds, *prev = NULL;
391: 	struct file *f = NULL;
392: 
393: 	while (head && fd >= head->basefd + NOFILE) {
394: 		prev = head;
395: 		head = head->next;
396: 	}
397: 	if (!head || fd < head->basefd) // not found
398: 		return NULL;
399: 
400: 	fd %= NOFILE;
401: 	f = head->arr[fd];
402: 	if (free) {
403: 		head->arr[fd] = NULL; // the file should be closed by caller
404: 		head->exec_close &= ~(1 << fd);
405: 		if (fd < head->nextfd) {  // make nextfd the smallest
406: 			head->nextfd = fd;
407: 		}
408: 		if (--head->used == 0 && prev) {  // if table is empty and is not the head, free it
409: 			prev->next = head->next;
410: 			kfree(head);
411: 			__debug_info("fd2file", "free %p\n", head);
412: 		}
413: 	}
414: 	return f;
415: }
416:

418: {
419: 	struct fdtable *fdnew = kmalloc(sizeof(struct fdtable));
420: 	if (fdnew == NULL) {

431: 
432: // Allocate a file descriptor for the given file.
433: // Takes over file reference from caller on success.
434: int fdalloc(struct file *f, int flag)
435: {
436: 	int fd = 0;
437: 	struct proc *p = myproc();
438: 	struct fdtable *fdt = &p->fds;
439: 	
440: 	while (fdt->nextfd == NOFILE) { // table full
441: 		if (!fdt->next ||                               // no next table
442: 				fdt->basefd + NOFILE != fdt->next->basefd)  // or next table is not continuous
443: 		{
444: 			struct fdtable *fdnew = newfdtable(fdt->basefd + NOFILE, fdt->next);
445: 			if (fdnew == NULL) {
446: 				return -1;
447: 			}
448: 			__debug_info("fdalloc", "alloc %p\n", fdnew);
449: 			fdt->next = fdnew;
450: 		}
451: 		fdt = fdt->next;
452: 	}
453: 
454: 	fd = fdt->nextfd;
455: 	fdt->arr[fd] = f;
456: 	fdt->used++;
457: 	if (flag)
458: 		fdt->exec_close |= 1 << fd;
459: 
460: 	// locate the next smallest free fd
461: 	// if not found, nextfd will stop at NOFILE, which means this table is full
462: 	while (++fdt->nextfd < NOFILE) {
463: 		if (fdt->arr[fdt->nextfd] == NULL) {
464: 			break;
465: 		}
466: 	}
467: 
468: 	return fd + fdt->basefd;
469: }
470:

472: {
473: 	if (fd < 0)
474: 		panic("fdalloc3");

471: int fdalloc3(struct file *f, int fd, int flag)
472: {
473: 	if (fd < 0)
474: 		panic("fdalloc3");
475: 
476: 	__debug_info("fdalloc3", "in fd=%d flag=%d\n", fd, flag);
477: 	struct proc *p = myproc();
478: 	struct fdtable *fdt = &p->fds, *prev = NULL;
479: 
480: 	while (fdt && fdt->basefd + NOFILE <= fd) {
481: 		prev = fdt;
482: 		fdt = fdt->next;
483: 	}
484: 	if (!fdt || fdt->basefd > fd) {  // no next table, or next table is not continuous
485: 		struct fdtable *fdnew = newfdtable(fd - fd % NOFILE, fdt);
486: 		if (fdnew == NULL) {
487: 			return -1;
488: 		}
489: 		prev->next = fdnew;
490: 		fdt = fdnew;
491: 		__debug_info("fdalloc3", "alloc %p base=%d\n",
492: 									fdnew, fdnew->basefd);
493: 	}
494: 
495: 	int fd2 = fd % NOFILE;
496: 	if (fdt->arr[fd2] != NULL) {
497: 		fdt->exec_close &= ~(1 << fd2);
498: 		fileclose(fdt->arr[fd2]);
499: 		fdt->arr[fd2] = f;
500: 	} else {
501: 		fdt->arr[fd2] = f;
502: 		fdt->used++;
503: 		if (fd2 == fdt->nextfd) { // unfortunately, we need to update nextfd
504: 			while (++fdt->nextfd < NOFILE) {
505: 				if (fdt->arr[fdt->nextfd] == NULL) {
506: 					break;
507: 				}
508: 			}
509: 		}
510: 	}
511: 	__debug_info("fdalloc3", "table %p base=%d, nextfd=%d, fd2=%d, fd=%d\n",
512: 								fdt, fdt->basefd, fdt->nextfd, fd2, fd);
513: 
514: 	if (flag)
515: 		fdt->exec_close |= (1 << fd2);
516: 
517: 	return fd;
518: }

520: void fdcloexec(struct fdtable *fdt)
521: {
522: 	struct fdtable *prev = NULL;
523: 
524: 	while (fdt) {
525: 		if (fdt->exec_close) { // quick peek
526: 			__debug_info("fdcloexec", "cloexec flag: 0x%x\n", fdt->exec_close);
527: 			for (int i = 0; i < NOFILE; i++) {
528: 				if (fdt->exec_close & (1 << i)) {
529: 					fileclose(fdt->arr[i]);
530: 					if (--fdt->used == 0) { // no need to loop
531: 						break;
532: 					}
533: 					fdt->arr[i] = NULL;
534: 					if (i < fdt->nextfd) {
535: 						fdt->nextfd = i;
536: 					}
537: 				}
538: 			}
539: 			fdt->exec_close = 0;
540: 		}
541: 		// we don't free the first table because it's in a proc
542: 		if (fdt->used == 0 && prev) {
543: 			prev->next = fdt->next;
544: 			kfree(fdt);
545: 			fdt = prev->next;
546: 		} else {
547: 			prev = fdt;
548: 			fdt = fdt->next;
549: 		}
550: 	}
551: }
552:

552: 
553: // allocate one greater or equal to fd
554: int fcntldup(struct file *f, int fd, int cloexec)
555: {
556: 	if (fd < 0)
557: 		panic("fcntldup");
558: 
559: 	struct proc *p = myproc();
560: 	struct fdtable *fdt = &p->fds, *prev = NULL;
561: 
562: 	while (fdt && fdt->basefd + NOFILE <= fd) {
563: 		prev = fdt;
564: 		fdt = fdt->next;
565: 	}
566: 
567: 	int base = fd - fd % NOFILE;
568: 	fd %= NOFILE;
569: 	for (;;) {
570: 		if (!fdt || fdt->basefd > base) {  // no next table, or next table is not continuous
571: 			struct fdtable *fdnew = newfdtable(base, fdt);
572: 			if (fdnew == NULL) {
573: 				return -1;
574: 			}
575: 			prev->next = fdnew;
576: 			fdt = fdnew;
577: 		}
578: 
579: 		while (fd < NOFILE && fdt->arr[fd] != NULL) {
580: 			fd++;
581: 		}
582: 
583: 		if (fd == NOFILE) {
584: 			fd = 0;
585: 			base += NOFILE;
586: 			fdt = fdt->next;
587: 		} else {
588: 			fdt->arr[fd] = f;
589: 			fdt->used++;
590: 			if (fd == fdt->nextfd) { // unfortunately, we need to update nextfd
591: 				while (++fdt->nextfd < NOFILE) {
592: 					if (fdt->arr[fdt->nextfd] == NULL) {
593: 						break;
594: 					}
595: 				}
596: 			}
597: 			break;
598: 		}
599: 	}
600: 	// __debug_info("fcntldup", "table %p base=%d, nextfd=%d, fd2=%d, fd=%d\n",
601: 	// 							fdt, fdt->basefd, fdt->nextfd, fd2, fd);
602: 
603: 	if (cloexec)
604: 		fdt->exec_close |= (1 << fd);
605: 
606: 	return base + fd;
607: }

xv6-k210@final / 来源候选源码

526: 		}
527: 	}
528: }

530: // allocate one greater or equal to fd
531: int fcntldup(struct file *f, int fd, int cloexec)
532: {
533: 	// if (fd < 0)
534: 	// 	panic("fcntldup");
535: 
536: 	struct proc *p = myproc();
537: 	struct fdtable *fdt = &p->fds, *prev = NULL;
538: 
539: 	while (fdt && fdt->basefd + NOFILE <= fd) {
540: 		prev = fdt;
541: 		fdt = fdt->next;
542: 	}
543: 
544: 	int base = fd - fd % NOFILE;
545: 	fd %= NOFILE;
546: 	for (;;) {
547: 		if (!fdt || fdt->basefd > base) {  // no next table, or next table is not continuous
548: 			struct fdtable *fdnew = newfdtable(base, fdt);
549: 			if (fdnew == NULL) {
550: 				return -1;
551: 			}
552: 			prev->next = fdnew;
553: 			fdt = fdnew;
554: 		}
555: 
556: 		while (fd < NOFILE && fdt->arr[fd] != NULL) {
557: 			fd++;
558: 		}
559: 
560: 		if (fd == NOFILE) {
561: 			fd = 0;
562: 			base += NOFILE;
563: 			fdt = fdt->next;
564: 		} else {
565: 			fdt->arr[fd] = f;
566: 			fdt->used++;
567: 			if (fd == fdt->nextfd) { // unfortunately, we need to update nextfd
568: 				while (++fdt->nextfd < NOFILE) {
569: 					if (fdt->arr[fdt->nextfd] == NULL) {
570: 						break;
571: 					}
572: 				}
573: 			}
574: 			break;
575: 		}
576: 	}
577: 	// __debug_info("fcntldup", "table %p base=%d, nextfd=%d, fd2=%d, fd=%d\n",
578: 	// 							fdt, fdt->basefd, fdt->nextfd, fd2, fd);
579: 
580: 	if (cloexec)
581: 		fdt->exec_close |= (1 << fd);
582: 
583: 	return base + fd;
584: }
