19: /**
20:  * File system inode
21:  * 
22:  */
23: 
24: struct inode *create(struct inode *dp, char *path, int mode)
25: {
26: 	struct inode *ip;
27: 	char name[MAXNAME + 1];
28: 	struct dentry *de;
29: 
30: 	if (dp != NULL && !S_ISDIR(dp->mode)) {
31: 		__debug_warn("create", "create on file\n");
32: 		return NULL;
33: 	}
34: 
35: 	if ((dp = nameiparentfrom(dp, path, name)) == NULL) {
36: 		__debug_warn("create", "%s doesn't exist\n", path);
37: 		// root doesn't have parent
38: 		if (strncmp(path, "/", MAXPATH) != 0 || !S_ISDIR(mode))
39: 			return NULL;
40: 		dp = namei("/");
41: 		ilock(dp);
42: 		return dp;
43: 	}
44: 
45: 	ilock(dp);
46: 	if (dp->state & I_STATE_FREE) {
47: 		__debug_warn("create", "%s doesn't exist\n", path);
48: 		iunlockput(dp);
49: 		return NULL;
50: 	}
51: 
52: 	struct superblock *sb = dp->sb;
53: 	acquire(&sb->cache_lock);
54: 	de = dp->entry->op->cache(dp->entry, name);
55: 	release(&sb->cache_lock);
56: 	if (de != NULL) {
57: 		iunlockput(dp);
58: 		ip = idup(de->inode);
59: 		if ((mode ^ ip->mode) & S_IFMT) { // unmatch file type
60: 			iput(ip);
61: 			__debug_warn("create", "%s exists in cache but type is wrong\n", path);
62: 			ip = NULL;
63: 		} else {
64: 			ilock(ip);
65: 		}
66: 		return ip;
67: 	}
68: 
69: 	if ((de = kmalloc(sizeof(struct dentry))) == NULL) {
70: 		iunlockput(dp);
71: 		return NULL;
72: 	}
73: 
74: 	if ((ip = dp->op->create(dp, name, mode)) == NULL) {
75: 		__debug_warn("create", "%s create fail\n", path);
76: 		iunlockput(dp);
77: 		kfree(de);
78: 		return NULL;
79: 	}
80: 
81: 	idup(ip);
82: 	ip->state = I_STATE_VALID;
83: 	ip->entry = de;
84: 
85: 	safestrcpy(de->filename, name, MAXNAME + 1);
86: 	de->child = NULL;
87: 	de->mount = NULL;
88: 	de->inode = ip;
89: 	de->op = dp->entry->op;
90: 
91: 	acquire(&sb->cache_lock);
92: 	de->parent = dp->entry;
93: 	de->next = dp->entry->child;
94: 	dp->entry->child = de;
95: 	release(&sb->cache_lock);
96: 
97: 	if ((mode ^ ip->mode) & S_IFMT) {
98: 		iunlockput(dp);
99: 		iput(ip);
100: 		__debug_warn("create", "%s exists but type is wrong\n", path);
101: 		return NULL;
102: 	}
103: 
104: 	iunlockput(dp);
105: 	ilock(ip);
106: 
107: 	return ip;
108: }
109: 
110: 
111: // Caller must hold ip->lock.
112: int unlink(struct inode *ip)
113: {

24: struct inode *create(struct inode *dp, char *path, int mode)
25: {
26: 	struct inode *ip;
27: 	char name[MAXNAME + 1];
28: 	struct dentry *de;
29: 
30: 	if (dp != NULL && !S_ISDIR(dp->mode)) {
31: 		__debug_warn("create", "create on file\n");
32: 		return NULL;
33: 	}
34: 
35: 	if ((dp = nameiparentfrom(dp, path, name)) == NULL) {
36: 		__debug_warn("create", "%s doesn't exist\n", path);
37: 		// root doesn't have parent
38: 		if (strncmp(path, "/", MAXPATH) != 0 || !S_ISDIR(mode))
39: 			return NULL;
40: 		dp = namei("/");

43: 	}
44: 
45: 	ilock(dp);

47: 		__debug_warn("create", "%s doesn't exist\n", path);
48: 		iunlockput(dp);
49: 		return NULL;
50: 	}
51: 
52: 	struct superblock *sb = dp->sb;
53: 	acquire(&sb->cache_lock);

55: 	release(&sb->cache_lock);
56: 	if (de != NULL) {
57: 		iunlockput(dp);
58: 		ip = idup(de->inode);
59: 		if ((mode ^ ip->mode) & S_IFMT) { // unmatch file type
60: 			iput(ip);

63: 		} else {

67: 	}

70: 		iunlockput(dp);

75: 		__debug_warn("create", "%s create fail\n", path);

78: 		return NULL;
79: 	}
80: 
81: 	idup(ip);
82: 	ip->state = I_STATE_VALID;
83: 	ip->entry = de;
84: 
85: 	safestrcpy(de->filename, name, MAXNAME + 1);

102: 	}

104: 	iunlockput(dp);
105: 	ilock(ip);
106: 
107: 	return ip;
108: }

111: // Caller must hold ip->lock.
112: int unlink(struct inode *ip)
113: {
114: 	struct superblock *sb;
115: 	struct dentry *de = ip->entry;
116: 
117: 	__debug_info("unlink", "unlink %s\n", de->filename);
118: 	for (sb = &rootfs; sb != NULL; sb = sb->next) {
119: 		if (sb->dev == ip) {
120: 			__debug_warn("unlink", "%s is busy\n", de->filename);
121: 			return -EBUSY;
122: 		}
123: 	}
124: 
125: 	sb = ip->sb;
126: 	if (de == sb->root) {
127: 		__debug_warn("unlink", "try to unlink root\n");
128: 		return -EACCES;
129: 	}
130: 
131: 	int ret = ip->op->unlink(ip);
132: 	if (ret < 0) {
133: 		__debug_warn("unlink", "fail\n");
134: 		return ret;
135: 	}
136: 
137: 	acquire(&sb->cache_lock);
138: 	// if (ip->nlink == 0) {
139: 		// No other files link to ip
140: 		ip->state |= I_STATE_FREE;
141: 		// Remove the dentry from cache, but do not free it.
142: 		de->op->delete(de);
143: 	// }
144: 	release(&sb->cache_lock);
145: 
146: 	return ret;
147: }

159: 
160: void iput(struct inode *ip)
161: {
162: 	// Lock the cache so that no one can get ip.
163: 	struct superblock *sb = ip->sb;
164: 	acquire(&sb->cache_lock);
165: 	sb->ref--;
166: 	if (ip->ref == 1) {
167: 		// ref == 1 means no other process can have ip locked,
168: 		// so this acquiresleep() won't block (or deadlock).
169: 		acquiresleep(&ip->lock);
170: 		release(&sb->cache_lock);
171: 
172: 		// This file is removed, so its dentry should have been
173: 		// deleted from the dentry tree.
174: 		// This inode is invisible, just free it.
175: 		if (ip->state & I_STATE_FREE) {
176: 			if (ip->nlink == 0)
177: 				ip->op->truncate(ip);
178: 			kfree(ip->entry);
179: 			sb->op.destroy_inode(ip);
180: 			return;
181: 		}
182: 		else if (ip->state & I_STATE_DIRTY) {
183: 			ip->op->update(ip);
184: 		}
185: 		releasesleep(&ip->lock);
186: 		acquire(&sb->cache_lock);
187: 	}
188: 	ip->ref--;
189: 	release(&sb->cache_lock);
190: }
191: 
192: 
193: void ilock(struct inode *ip)
194: {
195: 	if (ip == 0 || ip->ref < 1)

xv6-k210@final / 来源候选源码

155: 	release(&ip->sb->cache_lock);
156: 	return ip;
157: }

170: 		release(&sb->cache_lock);
171: 
172: 		// This file is removed, so its dentry should have been

174: 		// This inode is invisible, just free it.

176: 			if (ip->nlink == 0)

184: 		}
185: 		releasesleep(&ip->lock);
186: 		acquire(&sb->cache_lock);
187: 	}
188: 	ip->ref--;
189: 	release(&sb->cache_lock);
190: }

216: // by their whole path. But when parsing a path, we open all the directories through it, 
217: // which forms a linked list from the final file to the root. Thus, we use the "parent" pointer 
218: // to recognize whether an entry with the "name" as given is really the file we want in the right path.
219: // Should never get root by eget, it's easy to understand.
220: // Caller should hold superblock's cache_lock.
221: struct dentry *de_check_cache(struct dentry *parent, char *name)
222: {
223: 	struct dentry *pde = NULL;
224: 	struct dentry *de = parent->child;
225: 	for (; de != NULL; pde = de, de = de->next) {          // LRU algo
226: 		if (strncmp(de->filename, name, MAXNAME) == 0) {
227: 			if (de != parent->child) {
228: 				pde->next = de->next;
229: 				de->next = parent->child;
230: 				parent->child = de;
231: 			}
232: 			return de_mnt_in(de);
233: 		}
234: 	}
235: 	return NULL;
236: }
237:

241: {

239: // Caller must hold superblock's cache_lock.
240: int de_delete(struct dentry *de)
241: {
242: 	// if (de->child != NULL)
243: 	// 	panic("de_delete: has children");
244: 
245: 	struct dentry **pde;
246: 	for (pde = &de->parent->child; *pde != NULL; pde = &(*pde)->next) {
247: 		if (*pde == de) {
248: 			*pde = de->next;
249: 			return 0;
250: 		}
251: 	}
252: 
253: 	panic("de_delete: not in cache delete");
254: 	return -1;
255: }

257: void de_print(struct superblock *sb, int level);
258: 
259: static void do_de_print(struct dentry *de, int level)
260: {
261: 	struct dentry *child;
262: 	for (child = de->child; child != NULL; child = child->next) {
263: 		for (int i = 0; i < level; i++) {
264: 			printf("\t");
265: 		}
266: 		printf(__INFO("%d")" %s\n", child->inode->ref, child->filename);
267: 		do_de_print(child, level + 1);
268: 		if (child->mount) {
269: 			release(&child->inode->sb->cache_lock);
270: 			de_print(child->mount, level);
271: 			acquire(&child->inode->sb->cache_lock);
272: 		}
273: 	}
274: }

276: void de_print(struct superblock *sb, int level)
277: {
278: 	acquire(&sb->cache_lock);
279: 
280: 	struct dentry *root = sb->root;
281: 	for (int i = 0; i < level; i++) {
282: 		printf("\t");
283: 	}
284: 	if (sb->dev) {
285: 		printf(__INFO("%d/%d")" %s mounted at %s\n",
286: 			sb->ref, root->inode->ref, sb->dev->entry->filename, root->parent->filename);
287: 	} else {
288: 		printf(__INFO("%d/%d")" %s\n", sb->ref, root->inode->ref, root->filename);
289: 	}
290: 	do_de_print(root, level + 1);
291: 
292: 	release(&sb->cache_lock);
293: }

匹配分数 0.534 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 0.914, "exact_ast_shape": false, "exact_token_fp": false, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 0.141}

oskernel2023-zmz@recover 源码

314:  * Caller must hold entry->lock.
315:  * @param   dp          entry of a directory file
316:  * @param   filename    target filename
317:  * @param   poff        offset of proper empty entry slots from the beginning of the dir
318:  */
319: struct inode *dirlookup(struct inode *dir, char *filename, uint *poff)
320: {
321: 	if (!S_ISDIR(dir->mode))
322: 		panic("dirlookup");
323: 
324: 	__debug_info("dirlookup", "start searching %s\n", filename);
325: 	struct superblock *sb = dir->sb;
326: 	struct dentry *de, *parent;
327: 	if (strncmp(filename, ".", MAXNAME) == 0) {
328: 		de = de_mnt_in(dir->entry);
329: 		return idup(de->inode);
330: 	}
331: 	else if (strncmp(filename, "..", MAXNAME) == 0) {
332: 		de = dir->entry;
333: 		while (de == sb->root) { // It indicates that de is a root and may be a mount point.
334: 			de = de->parent;
335: 			if (de == NULL) { // Meet root of rootfs.
336: 				de = sb->root;
337: 				break;
338: 			}
339: 			sb = de->inode->sb;
340: 		}
341: 		de = de_mnt_in(de->parent == NULL ? de : de->parent); // Now we found the real parent.
342: 		return idup(de->inode);
343: 	}
344: 
345: 	__debug_info("dirlookup", "checking cache\n");
346: 	acquire(&sb->cache_lock);
347: 	de = dir->entry->op->cache(dir->entry, filename);
348: 	release(&sb->cache_lock);
349: 	if (de != NULL) {
350: 		__debug_info("dirlookup", "cache hit: %s\n", filename);
351: 		return idup(de->inode);
352: 	}
353: 
354: 	__debug_info("dirlookup", "look up on disk: %s\n", filename);
355: 	if ((de = kmalloc(sizeof(struct dentry))) == NULL) {
356: 		return NULL;
357: 	}
358: 
359: 	struct inode *ip = dir->op->lookup(dir, filename, poff);
360: 	if (ip == NULL) {
361: 		__debug_warn("dirlookup", "file not found: %s\n", filename);
362: 		kfree(de);
363: 		return NULL;
364: 	}
365: 	__debug_info("dirlookup", "found: %s\n", filename);
366: 
367: 	idup(ip);
368: 	ip->entry = de;
369: 	ip->state = I_STATE_VALID;
370: 
371: 	safestrcpy(de->filename, filename, MAXNAME + 1);
372: 	de->child = NULL;
373: 	de->mount = NULL;
374: 	de->inode = ip;
375: 	de->op = dir->entry->op;
376: 
377: 	acquire(&sb->cache_lock);
378: 	parent = dir->entry;
379: 	de->parent = parent;
380: 	de->next = parent->child;
381: 	parent->child = de;
382: 	release(&sb->cache_lock);
383: 
384: 	return ip;
385: }
386:

320: {
321: 	// if (!S_ISDIR(dir->mode))

323: 
324: 	__debug_info("dirlookup", "start searching %s\n", filename);
325: 	struct superblock *sb = dir->sb;
326: 	struct dentry *de, *parent;
327: 	if (strncmp(filename, ".", MAXNAME) == 0) {
328: 		de = de_mnt_in(dir->entry);
329: 		return idup(de->inode);
330: 	}
331: 	else if (strncmp(filename, "..", MAXNAME) == 0) {
332: 		de = dir->entry;
333: 		while (de == sb->root) { // It indicates that de is a root and may be a mount point.
334: 			de = de->parent;
335: 			if (de == NULL) { // Meet root of rootfs.
336: 				de = sb->root;
337: 				break;
338: 			}
339: 			sb = de->inode->sb;
340: 		}
341: 		de = de_mnt_in(de->parent == NULL ? de : de->parent); // Now we found the real parent.
342: 		return idup(de->inode);
343: 	}
344: 
345: 	__debug_info("dirlookup", "checking cache\n");
346: 	acquire(&sb->cache_lock);
347: 	de = dir->entry->op->cache(dir->entry, filename);
348: 	release(&sb->cache_lock);
349: 	if (de != NULL) {
350: 		__debug_info("dirlookup", "cache hit: %s\n", filename);
351: 		return idup(de->inode);
352: 	}
353: 
354: 	__debug_info("dirlookup", "look up on disk: %s\n", filename);
355: 	if ((de = kmalloc(sizeof(struct dentry))) == NULL) {
356: 		return NULL;
357: 	}
358: 
359: 	struct inode *ip = dir->op->lookup(dir, filename, poff);
360: 	if (ip == NULL) {
361: 		__debug_warn("dirlookup", "file not found: %s\n", filename);
362: 		kfree(de);
363: 		return NULL;
364: 	}
365: 	__debug_info("dirlookup", "found: %s\n", filename);
366: 
367: 	idup(ip);
368: 	ip->entry = de;
369: 	ip->state = I_STATE_VALID;
370: 
371: 	safestrcpy(de->filename, filename, MAXNAME + 1);
372: 	de->child = NULL;
373: 	de->mount = NULL;
374: 	de->inode = ip;
375: 	de->op = dir->entry->op;
376: 
377: 	acquire(&sb->cache_lock);
378: 	parent = dir->entry;
379: 	de->parent = parent;
380: 	de->next = parent->child;
381: 	parent->child = de;
382: 	release(&sb->cache_lock);
383: 
384: 	return ip;
385: }

388: static char *skipelem(char *path, char *name, int max)
389: {
390: 	while (*path == '/') {
391: 		path++;
392: 	}
393: 	if (*path == 0) { return NULL; }
394: 	char *s = path;
395: 	while (*path != '/' && *path != 0) {
396: 		path++;
397: 	}
398: 	int len = path - s;
399: 	if (len > max) {
400: 		len = max;
401: 	}
402: 	name[len] = 0;
403: 	memmove(name, s, len);
404: 	while (*path == '/') {
405: 		path++;
406: 	}
407: 	return path;
408: }
409:

410: 
411: // FAT32 version of namex in xv6's original file system.
412: static struct inode *lookup_path(struct inode *ip, char *path, int parent, char *name)
413: {
414: 	struct inode *next;
415: 	if (*path == '/') {
416: 		if (strncmp(path, "/proc/self/exe", 15) == 0) {
417: 			struct proc *p = myproc();
418: 			if (p->elf) return idup(p->elf);
419: 			return NULL;
420: 		}
421: 		ip = de_mnt_in(rootfs.root)->inode;
422: 		ip = idup(ip);
423: 	} else if (*path != '\0') {
424: 		if (ip != NULL)
425: 			ip = idup(ip);
426: 		else {
427: 			ip = idup(myproc()->cwd);
428: 		}
429: 	} else {
430: 		__debug_warn("lookup_path", "path invalid\n");
431: 		return NULL;
432: 	}
433: 
434: 	while ((path = skipelem(path, name, MAXNAME)) != 0) {
435: 		__debug_info("lookup_path", "lookup: %s\n", name);
436: 		ilock(ip);
437: 		if (!S_ISDIR(ip->mode)) {
438: 			iunlockput(ip);
439: 			return NULL;
440: 		}
441: 		if (parent && *path == '\0') {
442: 			iunlock(ip);
443: 			return ip;
444: 		}
445: 		if ((next = dirlookup(ip, name, 0)) == NULL) {
446: 			iunlockput(ip);
447: 			__debug_warn("lookup_path", "dirlookup returns a NULL\n");
448: 			return NULL;
449: 		}
450: 		__debug_info("lookup_path", "found: %s\n", next->entry->filename);
451: 		iunlockput(ip);
452: 		ip = next;
453: 	}
454: 	if (parent) {
455: 		iput(ip);
456: 		return NULL;
457: 	}
458: 	__debug_info("lookup_path", "finally: %s\n", ip->entry->filename);
459: 	return ip;
460: }
461: 
462: struct inode *namei(char *path)
463: {
464: 	char name[MAXNAME + 1];
465: 	return lookup_path(NULL, path, 0, name);

xv6-k210@final / 来源候选源码

407: 	return path;
408: }
409: 
410: 
411: // FAT32 version of namex in xv6's original file system.
412: static struct inode *lookup_path(struct inode *ip, char *path, int parent, char *name)
413: {
414: 	struct inode *next;
415: 	if (*path == '/') {
416: 		if (strncmp(path, "/proc/self/exe", 15) == 0) {
417: 			struct proc *p = myproc();
418: 			if (p->elf) return idup(p->elf);
419: 			return NULL;
420: 		}
421: 		ip = de_mnt_in(rootfs.root)->inode;
422: 		ip = idup(ip);
423: 	} else if (*path != '\0') {
424: 		if (ip != NULL)
425: 			ip = idup(ip);
426: 		else {
427: 			ip = idup(myproc()->cwd);
428: 		}
429: 	} else {
430: 		__debug_warn("lookup_path", "path invalid\n");
431: 		return NULL;
432: 	}
433: 
434: 	while ((path = skipelem(path, name, MAXNAME)) != 0) {
435: 		__debug_info("lookup_path", "lookup: %s\n", name);
436: 		ilock(ip);
437: 		if (!S_ISDIR(ip->mode)) {
438: 			iunlockput(ip);
439: 			return NULL;
440: 		}
441: 		if (parent && *path == '\0') {
442: 			iunlock(ip);
443: 			return ip;
444: 		}
445: 		if ((next = dirlookup(ip, name, 0)) == NULL) {
446: 			iunlockput(ip);
447: 			__debug_warn("lookup_path", "dirlookup returns a NULL\n");
448: 			return NULL;
449: 		}
450: 		__debug_info("lookup_path", "found: %s\n", next->entry->filename);
451: 		iunlockput(ip);
452: 		ip = next;
453: 	}
454: 	if (parent) {
455: 		iput(ip);
456: 		return NULL;
457: 	}
458: 	__debug_info("lookup_path", "finally: %s\n", ip->entry->filename);
459: 	return ip;
460: }
461: 
462: struct inode *namei(char *path)
463: {
464: 	char name[MAXNAME + 1];
465: 	return lookup_path(NULL, path, 0, name);

匹配分数 1.000 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": true, "exact_token_fp": true, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 1.0}

480: {
481: 	return lookup_path(ip, path, 1, name);
482: }
483: 
484: // path is kernel space, and max must be bigger than 2.
485: int namepath(struct inode *ip, char *path, int max)
486: {
487: 	// if (max < 2)
488: 	// 	panic("namepath: what do you want from me by a max less than 2");
489: 
490: 	struct superblock *sb = ip->sb;
491: 	struct dentry *de = ip->entry;
492: 
493: 	int len;
494: 	char *p = path + max - 1;
495: 	*p = '\0';
496: 
497: 	acquire(&sb->cache_lock);
498: 	
499: 	while (de == sb->root) {	// test mount
500: 		release(&sb->cache_lock);
501: 		if (sb == &rootfs) {	// Meet root of rootfs.
502: 			safestrcpy(path, rootfs.root->filename, max);	
503: 			return strlen(rootfs.root->filename) + 1;
504: 		}
505: 		de = de->parent;
506: 		sb = de->inode->sb;
507: 		acquire(&sb->cache_lock);
508: 	}
509: 	
510: 	for (;;) {
511: 		while (de == sb->root) { // It indicates that de is a root and may be a mount point.
512: 			if ((de = de->parent) == NULL) { // Meet root of rootfs.
513: 				break;
514: 			}
515: 			release(&sb->cache_lock);
516: 			sb = de->inode->sb;
517: 			acquire(&sb->cache_lock);
518: 		}
519: 		if (de == NULL)
520: 			break;
521: 		len = strlen(de->filename);
522: 		if ((p -= len) <= path) {
523: 			release(&sb->cache_lock);
524: 			return -1;
525: 		}
526: 		memmove(p, de->filename, len);
527: 		*--p = '/';
528: 		de = de->parent;
529: 	}
530: 	release(&sb->cache_lock);
531: 
532: 	len = max - (p - path);
533: 	memmove(path, p, len);		// memmove will handle overlap
534: 
535: 	return len;
536: }
537: 
538: 
539: /**
540:  * Other file system operations.
541:  *

484: // path is kernel space, and max must be bigger than 2.
485: int namepath(struct inode *ip, char *path, int max)
486: {
487: 	// if (max < 2)
488: 	// 	panic("namepath: what do you want from me by a max less than 2");
489: 
490: 	struct superblock *sb = ip->sb;
491: 	struct dentry *de = ip->entry;
492: 
493: 	int len;
494: 	char *p = path + max - 1;
495: 	*p = '\0';
496: 
497: 	acquire(&sb->cache_lock);
498: 	
499: 	while (de == sb->root) {	// test mount
500: 		release(&sb->cache_lock);
501: 		if (sb == &rootfs) {	// Meet root of rootfs.
502: 			safestrcpy(path, rootfs.root->filename, max);	
503: 			return strlen(rootfs.root->filename) + 1;
504: 		}
505: 		de = de->parent;
506: 		sb = de->inode->sb;
507: 		acquire(&sb->cache_lock);
508: 	}
509: 	
510: 	for (;;) {
511: 		while (de == sb->root) { // It indicates that de is a root and may be a mount point.
512: 			if ((de = de->parent) == NULL) { // Meet root of rootfs.
513: 				break;
514: 			}
515: 			release(&sb->cache_lock);
516: 			sb = de->inode->sb;
517: 			acquire(&sb->cache_lock);
518: 		}
519: 		if (de == NULL)
520: 			break;
521: 		len = strlen(de->filename);
522: 		if ((p -= len) <= path) {
523: 			release(&sb->cache_lock);
524: 			return -1;
525: 		}
526: 		memmove(p, de->filename, len);
527: 		*--p = '/';
528: 		de = de->parent;
529: 	}
530: 	release(&sb->cache_lock);
531: 
532: 	len = max - (p - path);
533: 	memmove(path, p, len);		// memmove will handle overlap
534: 
535: 	return len;
536: }

542:  * 
543:  */
544: 
545: 
546: // Is the directory dp empty except for "." and ".." ?
547: int isdirempty(struct inode *dp)
548: {
549: 	if (dp->entry->child)
550: 		return 0;
551: 
552: 	struct dirent dent;
553: 	int off = 0, ret;
554: 	while (1) {
555: 		ret = dp->fop->readdir(dp, &dent, off);
556: 		if (ret < 0)
557: 			return -1;
558: 		else if (ret == 0)
559: 			return 1;
560: 		else if ((dent.name[0] == '.' && dent.name[1] == '\0') ||     // skip the "." and ".."
561: 				 (dent.name[0] == '.' && dent.name[1] == '.' && dent.name[2] == '\0'))
562: 		{
563: 			off += ret;
564: 		}
565: 		else
566: 			return 0;
567: 	}
568: }
569: 
570: int do_rename(struct inode *iold, struct inode *dstdir, char *newname)
571: {
572: 	if (iold->sb != dstdir->sb)
573: 		return -EXDEV;

569: 
570: int do_rename(struct inode *iold, struct inode *dstdir, char *newname)
571: {
572: 	if (iold->sb != dstdir->sb)
573: 		return -EXDEV;
574: 	if (!dstdir->op->rename)
575: 		return -EACCES;
576: 
577: 	int ret = 0;
578: 	struct inode *inew = NULL;
579: 	// lock dstdir to check newname and avoid creating
580: 	ilock(dstdir);
581: 	if (trysleeplock(&iold->lock) < 0) { // just try, in case of deadlock
582: 		iunlock(dstdir);
583: 		return -EBUSY;
584: 	}
585: 
586: 	// In what universe can we move a directory into its child?
587: 	acquire(&iold->sb->cache_lock);
588: 	for (struct dentry *de = dstdir->entry; de != NULL; de = de->parent)
589: 		if (de == iold->entry) {
590: 			release(&iold->sb->cache_lock);
591: 			ret = -EINVAL;
592: 			goto end;
593: 		}
594: 	release(&iold->sb->cache_lock);
595: 
596: 	if ((inew = dirlookup(dstdir, newname, NULL)) != NULL) {	// new exists
597: 		if (trysleeplock(&inew->lock) < 0) {
598: 			iput(inew);
599: 			inew = NULL;
600: 			ret = -EBUSY;
601: 			goto end;
602: 		}
603: 		if (S_ISDIR(inew->mode)) {			// it's ok to overwrite an empty dir
604: 			if (!S_ISDIR(iold->mode)) {
605: 				ret = -EISDIR;
606: 				goto end;
607: 			} else if (isdirempty(inew) != 1) {
608: 				ret = -EEXIST;
609: 				goto end;
610: 			}
611: 			if (unlink(inew) < 0) {
612: 				ret = -EBUSY;
613: 				goto end;
614: 			}
615: 			iunlockput(inew);
616: 			inew = NULL;
617: 		} else {
618: 			ret = -EEXIST;
619: 			goto end;
620: 		}
621: 	}
622: 
623: 	ret = dstdir->op->rename(iold, dstdir, newname);
624: 	if (ret < 0) {
625: 		goto end;
626: 	}
627: 
628: 	struct dentry *oldent, *dstent;
629: 	oldent = iold->entry;
630: 	dstent = dstdir->entry;
631: 
632: 	acquire(&iold->sb->cache_lock);
633: 	de_delete(oldent);
634: 	oldent->parent = dstent;
635: 	oldent->next = dstent->child;
636: 	dstent->child = oldent;
637: 	release(&iold->sb->cache_lock);
638: 
639: end:
640: 	iunlock(iold);
641: 	iunlock(dstdir);
642: 	if (inew)
643: 		iunlockput(inew);
644: 	return ret;
645: }
646: 
647: void syncfs(void)
648: {
649: 	struct superblock *sb;
650: 	acquire(&rootfs.cache_lock);

566: 			return 0;
567: 	}
568: }

570: int do_rename(struct inode *iold, struct inode *dstdir, char *newname)
571: {
572: 	if (iold->sb != dstdir->sb)
573: 		return -EXDEV;
574: 	if (!dstdir->op->rename)
575: 		return -EACCES;
576: 
577: 	int ret = 0;
578: 	struct inode *inew = NULL;
579: 	// lock dstdir to check newname and avoid creating
580: 	ilock(dstdir);
581: 	if (trysleeplock(&iold->lock) < 0) { // just try, in case of deadlock
582: 		iunlock(dstdir);
583: 		return -EBUSY;
584: 	}
585: 
586: 	// In what universe can we move a directory into its child?
587: 	acquire(&iold->sb->cache_lock);
588: 	for (struct dentry *de = dstdir->entry; de != NULL; de = de->parent)
589: 		if (de == iold->entry) {
590: 			release(&iold->sb->cache_lock);
591: 			ret = -EINVAL;
592: 			goto end;
593: 		}
594: 	release(&iold->sb->cache_lock);
595: 
596: 	if ((inew = dirlookup(dstdir, newname, NULL)) != NULL) {	// new exists
597: 		if (trysleeplock(&inew->lock) < 0) {
598: 			iput(inew);
599: 			inew = NULL;
600: 			ret = -EBUSY;
601: 			goto end;
602: 		}
603: 		if (S_ISDIR(inew->mode)) {			// it's ok to overwrite an empty dir
604: 			if (!S_ISDIR(iold->mode)) {
605: 				ret = -EISDIR;
606: 				goto end;
607: 			} else if (isdirempty(inew) != 1) {
608: 				ret = -EEXIST;
609: 				goto end;
610: 			}
611: 			if (unlink(inew) < 0) {
612: 				ret = -EBUSY;
613: 				goto end;
614: 			}
615: 			iunlockput(inew);
616: 			inew = NULL;
617: 		} else {
618: 			ret = -EEXIST;
619: 			goto end;
620: 		}
621: 	}
622: 
623: 	ret = dstdir->op->rename(iold, dstdir, newname);
624: 	if (ret < 0) {
625: 		goto end;
626: 	}
627: 
628: 	struct dentry *oldent, *dstent;
629: 	oldent = iold->entry;
630: 	dstent = dstdir->entry;
631: 
632: 	acquire(&iold->sb->cache_lock);
633: 	de_delete(oldent);
634: 	oldent->parent = dstent;
635: 	oldent->next = dstent->child;
636: 	dstent->child = oldent;
637: 	release(&iold->sb->cache_lock);
638: 
639: end:
640: 	iunlock(iold);
641: 	iunlock(dstdir);
642: 	if (inew)
643: 		iunlockput(inew);
644: 	return ret;
645: }

647: void syncfs(void)
648: {
649: 	struct superblock *sb;
650: 	acquire(&rootfs.cache_lock);
651: 	for (sb = rootfs.next; sb != NULL; sb = sb->next) {
652: 		if (sb->op.sync) {
653: 			release(&rootfs.cache_lock);
654: 			sb->op.sync(sb);
655: 			acquire(&rootfs.cache_lock);
656: 		}
657: 	}
658: 	release(&rootfs.cache_lock);
659: }

xv6-k210@final / 来源候选源码

642: 	if (inew)
643: 		iunlockput(inew);
644: 	return ret;
645: }
646: 
647: void syncfs(void)
648: {
649: 	struct superblock *sb;
650: 	acquire(&rootfs.cache_lock);
651: 	for (sb = rootfs.next; sb != NULL; sb = sb->next) {
652: 		if (sb->op.sync) {
653: 			release(&rootfs.cache_lock);
654: 			sb->op.sync(sb);
655: 			acquire(&rootfs.cache_lock);
656: 		}
657: 	}
658: 	release(&rootfs.cache_lock);
659: }

xv6-k210/kernel/fs/mount.c::mountinfo_read
匹配分数 1.000 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": true, "exact_token_fp": true, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 1.0}

oskernel2023-zmz@recover 源码

11: #include "sprintf.h"
12: #include "utils/debug.h"
13: 
14: int either_copyout(int user_dst, uint64 dst, void *src, uint64 len);
15: 
16: static int mountinfo_read(struct inode *ip, int usr, uint64 dst, uint off, uint n)
17: {
18: 	uint tot = 0;
19: 	char *buf = allocpage();
20: 	if (buf == NULL)
21: 		return -ENOMEM;
22: 
23: 	char *pdev = buf + PGSIZE - MAXPATH;
24: 	char *pmnt = pdev - MAXPATH;
25: 
26: 	struct superblock *sb = rootfs.next;
27: 	for (; sb; sb = sb->next) {
28: 		if (sb->dev) {
29: 			namepath(sb->dev, pdev, MAXPATH);
30: 		} else {
31: 			safestrcpy(pdev, sb->type, sizeof(sb->type));
32: 		}
33: 		namepath(sb->root->inode, pmnt, MAXPATH);
34: 		int len = sprintf(buf + tot, PGSIZE - 2 * MAXPATH - tot,
35: 						"%s %s %s\n", pdev, pmnt, sb->type); 
36: 		if (len < 0)
37: 			break;
38: 		tot += len;
39: 		// p[len - 1] = ' ';
40: 		// p += len;
41: 		// tot += len;
42: 		// if (tot >= bufsz) {
43: 		// 	tot = bufsz;
44: 		// 	break;
45: 		// }
46: 		// len = namepath(sb->root->inode, p, bufsz - tot);
47: 		// p[len - 1] = ' ';
48: 		// p += len;
49: 		// tot += len;
50: 		// if (tot >= bufsz) {
51: 		// 	tot = bufsz;
52: 		// 	break;
53: 		// }
54: 		// len = strlen(sb->type);
55: 		// if (tot + len + 1 >= bufsz) {
56: 		// 	break;
57: 		// }
58: 		// safestrcpy(p, sb->type, sizeof(sb->type));
59: 		// p[len++] = '\n';
60: 		// p += len;
61: 		// tot += len;
62: 	}
63: 
64: 	uint ret = 0;
65: 	if (off < tot) {
66: 		ret = tot - off < n ? tot - off : n;
67: 		if (either_copyout(usr, dst, buf + off, ret) < 0)
68: 			ret = -EFAULT;
69: 	}
70: 
71: 	freepage(buf);
72: 	return ret;
73: }
74: 
75: 
76: extern int dummy_file_rw(struct inode *ip, int usr, uint64 dst, uint off, uint n);
77: extern int root_file_readdir(struct inode *ip, struct dirent *dent, uint off);
78: extern int dummy_file_rw_vec(struct inode *ip, struct iovec *iovecs, int count, uint off);

匹配分数 0.614 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 0.922, "exact_ast_shape": false, "exact_token_fp": false, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 0.406}

oskernel2023-zmz@recover 源码

88: extern struct superblock *fs_install(struct inode *dev);
89: extern void fs_uninstall(struct superblock *sb);
90: 
91: // Ignore flag and data in our implement.
92: // Caller must hold all inodes' locks.
93: int do_mount(struct inode *dev, struct inode *mntpoint, char *type, int flag, void *data)
94: {
95: 	if (strncmp("vfat", type, 5) != 0 &&
96: 		strncmp("fat32", type, 6) != 0)
97: 	{
98: 		__debug_warn("do_mount", "Unsupported fs type: %s\n", type);
99: 		return -1;
100: 	}
101: 	// if (mntpoint->entry == rootfs.root) {
102: 	// 	__debug_warn("do_mount", "can not mount at \"/\"\n");
103: 	// 	return -1;
104: 	// }
105: 
106: 	__debug_info("do_mount", "dev:%s mntpnt:%s\n", dev->entry->filename, mntpoint->entry->filename);
107: 
108: 	if (S_ISDIR(dev->mode) || !S_ISDIR(mntpoint->mode)) {
109: 		__debug_warn("do_mount", "Error file type: dev:%x mntpoint:%x\n",
110: 			dev->mode, mntpoint->mode);
111: 		return -ENOTBLK;
112: 	}
113: 
114: 	struct dentry *dmnt = mntpoint->entry;
115: 	// We planed only to support one mount at a time.
116: 	// But in fact, if mntpoint is mounted, namei will get its mounting dev,
117: 	// so this could be a root of a fs, at which can be mounted.
118: 	if (dmnt->mount != NULL) {
119: 		__debug_error("do_mount", "%s is already be mounted\n", dmnt->filename);
120: 		panic("do_mount");
121: 	}
122: 
123: 	struct superblock *sb;
124: 	sb = fs_install(dev);
125: 	if (sb == NULL)
126: 		return -1;
127: 
128: 	acquire(&rootfs.cache_lock); // borrow this lock
129: 
130: 	struct superblock *psb = &rootfs;
131: 	while (psb->next != NULL)
132: 		psb = psb->next;
133: 	psb->next = sb;
134: 	sb->root->parent = dmnt;
135: 	safestrcpy(sb->root->filename, dmnt->filename, sizeof(dmnt->filename));
136: 	safestrcpy(sb->type, type, sizeof(sb->type));
137: 	dmnt->mount = sb;
138: 
139: 	release(&rootfs.cache_lock);
140: 
141: 	idup(mntpoint);
142: 
143: 	return 0;
144: }
145: 
146: // Caller must hold mntpoint->lock.
147: // flag is of no use in out implement.
148: int do_umount(struct inode *mntpoint, int flag)
149: {

候选来源与匹配信号

xv6-k210/kernel/fs/mount.c::do_umount
匹配分数 1.000 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": true, "exact_token_fp": true, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 1.0}

oskernel2023-zmz@recover 源码

143: 	return 0;
144: }
145: 
146: // Caller must hold mntpoint->lock.
147: // flag is of no use in out implement.
148: int do_umount(struct inode *mntpoint, int flag)
149: {
150: 	if (!S_ISDIR(mntpoint->mode)) {
151: 		__debug_warn("do_umount", "try to umount file: %s\n", mntpoint->entry->filename);
152: 		return -ENOTDIR;
153: 	}
154: 
155: 	struct superblock *sb = mntpoint->sb;
156: 	if (mntpoint->entry != sb->root || sb == &rootfs || sb == &devfs || sb == &procfs) {
157: 		__debug_warn("do_umount", "%s is not a mount point\n", mntpoint->entry->filename);
158: 		return -1;
159: 	}
160: 
161: 	struct dentry *de = mntpoint->entry;
162: 	struct superblock *sb_prnt = NULL;
163: 	do { // Look up parent of the mount point.
164: 		de = de->parent;
165: 		if (de == NULL) { // Meet root of rootfs.
166: 			break;
167: 		}
168: 		sb_prnt = de->inode->sb;
169: 	} while (de == sb_prnt->root);
170: 
171: 	// Lock parent to block cache check.
172: 	acquire(&sb_prnt->cache_lock);
173: 	// Check whether only we hold this ref.
174: 	if (sb->ref > 1) {
175: 		__debug_warn("do_umount", "mount point is busy\n");
176: 		release(&sb_prnt->cache_lock);
177: 		return -EBUSY;
178: 	}
179: 	// Unmount from dentry tree.
180: 	de = mntpoint->entry;
181: 	de->parent->mount = NULL;
182: 	release(&sb_prnt->cache_lock);
183: 
184: 	// Put the inode it mounts at.
185: 	iput(de->parent->inode);
186: 
187: 	// Remove from superblock list.
188: 	acquire(&rootfs.cache_lock);
189: 	sb_prnt = &rootfs;
190: 	while (sb_prnt->next != sb)
191: 		sb_prnt = sb_prnt->next;
192: 	sb_prnt->next = sb->next;
193: 	release(&rootfs.cache_lock);
194: 
195: 	fs_uninstall(sb);
196: 
197: 	return 0;
198: }
199: 
200: int mountsysfs(struct superblock *sb, struct inode *mntpoint, char *type)
201: {
202: 	if (!S_ISDIR(mntpoint->mode))
203: 		return -1;

200: int mountsysfs(struct superblock *sb, struct inode *mntpoint, char *type)
201: {
202: 	if (!S_ISDIR(mntpoint->mode))
203: 		return -1;
204: 
205: 	acquire(&rootfs.cache_lock); // borrow this lock
206:

216: 	dmnt->mount = sb;

匹配分数 0.629 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 0.602, "exact_ast_shape": false, "exact_token_fp": false, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 0.562}

oskernel2023-zmz@recover 源码

33: // 	return pwait;
34: // }
35: 
36: static uint32 pipepoll(struct file *, struct poll_table *);
37: 
38: int
39: pipealloc(struct file **pf0, struct file **pf1)
40: {
41: 	struct pipe *pi = NULL;
42: 	struct file *f0, *f1 = NULL;
43: 
44: 	if ((f0 = filealloc()) == NULL ||
45: 		(f1 = filealloc()) == NULL ||
46: 		(pi = kmalloc(sizeof(struct pipe))) == NULL)
47: 	{
48: 		goto bad;
49: 	}
50: 
51: 	pi->readopen = 1;
52: 	pi->writeopen = 1;
53: 	pi->nwrite = 0;
54: 	pi->nread = 0;
55: 
56: 	initlock(&pi->lock, "pipe");
57: 	wait_queue_init(&pi->wqueue, "pipewritequeue");
58: 	wait_queue_init(&pi->rqueue, "pipereadqueue");
59: 
60: 	f0->type = FD_PIPE;
61: 	f0->readable = 1;
62: 	f0->writable = 0;
63: 	f0->pipe = pi;
64: 	f0->poll = pipepoll;
65: 
66: 	f1->type = FD_PIPE;
67: 	f1->readable = 0;
68: 	f1->writable = 1;
69: 	f1->pipe = pi;
70: 	f1->poll = pipepoll;
71: 
72: 	*pf0 = f0;
73: 	*pf1 = f1;
74: 	return 0;
75: 
76:  bad:
77: 	if (pi)
78: 		kfree(pi);
79: 	if (f0)
80: 		fileclose(f0);
81: 	if (f1)
82: 		fileclose(f1);
83: 	*pf0 = *pf1 = NULL;
84: 	return -ENOMEM;
85: }

38: 
39: int
40: pipealloc(struct file **pf0, struct file **pf1)
41: {
42: 	struct pipe *pi = NULL;
43: 	struct file *f0, *f1 = NULL;
44: 
45: 	if ((f0 = filealloc()) == NULL ||
46: 		(f1 = filealloc()) == NULL ||
47: 		//  (pi = kmalloc(sizeof(struct pipe))) == NULL)
48: 		(pi = allocpage()) == NULL)
49: 	{
50: 		goto bad;
51: 	}
52: 
53: 	pi->readopen = 1;
54: 	pi->writeopen = 1;
55: 	pi->nwrite = 0;
56: 	pi->nread = 0;
57: 
58: 	initlock(&pi->lock, "pipe");
59: 	wait_queue_init(&pi->wqueue, "pipewritequeue");
60: 	wait_queue_init(&pi->rqueue, "pipereadqueue");
61: 
62: 	f0->type = FD_PIPE;
63: 	f0->readable = 1;
64: 	f0->writable = 0;
65: 	f0->pipe = pi;
66: 	f0->poll = pipepoll;
67: 
68: 	f1->type = FD_PIPE;
69: 	f1->readable = 0;
70: 	f1->writable = 1;
71: 	f1->pipe = pi;
72: 	f1->poll = pipepoll;
73: 
74: 	*pf0 = f0;
75: 	*pf1 = f1;
76: 	return 0;
77: 
78:  bad:
79: 	if (pi)
80: 		// kfree(pi);
81: 	{
82: 		freepage(pi);
83: 	}
84: 	if (f0)
85: 		fileclose(f0);
86: 	if (f1)
87: 		fileclose(f1);
88: 	*pf0 = *pf1 = NULL;
89: 	return -ENOMEM;
90: }

105: static void pipeunlock(struct pipe *pi, struct wait_node *wait, int who)
106: {
107: 	struct wait_queue *q;
108: 	q = (who == PIPE_READER) ? &pi->rqueue : &pi->wqueue;
109: 
110: 	acquire(&q->lock);
111: 	if (wait_queue_empty(q))
112: 		panic("pipeunlock: empty queue");
113: 
114: 	if (wait != wait_queue_next(q))
115: 		panic("pipeunlock: not next");
116: 	
117: 	wait_queue_del(wait);			// walk out the queue
118: 	if (!wait_queue_empty(q)) {		// wake up the next one
119: 		wait = wait_queue_next(q);
120: 		wakeup(wait->chan);
121: 	}
122: 	release(&q->lock);
123: }

126: {
127: 	struct wait_queue *queue;

138: void
139: pipeclose(struct pipe *pi, int writable)
140: {
141: 	acquire(&pi->lock);
142: 	if (writable) {
143: 		pi->writeopen = 0;
144: 		pipewakeup(pi, PIPE_READER);
145: 	} else {
146: 		pi->readopen = 0;
147: 		pipewakeup(pi, PIPE_WRITER);
148: 	}
149: 	if (pi->readopen == 0 && pi->writeopen == 0) {
150: 		release(&pi->lock);
151: 		kfree(pi);
152: 	} else
153: 		release(&pi->lock);
154: }

155: 
156: static int pipewritable(struct pipe *pi)
157: {
158: 	struct proc *pr = myproc();
159: 	struct wait_node *wait;
160: 	int m;
161: 	
162: 	/**
163: 	 * We are the first one in the queue, and that's why
164: 	 * we can be in this function, so we are safe to read
165: 	 * our wait_node without locking.
166: 	 */
167: 	wait = wait_queue_next(&pi->wqueue);
168: 	
169: 	/**
170: 	 * It is not atomic visit that we hold this lock for.
171: 	 * The writer and reader will not change the opposite
172: 	 * fields, we don't worry about it.
173: 	 * First, this lock helps us get through the sleep().
174: 	 * Second, if it is the moment when we are on our way
175: 	 * to sleep() that the other end is trying to wake us
176: 	 * up, then we might miss this wakeup().
177: 	 * In a word, when waking up the other end, hold pi->lock.
178: 	 */
179: 	acquire(&pi->lock);
180: 	while ((m = pi->nwrite - pi->nread) == PIPESIZE) {		// pipe is full
181: 		if (pi->readopen == 0 || pr->killed) {
182: 			m = -1;
183: 			break;
184: 		}
185: 		pipewakeup(pi, PIPE_READER);
186: 		sleep(wait->chan, &pi->lock);
187: 	}
188: 	release(&pi->lock);
189: 	return m;
190: }
191: 
192: static int pipereadable(struct pipe *pi)
193: {
194: 	struct proc *pr = myproc();
195: 	struct wait_node *wait;

159: 	} else
160: 		release(&pi->lock);
161: }

173: 	 */
174: 	wait = wait_queue_next(&pi->wqueue);

176: 	/**
177: 	 * It is not atomic visit that we hold this lock for.
178: 	 * The writer and reader will not change the opposite
179: 	 * fields, we don't worry about it.
180: 	 * First, this lock helps us get through the sleep().
181: 	 * Second, if it is the moment when we are on our way
182: 	 * to sleep() that the other end is trying to wake us
183: 	 * up, then we might miss this wakeup().
184: 	 * In a word, when waking up the other end, hold pi->lock.
185: 	 */
186: 	acquire(&pi->lock);

189: 			m = -1;
190: 			break;
191: 		}
192: 		pipewakeup(pi, PIPE_READER);
193: 		sleep(wait->chan, &pi->lock);
194: 	}
195: 	release(&pi->lock);
196: 	return m;
197: }

192: static int pipereadable(struct pipe *pi)
193: {
194: 	struct proc *pr = myproc();
195: 	struct wait_node *wait;
196: 	int m;
197: 
198: 	// Need no lock here. See pipewritable().
199: 	wait = wait_queue_next(&pi->rqueue);

202: 	while ((m = pi->nwrite - pi->nread) == 0 && pi->writeopen) {	// pipe is empty
203: 		if (pr->killed) {
204: 			m = -1;
205: 			break;
206: 		}
207: 		sleep(wait->chan, &pi->lock);
208: 	}
209: 	release(&pi->lock);

211: }

212: 
213: int
214: pipewrite(struct pipe *pi, uint64 addr, int n)
215: {
216: 	int i, m;
217: 	char *const pipebound = pi->data + PIPESIZE;
218: 	struct wait_node wait;
219: 
220: 	// pwait = waitinit(&wait);
221: 	wait.chan = &wait;
222: 	pipelock(pi, &wait, PIPE_WRITER);		// block other writers
223: 	for (i = 0; i < n;) {
224: 		if ((m = pipewritable(pi)) < 0) {
225: 			i = -EPIPE;
226: 			goto out;
227: 		}
228: 		m = (PIPESIZE - m < n - i) ?	// amount of bytes to write
229: 			PIPESIZE - m : n - i;
230: 
231: 		while (m > 0) {					// pipe is a loop in a buf
232: 			char *paddr = pi->data + pi->nwrite % PIPESIZE;
233: 			int count = (pipebound - paddr < m) ? pipebound - paddr : m;
234: 
235: 			if (copyin_nocheck(paddr, addr + i, count) < 0)
236: 				break;
237: 			i += count;
238: 			pi->nwrite += count;
239: 			m -= count;
240: 		}
241: 		if (m > 0)
242: 			break;
243: 	}
244: 	acquire(&pi->lock);		// see pipewritable()
245: 	pipewakeup(pi, PIPE_READER);
246: 	release(&pi->lock);
247: out:
248: 	pipeunlock(pi, &wait, PIPE_WRITER);
249: 	// __debug_info("pipewrite", "written %d/%d\n", i, n);
250: 	return i;
251: }
252:

255: {
256: 	int i = -1, m;

241:

247: 		}

253: int
254: piperead(struct pipe *pi, uint64 addr, int n)
255: {
256: 	int i = -1, m;
257: 	char *const pipebound = pi->data + PIPESIZE;
258: 	struct wait_node wait;
259: 
260: 	// pwait = waitinit(&wait);
261: 	wait.chan = &wait;
262: 	pipelock(pi, &wait, PIPE_READER);	// block other readers
263: 
264: 	if ((m = pipereadable(pi)) < 0) {
265: 		goto out;
266: 	}
267: 	if (m > n)
268: 		m = n;
269: 	for (i = 0; i < m;) {
270: 		char *paddr = pi->data + pi->nread % PIPESIZE;
271: 		int count = (pipebound - paddr < m - i) ? pipebound - paddr : m - i;
272: 
273: 		if (copyout_nocheck(addr + i, paddr, count) < 0)
274: 			break;
275: 		pi->nread += count;
276: 		i += count;
277: 	}
278: 	acquire(&pi->lock);		// see pipewritable()
279: 	pipewakeup(pi, PIPE_WRITER);
280: 	release(&pi->lock);
281: out:
282: 	pipeunlock(pi, &wait, PIPE_READER);
283: 	// __debug_info("piperead", "read %d\n", i);
284: 	return i;
285: }

260: int
261: piperead(struct pipe *pi, uint64 addr, int n)
262: {
263: 	int i = -1, m;
264: 	char *const pipebound = pi->data + PIPESIZE;
265: 	struct wait_node wait;
266: 
267: 	// pwait = waitinit(&wait);
268: 	wait.chan = &wait;
269: 	pipelock(pi, &wait, PIPE_READER);	// block other readers
270: 
271: 	if ((m = pipereadable(pi)) < 0) {
272: 		goto out;
273: 	}
274: 	if (m > n)
275: 		m = n;
276: 	for (i = 0; i < m;) {
277: 		char *paddr = pi->data + pi->nread % PIPESIZE;
278: 		int count = (pipebound - paddr < m - i) ? pipebound - paddr : m - i;
279: 
280: 		if (copyout_nocheck(addr + i, paddr, count) < 0)
281: 			break;
282: 		pi->nread += count;
283: 		i += count;
284: 	}
285: 	acquire(&pi->lock);		// see pipewritable()
286: 	pipewakeup(pi, PIPE_WRITER);
287: 	release(&pi->lock);
288: out:
289: 	pipeunlock(pi, &wait, PIPE_READER);
290: 	// __debug_info("piperead", "read %d\n", i);
291: 	return i;
292: }
293:

296: 	int ret = 0;

287: int pipewritev(struct pipe *pi, struct iovec ioarr[], int count)
288: {
289: 	int ret = 0;
290: 	char *const pipebound = pi->data + PIPESIZE;
291: 	struct wait_node wait;
292: 
293: 	// pwait = waitinit(&wait);
294: 	wait.chan = &wait;
295: 	pipelock(pi, &wait, PIPE_WRITER);	// block other writers
296: 
297: 	for (int i = 0; i < count; i++) {
298: 		uint64 addr = (uint64)ioarr[i].iov_base;
299: 		uint64 n = ioarr[i].iov_len;
300: 		int j;
301: 		
302: 		if (!rangeinseg(addr, addr + n))
303: 			break;
304: 		for (j = 0; j < n;) {
305: 			int m = pipewritable(pi);
306: 			if (m < 0) {
307: 				ret = -EPIPE;
308: 				goto out2;
309: 			}
310: 			m = (PIPESIZE - m < n - j) ? PIPESIZE - m : n - j;		// amount of bytes to write
311: 			while (m > 0) {							// pipe is a loop in a buf
312: 				char *paddr = pi->data + pi->nwrite % PIPESIZE;
313: 				int cnt = (pipebound - paddr < m) ? pipebound - paddr : m;
314: 
315: 				if (copyin_nocheck(paddr, addr + j, cnt) < 0)
316: 					goto out1;
317: 				m -= cnt;
318: 				j += cnt;
319: 				pi->nwrite += cnt;
320: 				ret += cnt;
321: 			}
322: 		}
323: 	}
324: out1:
325: 	acquire(&pi->lock);		// see pipewritable()
326: 	pipewakeup(pi, PIPE_READER);
327: 	release(&pi->lock);
328: out2:
329: 	pipeunlock(pi, &wait, PIPE_WRITER);
330: 	return ret;
331: }

342:

346: 	}

348: 		uint64 addr = (uint64)ioarr[i].iov_base;

376: 
377: static
378: uint32 pipepoll(struct file *fp, struct poll_table *pt)
379: {
380: 	uint32 mask = 0;
381: 	struct pipe *pi = fp->pipe;
382: 
383: 	__debug_info("pipepoll", "r/w=%d/%d | ro/wo=%d/%d | rq/wq=%d/%d rn/wn=%d/%d\n",
384: 				fp->readable, fp->writable, pi->readopen, pi->writeopen,
385: 				!wait_queue_empty(&pi->rqueue), !wait_queue_empty(&pi->wqueue),
386: 				pi->nread, pi->nwrite);
387: 
388: 	if (fp->readable)
389: 		poll_wait(fp, &pi->rqueue, pt);
390: 	if (fp->writable)
391: 		poll_wait(fp, &pi->wqueue, pt);
392: 
393: 	if (fp->readable) {
394: 		if (pi->nwrite - pi->nread > 0)			// has something to read
395: 			mask |= POLLIN;
396: 		if (!pi->writeopen) {
397: 			// if (pi->nwrite - pi->nread == 0)
398: 			// 	mask |= POLLPRI;
399: 			// else
400: 				mask |= POLLHUP;
401: 		}
402: 	}
403: 
404: 	if (fp->writable) {
405: 		if (pi->nwrite - pi->nread < PIPESIZE)	// has room to write
406: 			mask |= POLLOUT;
407: 		if (!pi->readopen)
408: 			mask |= POLLERR;
409: 	}
410: 
411: 	return mask;
412: }

381: }

384: static
385: uint32 pipepoll(struct file *fp, struct poll_table *pt)
386: {
387: 	uint32 mask = 0;
388: 	struct pipe *pi = fp->pipe;
389: 
390: 	__debug_info("pipepoll", "r/w=%d/%d | ro/wo=%d/%d | rq/wq=%d/%d rn/wn=%d/%d\n",
391: 				fp->readable, fp->writable, pi->readopen, pi->writeopen,
392: 				!wait_queue_empty(&pi->rqueue), !wait_queue_empty(&pi->wqueue),
393: 				pi->nread, pi->nwrite);
394: 
395: 	if (fp->readable)
396: 		poll_wait(fp, &pi->rqueue, pt);
397: 	if (fp->writable)
398: 		poll_wait(fp, &pi->wqueue, pt);
399: 
400: 	if (fp->readable) {
401: 		if (pi->nwrite - pi->nread > 0)			// has something to read
402: 			mask |= POLLIN;
403: 		if (!pi->writeopen) {
404: 			// if (pi->nwrite - pi->nread == 0)
405: 			// 	mask |= POLLPRI;
406: 			// else
407: 				mask |= POLLHUP;
408: 		}
409: 	}
410: 
411: 	if (fp->writable) {
412: 		if (pi->nwrite - pi->nread < PIPESIZE)	// has room to write
413: 			mask |= POLLOUT;
414: 		if (!pi->readopen)
415: 			mask |= POLLERR;
416: 	}
417: 
418: 	return mask;
419: }

18: 
19: static void __poll_func(struct file *f, struct wait_queue *wq, struct poll_table *pt)
20: {
21: 	struct poll_wait_queue *pwq = container_of(pt, struct poll_wait_queue, pt);
22: 	struct poll_wait_node *pwn;
23: 	if (pwq->index >= ON_STACK_PWN_NUM)	// we can allocate pages
24: 		return;
25: 
26: 	pwn = &pwq->nodes[pwq->index++];
27: 	pwn->node.chan = pwq;
28: 	pwn->queue = wq;
29: 	wait_queue_add_locked(wq, &pwn->node);
30: }
31: 
32:

34: {
35: 	pwq->pt.func = __poll_func;

42: static void poll_end(struct poll_wait_queue *pwq)
43: {
44: 	for (int i = 0; i < pwq->index; i++) {
45: 		struct poll_wait_node *pwn = pwq->nodes + i;
46: 		struct wait_queue *wq = pwn->queue;
47: 		acquire(&wq->lock);
48: 		wait_queue_del(&pwn->node);
49: 		if (!wait_queue_empty(wq)) {
50: 			struct wait_node *node = wait_queue_next(wq);
51: 			wakeup(node->chan);
52: 		}
53: 		release(&wq->lock);
54: 	}
55: }

55: }
56: 
57: 
58: static uint32 file_poll(struct file *fp, struct poll_table *pt)
59: {
60: 	if (!fp->poll)
61: 		return POLLIN|POLLOUT;
62: 	return fp->poll(fp, pt);
63: }

65: 
66: static int poll_sched_timeout(struct poll_wait_queue *pwq, uint64 expire)
67: {
68: 	struct proc *p = myproc();
69: 	struct proc *volatile vp = p;
70: 	int ret = 0;
71: 
72: 	acquire(&p->lk);	// Hold this for sleep().
73: 	
74: 	p->sleep_expire = expire;
75: 	sleep(pwq, &p->lk);
76: 	
77: 	// Check whether we are waken up by timeout.
78: 	// If so, this field will be set to zero.
79: 	if (vp->sleep_expire == 0)
80: 		ret = 1;
81: 	else
82: 		p->sleep_expire = 0;
83: 	release(&p->lk);
84: 
85: 	__debug_info("poll_sched_timeout", "pwq=%p, ret=%d\n", pwq, ret);
86: 
87: 	return ret;
88: }
89: 
90: 
91: int ppoll(struct pollfd *pfds, int nfds, struct timespec *timeout, __sigset_t *sigmask)
92: {
93: 	// uint64 now = readtime();

106: // Copy fdset from arguments, since we need to write results into them later.
107: static inline void fdset_work_init(struct fdset *rfds, struct fdset *wfds, struct fdset *exfds,
108: 							struct fdset *rarg, struct fdset *warg, struct fdset *exarg)
109: {
110: 	if (rarg)
111: 		memmove(rfds, rarg, sizeof(struct fdset));
112: 	else
113: 		memset(rfds, 0, sizeof(struct fdset));
114: 
115: 	if (warg)
116: 		memmove(wfds, warg, sizeof(struct fdset));
117: 	else
118: 		memset(wfds, 0, sizeof(struct fdset));
119: 
120: 	if (exarg)
121: 		memmove(exfds, exarg, sizeof(struct fdset));
122: 	else
123: 		memset(exfds, 0, sizeof(struct fdset));
124: }
125:

106: // Copy fdset from arguments, since we need to write results into them later.
107: static inline void fdset_work_init(struct fdset *rfds, struct fdset *wfds, struct fdset *exfds,

113: 		memset(rfds, 0, sizeof(struct fdset));

匹配分数 1.000 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": true, "exact_token_fp": true, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 1.0}

125: 
126: 
127: int pselect(int nfds, struct fdset *readfds, struct fdset *writefds, struct fdset *exceptfds,
128: 			struct timespec *timeout, __sigset_t *sigmask)
129: {
130: 	struct fdset rfds, wfds, exfds;
131: 	uint64 expire;
132: 	struct poll_wait_queue wait;
133: 	// struct poll_wait_queue *pwait;
134: 	int immediate = 0;
135: 	
136: 	
137: 	__debug_info("pselect", "timeout={%ds, %dns}\n", 
138: 				timeout ? timeout->sec : -1, timeout ? timeout->nsec : -1);
139: 
140: 	if (timeout) {
141: 		expire = convert_from_timespec(timeout);
142: 		if (expire == 0)
143: 			immediate = 1;			// don't sleep
144: 		else 
145: 			expire += readtime();	// overflow?
146: 	} else
147: 		expire = 0;					// infinity
148: 
149: 	fdset_work_init(&rfds, &wfds, &exfds, readfds, writefds, exceptfds);
150: 
151: 	// Since the node is on stack, if we want other process to get us, we should use pa.
152: 	// pwait = (struct poll_wait_queue *)kwalkaddr(myproc()->pagetable, (uint64)&wait);
153: 	// pwait = &wait;
154: 	poll_init(&wait);
155: 	if (immediate)
156: 		wait.pt.func = NULL;		// we won't be inserted into any queue in later poll()s
157: 
158: 	int ret = 0;
159: 	for (;;)
160: 	{
161: 		int i = 0;	// fd iterator
162: 		for (int idx = 0; idx <= (nfds - 1) / NFDBIT; idx++)
163: 		{
164: 			uint64 r, w, ex, all;
165: 			uint64 rres = 0, wres = 0, exres = 0;
166: 
167: 			r = rfds.fd_bits[idx];		// extract fd bits
168: 			w = wfds.fd_bits[idx];
169: 			ex = exfds.fd_bits[idx];
170: 			all = r | w | ex;
171: 			if (all == 0) {				// no fd in this part
172: 				i += NFDBIT;
173: 				continue;
174: 			}
175: 
176: 			for (int b = 0; b < NFDBIT; b++, i++) {
177: 				if (i >= nfds)			// reach end
178: 					break;
179: 				uint64 bit = 1L << b;
180: 				if (!(bit & all))		// no fd in this bit
181: 					continue;
182: 
183: 				struct file *fp = fd2file(i, 0);
184: 				if (!fp)				// bad fd
185: 					continue;
186: 				
187: 				__debug_info("pselect", "fd=%d\n", i);
188: 				
189: 				wait.pt.key = POLLEX_SET;
190: 				if (r & bit)
191: 					wait.pt.key |= POLLIN_SET;
192: 				if (w & bit)
193: 					wait.pt.key |= POLLOUT_SET;
194: 
195: 				uint32 mask = file_poll(fp, &wait.pt);
196: 				__debug_info("pselect", "mask=%d\n", mask);
197: 
198: 				if ((mask & POLLIN_SET) && (r & bit)) {
199: 					rres |= bit;
200: 					ret++;
201: 					wait.pt.func = NULL;
202: 				}
203: 				if ((mask & POLLOUT_SET) && (w & bit)) {
204: 					wres |= bit;
205: 					ret++;
206: 					wait.pt.func = NULL;
207: 				}
208: 				if ((mask & POLLEX_SET) && (ex & bit)) {
209: 					exres |= bit;
210: 					ret++;
211: 					wait.pt.func = NULL;
212: 				}
213: 			}
214: 			if (readfds)
215: 				readfds->fd_bits[idx] = rres;
216: 			if (writefds)
217: 				writefds->fd_bits[idx] = wres;
218: 			if (exceptfds)
219: 				exceptfds->fd_bits[idx] = exres;
220: 		}
221: 		wait.pt.func = NULL;		// only need to be called once for each file
222: 
223: 		if (ret > 0 || immediate)	// got results or don't sleep-wait
224: 			break;
225: 		if (wait.error) {
226: 			ret = wait.error;
227: 			break;
228: 		}
229: 
230: 		// at this point, maybe we are already waken up by some
231: 		if (poll_sched_timeout(&wait, expire))
232: 			immediate = 1;
233: 		if (myproc()->killed) {
234: 			immediate = 1;
235: 			wait.error = -EINTR;
236: 		}
237: 	}
238: 
239: 	poll_end(&wait);
240: 
241: 	__debug_info("pselect", "return %d\n", ret);
242: 	return ret;
243: }

xv6-k210@final / 来源候选源码

122: 	else
123: 		memset(exfds, 0, sizeof(struct fdset));
124: }
125: 
126: 
127: int pselect(int nfds, struct fdset *readfds, struct fdset *writefds, struct fdset *exceptfds,
128: 			struct timespec *timeout, __sigset_t *sigmask)
129: {
130: 	struct fdset rfds, wfds, exfds;
131: 	uint64 expire;
132: 	struct poll_wait_queue wait;
133: 	// struct poll_wait_queue *pwait;
134: 	int immediate = 0;
135: 	
136: 	
137: 	__debug_info("pselect", "timeout={%ds, %dns}\n", 
138: 				timeout ? timeout->sec : -1, timeout ? timeout->nsec : -1);
139: 
140: 	if (timeout) {
141: 		expire = convert_from_timespec(timeout);
142: 		if (expire == 0)
143: 			immediate = 1;			// don't sleep
144: 		else 
145: 			expire += readtime();	// overflow?
146: 	} else
147: 		expire = 0;					// infinity
148: 
149: 	fdset_work_init(&rfds, &wfds, &exfds, readfds, writefds, exceptfds);
150: 
151: 	// Since the node is on stack, if we want other process to get us, we should use pa.
152: 	// pwait = (struct poll_wait_queue *)kwalkaddr(myproc()->pagetable, (uint64)&wait);
153: 	// pwait = &wait;
154: 	poll_init(&wait);
155: 	if (immediate)
156: 		wait.pt.func = NULL;		// we won't be inserted into any queue in later poll()s
157: 
158: 	int ret = 0;
159: 	for (;;)
160: 	{
161: 		int i = 0;	// fd iterator
162: 		for (int idx = 0; idx <= (nfds - 1) / NFDBIT; idx++)
163: 		{
164: 			uint64 r, w, ex, all;
165: 			uint64 rres = 0, wres = 0, exres = 0;
166: 
167: 			r = rfds.fd_bits[idx];		// extract fd bits
168: 			w = wfds.fd_bits[idx];
169: 			ex = exfds.fd_bits[idx];
170: 			all = r | w | ex;
171: 			if (all == 0) {				// no fd in this part
172: 				i += NFDBIT;
173: 				continue;
174: 			}
175: 
176: 			for (int b = 0; b < NFDBIT; b++, i++) {
177: 				if (i >= nfds)			// reach end
178: 					break;
179: 				uint64 bit = 1L << b;
180: 				if (!(bit & all))		// no fd in this bit
181: 					continue;
182: 
183: 				struct file *fp = fd2file(i, 0);
184: 				if (!fp)				// bad fd
185: 					continue;
186: 				
187: 				__debug_info("pselect", "fd=%d\n", i);
188: 				
189: 				wait.pt.key = POLLEX_SET;
190: 				if (r & bit)
191: 					wait.pt.key |= POLLIN_SET;
192: 				if (w & bit)
193: 					wait.pt.key |= POLLOUT_SET;
194: 
195: 				uint32 mask = file_poll(fp, &wait.pt);
196: 				__debug_info("pselect", "mask=%d\n", mask);
197: 
198: 				if ((mask & POLLIN_SET) && (r & bit)) {
199: 					rres |= bit;
200: 					ret++;
201: 					wait.pt.func = NULL;
202: 				}
203: 				if ((mask & POLLOUT_SET) && (w & bit)) {
204: 					wres |= bit;
205: 					ret++;
206: 					wait.pt.func = NULL;
207: 				}
208: 				if ((mask & POLLEX_SET) && (ex & bit)) {
209: 					exres |= bit;
210: 					ret++;
211: 					wait.pt.func = NULL;
212: 				}
213: 			}
214: 			if (readfds)
215: 				readfds->fd_bits[idx] = rres;
216: 			if (writefds)
217: 				writefds->fd_bits[idx] = wres;
218: 			if (exceptfds)
219: 				exceptfds->fd_bits[idx] = exres;
220: 		}
221: 		wait.pt.func = NULL;		// only need to be called once for each file
222: 
223: 		if (ret > 0 || immediate)	// got results or don't sleep-wait
224: 			break;
225: 		if (wait.error) {
226: 			ret = wait.error;
227: 			break;
228: 		}
229: 
230: 		// at this point, maybe we are already waken up by some
231: 		if (poll_sched_timeout(&wait, expire))
232: 			immediate = 1;
233: 		if (myproc()->killed) {
234: 			immediate = 1;
235: 			wait.error = -EINTR;
236: 		}
237: 	}
238: 
239: 	poll_end(&wait);
240: 
241: 	__debug_info("pselect", "return %d\n", ret);
242: 	return ret;
243: }

23: /**
24:  * Root file system functions.
25:  * Deny most of them, because this is a virtual in-memory file system.
26:  */
27: 
28: struct inode *dummy_create(struct inode *ip, char *name, int mode) { return NULL; }
29: struct inode *dummy_lookup(struct inode *dir, char *filename, uint *poff) { return NULL; }
30: int dummy_iop1(struct inode *ip) { return -1; }
31: int dummy_setattr(struct inode *ip, struct kstat *st) { return -1; }
32: int dummy_rename(struct inode *ip, struct inode *dp, char *newname) { return -1; }
33: int dummy_file_rw(struct inode *ip, int usr, uint64 dst, uint off, uint n) { return 0; }

36: 
37: static int rootfs_getattr(struct inode *ip, struct kstat *st)
38: {
39: 	memset(st, 0, sizeof(struct kstat));
40: 	st->blksize = ip->sb->blocksz;
41: 	st->size = ip->size;
42: 	st->blocks = (st->size + st->blksize - 1) / st->blksize;
43: 	st->dev = ip->sb->devnum;
44: 	st->rdev = ip->dev;
45: 	st->ino = ip->inum;
46: 	st->mode = ip->mode;
47: 	st->nlink = ip->nlink;
48: 	return 0;
49: }
50:

53: {
54: 	struct dentry *entry = dp->entry;

匹配分数 1.000 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": true, "exact_token_fp": true, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 1.0}

50: 
51: 
52: int root_file_readdir(struct inode *dp, struct dirent *dent, uint off)
53: {
54: 	struct dentry *entry = dp->entry;
55: 	if (!S_ISDIR(dp->mode))
56: 		return -1;
57: 
58: 	int childnum = off >> 5;
59: 	acquire(&rootfs.cache_lock);
60: 	struct dentry *child;
61: 	for (child = entry->child; child != NULL && childnum > 0; child = child->next)
62: 	{
63: 		childnum--;
64: 	}
65: 	release(&rootfs.cache_lock);
66: 
67: 	if (!child)
68: 		return 0;
69: 
70: 	struct inode *ip = child->inode;
71: 	safestrcpy(dent->name, child->filename, 128);
72: 	int size = sizeof(struct dirent) - sizeof(dent->name) + strlen(dent->name) + 1;
73: 	size += (sizeof(uint64) - (size % sizeof(uint64))) % sizeof(uint64); // Align to 8.
74: 	dent->ino = ip->inum;
75: 	dent->off = off;
76: 	dent->reclen = size;
77: 	dent->type = S_ISDIR(ip->mode) ? T_DIR : (ip->dev ? T_DEVICE : T_FILE);
78: 	return 32;
79: }
80: 
81: 
82: int zero_read(struct inode *ip, int usr, uint64 dst, uint off, uint n)
83: {
84: #define ZBUFSZ		128

48: 	return 0;
49: }

52: int root_file_readdir(struct inode *dp, struct dirent *dent, uint off)
53: {
54: 	struct dentry *entry = dp->entry;
55: 	if (!S_ISDIR(dp->mode))
56: 		return -1;
57: 
58: 	int childnum = off >> 5;
59: 	acquire(&rootfs.cache_lock);
60: 	struct dentry *child;
61: 	for (child = entry->child; child != NULL && childnum > 0; child = child->next)
62: 	{
63: 		childnum--;
64: 	}
65: 	release(&rootfs.cache_lock);
66: 
67: 	if (!child)
68: 		return 0;
69: 
70: 	struct inode *ip = child->inode;
71: 	safestrcpy(dent->name, child->filename, 128);
72: 	int size = sizeof(struct dirent) - sizeof(dent->name) + strlen(dent->name) + 1;
73: 	size += (sizeof(uint64) - (size % sizeof(uint64))) % sizeof(uint64); // Align to 8.
74: 	dent->ino = ip->inum;
75: 	dent->off = off;
76: 	dent->reclen = size;
77: 	dent->type = S_ISDIR(ip->mode) ? T_DIR : (ip->dev ? T_DEVICE : T_FILE);
78: 	return 32;
79: }

80: 
81: 
82: int zero_read(struct inode *ip, int usr, uint64 dst, uint off, uint n)
83: {
84: #define ZBUFSZ		128
85: #define ZBIGBUFSZ	512
86: 
87: 	uint bufsz = ZBUFSZ;
88: 	char sbuf[ZBUFSZ];
89: 	char *buf = sbuf;
90: 
91: 	if (n > ZBUFSZ * 8) {
92: 		buf = kmalloc(ZBIGBUFSZ);
93: 		if (buf == NULL)
94: 			buf = sbuf;
95: 		else
96: 			bufsz = ZBIGBUFSZ;
97: 	}
98: 
99: 	memset(buf, 0, bufsz);
100: 
101: 	uint tot, m = bufsz;
102: 	for (tot = 0; tot < n; tot += m, dst += m) {
103: 		if (n - tot < m)
104: 			m = n - tot;
105: 		if (either_copyout_nocheck(usr, dst, buf, m) < 0)
106: 			break;
107: 	}
108: 
109: 	if (bufsz == ZBIGBUFSZ)
110: 		kfree(buf);
111: 
112: 	return tot;
113: }
114:

116: #define LEN_STORE_BUF 32
117: 
118: int intr_read(struct inode *ip, int usr, uint64 dst, uint off, uint n)

118: int intr_read(struct inode *ip, int usr, uint64 dst, uint off, uint n)
119: {
120: 	return 0;

127: 	char right[LEN_STORE_BUF];

131: 		if(c1 = CountInteruptInfo[i]){

135: 			Lx1 = Int2String(x1,10,0,left);
136: 			Lc1 = Int2String(c1,10,0,right);
137: 			right[Lc1++] = '\n';
138: 
139: 			either_copyout_nocheck(usr, dst, left, Lx1);

141: 			either_copyout_nocheck(usr, dst, right, Lc1);

155: 			break;

157: }

171: static struct dentry *de_rootfs_cache(struct dentry *parent, char *name)
172: {
173: 	struct dentry *de;
174: 	for (de = parent->child; de != NULL; de = de->next) {
175: 		if (strncmp(de->filename, name, MAXNAME) == 0) {
176: 			return de_mnt_in(de);
177: 		}
178: 	}
179: 	return NULL;
180: }

230: 
231: static struct dentry *de_root_generate(struct superblock *sb,
232: 							struct dentry *parent, char *name,
233: 							int inum, int mode, int devnum)
234: {
235: 	struct dentry *de;
236: 	struct inode *ip;
237: 	if ((de = kmalloc(sizeof(struct dentry))) == NULL ||
238: 		(ip = kmalloc(sizeof(struct inode))) == NULL)
239: 	{
240: 		__debug_warn("de_root_generate", "fail\n");
241: 		if (de)
242: 			kfree(de);
243: 		return NULL;
244: 	}
245: 	memset(de, 0, sizeof(struct dentry));
246: 	memset(ip, 0, sizeof(struct inode));
247: 
248: 	// insert those files into root's dentry tree
249: 	de->inode = ip;
250: 	de->op = &rootfs_dentry_op;
251: 	ip->entry = de;
252: 	ip->sb = sb;
253: 	
254: 	if (parent) {
255: 		de->next = parent->child;
256: 		parent->child = de;
257: 		de->parent = parent;
258: 	}
259: 
260: 	ip->inum = inum;
261: 	ip->dev = devnum;
262: 	ip->op = &rootfs_inode_op;
263: 	ip->fop = &rootfs_file_op;
264: 	ip->state = I_STATE_VALID;	// not necessary
265: 	ip->mode = mode;
266: 	ip->nlink = 1;
267: 	
268: 	initsleeplock(&ip->lock, "inode");
269: 	initlock(&ip->ilock, "inode2");
270: 	safestrcpy(de->filename, name, MAXNAME);
271: 	__debug_info("de_root_generate", "done\n");
272: 
273: 	return de;
274: }
275: 
276: struct inode *proc_INT;
277: struct dentry *entry_INT;
278: 
279: // Must be called by initcode.

181: };
182: 
183: static struct dentry *de_root_generate(struct superblock *sb,
184: 							struct dentry *parent, char *name,
185: 							int inum, int mode, int devnum)
186: {
187: 	struct dentry *de;
188: 	struct inode *ip;
189: 	if ((de = kmalloc(sizeof(struct dentry))) == NULL ||
190: 		(ip = kmalloc(sizeof(struct inode))) == NULL)
191: 	{
192: 		__debug_warn("de_root_generate", "fail\n");
193: 		if (de)
194: 			kfree(de);
195: 		return NULL;
196: 	}
197: 	memset(de, 0, sizeof(struct dentry));
198: 	memset(ip, 0, sizeof(struct inode));
199: 
200: 	// insert those files into root's dentry tree
201: 	de->inode = ip;
202: 	de->op = &rootfs_dentry_op;
203: 	ip->entry = de;
204: 	ip->sb = sb;
205: 	
206: 	if (parent) {
207: 		de->next = parent->child;
208: 		parent->child = de;
209: 		de->parent = parent;
210: 	}
211: 
212: 	ip->inum = inum;
213: 	ip->dev = devnum;
214: 	ip->op = &rootfs_inode_op;
215: 	ip->fop = &rootfs_file_op;
216: 	ip->state = I_STATE_VALID;	// not necessary
217: 	ip->mode = mode;
218: 	ip->nlink = 1;
219: 	
220: 	initsleeplock(&ip->lock, "inode");
221: 	initlock(&ip->ilock, "inode2");
222: 	safestrcpy(de->filename, name, MAXNAME);
223: 	__debug_info("de_root_generate", "done\n");
224: 
225: 	return de;
226: }

231: {

匹配分数 0.691 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 0.75, "exact_ast_shape": false, "exact_token_fp": false, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 0.719}

275: 
276: struct inode *proc_INT;
277: struct dentry *entry_INT;
278: 
279: // Must be called by initcode.
280: void rootfs_init()
281: {
282: 	__debug_info("rootfs_init", "enter\n");
283: 
284: 	int inum = 0;
285: 
286: 	// init rootfs
287: 	memset(&rootfs, 0, sizeof(struct superblock));
288: 	initsleeplock(&rootfs.sb_lock, "rootfs_sb");
289: 	initlock(&rootfs.cache_lock, "rootfs_dcache");
290: 
291: 	if ((rootfs.root = de_root_generate(&rootfs, NULL, "/", inum++, S_IFDIR, 0)) == NULL)
292: 		panic("rootfs_init: /");
293: 
294: 	// init devfs
295: 	struct dentry *con, *vda, *zero, *null;
296: 	memset(&devfs, 0, sizeof(struct superblock));
297: 	initsleeplock(&devfs.sb_lock, "devfs_sb");
298: 	initlock(&devfs.cache_lock, "devfs_dcache");
299: 	if ((devfs.root = de_root_generate(&devfs, NULL, "/", inum++, S_IFDIR, 0)) == NULL)
300: 		panic("rootfs_init: devfs /");
301: 	if ((con = de_root_generate(&devfs, devfs.root, "console", inum++, S_IFCHR, 2)) == NULL)
302: 		panic("rootfs_init: devfs console");
303: 	if ((vda = de_root_generate(&devfs, devfs.root, "vda2", inum++, S_IFBLK, ROOTDEV)) == NULL)
304: 		panic("rootfs_init: devfs vda2");
305: 	if ((zero = de_root_generate(&devfs, devfs.root, "zero", inum++, S_IFCHR, 3)) == NULL)
306: 		panic("rootfs_init: devfs zero");
307: 	if ((null = de_root_generate(&devfs, devfs.root, "null", inum++, S_IFCHR, 4)) == NULL)
308: 		panic("rootfs_init: devfs null");
309: 
310: 	extern struct file_op console_op;
311: 	con->inode->fop = &console_op;
312: 
313: 	zero->inode->fop = &zero_op;
314: 	null->inode->fop = &null_op;
315: 
316: 	// init procfs
317: 	struct dentry *mount;
318: 	memset(&procfs, 0, sizeof(struct superblock));
319: 	initsleeplock(&procfs.sb_lock, "procfs_sb");
320: 	initlock(&procfs.cache_lock, "procfs_dcache");
321: 	if ((procfs.root = de_root_generate(&procfs, NULL, "/", inum++, S_IFDIR, 0)) == NULL)
322: 		panic("rootfs_init: procfs /");
323: 	if ((mount = de_root_generate(&procfs, procfs.root, "mounts", inum++, S_IFREG, 0)) == NULL)
324: 		panic("rootfs_init: procfs mounts");
325: 	if (de_root_generate(&procfs, procfs.root, "meminfo", inum++, S_IFREG, 0) == NULL)
326: 		panic("rootfs_init: procfs meminfo");
327: 
328: 	// AAA1 solution2
329: 	if ((entry_INT = de_root_generate(&procfs, procfs.root, "interrupts", inum++, S_IFREG, 0)) == NULL)
330: 		panic("rootfs_init: procfs meminfo");
331: 	proc_INT = entry_INT->inode;
332: 	proc_INT->fop = &intr_file_op;
333: 	// AAA
334: 
335: 
336: 	extern struct file_op mountinfo_fop;
337: 	mount->inode->fop = &mountinfo_fop;
338: 
339: 	// mount disk
340: 	if (do_mount(vda->inode, rootfs.root->inode, "fat32", 0, 0) < 0)
341: 		panic("rootfs_init: mount disk");
342: 
343: 	// make directories and mount
344: 	struct inode *dir;
345: 
346: 	if ((dir = create(NULL, "/dev", S_IFDIR)) == NULL)
347: 		panic("rootfs_init: on disk /dev");
348: 	if (mountsysfs(&devfs, dir, "devfs") < 0)
349: 		panic("rootfs_init: mount devfs");
350: 	iunlockput(dir);
351: 
352: 	if ((dir = create(NULL, "/proc", S_IFDIR)) == NULL)
353: 		panic("rootfs_init: on disk /proc");
354: 	if (mountsysfs(&procfs, dir, "procfs") < 0)
355: 		panic("rootfs_init: mount procfs");
356: 	
357: 	struct inode *store = dir;
358: 
359: 	/* // solution1
360: 	if ((dir = create(NULL, "/proc/interrupts", S_IFREG)) != NULL){
361: 		proc_INT = dir;
362: 	}
363: 	else{
364: 		panic("rootfs_init: on disk /proc/interrupts");
365: 	}*/
366: 	//iunlockput(dir);
367: 	iunlockput(store);
368: 
369: 	__debug_info("rootfs_init", "done\n");
370: }

226: }

228: 
229: // Must be called by initcode.
230: void rootfs_init()
231: {
232: 	__debug_info("rootfs_init", "enter\n");
233: 
234: 	int inum = 0;
235: 
236: 	// init rootfs
237: 	memset(&rootfs, 0, sizeof(struct superblock));
238: 	initsleeplock(&rootfs.sb_lock, "rootfs_sb");
239: 	initlock(&rootfs.cache_lock, "rootfs_dcache");
240: 
241: 	if ((rootfs.root = de_root_generate(&rootfs, NULL, "/", inum++, S_IFDIR, 0)) == NULL)
242: 		panic("rootfs_init: /");
243: 
244: 	// init devfs
245: 	struct dentry *con, *vda, *zero, *null;
246: 	memset(&devfs, 0, sizeof(struct superblock));
247: 	initsleeplock(&devfs.sb_lock, "devfs_sb");
248: 	initlock(&devfs.cache_lock, "devfs_dcache");
249: 	if ((devfs.root = de_root_generate(&devfs, NULL, "/", inum++, S_IFDIR, 0)) == NULL)
250: 		panic("rootfs_init: devfs /");
251: 	if ((con = de_root_generate(&devfs, devfs.root, "console", inum++, S_IFCHR, 2)) == NULL)
252: 		panic("rootfs_init: devfs console");
253: 	if ((vda = de_root_generate(&devfs, devfs.root, "vda2", inum++, S_IFBLK, ROOTDEV)) == NULL)
254: 		panic("rootfs_init: devfs vda2");
255: 	if ((zero = de_root_generate(&devfs, devfs.root, "zero", inum++, S_IFCHR, 3)) == NULL)
256: 		panic("rootfs_init: devfs zero");
257: 	if ((null = de_root_generate(&devfs, devfs.root, "null", inum++, S_IFCHR, 4)) == NULL)
258: 		panic("rootfs_init: devfs null");
259: 
260: 	extern struct file_op console_op;
261: 	con->inode->fop = &console_op;
262: 
263: 	zero->inode->fop = &zero_op;
264: 	null->inode->fop = &null_op;
265: 
266: 	// init procfs
267: 	struct dentry *mount;
268: 	memset(&procfs, 0, sizeof(struct superblock));
269: 	initsleeplock(&procfs.sb_lock, "procfs_sb");
270: 	initlock(&procfs.cache_lock, "procfs_dcache");
271: 	if ((procfs.root = de_root_generate(&procfs, NULL, "/", inum++, S_IFDIR, 0)) == NULL)
272: 		panic("rootfs_init: procfs /");
273: 	if ((mount = de_root_generate(&procfs, procfs.root, "mounts", inum++, S_IFREG, 0)) == NULL)
274: 		panic("rootfs_init: procfs mounts");
275: 	if (de_root_generate(&procfs, procfs.root, "meminfo", inum++, S_IFREG, 0) == NULL)
276: 		panic("rootfs_init: procfs meminfo");
277: 
278: 	extern struct file_op mountinfo_fop;
279: 	mount->inode->fop = &mountinfo_fop;
280: 
281: 	// mount disk
282: 	if (do_mount(vda->inode, rootfs.root->inode, "fat32", 0, 0) < 0)
283: 		panic("rootfs_init: mount disk");
284: 
285: 	// make directories and mount
286: 	struct inode *dir;
287: 
288: 	if ((dir = create(NULL, "/dev", S_IFDIR)) == NULL)
289: 		panic("rootfs_init: on disk /dev");
290: 	iunlockput(dir);
291: 	dir = namei("/dev");
292: 	ilock(dir);
293: 	if (mountsysfs(&devfs, dir, "devfs") < 0)
294: 		panic("rootfs_init: mount devfs");
295: 	iunlockput(dir);
296: 
297: 	if ((dir = create(NULL, "/proc", S_IFDIR)) == NULL)
298: 		panic("rootfs_init: on disk /proc");
299: 	iunlockput(dir);
300: 	dir = namei("/proc");
301: 	ilock(dir);
302: 	if (mountsysfs(&procfs, dir, "procfs") < 0)
303: 		panic("rootfs_init: mount procfs");
304: 	iunlockput(dir);
305: 
306: 	if ((dir = create(NULL, "/bin", S_IFDIR)) == NULL)
307: 		panic("rootfs_init: on disk /bin");
308: 	iunlockput(dir);
309: 
310: 	syncfs();
311: 
312: 	__debug_info("rootfs_init", "done\n");
313: }

74: void dmac_channel_enable(dmac_channel_number_t channel_num)
75: {
76: 	dmac_chen_u_t chen;
77: 
78: 	chen.data = readq(&dmac->chen);
79: 
80: 	switch(channel_num)
81: 	{
82: 		case DMAC_CHANNEL0:
83: 			chen.dmac_chen.ch1_en = 1;
84: 			chen.dmac_chen.ch1_en_we = 1;
85: 			break;
86: 		case DMAC_CHANNEL1:
87: 			chen.dmac_chen.ch2_en = 1;
88: 			chen.dmac_chen.ch2_en_we = 1;
89: 			break;
90: 		case DMAC_CHANNEL2:
91: 			chen.dmac_chen.ch3_en = 1;
92: 			chen.dmac_chen.ch3_en_we = 1;
93: 			break;
94: 		case DMAC_CHANNEL3:
95: 			chen.dmac_chen.ch4_en = 1;
96: 			chen.dmac_chen.ch4_en_we = 1;
97: 			break;
98: 		case DMAC_CHANNEL4:
99: 			chen.dmac_chen.ch5_en = 1;
100: 			chen.dmac_chen.ch5_en_we = 1;
101: 			break;
102: 		case DMAC_CHANNEL5:
103: 			chen.dmac_chen.ch6_en = 1;
104: 			chen.dmac_chen.ch6_en_we = 1;
105: 			break;
106: 		default:
107: 			break;
108: 	}
109: 
110: 	writeq(chen.data, &dmac->chen);
111: }

90: 		case DMAC_CHANNEL2:

92: 			chen.dmac_chen.ch3_en_we = 1;
93: 			break;
94: 		case DMAC_CHANNEL3:
95: 			chen.dmac_chen.ch4_en = 1;

97: 			break;
98: 		case DMAC_CHANNEL4:
99: 			chen.dmac_chen.ch5_en = 1;
100: 			chen.dmac_chen.ch5_en_we = 1;
101: 			break;
102: 		case DMAC_CHANNEL5:
103: 			chen.dmac_chen.ch6_en = 1;
104: 			chen.dmac_chen.ch6_en_we = 1;
105: 			break;
106: 		default:
107: 			break;
108: 	}
109: 
110: 	writeq(chen.data, &dmac->chen);
111: }

xv6-k210/kernel/hal/dmac.c::dmac_set_channel_param
匹配分数 1.000 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": true, "exact_token_fp": true, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 1.0}

oskernel2023-zmz@recover 源码

191: static void dmac_channel_interrupt_clear(dmac_channel_number_t channel_num)
192: {
193: 	writeq(0xffffffff, &dmac->channel[channel_num].intclear);
194: }
195: 
196: int dmac_set_channel_param(dmac_channel_number_t channel_num,
197: 						   const void *src, void *dest, dmac_address_increment_t src_inc, dmac_address_increment_t dest_inc,
198: 						   dmac_burst_trans_length_t dmac_burst_size,
199: 						   dmac_transfer_width_t dmac_trans_width,
200: 						   uint32 blockSize)
201: {
202: 	dmac_ch_ctl_u_t ctl;
203: 	dmac_ch_cfg_u_t cfg_u;
204: 
205: 	int mem_type_src = is_memory((uintptr_t)src), mem_type_dest = is_memory((uintptr_t)dest);
206: 	dmac_transfer_flow_t flow_control;
207: 	if(mem_type_src == 0 && mem_type_dest == 0)
208: 	{
209: 		flow_control = DMAC_PRF2PRF_DMA;
210: 	} else if(mem_type_src == 1 && mem_type_dest == 0)
211: 		flow_control = DMAC_MEM2PRF_DMA;
212: 	else if(mem_type_src == 0 && mem_type_dest == 1)
213: 		flow_control = DMAC_PRF2MEM_DMA;
214: 	else
215: 		flow_control = DMAC_MEM2MEM_DMA;
216: 
217: 	/**
218: 	 * cfg register must configure before ts_block and
219: 	 * sar dar register
220: 	 */
221: 	cfg_u.data = readq(&dmac->channel[channel_num].cfg);
222: 
223: 	cfg_u.ch_cfg.tt_fc = flow_control;
224: 	cfg_u.ch_cfg.hs_sel_src = mem_type_src ? DMAC_HS_SOFTWARE : DMAC_HS_HARDWARE;
225: 	cfg_u.ch_cfg.hs_sel_dst = mem_type_dest ? DMAC_HS_SOFTWARE : DMAC_HS_HARDWARE;
226: 	cfg_u.ch_cfg.src_per = channel_num;
227: 	cfg_u.ch_cfg.dst_per = channel_num;
228: 	cfg_u.ch_cfg.src_multblk_type = 0;
229: 	cfg_u.ch_cfg.dst_multblk_type = 0;
230: 
231: 	writeq(cfg_u.data, &dmac->channel[channel_num].cfg);
232: 
233: 	dmac->channel[channel_num].sar = (uint64)src;
234: 	dmac->channel[channel_num].dar = (uint64)dest;
235: 
236: 	ctl.data = readq(&dmac->channel[channel_num].ctl);
237: 	ctl.ch_ctl.sms = DMAC_MASTER1;
238: 	ctl.ch_ctl.dms = DMAC_MASTER2;
239: 	/* master select */
240: 	ctl.ch_ctl.sinc = src_inc;
241: 	ctl.ch_ctl.dinc = dest_inc;
242: 	/* address incrememt */
243: 	ctl.ch_ctl.src_tr_width = dmac_trans_width;
244: 	ctl.ch_ctl.dst_tr_width = dmac_trans_width;
245: 	/* transfer width */
246: 	ctl.ch_ctl.src_msize = dmac_burst_size;
247: 	ctl.ch_ctl.dst_msize = dmac_burst_size;
248: 
249: 	writeq(ctl.data, &dmac->channel[channel_num].ctl);
250: 
251: 	writeq(blockSize - 1, &dmac->channel[channel_num].block_ts);
252: 	/*the number of (blcok_ts +1) data of width SRC_TR_WIDTF to be */
253: 	/* transferred in a dma block transfer */
254: 	return 0;
255: }
256: 
257: struct spinlock dmac_useless_lock;
258: void dmac_init(void)
259: {
260: 	uint64 tmp;

xv6-k210@final / 来源候选源码

191: static void dmac_channel_interrupt_clear(dmac_channel_number_t channel_num)

194: }
195: 
196: int dmac_set_channel_param(dmac_channel_number_t channel_num,
197: 						   const void *src, void *dest, dmac_address_increment_t src_inc, dmac_address_increment_t dest_inc,
198: 						   dmac_burst_trans_length_t dmac_burst_size,
199: 						   dmac_transfer_width_t dmac_trans_width,
200: 						   uint32 blockSize)
201: {
202: 	dmac_ch_ctl_u_t ctl;
203: 	dmac_ch_cfg_u_t cfg_u;
204: 
205: 	int mem_type_src = is_memory((uintptr_t)src), mem_type_dest = is_memory((uintptr_t)dest);
206: 	dmac_transfer_flow_t flow_control;
207: 	if(mem_type_src == 0 && mem_type_dest == 0)
208: 	{
209: 		flow_control = DMAC_PRF2PRF_DMA;
210: 	} else if(mem_type_src == 1 && mem_type_dest == 0)
211: 		flow_control = DMAC_MEM2PRF_DMA;
212: 	else if(mem_type_src == 0 && mem_type_dest == 1)
213: 		flow_control = DMAC_PRF2MEM_DMA;
214: 	else
215: 		flow_control = DMAC_MEM2MEM_DMA;
216: 
217: 	/**
218: 	 * cfg register must configure before ts_block and
219: 	 * sar dar register
220: 	 */
221: 	cfg_u.data = readq(&dmac->channel[channel_num].cfg);
222: 
223: 	cfg_u.ch_cfg.tt_fc = flow_control;
224: 	cfg_u.ch_cfg.hs_sel_src = mem_type_src ? DMAC_HS_SOFTWARE : DMAC_HS_HARDWARE;
225: 	cfg_u.ch_cfg.hs_sel_dst = mem_type_dest ? DMAC_HS_SOFTWARE : DMAC_HS_HARDWARE;
226: 	cfg_u.ch_cfg.src_per = channel_num;
227: 	cfg_u.ch_cfg.dst_per = channel_num;
228: 	cfg_u.ch_cfg.src_multblk_type = 0;
229: 	cfg_u.ch_cfg.dst_multblk_type = 0;
230: 
231: 	writeq(cfg_u.data, &dmac->channel[channel_num].cfg);
232: 
233: 	dmac->channel[channel_num].sar = (uint64)src;
234: 	dmac->channel[channel_num].dar = (uint64)dest;
235: 
236: 	ctl.data = readq(&dmac->channel[channel_num].ctl);
237: 	ctl.ch_ctl.sms = DMAC_MASTER1;
238: 	ctl.ch_ctl.dms = DMAC_MASTER2;
239: 	/* master select */
240: 	ctl.ch_ctl.sinc = src_inc;
241: 	ctl.ch_ctl.dinc = dest_inc;
242: 	/* address incrememt */
243: 	ctl.ch_ctl.src_tr_width = dmac_trans_width;
244: 	ctl.ch_ctl.dst_tr_width = dmac_trans_width;
245: 	/* transfer width */
246: 	ctl.ch_ctl.src_msize = dmac_burst_size;
247: 	ctl.ch_ctl.dst_msize = dmac_burst_size;
248: 
249: 	writeq(ctl.data, &dmac->channel[channel_num].ctl);
250: 
251: 	writeq(blockSize - 1, &dmac->channel[channel_num].block_ts);
252: 	/*the number of (blcok_ts +1) data of width SRC_TR_WIDTF to be */
253: 	/* transferred in a dma block transfer */
254: 	return 0;
255: }
256: 
257: struct spinlock dmac_useless_lock;
258: void dmac_init(void)
259: {
260: 	uint64 tmp;
dmac_init完全一致
oskernel2023-zmz@recover 第 258 行 · 主要确定性来源：xv6-k210/kernel/hal/dmac.c::dmac_init

候选来源与匹配信号

xv6-k210/kernel/hal/dmac.c::dmac_init
匹配分数 1.000 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": true, "exact_token_fp": true, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 1.0}

oskernel2023-zmz@recover 源码

253: 	/* transferred in a dma block transfer */
254: 	return 0;
255: }
256: 
257: struct spinlock dmac_useless_lock;
258: void dmac_init(void)
259: {
260: 	uint64 tmp;
261: 	dmac_commonreg_intclear_u_t intclear;
262: 	dmac_cfg_u_t dmac_cfg;
263: 	dmac_reset_u_t dmac_reset;
264: 
265: 	initlock(&dmac_useless_lock, "useless_lock");
266: 
267: 	sysctl_clock_enable(SYSCTL_CLOCK_DMA);
268: 	// printf("[dmac_init] dma clk=%d\n", sysctl_clock_get_freq(SYSCTL_CLOCK_DMA));
269: 
270: 	dmac_reset.data = readq(&dmac->reset);
271: 	dmac_reset.reset.rst = 1;
272: 	writeq(dmac_reset.data, &dmac->reset);
273: 	while(dmac_reset.reset.rst)
274: 		dmac_reset.data = readq(&dmac->reset);
275: 
276: 	/*reset dmac */
277: 
278: 	intclear.data = readq(&dmac->com_intclear);
279: 	intclear.com_intclear.clear_slvif_dec_err_intstat = 1;
280: 	intclear.com_intclear.clear_slvif_wr2ro_err_intstat = 1;
281: 	intclear.com_intclear.clear_slvif_rd2wo_err_intstat = 1;
282: 	intclear.com_intclear.clear_slvif_wronhold_err_intstat = 1;
283: 	intclear.com_intclear.clear_slvif_undefinedreg_dec_err_intstat = 1;
284: 	writeq(intclear.data, &dmac->com_intclear);
285: 	/* clear common register interrupt */
286: 
287: 	dmac_cfg.data = readq(&dmac->cfg);
288: 	dmac_cfg.cfg.dmac_en = 0;
289: 	dmac_cfg.cfg.int_en = 0;
290: 	writeq(dmac_cfg.data, &dmac->cfg);
291: 	/* disable dmac and disable interrupt */
292: 
293: 	while(readq(&dmac->cfg))
294: 		;
295: 	tmp = readq(&dmac->chen);
296: 	tmp &= ~0xf;
297: 	writeq(tmp, &dmac->chen);
298: 	/* disable all channel before configure */
299: 	dmac_enable();
300: }
301: 
302: void dmac_set_single_mode(dmac_channel_number_t channel_num,
303: 						  const void *src, void *dest, dmac_address_increment_t src_inc,
304: 						  dmac_address_increment_t dest_inc,
305: 						  dmac_burst_trans_length_t dmac_burst_size,

xv6-k210@final / 来源候选源码

254: 	return 0;
255: }
256: 
257: struct spinlock dmac_useless_lock;
258: void dmac_init(void)
259: {
260: 	uint64 tmp;
261: 	dmac_commonreg_intclear_u_t intclear;
262: 	dmac_cfg_u_t dmac_cfg;
263: 	dmac_reset_u_t dmac_reset;
264: 
265: 	initlock(&dmac_useless_lock, "useless_lock");
266: 
267: 	sysctl_clock_enable(SYSCTL_CLOCK_DMA);
268: 	// printf("[dmac_init] dma clk=%d\n", sysctl_clock_get_freq(SYSCTL_CLOCK_DMA));
269: 
270: 	dmac_reset.data = readq(&dmac->reset);
271: 	dmac_reset.reset.rst = 1;
272: 	writeq(dmac_reset.data, &dmac->reset);
273: 	while(dmac_reset.reset.rst)
274: 		dmac_reset.data = readq(&dmac->reset);
275: 
276: 	/*reset dmac */
277: 
278: 	intclear.data = readq(&dmac->com_intclear);
279: 	intclear.com_intclear.clear_slvif_dec_err_intstat = 1;
280: 	intclear.com_intclear.clear_slvif_wr2ro_err_intstat = 1;
281: 	intclear.com_intclear.clear_slvif_rd2wo_err_intstat = 1;
282: 	intclear.com_intclear.clear_slvif_wronhold_err_intstat = 1;
283: 	intclear.com_intclear.clear_slvif_undefinedreg_dec_err_intstat = 1;
284: 	writeq(intclear.data, &dmac->com_intclear);
285: 	/* clear common register interrupt */
286: 
287: 	dmac_cfg.data = readq(&dmac->cfg);
288: 	dmac_cfg.cfg.dmac_en = 0;
289: 	dmac_cfg.cfg.int_en = 0;
290: 	writeq(dmac_cfg.data, &dmac->cfg);
291: 	/* disable dmac and disable interrupt */
292: 
293: 	while(readq(&dmac->cfg))
294: 		;
295: 	tmp = readq(&dmac->chen);
296: 	tmp &= ~0xf;
297: 	writeq(tmp, &dmac->chen);
298: 	/* disable all channel before configure */
299: 	dmac_enable();
300: }

匹配分数 1.000 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": true, "exact_token_fp": true, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 1.0}
