4729: //     /* Atomic write register */
4730: //     fpioa->io[number] = *cfg;
4731: //     return 0;
4732: // }
4733: 
4734: int fpioa_set_io_pull(int number, fpioa_pull_t pull)
4735: {
4736: 	/* Check parameters */
4737: 	if(number < 0 || number >= FPIOA_NUM_IO || pull >= FPIOA_PULL_MAX)
4738: 		return -1;
4739: 
4740: 	/* Atomic read register */
4741: 	fpioa_io_config_t cfg = fpioa->io[number];
4742: 
4743: 	switch(pull)
4744: 	{
4745: 		case FPIOA_PULL_NONE:
4746: 			cfg.pu = 0;
4747: 			cfg.pd = 0;
4748: 			break;
4749: 		case FPIOA_PULL_DOWN:
4750: 			cfg.pu = 0;
4751: 			cfg.pd = 1;
4752: 			break;
4753: 		case FPIOA_PULL_UP:
4754: 			cfg.pu = 1;
4755: 			cfg.pd = 0;
4756: 			break;
4757: 		default:
4758: 			break;
4759: 	}
4760: 	/* Atomic write register */
4761: 	fpioa->io[number] = cfg;
4762: 	return 0;
4763: }
4764:

4853: int fpioa_set_function_raw(int number, fpioa_function_t function)
4854: {

4857: 		return -1;

4859: 	fpioa->io[number] = (const fpioa_io_config_t){
4860: 		.ch_sel = function_config[function].ch_sel,
4861: 		.ds = function_config[function].ds,

4870: 		.ie_inv = function_config[function].ie_inv,

4872: 		.st = function_config[function].st,
4873: 		/* resv and pad_di do not need initialization */
4874: 	};
4875: 	return 0;
4876: }

匹配分数 1.000 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": true, "exact_token_fp": true, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 1.0}

19: //     void *context;
20: // } gpiohs_pin_instance_t;
21: 
22: // static gpiohs_pin_instance_t pin_instance[32];
23: 
24: void gpiohs_set_drive_mode(uint8 pin, gpio_drive_mode_t mode)
25: {
26: 	// configASSERT(pin < GPIOHS_MAX_PINNO);
27: 	int io_number = fpioa_get_io_by_function(FUNC_GPIOHS0 + pin);
28: 	// configASSERT(io_number >= 0);
29: 
30: 	fpioa_pull_t pull = FPIOA_PULL_NONE;
31: 	uint32 dir = 0;
32: 
33: 	switch(mode)
34: 	{
35: 		case GPIO_DM_INPUT:
36: 			pull = FPIOA_PULL_NONE;
37: 			dir = 0;
38: 			break;
39: 		case GPIO_DM_INPUT_PULL_DOWN:
40: 			pull = FPIOA_PULL_DOWN;
41: 			dir = 0;
42: 			break;
43: 		case GPIO_DM_INPUT_PULL_UP:
44: 			pull = FPIOA_PULL_UP;
45: 			dir = 0;
46: 			break;
47: 		case GPIO_DM_OUTPUT:
48: 			pull = FPIOA_PULL_DOWN;
49: 			dir = 1;
50: 			break;
51: 		default:
52: 			// configASSERT(!"GPIO drive mode is not supported.") 
53: 			break;
54: 	}
55: 
56: 	fpioa_set_io_pull(io_number, pull);
57: 	volatile uint32 *reg = dir ? gpiohs->output_en.u32 : gpiohs->input_en.u32;
58: 	volatile uint32 *reg_d = !dir ? gpiohs->output_en.u32 : gpiohs->input_en.u32;
59: 	set_gpio_bit(reg_d, pin, 0);
60: 	set_gpio_bit(reg, pin, 1);
61: }

匹配分数 1.000 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": true, "exact_token_fp": true, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 1.0}

31: }
32: 
33: void
34: plicinithart(void)
35: {
36: 	int hart = cpuid();
37: 	#ifdef QEMU
38: 	// set uart's enable bit for this hart's S-mode. 
39: 	*(uint32*)PLIC_SENABLE(hart)= (1 << UART_IRQ) | (1 << DISK_IRQ);
40: 	// set this hart's S-mode priority threshold to 0.
41: 	*(uint32*)PLIC_SPRIORITY(hart) = 0;
42: 	#else
43: 	uint32 *hart_m_enable = (uint32*)PLIC_MENABLE(hart);
44: 	*(hart_m_enable) = readd(hart_m_enable) | (1 << DISK_IRQ);
45: 	uint32 *hart0_m_int_enable_hi = hart_m_enable + 1;
46: 	*(hart0_m_int_enable_hi) = readd(hart0_m_int_enable_hi) | (1 << (UART_IRQ % 32));
47: 
48: 	// Sometimes on k210 the PLIC may not be initialized correctly, 
49: 	// and may enable external interrupts on Supervisor as described above. 
50: 	// Disable Supervisor External Interrupts on PLIC.
51: 	uint32 *hart_s_enable = (uint32*)PLIC_SENABLE(hart);
52: 	uint32 *hart_s_enable_hi = hart_s_enable + 1;
53: 	*(hart_s_enable) = 0;
54: 	*(hart_s_enable_hi) = 0;
55: 	#endif
56: }
57: 
58: // ask the PLIC what interrupt we should serve.
59: int
60: plic_claim(void)
61: {

xv6-k210@final / 来源候选源码

60: plic_claim(void)
61: {
62: 	int hart = cpuid();
63: 	int irq;
64: 	#ifndef QEMU
65: 	irq = *(uint32*)PLIC_MCLAIM(hart);
66: 	#else
67: 	irq = *(uint32*)PLIC_SCLAIM(hart);
68: 	#endif
69: 	return irq;
70: }

74: plic_complete(int irq)

匹配分数 1.000 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": true, "exact_token_fp": true, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 1.0}

oskernel2023-zmz@recover 源码

77:  * @param  Cmd: The user expected command to send to SD card.
78:  * @param  Arg: The command argument.
79:  * @param  Crc: The CRC.
80:  * @retval None
81:  */
82: static void sd_send_cmd(uint8 cmd, uint32 arg, uint8 crc) {
83: 	uint8 frame[6];
84: 	frame[0] = (cmd | 0x40);
85: 	frame[1] = (uint8)(arg >> 24);
86: 	frame[2] = (uint8)(arg >> 16);
87: 	frame[3] = (uint8)(arg >> 8);
88: 	frame[4] = (uint8)(arg);
89: 	frame[5] = (crc);
90: 	SD_CS_LOW();
91: 	sd_write_data(frame, 6);
92: }
93: 
94: static void sd_end_cmd(void) {
95: 	uint8 frame[1] = {0xFF};
96: 	/*!< SD chip select high */
97: 	SD_CS_HIGH();

匹配分数 1.000 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": true, "exact_token_fp": true, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 1.0}

120: #define SD_ACMD23 	23 		// SET_WR_BLK_ERASE_COUNT
121: 
122: /*
123:  * Read sdcard response in R1 type. 
124:  */
125: static uint8 sd_get_response_R1(void) {
126: 	uint8 result = 0xff;
127: 	uint16 timeout = 0xff;
128: 
129: 	while (timeout--) {
130: 		sd_read_data(&result, 1);
131: 		if (result != 0xff)
132: 			return result;
133: 	}
134: 
135: 	// timeout!
136: 	return 0xff;
137: }
138: 
139: /* 
140:  * Read the rest of R3 response 
141:  * Be noticed: frame should be at least 4-byte long 
142:  */

xv6-k210@final / 来源候选源码

120: #define SD_ACMD23 	23 		// SET_WR_BLK_ERASE_COUNT
121: 
122: /*
123:  * Read sdcard response in R1 type. 
124:  */
125: static uint8 sd_get_response_R1(void) {
126: 	uint8 result = 0xff;
127: 	uint64 timeout = 0xfffff;
128: 
129: 	while (timeout--) {
130: 		sd_read_data(&result, 1);
131: 		if (result != 0xff)
132: 			return result;
133: 	}
134:

155: static int switch_to_SPI_mode(void) {
156: 	int timeout = 0xff;
157: 
158: 	while (--timeout) {

162:

169: 
170: 	return 0;

173: // verify supply voltage range 
174: static int verify_operation_condition(void) {
175: 	uint64 result = 0xff;
176: 
177: 	// Stores the response reversely. 
178: 	// That means 
179: 	// frame[2] - VCA 
180: 	// frame[3] - Check Pattern 
181: 	uint8 frame[4];
182: 
183: 	sd_send_cmd(SD_CMD8, 0x01aa, 0x87);
184: 	result = sd_get_response_R1();
185: 	sd_get_response_R7_rest(frame);
186: 	sd_end_cmd();
187: 
188: 	if (0x09 == result) {
189: 		printf("invalid CRC for CMD8\n");
190: 		return 0xff;
191: 	}
192: 	else if (0x01 == result && 0x01 == (frame[2] & 0x0f) && 0xaa == frame[3]) {
193: 		return 0x00;
194: 	}
195: 
196: 	printf("verify_operation_condition() fail!\n");
197: 	return 0xff;
198: }
199: 
200: // read OCR register to check if the voltage range is valid 
201: // this step is not mandotary, but I advise to use it 
202: static int read_OCR(void) {
203: 	uint64 result = 0xff;

200: // read OCR register to check if the voltage range is valid 
201: // this step is not mandotary, but I advise to use it 
202: static int read_OCR(void) {
203: 	uint64 result = 0xff;
204: 	uint8 ocr[4];
205: 
206: 	int timeout = 0xffffff;
207: 	while (--timeout) {
208: 		sd_send_cmd(SD_CMD58, 0, 0);
209: 		result = sd_get_response_R1();
210: 		sd_get_response_R3_rest(ocr);
211: 		sd_end_cmd();
212: 
213: 		if (
214: 			0x01 == result && // R1 response in idle status 
215: 			(ocr[1] & 0x1f) && (ocr[2] & 0x80) 	// voltage range valid 
216: 		) {
217: 			return 0;
218: 		}
219: 	}
220: 
221: 	// timeout!
222: 	printf("read_OCR() timeout!\n");
223: 	printf("result = %d\n", result);
224: 	return 0xff;
225: }

匹配分数 1.000 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": true, "exact_token_fp": true, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 1.0}

oskernel2023-zmz@recover 源码

255: // Used to differ whether sdcard is SDSC type. 
256: static int is_standard_sd = 0;
257: 
258: // check OCR register to see the type of sdcard, 
259: // thus determine whether block size is suitable to buffer size
260: static int check_block_size(void) {
261: 	uint8 result = 0xff;
262: 	uint8 ocr[4];
263: 
264: 	int timeout = 0xffffff;
265: 	while (timeout --) {
266: 		sd_send_cmd(SD_CMD58, 0, 0);
267: 		result = sd_get_response_R1();
268: 		sd_get_response_R3_rest(ocr);
269: 		sd_end_cmd();
270: 
271: 		if (0 == result) {
272: 			if (ocr[0] & 0x40) {
273: 				printf("SDHC/SDXC detected\n");
274: 				if (512 != BSIZE) {
275: 					printf("BSIZE != 512\n");
276: 					return 0xff;
277: 				}
278: 
279: 				is_standard_sd = 0;
280: 			}
281: 			else {
282: 				printf("SDSC detected, setting block size\n");
283: 
284: 				// setting SD card block size to BSIZE 
285: 				int timeout = 0xff;
286: 				int result = 0xff;
287: 				while (--timeout) {
288: 					sd_send_cmd(SD_CMD16, BSIZE, 0);
289: 					result = sd_get_response_R1();
290: 					sd_end_cmd();
291: 
292: 					if (0 == result) break;
293: 				}
294: 				if (0 == timeout) {
295: 					printf("check_OCR(): fail to set block size");
296: 					return 0xff;
297: 				}
298: 
299: 				is_standard_sd = 1;
300: 			}
301: 
302: 			return 0;
303: 		}
304: 	}
305: 
306: 	// timeout! 
307: 	printf("check_OCR() timeout!\n");
308: 	printf("result = %d\n", result);
309: 	return 0xff;
310: }
311:

313:  * @brief  Initializes the SD/SD communication.
314:  * @param  None
315:  * @retval The SD Response:

xv6-k210@final / 来源候选源码

255: // Used to differ whether sdcard is SDSC type.

257: 
258: // check OCR register to see the type of sdcard,

260: static int check_block_size(void) {
261: 	uint8 result = 0xff;
262: 	uint8 ocr[4];
263: 
264: 	int timeout = 0xffffff;
265: 	while (timeout --) {
266: 		sd_send_cmd(SD_CMD58, 0, 0);
267: 		result = sd_get_response_R1();
268: 		sd_get_response_R3_rest(ocr);
269: 		sd_end_cmd();
270: 
271: 		if (0 == result) {
272: 			if (ocr[0] & 0x40) {
273: 				printf("SDHC/SDXC detected\n");
274: 				if (512 != BSIZE) {
275: 					printf("BSIZE != 512\n");
276: 					return 0xff;
277: 				}
278: 
279: 				is_standard_sd = 0;
280: 			}
281: 			else {
282: 				printf("SDSC detected, setting block size\n");
283: 
284: 				// setting SD card block size to BSIZE 
285: 				int timeout = 0xff;
286: 				int result = 0xff;
287: 				while (--timeout) {
288: 					sd_send_cmd(SD_CMD16, BSIZE, 0);
289: 					result = sd_get_response_R1();
290: 					sd_end_cmd();
291: 
292: 					if (0 == result) break;
293: 				}
294: 				if (0 == timeout) {
295: 					printf("check_OCR(): fail to set block size");
296: 					return 0xff;
297: 				}
298: 
299: 				is_standard_sd = 1;
300: 			}
301: 
302: 			return 0;
303: 		}
304: 	}
305: 
306: 	// timeout! 
307: 	printf("check_OCR() timeout!\n");
308: 	printf("result = %d\n", result);
309: 	return 0xff;
310: }

314:  * @param  None
315:  * @retval The SD Response:
316:  *         - 0xFF: Sequence failed
317:  *         - 0: Sequence succeed
318:  */
319: static int sd_init(void) {
320: 	uint8 frame[10];
321: 
322: 	sd_lowlevel_init(0);
323: 	//SD_CS_HIGH();
324: 	SD_CS_LOW();
325: 
326: 	// send dummy bytes for 80 clock cycles 
327: 	for (int i = 0; i < 10; i ++) 
328: 		frame[i] = 0xff;
329: 	sd_write_data(frame, 10);
330: 
331: 	if (0 != switch_to_SPI_mode()) 
332: 		return 0xff;
333: 	if (0 != verify_operation_condition()) 
334: 		return 0xff;
335: 	if (0 != read_OCR()) 
336: 		return 0xff;
337: 	if (0 != set_SDXC_capacity()) 
338: 		return 0xff;
339: 	if (0 != check_block_size()) 
340: 		return 0xff;
341: 	SD_HIGH_SPEED_ENABLE();
342: 	return 0;
343: }
344:

346: 
347: // Protected by sdcard_lock above.
348: static struct {

匹配分数 0.709 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": false, "exact_token_fp": false, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 0.695}

354: 
355: // Protected by their own locks.
356: static struct wait_queue sd_rqueue;
357: static struct wait_queue sd_wqueue;
358: 
359: void sdcard_init(void) {
360: 	int result = sd_init();
361: 	initsleeplock(&sdcard_lock, "sdcard");
362: 	wait_queue_init(&sd_rqueue, "sdrq");
363: 	wait_queue_init(&sd_wqueue, "sdwq");
364: 	sd_status.busy = 0;
365: 	sd_status.rpending = 0;
366: 	sd_status.wpending = 0;
367: 	sd_status.wcount = 0;
368: 
369: 	if (0 != result) {
370: 		panic("sdcard_init failed");
371: 	}
372: 	#ifdef DEBUG
373: 	printf("sdcard_init\n");
374: 	#endif
375: }

521: // for interrupt handler
522: #define BUSY_READ	1
523: #define BUSY_WRITE	2
524: 
525: 
526: static void sdcard_read_wait(struct buf *b)
527: {
528: 	acquire(&sd_rqueue.lock);
529: 	// Register to read queue, add to tail.
530: 	dlist_add_before(&sd_rqueue.head, &b->list);
531: 	// whether we are the first one
532: 	while (&b->list != sd_rqueue.head.next)
533: 		// sleep on list to get wakened orderly, unlike sleeplock
534: 		sleep(&b->list, &sd_rqueue.lock);
535: 	
536: 	// watch out the sequence
537: 	acquire(&sdcard_lock.lk);
538: 	release(&sd_rqueue.lock);
539: 
540: 	// Check sdcard working status.
541: 	while (sd_status.busy) {
542: 		if (sd_status.busy != BUSY_WRITE)
543: 			panic("sdcard_read_wait: status");
544: 		// let writers see us
545: 		sd_status.rpending = 1;
546: 		sleep(&sd_status.rpending, &sdcard_lock.lk);
547: 	}
548: 	sd_status.rpending = 0;
549: 	sd_status.busy = BUSY_READ;
550: 	release(&sdcard_lock.lk);
551: }
552:

556: 	acquire(&sd_rqueue.lock);

553: 
554: static int sdcard_read_done(struct buf *b)
555: {
556: 	acquire(&sd_rqueue.lock);
557: 	if (&b->list != sd_rqueue.head.next)
558: 		panic("sdcard_read_done: wrong order");
559: 	
560: 	dlist_del(&b->list);
561: 
562: 	struct d_list *dl = sd_rqueue.head.next;
563: 	int done = 0;
564: 	// more readers?
565: 	acquire(&sdcard_lock.lk);
566: 	sd_status.busy = 0;
567: 	if (dl != &sd_rqueue.head) {
568: 		wakeup(dl);
569: 		sd_status.rpending = 1;
570: 	}
571: 	else if (sd_status.wpending)
572: 		done = 1;
573: 
574: 	release(&sd_rqueue.lock);
575: 	release(&sdcard_lock.lk);
576: 	return done;
577: }
578:

579: 
580: int sdcard_read(struct buf *b)
581: {
582: 	int ret = -EIO;
583: 	uint8 dummy_crc[2];
584: 	uint8 result = 0xff;
585: 	uint32 address = is_standard_sd ?
586: 						b->sectorno << 9 :
587: 						b->sectorno;
588: 
589: 	// wait our turn
590: 	sdcard_read_wait(b);
591: 
592: 	sd_send_cmd(SD_CMD17, address, 0);
593: 	if (sd_get_response_R1() != 0) {
594: 		printf(__ERROR("sdcard")" CMD17 fail\n");	// we'd better print it
595: 		goto end;
596: 	}
597: 
598: 	for (int timeout = 0xffff; timeout > 0 && result != 0xfe; timeout--)
599: 		sd_read_data(&result, 1);
600: 		
601: 	if (result != 0xfe) {
602: 		printf(__ERROR("sdcard")" CMD17 read fail\n");
603: 		goto end;
604: 	}
605: 
606: 	sd_read_data_dma(b->data, BSIZE);
607: 	sd_read_data(dummy_crc, 2);
608: 	ret = 0;
609: 
610: end:
611: 	sd_end_cmd();
612: 	if (sdcard_read_done(b))
613: 		sdcard_write_start();
614: 	return ret;
615: }
616:

633: 	}

677: // Caller must hold the control before calling to here
678: static int sdcard_multiple_write(struct buf *b, int nbuf)
679: {
680: 	uint32 address = is_standard_sd ?
681: 						b->sectorno << 9 :
682: 						b->sectorno;
683: 
684: 	int ret;
685: 	sd_send_cmd(SD_CMD55, 0, 0);
686: 	ret = sd_get_response_R1();
687: 	sd_end_cmd();
688: 	if (ret != 0) {
689: 		printf(__ERROR("CMD55")" error\n");
690: 		return -EIO;
691: 	}
692: 
693: 	sd_send_cmd(SD_ACMD23, nbuf, 0);
694: 	ret = sd_get_response_R1();
695: 	sd_end_cmd();
696: 	if (ret != 0) {
697: 		printf(__ERROR("ACMD23")" error\n");
698: 		return -EIO;
699: 	}
700: 
701: 	sd_send_cmd(SD_CMD25, address, 0);
702: 	if (sd_get_response_R1() != 0) {
703: 		sd_end_cmd();
704: 		printf(__ERROR("CMD25")" error\n");
705: 		return -EIO;
706: 	}
707: 	
708: 	sd_status.wcount = nbuf;
709: 	sdcard_write(b);
710: 
711: 	// see you in the interrupt handler	
712: 	return 0;
713: }

684: 						b->sectorno;
685:

689: 		ret = sd_get_response_R1();
690: 		sd_end_cmd();
691: 		if (ret != 0) {
692: 			printf(__ERROR("CMD55")" error\n");
693: 			return -EIO;
694: 		}
695: 
696: 		sd_send_cmd(SD_ACMD23, nbuf, 0);
697: 		ret = sd_get_response_R1();

700: 			printf(__ERROR("ACMD23")" error\n");

702: 		}
703: 	}
704:

709: 		return -EIO;
710: 	}
711: 	
712: 	sd_status.wcount = nbuf;
713: 	sdcard_write(b);
714: 
715: 	// see you in the interrupt handler	
716: 	return 0;
717: }

715: 
716: // clean up last write
717: static void sdcard_multiple_write_wait(void)
718: {
719: 	dmac_ch_clear_intr(DMAC_CHANNEL0, 0);
720: 	spi_send_data_dma_clean_up(SPI_DEVICE_0);
721: 
722: 	uint8 dummy_crc[2] = {0xff, 0xff};
723: 	sd_write_data(dummy_crc, 2);
724: 
725: 	// waiting sdcard programming, dma can't do this
726: 	uint8 result = 0xff;
727: 	int timeout;
728: 	for (timeout = 0xfffff; timeout >= 0 && (result & 0x1f) != 0x5; timeout--)
729: 		sd_read_data(&result, 1);
730: 	if (timeout < 0)
731: 		panic("sdcard_intr: response 1");	// really don't know what to do
732: 	
733: 	for (timeout = 0xfffff, result = 0; timeout >= 0 && result == 0; timeout--)
734: 		sd_read_data(&result, 1);
735: 	if (timeout < 0)
736: 		panic("sdcard_intr: response 2");	// really don't know what to do
737: }
738: 
739:

720: // clean up last write
721: static void sdcard_multiple_write_wait(void)
722: {
723: 	dmac_ch_clear_intr(DMAC_CHANNEL0, 0);
724: 	spi_send_data_dma_clean_up(SPI_DEVICE_0);
725: 
726: 	uint8 dummy_crc[2] = {0xff, 0xff};
727: 	sd_write_data(dummy_crc, 2);
728: 
729: 	// waiting sdcard programming, dma can't do this
730: 	uint8 result = 0xff;
731: 	int64 timeout;
732: 	for (timeout = 0xffffff; timeout >= 0 && (result & 0x1f) != 0x5; timeout--)
733: 		sd_read_data(&result, 1);
734: 	// if (timeout < 0)
735: 	// 	panic("sdcard_intr: response 1");	// really don't know what to do
736: 	
737: 	for (timeout = 0xffffff, result = 0; timeout >= 0 && result == 0; timeout--)
738: 		sd_read_data(&result, 1);
739: 	// if (timeout < 0)
740: 	// 	panic("sdcard_intr: response 2");	// really don't know what to do
741: }

746: 	uint8 token = 0xfd;		// stop token

739: 
740: static void sdcard_multiple_write_stop(void)
741: {
742: 	uint8 token = 0xfd;		// stop token
743: 	sd_write_data(&token, 1);
744: 
745: 	for (int timeout = 0xffff; timeout >= 0 && token != 0xff; timeout--)
746: 		sd_read_data(&token, 1);
747: 	
748: 	sd_end_cmd();
749: 
750: 	sd_send_cmd(SD_CMD12, 0, 0);
751: 	sd_get_response_R1();
752: 	sd_end_cmd();
753: 
754: 	// check writing result
755: 	sd_send_cmd(SD_CMD13, 0, 0);
756: 	if (sd_get_response_R1() != 0)
757: 		panic("sdcard_intr: CMD13 bad response");
758: 
759: 	uint8 error = 0xff;
760: 	sd_read_data(&error, 1);
761: 	sd_end_cmd();
762: 	if (error) {
763: 		printf(__ERROR("sdcard_intr")" write error %x\n", error);
764: 		panic("sdcard_intr");
765: 	}
766: }
767:

770: {
771: 	acquire(&sd_wqueue.lock);

744: static void sdcard_multiple_write_stop(void)
745: {
746: 	uint8 token = 0xfd;		// stop token
747: 	sd_write_data(&token, 1);
748: 
749: 	for (int timeout = 0xffff; timeout >= 0 && token != 0xff; timeout--)
750: 		sd_read_data(&token, 1);
751: 	
752: 	sd_end_cmd();
753: 
754: 	sd_send_cmd(SD_CMD12, 0, 0);
755: 	sd_get_response_R1();
756: 	sd_end_cmd();
757: 
758: 	// check writing result
759: 	sd_send_cmd(SD_CMD13, 0, 0);
760: 	if (sd_get_response_R1() != 0)
761: 		panic("sdcard_intr: CMD13 bad response");
762: 
763: 	uint8 error = 0xff;
764: 	sd_read_data(&error, 1);
765: 	sd_end_cmd();
766: 	if (error) {
767: 		printf(__ERROR("sdcard_intr")" write error %x\n", error);
768: 		panic("sdcard_intr");
769: 	}
770: }

767: 
768: 
769: void sdcard_write_start(void)
770: {
771: 	acquire(&sd_wqueue.lock);
772: 
773: 	struct d_list *dl = sd_wqueue.head.next;
774: 	// is the queue empty?
775: 	if (dl == &sd_wqueue.head) {
776: 		release(&sd_wqueue.lock);
777: 		return;
778: 	}
779: 
780: 	acquire(&sdcard_lock.lk);
781: 	// check sdcard status
782: 	if (sd_status.busy || sd_status.rpending) {
783: 		/**
784: 		 * If we enter here where busy == 0 and rpending is non-zero,
785: 		 * it means this rpending is set in sdcard_read_done(), where
786: 		 * the proc there will waken the next reader. Thus, we don't
787: 		 * need to do a wake up.
788: 		 */
789: 		sd_status.wpending = 1;
790: 		release(&sdcard_lock.lk);
791: 		release(&sd_wqueue.lock);
792: 		return;
793: 	}
794: 
795: 	// we get the control now
796: 	sd_status.busy = BUSY_WRITE;
797: 	release(&sdcard_lock.lk);
798: 
799: 	struct buf *b = container_of(dl, struct buf, list);
800: 	int nbuf = 1;
801: 
802: 	uint32 sec = b->sectorno;
803: 	for (dl = dl->next; dl != &sd_wqueue.head; dl = dl->next) {
804: 		struct buf *next = container_of(dl, struct buf, list);
805: 		if (next->sectorno == ++sec)	// consecutive sector
806: 			nbuf++;
807: 		else
808: 			break;
809: 	}
810: 	
811: 	b->disk = 1;
812: 	b->dirty = 0;
813: 	release(&sd_wqueue.lock);
814: 
815: 	if (sdcard_multiple_write(b, nbuf) < 0) {
816: 		// unset status
817: 		acquire(&sd_wqueue.lock);
818: 		b->disk = 0;
819: 		b->dirty = 1;
820: 
821: 		acquire(&sdcard_lock.lk);
822: 		release(&sd_wqueue.lock);
823: 
824: 		sd_status.busy = 0;
825: 		sd_status.wpending = 1;
826: 		if (sd_status.rpending)
827: 			/**
828: 			 * We enter here because rpending is non-zero.
829: 			 * But this case is different, since the busy
830: 			 * flag was set by us. So this rpending is set
831: 			 * in sdcard_read(), where there is a reader
832: 			 * waiting for the control, and only us can
833: 			 * wake him up.
834: 			 */
835: 			wakeup(&sd_status.rpending);
836: 		release(&sdcard_lock.lk);
837: 	}
838: 
839: }
840: 
841: 
842: int sdcard_submit(struct buf *b)
843: {
844: 	int res = 0;

xv6-k210@final / 来源候选源码

770: }

772: #define	WRITE_SHRESHOLD	512
773: 
774: void sdcard_write_start(void)
775: {
776: 	acquire(&sd_wqueue.lock);
777: 
778: 	struct d_list *dl = sd_wqueue.head.next;
779: 	// is the queue empty?
780: 	if (dl == &sd_wqueue.head || sd_wqueue_num < WRITE_SHRESHOLD) {
781: 		release(&sd_wqueue.lock);
782: 		return;
783: 	}
784: 
785: 	acquire(&sdcard_lock.lk);
786: 	// check sdcard status
787: 	if (sd_status.busy || sd_status.rpending) {
788: 		/**
789: 		 * If we enter here where busy == 0 and rpending is non-zero,
790: 		 * it means this rpending is set in sdcard_read_done(), where
791: 		 * the proc there will waken the next reader. Thus, we don't
792: 		 * need to do a wake up.
793: 		 */
794: 		sd_status.wpending = 1;
795: 		release(&sdcard_lock.lk);
796: 		release(&sd_wqueue.lock);
797: 		return;
798: 	}
799: 
800: 	// we get the control now
801: 	sd_status.busy = BUSY_WRITE;
802: 	release(&sdcard_lock.lk);
803: 
804: 	struct buf *b = container_of(dl, struct buf, list);
805: 	int nbuf = 1;
806: 
807: 	#define __NBUF_WRITE	32
808: 
809: 	uint32 sec = b->sectorno;
810: 	for (dl = dl->next; dl != &sd_wqueue.head && nbuf < __NBUF_WRITE; dl = dl->next) {
811: 		struct buf *next = container_of(dl, struct buf, list);
812: 		if (next->sectorno == ++sec)	// consecutive sector
813: 			nbuf++;
814: 		else
815: 			break;
816: 	}
817: 	
818: 	b->disk = 1;
819: 	b->dirty = 0;
820: 	release(&sd_wqueue.lock);
821: 
822: 	if (sdcard_multiple_write(b, nbuf) < 0) {
823: 		// unset status
824: 		acquire(&sd_wqueue.lock);
825: 		b->disk = 0;
826: 		b->dirty = 1;
827: 
828: 		acquire(&sdcard_lock.lk);
829: 		release(&sd_wqueue.lock);
830: 
831: 		sd_status.busy = 0;
832: 		sd_status.wpending = 1;
833: 		if (sd_status.rpending)
834: 			/**
835: 			 * We enter here because rpending is non-zero.
836: 			 * But this case is different, since the busy
837: 			 * flag was set by us. So this rpending is set
838: 			 * in sdcard_read(), where there is a reader
839: 			 * waiting for the control, and only us can
840: 			 * wake him up.
841: 			 */
842: 			wakeup(&sd_status.rpending);
843: 		release(&sdcard_lock.lk);
844: 	}
845: 
846: }
847: 
848: 
849: int sdcard_submit(struct buf *b)
850: {
851: 	int res = 0;

匹配分数 0.676 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": false, "exact_token_fp": false, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 0.586}

841: 
842: int sdcard_submit(struct buf *b)
843: {
844: 	int res = 0;
845: 	acquire(&sd_wqueue.lock);
846: 
847: 	if (b->dirty == 0 && b->disk == 0) {
848: 		dlist_add_before(&sd_wqueue.head, &b->list);
849: 		res = 0x1;
850: 	}
851: 	b->dirty = 1;
852: 
853: 	acquire(&sdcard_lock.lk);
854: 	release(&sd_wqueue.lock);
855: 	sd_status.wpending = 1;
856: 	if (sd_status.busy)	// tell the caller that transmission is on
857: 		res |= 0x2;
858: 	release(&sdcard_lock.lk);
859: 	return res;
860: }
861:

849: int sdcard_submit(struct buf *b)
850: {
851: 	int res = 0;
852: 	acquire(&sd_wqueue.lock);
853: 
854: 	if (b->dirty == 0 && b->disk == 0) {
855: 		dlist_add_before(&sd_wqueue.head, &b->list);
856: 		res = 0x1;
857: 		sd_wqueue_num++;
858: 	}
859: 	b->dirty = 1;
860: 
861: 	acquire(&sdcard_lock.lk);
862: 	release(&sd_wqueue.lock);
863: 	sd_status.wpending = 1;
864: 	if (sd_status.busy)	// tell the caller that transmission is on
865: 		res |= 0x2;
866: 	release(&sdcard_lock.lk);
867: 	return res;
868: }

862: 
863: // Called by dmac_intr(), if it is a read done,
864: // tell him to wake up the relative proc.
865: void sdcard_intr(void)
866: {
867: 	// acquire(&sdcard_lock.lk);
868: 	if (sd_status.busy == BUSY_READ) {
869: 		// release(&sdcard_lock.lk);
870: 		dmac_ch_clear_intr(DMAC_CHANNEL0, 1);
871: 		// reader is waiting out there
872: 		// him will do the clean work himself
873: 		return;
874: 	} else if (sd_status.busy != BUSY_WRITE)
875: 		panic("sdcard_intr: unknown busy");
876: 	
877: 	// but writer doesn't wait, so we need to clean up for him
878: 	// release(&sdcard_lock.lk);
879: 
880: 	// waiting for programming
881: 	sdcard_multiple_write_wait();
882: 
883: 	acquire(&sd_wqueue.lock);
884: 	struct d_list *dl = sd_wqueue.head.next;
885: 	struct buf *b = container_of(dl, struct buf, list);
886: 	struct buf *bnext;
887: 	int redo = 0;
888: 	
889: 	dlist_del(dl);
890: 	b->disk = 0;
891: 	// whether some one wrote this buf when we transmitting it
892: 	if (b->dirty) {
893: 		redo = 1;
894: 		dlist_add_before(&sd_wqueue.head, dl);
895: 	}
896: 
897: 	dl = sd_wqueue.head.next;
898: 	bnext = (dl == &sd_wqueue.head) ? NULL :
899: 			container_of(dl, struct buf, list);
900: 	
901: 	if (--sd_status.wcount > 0) {
902: 		if (bnext == NULL || bnext->sectorno != b->sectorno + 1) {
903: 			sdcard_multiple_write_stop();
904: 			panic("sdcard_intr: bad sd_status.wcount");
905: 		}
906: 		bnext->dirty = 0;
907: 		bnext->disk = 1;
908: 		release(&sd_wqueue.lock);
909: 		sdcard_write(bnext);
910: 		goto out;
911: 	}
912: 	else if (sd_status.rpending || bnext == NULL) {	// race read
913: 		release(&sd_wqueue.lock);
914: 		sdcard_multiple_write_stop();
915: 	}
916: 	else {
917: 		int nbuf = 1;
918: 		uint32 sec = bnext->sectorno;
919: 
920: 		// preview the consecutive count
921: 		for (dl = dl->next; dl != &sd_wqueue.head; dl = dl->next) {
922: 			struct buf *next = container_of(dl, struct buf, list);
923: 			if (next->sectorno == ++sec)	// consecutive sector
924: 				nbuf++;
925: 			else
926: 				break;
927: 		}
928: 
929: 		bnext->dirty = 0;
930: 		bnext->disk = 1;
931: 		release(&sd_wqueue.lock);
932: 		// printf("nbuf = %d\n", nbuf);
933: 		if (nbuf <= 2 && bnext->sectorno == b->sectorno + 1) {
934: 			sd_status.wcount = nbuf;
935: 			sdcard_write(bnext);
936: 		} else {
937: 			sdcard_multiple_write_stop();
938: 
939: 			if (sdcard_multiple_write(bnext, nbuf) < 0) {
940: 				// unset status
941: 				acquire(&sd_wqueue.lock);
942: 				bnext->dirty = 1;
943: 				bnext->disk = 0;
944: 				release(&sd_wqueue.lock);
945: 				goto stop_out;
946: 			}
947: 		}
948: 		goto out;
949: 	}
950: 
951: stop_out:
952: 	acquire(&sdcard_lock.lk);
953: 	sd_status.busy = 0;
954: 	if (sd_status.rpending)
955: 		wakeup(&sd_status.rpending);
956: 	if (bnext)
957: 		sd_status.wpending = 1;
958: 	release(&sdcard_lock.lk);
959: 
960: out:
961: 	if (!redo)
962: 		bput(b);
963: }
964: 
965: int sdcard_wqueue_empty(void)
966: {
967: 	// race test
968: 	return sd_wqueue.head.next == &sd_wqueue.head &&

871: // Called by dmac_intr(), if it is a read done,
872: // tell him to wake up the relative proc.
873: void sdcard_intr(void)
874: {
875: 	// acquire(&sdcard_lock.lk);
876: 	if (sd_status.busy == BUSY_READ) {
877: 		// release(&sdcard_lock.lk);
878: 		dmac_ch_clear_intr(DMAC_CHANNEL0, 1);
879: 		// reader is waiting out there
880: 		// him will do the clean work himself
881: 		return;
882: 	} else if (sd_status.busy != BUSY_WRITE)
883: 		panic("sdcard_intr: unknown busy");
884: 	
885: 	// but writer doesn't wait, so we need to clean up for him
886: 	// release(&sdcard_lock.lk);
887: 
888: 	// waiting for programming
889: 	sdcard_multiple_write_wait();
890: 
891: 	acquire(&sd_wqueue.lock);
892: 	struct d_list *dl = sd_wqueue.head.next;
893: 	struct buf *b = container_of(dl, struct buf, list);
894: 	struct buf *bnext;
895: 	int redo = 0;
896: 	
897: 	dlist_del(dl);
898: 	b->disk = 0;
899: 	// whether some one wrote this buf when we transmitting it
900: 	if (b->dirty) {
901: 		redo = 1;
902: 		dlist_add_before(&sd_wqueue.head, dl);
903: 	} else {
904: 		sd_wqueue_num--;
905: 	}
906: 
907: 	dl = sd_wqueue.head.next;
908: 	bnext = (dl == &sd_wqueue.head) ? NULL :
909: 			container_of(dl, struct buf, list);
910: 	
911: 	if (--sd_status.wcount > 0) {
912: 		// if (bnext == NULL || bnext->sectorno != b->sectorno + 1) {
913: 		// 	sdcard_multiple_write_stop();
914: 		// 	panic("sdcard_intr: bad sd_status.wcount");
915: 		// }
916: 		bnext->dirty = 0;
917: 		bnext->disk = 1;
918: 		release(&sd_wqueue.lock);
919: 		sdcard_write(bnext);
920: 		goto out;
921: 	}
922: 	else if (sd_status.rpending || bnext == NULL ||
923: 			sd_wqueue_num < WRITE_SHRESHOLD / 4)
924: 	{	// race read
925: 		release(&sd_wqueue.lock);
926: 		sdcard_multiple_write_stop();
927: 	}
928: 	else {
929: 		int nbuf = 1;
930: 		uint32 sec = bnext->sectorno;
931: 
932: 		// preview the consecutive count
933: 		for (dl = dl->next; dl != &sd_wqueue.head && nbuf < __NBUF_WRITE; dl = dl->next) {
934: 			struct buf *next = container_of(dl, struct buf, list);
935: 			if (next->sectorno == ++sec)	// consecutive sector
936: 				nbuf++;
937: 			else
938: 				break;
939: 		}
940: 
941: 		bnext->dirty = 0;
942: 		bnext->disk = 1;
943: 		release(&sd_wqueue.lock);
944: 		// printf("nbuf = %d\n", nbuf);
945: 		if (nbuf < 16 && bnext->sectorno == b->sectorno + 1) {
946: 			sd_status.wcount = nbuf;
947: 			sdcard_write(bnext);
948: 		} else {
949: 			sdcard_multiple_write_stop();
950: 
951: 			if (sdcard_multiple_write(bnext, nbuf) < 0) {
952: 				// unset status
953: 				acquire(&sd_wqueue.lock);
954: 				bnext->dirty = 1;
955: 				bnext->disk = 0;
956: 				release(&sd_wqueue.lock);
957: 				goto stop_out;
958: 			}
959: 		}
960: 		goto out;
961: 	}
962: 
963: stop_out:
964: 	acquire(&sdcard_lock.lk);
965: 	sd_status.busy = 0;
966: 	if (sd_status.rpending)
967: 		wakeup(&sd_status.rpending);
968: 	if (bnext)
969: 		sd_status.wpending = 1;
970: 	release(&sdcard_lock.lk);
971: 
972: out:
973: 	if (!redo)
974: 		bput(b);
975: }

20: /* Since sdcard module keeps a lock, we don't need to keep another one at present. */
21: #define SPI_BUF_SIZE (16)
22: static uint8 __attribute__ ((aligned (sizeof(uint32)))) __spi_buf[SPI_BUF_SIZE];
23: static void *spi_buf = (void*)__spi_buf;
24: 
25: void spi_init(spi_device_num_t spi_num, spi_work_mode_t work_mode, spi_frame_format_t frame_format,
26: 			  uint64 data_bit_length, uint32 endian)
27: {
28: 	// configASSERT(data_bit_length >= 4 && data_bit_length <= 32);
29: 	// configASSERT(spi_num < SPI_DEVICE_MAX && spi_num != 2);
30: 	// spi_clk_init(spi_num);
31: 
32: 	// uint8 dfs_offset, frf_offset, work_mode_offset;
33: 	uint8 dfs_offset = 0;
34: 	uint8 frf_offset = 0;
35: 	uint8 work_mode_offset = 0;
36: 	switch(spi_num)
37: 	{
38: 		case 0:
39: 		case 1:
40: 			dfs_offset = 16;
41: 			frf_offset = 21;
42: 			work_mode_offset = 6;
43: 			break;
44: 		case 2:
45: 			// configASSERT(!"Spi Bus 2 Not Support!");
46: 			break;
47: 		case 3:
48: 		default:
49: 			dfs_offset = 0;
50: 			frf_offset = 22;
51: 			work_mode_offset = 8;
52: 			break;
53: 	}
54: 
55: 	switch(frame_format)
56: 	{
57: 		case SPI_FF_DUAL:
58: 			// configASSERT(data_bit_length % 2 == 0);
59: 			break;
60: 		case SPI_FF_QUAD:
61: 			// configASSERT(data_bit_length % 4 == 0);
62: 			break;
63: 		case SPI_FF_OCTAL:
64: 			// configASSERT(data_bit_length % 8 == 0);
65: 			break;
66: 		default:
67: 			break;
68: 	}
69: 	volatile spi_t *spi_adapter = spi[spi_num];
70: 	if(spi_adapter->baudr == 0)
71: 		spi_adapter->baudr = 0x14;
72: 	spi_adapter->imr = 0x00;
73: 	spi_adapter->dmacr = 0x00;
74: 	spi_adapter->dmatdlr = 0x10;
75: 	spi_adapter->dmardlr = 0x00;
76: 	spi_adapter->ser = 0x00;
77: 	spi_adapter->ssienr = 0x00;
78: 	spi_adapter->ctrlr0 = (work_mode << work_mode_offset) | (frame_format << frf_offset) | ((data_bit_length - 1) << dfs_offset);
79: 	spi_adapter->spi_ctrlr0 = 0;
80: 	spi_adapter->endian = endian;
81: }
82:

85: {

84: static void spi_set_tmod(uint8 spi_num, uint32 tmod)
85: {
86: 	// configASSERT(spi_num < SPI_DEVICE_MAX);
87: 	volatile spi_t *spi_handle = spi[spi_num];
88: 	uint8 tmod_offset = 0;
89: 	switch(spi_num)
90: 	{
91: 		case 0:
92: 		case 1:
93: 		case 2:
94: 			tmod_offset = 8;
95: 			break;
96: 		case 3:
97: 		default:
98: 			tmod_offset = 10;
99: 			break;
100: 	}
101: 	set_bit(&spi_handle->ctrlr0, 3 << tmod_offset, tmod << tmod_offset);
102: }

105: {
106: 	if(data_bit_length < 8)
107: 		return SPI_TRANS_CHAR;

104: static spi_transfer_width_t spi_get_frame_size(uint64 data_bit_length)
105: {
106: 	if(data_bit_length < 8)
107: 		return SPI_TRANS_CHAR;

109: 		return SPI_TRANS_SHORT;
110: 	return SPI_TRANS_INT;

112: 
113: void spi_send_data_normal(spi_device_num_t spi_num, spi_chip_select_t chip_select, const uint8 *tx_buff, uint64 tx_len)
114: {
115: 	// configASSERT(spi_num < SPI_DEVICE_MAX && spi_num != 2);
116: 
117: 	uint64 index, fifo_len;
118: 	spi_set_tmod(spi_num, SPI_TMOD_TRANS);
119: 
120: 	volatile spi_t *spi_handle = spi[spi_num];
121: 
122: 	// uint8 dfs_offset;
123: 	uint8 dfs_offset = 0;
124: 	switch(spi_num)
125: 	{
126: 		case 0:
127: 		case 1:
128: 			dfs_offset = 16;
129: 			break;
130: 		case 2:
131: 			// configASSERT(!"Spi Bus 2 Not Support!");
132: 			break;
133: 		case 3:
134: 		default:
135: 			dfs_offset = 0;
136: 			break;
137: 	}
138: 	uint32 data_bit_length = (spi_handle->ctrlr0 >> dfs_offset) & 0x1F;
139: 	spi_transfer_width_t frame_width = spi_get_frame_size(data_bit_length);
140: 
141: 	uint8 v_misalign_flag = 0;
142: 	uint32 v_send_data;
143: 	if((uintptr_t)tx_buff % frame_width)
144: 		v_misalign_flag = 1;
145: 
146: 	spi_handle->ssienr = 0x01;
147: 	spi_handle->ser = 1U << chip_select;
148: 	uint32 i = 0;
149: 	while(tx_len)
150: 	{
151: 		fifo_len = 32 - spi_handle->txflr;
152: 		fifo_len = fifo_len < tx_len ? fifo_len : tx_len;
153: 		switch(frame_width)
154: 		{
155: 			case SPI_TRANS_INT:
156: 				fifo_len = fifo_len / 4 * 4;
157: 				if(v_misalign_flag)
158: 				{
159: 					for(index = 0; index < fifo_len; index += 4)
160: 					{
161: 						// memcpy(&v_send_data, tx_buff + i, 4);
162: 						memmove(&v_send_data, tx_buff + i, 4);
163: 						spi_handle->dr[0] = v_send_data;
164: 						i += 4;
165: 					}
166: 				} else
167: 				{
168: 					for(index = 0; index < fifo_len / 4; index++)
169: 						spi_handle->dr[0] = ((uint32 *)tx_buff)[i++];
170: 				}
171: 				break;
172: 			case SPI_TRANS_SHORT:
173: 				fifo_len = fifo_len / 2 * 2;
174: 				if(v_misalign_flag)
175: 				{
176: 					for(index = 0; index < fifo_len; index += 2)
177: 					{
178: 						// memcpy(&v_send_data, tx_buff + i, 2);
179: 						memmove(&v_send_data, tx_buff + i, 2);
180: 						spi_handle->dr[0] = v_send_data;
181: 						i += 2;
182: 					}
183: 				} else
184: 				{
185: 					for(index = 0; index < fifo_len / 2; index++)
186: 						spi_handle->dr[0] = ((uint16 *)tx_buff)[i++];
187: 				}
188: 				break;
189: 			default:
190: 				for(index = 0; index < fifo_len; index++)
191: 					spi_handle->dr[0] = tx_buff[i++];
192: 				break;
193: 		}
194: 		tx_len -= fifo_len;
195: 	}
196: 	while((spi_handle->sr & 0x05) != 0x04)
197: 		;
198: 	spi_handle->ser = 0x00;
199: 	spi_handle->ssienr = 0x00;
200: }
201:

203: 							uint64 cmd_len, const uint8 *tx_buff, uint64 tx_len)
204: {

113: void spi_send_data_normal(spi_device_num_t spi_num, spi_chip_select_t chip_select, const uint8 *tx_buff, uint64 tx_len)
114: {
115: 	// configASSERT(spi_num < SPI_DEVICE_MAX && spi_num != 2);
116: 
117: 	uint64 index, fifo_len;
118: 	spi_set_tmod(spi_num, SPI_TMOD_TRANS);
119: 
120: 	volatile spi_t *spi_handle = spi[spi_num];
121: 
122: 	// uint8 dfs_offset;
123: 	uint8 dfs_offset = 0;
124: 	switch(spi_num)
125: 	{
126: 		case 0:
127: 		case 1:
128: 			dfs_offset = 16;
129: 			break;
130: 		case 2:
131: 			// configASSERT(!"Spi Bus 2 Not Support!");
132: 			break;
133: 		case 3:
134: 		default:
135: 			dfs_offset = 0;
136: 			break;
137: 	}
138: 	uint32 data_bit_length = (spi_handle->ctrlr0 >> dfs_offset) & 0x1F;
139: 	spi_transfer_width_t frame_width = spi_get_frame_size(data_bit_length);
140: 
141: 	uint8 v_misalign_flag = 0;
142: 	uint32 v_send_data;
143: 	if((uintptr_t)tx_buff % frame_width)
144: 		v_misalign_flag = 1;
145: 
146: 	spi_handle->ssienr = 0x01;
147: 	spi_handle->ser = 1U << chip_select;
148: 	uint32 i = 0;
149: 	while(tx_len)
150: 	{
151: 		fifo_len = 32 - spi_handle->txflr;
152: 		fifo_len = fifo_len < tx_len ? fifo_len : tx_len;
153: 		switch(frame_width)
154: 		{
155: 			case SPI_TRANS_INT:
156: 				fifo_len = fifo_len / 4 * 4;
157: 				if(v_misalign_flag)
158: 				{
159: 					for(index = 0; index < fifo_len; index += 4)
160: 					{
161: 						// memcpy(&v_send_data, tx_buff + i, 4);
162: 						memmove(&v_send_data, tx_buff + i, 4);
163: 						spi_handle->dr[0] = v_send_data;
164: 						i += 4;
165: 					}
166: 				} else
167: 				{
168: 					for(index = 0; index < fifo_len / 4; index++)
169: 						spi_handle->dr[0] = ((uint32 *)tx_buff)[i++];
170: 				}
171: 				break;
172: 			case SPI_TRANS_SHORT:
173: 				fifo_len = fifo_len / 2 * 2;
174: 				if(v_misalign_flag)
175: 				{
176: 					for(index = 0; index < fifo_len; index += 2)
177: 					{
178: 						// memcpy(&v_send_data, tx_buff + i, 2);
179: 						memmove(&v_send_data, tx_buff + i, 2);
180: 						spi_handle->dr[0] = v_send_data;
181: 						i += 2;
182: 					}
183: 				} else
184: 				{
185: 					for(index = 0; index < fifo_len / 2; index++)
186: 						spi_handle->dr[0] = ((uint16 *)tx_buff)[i++];
187: 				}
188: 				break;
189: 			default:
190: 				for(index = 0; index < fifo_len; index++)
191: 					spi_handle->dr[0] = tx_buff[i++];
192: 				break;
193: 		}
194: 		tx_len -= fifo_len;
195: 	}
196: 	while((spi_handle->sr & 0x05) != 0x04)
197: 		;
198: 	spi_handle->ser = 0x00;
199: 	spi_handle->ssienr = 0x00;
200: }

201: 
202: void spi_send_data_standard(spi_device_num_t spi_num, spi_chip_select_t chip_select, const uint8 *cmd_buff,
203: 							uint64 cmd_len, const uint8 *tx_buff, uint64 tx_len)
204: {
205: 	// configASSERT(spi_num < SPI_DEVICE_MAX && spi_num != 2);
206: 	// uint8 *v_buf = malloc(cmd_len + tx_len);
207: 	if (SPI_BUF_SIZE < cmd_len + tx_len)
208: 		panic("spi: spi_buf too small");
209: 	uint8 *v_buf = spi_buf;
210: 	uint64 i;
211: 	for(i = 0; i < cmd_len; i++)
212: 		v_buf[i] = cmd_buff[i];
213: 	for(i = 0; i < tx_len; i++)
214: 		v_buf[cmd_len + i] = tx_buff[i];
215: 
216: 	spi_send_data_normal(spi_num, chip_select, v_buf, cmd_len + tx_len);
217: 	// free((void *)v_buf);
218: 	// freepage((void *)v_buf);
219: }
220:

203: 							uint64 cmd_len, const uint8 *tx_buff, uint64 tx_len)
204: {
205: 	// configASSERT(spi_num < SPI_DEVICE_MAX && spi_num != 2);
206: 	// uint8 *v_buf = malloc(cmd_len + tx_len);
207: 	// if (SPI_BUF_SIZE < cmd_len + tx_len)
208: 	// 	panic("spi: spi_buf too small");

212: 		v_buf[i] = cmd_buff[i];

214: 		v_buf[cmd_len + i] = tx_buff[i];
215:

220: 
221: void spi_receive_data_standard(spi_device_num_t spi_num, spi_chip_select_t chip_select, const uint8 *cmd_buff,
222: 							   uint64 cmd_len, uint8 *rx_buff, uint64 rx_len)
223: {
224: 	// configASSERT(spi_num < SPI_DEVICE_MAX && spi_num != 2);
225: 	uint64 index, fifo_len;
226: 	if(cmd_len == 0)
227: 		spi_set_tmod(spi_num, SPI_TMOD_RECV);
228: 	else
229: 		spi_set_tmod(spi_num, SPI_TMOD_EEROM);
230: 	volatile spi_t *spi_handle = spi[spi_num];
231: 
232: 	// uint8 dfs_offset;
233: 	uint8 dfs_offset = 0;
234: 	switch(spi_num)
235: 	{
236: 		case 0:
237: 		case 1:
238: 			dfs_offset = 16;
239: 			break;
240: 		case 2:
241: 			// configASSERT(!"Spi Bus 2 Not Support!");
242: 			break;
243: 		case 3:
244: 		default:
245: 			dfs_offset = 0;
246: 			break;
247: 	}
248: 	uint32 data_bit_length = (spi_handle->ctrlr0 >> dfs_offset) & 0x1F;
249: 	spi_transfer_width_t frame_width = spi_get_frame_size(data_bit_length);
250: 
251: 	uint32 i = 0;
252: 	uint64 v_cmd_len = cmd_len / frame_width;
253: 	uint32 v_rx_len = rx_len / frame_width;
254: 
255: 	spi_handle->ctrlr1 = (uint32)(v_rx_len - 1);
256: 	spi_handle->ssienr = 0x01;
257: 
258: 	while(v_cmd_len)
259: 	{
260: 		fifo_len = 32 - spi_handle->txflr;
261: 		fifo_len = fifo_len < v_cmd_len ? fifo_len : v_cmd_len;
262: 		switch(frame_width)
263: 		{
264: 			case SPI_TRANS_INT:
265: 				for(index = 0; index < fifo_len; index++)
266: 					spi_handle->dr[0] = ((uint32 *)cmd_buff)[i++];
267: 				break;
268: 			case SPI_TRANS_SHORT:
269: 				for(index = 0; index < fifo_len; index++)
270: 					spi_handle->dr[0] = ((uint16 *)cmd_buff)[i++];
271: 				break;
272: 			default:
273: 				for(index = 0; index < fifo_len; index++)
274: 					spi_handle->dr[0] = cmd_buff[i++];
275: 				break;
276: 		}
277: 		spi_handle->ser = 1U << chip_select;
278: 		v_cmd_len -= fifo_len;
279: 	}
280: 
281: 	if(cmd_len == 0)
282: 	{
283: 		spi_handle->dr[0] = 0xffffffff;
284: 		spi_handle->ser = 1U << chip_select;
285: 	}
286: 
287: 	i = 0;
288: 	while(v_rx_len)
289: 	{
290: 		fifo_len = spi_handle->rxflr;
291: 		fifo_len = fifo_len < v_rx_len ? fifo_len : v_rx_len;
292: 		switch(frame_width)
293: 		{
294: 			case SPI_TRANS_INT:
295: 				for(index = 0; index < fifo_len; index++)
296: 					((uint32 *)rx_buff)[i++] = spi_handle->dr[0];
297: 				break;
298: 			case SPI_TRANS_SHORT:
299: 				for(index = 0; index < fifo_len; index++)
300: 					((uint16 *)rx_buff)[i++] = (uint16)spi_handle->dr[0];
301: 				break;
302: 			default:
303: 				for(index = 0; index < fifo_len; index++)
304: 					rx_buff[i++] = (uint8)spi_handle->dr[0];
305: 				break;
306: 		}
307: 
308: 		v_rx_len -= fifo_len;
309: 	}
310: 
311: 	spi_handle->ser = 0x00;
312: 	spi_handle->ssienr = 0x00;
313: }
314: 
315: static spi_frame_format_t spi_get_frame_format(spi_device_num_t spi_num)
316: {
317: 	uint8 frf_offset = 0;
318: 	switch(spi_num)

xv6-k210@final / 来源候选源码

216: 	spi_send_data_normal(spi_num, chip_select, v_buf, cmd_len + tx_len);
217: 	// free((void *)v_buf);
218: 	// freepage((void *)v_buf);
219: }

239: 			break;
240: 		case 2:

242: 			break;

250: 
251: 	uint32 i = 0;
252: 	uint64 v_cmd_len = cmd_len / frame_width;
253: 	uint32 v_rx_len = rx_len / frame_width;
254: 
255: 	spi_handle->ctrlr1 = (uint32)(v_rx_len - 1);
256: 	spi_handle->ssienr = 0x01;
257: 
258: 	while(v_cmd_len)
259: 	{
260: 		fifo_len = 32 - spi_handle->txflr;
261: 		fifo_len = fifo_len < v_cmd_len ? fifo_len : v_cmd_len;
262: 		switch(frame_width)
263: 		{
264: 			case SPI_TRANS_INT:
265: 				for(index = 0; index < fifo_len; index++)
266: 					spi_handle->dr[0] = ((uint32 *)cmd_buff)[i++];
267: 				break;
268: 			case SPI_TRANS_SHORT:
269: 				for(index = 0; index < fifo_len; index++)

277: 		spi_handle->ser = 1U << chip_select;
278: 		v_cmd_len -= fifo_len;
279: 	}
280: 
281: 	if(cmd_len == 0)
282: 	{
283: 		spi_handle->dr[0] = 0xffffffff;
284: 		spi_handle->ser = 1U << chip_select;
285: 	}
286: 
287: 	i = 0;
288: 	while(v_rx_len)
289: 	{
290: 		fifo_len = spi_handle->rxflr;
291: 		fifo_len = fifo_len < v_rx_len ? fifo_len : v_rx_len;
292: 		switch(frame_width)
293: 		{
294: 			case SPI_TRANS_INT:
295: 				for(index = 0; index < fifo_len; index++)
296: 					((uint32 *)rx_buff)[i++] = spi_handle->dr[0];
297: 				break;
298: 			case SPI_TRANS_SHORT:
299: 				for(index = 0; index < fifo_len; index++)
300: 					((uint16 *)rx_buff)[i++] = (uint16)spi_handle->dr[0];
301: 				break;
302: 			default:
303: 				for(index = 0; index < fifo_len; index++)
304: 					rx_buff[i++] = (uint8)spi_handle->dr[0];
305: 				break;
306: 		}
307: 
308: 		v_rx_len -= fifo_len;
309: 	}
310: 
311: 	spi_handle->ser = 0x00;
312: 	spi_handle->ssienr = 0x00;
313: }

315: static spi_frame_format_t spi_get_frame_format(spi_device_num_t spi_num)
316: {
317: 	uint8 frf_offset = 0;

577: void spi_receive_data_no_cmd_dma(dmac_channel_number_t dma_receive_channel_num,
578: 								spi_device_num_t spi_num, spi_chip_select_t chip_select,
579: 								uint8 *rx_buff, uint64 rx_len)
580: {
581: 	volatile spi_t *spi_handle = spi[spi_num];
582: 
583: 	spi_set_tmod(spi_num, SPI_TMOD_RECV);
584: 
585: 	spi_handle->ctrlr1 = (uint32)(rx_len - 1);
586: 	spi_handle->dmacr = 0x3;
587: 	spi_handle->ssienr = 0x01;
588: 
589: 	sysctl_dma_select((sysctl_dma_channel_t)dma_receive_channel_num, SYSCTL_DMA_SELECT_SSI0_RX_REQ + spi_num * 2);
590: 	
591: 	dmac_set_single_mode(dma_receive_channel_num, (void *)(&spi_handle->dr[0]), rx_buff,
592: 						DMAC_ADDR_NOCHANGE, DMAC_ADDR_INCREMENT,
593: 						DMAC_MSIZE_1, DMAC_TRANS_WIDTH_32, rx_len);
594: 
595: 	if (spi_get_frame_format(spi_num) == SPI_FF_STANDARD)
596: 		spi[spi_num]->dr[0] = 0xffffffff;
597: 
598: 	spi_handle->ser = 1U << chip_select;
599: 	dmac_wait_done(dma_receive_channel_num);
600: 
601: 	spi_handle->ser = 0x00;
602: 	spi_handle->ssienr = 0x00;
603: }

605: void spi_send_data_no_cmd_dma(dmac_channel_number_t channel_num,
606: 							spi_device_num_t spi_num, spi_chip_select_t chip_select,
607: 							uint8 const *tx_buff, uint64 tx_len, int wait)

609: 	volatile spi_t *spi_handle = spi[spi_num];
610: 
611: 	spi_set_tmod(spi_num, SPI_TMOD_TRANS);
612: 	spi_handle->dmacr = 0x2;
613: 	spi_handle->ssienr = 0x01;
614: 
615: 	sysctl_dma_select((sysctl_dma_channel_t)channel_num, SYSCTL_DMA_SELECT_SSI0_TX_REQ + spi_num * 2);
616: 	dmac_set_single_mode(channel_num, tx_buff, (void *)(&spi_handle->dr[0]), DMAC_ADDR_INCREMENT, DMAC_ADDR_NOCHANGE,
617: 						 DMAC_MSIZE_4, DMAC_TRANS_WIDTH_32, tx_len);
618: 	spi_handle->ser = 1U << chip_select;

621: 		dmac_wait_done(channel_num);
622:

624: 			;
625: 		spi_handle->ser = 0x00;
626: 		spi_handle->ssienr = 0x00;
627: 	}
628: }

630: void spi_send_data_dma_clean_up(spi_device_num_t spi_num)

51: uint32 sysctl_get_freq(void)
52: {
53: 	return sysctl->clk_freq.clk_freq;
54: }
55: 
56: static int sysctl_clock_bus_en(sysctl_clock_t clock, uint8 en)
57: {
58: 	/*
59: 	 * The timer is under APB0, to prevent apb0_clk_en1 and apb0_clk_en0
60: 	 * on same register, we split it to peripheral and central two
61: 	 * registers, to protect CPU close apb0 clock accidentally.
62: 	 *
63: 	 * The apb0_clk_en0 and apb0_clk_en1 have same function,
64: 	 * one of them set, the APB0 clock enable.
65: 	 */
66: 
67: 	/* The APB clock should carefully disable */
68: 	if(en)
69: 	{
70: 		switch(clock)
71: 		{
72: 			/*
73: 			 * These peripheral devices are under APB0
74: 			 * GPIO, UART1, UART2, UART3, SPI_SLAVE, I2S0, I2S1,
75: 			 * I2S2, I2C0, I2C1, I2C2, FPIOA, SHA256, TIMER0,
76: 			 * TIMER1, TIMER2
77: 			 */
78: 			case SYSCTL_CLOCK_GPIO:
79: 			case SYSCTL_CLOCK_SPI2:
80: 			case SYSCTL_CLOCK_I2S0:
81: 			case SYSCTL_CLOCK_I2S1:
82: 			case SYSCTL_CLOCK_I2S2:
83: 			case SYSCTL_CLOCK_I2C0:
84: 			case SYSCTL_CLOCK_I2C1:
85: 			case SYSCTL_CLOCK_I2C2:
86: 			case SYSCTL_CLOCK_UART1:
87: 			case SYSCTL_CLOCK_UART2:
88: 			case SYSCTL_CLOCK_UART3:
89: 			case SYSCTL_CLOCK_FPIOA:
90: 			case SYSCTL_CLOCK_TIMER0:
91: 			case SYSCTL_CLOCK_TIMER1:
92: 			case SYSCTL_CLOCK_TIMER2:
93: 			case SYSCTL_CLOCK_SHA:
94: 				sysctl->clk_en_cent.apb0_clk_en = en;
95: 				break;
96: 
97: 			/*
98: 			 * These peripheral devices are under APB1
99: 			 * WDT, AES, OTP, DVP, SYSCTL
100: 			 */
101: 			case SYSCTL_CLOCK_AES:
102: 			case SYSCTL_CLOCK_WDT0:
103: 			case SYSCTL_CLOCK_WDT1:
104: 			case SYSCTL_CLOCK_OTP:
105: 			case SYSCTL_CLOCK_RTC:
106: 				sysctl->clk_en_cent.apb1_clk_en = en;
107: 				break;
108: 
109: 			/*
110: 			 * These peripheral devices are under APB2
111: 			 * SPI0, SPI1
112: 			 */
113: 			case SYSCTL_CLOCK_SPI0:
114: 			case SYSCTL_CLOCK_SPI1:
115: 				sysctl->clk_en_cent.apb2_clk_en = en;
116: 				break;
117: 
118: 			default:
119: 				break;
120: 		}
121: 	}
122: 
123: 	return 0;
124: }
125: 
126: static int sysctl_clock_device_en(sysctl_clock_t clock, uint8 en)
127: {
128: 	switch(clock)
129: 	{

xv6-k210@final / 来源候选源码

56: static int sysctl_clock_bus_en(sysctl_clock_t clock, uint8 en)
57: {
58: 	/*
59: 	 * The timer is under APB0, to prevent apb0_clk_en1 and apb0_clk_en0
60: 	 * on same register, we split it to peripheral and central two
61: 	 * registers, to protect CPU close apb0 clock accidentally.
62: 	 *
63: 	 * The apb0_clk_en0 and apb0_clk_en1 have same function,
64: 	 * one of them set, the APB0 clock enable.
65: 	 */
66: 
67: 	/* The APB clock should carefully disable */
68: 	if(en)
69: 	{
70: 		switch(clock)
71: 		{
72: 			/*
73: 			 * These peripheral devices are under APB0
74: 			 * GPIO, UART1, UART2, UART3, SPI_SLAVE, I2S0, I2S1,
75: 			 * I2S2, I2C0, I2C1, I2C2, FPIOA, SHA256, TIMER0,
76: 			 * TIMER1, TIMER2
77: 			 */
78: 			case SYSCTL_CLOCK_GPIO:
79: 			case SYSCTL_CLOCK_SPI2:
80: 			case SYSCTL_CLOCK_I2S0:
81: 			case SYSCTL_CLOCK_I2S1:
82: 			case SYSCTL_CLOCK_I2S2:
83: 			case SYSCTL_CLOCK_I2C0:
84: 			case SYSCTL_CLOCK_I2C1:
85: 			case SYSCTL_CLOCK_I2C2:
86: 			case SYSCTL_CLOCK_UART1:
87: 			case SYSCTL_CLOCK_UART2:
88: 			case SYSCTL_CLOCK_UART3:
89: 			case SYSCTL_CLOCK_FPIOA:
90: 			case SYSCTL_CLOCK_TIMER0:
91: 			case SYSCTL_CLOCK_TIMER1:
92: 			case SYSCTL_CLOCK_TIMER2:
93: 			case SYSCTL_CLOCK_SHA:
94: 				sysctl->clk_en_cent.apb0_clk_en = en;
95: 				break;
96: 
97: 			/*
98: 			 * These peripheral devices are under APB1
99: 			 * WDT, AES, OTP, DVP, SYSCTL
100: 			 */
101: 			case SYSCTL_CLOCK_AES:
102: 			case SYSCTL_CLOCK_WDT0:
103: 			case SYSCTL_CLOCK_WDT1:
104: 			case SYSCTL_CLOCK_OTP:
105: 			case SYSCTL_CLOCK_RTC:
106: 				sysctl->clk_en_cent.apb1_clk_en = en;
107: 				break;
108: 
109: 			/*
110: 			 * These peripheral devices are under APB2
111: 			 * SPI0, SPI1
112: 			 */
113: 			case SYSCTL_CLOCK_SPI0:
114: 			case SYSCTL_CLOCK_SPI1:
115: 				sysctl->clk_en_cent.apb2_clk_en = en;
116: 				break;
117: 
118: 			default:
119: 				break;
120: 		}
121: 	}
122: 
123: 	return 0;
124: }

128: 	switch(clock)

168: 			sysctl->clk_en_peri.dma_clk_en = en;
169: 			break;
170: 		case SYSCTL_CLOCK_AI:
171: 			sysctl->clk_en_peri.ai_clk_en = en;
172: 			break;
173: 		case SYSCTL_CLOCK_DVP:
174: 			sysctl->clk_en_peri.dvp_clk_en = en;
175: 			break;
176: 		case SYSCTL_CLOCK_FFT:
177: 			sysctl->clk_en_peri.fft_clk_en = en;
178: 			break;
179: 		case SYSCTL_CLOCK_SPI3:
180: 			sysctl->clk_en_peri.spi3_clk_en = en;
181: 			break;
182: 
183: 		/*
184: 		 * These peripheral devices are under APB0
185: 		 * GPIO, UART1, UART2, UART3, SPI_SLAVE, I2S0, I2S1,
186: 		 * I2S2, I2C0, I2C1, I2C2, FPIOA, SHA256, TIMER0,
187: 		 * TIMER1, TIMER2
188: 		 */
189: 		case SYSCTL_CLOCK_GPIO:
190: 			sysctl->clk_en_peri.gpio_clk_en = en;
191: 			break;
192: 		case SYSCTL_CLOCK_SPI2:
193: 			sysctl->clk_en_peri.spi2_clk_en = en;
194: 			break;
195: 		case SYSCTL_CLOCK_I2S0:
196: 			sysctl->clk_en_peri.i2s0_clk_en = en;
197: 			break;
198: 		case SYSCTL_CLOCK_I2S1:
199: 			sysctl->clk_en_peri.i2s1_clk_en = en;
200: 			break;
201: 		case SYSCTL_CLOCK_I2S2:
202: 			sysctl->clk_en_peri.i2s2_clk_en = en;
203: 			break;
204: 		case SYSCTL_CLOCK_I2C0:
205: 			sysctl->clk_en_peri.i2c0_clk_en = en;
206: 			break;
207: 		case SYSCTL_CLOCK_I2C1:
208: 			sysctl->clk_en_peri.i2c1_clk_en = en;
209: 			break;
210: 		case SYSCTL_CLOCK_I2C2:
211: 			sysctl->clk_en_peri.i2c2_clk_en = en;
212: 			break;
213: 		case SYSCTL_CLOCK_UART1:
214: 			sysctl->clk_en_peri.uart1_clk_en = en;
215: 			break;
216: 		case SYSCTL_CLOCK_UART2:
217: 			sysctl->clk_en_peri.uart2_clk_en = en;
218: 			break;
219: 		case SYSCTL_CLOCK_UART3:
220: 			sysctl->clk_en_peri.uart3_clk_en = en;
221: 			break;
222: 		case SYSCTL_CLOCK_FPIOA:
223: 			sysctl->clk_en_peri.fpioa_clk_en = en;
224: 			break;
225: 		case SYSCTL_CLOCK_TIMER0:
226: 			sysctl->clk_en_peri.timer0_clk_en = en;
227: 			break;
228: 		case SYSCTL_CLOCK_TIMER1:
229: 			sysctl->clk_en_peri.timer1_clk_en = en;
230: 			break;
231: 		case SYSCTL_CLOCK_TIMER2:
232: 			sysctl->clk_en_peri.timer2_clk_en = en;
233: 			break;
234: 		case SYSCTL_CLOCK_SHA:
235: 			sysctl->clk_en_peri.sha_clk_en = en;
236: 			break;
237: 
238: 		/*
239: 		 * These peripheral devices are under APB1
240: 		 * WDT, AES, OTP, DVP, SYSCTL
241: 		 */
242: 		case SYSCTL_CLOCK_AES:
243: 			sysctl->clk_en_peri.aes_clk_en = en;
244: 			break;
245: 		case SYSCTL_CLOCK_WDT0:
246: 			sysctl->clk_en_peri.wdt0_clk_en = en;
247: 			break;
248: 		case SYSCTL_CLOCK_WDT1:
249: 			sysctl->clk_en_peri.wdt1_clk_en = en;
250: 			break;
251: 		case SYSCTL_CLOCK_OTP:
252: 			sysctl->clk_en_peri.otp_clk_en = en;
253: 			break;
254: 		case SYSCTL_CLOCK_RTC:
255: 			sysctl->clk_en_peri.rtc_clk_en = en;
256: 			break;
257: 
258: 		/*
259: 		 * These peripheral devices are under APB2
260: 		 * SPI0, SPI1
261: 		 */
262: 		case SYSCTL_CLOCK_SPI0:
263: 			sysctl->clk_en_peri.spi0_clk_en = en;
264: 			break;
265: 		case SYSCTL_CLOCK_SPI1:
266: 			sysctl->clk_en_peri.spi1_clk_en = en;
267: 			break;
268: 
269: 		default:
270: 			break;
271: 	}
272: 
273: 	return 0;
274: }

276: int sysctl_clock_enable(sysctl_clock_t clock)

284: 
285: int sysctl_dma_select(sysctl_dma_channel_t channel, sysctl_dma_select_t select)
286: {
287: 	sysctl_dma_sel0_t dma_sel0;
288: 	sysctl_dma_sel1_t dma_sel1;
289: 
290: 	/* Read register from bus */
291: 	dma_sel0 = sysctl->dma_sel0;
292: 	dma_sel1 = sysctl->dma_sel1;
293: 	switch(channel)
294: 	{
295: 		case SYSCTL_DMA_CHANNEL_0:
296: 			dma_sel0.dma_sel0 = select;
297: 			break;
298: 
299: 		case SYSCTL_DMA_CHANNEL_1:
300: 			dma_sel0.dma_sel1 = select;
301: 			break;
302: 
303: 		case SYSCTL_DMA_CHANNEL_2:
304: 			dma_sel0.dma_sel2 = select;
305: 			break;
306: 
307: 		case SYSCTL_DMA_CHANNEL_3:
308: 			dma_sel0.dma_sel3 = select;
309: 			break;
310: 
311: 		case SYSCTL_DMA_CHANNEL_4:
312: 			dma_sel0.dma_sel4 = select;
313: 			break;
314: 
315: 		case SYSCTL_DMA_CHANNEL_5:
316: 			dma_sel1.dma_sel5 = select;
317: 			break;
318: 
319: 		default:
320: 			return -1;
321: 	}
322: 
323: 	/* Write register back to bus */
324: 	sysctl->dma_sel0 = dma_sel0;
325: 	sysctl->dma_sel1 = dma_sel1;
326: 
327: 	return 0;
328: }

xv6-k210@final / 来源候选源码

280: 	sysctl_clock_bus_en(clock, 1);
281: 	sysctl_clock_device_en(clock, 1);
282: 	return 0;
283: }
284: 
285: int sysctl_dma_select(sysctl_dma_channel_t channel, sysctl_dma_select_t select)
286: {
287: 	sysctl_dma_sel0_t dma_sel0;
288: 	sysctl_dma_sel1_t dma_sel1;
289: 
290: 	/* Read register from bus */
291: 	dma_sel0 = sysctl->dma_sel0;
292: 	dma_sel1 = sysctl->dma_sel1;
293: 	switch(channel)
294: 	{
295: 		case SYSCTL_DMA_CHANNEL_0:
296: 			dma_sel0.dma_sel0 = select;
297: 			break;
298: 
299: 		case SYSCTL_DMA_CHANNEL_1:
300: 			dma_sel0.dma_sel1 = select;
301: 			break;
302: 
303: 		case SYSCTL_DMA_CHANNEL_2:
304: 			dma_sel0.dma_sel2 = select;
305: 			break;
306: 
307: 		case SYSCTL_DMA_CHANNEL_3:
308: 			dma_sel0.dma_sel3 = select;
309: 			break;
310: 
311: 		case SYSCTL_DMA_CHANNEL_4:
312: 			dma_sel0.dma_sel4 = select;
313: 			break;
314: 
315: 		case SYSCTL_DMA_CHANNEL_5:
316: 			dma_sel1.dma_sel5 = select;
317: 			break;
318: 
319: 		default:
320: 			return -1;
321: 	}
322: 
323: 	/* Write register back to bus */
324: 	sysctl->dma_sel0 = dma_sel0;
325: 	sysctl->dma_sel1 = dma_sel1;
326: 
327: 	return 0;
328: }

92: } __attribute__ ((aligned (PGSIZE))) disk;
93: 
94: void
95: virtio_disk_init(void)
96: {
97: 	uint32 status = 0;
98: 
99: 	initlock(&disk.vdisk_lock, "virtio_disk");
100: 	wait_queue_init(&disk.queue, "vdisk_queue");
101: 	disk.queue_running = 0;
102: 	disk.nwait = 0;
103: 
104: 	if (*R(VIRTIO_MMIO_MAGIC_VALUE) != 0x74726976 ||
105: 		*R(VIRTIO_MMIO_VERSION) != 1 ||
106: 		*R(VIRTIO_MMIO_DEVICE_ID) != 2 ||
107: 		*R(VIRTIO_MMIO_VENDOR_ID) != 0x554d4551)
108: 	{
109: 		panic("could not find virtio disk");
110: 	}
111: 	
112: 	status |= VIRTIO_CONFIG_S_ACKNOWLEDGE;
113: 	*R(VIRTIO_MMIO_STATUS) = status;
114: 
115: 	status |= VIRTIO_CONFIG_S_DRIVER;
116: 	*R(VIRTIO_MMIO_STATUS) = status;
117: 
118: 	// negotiate features
119: 	uint64 features = *R(VIRTIO_MMIO_DEVICE_FEATURES);
120: 	features &= ~(1 << VIRTIO_BLK_F_RO);
121: 	features &= ~(1 << VIRTIO_BLK_F_SCSI);
122: 	features &= ~(1 << VIRTIO_BLK_F_CONFIG_WCE);
123: 	features &= ~(1 << VIRTIO_BLK_F_MQ);
124: 	features &= ~(1 << VIRTIO_F_ANY_LAYOUT);
125: 	features &= ~(1 << VIRTIO_RING_F_EVENT_IDX);
126: 	features &= ~(1 << VIRTIO_RING_F_INDIRECT_DESC);
127: 	*R(VIRTIO_MMIO_DRIVER_FEATURES) = features;
128: 
129: 	// tell device that feature negotiation is complete.
130: 	status |= VIRTIO_CONFIG_S_FEATURES_OK;
131: 	*R(VIRTIO_MMIO_STATUS) = status;
132: 
133: 	// tell device we're completely ready.
134: 	status |= VIRTIO_CONFIG_S_DRIVER_OK;
135: 	*R(VIRTIO_MMIO_STATUS) = status;
136: 
137: 	*R(VIRTIO_MMIO_GUEST_PAGE_SIZE) = PGSIZE;
138: 
139: 	// initialize queue 0.
140: 	*R(VIRTIO_MMIO_QUEUE_SEL) = 0;
141: 	uint32 max = *R(VIRTIO_MMIO_QUEUE_NUM_MAX);
142: 	if (max == 0)
143: 		panic("virtio disk has no queue 0");
144: 	if (max < NUM)
145: 		panic("virtio disk max queue too short");
146: 	*R(VIRTIO_MMIO_QUEUE_NUM) = NUM;
147: 	memset(disk.pages, 0, sizeof(disk.pages));
148: 	*R(VIRTIO_MMIO_QUEUE_PFN) = ((uint64)disk.pages) >> PGSHIFT;
149: 
150: 	// desc = pages -- num * virtq_desc
151: 	// avail = pages + 0x40 -- 2 * uint16, then num * uint16
152: 	// used = pages + 4096 -- 2 * uint16, then num * vRingUsedElem
153: 
154: 	disk.desc = (struct virtq_desc *) disk.pages;
155: 	disk.avail = (struct virtq_avail *)(disk.pages + NUM * sizeof(struct virtq_desc));
156: 	disk.used = (struct virtq_used *) (disk.pages + PGSIZE);
157: 
158: 	// all NUM descriptors start out unused.
159: 	for (int i = 0; i < NUM; i++)
160: 		disk.free[i] = 1;
161: 
162: 	// plic.c and trap.c arrange for interrupts from VIRTIO0_IRQ.
163: }
164: 
165: // find a free descriptor, mark it non-free, return its index.
166: static int
167: alloc_desc()
168: {

94: void
95: virtio_disk_init(void)
96: {
97: 	uint32 status = 0;
98: 
99: 	initlock(&disk.vdisk_lock, "virtio_disk");
100: 	wait_queue_init(&disk.queue, "vdisk_queue");
101: 	disk.queue_running = 0;
102: 	disk.nwait = 0;
103: 
104: 	// if (*R(VIRTIO_MMIO_MAGIC_VALUE) != 0x74726976 ||
105: 	// 	*R(VIRTIO_MMIO_VERSION) != 1 ||
106: 	// 	*R(VIRTIO_MMIO_DEVICE_ID) != 2 ||
107: 	// 	*R(VIRTIO_MMIO_VENDOR_ID) != 0x554d4551)
108: 	// {
109: 	// 	panic("could not find virtio disk");
110: 	// }
111: 	
112: 	status |= VIRTIO_CONFIG_S_ACKNOWLEDGE;
113: 	*R(VIRTIO_MMIO_STATUS) = status;
114: 
115: 	status |= VIRTIO_CONFIG_S_DRIVER;
116: 	*R(VIRTIO_MMIO_STATUS) = status;
117: 
118: 	// negotiate features
119: 	uint64 features = *R(VIRTIO_MMIO_DEVICE_FEATURES);
120: 	features &= ~(1 << VIRTIO_BLK_F_RO);
121: 	features &= ~(1 << VIRTIO_BLK_F_SCSI);
122: 	features &= ~(1 << VIRTIO_BLK_F_CONFIG_WCE);
123: 	features &= ~(1 << VIRTIO_BLK_F_MQ);
124: 	features &= ~(1 << VIRTIO_F_ANY_LAYOUT);
125: 	features &= ~(1 << VIRTIO_RING_F_EVENT_IDX);
126: 	features &= ~(1 << VIRTIO_RING_F_INDIRECT_DESC);
127: 	*R(VIRTIO_MMIO_DRIVER_FEATURES) = features;
128: 
129: 	// tell device that feature negotiation is complete.
130: 	status |= VIRTIO_CONFIG_S_FEATURES_OK;
131: 	*R(VIRTIO_MMIO_STATUS) = status;
132: 
133: 	// tell device we're completely ready.
134: 	status |= VIRTIO_CONFIG_S_DRIVER_OK;
135: 	*R(VIRTIO_MMIO_STATUS) = status;
136: 
137: 	*R(VIRTIO_MMIO_GUEST_PAGE_SIZE) = PGSIZE;
138: 
139: 	// initialize queue 0.
140: 	*R(VIRTIO_MMIO_QUEUE_SEL) = 0;
141: 	uint32 max = *R(VIRTIO_MMIO_QUEUE_NUM_MAX);
142: 	// if (max == 0)
143: 	// 	panic("virtio disk has no queue 0");
144: 	// if (max < NUM)
145: 	// 	panic("virtio disk max queue too short");
146: 	*R(VIRTIO_MMIO_QUEUE_NUM) = NUM;
147: 	memset(disk.pages, 0, sizeof(disk.pages));
148: 	*R(VIRTIO_MMIO_QUEUE_PFN) = ((uint64)disk.pages) >> PGSHIFT;
149: 
150: 	// desc = pages -- num * virtq_desc
151: 	// avail = pages + 0x40 -- 2 * uint16, then num * uint16
152: 	// used = pages + 4096 -- 2 * uint16, then num * vRingUsedElem
153: 
154: 	disk.desc = (struct virtq_desc *) disk.pages;
155: 	disk.avail = (struct virtq_avail *)(disk.pages + NUM * sizeof(struct virtq_desc));
156: 	disk.used = (struct virtq_used *) (disk.pages + PGSIZE);
157: 
158: 	// all NUM descriptors start out unused.
159: 	for (int i = 0; i < NUM; i++)
160: 		disk.free[i] = 1;
161: 
162: 	// plic.c and trap.c arrange for interrupts from VIRTIO0_IRQ.
163: }

178: // mark a descriptor as free.
179: static void
180: free_desc(int i)
181: {
182: 	if (i >= NUM)
183: 		panic("free_desc 1");
184: 	if (disk.free[i])
185: 		panic("free_desc 2");
186: 	disk.desc[i].addr = 0;
187: 	disk.desc[i].len = 0;
188: 	disk.desc[i].flags = 0;
189: 	disk.desc[i].next = 0;
190: 	disk.free[i] = 1;
191: 	if (disk.nwait > 0)
192: 		wakeup(&disk.free[0]);
193: }
194:

196: static void
197: free_chain(int i)
198: {

195: // free a chain of descriptors.
196: static void
197: free_chain(int i)
198: {
199: 	while (1) {
200: 		int flag = disk.desc[i].flags;
201: 		int nxt = disk.desc[i].next;
202: 		free_desc(i);
203: 		if (flag & VRING_DESC_F_NEXT)
204: 			i = nxt;
205: 		else
206: 			break;
207: 	}
208: }

195: // free a chain of descriptors.
196: static void
197: free_chain(int i)
198: {
199: 	while (1) {
200: 		int flag = disk.desc[i].flags;
201: 		int nxt = disk.desc[i].next;
202: 		free_desc(i);

228: static int alloc_descs(int * restrict idx, int n)
229: {
230: 	for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
231: 		idx[i] = alloc_desc();
232: 		if (idx[i] < 0) {
233: 			for (int j = 0; j < i; j++)

235: 			return -1;
236: 		}
237: 	}
238: 	return 0;

228: static int alloc_descs(int * restrict idx, int n)
229: {
230: 	for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
231: 		idx[i] = alloc_desc();
232: 		if (idx[i] < 0) {

240: 
241: static int
242: virtio_disk_rw(struct buf * restrict bufs[], int nbuf, int write)
243: {
244: 	int ndesc = nbuf + 2;
245: 	if (ndesc > NUM)
246: 		return -1;
247: 
248: 	acquire(&disk.vdisk_lock);
249: 	// the spec's Section 5.2 says that legacy block operations use
250: 	// three descriptors: one for type/reserved/sector, one for the
251: 	// data, one for a 1-byte status result.
252: 
253: 	// allocate the three descriptors.
254: 	int idx[NUM];
255: 	while (1) {
256: 		if (alloc_descs(idx, ndesc) == 0)
257: 			break;
258: 		if (write) {	// write don't wait
259: 			release(&disk.vdisk_lock);
260: 			return -1;
261: 		}
262: 		disk.nwait++;
263: 		sleep(&disk.free[0], &disk.vdisk_lock);
264: 		disk.nwait--;
265: 	}
266: 
267: 	// format the three descriptors.
268: 	// qemu's virtio-blk.c reads them.
269: 	
270: 	struct virtio_blk_req *buf0 = &disk.ops[idx[0]];
271: 	struct virtq_desc *desc;
272: 
273: 	buf0->type = write ? VIRTIO_BLK_T_OUT :	// write the disk
274: 						VIRTIO_BLK_T_IN;	// read the disk
275: 	buf0->reserved = 0;
276: 	buf0->sector = bufs[0]->sectorno;
277: 
278: 	// first
279: 	desc = &disk.desc[idx[0]];
280: 	desc->addr = (uint64) buf0;
281: 	desc->len = sizeof(struct virtio_blk_req);
282: 	desc->flags = VRING_DESC_F_NEXT;
283: 	desc->next = idx[1];
284: 
285: 	for (int i = 1; i <= nbuf; i++) {
286: 		desc = &disk.desc[idx[i]];
287: 		desc->addr = (uint64) bufs[i - 1]->data;
288: 		desc->len = BSIZE;
289: 		desc->flags = write ? 0 :				// device reads b->data
290: 							VRING_DESC_F_WRITE;	// device writes b->data
291: 		desc->flags |= VRING_DESC_F_NEXT;
292: 		desc->next = idx[i + 1];
293: 	}
294: 
295: 	// last
296: 	desc = &disk.desc[idx[ndesc - 1]];
297: 	desc->addr = (uint64) &disk.info[idx[0]].status;
298: 	desc->len = 1;
299: 	desc->flags = VRING_DESC_F_WRITE;		// device writes the status
300: 	desc->next = 0;
301: 
302: 	/**
303: 	 * Record struct buf for virtio_disk_intr().
304: 	 * If we are committing a wirte, we are not going to wait here.
305: 	 * After the write is done, the interrupt handler will use idx1
306: 	 * to free the desc chain that we allocated. In other way, this
307: 	 * is also a hint that this is a write buf, the handler needs to
308: 	 * do something about it.
309: 	 */
310: 	disk.info[idx[0]].idx1 = write ? idx[0] : -1;
311: 	disk.info[idx[0]].status = 0xff;		// device writes 0 on success
312: 	disk.info[idx[0]].b = bufs[0];
313: 
314: 	// tell the device the first index in our chain of descriptors.
315: 	disk.avail->ring[disk.avail->idx % NUM] = idx[0];
316: 
317: 	__sync_synchronize();
318: 
319: 	// tell the device another avail ring entry is available.
320: 	disk.avail->idx += 1; // not % NUM ...
321: 
322: 	__sync_synchronize();
323: 
324: 	*R(VIRTIO_MMIO_QUEUE_NOTIFY) = 0; // value is queue number
325: 
326: 	int res = 0;
327: 	if (!write) {
328: 		bufs[0]->disk = 1;
329: 		// Wait for virtio_disk_intr() to say request has finished.
330: 		while (bufs[0]->disk == 1) {
331: 			// printf(__INFO("virtio_disk_rw")" sleep on %d\n", b->sectorno);
332: 			sleep(bufs[0], &disk.vdisk_lock);
333: 		}
334: 		res = -(disk.info[idx[0]].status != 0);
335: 		free_chain(idx[0]);
336: 	}
337: 
338: 	release(&disk.vdisk_lock);
339: 	return res;
340: }
341: 
342: void
343: virtio_disk_intr()
344: {
345: 	struct buf *b;

xv6-k210@final / 来源候选源码

236: 		}
237: 	}
238: 	return 0;
239: }
240: 
241: static int
242: virtio_disk_rw(struct buf * restrict bufs[], int nbuf, int write)
243: {
244: 	int ndesc = nbuf + 2;
245: 	if (ndesc > NUM)
246: 		return -1;
247: 
248: 	acquire(&disk.vdisk_lock);
249: 	// the spec's Section 5.2 says that legacy block operations use
250: 	// three descriptors: one for type/reserved/sector, one for the
251: 	// data, one for a 1-byte status result.
252: 
253: 	// allocate the three descriptors.
254: 	int idx[NUM];
255: 	while (1) {
256: 		if (alloc_descs(idx, ndesc) == 0)
257: 			break;
258: 		if (write) {	// write don't wait
259: 			release(&disk.vdisk_lock);
260: 			return -1;
261: 		}
262: 		disk.nwait++;
263: 		sleep(&disk.free[0], &disk.vdisk_lock);
264: 		disk.nwait--;
265: 	}
266: 
267: 	// format the three descriptors.
268: 	// qemu's virtio-blk.c reads them.
269: 	
270: 	struct virtio_blk_req *buf0 = &disk.ops[idx[0]];
271: 	struct virtq_desc *desc;
272: 
273: 	buf0->type = write ? VIRTIO_BLK_T_OUT :	// write the disk
274: 						VIRTIO_BLK_T_IN;	// read the disk
275: 	buf0->reserved = 0;
276: 	buf0->sector = bufs[0]->sectorno;
277: 
278: 	// first
279: 	desc = &disk.desc[idx[0]];
280: 	desc->addr = (uint64) buf0;
281: 	desc->len = sizeof(struct virtio_blk_req);
282: 	desc->flags = VRING_DESC_F_NEXT;
283: 	desc->next = idx[1];
284: 
285: 	for (int i = 1; i <= nbuf; i++) {
286: 		desc = &disk.desc[idx[i]];
287: 		desc->addr = (uint64) bufs[i - 1]->data;
288: 		desc->len = BSIZE;
289: 		desc->flags = write ? 0 :				// device reads b->data
290: 							VRING_DESC_F_WRITE;	// device writes b->data
291: 		desc->flags |= VRING_DESC_F_NEXT;
292: 		desc->next = idx[i + 1];
293: 	}
294: 
295: 	// last
296: 	desc = &disk.desc[idx[ndesc - 1]];
297: 	desc->addr = (uint64) &disk.info[idx[0]].status;
298: 	desc->len = 1;
299: 	desc->flags = VRING_DESC_F_WRITE;		// device writes the status
300: 	desc->next = 0;
301: 
302: 	/**
303: 	 * Record struct buf for virtio_disk_intr().
304: 	 * If we are committing a wirte, we are not going to wait here.
305: 	 * After the write is done, the interrupt handler will use idx1
306: 	 * to free the desc chain that we allocated. In other way, this
307: 	 * is also a hint that this is a write buf, the handler needs to
308: 	 * do something about it.
309: 	 */
310: 	disk.info[idx[0]].idx1 = write ? idx[0] : -1;
311: 	disk.info[idx[0]].status = 0xff;		// device writes 0 on success
312: 	disk.info[idx[0]].b = bufs[0];
313: 
314: 	// tell the device the first index in our chain of descriptors.
315: 	disk.avail->ring[disk.avail->idx % NUM] = idx[0];
316: 
317: 	__sync_synchronize();
318: 
319: 	// tell the device another avail ring entry is available.
320: 	disk.avail->idx += 1; // not % NUM ...
321: 
322: 	__sync_synchronize();
323: 
324: 	*R(VIRTIO_MMIO_QUEUE_NOTIFY) = 0; // value is queue number
325: 
326: 	int res = 0;
327: 	if (!write) {
328: 		bufs[0]->disk = 1;
329: 		// Wait for virtio_disk_intr() to say request has finished.
330: 		while (bufs[0]->disk == 1) {
331: 			// printf(__INFO("virtio_disk_rw")" sleep on %d\n", b->sectorno);
332: 			sleep(bufs[0], &disk.vdisk_lock);
333: 		}
334: 		res = -(disk.info[idx[0]].status != 0);
335: 		free_chain(idx[0]);
336: 	}
337: 
338: 	release(&disk.vdisk_lock);
339: 	return res;
340: }
341: 
342: void
343: virtio_disk_intr()
344: {
345: 	struct buf *b;

匹配分数 0.678 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": false, "exact_token_fp": false, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 0.594}

339: 	return res;

341: 
342: void
343: virtio_disk_intr()
344: {
345: 	struct buf *b;
346: 	struct buf *bufs[NUM];	// bufs that finished writing
347: 	int cnt = 0, i;
348: 
349: 	acquire(&disk.vdisk_lock);
350: 
351: 	// the device won't raise another interrupt until we tell it
352: 	// we've seen this interrupt, which the following line does.
353: 	// this may race with the device writing new entries to
354: 	// the "used" ring, in which case we may process the new
355: 	// completion entries in this interrupt, and have nothing to do
356: 	// in the next interrupt, which is harmless.
357: 	*R(VIRTIO_MMIO_INTERRUPT_ACK) = *R(VIRTIO_MMIO_INTERRUPT_STATUS) & 0x3;
358: 
359: 	__sync_synchronize();
360: 
361: 	// the device increments disk.used->idx when it
362: 	// adds an entry to the used ring.
363: 	while (disk.used_idx != disk.used->idx) {
364: 		__sync_synchronize();
365: 		int id = disk.used->ring[disk.used_idx % NUM].id;
366: 		disk.used_idx += 1;
367: 
368: 		if (disk.info[id].status != 0)
369: 			panic("virtio_disk_intr status");
370: 
371: 		b = disk.info[id].b;
372: 		// disk.info[id].b = 0;
373: 		if (disk.info[id].idx1 < 0) {	// read interrupt
374: 			b->disk = 0;   				// disk is done with buf
375: 			wakeup(b);
376: 		} else {						// write interrupt
377: 			free_chain(disk.info[id].idx1);
378: 			bufs[cnt++] = b;			// collect for convenience
379: 		}
380: 	}
381: 	release(&disk.vdisk_lock);
382: 
383: 	if (cnt > 0) {
384: 		acquire(&disk.queue.lock);
385: 		for (i = 0; i < cnt; i++) {			// clean write bufs
386: 			bufs[i]->disk = 0;				// disk is done with buf
387: 			disk.queue_running--;
388: 			if (bufs[i]->dirty)				// some one has touched it again
389: 				bufs[i] = NULL;				// we have to keep it and write again
390: 			else
391: 				dlist_del(&bufs[i]->list);	// done, let go
392: 		}
393: 		release(&disk.queue.lock);
394: 
395: 		for (i = 0; i < cnt; i++) {
396: 			if (bufs[i])
397: 				bput(bufs[i]);
398: 		}
399: 	}
400: 
401: 	acquire(&disk.queue.lock);
402: 	struct d_list *l = disk.queue.head.next;
403: 	if (l == &disk.queue.head) {			// nothing to write
404: 		release(&disk.queue.lock);
405: 		return;
406: 	}
407: 
408: 	for (; l != &disk.queue.head; l = l->next) {
409: 		b = container_of(l, struct buf, list);
410: 		if (!b->disk) {
411: 			b->disk = 1;
412: 			b->dirty = 0;
413: 			disk.queue_running++;
414: 			release(&disk.queue.lock);
415: 			bufs[0] = b;
416: 			if (virtio_disk_rw(bufs, 1, 1) < 0) {
417: 				acquire(&disk.queue.lock);
418: 				b->disk = 0;
419: 				b->dirty = 1;
420: 				disk.queue_running--;
421: 				release(&disk.queue.lock);
422: 			}
423: 			return;
424: 		}
425: 	}
426: 	release(&disk.queue.lock);
427: }
428: 
429: void virtio_disk_write_start(void)
430: {
431: 	acquire(&disk.queue.lock);
432: 	if (disk.queue_running > 0) {

338: 	release(&disk.vdisk_lock);
339: 	return res;
340: }

342: void
343: virtio_disk_intr()
344: {
345: 	struct buf *b;
346: 	struct buf *bufs[NUM];	// bufs that finished writing
347: 	int cnt = 0, i;
348: 
349: 	acquire(&disk.vdisk_lock);
350: 
351: 	// the device won't raise another interrupt until we tell it
352: 	// we've seen this interrupt, which the following line does.
353: 	// this may race with the device writing new entries to
354: 	// the "used" ring, in which case we may process the new
355: 	// completion entries in this interrupt, and have nothing to do
356: 	// in the next interrupt, which is harmless.
357: 	*R(VIRTIO_MMIO_INTERRUPT_ACK) = *R(VIRTIO_MMIO_INTERRUPT_STATUS) & 0x3;
358: 
359: 	__sync_synchronize();
360: 
361: 	// the device increments disk.used->idx when it
362: 	// adds an entry to the used ring.
363: 	while (disk.used_idx != disk.used->idx) {
364: 		__sync_synchronize();
365: 		int id = disk.used->ring[disk.used_idx % NUM].id;
366: 		disk.used_idx += 1;
367: 
368: 		// if (disk.info[id].status != 0)
369: 		// 	panic("virtio_disk_intr status");
370: 
371: 		b = disk.info[id].b;
372: 		// disk.info[id].b = 0;
373: 		if (disk.info[id].idx1 < 0) {	// read interrupt
374: 			b->disk = 0;   				// disk is done with buf
375: 			wakeup(b);
376: 		} else {						// write interrupt
377: 			free_chain(disk.info[id].idx1);
378: 			bufs[cnt++] = b;			// collect for convenience
379: 		}
380: 	}
381: 	release(&disk.vdisk_lock);
382: 
383: 	if (cnt > 0) {
384: 		acquire(&disk.queue.lock);
385: 		for (i = 0; i < cnt; i++) {			// clean write bufs
386: 			bufs[i]->disk = 0;				// disk is done with buf
387: 			disk.queue_running--;
388: 			if (bufs[i]->dirty)				// some one has touched it again
389: 				bufs[i] = NULL;				// we have to keep it and write again
390: 			else
391: 				dlist_del(&bufs[i]->list);	// done, let go
392: 		}
393: 		release(&disk.queue.lock);
394: 
395: 		for (i = 0; i < cnt; i++) {
396: 			if (bufs[i])
397: 				bput(bufs[i]);
398: 		}
399: 	}
400: 
401: 	acquire(&disk.queue.lock);
402: 	struct d_list *l = disk.queue.head.next;
403: 	if (l == &disk.queue.head) {			// nothing to write
404: 		release(&disk.queue.lock);
405: 		return;
406: 	}
407: 
408: 	for (; l != &disk.queue.head; l = l->next) {
409: 		b = container_of(l, struct buf, list);
410: 		if (!b->disk) {
411: 			b->disk = 1;
412: 			b->dirty = 0;
413: 			disk.queue_running++;
414: 			release(&disk.queue.lock);
415: 			bufs[0] = b;
416: 			if (virtio_disk_rw(bufs, 1, 1) < 0) {
417: 				acquire(&disk.queue.lock);
418: 				b->disk = 0;
419: 				b->dirty = 1;
420: 				disk.queue_running--;
421: 				release(&disk.queue.lock);
422: 			}
423: 			return;
424: 		}
425: 	}
426: 	release(&disk.queue.lock);
427: }

431: 	acquire(&disk.queue.lock);
432: 	if (disk.queue_running > 0) {

匹配分数 0.594 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": false, "exact_token_fp": false, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 0.312}

429: void virtio_disk_write_start(void)
430: {
431: 	acquire(&disk.queue.lock);
432: 	if (disk.queue_running > 0) {
433: 		release(&disk.queue.lock);
434: 		return;
435: 	} else if (disk.queue_running < 0)
436: 		panic("virtio_disk_write_start: bad counter");
437: 
438: 	struct d_list *l = disk.queue.head.next;
439: 	if (l == &disk.queue.head) {
440: 		release(&disk.queue.lock);
441: 		return;
442: 	}
443: 	
444: 	struct buf *b[1];
445: 	b[0] = container_of(l, struct buf, list);
446: 	if (b[0]->disk)
447: 		panic("virtio_disk_write_start: on-flight buf");
448: 
449: 	b[0]->disk = 1;
450: 	b[0]->dirty = 0;
451: 	disk.queue_running = 1;
452: 	release(&disk.queue.lock);
453: 
454: 	if (virtio_disk_rw(b, 1, 1) < 0) {
455: 		/**
456: 		 * This happens when lacking of descs,
457: 		 * but don't worry, since lacking of
458: 		 * descs means some one is reading disk,
459: 		 * so when its interrupt comes, it will
460: 		 * start us.
461: 		 */
462: 		acquire(&disk.queue.lock);
463: 		b[0]->disk = 0;
464: 		b[0]->dirty = 1;
465: 		disk.queue_running--;
466: 		release(&disk.queue.lock);
467: 	}
468: 
469: }
470: 
471: int virtio_disk_submit(struct buf *b)
472: {
473: 	int res = 0;

xv6-k210@final / 来源候选源码

424: 		}
425: 	}
426: 	release(&disk.queue.lock);
427: }
428: 
429: void virtio_disk_write_start(void)
430: {
431: 	acquire(&disk.queue.lock);
432: 	if (disk.queue_running > 0) {
433: 		release(&disk.queue.lock);
434: 		return;
435: 	} 
436: 	// else if (disk.queue_running < 0)
437: 	// 	panic("virtio_disk_write_start: bad counter");
438: 
439: 	struct d_list *l = disk.queue.head.next;
440: 	if (l == &disk.queue.head) {
441: 		release(&disk.queue.lock);
442: 		return;
443: 	}
444: 	
445: 	struct buf *b[1];
446: 	b[0] = container_of(l, struct buf, list);
447: 	// if (b[0]->disk)
448: 	// 	panic("virtio_disk_write_start: on-flight buf");
449: 
450: 	b[0]->disk = 1;
451: 	b[0]->dirty = 0;
452: 	disk.queue_running = 1;
453: 	release(&disk.queue.lock);
454: 
455: 	if (virtio_disk_rw(b, 1, 1) < 0) {
456: 		/**
457: 		 * This happens when lacking of descs,
458: 		 * but don't worry, since lacking of
459: 		 * descs means some one is reading disk,
460: 		 * so when its interrupt comes, it will
461: 		 * start us.
462: 		 */
463: 		acquire(&disk.queue.lock);
464: 		b[0]->disk = 0;
465: 		b[0]->dirty = 1;
466: 		disk.queue_running--;
467: 		release(&disk.queue.lock);
468: 	}
469: 
470: }
471: 
472: int virtio_disk_submit(struct buf *b)
473: {
474: 	int res = 0;
475: 	acquire(&disk.queue.lock);
virtio_disk_submit完全一致

466: 		release(&disk.queue.lock);

468:

470: 
471: int virtio_disk_submit(struct buf *b)
472: {
473: 	int res = 0;
474: 	acquire(&disk.queue.lock);
475: 
476: 	/**
477: 	 * Test whether this is a new coming member.
478: 	 * When carrying the buf to disk, b->disk
479: 	 * will be set and b->dirty will be cleared.
480: 	 * One of them is non-zero means that this
481: 	 * buf is already in queue.
482: 	 */
483: 	if (b->dirty == 0 && b->disk == 0) {
484: 		dlist_add_before(&disk.queue.head, &b->list);
485: 		res = 0x1;
486: 	}
487: 	b->dirty = 1;
488: 	if (disk.queue_running > 0)	// inform that the queue is on
489: 		res |= 0x2;
490: 	release(&disk.queue.lock);
491: 	return res;
492: }
493: 
494: int virtio_disk_read(struct buf *b)
495: {
496: 	struct buf *bufs[1];
497: 	bufs[0] = b;

468: 	}
469: 
470: }

481: 	 * One of them is non-zero means that this

483: 	 */
484: 	if (b->dirty == 0 && b->disk == 0) {
485: 		dlist_add_before(&disk.queue.head, &b->list);

487: 	}

489: 	if (disk.queue_running > 0)	// inform that the queue is on
490: 		res |= 0x2;
491: 	release(&disk.queue.lock);

493: }

6: 
7: // push_off/pop_off are like intr_off()/intr_on() except that they are matched:
8: // it takes two pop_off()s to undo two push_off()s.  Also, if interrupts
9: // are initially off, then push_off, pop_off leaves them off.
10: 
11: void
12: push_off(void)
13: {
14: 	int old = intr_get();
15: 
16: 	intr_off();
17: 	struct cpu *c = mycpu();
18: 	//printf("\e[32mpush_off()\e[0m: cpuid(): %d\n", cpuid());
19: 	if (c->noff == 0)
20: 		c->intena = old;
21: 	c->noff += 1;
22: }
23: 
24: void
25: pop_off(void)
26: {
27: 	struct cpu *c = mycpu();

36: 		if(i%10000000==0){
37: 			printf("start waiting\n");
