40: 
41: void
42: main(unsigned long hartid, unsigned long dtb_pa)
43: {
44: 	inithartid(hartid);
45: 	
46: 	if (hartid == 0) {
47: 		started = 0;
48: 		cpuinit();
49: 		floatinithart();
50: 		consoleinit();
51: 		printfinit();   // init a lock for printf 
52: 		print_logo();
53: 		kpminit();       // physical page allocator
54: 		kvminit();       // create kernel page table
55: 		kvminithart();   // turn on paging
56: 		kmallocinit();   // small physical memory allocator
57: 		// timerinit();     // init a lock for timer
58: 		trapinithart();  // install kernel trap vector, including interrupt handler
59: 		procinit();
60: 		plicinit();
61: 		plicinithart();
62: 		#ifndef QEMU
63: 		fpioa_pin_init();
64: 		dmac_init();
65: 		#endif 
66: 		disk_init();
67: 		binit();         // buffer cache
68: 		// fileinit();      // file table
69: 		printf("hart 0 init done\n");
70: 		
71: 		delay(100000000); 
72: 		userinit();      // first user process
73: 		
74: 
75: 		// we need IPI to wake up other hart(s)
76: 		for (int i = 1; i < NCPU; i ++) {
77: 			unsigned long mask = 1 << i;
78: 			struct sbiret res = sbi_send_ipi(mask, 0);
79: 			sbi_send_ipi(mask, 0);
80: 			__debug_assert("main", SBI_SUCCESS == res.error, "sbi_send_ipi failed");
81: 		}
82: 		__sync_synchronize();
83: 		started = 1;
84: 	}
85: 	else
86: 	{
87: 		// hart 1
88: 		while (started == 0)
89: 			;
90: 		__sync_synchronize();
91: 		floatinithart();
92: 		kvminithart();
93: 		trapinithart();
94: 		plicinithart();  // ask PLIC for device interrupts
95: 		printf("hart 1 init done\n");
96: 	}
97: 	__debug_info("main", "hart %d enter main()...\n", hartid);
98: 
99: 	// shrink the boot stack
100: 	extern char boot_stack[];
101: 	uint64 kstack = (uint64)boot_stack + hartid * 4 * PGSIZE;
102: 	freepage_n(kstack, 3);
103: 
104: 	scheduler();
105: }

xv6-k210@final / 来源候选源码

31: 
32: volatile static int started = 0;
33: 
34: void
35: main(unsigned long hartid, unsigned long dtb_pa)
36: {
37: 	inithartid(hartid);
38: 	
39: 	if (hartid == 0) {
40: 		started = 0;
41: 		cpuinit();
42: 		floatinithart();
43: 		consoleinit();
44: 		printfinit();   // init a lock for printf 
45: 		print_logo();
46: 		kpminit();       // physical page allocator
47: 		kvminit();       // create kernel page table
48: 		kvminithart();   // turn on paging
49: 		kmallocinit();   // small physical memory allocator
50: 		// timerinit();     // init a lock for timer
51: 		trapinithart();  // install kernel trap vector, including interrupt handler
52: 		procinit();
53: 		plicinit();
54: 		plicinithart();
55: 		#ifndef QEMU
56: 		fpioa_pin_init();
57: 		dmac_init();
58: 		#endif 
59: 		disk_init();
60: 		binit();         // buffer cache
61: 		// fileinit();      // file table
62: 		userinit();      // first user process
63: 		printf("hart 0 init done\n");
64: 
65: 		// we need IPI to wake up other hart(s)
66: 		for (int i = 1; i < NCPU; i ++) {
67: 			unsigned long mask = 1 << i;
68: 			// struct sbiret res = sbi_send_ipi(mask, 0);
69: 			sbi_send_ipi(mask, 0);
70: 			__debug_assert("main", SBI_SUCCESS == res.error, "sbi_send_ipi failed");
71: 		}
72: 		__sync_synchronize();
73: 		started = 1;
74: 	}
75: 	else
76: 	{
77: 		// hart 1
78: 		while (started == 0)
79: 			;
80: 		__sync_synchronize();
81: 		floatinithart();
82: 		kvminithart();
83: 		trapinithart();
84: 		plicinithart();  // ask PLIC for device interrupts
85: 		printf("hart 1 init done\n");
86: 	}
87: 	__debug_info("main", "hart %d enter main()...\n", hartid);
88: 
89: 	// shrink the boot stack
90: 	extern char boot_stack[];
91: 	uint64 kstack = (uint64)boot_stack + hartid * 4 * PGSIZE;
92: 	// freepage_n(kstack, 3);
93: 	for (int i = 0; i < 3; i ++) {
94: 		freepage(kstack + PGSIZE * i);
95: 	}
96: 
97: 	scheduler();
98: }

65: #define ROUNDUP16(n) \
66: 	(((n) + 15) & ~0x0f)
67: 
68: // as kmalloc() use allocpage() and freepage, 
69: // kmallocinit() should be called at least after kpminit() 
70: void kmallocinit(void) {
71: 	// init adam 
72: 	initlock(&(kmem_adam.lock), "kmem_adam");
73: 	kmem_adam.list = NULL;
74: 	kmem_adam.next = NULL;
75: 	kmem_adam.npages = 0;
76: 	kmem_adam.nobjs = 0;
77: 	kmem_adam.obj_size = 
78: 			ROUNDUP16(sizeof(struct kmem_allocator));
79: 
80: 	// init kmem_table 
81: 	for (uint8 i = 0; i < KMEM_TABLE_SIZE; i++) {
82: 		kmem_table[i] = NULL;
83: 	}
84: 	int hash = _hash(kmem_adam.obj_size);
85: 	kmem_table[hash] = &kmem_adam;
86: 	initlock(&kmem_table_lock, "kmem_table");
87: 
88: 	__debug_info("kmallocinit", "KMEM_NODE_FIX: %d\n", KMEM_NODE_FIX);
89: 	__debug_info("kmallocinit", "init\n");
90: }
91: 
92: // the question comes that whether we should free an allocator? 
93: // when should we do this? 
94: 
95: // retrhelo: It's not necessary for the kernel to free an allocator,

68: // as kmalloc() use allocpage() and freepage,

70: void kmallocinit(void) {
71: 	// init adam 
72: 	initlock(&(kmem_adam.lock), "kmem_adam");
73: 	kmem_adam.list = NULL;
74: 	kmem_adam.next = NULL;
75: 	kmem_adam.npages = 0;
76: 	kmem_adam.nobjs = 0;
77: 	kmem_adam.obj_size = 
78: 			ROUNDUP16(sizeof(struct kmem_allocator));

80: 	// init kmem_table

82: 		kmem_table[i] = NULL;
83: 	}
84: 	int hash = _hash(kmem_adam.obj_size);
85: 	kmem_table[hash] = &kmem_adam;

87: 
88: 	__debug_info("kmallocinit", "KMEM_NODE_FIX: %d\n", KMEM_NODE_FIX);

oskernel2023-zmz@recover 源码

107: #define _calc_capa(roundup_size) \
108: 	((PGSIZE - ROUNDUP16(KMEM_NODE_FIX)) / ((roundup_size) + 1))
109: 
110: // get the allocator for coming allocation 
111: // `raw_size` means the size may not be aligned 
112: static struct kmem_allocator *get_allocator(uint64 raw_size) {
113: 	uint64 roundup_size = ROUNDUP16(raw_size);
114: 	uint64 hash = _hash(roundup_size);
115: 
116: 	// search if allocator already in table 
117: 	// if allocator exists, then kem_table_lock wouldn't be acquired 
118: 	for (struct kmem_allocator *tmp = kmem_table[hash]; 
119: 			NULL != tmp; tmp = tmp->next) {
120: 		if (roundup_size == tmp->obj_size) {
121: 			return tmp;
122: 		}
123: 	}
124: 
125: 	// enter critical section 
126: 	acquire(&kmem_table_lock);
127: 	// if the previous update have created the allocator 
128: 	// needed here
129: 	if (NULL != kmem_table[hash] && 
130: 			kmem_table[hash]->obj_size == roundup_size) {
131: 		release(&kmem_table_lock);
132: 		return kmem_table[hash];
133: 	}
134: 
135: 	// if not found, then create 
136: 
137: 	// as sizeof(struct kmem_allocator) is guaranteed an 
138: 	// allocator at init time, `_malloc_allocator()` should 
139: 	// not enter `critical section` when calling `get_allocator()`
140: 	struct kmem_allocator *tmp = _malloc_allocator();
141: 	if (NULL != tmp) {
142: 		initlock(&(tmp->lock), "kmem_alloc");
143: 		tmp->list = NULL;
144: 		tmp->obj_size = roundup_size;
145: 		tmp->npages = 0;
146: 		tmp->nobjs = 0;
147: 		tmp->next = kmem_table[hash];
148: 		kmem_table[hash] = tmp;
149: 	}
150: 
151: 	release(&kmem_table_lock);
152: 	// leave critical section 
153: 
154: 	return tmp;
155: }
156: 
157: void *kmalloc(uint size) {
158: 	// border check for `size`
159: 	if (KMEM_OBJ_MIN_SIZE > size) {
160: 		__debug_warn("kmalloc", "size %d too small, reset to %d\n", size, KMEM_OBJ_MIN_SIZE);

109: 
110: // get the allocator for coming allocation

113: 	uint64 roundup_size = ROUNDUP16(raw_size);
114: 	uint64 hash = _hash(roundup_size);
115: 
116: 	// search if allocator already in table 
117: 	// if allocator exists, then kem_table_lock wouldn't be acquired 
118: 	for (struct kmem_allocator *tmp = kmem_table[hash]; 
119: 			NULL != tmp; tmp = tmp->next) {
120: 		if (roundup_size == tmp->obj_size) {
121: 			return tmp;
122: 		}
123: 	}
124: 
125: 	// enter critical section 
126: 	acquire(&kmem_table_lock);
127: 	// if the previous update have created the allocator 
128: 	// needed here
129: 	if (NULL != kmem_table[hash] && 
130: 			kmem_table[hash]->obj_size == roundup_size) {
131: 		release(&kmem_table_lock);
132: 		return kmem_table[hash];
133: 	}
134: 
135: 	// if not found, then create 
136: 
137: 	// as sizeof(struct kmem_allocator) is guaranteed an

142: 		initlock(&(tmp->lock), "kmem_alloc");

150:

153:

匹配分数 1.000 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 0.93, "exact_ast_shape": true, "exact_token_fp": true, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 1.0}

156: 
157: void *kmalloc(uint size) {
158: 	// border check for `size`
159: 	if (KMEM_OBJ_MIN_SIZE > size) {
160: 		__debug_warn("kmalloc", "size %d too small, reset to %d\n", size, KMEM_OBJ_MIN_SIZE);
161: 		size = KMEM_OBJ_MIN_SIZE;
162: 	}
163: 	else if (KMEM_OBJ_MAX_SIZE < size) {
164: 		__debug_error("kmalloc", "size %d out of border\n", size);
165: 		return NULL;
166: 	}
167: 	struct kmem_allocator *alloc = get_allocator(size);
168: 
169: 	// if failed to alloc 
170: 	if (NULL == alloc) {
171: 		__debug_error("kmalloc", "fail to get allocator\n");
172: 		return NULL;
173: 	}
174: 
175: 	// enter critical section `alloc`
176: 	acquire(&(alloc->lock));
177: 
178: 	// if no page available 
179: 	if (NULL == alloc->list) {
180: 		struct kmem_node *tmp = (struct kmem_node*)allocpage();
181: 		if (NULL == tmp) {
182: 			release(&(alloc->lock));
183: 			__debug_warn("kmalloc", "fail to allocate a node\n");
184: 			return NULL;
185: 		}
186: 		alloc->npages++;
187: 
188: 		uint roundup_size = ROUNDUP16(size);
189: 		uint8 capa = _calc_capa(roundup_size);
190: 		tmp->next = NULL;
191: 		tmp->config.obj_size = roundup_size;
192: 		tmp->config.obj_addr = (uint64)tmp + ROUNDUP16(KMEM_NODE_FIX + capa);
193: 
194: 		tmp->avail = 0;
195: 		tmp->cnt = 0;
196: 		for (uint8 i = 0; i < capa - 1; i ++) {
197: 			tmp->table[i] = i + 1;
198: 		}
199: 		tmp->table[capa - 1] = TABLE_END;
200: 
201: 		alloc->list = tmp;
202: 	}
203: 
204: 	alloc->nobjs++;
205: 
206: 	// now the allocator should be ready 
207: 	struct kmem_node *node = alloc->list;
208: 	void *ret;		// the address to be returned 
209: 	ret = (void*)(node->config.obj_addr + 
210: 			((uint64)node->avail) * node->config.obj_size);
211: 	// update `avail` and `cnt`
212: 	node->cnt += 1;
213: 	node->avail = node->table[node->avail];
214: 
215: 	// if kmem_node is fully allocated 
216: 	if (TABLE_END == node->avail) {
217: 		alloc->list = node->next;
218: 	}
219: 
220: 	release(&(alloc->lock));
221: 	// leave critical section `alloc`
222: 
223: 	return ret;
224: }
225: 
226: // `addr` must be an address that's allocated before, pass an unallocated 
227: // address may cause undetectable troubles. 
228: void kfree(void *addr) {
229: 	struct kmem_node *node = (struct kmem_node*)PGROUNDDOWN((uint64)addr);

153: 
154: 	return tmp;
155: }
156: 
157: void *kmalloc(uint size) {
158: 	// border check for `size`
159: 	if (KMEM_OBJ_MIN_SIZE > size) {
160: 		__debug_warn("kmalloc", "size %d too small, reset to %d\n", size, KMEM_OBJ_MIN_SIZE);
161: 		size = KMEM_OBJ_MIN_SIZE;
162: 	}
163: 	else if (KMEM_OBJ_MAX_SIZE < size) {
164: 		__debug_error("kmalloc", "size %d out of border\n", size);
165: 		return NULL;
166: 	}
167: 	struct kmem_allocator *alloc = get_allocator(size);
168: 
169: 	// if failed to alloc 
170: 	if (NULL == alloc) {
171: 		__debug_error("kmalloc", "fail to get allocator\n");
172: 		return NULL;
173: 	}
174: 
175: 	// enter critical section `alloc`
176: 	acquire(&(alloc->lock));
177: 
178: 	// if no page available 
179: 	if (NULL == alloc->list) {
180: 		struct kmem_node *tmp = (struct kmem_node*)allocpage();
181: 		if (NULL == tmp) {
182: 			release(&(alloc->lock));
183: 			__debug_warn("kmalloc", "fail to allocate a node\n");
184: 			return NULL;
185: 		}
186: 		alloc->npages++;
187: 
188: 		uint roundup_size = ROUNDUP16(size);
189: 		uint8 capa = _calc_capa(roundup_size);
190: 		tmp->next = NULL;
191: 		tmp->config.obj_size = roundup_size;
192: 		tmp->config.obj_addr = (uint64)tmp + ROUNDUP16(KMEM_NODE_FIX + capa);
193: 
194: 		tmp->avail = 0;
195: 		tmp->cnt = 0;
196: 		for (uint8 i = 0; i < capa - 1; i ++) {
197: 			tmp->table[i] = i + 1;
198: 		}
199: 		tmp->table[capa - 1] = TABLE_END;
200: 
201: 		alloc->list = tmp;
202: 	}
203: 
204: 	alloc->nobjs++;
205: 
206: 	// now the allocator should be ready 
207: 	struct kmem_node *node = alloc->list;
208: 	void *ret;		// the address to be returned 
209: 	ret = (void*)(node->config.obj_addr + 
210: 			((uint64)node->avail) * node->config.obj_size);
211: 	// update `avail` and `cnt`
212: 	node->cnt += 1;
213: 	node->avail = node->table[node->avail];
214: 
215: 	// if kmem_node is fully allocated 
216: 	if (TABLE_END == node->avail) {
217: 		alloc->list = node->next;
218: 	}
219: 
220: 	release(&(alloc->lock));
221: 	// leave critical section `alloc`
222: 
223: 	return ret;
224: }

229: 	struct kmem_node *node = (struct kmem_node*)PGROUNDDOWN((uint64)addr);

xv6-k210/kernel/mm/kmalloc.c::kfree
匹配分数 0.710 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 0.969, "exact_ast_shape": false, "exact_token_fp": false, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 0.711}

oskernel2023-zmz@recover 源码

223: 	return ret;
224: }
225: 
226: // `addr` must be an address that's allocated before, pass an unallocated 
227: // address may cause undetectable troubles. 
228: void kfree(void *addr) {
229: 	struct kmem_node *node = (struct kmem_node*)PGROUNDDOWN((uint64)addr);
230: 	uint8 avail = ((uint64)addr - node->config.obj_addr) / node->config.obj_size;
231: 
232: 	struct kmem_allocator *alloc = get_allocator(node->config.obj_size);
233: 
234: 	__debug_info("kfree", "alloc: %p, addr: %p\n", alloc, addr);
235: 	// enter critical section `alloc`
236: 	acquire(&(alloc->lock));
237: 
238: 	alloc->nobjs--;
239: 
240: 	// if `node` used to be fully allocated, then re-link it to `alloc`
241: 	if (TABLE_END == node->avail) {
242: 		node->next = alloc->list;
243: 		alloc->list = node;
244: 		__debug_info("kfree", "pickup\n");
245: 	}
246: 
247: 	// node should be on alloc->list 
248: 	node->table[avail] = node->avail;
249: 	node->avail = avail;
250: 	node->cnt -= 1;
251: 
252: 	// if kmem_node has no allocated obj 
253: 	if (0 == node->cnt) {
254: 		__debug_info("kfree", "drop\n");
255: 		struct kmem_node **pprev = &(alloc->list);
256: 		struct kmem_node *tmp = alloc->list;
257: 
258: 		while (NULL != tmp && node != tmp) {
259: 			pprev = &(tmp->next);
260: 			tmp = tmp->next;
261: 		}
262: 		if (NULL == tmp) {
263: 			__debug_error("free", "NULL == tmp\n");
264: 			panic("kfree(): linked list broken!\n");
265: 		}
266: 
267: 		#ifdef DEBUG 
268: 		// display linked list of kmem_allocator 
269: 		for (struct kmem_node *it = alloc->list; NULL != it; it = it->next) {
270: 			printf("%p -> ", it);
271: 		}
272: 		printf("\n");
273: 		#endif 
274: 
275: 		*pprev = tmp->next;
276: 		__debug_info("kfree", "alloc->list = %p\n", alloc->list);
277: 		__debug_info("kfree", "tmp = %p\n", tmp);
278:

225: 
226: // `addr` must be an address that's allocated before, pass an unallocated 
227: // address may cause undetectable troubles. 
228: void kfree(void *addr) {
229: 	struct kmem_node *node = (struct kmem_node*)PGROUNDDOWN((uint64)addr);
230: 	uint8 avail = ((uint64)addr - node->config.obj_addr) / node->config.obj_size;
231: 
232: 	struct kmem_allocator *alloc = get_allocator(node->config.obj_size);
233: 
234: 	__debug_info("kfree", "alloc: %p, addr: %p\n", alloc, addr);
235: 	// enter critical section `alloc`
236: 	acquire(&(alloc->lock));
237: 
238: 	alloc->nobjs--;
239: 
240: 	// if `node` used to be fully allocated, then re-link it to `alloc`
241: 	if (TABLE_END == node->avail) {
242: 		node->next = alloc->list;
243: 		alloc->list = node;
244: 		__debug_info("kfree", "pickup\n");
245: 	}
246: 
247: 	// node should be on alloc->list 
248: 	node->table[avail] = node->avail;
249: 	node->avail = avail;
250: 	node->cnt -= 1;
251: 
252: 	// if kmem_node has no allocated obj 
253: 	if (0 == node->cnt) {
254: 		__debug_info("kfree", "drop\n");
255: 		struct kmem_node **pprev = &(alloc->list);
256: 		struct kmem_node *tmp = alloc->list;
257: 
258: 		while (NULL != tmp && node != tmp) {
259: 			pprev = &(tmp->next);
260: 			tmp = tmp->next;
261: 		}
262: 		// if (NULL == tmp) {
263: 		// 	__debug_error("free", "NULL == tmp\n");
264: 		// 	panic("kfree(): linked list broken!\n");
265: 		// }
266: 
267: 		#ifdef DEBUG 
268: 		// display linked list of kmem_allocator 
269: 		for (struct kmem_node *it = alloc->list; NULL != it; it = it->next) {
270: 			printf("%p -> ", it);
271: 		}
272: 		printf("\n");
273: 		#endif 
274: 
275: 		*pprev = tmp->next;
276: 		__debug_info("kfree", "alloc->list = %p\n", alloc->list);
277: 		__debug_info("kfree", "tmp = %p\n", tmp);
278: 
279: 		freepage(node);
280: 		alloc->npages--;
281: 	}
282: 
283: 	release(&(alloc->lock));
284: 	// leave critical section `alloc`
285: }
286: 
287: // display the content of kmem_allocator 
288: static void km_view_allocator(struct kmem_allocator *alloc) {
289: 	// enter critical section 
290: 	acquire(&(alloc->lock));

287: // display the content of kmem_allocator 
288: static void km_view_allocator(struct kmem_allocator *alloc) {
289: 	// enter critical section 
290: 	acquire(&(alloc->lock));
291: 
292: 	printf("\n"__INFO("kmem_allocator")": size %d, addr: %p taken %d page(s), allocated %d obj(s)\n",
293: 			alloc->obj_size, alloc, alloc->npages, alloc->nobjs);
294: 	// __debug_info("kmem_allocator", "size %d, addr: %p\n", 
295: 	// 		alloc->obj_size, alloc);
296: 	for (struct kmem_node *node = alloc->list; NULL != node; node = node->next) {
297: 		printf(
298: 			"\t"__INFO("kmem_node")":\n"
299: 			"\tconfig.obj_size: %d\n"
300: 			"\tconfig.obj_addr: %p\n"
301: 			"\tavail: %d\n"
302: 			"\tcnt: %d\n", 
303: 			node->config.obj_size, 
304: 			node->config.obj_addr, 
305: 			node->avail, 
306: 			node->cnt
307: 		);
308: 		printf("\t[");
309: 		for (uint8 i = node->avail; TABLE_END != i; i = node->table[i]) {
310: 			printf("%d ", i);
311: 		}
312: 		printf("]\n");
313: 	}
314: 
315: 	release(&(alloc->lock));
316: 	// leave critical section 
317: }

324: 	// enter critical section

58: {
59: 	acquire(&fp->lock);

77: static struct mmap_page *get_mmap_page(struct rb_root *root, uint64 off)
78: {

81: 	while (node) {
82: 		struct mmap_page *map = rb_entry(node, struct mmap_page, rb);

85: 		else if (off > map->f_off)
86: 			node = node->rb_right;
87: 		else
88: 			return map;

92: }

105: static struct mmap_page *get_mmap_with_parent(struct rb_root *root,
106: 								uint64 off, struct rb_node **pparent,
107: 								struct rb_node ***pplink)
108: {
109: 	struct rb_node **p = &root->rb_node;
110: 	struct rb_node *parent = NULL;
111: 	
112: 	while (*p) {
113: 		parent = *p;
114: 		struct mmap_page *map = rb_entry(parent, struct mmap_page, rb);
115: 		if (off < map->f_off)
116: 			p = &parent->rb_left;
117: 		else if (off > map->f_off)
118: 			p = &parent->rb_right;
119: 		else
120: 			return map;
121: 	}
122: 
123: 	*pparent = parent;
124: 	*pplink = p;
125: 	return NULL;
126: }
127:

128: static struct mmap_page *get_adjacent_page(struct mmap_page *map, int prev)
129: {
130: 	struct rb_node *node;
131: 	node = prev ? rb_prev(&map->rb) : rb_next(&map->rb);
132: 	if (!node)
133: 		return NULL;
134: 
135: 	return rb_entry(node, struct mmap_page, rb);
136: }

125: 	return NULL;

127: 
128: static struct mmap_page *get_adjacent_page(struct mmap_page *map, int prev)
129: {
130: 	struct rb_node *node;
131: 	node = prev ? rb_prev(&map->rb) : rb_next(&map->rb);
132: 	if (!node)
133: 		return NULL;
134: 
135: 	return rb_entry(node, struct mmap_page, rb);
136: }

137: 
138: /**
139:  * Functions applied when processes fork or exit.
140:  */
141: 
142: static void __anon_mmapdel(struct seg *seg)
143: {
144: 	if (!MMAP_SHARE(seg->mmap))
145: 		return;
146: 
147: 	struct anonfile *fp = MMAP_FILE(seg->mmap);
148: 	uint64 off = seg->f_off;
149: 	uint64 end = off + seg->sz;
150: 
151: 	acquire(&fp->lock);
152: 	for (; off < end; off += PGSIZE) {
153: 		struct mmap_page *map = get_mmap_page(&fp->mapping, off);
154: 		if (map == NULL)
155: 			panic("__anon_mmapdel: no map node\n");
156: 		
157: 		put_mmap_page(map, &fp->mapping);
158: 	}
159: 	release(&fp->lock);
160: 
161: 	close_anonfile(fp);
162: 	seg->mmap = NULL;
163: }
164:

166: {
167: 	__debug_info("__file_mmapdel", "enter seg=%p, sync=%d\n", seg, sync);

164: 
165: static void __file_mmapdel(struct seg *seg, int sync)
166: {
167: 	__debug_info("__file_mmapdel", "enter seg=%p, sync=%d\n", seg, sync);
168: 
169: 	struct file *fp = MMAP_FILE(seg->mmap);
170: 	if (!MMAP_SHARE(seg->mmap))
171: 		goto out;
172: 
173: 	struct inode *ip = fp->ip;
174: 	uint64 off = seg->f_off;
175: 	uint64 end = off + seg->sz;
176: 
177: 	acquire(&ip->ilock);
178: 	for (; off < end; off += PGSIZE) {
179: 		struct mmap_page *map = get_mmap_page(&ip->mapping, off);
180: 		if (map == NULL)
181: 			panic("__file_mmapdel: no map node\n");
182: 		
183: 		__debug_info("__file_mmapdel", "v=%d, off=%p, len=%d\n",
184: 					map->valid, off, map->f_len);
185: 		if (sync && (seg->flag & PTE_W) && map->pa && off < ip->size) {
186: 			uint64 len = (ip->size - off < map->f_len) ?
187: 							ip->size - off : map->f_len;
188: 			release(&ip->ilock);
189: 			ilock(ip);
190: 		
191: 			// This op may fail, but what can we do about it?
192: 			if (map->valid) {
193: 				__debug_info("__file_mmapdel", "writing: off=%p, len=%d\n", off, map->f_len);
194: 				ip->fop->write(ip, 0, (uint64)map->pa, off, len);
195: 			}
196: 			iunlock(ip);
197: 			acquire(&ip->ilock);
198: 		}
199: 
200: 		put_mmap_page(map, &ip->mapping);
201: 	}
202: 	release(&ip->ilock);
203: 
204: out:
205: 	fileclose(fp);
206: }
207:

211: 	__debug_info("mmapdel", "off=%p, sz=%d\n", seg->f_off, seg->sz);

220: static void __anon_mmapdup(struct seg *seg)
221: {
222: 	if (!MMAP_SHARE(seg->mmap))
223: 		return;
224: 
225: 	struct anonfile *fp = MMAP_FILE(seg->mmap);
226: 	uint64 off = seg->f_off;
227: 	uint64 end = off + seg->sz;
228: 
229: 	acquire(&fp->lock);
230: 	struct mmap_page *map = get_mmap_page(&fp->mapping, off);
231: 	for (; off < end; off += PGSIZE) {
232: 		if (map == NULL || map->f_off != off)
233: 			panic("__anon_mmapdup: no map node\n");
234: 		map->ref++;
235: 		map = get_adjacent_page(map, 0);
236: 	}
237: 	release(&fp->lock);
238: 
239: 	dup_anonfile(fp);
240: }

280: /**
281:  * System call support.
282:  */
283: 
284: static void __mmap_filedup(uint64 mmap)
285: {
286: 	if (MMAP_ANONY(mmap)) {
287: 		struct anonfile *fp = MMAP_FILE(mmap);
288: 		if (fp)
289: 			dup_anonfile(fp);
290: 	} else {
291: 		filedup(MMAP_FILE(mmap));
292: 	}
293: }
294: 
295: /**
296:  * Cut the given segment at start and end. There are three cases to do this:
297:  * 1. seg is covered by [start, end], then no split at all
298:  * 2. it's either start or end that falls in the range of seg, then split seg into two

xv6-k210@final / 来源候选源码

279: 
280: /**
281:  * System call support.
282:  */
283: 
284: static void __mmap_filedup(uint64 mmap)
285: {
286: 	if (MMAP_ANONY(mmap)) {
287: 		struct anonfile *fp = MMAP_FILE(mmap);
288: 		if (fp)
289: 			dup_anonfile(fp);
290: 	} else {
291: 		filedup(MMAP_FILE(mmap));
292: 	}
293: }
294: 
295: /**
296:  * Cut the given segment at start and end. There are three cases to do this:
297:  * 1. seg is covered by [start, end], then no split at all
298:  * 2. it's either start or end that falls in the range of seg, then split seg into two
split_segment修改候选
oskernel2023-zmz@recover 第 302 行 · 主要确定性来源：xv6-k210/kernel/mm/mmap.c::split_segment

候选来源与匹配信号

xv6-k210/kernel/mm/mmap.c::split_segment
匹配分数 0.535 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": false, "exact_token_fp": false, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 0.117}

oskernel2023-zmz@recover 源码

297:  * 1. seg is covered by [start, end], then no split at all
298:  * 2. it's either start or end that falls in the range of seg, then split seg into two
299:  * 3. both start and end fall in the range of seg, then split it into three
300:  * In any case discussed above, this returns the spliting seg in [start, end].
301:  */
302: static struct seg *split_segment(struct seg *s, uint64 start, uint64 end)
303: {
304: 	uint64 segend = s->addr + s->sz;
305: 	if (end < start || (start <= PGROUNDDOWN(s->addr) && end >= PGROUNDUP(segend))) {
306: 		__debug_warn("split_segment", "range [0x%x, 0x%x] is cover seg:{addr=%p, size=0x%x}\n",
307: 						start, end, s->addr, s->sz);
308: 		return s;
309: 	}
310: 
311: 	struct seg *s1, *s2 = NULL;
312: 	start = PGROUNDDOWN(start);
313: 	end = PGROUNDUP(end);
314: 	s1 = kmalloc(sizeof(struct seg));
315: 	if (s1 == NULL)
316: 		return NULL;
317: 	
318: 	// split into three part
319: 	if (start > s->addr && end < segend) {
320: 		if ((s2 = kmalloc(sizeof(struct seg))) == NULL) {
321: 			kfree(s1);
322: 			return NULL;
323: 		}
324: 	}
325: 	
326: 	*s1 = *s;
327: 	if (!s2) {
328: 		uint64 spliter;
329: 		if (start <= s->addr) {
330: 			spliter = end;
331: 			s2 = s;
332: 		} else if (end >= s->addr + s->sz) {
333: 			spliter = start;
334: 			s2 = s1;
335: 		} else
336: 			panic("split_segment: funny case");
337: 
338: 		s1->addr = spliter;
339: 		s1->sz = segend - spliter;
340: 		s->sz -= s1->sz;
341: 
342: 		if (s->type == MMAP) {
343: 			__mmap_filedup(s->mmap);
344: 			s1->f_off += spliter - s->addr;
345: 		}
346: 
347: 		s1->next = s->next;
348: 		s->next = s1;
349: 	}
350: 	else {
351: 		*s2 = *s;
352: 		s2->addr = start;
353: 		s2->sz = end - start;
354: 		s1->addr = end;
355: 		s1->sz = segend - end;
356: 		s->sz = start - s->addr;
357: 
358: 		if (s->type == MMAP) {
359: 			__mmap_filedup(s->mmap);	// twice for two segs
360: 			__mmap_filedup(s->mmap);
361: 			s1->f_off += end - s->addr;
362: 			s2->f_off += start - s->addr;
363: 		}
364: 
365: 		s1->next = s->next;
366: 		s2->next = s1;
367: 		s->next = s2;
368: 	}
369: 
370: 	return s2;
371: }

373: // static int msync_file(struct seg *s, int close)
374: // {
375: // 	if (MMAP_ANONY(s->mmap)) {
376: // 		if (close) {

xv6-k210@final / 来源候选源码

297:  * 1. seg is covered by [start, end], then no split at all
298:  * 2. it's either start or end that falls in the range of seg, then split seg into two
299:  * 3. both start and end fall in the range of seg, then split it into three
300:  * In any case discussed above, this returns the spliting seg in [start, end].
301:  */
302: static struct seg *split_segment(struct seg *s, uint64 start, uint64 end)
303: {
304: 	uint64 segend = s->addr + s->sz;
305: 	if (end < start || (start <= PGROUNDDOWN(s->addr) && end >= PGROUNDUP(segend))) {
306: 		__debug_warn("split_segment", "range [0x%x, 0x%x] is cover seg:{addr=%p, size=0x%x}\n",
307: 						start, end, s->addr, s->sz);
308: 		return s;
309: 	}
310: 
311: 	struct seg *s1, *s2 = NULL;
312: 	start = PGROUNDDOWN(start);
313: 	end = PGROUNDUP(end);
314: 	s1 = kmalloc(sizeof(struct seg));
315: 	if (s1 == NULL)
316: 		return NULL;
317: 	
318: 	// split into three part
319: 	if (start > s->addr && end < segend) {
320: 		if ((s2 = kmalloc(sizeof(struct seg))) == NULL) {
321: 			kfree(s1);
322: 			return NULL;
323: 		}
324: 	}
325: 	
326: 	*s1 = *s;
327: 	if (!s2) {
328: 		uint64 spliter;
329: 		if (start <= s->addr) {
330: 			spliter = end;
331: 			s2 = s;
332: 		} else if (end >= s->addr + s->sz) {
333: 			spliter = start;
334: 			s2 = s1;
335: 		} 
336: 		// else
337: 		// 	panic("split_segment: funny case");
338: 
339: 		s1->addr = spliter;
340: 		s1->sz = segend - spliter;
341: 		s->sz -= s1->sz;
342: 
343: 		if (s->type == MMAP) {
344: 			__mmap_filedup(s->mmap);
345: 			s1->f_off += spliter - s->addr;
346: 		}
347: 
348: 		s1->next = s->next;
349: 		s->next = s1;
350: 	}
351: 	else {
352: 		*s2 = *s;
353: 		s2->addr = start;
354: 		s2->sz = end - start;
355: 		s1->addr = end;
356: 		s1->sz = segend - end;
357: 		s->sz = start - s->addr;
358: 
359: 		if (s->type == MMAP) {
360: 			__mmap_filedup(s->mmap);	// twice for two segs
361: 			__mmap_filedup(s->mmap);
362: 			s1->f_off += end - s->addr;
363: 			s2->f_off += start - s->addr;
364: 		}
365: 
366: 		s1->next = s->next;
367: 		s2->next = s1;
368: 		s->next = s2;
369: 	}
370: 
371: 	return s2;
372: }
373:

377: // 		if (close) {

匹配分数 0.717 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": true, "exact_token_fp": false, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 0.055}

oskernel2023-zmz@recover 源码

439:  *         /     \
440:  * s0 --> s1 --> s2 --> s3
441:  *  \                   /
442:  *  prev            new->next
443:  */
444: static int lookup_fixed_segment(uint64 start, uint64 end,
445: 		struct seg **pprev, struct seg **pnew)
446: {
447: 	if (end > MAXUVA) {
448: 		__debug_warn("lookup_fixed_segment", "Not enough user space in mmap!\n");
449: 		return -EINVAL;
450: 	}
451: 	if (end < start)
452: 		return -EOVERFLOW;
453: 
454: 
455: 	struct proc *p = myproc();
456: 	struct seg *s, *s2;
457: 	struct seg *prev = NULL;
458: 
459: 	for (s = p->segment; s != NULL;)
460: 	{
461: 		if (start >= s->addr + s->sz) {
462: 			prev = s;
463: 			s = s->next;
464: 		} else if (s->addr >= end) {
465: 			break;
466: 		}
467: 		else {
468: 			s2 = split_segment(s, start, end);
469: 			if (s2 == NULL)
470: 				return -ENOMEM;
471: 
472: 			if (s2 != s) {
473: 				/**
474: 				 * After spliting, s2 is not the first part.
475: 				 * Adjust prev and s. Once this happened,
476: 				 * if we still have some segs to split,
477: 				 * s2 should always be s, because we maintain
478: 				 * seg list in order. In other words, prev is settled.
479: 				 */
480: 				for (prev = s; prev->next != s2; prev = prev->next);
481: 				s = s2;
482: 			}
483: 			s = s -> next;
484: 		}
485: 	}
486: 
487: 	*pprev = prev;
488: 
489: 	s2 = kmalloc(sizeof(struct seg));
490: 	if (s2 == NULL)
491: 		return -ENOMEM;
492: 
493: 	memset(s2, 0, sizeof(struct seg));
494: 	s2->addr = start;
495: 	s2->sz = end - start;
496: 	s2->type = MMAP;
497: 	s2->next = s;
498: 	*pnew = s2;
499: 
500: 	return 0;
501: }
502: 
503: 
504: static int lookup_segment(uint64 sz, struct seg **pprev, struct seg **pnewseg)
505: {
506: 	struct proc *p = myproc();

445: static int lookup_fixed_segment(uint64 start, uint64 end,

447: {
448: 	if (end > MAXUVA) {
449: 		__debug_warn("lookup_fixed_segment", "Not enough user space in mmap!\n");
450: 		return -EINVAL;
451: 	}
452: 	if (end < start)

477: 				 * if we still have some segs to split,
478: 				 * s2 should always be s, because we maintain
479: 				 * seg list in order. In other words, prev is settled.
480: 				 */
481: 				for (prev = s; prev->next != s2; prev = prev->next);

483: 			}
484: 			s = s -> next;

486: 	}
487:

497: 	s2->type = MMAP;

499: 	*pnew = s2;
500: 
501: 	return 0;
502: }

匹配分数 1.000 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": true, "exact_token_fp": true, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 1.0}

503: 
504: static int lookup_segment(uint64 sz, struct seg **pprev, struct seg **pnewseg)
505: {
506: 	struct proc *p = myproc();
507: 	struct seg *s = getseg(p->segment, STACK);
508: 	uint64 base = VUMMAP;
509: 	if (base + sz > MAXUVA) {
510: 		__debug_warn("lookup_segment", "Not enough user space in mmap!\n");
511: 		return -EINVAL;
512: 	}
513: 	if (base + sz < base)
514: 		return -EOVERFLOW;
515: 
516: 	for (struct seg *ss = s->next; ss != NULL; ss = ss->next) {
517: 		if (ss->addr >= base + sz)
518: 			break;
519: 		s = ss;
520: 		base = PGROUNDUP(ss->addr + ss->sz);
521: 	}
522: 	if (base + sz > MAXUVA) {
523: 		__debug_warn("lookup_segment", "Not enough user space in mmap!\n");
524: 		return -EINVAL;
525: 	}
526: 
527: 	struct seg* newseg = kmalloc(sizeof(struct seg));
528: 	if (newseg == NULL) {
529: 		__debug_warn("lookup_segment", "newseg failed in mmap!\n");
530: 		return -ENOMEM;
531: 	}
532: 
533: 	newseg->type = MMAP;
534: 	newseg->addr = base;
535: 	newseg->sz = sz;
536: 	newseg->mmap = NULL;
537: 	newseg->next = s->next;
538: 	*pprev = s;
539: 	*pnewseg = newseg;
540: 	return 0;
541: }

542: 
543: static int mmap_file(struct seg *s, uint64 len, int flags, struct file *f, int64 off)
544: {
545: 	struct inode *ip = f->ip;
546: 
547: 	s->f_off = off;
548: 	s->f_sz = 0;	// this is for elf, we don't care about it here
549: 	s->mmap = (uint64) filedup(f);
550: 
551: 	if (!(flags & MAP_SHARED))
552: 		return 0;
553: 
554: 	uint64 n;
555: 	struct mmap_page *map;
556: 	struct rb_node *parent;
557: 	struct rb_node **plink;
558: 
559: 	acquire(&ip->ilock);
560: 	for (n = 0; n < len; n += PGSIZE) {
561: 		map = get_mmap_with_parent(&ip->mapping, off + n, &parent, &plink);
562: 		if (map) {
563: 			map->ref++;
564: 		} else {
565: 			map = kmalloc(sizeof(struct mmap_page));
566: 			if (map == NULL)
567: 				goto fail;
568: 
569: 			map->f_off = off + n;
570: 			map->f_len = (PGSIZE < len - n) ? PGSIZE : len - n;
571: 			map->pa = NULL;
572: 			map->ref = 1;
573: 			map->valid = 0;
574: 			rb_link_node(&map->rb, parent, plink);
575: 			rb_insert_color(&map->rb, &ip->mapping);
576: 		}
577: 	}
578: 	release(&ip->ilock);
579: 
580: 	s->mmap |= MMAP_SHARE_FLAG;
581: 	return 0;
582: 
583: fail:
584: 	for (n -= PGSIZE; n >= 0; n -= PGSIZE) {
585: 		map = get_mmap_page(&ip->mapping, off + n);
586: 		put_mmap_page(map, &ip->mapping);
587: 	}
588: 	release(&ip->ilock);
589: 	fileclose(f);
590: 	return -ENOMEM;
591: }
592:

594: {
595: 	/**
596: 	 * We can map the first page referring to locality.

544: static int mmap_file(struct seg *s, uint64 len, int flags, struct file *f, int64 off)

558: 	struct rb_node **plink = NULL;
559: 
560: 	acquire(&ip->ilock);

562: 		map = get_mmap_with_parent(&ip->mapping, off + n, &parent, &plink);

564: 			map->ref++;

569:

571: 			map->f_len = (PGSIZE < len - n) ? PGSIZE : len - n;
572: 			map->pa = NULL;
573: 			map->ref = 1;
574: 			map->valid = 0;
575: 			rb_link_node(&map->rb, parent, plink);
576: 			rb_insert_color(&map->rb, &ip->mapping);
577: 		}
578: 	}
579: 	release(&ip->ilock);
580: 
581: 	s->mmap |= MMAP_SHARE_FLAG;
582: 	return 0;
583:

585: 	for (n -= PGSIZE; n >= 0; n -= PGSIZE) {
586: 		map = get_mmap_page(&ip->mapping, off + n);
587: 		put_mmap_page(map, &ip->mapping);
588: 	}
589: 	release(&ip->ilock);
590: 	fileclose(f);
591: 	return -ENOMEM;
592: }

匹配分数 0.718 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": false, "exact_token_fp": false, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 0.727}

592: 
593: static int mmap_anonymous(struct seg *s, int flags)
594: {
595: 	/**
596: 	 * We can map the first page referring to locality.
597: 	 * Other pages will be mapped in page fault handler.
598: 	 * But there may be some original mappings in the pagetable,
599: 	 * We'd better leave all the work for page fault handler.
600: 	 */
601: 
602: 	if (!(flags & MAP_SHARED)) {
603: 		s->mmap = NULL;
604: 		goto out;
605: 	}
606: 
607: 	struct anonfile *fp = alloc_anonfile();
608: 	if (!fp)
609: 		return -ENOMEM;
610: 
611: 	uint64 off;
612: 	struct mmap_page *map;
613: 	struct rb_node *parent;
614: 	struct rb_node **plink;
615: 
616: 	/**
617: 	 * Build a mmap pages tree at the beginning, the tree nodes
618: 	 * will keep references of the pages (not now), in case of
619: 	 * those shared mapping pages are free due to some processes
620: 	 * exit(). There may be still other processes who are mapping
621: 	 * with those pages, but haven't really mapped them into the
622: 	 * pagetable yet.
623: 	 */
624: 	for (off = 0; off < s->sz; off += PGSIZE) {
625: 		map = kmalloc(sizeof(struct mmap_page));
626: 		if (map == NULL)
627: 			goto fail;
628: 
629: 		map->f_off = off;
630: 		map->f_len = PGSIZE;
631: 		map->pa = NULL;
632: 		map->ref = 1;
633: 		map->valid = 0;
634: 
635: 		// shouldn't get result since we haven't put "off" into it
636: 		if (get_mmap_with_parent(&fp->mapping, off, &parent, &plink))
637: 			panic("mmap_anonymous: rbtree");
638: 		rb_link_node(&map->rb, parent, plink);
639: 		rb_insert_color(&map->rb, &fp->mapping);
640: 	}
641: 
642: 	s->mmap = MMAP_SHARE_FLAG | (uint64)fp;
643: 
644: out:
645: 	s->mmap |= MMAP_ANONY_FLAG;
646: 	s->f_off = 0;
647: 	s->f_sz = 0;
648: 
649: 	return 0;
650: 
651: fail:
652: 	for (off -= PGSIZE; off >= 0; off -= PGSIZE) {
653: 		map = get_mmap_page(&fp->mapping, off);
654: 		put_mmap_page(map, &fp->mapping);
655: 	}
656: 	close_anonfile(fp);
657: 	return -ENOMEM;
658: }
659:

661: {
662: 	if (f) {
663: 		struct inode *ip = f->ip;

xv6-k210@final / 来源候选源码

589: 	release(&ip->ilock);
590: 	fileclose(f);
591: 	return -ENOMEM;
592: }
593: 
594: static int mmap_anonymous(struct seg *s, int flags)
595: {
596: 	/**
597: 	 * We can map the first page referring to locality.
598: 	 * Other pages will be mapped in page fault handler.
599: 	 * But there may be some original mappings in the pagetable,
600: 	 * We'd better leave all the work for page fault handler.
601: 	 */
602: 
603: 	if (!(flags & MAP_SHARED)) {
604: 		s->mmap = NULL;
605: 		goto out;
606: 	}
607: 
608: 	struct anonfile *fp = alloc_anonfile();
609: 	if (!fp)
610: 		return -ENOMEM;
611: 
612: 	uint64 off;
613: 	struct mmap_page *map;
614: 	struct rb_node *parent = NULL;
615: 	struct rb_node **plink = NULL;
616: 
617: 	/**
618: 	 * Build a mmap pages tree at the beginning, the tree nodes
619: 	 * will keep references of the pages (not now), in case of
620: 	 * those shared mapping pages are free due to some processes
621: 	 * exit(). There may be still other processes who are mapping
622: 	 * with those pages, but haven't really mapped them into the
623: 	 * pagetable yet.
624: 	 */
625: 	for (off = 0; off < s->sz; off += PGSIZE) {
626: 		map = kmalloc(sizeof(struct mmap_page));
627: 		if (map == NULL)
628: 			goto fail;
629: 
630: 		map->f_off = off;
631: 		map->f_len = PGSIZE;
632: 		map->pa = NULL;
633: 		map->ref = 1;
634: 		map->valid = 0;
635: 
636: 		// shouldn't get result since we haven't put "off" into it
637: 		// if (get_mmap_with_parent(&fp->mapping, off, &parent, &plink))
638: 		// 	panic("mmap_anonymous: rbtree");
639: 		get_mmap_with_parent(&fp->mapping, off, &parent, &plink);
640: 		rb_link_node(&map->rb, parent, plink);
641: 		rb_insert_color(&map->rb, &fp->mapping);
642: 	}
643: 
644: 	s->mmap = MMAP_SHARE_FLAG | (uint64)fp;
645: 
646: out:
647: 	s->mmap |= MMAP_ANONY_FLAG;
648: 	s->f_off = 0;
649: 	s->f_sz = 0;
650: 
651: 	return 0;
652: 
653: fail:
654: 	for (off -= PGSIZE; off >= 0; off -= PGSIZE) {
655: 		map = get_mmap_page(&fp->mapping, off);
656: 		put_mmap_page(map, &fp->mapping);
657: 	}
658: 	close_anonfile(fp);
659: 	return -ENOMEM;
660: }

659: 
660: uint64 do_mmap(uint64 start, uint64 len, int prot, int flags, struct file *f, int64 off)
661: {
662: 	if (f) {
663: 		struct inode *ip = f->ip;
664: 		if (off >= ip->size) {
665: 			__debug_warn("mmap", "length overflows!\n");
666: 			return -EINVAL;
667: 		}
668: 		if (S_ISDIR(ip->mode))
669: 			return -EISDIR;
670: 		if ((f->readable ^ (prot & PROT_READ)) || (f->writable ^ ((prot & PROT_WRITE) >> 1))) {
671: 			__debug_warn("mmap", "unmatched priviledge: w/r=%d/%d, prot x/w/r=%d/%d/%d\n",
672: 						f->writable, f->readable,
673: 						!!(prot & PROT_EXEC), !!(prot & PROT_WRITE), !!(prot & PROT_READ));
674: 			return -EPERM;
675: 		}
676: 	} else if (!(flags & MAP_ANONYMOUS))
677: 		panic("do_mmap: bad file");	// sys_mmap should check this
678: 
679: 	uint64 sz = PGROUNDUP(len);
680: 	struct seg *prev, *new;
681: 	int ret = 0;
682: 	if (flags & MAP_FIXED)
683: 		ret = lookup_fixed_segment(start, start + sz, &prev, &new);
684: 	else
685: 		ret = lookup_segment(sz, &prev, &new);
686: 
687: 	if (ret < 0)
688: 		return ret;
689: 
690: 	new->flag = (prot << 1) & (PTE_X|PTE_W|PTE_R);
691: 
692: 	if (f)
693: 		ret = mmap_file(new, len, flags, f, off);
694: 	else
695: 		ret = mmap_anonymous(new, flags);
696: 
697: 	if (ret < 0) {
698: 		kfree(new);
699: 		return ret;
700: 	}
701: 
702: 	/**
703: 	 * Clean up existing mapping if it is fixed mapping.
704: 	 * Otherwise, we have prev->next == new->next, so the
705: 	 * deletion below won't be carried.
706: 	 */
707: 	struct proc *p = myproc();
708: 	struct seg *del = prev ? prev->next : p->segment;
709: 	while (del != new->next) {
710: 		del = delseg(p->pagetable, del);
711: 	}
712: 
713: 	if (prev) {
714: 		prev->next = new;
715: 	} else {
716: 		p->segment = new;
717: 	}
718: 	sfence_vma();
719: 	return new->addr;
720: }
721:

723: {
724: 	uint64 sz = PGROUNDUP(len);
725: 	uint64 end = start + sz;

658: 	close_anonfile(fp);

662: uint64 do_mmap(uint64 start, uint64 len, int prot, int flags, struct file *f, int64 off)
663: {
664: 	if (f) {
665: 		struct inode *ip = f->ip;
666: 		if (off >= ip->size) {
667: 			__debug_warn("mmap", "length overflows!\n");
668: 			return -EINVAL;
669: 		}
670: 		if (S_ISDIR(ip->mode))
671: 			return -EISDIR;
672: 		if ((f->readable ^ (prot & PROT_READ)) || (f->writable ^ ((prot & PROT_WRITE) >> 1))) {
673: 			__debug_warn("mmap", "unmatched priviledge: w/r=%d/%d, prot x/w/r=%d/%d/%d\n",
674: 						f->writable, f->readable,
675: 						!!(prot & PROT_EXEC), !!(prot & PROT_WRITE), !!(prot & PROT_READ));
676: 			return -EPERM;
677: 		}
678: 	} 
679: 	// else if (!(flags & MAP_ANONYMOUS))
680: 	// 	panic("do_mmap: bad file");	// sys_mmap should check this
681: 
682: 	uint64 sz = PGROUNDUP(len);
683: 	struct seg *prev, *new;
684: 	int ret = 0;
685: 	if (flags & MAP_FIXED)
686: 		ret = lookup_fixed_segment(start, start + sz, &prev, &new);
687: 	else
688: 		ret = lookup_segment(sz, &prev, &new);
689: 
690: 	if (ret < 0)
691: 		return ret;
692: 
693: 	new->flag = (prot << 1) & (PTE_X|PTE_W|PTE_R);
694: 
695: 	if (f)
696: 		ret = mmap_file(new, len, flags, f, off);
697: 	else
698: 		ret = mmap_anonymous(new, flags);
699: 
700: 	if (ret < 0) {
701: 		kfree(new);
702: 		return ret;
703: 	}
704: 
705: 	/**
706: 	 * Clean up existing mapping if it is fixed mapping.
707: 	 * Otherwise, we have prev->next == new->next, so the
708: 	 * deletion below won't be carried.
709: 	 */
710: 	struct proc *p = myproc();
711: 	struct seg *del = prev ? prev->next : p->segment;
712: 	while (del != new->next) {
713: 		del = delseg(p->pagetable, del);
714: 	}
715: 
716: 	if (prev) {
717: 		prev->next = new;
718: 	} else {
719: 		p->segment = new;
720: 	}
721: 	sfence_vma();
722: 	return new->addr;
723: }

匹配分数 0.712 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": true, "exact_token_fp": false, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 0.039}

721: 
722: int do_munmap(uint64 start, uint64 len)
723: {
724: 	uint64 sz = PGROUNDUP(len);
725: 	uint64 end = start + sz;
726: 	struct proc *p;
727: 	struct seg *prev = NULL;
728: 	struct seg *s, *del;
729: 	int ret;
730: 
731: 	if (end < start)
732: 		return -EOVERFLOW;
733: 
734: 	/**
735: 	 * This function takes care of the seg-split problem,
736: 	 * which allows us to take advantage of it: simply free
737: 	 * the segs it returns to us. But it also returns a new
738: 	 * seg we don't use at all, just free it at last.
739: 	 */
740: 	ret = lookup_fixed_segment(start, end, &prev, &s);
741: 	if (ret < 0)
742: 		return ret;
743: 
744: 	p = myproc();
745: 	del = prev ? prev->next : p->segment;
746: 	ret = 0;
747: 	if (del != s->next) {
748: 		ret = 1;
749: 		while (del != s->next) {
750: 			del = delseg(p->pagetable, del);
751: 		}
752: 	}
753: 
754: 	if (ret) {	// it did delete some segs
755: 		if (prev) {
756: 			prev->next = s->next;
757: 		} else {
758: 			p->segment = s->next;
759: 		}
760: 		sfence_vma();
761: 	}
762: 
763: 	kfree(s);
764: 	return 0;
765: }
766: 
767:

769: {
770: 	struct proc *p = myproc();

721: 	sfence_vma();
722: 	return new->addr;
723: }

738: 	 * This function takes care of the seg-split problem,

741: 	 * seg we don't use at all, just free it at last.
742: 	 */
743: 	ret = lookup_fixed_segment(start, end, &prev, &s);

745: 		return ret;

760: 		} else {
761: 			p->segment = s->next;
762: 		}
763: 		sfence_vma();
764: 	}
765: 
766: 	kfree(s);
767: 	return 0;
768: }

匹配分数 0.699 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": false, "exact_token_fp": false, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 0.664}

766: 
767: 
768: int do_msync(uint64 addr, uint64 len, int flags)
769: {
770: 	struct proc *p = myproc();
771: 	struct seg *s = partofseg(p->segment, addr, addr + len);
772: 	if (s == NULL)
773: 		return -EFAULT;
774: 
775: 	// private mapping and anonymous mapping can not be synchronized
776: 	if (s->type != MMAP || MMAP_ANONY(s->mmap) || !MMAP_SHARE(s->mmap))
777: 		return 0;
778: 
779: 	// MS_INVALIDATE does nothing on our system
780: 	if (!(flags & (MS_ASYNC|MS_SYNC)))
781: 		goto out;
782: 
783: 	struct file *f = MMAP_FILE(s->mmap);
784: 	struct inode *ip = f->ip;
785: 	int ret = 0;
786: 
787: 	uint64 foff = s->f_off + (addr - s->addr);
788: 	uint64 sz = s->sz < len ? s->sz : len;
789: 	uint64 end = foff + sz;
790: 
791: 	acquire(&ip->ilock);
792: 	for (; foff < end; foff += PGSIZE) {
793: 		struct mmap_page *map = get_mmap_page(&ip->mapping, foff);
794: 		if (map == NULL)
795: 			panic("__file_mmapdel: no map node\n");
796: 		
797: 		if (map->pa && foff < ip->size) {
798: 			uint64 flen = (ip->size - foff < map->f_len) ?
799: 							ip->size - foff : map->f_len;
800: 			release(&ip->ilock);
801: 			ilock(ip);
802: 		
803: 			// This op may fail, but what can we do about it?
804: 			if (map->valid) {
805: 				ip->fop->write(ip, 0, (uint64)map->pa, foff, flen);
806: 			}
807: 			iunlock(ip);
808: 			acquire(&ip->ilock);
809: 		}
810: 	}
811: 	release(&ip->ilock);
812: 
813: out:
814: 	return ret;
815: }
816: 
817: 
818: /**
819:  * Page fault handler, the real allocator.
820:  */

771: int do_msync(uint64 addr, uint64 len, int flags)
772: {
773: 	struct proc *p = myproc();
774: 	struct seg *s = partofseg(p->segment, addr, addr + len);
775: 	if (s == NULL)
776: 		return -EFAULT;
777: 
778: 	// private mapping and anonymous mapping can not be synchronized
779: 	if (s->type != MMAP || MMAP_ANONY(s->mmap) || !MMAP_SHARE(s->mmap))
780: 		return 0;
781: 
782: 	// MS_INVALIDATE does nothing on our system
783: 	if (!(flags & (MS_ASYNC|MS_SYNC))) {
784: 		// goto out;
785: 		return -1;
786: 	}
787: 
788: 	struct file *f = MMAP_FILE(s->mmap);
789: 	struct inode *ip = f->ip;
790: 	int ret = 0;
791: 
792: 	uint64 foff = s->f_off + (addr - s->addr);
793: 	uint64 sz = s->sz < len ? s->sz : len;
794: 	uint64 end = foff + sz;
795: 
796: 	acquire(&ip->ilock);
797: 	for (; foff < end; foff += PGSIZE) {
798: 		struct mmap_page *map = get_mmap_page(&ip->mapping, foff);
799: 		// if (map == NULL)
800: 		// 	panic("__file_mmapdel: no map node\n");
801: 		
802: 		if (map->pa && foff < ip->size) {
803: 			uint64 flen = (ip->size - foff < map->f_len) ?
804: 							ip->size - foff : map->f_len;
805: 			release(&ip->ilock);
806: 			ilock(ip);
807: 		
808: 			// This op may fail, but what can we do about it?
809: 			if (map->valid) {
810: 				ip->fop->write(ip, 0, (uint64)map->pa, foff, flen);
811: 			}
812: 			iunlock(ip);
813: 			acquire(&ip->ilock);
814: 		}
815: 	}
816: 	release(&ip->ilock);
817: 
818: out:
819: 	return ret;
820: }

匹配分数 0.648 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": false, "exact_token_fp": false, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 0.492}

817: 
818: /**
819:  * Page fault handler, the real allocator.
820:  */
821: 
822: static int handle_anonymous_shared(uint64 badaddr, struct seg *s)
823: {
824: 	struct anonfile *fp = MMAP_FILE(s->mmap);
825: 	
826: 	uint64 off = s->f_off + (PGROUNDDOWN(badaddr) - s->addr);
827: 	struct mmap_page *map;
828: 	
829: 	acquire(&fp->lock);
830: 	map = get_mmap_page(&fp->mapping, off);
831: 	if (map == NULL)
832: 		panic("handle_anonymous_shared: no map node");
833: 
834: 	if (map->pa == NULL) {
835: 		map->pa = allocpage();
836: 		if (map->pa == NULL) {
837: 			release(&fp->lock);
838: 			return -ENOMEM;
839: 		}
840: 		memset(map->pa, 0, PGSIZE);
841: 		pagereg((uint64)map->pa, 1);	// keep a reference on the page
842: 	}
843: 	release(&fp->lock);
844: 
845: 	struct proc *p = myproc();
846: 	if (mappages(p->pagetable, PGROUNDDOWN(badaddr), PGSIZE,
847: 				(uint64)map->pa, s->flag|PTE_U) < 0)
848: 		return -ENOMEM;
849: 
850: 	sfence_vma();
851: 	return 0;
852: }

857: 	struct seg *s = locateseg(p->segment, badaddr);

853: 
854: static void *__page_file_swap(struct inode *ip, uint64 foff, uint64 badaddr)
855: {
856: 	struct proc *p = myproc();
857: 	struct seg *s = locateseg(p->segment, badaddr);
858: 	uint64 start = s->f_off;	// start offset
859: 	uint64 end = start + s->sz;	// end offset
860: 	void *page = NULL;
861: 
862: 	if (s->sz == PGSIZE)		// can't spare any page
863: 		return NULL;
864: 
865: 	acquire(&ip->ilock);
866: 
867: 	uint64 off = (foff == start) ? end - PGSIZE : foff - PGSIZE;
868: 	struct mmap_page *map = get_mmap_page(&ip->mapping, off);
869: 	int miss = 0, free = 0;
870: 
871: 	while (off != foff)
872: 	{
873: 		if (map->ref == 1 && map->pa) {	// only us
874: 			void *victim = map->pa;
875: 			map->pa = NULL;
876: 			release(&ip->ilock);
877: 
878: 			ilock(ip);
879: 			if ((s->flag & PTE_W) && map->valid && off < ip->size) {
880: 				uint64 len = (ip->size - off < map->f_len) ?
881: 								ip->size - off : map->f_len;
882: 				ip->fop->write(ip, 0, (uint64)victim, off, len);
883: 			}
884: 			map->valid = 0;
885: 			iunlock(ip);
886: 
887: 			unmappages(p->pagetable, s->addr + (off - s->f_off), 1, VM_USER|VM_FREE);
888: 			
889: 			if (pageput((uint64)victim) != 0)
890: 				panic("__page_file_swap: bad page ref");
891: 			if (!page)
892: 				page = victim;
893: 			else
894: 				freepage(victim);
895: 
896: 			free++;
897: 			miss = 0;
898: 
899: 			acquire(&ip->ilock);
900: 		} else {
901: 			miss++;
902: 			if (free >= 10 && miss >= 5) {		// enough
903: 				printf(__INFO("mmap file swap")" evict enough %d\n", free);
904: 				break;
905: 			}
906: 		}
907: 		if (off == start) {
908: 			off = end - PGSIZE;
909: 			map = get_mmap_page(&ip->mapping, off);
910: 		} else {
911: 			off -= PGSIZE;
912: 			map = get_adjacent_page(map, 1);
913: 		}
914: 		// if (!map || map->f_off != off)
915: 		// 	panic("__page_file_swap: bad map node");
916: 	}
917: 	release(&ip->ilock);
918: 
919: 	return page;
920: }
921:

923: {

925: 		return -EIO;

922: static int __page_file_read(struct inode *ip, uint64 off, uint64 page)
923: {
924: 	if (off >= ip->size) {	// offset out of file size
925: 		return -EIO;				
926: 	}
927: 	uint64 len = (PGSIZE < ip->size - off) ? PGSIZE : ip->size - off;
928: 
929: 	/**
930: 	 * This read could meet some problem. If the result len is not
931: 	 * equal to the expected len, should we sail that's a fault?
932: 	 * or just ignore this? We choose the later at present.
933: 	 */
934: 	len = ip->fop->read(ip, 0, page, off, len);
935: 	if (len < PGSIZE)
936: 		memset((void *)(page + len), 0, PGSIZE - len);
937: 
938: 	return 0;
939: }

941: static int handle_file_mmap(uint64 badaddr, struct seg *s)
942: {
943: 	int share = MMAP_SHARE(s->mmap);
944: 	struct file *fp = MMAP_FILE(s->mmap);
945: 	struct inode *ip = fp->ip;
946: 	uint64 off = s->f_off + (PGROUNDDOWN(badaddr) - s->addr);
947: 	struct mmap_page *map;
948: 	void *pa;
949: 
950: 	acquire(&ip->ilock);
951: 	map = get_mmap_page(&ip->mapping, off);
952: 
953: 	if (share) {
954: 		if (map == NULL)
955: 			panic("handle_file_mmap: no map node");
956: 		
957: 		/**
958: 		 * Here is another problem. The first fault allocates a page here,
959: 		 * then releases the ilock to read the file. At this moment, the
960: 		 * second fault (by other process) arrives, sees that the page is
961: 		 * ready, then maps and leaves, which could totally happen before
962: 		 * the first one finishing its reading.
963: 		 * So we use a 'valid' field and the sleeplock to sync this.
964: 		 */
965: 		if (map->pa == NULL) {
966: 			pa = allocpage();
967: 			if (pa == NULL) {
968: 				release(&ip->ilock);
969: 				pa = __page_file_swap(ip, off, badaddr);
970: 				if (pa == NULL)
971: 					return -ENOMEM;
972: 
973: 				acquire(&ip->ilock);
974: 				if (map->pa == NULL) {
975: 					pagereg((uint64)pa, 1);
976: 					map->pa = pa;
977: 				} else {
978: 					freepage(pa);
979: 					pa = map->pa;
980: 				}
981: 			} else {
982: 				pagereg((uint64)pa, 1);		// keep refs on shared mappings
983: 				map->pa = pa;
984: 			}
985: 		} else {
986: 			pa = map->pa;
987: 		}
988: 		release(&ip->ilock);
989: 		
990: 		/**
991: 		 * There is the page, but is it valid? That means the contents of the
992: 		 * file may be still on disk. Or aren't they? That's hard to answer.
993: 		 * The reading might fail due to the exceeding offset or other reasons,
994: 		 * leaving an empty page without proper file contents.
995: 		 * The 'valid' flag is to deal with this case. Besides, the sleeplock
996: 		 * solves the problem we named above.
997: 		 */
998: 		ilock(ip);
999: 		if (!map->valid) {
1000: 			if (__page_file_read(ip, off, (uint64)pa) < 0) {
1001: 				iunlock(ip);
1002: 				return -EIO;
1003: 			}
1004: 			map->valid = 1;
1005: 		}
1006: 		iunlock(ip);
1007: 	}
1008: 	else {
1009: 		pa = allocpage();
1010: 		if (!pa) {
1011: 			release(&ip->ilock);
1012: 			return -ENOMEM;
1013: 		}
1014: 		pagereg((uint64)pa, 0);				// don't keep refs on private mappings
1015: 
1016: 		if (map && map->pa && map->valid) {	// copy from shared page if existing
1017: 			memmove(pa, map->pa, PGSIZE);
1018: 			release(&ip->ilock);
1019: 		} else {
1020: 			release(&ip->ilock);
1021: 			ilock(ip);
1022: 			if (__page_file_read(ip, off, (uint64)pa) < 0) {
1023: 				iunlock(ip);
1024: 				freepage(pa);
1025: 				return -EIO;
1026: 			}
1027: 			iunlock(ip);
1028: 		}
1029: 	}
1030: 	
1031: 	struct proc *p = myproc();
1032: 	if (mappages(p->pagetable, PGROUNDDOWN(badaddr), PGSIZE,
1033: 				(uint64)pa, s->flag|PTE_U) < 0)
1034: 	{
1035: 		if (!share)
1036: 			freepage(pa);	
1037: 		return -ENOMEM;
1038: 	}
1039: 
1040: 	sfence_vma();
1041: 	return 0;
1042: }
1043: 
1044: /**
1045:  * @param	kind	load-0 | store-1 | execute-2
1046:  */
1047: int handle_page_fault_mmap(int kind, uint64 badaddr, struct seg *s)

946: 		memset((void *)(page + len), 0, PGSIZE - len);
947: 
948: 	return 0;
949: }

951: static int handle_file_mmap(uint64 badaddr, struct seg *s)
952: {
953: 	int share = MMAP_SHARE(s->mmap);
954: 	struct file *fp = MMAP_FILE(s->mmap);
955: 	struct inode *ip = fp->ip;
956: 	uint64 off = s->f_off + (PGROUNDDOWN(badaddr) - s->addr);
957: 	struct mmap_page *map;
958: 	void *pa;
959: 
960: 	acquire(&ip->ilock);
961: 	map = get_mmap_page(&ip->mapping, off);
962: 
963: 	if (share) {
964: 		// if (map == NULL)
965: 		// 	panic("handle_file_mmap: no map node");
966: 		
967: 		/**
968: 		 * Here is another problem. The first fault allocates a page here,
969: 		 * then releases the ilock to read the file. At this moment, the
970: 		 * second fault (by other process) arrives, sees that the page is
971: 		 * ready, then maps and leaves, which could totally happen before
972: 		 * the first one finishing its reading.
973: 		 * So we use a 'valid' field and the sleeplock to sync this.
974: 		 */
975: 		if (map->pa == NULL) {
976: 			pa = allocpage();
977: 			if (pa == NULL) {
978: 				release(&ip->ilock);
979: 				// pa = __page_file_swap(ip, off, badaddr);
980: 				// if (pa == NULL)
981: 				return -ENOMEM;
982: 
983: 				// acquire(&ip->ilock);
984: 				// if (map->pa == NULL) {
985: 				// 	pagereg((uint64)pa, 1);
986: 				// 	map->pa = pa;
987: 				// } else {
988: 				// 	freepage(pa);
989: 				// 	pa = map->pa;
990: 				// }
991: 			} else {
992: 				pagereg((uint64)pa, 1);		// keep refs on shared mappings
993: 				map->pa = pa;
994: 			}
995: 		} else {
996: 			pa = map->pa;
997: 		}
998: 		release(&ip->ilock);
999: 		
1000: 		/**
1001: 		 * There is the page, but is it valid? That means the contents of the
1002: 		 * file may be still on disk. Or aren't they? That's hard to answer.
1003: 		 * The reading might fail due to the exceeding offset or other reasons,
1004: 		 * leaving an empty page without proper file contents.
1005: 		 * The 'valid' flag is to deal with this case. Besides, the sleeplock
1006: 		 * solves the problem we named above.
1007: 		 */
1008: 		ilock(ip);
1009: 		if (!map->valid) {
1010: 			if (__page_file_read(ip, off, (uint64)pa) < 0) {
1011: 				iunlock(ip);
1012: 				return -EIO;
1013: 			}
1014: 			map->valid = 1;
1015: 		}
1016: 		iunlock(ip);
1017: 	}
1018: 	else {
1019: 		pa = allocpage();
1020: 		if (!pa) {
1021: 			release(&ip->ilock);
1022: 			return -ENOMEM;
1023: 		}
1024: 		pagereg((uint64)pa, 0);				// don't keep refs on private mappings
1025: 
1026: 		if (map && map->pa && map->valid) {	// copy from shared page if existing
1027: 			memmove(pa, map->pa, PGSIZE);
1028: 			release(&ip->ilock);
1029: 		} else {
1030: 			release(&ip->ilock);
1031: 			ilock(ip);
1032: 			if (__page_file_read(ip, off, (uint64)pa) < 0) {
1033: 				iunlock(ip);
1034: 				freepage(pa);
1035: 				return -EIO;
1036: 			}
1037: 			iunlock(ip);
1038: 		}
1039: 	}
1040: 	
1041: 	struct proc *p = myproc();
1042: 	if (mappages(p->pagetable, PGROUNDDOWN(badaddr), PGSIZE,
1043: 				(uint64)pa, s->flag|PTE_U) < 0)
1044: 	{
1045: 		if (!share)
1046: 			freepage(pa);	
1047: 		return -ENOMEM;
1048: 	}
1049: 
1050: 	sfence_vma();
1051: 	return 0;
1052: }

1055:  * @param	kind	load-0 | store-1 | execute-2

1057: int handle_page_fault_mmap(int kind, uint64 badaddr, struct seg *s)

oskernel2023-zmz@recover 第 1047 行 · 主要确定性来源：xv6-k210/kernel/mm/mmap.c::handle_page_fault_mmap

候选来源与匹配信号

xv6-k210/kernel/mm/mmap.c::handle_page_fault_mmap
匹配分数 1.000 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": true, "exact_token_fp": true, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 1.0}

oskernel2023-zmz@recover 源码

1042: }
1043: 
1044: /**
1045:  * @param	kind	load-0 | store-1 | execute-2
1046:  */
1047: int handle_page_fault_mmap(int kind, uint64 badaddr, struct seg *s)
1048: {
1049: 	int illegel;
1050: 	switch (kind) {
1051: 		case 0: illegel = !(s->flag & PTE_R); break;
1052: 		case 1: illegel = !(s->flag & PTE_W); break;
1053: 		case 2: illegel = !(s->flag & PTE_X); break;
1054: 		default:
1055: 			illegel = 0;
1056: 			panic("handle_page_fault_mmap: kind");
1057: 	}
1058: 	if (illegel) {	// the access broke the protection
1059: 		__debug_warn("handle_page_fault_mmap", "illegal access: kind=%d, prot=0x%x\n",
1060: 					kind, s->flag);
1061: 		return -EFAULT;
1062: 	}
1063: 
1064: 	if (MMAP_ANONY(s->mmap)) {
1065: 		if (!MMAP_SHARE(s->mmap)) {		// private mapping, just like heap
1066: 			struct proc *p = myproc();
1067: 			uint64 pa = PGROUNDDOWN(badaddr);
1068: 
1069: 			if (uvmalloc(p->pagetable, pa, pa + PGSIZE, s->flag) == 0)
1070: 				return -ENOMEM;
1071: 
1072: 			sfence_vma();
1073: 			return 0;
1074: 		}
1075: 		else
1076: 			return handle_anonymous_shared(badaddr, s);
1077: 	}
1078: 
1079: 	return handle_file_mmap(badaddr, s);
1080: }

1058: {
1059: 	int illegel;
1060: 	switch (kind) {
1061: 		case 0: illegel = !(s->flag & PTE_R); break;
1062: 		case 1: illegel = !(s->flag & PTE_W); break;
1063: 		case 2: illegel = !(s->flag & PTE_X); break;
1064: 		default:
1065: 			illegel = 0;
1066: 			panic("handle_page_fault_mmap: kind");
1067: 	}
1068: 	if (illegel) {	// the access broke the protection
1069: 		__debug_warn("handle_page_fault_mmap", "illegal access: kind=%d, prot=0x%x\n",
1070: 					kind, s->flag);
1071: 		return -EFAULT;
1072: 	}
1073: 
1074: 	if (MMAP_ANONY(s->mmap)) {
1075: 		if (!MMAP_SHARE(s->mmap)) {		// private mapping, just like heap
1076: 			struct proc *p = myproc();
1077: 			uint64 pa = PGROUNDDOWN(badaddr);
1078: 
1079: 			if (uvmalloc(p->pagetable, pa, pa + PGSIZE, s->flag) == 0)
1080: 				return -ENOMEM;
1081: 
1082: 			sfence_vma();
1083: 			return 0;
1084: 		}
1085: 		else
1086: 			return handle_anonymous_shared(badaddr, s);
1087: 	}
1088: 
1089: 	return handle_file_mmap(badaddr, s);
1090: }

53: // Allocate n pages 
54: static void *__mul_alloc_no_lock(uint64 n) {
55: 	struct run *pa;
56: 	struct run **pprev;
57: 
58: 	pa = multiple.freelist;
59: 	pprev = &(multiple.freelist);
60: 
61: 	while (NULL != pa) {
62: 		if (pa->npage >= n) {
63: 			struct run *ret = (struct run*)(
64: 				(uint64)pa + PGSIZE * (pa->npage - n)
65: 			);
66: 			if (pa == ret) {	// this block is used up, remove it
67: 				*pprev = pa->next;
68: 			}
69: 			else {
70: 				pa->npage -= n;
71: 				pa = ret;
72: 			}
73: 
74: 			multiple.npage -= n;
75: 			break;
76: 		}
77: 
78: 		pprev = &(pa->next);
79: 		pa = pa->next;
80: 	}
81: 
82: 	return (void*)pa;
83: }
84:

87: 	if (NULL == multiple.freelist) {
88: 		multiple.freelist = (struct run*)start;

81:

84: 
85: // free n pages
86: static void __mul_free_no_lock(uint64 start, uint64 n) {
87: 	if (NULL == multiple.freelist) {
88: 		multiple.freelist = (struct run*)start;
89: 		multiple.freelist->npage = n;
90: 		multiple.freelist->next = NULL;
91: 		multiple.npage = n;
92: 
93: 		return ;
94: 	}
95: 
96: 	struct run *prev = NULL;
97: 	struct run *next = multiple.freelist;
98: 	while (NULL != next) {
99: 		if ((uint64)next > start) {
100: 			break;
101: 		}
102: 		else {
103: 			prev = next;
104: 			next = next->next;
105: 		}
106: 	}
107: 
108: 	struct run *pa = (struct run*)start;
109: 	pa->next = next;
110: 	pa->npage = n;
111: 
112: 	// merge with previous 
113: 	if (NULL != prev) {
114: 		if (((uint64)prev + prev->npage * PGSIZE) == (uint64)pa) {
115: 			prev->npage += n;
116: 			pa = prev;
117: 		}
118: 		else {
119: 			prev->next = pa;
120: 		}
121: 	}
122: 	else {
123: 		multiple.freelist = pa;
124: 		pa->npage = n;
125: 	}
126: 
127: 	// merge with next 
128: 	if (NULL != next) {
129: 		if (((uint64)pa + pa->npage * PGSIZE) == (uint64)next) {
130: 			// mergable 
131: 			pa->npage += next->npage;
132: 			pa->next = next->next;
133: 		}
134: 	}
135: 
136: 	multiple.npage += n;
137: }
138:

140: 	uint64 start = PGROUNDUP(pa_start);
141: 	uint64 npage = (pa_end - start) / PGSIZE;
142:

168: static void __sin_freerange(uint64 pa_start, uint64 pa_end) {
169: 	uint64 start = PGROUNDUP(pa_start);
170: 
171: 	__enter_sin_cs 
172: 	while (start < pa_end) {
173: 		__sin_free_no_lock(start);
174: 		start += PGSIZE;
175: 	}
176: 	__leave_sin_cs 
177: }

179: void kpminit(void) {
180: 	// init multiple 
181: 	multiple.freelist = NULL;
182: 	multiple.npage = 0;
183: 	initlock(&multiple.lock, "multi-lock");
184: 
185: 	// init single 
186: 	single.freelist = NULL;
187: 	single.npage = 0;
188: 	initlock(&single.lock, "single-lock");
189: 
190: 	__assert("kpminit", START_SINGLE - (uint64)boot_stack_top >= PGSIZE, 
191: 			"START_SINGLE = %p, boot_stack_top = %p\n", 
192: 			START_SINGLE, boot_stack_top);
193: 	__mul_freerange((uint64)boot_stack_top, START_SINGLE);
194: 	__sin_freerange(START_SINGLE, PHYSTOP);
195: 
196: 	__debug_info("kpminit", "kernel_end: %p, start_single: %p, phystop: %p, npage %d\n", 
197: 			kernel_end, (void*)START_SINGLE, (void*)PHYSTOP, multiple.npage + single.npage);
198: }
199: 
200: void *allocpage_n(uint64 n) {
201: 	__debug_assert("allocpage_n", n > 1, "n = %d\n", n);
202: 
203: 	void *ret;

39: 	struct spinlock lock;
40: 	struct run *freelist;

43: 
44: void
45: kpminit()
46: {
47: 	initlock(&kmem.lock, "kmem");
48: 	kmem.freelist = 0;
49: 	kmem.npage = 0;
50: 	freerange(boot_stack_top, (void*)PHYSTOP);
51: 	__debug_info("kpminit", "kernel_end: %p, phystop: %p, npage %d\n", kernel_end, (void*)PHYSTOP, kmem.npage);
52: }
53: 
54: // Free the page of physical memory pointed at by v,
55: // which normally should have been returned by a
56: // call to allocpage().  (The exception is when
57: // initializing the allocator; see kpminit above.)

200: void *allocpage_n(uint64 n) {
201: 	__debug_assert("allocpage_n", n > 1, "n = %d\n", n);
202: 
203: 	void *ret;
204: 	__enter_mul_cs 
205: 	ret = __mul_alloc_no_lock(n);
206: 	__leave_mul_cs 
207: 
208: 	#ifdef DEBUG
209: 	if (NULL != ret) {
210: 		memset(ret, 0x5, n * PGSIZE);
211: 	}
212: 	#endif 
213: 	__debug_info("allocpage_n", "%p %d page(s)\n", ret, n);
214: 
215: 	return ret;
216: }
217:

232: uint64 _allocpage(void) {
233: 	struct run *ret;
234: 
235: 	__enter_sin_cs 
236: 	ret = __sin_alloc_no_lock();
237: 	__leave_sin_cs 
238: 
239: 	if (NULL == ret) {
240: 		// we can't allocate one from single, let's borrow one from multiple
241: 		__enter_mul_cs 
242: 		ret = __mul_alloc_no_lock(1);
243: 		__leave_mul_cs 
244: 	}
245: 
246: 	#ifdef DEBUG
247: 	if (NULL != ret) {
248: 		memset(ret, 5, PGSIZE);
249: 	}
250: 	#endif 
251: 	__debug_info("allocpage", "%p\n", ret);
252: 
253: 	return (uint64)ret;
254: }
255:

259: 	__debug_assert("freepage",

oskernel2023-zmz@recover 源码

278: 
279: uint64 idlepages(void) {
280: 	return multiple.npage + single.npage;
281: }
282: 
283: void pm_dump(void) {
284: 	__enter_mul_cs 
285: 	struct run *pa = multiple.freelist;
286: 	printf("multiple avail: %d\n", multiple.npage);
287: 	while (NULL != pa) {
288: 		printf("\t%p %d\n", pa, pa->npage);
289: 		pa = pa->next;
290: 	}
291: 	__leave_mul_cs 
292: 
293: 	__enter_sin_cs 
294: 	printf("single avail: %d\n", single.npage);
295: 	__leave_sin_cs 
296: }

11: #include "mm/vm.h"
12: #include "utils/string.h"
13: #include "utils/debug.h"
14: #include "errno.h"
15: 
16: struct seg *newseg(pagetable_t pagetable, struct seg *head,
17: 				enum segtype type, uint64 offset, uint64 sz, long flag)
18: {
19: 	uint64 nstart, nend;
20: 	nstart = PGROUNDDOWN(offset);
21: 	nend = offset + sz;
22: 	nend = PGROUNDUP(nend);
23: 
24: 	// __debug_info("newseg", "type=%d nstart=%p nend=%p\n", type, nstart, nend);
25: 	// __debug_info("newseg", "sizeof(struct seg): %d\n", sizeof(struct seg));
26: 	struct seg *seg = NULL;
27: 	for (struct seg *s = head; s != NULL; s = s->next) {
28: 		// __debug_info("newseg", "type=%d start=%p end=%p\n", s->type, start, end);
29: 		if (nend <= s->addr) {
30: 			break;
31: 		} else if (nstart >= s->addr + s->sz) {
32: 			seg = s;
33: 		} else {
34: 			__debug_error("newseg", "segments overlap\n");
35: 			return NULL;
36: 		}
37: 	}
38: 
39: 	// __debug_info("newseg", "calling to kmalloc\n");
40: 	struct seg *p;
41: 	if ((p = (struct seg *)kmalloc(sizeof(struct seg))) == NULL) {
42: 		__debug_error("newseg", "fail to kmalloc\n");
43: 		return NULL;
44: 	}
45: /*
46: 	// __debug_info("newseg", "calling to uvmalloc\n");
47: 	if(uvmalloc(pagetable, nstart, nend, flag) == 0)
48: 	{
49: 		kfree(p);
50: 		__debug_error("newseg", "fail to uvmalloc\n");
51: 		return NULL;
52: 	}
53: */
54: 	if (seg == NULL) {
55: 		p->next = head;
56: 		head = p;
57: 	} else {
58: 		p->next = seg->next;
59: 		seg->next = p;
60: 	}
61: 	p->addr = offset;
62: 	p->sz = sz;
63: 	p->type = type;
64: 	p->flag = flag;
65: 	p->mmap = NULL;
66: 
67: 	// __debug_info("newseg", "done\n");
68: 	// return p;
69: 	return head;
70: }
71:

120: struct seg *locateseg(struct seg *head, uint64 addr)

134: 			return NULL;
135: 		}
136: 	}

171: struct seg *delseg(pagetable_t pagetable, struct seg *s)
172: {
173: 	struct seg *next = s->next;
174: 	// __debug_info("delseg", "s = %p\n", s);
175: 	// __debug_info("delseg", "s->type: %d\n", s->type);

177: 		mmapdel(s, 1); // mmap的free工作是单独处理的
178: 	}
179: 	uvmdealloc(pagetable, PGROUNDDOWN(s->addr), s->addr + s->sz);
180: 	kfree(s);
181: 	return next;
182: }

190: 
191: struct seg *copysegs(pagetable_t pt, struct seg *seg, pagetable_t pt2)
192: {
193: 	struct seg *head = NULL, *tail = NULL;
194: 	for (; seg != NULL; seg = seg->next) {
195: 		struct seg *s = kmalloc(sizeof(struct seg));
196: 		if (s == NULL) {
197: 			__debug_warn("copysegs", "seg kmalloc fail\n");
198: 			goto bad;
199: 		}
200: 
201: 		// This means that the mmap segment is shared, shouldn't do COW.
202: 		// If seg->mmap is NULL, it means the mmap is private.
203: 		int cow = (seg->type == MMAP && MMAP_SHARE(seg->mmap)) ? 0 : 1;
204: 		// Copy user memory from parent to child.
205: 		if (uvmcopy(pt, pt2, PGROUNDDOWN(seg->addr), seg->addr + seg->sz, cow) < 0) 
206: 		{
207: 			__debug_warn("copysegs", "uvmcopy_cow fails\n");
208: 			__debug_warn("copysegs", "%p, %p, %d\n", 
209: 					seg->addr, seg->addr + seg->sz, seg->type);
210: 			kfree(s);
211: 			__debug_warn("copysegs", "exit kfree\n");
212: 			goto bad;
213: 		}
214: 		memmove(s, seg, sizeof(struct seg));
215: 		if (seg->type == MMAP) {
216: 			mmapdup(seg);
217: 		}
218: 		s->next = NULL;
219: 		if (tail == NULL) {
220: 			head = tail = s;
221: 		} else {
222: 			tail->next = s;
223: 			tail = s;
224: 		}
225: 	}
226: 	return head;
227: 
228: bad:
229: 	while (head) {
230: 		tail = head->next;
231: 		if (head->type == MMAP)
232: 			mmapdel(head, 0);
233: 		uvmdealloc(pt2, PGROUNDDOWN(head->addr), head->addr + head->sz);
234: 		kfree(head);
235: 		head = tail;
236: 	}
237: 	return NULL;
238: }
239:

242: 	for (; seg != NULL; seg = seg->next) {
243: 		printf("type:%d\tstart:%p\tsize:%d\n",seg->type,seg->addr,seg->sz);

143: struct seg *copysegs(pagetable_t pt, struct seg *seg, pagetable_t pt2)
144: {
145: 	struct seg *head = NULL, *tail = NULL;
146: 	for (; seg != NULL; seg = seg->next) {
147: 		struct seg *s = kmalloc(sizeof(struct seg));
148: 		if (s == NULL) {
149: 			__debug_warn("copysegs", "seg kmalloc fail\n");
150: 			goto bad;
151: 		}
152: 
153: 		// This means that the mmap segment is shared, shouldn't do COW.
154: 		// If seg->mmap is NULL, it means the mmap is private.
155: 		int cow = (seg->type == MMAP && MMAP_SHARE(seg->mmap)) ? 0 : 1;
156: 		// Copy user memory from parent to child.
157: 		if (uvmcopy(pt, pt2, PGROUNDDOWN(seg->addr), seg->addr + seg->sz, cow) < 0) 
158: 		{
159: 			__debug_warn("copysegs", "uvmcopy_cow fails\n");
160: 			__debug_warn("copysegs", "%p, %p, %d\n", 
161: 					seg->addr, seg->addr + seg->sz, seg->type);
162: 			kfree(s);
163: 			__debug_warn("copysegs", "exit kfree\n");
164: 			goto bad;
165: 		}
166: 		memmove(s, seg, sizeof(struct seg));
167: 		if (seg->type == MMAP) {
168: 			mmapdup(seg);
169: 		}
170: 		s->next = NULL;
171: 		if (tail == NULL) {
172: 			head = tail = s;
173: 		} else {
174: 			tail->next = s;
175: 			tail = s;
176: 		}
177: 	}
178: 	return head;
179: 
180: bad:
181: 	while (head) {
182: 		tail = head->next;
183: 		if (head->type == MMAP)
184: 			mmapdel(head, 0);
185: 		uvmdealloc(pt2, PGROUNDDOWN(head->addr), head->addr + head->sz);
186: 		kfree(head);
187: 		head = tail;
188: 	}
189: 	return NULL;
190: }

248: 
249: // caller must hold ip->lock
250: int loadseg(pagetable_t pagetable, uint64 va, struct seg *s, struct inode *ip)
251: {
252: 	// seg是对实际虚拟地址块的管理
253: 	// 这里取真实va的较大者
254: 	//printf("Enter load seg\n");
255: 	va = PGROUNDDOWN(va) >= s->addr ? PGROUNDDOWN(va) : s->addr;
256: 	uint64 off = s->f_off + (va - s->addr);
257: 	int64 size = s->f_sz - (va - s->addr);
258: 	uint64 end = va + PGSIZE;
259: 	end = PGROUNDDOWN(end);
260: 	// 处理尾部小于一个页面的情况
261: 	if ((int64)(end - va) < size)
262: 		size = end - va;
263: 
264: 	//printf("NNN: loadseg base=0x%x, off=0x%x, size=0x%x, end=0x%x\n", va, off, size, end);
265: 	//printf("NNN: loadseg saddr=0x%x, sz=0x%x, f_off=0x%x, f_zs=0x%x, flag=0x%x\n", s->addr, s->sz, s->f_off, s->f_sz, s->flag);
266: 	//printf("NNN: loadseg base=0x%x, off=%d, size=%d, end=0x%x\n", va, off, size, end);
267: 	//printf("NNN: loadseg s->saddr=0x%x, s->sz=%d, f_off=%d, f_zs=%d, flag=0x%x\n", s->addr, s->sz, s->f_off, s->f_sz, s->flag);
268: 	//printf("NNN: real size mapped: %d\n",size);
269: 	void *pa = allocpage();
270: 	if (pa == NULL){
271: 		printf("load seg 0\n");
272: 		return -ENOMEM;
273: 	}
274: 
275: 	memset(pa, 0, PGSIZE);
276: 	__debug_info("loadseg", "pa=%p, base=0x%x, f_off=0x%x, size=%d\n", pa, va, off, size);
277: 	if (size > 0 && ip->fop->read(ip, 0, (uint64)pa + va % PGSIZE, off, size) != size) {
278: 		__debug_warn("loadseg", "fail to load! off=0x%x, size=%d\n", off, size);
279: 		printf("load seg 1\n");
280: 		goto bad;
281: 	}
282: 	pagereg((uint64)pa, 0);
283: 	if (mappages(pagetable, va, end - va, (uint64)pa, s->flag|PTE_U) < 0) {
284: 	//if (mappages(pagetable, va, end - va, (uint64)pa, s->flag) < 0) {
285: 		__debug_warn("loadseg", "fail to map! va=0x%x, end=0x%x\n", va, end);
286: 		printf("load seg 2\n");
287: 		goto bad;
288: 	}
289: 	//printf("NNN: reach here!!!\n");
290: 	return 0;
291: 	//return size;
292: 
293: bad:
294: 
295: 	freepage(pa);
296: 	return -1;
297: }

192: // caller must hold ip->lock
193: int loadseg(pagetable_t pagetable, uint64 va, struct seg *s, struct inode *ip)
194: {
195: 	va = PGROUNDDOWN(va) >= s->addr ? PGROUNDDOWN(va) : s->addr;
196: 	uint64 off = s->f_off + (va - s->addr);
197: 	int64 size = s->f_sz - (va - s->addr);
198: 	uint64 end = va + PGSIZE;
199: 	end = PGROUNDDOWN(end);
200: 	if ((int64)(end - va) < size)
201: 		size = end - va;
202: 
203: 	__debug_info("loadseg", "base=0x%x, off=0x%x, size=0x%x, end=0x%x\n", va, off, size, end);
204: 	__debug_info("loadseg", "saddr=0x%x, sz=0x%x, f_off=0x%x, f_zs=0x%x, flag=0x%x\n", s->addr, s->sz, s->f_off, s->f_sz, s->flag);
205: 
206: 	void *pa = allocpage();
207: 	if (pa == NULL)
208: 		return -ENOMEM;
209: 
210: 	memset(pa, 0, PGSIZE);
211: 	__debug_info("loadseg", "pa=%p, base=0x%x, f_off=0x%x, size=%d\n", pa, va, off, size);
212: 	if (size > 0 && ip->fop->read(ip, 0, (uint64)pa + va % PGSIZE, off, size) != size) {
213: 		__debug_warn("loadseg", "fail to load! off=0x%x, size=%d\n", off, size);
214: 		goto bad;
215: 	}
216: 	pagereg((uint64)pa, 0);
217: 	if (mappages(pagetable, va, end - va, (uint64)pa, s->flag|PTE_U) < 0) {
218: 		__debug_warn("loadseg", "fail to map! va=0x%x, end=0x%x\n", va, end);
219: 		goto bad;
220: 	}
221: 
222: 	return 0;
223: 
224: bad:
225: 	freepage(pa);
226: 	return -1;
227: }

229: // Test whether the given range is in any part of the current proc's v-space
230: int rangeinseg(uint64 start, uint64 end)
231: {
232: 	return partofseg(myproc()->segment, start, end) ? 1 : 0;

323: 
324: 	memset(pa, 0, PGSIZE);
325: 	__debug_info("loadseg", "pa=%p, base=0x%x, f_off=0x%x, size=%d\n", pa, va, off, size);
326: 	if (size > 0 && ip->fop->read(ip, 0, (uint64)pa + va % PGSIZE, off, size) != size) {
327: 		__debug_warn("loadseg", "fail to load! off=0x%x, size=%d\n", off, size);
328: 		printf("load seg 1\n");
329: 		goto bad;
330: 	}
331: 	pagereg((uint64)pa, 0);
332: 	if (mappages(pagetable, va, end - va, (uint64)pa, s->flag|PTE_U) < 0) {
333: 	//if (mappages(pagetable, va, end - va, (uint64)pa, s->flag) < 0) {
334: 		__debug_warn("loadseg", "fail to map! va=0x%x, end=0x%x\n", va, end);
335: 		printf("load seg 2\n");
336: 		goto bad;
337: 	}
338: 	//printf("NNN: reach here!!!\n");
339: 	*get_size = size;
340: 	return 0;
341: 	//return size;
342: 
343: bad:
344: 
345: 	freepage(pa);
346: 	return -1;
347: }
348:

350: int loadseg_ext(pagetable_t pagetable, uint64 va, struct seg *s, struct inode *ip)
351: {
352: 	uint64 i,n;

360: // caller must hold ip->lock
361: // USE IN EXE NNN1
362: int loadseg_ext2(pagetable_t pagetable, uint64 va, struct seg *s, struct inode *ip)
363: {
364: 	printf("va_start: %d\tsize to load:%d\n",va,s->f_sz);
365: 	uint64 i,t=0,cnt=0;
366: 	uint64 tot = s->f_sz;
367: 	for(i=0;i<tot;i+=t){
368: 		loadseg3(pagetable,va+i,s,ip,&t);
369: 		s->f_off += t;
370: 		s->f_sz -=t;
371: 		cnt+=t;
372: 	}
373: 	printf("Have mapped: %d\n",cnt);
374: 	return 0;
375: }

386: void pageDump(char *start)
387: {
388: 	int i = 0;
389: 	uint64 x = PGROUNDDOWN((uint64)(start));
390: 	start = x;
391: 	for(;i<PGSIZE;i+=32){
392: 		dumpLine(start+i);
393: 		printf("\n");
394: 	}
395: }

匹配分数 0.519 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 0.742, "exact_ast_shape": false, "exact_token_fp": false, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 0.148}

35: extern char trampoline[]; // trampoline.S
36: extern char kernel_end[]; // first address after kernel.
37: /*
38:  * create a direct-map page table for the kernel.
39:  */
40: void
41: kvminit()
42: {
43: 	save_point = 0;
44: 
45: 	if (idlepages() > MAX_PAGES_NUM) {
46: 		pm_dump();
47: 		panic("kvminit: page_ref_table[] not enough");
48: 	}
49: 
50: 	initlock(&page_ref_lock, "page_ref_lock");
51: 	memset(page_ref_table, 0, sizeof(page_ref_table));
52: 
53: 	kernel_pagetable = (pagetable_t) allocpage();
54: 	// printf("kernel_pagetable: %p\n", kernel_pagetable);
55: 
56: 	memset(kernel_pagetable, 0, PGSIZE);
57: 
58: 	// uart registers
59: 	kvmmap(UART_V, UART, PGSIZE, PTE_R | PTE_W);
60: 	
61: 	#ifdef QEMU
62: 	// virtio mmio disk interface
63: 	kvmmap(VIRTIO0_V, VIRTIO0, PGSIZE, PTE_R | PTE_W);
64: 	#endif
65: 	// CLINT
66: 	kvmmap(CLINT_V, CLINT, 0x10000, PTE_R | PTE_W);
67: 
68: 	// PLIC
69: 	kvmmap(PLIC_V, PLIC, 0x4000, PTE_R | PTE_W);
70: 	kvmmap(PLIC_V + 0x200000, PLIC + 0x200000, 0x4000, PTE_R | PTE_W);
71: 
72: 	#ifndef QEMU
73: 	// GPIOHS
74: 	kvmmap(GPIOHS_V, GPIOHS, 0x1000, PTE_R | PTE_W);
75: 
76: 	// DMAC
77: 	kvmmap(DMAC_V, DMAC, 0x1000, PTE_R | PTE_W);
78: 
79: 	// GPIO
80: 	// kvmmap(GPIO_V, GPIO, 0x1000, PTE_R | PTE_W);
81: 
82: 	// SPI_SLAVE
83: 	kvmmap(SPI_SLAVE_V, SPI_SLAVE, 0x1000, PTE_R | PTE_W);
84: 
85: 	// FPIOA
86: 	kvmmap(FPIOA_V, FPIOA, 0x1000, PTE_R | PTE_W);
87: 
88: 	// SPI0
89: 	kvmmap(SPI0_V, SPI0, 0x1000, PTE_R | PTE_W);
90: 
91: 	// SPI1
92: 	kvmmap(SPI1_V, SPI1, 0x1000, PTE_R | PTE_W);
93: 
94: 	// SPI2
95: 	kvmmap(SPI2_V, SPI2, 0x1000, PTE_R | PTE_W);
96: 
97: 	// SYSCTL
98: 	kvmmap(SYSCTL_V, SYSCTL, 0x1000, PTE_R | PTE_W);
99: 	
100: 	#endif
101: 	
102: 	// map rustsbi
103: 	// kvmmap(RUSTSBI_BASE, RUSTSBI_BASE, KERNBASE - RUSTSBI_BASE, PTE_R | PTE_X);
104: 	// map kernel text executable and read-only.
105: 	kvmmap(KERNBASE, KERNBASE, (uint64)etext - KERNBASE, PTE_R | PTE_X);
106: 	// map kernel data and the physical RAM we'll make use of.
107: 	kvmmap((uint64)etext, (uint64)etext, PHYSTOP - (uint64)etext, PTE_R | PTE_W);
108: 	// map the trampoline for trap entry/exit to
109: 	// the highest virtual address in the kernel.
110: 	kvmmap(TRAMPOLINE, (uint64)trampoline, PGSIZE, PTE_R | PTE_X);
111: 
112: 	#ifdef DEBUG
113: 	printf("kvminit\n");
114: 	#endif
115: }
116: 
117: // Switch h/w page table register to the kernel's page table,
118: // and enable paging.
119: void
120: kvminithart()

122: 	// w_satp(MAKE_SATP(kernel_pagetable));

135: 
136: static int __hash_page_idx(uint64 pa)
137: {
138: 	extern char sig_trampoline[];
139: 
140: 	if (pa % PGSIZE || !(
141: 		(pa >= PGROUNDUP((uint64)kernel_end) && pa < PHYSTOP) || 
142: 		((uint64)sig_trampoline == pa)
143: 	)) {
144: 		__debug_error("__hash_page_idx", "%p not in [%p, %p]\n",
145: 					pa, PGROUNDUP((uint64)kernel_end), PHYSTOP);
146: 		panic("__hash_page");
147: 	}
148: 	return (pa - PGROUNDUP((uint64)kernel_end)) >> PGSHIFT;
149: }
150:

154: {

158: }

160: // This is used in page fault handler. If two process write the same page
161: // in the same time, let the process who gets the lock monopolize the page.
162: static int monopolizepage(uint64 pa)
163: {
164: 	acquire(&page_ref_lock);
165: 	int idx = __hash_page_idx(pa);
166: 	if (page_ref_table[idx] == 1) {
167: 		release(&page_ref_lock);
168: 		return 1;
169: 	}
170: 	page_ref_table[idx]--; // should hold the lock until copying done
171: 	return 0;
172: }
173: 
174: static inline void pagecopydone(void)
175: {
176: 	release(&page_ref_lock);
177: }

161: // This is used in page fault handler. If two process write the same page
162: // in the same time, let the process who gets the lock monopolize the page.
163: static int monopolizepage(uint64 pa)
164: {
165: 	acquire(&page_ref_lock);

167: 	if (page_ref_table[idx] == 1) {
168: 		release(&page_ref_lock);
169: 		return 1;
170: 	}
171: 	page_ref_table[idx]--; // should hold the lock until copying done

匹配分数 0.566 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": false, "exact_token_fp": false, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 0.219}
