205: //   39..63 -- must be zero.
206: //   30..38 -- 9 bits of level-2 index.
207: //   21..29 -- 9 bits of level-1 index.
208: //   12..20 -- 9 bits of level-0 index.
209: //    0..11 -- 12 bits of byte offset within the page.
210: pte_t *
211: walk(pagetable_t pagetable, uint64 va, int alloc)
212: {
213: 	
214: 	if(va >= MAXVA)
215: 		panic("walk");
216: 
217: 	for(int level = 2; level > 0; level--) {
218: 		pte_t *pte = &pagetable[PX(level, va)];
219: 		if(*pte & PTE_V) {
220: 			pagetable = (pagetable_t)PTE2PA(*pte);
221: 		} else {
222: 			if(!alloc || (pagetable = (pde_t*)allocpage()) == NULL)
223: 				return NULL;
224: 			memset(pagetable, 0, PGSIZE);
225: 			*pte = PA2PTE(pagetable) | PTE_V;
226: 		}
227: 	}
228: 	return &pagetable[PX(0, va)];
229: }
230: 
231: // Look up a virtual address, return the physical address,
232: // or 0 if not mapped.
233: // Can only be used to look up user pages.
234: uint64

274: uint64
275: kwalkaddr(pagetable_t kpt, uint64 va)
276: {
277: 	uint64 off = va % PGSIZE;
278: 	pte_t *pte;
279: 	uint64 pa;
280: 	
281: 	pte = walk(kpt, va, 0);
282: 	if(pte == 0)
283: 		panic("kwalkaddr1");
284: 	if((*pte & PTE_V) == 0)
285: 		panic("kwalkaddr2");
286: 	if((*pte & PTE_U))
287: 		panic("kwalkaddr3");
288: 	pa = PTE2PA(*pte);
289: 	return pa+off;
290: }
291:

匹配分数 0.693 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 0.938, "exact_ast_shape": false, "exact_token_fp": false, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 0.664}

oskernel2023-zmz@recover 源码

291: 
292: // Create PTEs for virtual addresses starting at va that refer to
293: // physical addresses starting at pa. va and size might not
294: // be page-aligned. Returns 0 on success, -1 if walk() couldn't
295: // allocate a needed page-table page.
296: int
297: mappages(pagetable_t pagetable, uint64 va, uint64 size, uint64 pa, int perm)
298: {
299: 	uint64 a, last;
300: 	pte_t *pte;
301: 
302: 	a = PGROUNDDOWN(va);
303: 	last = PGROUNDDOWN(va + size - 1);
304: 
305: 	int usr = perm & PTE_U;
306: 	for(;;){
307: 		if((pte = walk(pagetable, a, 1)) == NULL)
308: 			return -1;
309: 		if(*pte & PTE_V)
310: 			panic("remap");
311: 		if (*pte & PTE_U) { // mprotect might take care of perm ahead
312: 			__debug_assert("mappages", PTE2PA(*pte) == NULL, "invalid page with ppn\n");
313: 			*pte |= PA2PTE(pa) | PTE_V;
314: 		} else {
315: 			*pte = PA2PTE(pa) | perm | PTE_V;
316: 		}
317: 		if (usr)
318: 			pagedup(PGROUNDDOWN(pa));  // increase ref to parent's page
319: 		if(a == last)
320: 			break;
321: 		a += PGSIZE;
322: 		pa += PGSIZE;
323: 	}
324: 	return 0;
325: }
326: 
327: // Remove npages of mappings starting from va. va must be
328: // page-aligned. The mappings must exist.
329: // Optionally free the physical memory.
330: /**

xv6-k210@final / 来源候选源码

293: 
294: // Create PTEs for virtual addresses starting at va that refer to
295: // physical addresses starting at pa. va and size might not
296: // be page-aligned. Returns 0 on success, -1 if walk() couldn't
297: // allocate a needed page-table page.
298: int
299: mappages(pagetable_t pagetable, uint64 va, uint64 size, uint64 pa, int perm)
300: {
301: 	uint64 a, last;
302: 	pte_t *pte;
303: 
304: 	a = PGROUNDDOWN(va);
305: 	last = PGROUNDDOWN(va + size - 1);
306: 
307: 	int usr = perm & PTE_U;
308: 	for(;;){
309: 		if((pte = walk(pagetable, a, 1)) == NULL)
310: 			return -1;
311: 		// if(*pte & PTE_V)
312: 		// 	panic("remap");
313: 		if (*pte & PTE_U) { // mprotect might take care of perm ahead
314: 			__debug_assert("mappages", PTE2PA(*pte) == NULL, "invalid page with ppn\n");
315: 			*pte |= PA2PTE(pa) | PTE_V;
316: 		} else {
317: 			*pte = PA2PTE(pa) | perm | PTE_V;
318: 		}
319: 		if (usr)
320: 			pagedup(PGROUNDDOWN(pa));  // increase ref to parent's page
321: 		if(a == last)
322: 			break;
323: 		a += PGSIZE;
324: 		pa += PGSIZE;
325: 	}
326: 	return 0;
327: }
328:

331: // Optionally free the physical memory.
332: /**
unmappages修改候选
oskernel2023-zmz@recover 第 333 行 · 主要确定性来源：xv6-k210/kernel/mm/vm.c::unmappages

匹配分数 0.556 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 0.812, "exact_ast_shape": false, "exact_token_fp": false, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 0.25}

oskernel2023-zmz@recover 源码

328: // page-aligned. The mappings must exist.
329: // Optionally free the physical memory.
330: /**
331:  * @param     flag  see include/vm.h
332:  */
333: void
334: unmappages(pagetable_t pagetable, uint64 va, uint64 npages, int flag)
335: {
336: 	uint64 a;
337: 	pte_t *pte;
338: 
339: 	if((va % PGSIZE) != 0)
340: 		panic("unmappages: not aligned");
341: 
342: 	int do_free = flag & VM_FREE;
343: 	int usr = flag & VM_USER;
344: 
345: 	uint64 end = va + npages * PGSIZE;
346: 	for (a = va; a < end; a += PGSIZE) {
347: 		if ((pte = walk(pagetable, a, 0)) == NULL || !(*pte & PTE_V)) {
348: 			// if (allowhole) continue;  // it might be a lazy-allocated page which is not really allocated, but how could we verify that?
349: 			// __debug_error("unmappages", "va=%p flag=%d\n", va, flag);
350: 			// panic("unmappages: unmapped pte");
351: 			/**
352: 			 * At this stage, we introduce several kinds of lazy allocation
353: 			 * into xv6-k210, there are lots of so-call "hole" in the
354: 			 * pagetable. We just let them go...
355: 			 */
356: 			continue;
357: 		}
358: 		if(PTE_FLAGS(*pte) == PTE_V)
359: 			panic("unmappages: not a leaf");
360: 		uint64 pa = PTE2PA(*pte);
361: 		if (do_free && (!usr || pageput(pa) == 0)) {
362: 			freepage((void*)pa);
363: 		}
364: 		*pte = 0;
365: 	}
366: }
367: 
368: // create an empty user page table.
369: // returns 0 if out of memory.
370: /**
371:  * With one page shared by kernel and user proc,

360: 		// if(PTE_FLAGS(*pte) == PTE_V)
361: 		// 	panic("unmappages: not a leaf");
362: 		uint64 pa = PTE2PA(*pte);
363: 		if (do_free && (!usr || pageput(pa) == 0)) {
364: 			freepage((void*)pa);
365: 		}
366: 		*pte = 0;
367: 	}
368: }

384: 
385: // Load the user initcode into address 0 of pagetable,
386: // for the very first process.
387: // sz must be less than a page.
388: void
389: uvminit(pagetable_t pagetable, uchar *src, uint sz)
390: {
391: 	char *mem;
392: 	extern char sig_trampoline[];
393: 
394: 	if(sz >= PGSIZE)
395: 		panic("inituvm: more than a page");
396: 	mem = allocpage();
397: 	memset(mem, 0, PGSIZE);
398: 	pagereg((uint64)mem, 0);	// mappages will increase it
399: 	mappages(pagetable, 0, PGSIZE, (uint64)mem, PTE_W|PTE_R|PTE_X|PTE_U);
400: 	mappages(pagetable, SIG_TRAMPOLINE, PGSIZE, (uint64)sig_trampoline, PTE_R | PTE_X | PTE_U);
401: 	memmove(mem, src, sz);
402: }
403:

407:  * @param   start   user virtual addr that start to be allocated
408:  * @param   end     the ending user virtual addr of the alloction
409:  * @param   perm    permission flags, PTE_W|R|X (PTE_U is a default flag)
410:  * @return          param end if successful else 0
411:  */
412: uint64
413: uvmalloc(pagetable_t pagetable, uint64 start, uint64 end, int perm)
414: {
415: 	char *mem;
416: 	uint64 a;
417: 
418: 	if (end > MAXUVA)
419: 		return 0;
420: 	if(end < start)
421: 		return start;
422: 
423: 	for(a = PGROUNDUP(start); a < end; a += PGSIZE){
424: 		mem = allocpage();
425: 		if (mem == NULL) {
426: 			uvmdealloc(pagetable, start, a);
427: 			return 0;
428: 		}
429: 		memset(mem, 0, PGSIZE);
430: 		pagereg((uint64)mem, 0);
431: 		if (mappages(pagetable, a, PGSIZE, (uint64)mem, perm|PTE_U) != 0) {
432: 			freepage(mem);
433: 			uvmdealloc(pagetable, start, a);
434: 			return 0;
435: 		}
436: 	}
437: 	return end;
438: }
439:

443: // process size.  Returns the new process size.

414: uint64
415: uvmalloc(pagetable_t pagetable, uint64 start, uint64 end, int perm)
416: {
417: 	char *mem;

420: 	if (end > MAXUVA)

447:  * @param   start   user virtual addr that start to be deleted (low addr)
448:  * @param   end     the ending user virtual addr of the deletion
449:  * @param   segt    type of the segment where we perform the deletion
450:  * @return          param start if successful else 0
451:  */
452: uint64
453: uvmdealloc(pagetable_t pagetable, uint64 start, uint64 end)
454: {
455: 	if(start >= end)
456: 		return end;
457: 
458: 	if (PGROUNDUP(start) < PGROUNDUP(end)) {
459: 		int npages = (PGROUNDUP(end) - PGROUNDUP(start)) / PGSIZE;
460: 		unmappages(pagetable, PGROUNDUP(start), npages, VM_FREE|VM_USER);
461: 	}
462: 
463: 	return start;
464: }

465: 
466: // Recursively free page-table pages.
467: // All leaf mappings must already have been removed.
468: void
469: freewalk(pagetable_t pagetable)
470: {
471: 	// there are 2^9 = 512 PTEs in a page table.
472: 	for(int i = 0; i < 512; i++){
473: 		pte_t pte = pagetable[i];
474: 		if((pte & PTE_V) && (pte & (PTE_R|PTE_W|PTE_X)) == 0){
475: 			// this PTE points to a lower-level page table.
476: 			uint64 child = PTE2PA(pte);
477: 			freewalk((pagetable_t)child);
478: 			pagetable[i] = 0;
479: 		} else if(pte & PTE_V){
480: 			panic("freewalk: leaf");
481: 		}
482: 	}
483: 	freepage((void*)pagetable);
484: }
485:

488:  * the user part of the pagetable. The kernel part is handled
489:  * by kvmfree()

486: /**

491: void

548:  * @param     segt  segment type, the kernel won't panic 
549:  *                  where pte == NULL or pte invalid if segt is HEAP
550:  * @param     cow   whether to activate COW mechanism
551:  *                  only mapping shared pages without this, e.g. shared mmap pages
552:  */
553: int uvmcopy(pagetable_t old, pagetable_t new, uint64 start, uint64 end, int cow)
554: {
555: 	pte_t *pte;
556: 	uint64 pa, i;
557: 	uint flags;
558: 
559: 	for (i = start; i < end; i += PGSIZE) {
560: 		if ((pte = walk(old, i, 0)) == NULL || !(*pte & PTE_V)) {
561: 			continue;
562: 		}
563: 		pa = PTE2PA(*pte);
564: 		if (cow && (*pte & PTE_W)) {
565: 			*pte = (*pte|PTE_COW) & ~PTE_W;  // cancel 'W', and mark COW
566: 		}
567: 		flags = PTE_FLAGS(*pte);
568: 		if(mappages(new, i, PGSIZE, pa, flags) != 0) {
569: 			goto err;
570: 		}
571: 	}
572: 
573: 	// Copy successfully, now cancel 'W' for parent.
574: 	// In fact, it's OK to mark COW for parent's pagetable at previous step
575: 	// for (i = start; i < end; i += PGSIZE) {
576: 	// 	if ((pte = walk(old, i, 0)) == NULL || !(*pte & PTE_V))
577: 	// 		continue;
578: 	// 	if (PTE_FLAGS(*pte) & PTE_W) {
579: 	// 		*pte &= ~PTE_W;
580: 	// 		*pte |= PTE_COW;
581: 	// 	}
582: 	// }
583: 	// Should we perform an sfence.vma?
584: 	sfence_vma();
585: 	return 0;
586: 
587:  err:
588: 	unmappages(new, start, (i - start) / PGSIZE, VM_FREE|VM_USER);
589: 	return -1;
590: }

uvmprotect修改候选

候选来源与匹配信号

xv6-k210/kernel/mm/vm.c::uvmprotect
匹配分数 0.525 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 0.758, "exact_ast_shape": false, "exact_token_fp": false, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 0.164}

oskernel2023-zmz@recover 源码

609:  * Caller must check legality via usrmm module before calling to this.
610:  * TODO: If protecting legal but not-valid-at-present pages, how can we maintain the
611:  *       protect flags we set in this function? Since mappages may over-write them.
612:  * --DONE
613:  */
614: int
615: uvmprotect(pagetable_t pagetable, uint64 va, uint64 len, int prot)
616: {
617: 	if (va % PGSIZE || prot & ~(PTE_X|PTE_W|PTE_R)) {
618: 		__debug_error("uvmprotect", "va=%p, prot=0x%0x\n", va, prot);
619: 		panic("uvmprotect");
620: 	}
621: 	
622: 	int protw = prot & PTE_W;
623: 	int fence = 0;
624: 	uint64 i;
625: 	for (i = 0; i < len; i += PGSIZE) {
626: 		pte_t *pte = walk(pagetable, va + i, 1);
627: 		if (pte == NULL)
628: 			break;
629: 
630: 		if (*pte & PTE_V) {
631: 			fence = 1;
632: 			if (protw && !(*pte & (PTE_W|PTE_COW))) { // may lead to conflict
633: 				/**
634: 				 * Case: the page wasn't writable, but user wants to write it.
635: 				 * Only mark it as copy-on-write, don't mark it writable directly,
636: 				 * let page fault handler take care of that.
637: 				 */
638: 				*pte = (*pte & ~(PTE_X|PTE_W|PTE_R)) | (prot & ~PTE_W) | PTE_COW;
639: 
640: 			} else if ((*pte & (PTE_W|PTE_COW)) && !protw) { // cancel writable
641: 				/**
642: 				 * Case: the page was writable, but user wants to cancel it.
643: 				 * Unmark PTE_COW no matter whether the pte has this marked or not.
644: 				 */
645: 				*pte = (*pte & ~(PTE_COW|PTE_X|PTE_W|PTE_R)) | prot;
646: 
647: 			} else {
648: 				*pte = (*pte & ~(PTE_X|PTE_W|PTE_R)) | prot;
649: 			}
650: 		}
651: 		else {
652: 			/**
653: 			 * Case: protecting a not-present page.
654: 			 * Pre-mark a PTE_U here, if mappages() meets a pte without PTE_V but PTE_U,
655: 			 * it knows how to handle that.
656: 			 */
657: 			*pte = prot | PTE_U;
658: 		}
659: 	}
660: 
661: 	if (fence)
662: 		sfence_vma();
663: 
664: 	return i < len ? -1 : 0;
665: }
666: 
667: /**
668:  * Initialize a kernel pagetable for each process, which is a copy of kernel_pagetable
669:  */
670: pagetable_t

xv6-k210@final / 来源候选源码

612:  * Caller must check legality via usrmm module before calling to this.
613:  * TODO: If protecting legal but not-valid-at-present pages, how can we maintain the
614:  *       protect flags we set in this function? Since mappages may over-write them.
615:  * --DONE
616:  */
617: int
618: uvmprotect(pagetable_t pagetable, uint64 va, uint64 len, int prot)
619: {
620: 	// if (va % PGSIZE || prot & ~(PTE_X|PTE_W|PTE_R)) {
621: 	// 	__debug_error("uvmprotect", "va=%p, prot=0x%0x\n", va, prot);
622: 	// 	panic("uvmprotect");
623: 	// }
624: 	
625: 	int protw = prot & PTE_W;
626: 	int fence = 0;
627: 	uint64 i;
628: 	for (i = 0; i < len; i += PGSIZE) {
629: 		pte_t *pte = walk(pagetable, va + i, 1);
630: 		if (pte == NULL)
631: 			break;
632: 
633: 		if (*pte & PTE_V) {
634: 			fence = 1;
635: 			if (protw && !(*pte & (PTE_W|PTE_COW))) { // may lead to conflict
636: 				/**
637: 				 * Case: the page wasn't writable, but user wants to write it.
638: 				 * Only mark it as copy-on-write, don't mark it writable directly,
639: 				 * let page fault handler take care of that.
640: 				 */
641: 				*pte = (*pte & ~(PTE_X|PTE_W|PTE_R)) | (prot & ~PTE_W) | PTE_COW;
642: 
643: 			} else if ((*pte & (PTE_W|PTE_COW)) && !protw) { // cancel writable
644: 				/**
645: 				 * Case: the page was writable, but user wants to cancel it.
646: 				 * Unmark PTE_COW no matter whether the pte has this marked or not.
647: 				 */
648: 				*pte = (*pte & ~(PTE_COW|PTE_X|PTE_W|PTE_R)) | prot;
649: 
650: 			} else {
651: 				*pte = (*pte & ~(PTE_X|PTE_W|PTE_R)) | prot;
652: 			}
653: 		}
654: 		else {
655: 			/**
656: 			 * Case: protecting a not-present page.
657: 			 * Pre-mark a PTE_U here, if mappages() meets a pte without PTE_V but PTE_U,
658: 			 * it knows how to handle that.
659: 			 */
660: 			*pte = prot | PTE_U;
661: 		}
662: 	}
663: 
664: 	if (fence)
665: 		sfence_vma();
666: 
667: 	return i < len ? -1 : 0;
668: }
669: 
670: /**
671:  * Initialize a kernel pagetable for each process, which is a copy of kernel_pagetable
672:  */
673: pagetable_t

666: 
667: /**
668:  * Initialize a kernel pagetable for each process, which is a copy of kernel_pagetable
669:  */
670: pagetable_t
671: kvmcreate()
672: {
673: 	pagetable_t pagetable;
674: 	if (NULL == (pagetable = allocpage())) {
675: 		return NULL;
676: 	}
677: 
678: 	memmove(pagetable, kernel_pagetable, PGSIZE);
679: 
680: 	return pagetable;
681: }

匹配分数 0.558 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 0.922, "exact_ast_shape": false, "exact_token_fp": false, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 0.219}

711: {
712: 	myproc()->badaddr = badaddr; // this field is useless, borrow it, set unsafe for tht current hart
713: 	return save_point;
714: }
715: 
716: static uint64 safememmove(char *dst, char *src, uint64 len)
717: {
718: 	struct proc * volatile p = myproc();
719: 	uint64 volatile old = p->badaddr;			// p->badaddr might store the stval later
720: 
721: 	// kerneltrap() in trap.c can look up for this safe escape.
722: 	uint64 safe_pc;
723: 	asm volatile("auipc %0, 0x0" : "=r" (safe_pc));
724: 	if (!save_point) {
725: 		__debug_info("do_safememmove", "save point=%p\n", safe_pc);
726: 		save_point = safe_pc;
727: 	}
728: 
729: 	// __sync_synchronize();
730: 
731: 	uint64 badaddr = p->badaddr;
732: 	if (badaddr == old) {
733: 		permit_usr_mem();
734: 		while (len--) {         // There is no overlap between user and kernel space
735: 			*dst++ = *src++;  // this is possible to raise a page fault
736: 		}
737: 		badaddr = 0;
738: 	} else {
739: 		p->badaddr = old;
740: 	}
741: 	protect_usr_mem();
742: 	
743: 	__debug_info("do_safememmove", "badaddr=0x%x\n", badaddr);
744: 	return badaddr;
745: }
746: 
747: // Copy from kernel to user.
748: // Copy len bytes from src to virtual address dstva in a given page table.
749: // Return 0 on success, -1 on error.
750: int

718: 
719: static uint64 safememmove(char *dst, char *src, uint64 len, int srcinc)
720: {
721: 	struct proc * volatile p = myproc();
722: 	uint64 volatile old = p->badaddr;			// p->badaddr might store the stval later
723: 
724: 	// kerneltrap() in trap.c can look up for this safe escape.
725: 	uint64 safe_pc;
726: 	asm volatile("auipc %0, 0x0" : "=r" (safe_pc));
727: 	if (!save_point) {
728: 		__debug_info("do_safememmove", "save point=%p\n", safe_pc);
729: 		save_point = safe_pc;
730: 	}
731: 
732: 	// __sync_synchronize();
733: 
734: 	uint64 badaddr = p->badaddr;
735: 	if (badaddr == old) {
736: 		permit_usr_mem();
737: 		if (srcinc) {
738: 			while (len--) {         // There is no overlap between user and kernel space
739: 				*dst++ = *src++;  // this is possible to raise a page fault
740: 			}
741: 		} else {
742: 			while (len --) {
743: 				*dst++ = *src;
744: 			}
745: 		}
746: 		badaddr = 0;
747: 	} else {
748: 		p->badaddr = old;
749: 	}
750: 	protect_usr_mem();
751: 	
752: 	__debug_info("do_safememmove", "badaddr=0x%x\n", badaddr);
753: 	return badaddr;
754: }
755: 
756: int copyout_noinc_nocheck(uint64 dstva, char *src, uint64 len)
757: {
758: 	return safememmove((char *)dstva, src, len, 0) == 0 ? 0 : -1;
759: }

745: }
746: 
747: // Copy from kernel to user.
748: // Copy len bytes from src to virtual address dstva in a given page table.
749: // Return 0 on success, -1 on error.
750: int
751: copyout(pagetable_t pagetable, uint64 dstva, char *src, uint64 len)
752: {
753: 	uint64 n, va0, pa0;
754: 
755: 	while(len > 0){
756: 		va0 = PGROUNDDOWN(dstva);
757: 		pa0 = walkaddr(pagetable, va0);
758: 		if(pa0 == NULL)
759: 			return -1;
760: 		n = PGSIZE - (dstva - va0);
761: 		if(n > len)
762: 			n = len;
763: 		memmove((void *)(pa0 + (dstva - va0)), src, n);
764: 
765: 		len -= n;
766: 		src += n;
767: 		dstva = va0 + PGSIZE;
768: 	}
769: 	return 0;
770: }
771: 
772: int
773: copyout2(uint64 dstva, char *src, uint64 len)
774: {
775: 	struct proc *p = myproc();

888: 
889: int
890: copyinstr2(char *dst, uint64 srcva, uint64 max)
891: {
892: 	struct seg *seg = locateseg(myproc()->segment, srcva);
893: 	if (seg == NULL)
894: 		return -1;
895: 	
896: 	// max is given by kernel, we should also check the max of user
897: 	uint64 umax = seg->addr + seg->sz - srcva;
898: 	max = (max <= umax) ? max : umax;

902: 
903: int
904: copyinstr2(char *dst, uint64 srcva, uint64 max)
905: {
906: 	struct seg *seg = locateseg(myproc()->segment, srcva);
907: 	if (seg == NULL)
908: 		return -1;
909: 	
910: 	// max is given by kernel, we should also check the max of user
911: 	uint64 umax = seg->addr + seg->sz - srcva;
912: 	max = (max <= umax) ? max : umax;
913: 	if (copyin2(dst, srcva, max) < 0) {
914: 		return -1;
915: 	}
916: 
917: 	char *old = dst;
918: 	int got_null = 0;
919: 	while (max--) {
920: 		if (*dst == '\0') {
921: 			got_null = 1;
922: 			break;
923: 		}
924: 		dst++;
925: 	}
926: 	if(got_null){
927: 		return dst - old;
928: 	} else {
929: 		return -1;
930: 	}
931: }
932:

936: 		return copyout_nocheck(dst, src, len);

935: void vmprint(pagetable_t pagetable)
936: {
937: 	const int capacity = 512;
938: 	printf("page table %p\n", pagetable);
939: 	for (pte_t *pte = (pte_t *) pagetable; pte < pagetable + capacity; pte++) {
940: 		if (*pte & PTE_V)
941: 		{
942: 			pagetable_t pt2 = (pagetable_t) PTE2PA(*pte); 
943: 			printf("..%d: pte %p pa %p\n", pte - pagetable, *pte, pt2);
944: 
945: 			for (pte_t *pte2 = (pte_t *) pt2; pte2 < pt2 + capacity; pte2++) {
946: 				if (*pte2 & PTE_V)
947: 				{
948: 					pagetable_t pt3 = (pagetable_t) PTE2PA(*pte2);
949: 					printf(".. ..%d: pte %p pa %p\n", pte2 - pt2, *pte2, pt3);
950: 
951: 					for (pte_t *pte3 = (pte_t *) pt3; pte3 < pt3 + capacity; pte3++)
952: 						if (*pte3 & PTE_V)
953: 							printf(".. .. ..%d: pte %p pa %p\n", pte3 - pt3, *pte3, PTE2PA(*pte3));
954: 				}
955: 			}
956: 		}
957: 	}
958: 	return;
959: }
960:

961: static int handle_store_page_fault_cow(pte_t *ptep)
962: {
963: 	pte_t pte = *ptep;
964: 	uint64 pa = PTE2PA(pte);
965: 
966: 	__debug_info("handle_store_page_fault_cow", "dst=%p\n", pa);
967: 	if (monopolizepage(pa)) {    // Only this process holds this page.
968: 		pte |= PTE_W;
969: 	} else {
970: 		char *copy = (char *)allocpage();
971: 		if (copy == NULL) {
972: 			pagecopydone();
973: 			return -1;
974: 		}
975: 		memmove(copy, (char *)pa, PGSIZE);
976: 		pagecopydone();
977: 		pagereg((uint64)copy, 1);
978: 		pte = PA2PTE(copy) | PTE_FLAGS(pte) | PTE_W;
979: 	}
980: 
981: 	pte &= ~PTE_COW;	// cancel COW mark
982: 	*ptep = pte;
983: 	sfence_vma();
984: 
985: 	return 0;
986: }
987:

988: static int handle_page_fault_lazy(uint64 badaddr, struct seg *s)
989: {
990: 	__debug_info("handle_page_fault_lazy", "badaddr=%p\n", badaddr);
991: 	struct proc *p = myproc();
992: 
993: 	uint64 pa = PGROUNDDOWN(badaddr);  // in uvmalloc(), oldsz will round up
994: 	if (uvmalloc(p->pagetable, pa, pa + PGSIZE, s->flag) == 0) {
995: 		__debug_warn("handle_page_fault_lazy", "fail\n");
996: 		return -1;
997: 	}
998: 
999: 	sfence_vma();
1000: 	__debug_info("handle_page_fault_lazy", "success\n");
1001: 	return 0;
1002: }

1004: static int handle_page_fault_loadelf(uint64 badaddr, struct seg *s)
1005: {
1006: 	struct proc *p = myproc();
1007: 	__debug_info("handle_page_fault_loadelf", "badaddr=%p, pid=%d, name=%s\n", badaddr, p->pid, p->name);
1008: 	ilock(p->elf);
1009: 	if (loadseg(p->pagetable, badaddr, s, p->elf) < 0) {
1010: 		__debug_warn("handle_page_fault_loadelf", "fail to load at badaddr=%p\n", badaddr);
1011: 		iunlock(p->elf);
1012: 		return -1;
1013: 	}
1014: 	iunlock(p->elf);
1015: 	sfence_vma();
1016: 	return 0;
1017: }

匹配分数 1.000 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": true, "exact_token_fp": true, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 1.0}

1020: 
1021: /**
1022:  * @param   kind      load-0 | store-1 | execute-2 | 
1023:  * @param   badaddr   the stval
1024:  */
1025: int handle_page_fault(int kind, uint64 badaddr)
1026: {
1027: 	struct proc *p = myproc();
1028: 	struct seg *seg = locateseg(p->segment, badaddr);
1029: 	if (seg == NULL) {
1030: 		return -1;
1031: 	}
1032: 
1033: 	pte_t *pte = walk(p->pagetable, badaddr, 0);
1034: 	__debug_info("handle_page_fault", 
1035: 				"\nbadaddr=%p, pte=%p, *pte=%p, kind=%d, segtype=%d\n",
1036: 				badaddr, pte, pte == NULL ? 0 : *pte, kind, seg->type);
1037: 
1038: 	if (pte) {
1039: 		if (kind == 1 && (*pte & PTE_COW)) {
1040: 			// mapped and store-type, might be a COW fault
1041: 			return handle_store_page_fault_cow(pte);
1042: 		}
1043: 	 	if (*pte & PTE_V) {
1044: 			/**
1045: 			 * The page has been mapped,
1046: 			 * so it's not an elf-load/lazy-alloc problem,
1047: 			 * but a real illegal access.
1048: 			 */
1049: 			return -1;
1050: 		}
1051: 		if (*pte & PTE_U) {
1052: 			/**
1053: 			 * If the page is without PTE_V but with PTE_U,
1054: 			 * it indicates that the page has been pre-marked.
1055: 			 * Check whether the access kind breaks the protection
1056: 			 * before we handle it.
1057: 			 */
1058: 			int illegel;
1059: 			switch (kind) {
1060: 			case 0:
1061: 				illegel = !(*pte & PTE_R); break;
1062: 			case 1:
1063: 				illegel = !(*pte & PTE_W); break;
1064: 			case 2:
1065: 				illegel = !(*pte & PTE_X); break;
1066: 			default:
1067: 				illegel = 0;
1068: 				panic("handle_page_fault: kind");
1069: 			}
1070: 			if (illegel)
1071: 				return -1;
1072: 		}
1073: 	}
1074: 
1075: 	switch (seg->type) {
1076: 		case LOAD:
1077: 			return handle_page_fault_loadelf(badaddr, seg);
1078: 
1079: 		case HEAP:
1080: 		case STACK:
1081: 			return handle_page_fault_lazy(badaddr, seg);
1082: 
1083: 		case MMAP:
1084: 			return handle_page_fault_mmap(kind, badaddr, seg);
1085: 
1086: 		default:
1087: 			return -1;
1088: 	}
1089: 
1090: 	return -1;
1091: }

xv6-k210@final / 来源候选源码

1034: 
1035: /**
1036:  * @param   kind      load-0 | store-1 | execute-2 | 
1037:  * @param   badaddr   the stval
1038:  */
1039: int handle_page_fault(int kind, uint64 badaddr)
1040: {
1041: 	struct proc *p = myproc();
1042: 	struct seg *seg = locateseg(p->segment, badaddr);
1043: 	if (seg == NULL) {
1044: 		return -1;
1045: 	}
1046: 
1047: 	pte_t *pte = walk(p->pagetable, badaddr, 0);
1048: 	__debug_info("handle_page_fault", 
1049: 				"\nbadaddr=%p, pte=%p, *pte=%p, kind=%d, segtype=%d\n",
1050: 				badaddr, pte, pte == NULL ? 0 : *pte, kind, seg->type);
1051: 
1052: 	if (pte) {
1053: 		if (kind == 1 && (*pte & PTE_COW)) {
1054: 			// mapped and store-type, might be a COW fault
1055: 			return handle_store_page_fault_cow(pte);
1056: 		}
1057: 	 	if (*pte & PTE_V) {
1058: 			/**
1059: 			 * The page has been mapped,
1060: 			 * so it's not an elf-load/lazy-alloc problem,
1061: 			 * but a real illegal access.
1062: 			 */
1063: 			return -1;
1064: 		}
1065: 		if (*pte & PTE_U) {
1066: 			/**
1067: 			 * If the page is without PTE_V but with PTE_U,
1068: 			 * it indicates that the page has been pre-marked.
1069: 			 * Check whether the access kind breaks the protection
1070: 			 * before we handle it.
1071: 			 */
1072: 			int illegel;
1073: 			switch (kind) {
1074: 			case 0:
1075: 				illegel = !(*pte & PTE_R); break;
1076: 			case 1:
1077: 				illegel = !(*pte & PTE_W); break;
1078: 			case 2:
1079: 				illegel = !(*pte & PTE_X); break;
1080: 			default:
1081: 				illegel = 0;
1082: 				panic("handle_page_fault: kind");
1083: 			}
1084: 			if (illegel)
1085: 				return -1;
1086: 		}
1087: 	}
1088: 
1089: 	switch (seg->type) {
1090: 		case LOAD:
1091: 			return handle_page_fault_loadelf(badaddr, seg);
1092: 
1093: 		case HEAP:
1094: 		case STACK:
1095: 			return handle_page_fault_lazy(badaddr, seg);
1096: 
1097: 		case MMAP:
1098: 			return handle_page_fault_mmap(kind, badaddr, seg);
1099: 
1100: 		default:
1101: 			return -1;
1102: 	}
1103: 
1104: 	return -1;
1105: }

32: static void
33: printint(int xx, int base, int sign)
34: {
35: 	char buf[16];
36: 	int i;
37: 	uint x;
38: 
39: 	if(sign && (sign = xx < 0))
40: 		x = -xx;
41: 	else
42: 		x = xx;
43: 
44: 	i = 0;
45: 	do {
46: 		buf[i++] = digits[x % base];
47: 	} while((x /= base) != 0);
48: 
49: 	if(sign)
50: 		buf[i++] = '-';
51: 
52: 	while(--i >= 0)
53: 		consputc(buf[i]);
54: }
55: 
56:

58: printptr(uint64 x)
59: {

67: // Print to the console. only understands %d, %x, %p, %s.
68: void
69: printf(char *fmt, ...)
70: {
71: 	va_list ap;
72: 	int i, c;
73: 	int locking;
74: 	char *s;
75: 
76: 	locking = pr.locking;
77: 	if(locking)
78: 		acquire(&pr.lock);
79: 	
80: 	if (fmt == 0)
81: 		panic("null fmt");
82: 
83: 	va_start(ap, fmt);
84: 	for(i = 0; (c = fmt[i] & 0xff) != 0; i++){
85: 		if(c != '%'){
86: 			consputc(c);
87: 			continue;
88: 		}
89: 		c = fmt[++i] & 0xff;
90: 		if(c == 0)
91: 			break;
92: 		switch(c){
93: 		case 'd':
94: 			printint(va_arg(ap, int), 10, 1);
95: 			break;
96: 		case 'x':
97: 			printint(va_arg(ap, int), 16, 1);
98: 			break;
99: 		case 'p':
100: 			printptr(va_arg(ap, uint64));
101: 			break;
102: 		case 's':
103: 			if((s = va_arg(ap, char*)) == 0)
104: 				s = "(null)";
105: 			for(; *s; s++)
106: 				consputc(*s);
107: 			break;
108: 		case '%':
109: 			consputc('%');
110: 			break;
111: 		default:
112: 			// Print unknown % sequence to draw attention.
113: 			consputc('%');
114: 			consputc(c);
115: 			break;
116: 		}
117: 	}
118: 	if(locking)
119: 		release(&pr.lock);
120: }
121: 
122: void
123: __panic(char *s)
124: {
125: 	printf(__ERROR("panic")": ");

135: void backtrace()
136: {
137: 	uint64 *fp = (uint64 *)r_fp();
138: 	uint64 *bottom = (uint64 *)PGROUNDUP((uint64)fp);
139: 	printf("backtrace:\n");
140: 	while (fp < bottom) {
141: 		uint64 ra = *(fp - 1);
142: 		printf("%p\n", ra - 4);
143: 		fp = (uint64 *)*(fp - 2);
144: 	}
145: }

44: #define __leave_hash_cs \
45: 	release(&hash_lock);
46: 
47: // Always insert new proc to the front of hash list 
48: // so procs on list is in descending order. 
49: static void hash_insert_no_lock(struct proc *p) {
50: 	__debug_assert("hash_insert", NULL != p, 
51: 			"p is NULL\n");
52: 	
53: 	int key = __hash(p->pid);
54: 	p->hash_next = pid_hash[key];
55: 	p->hash_pprev = &(pid_hash[key]);
56: 	if (NULL != pid_hash[key]) {
57: 		pid_hash[key]->hash_pprev = &(p->hash_next);
58: 	}
59: 	pid_hash[key] = p;
60: }
61:

74: static struct proc *hash_search_no_lock(int pid) {
75: 	__debug_assert("hash_search", pid >= 1, "pid %d too small\n", pid);
76: 
77: 
78: 	int key = __hash(pid);
79: 	struct proc *tmp = pid_hash[key];
80: 	while (tmp && tmp->pid != pid) {
81: 		if (tmp->pid < pid) {
82: 			return NULL;
83: 		}
84: 		else {
85: 			tmp = tmp->hash_next;
86: 		}
87: 	}
88: 
89: 	return tmp;
90: }
91:

93: void cpuinit(void) {
94: 	memset(cpus, 0, sizeof(cpus));
95: }

75: static struct proc *hash_search_no_lock(int pid) {
76: 	__debug_assert("hash_search", pid >= 1, "pid %d too small\n", pid);
77: 
78: 
79: 	int key = __hash(pid);
80: 	struct proc *tmp = pid_hash[key];
81: 	while (tmp && tmp->pid != pid) {
82: 		if (tmp->pid < pid) {
83: 			return NULL;
84: 		}
85: 		else {
86: 			tmp = tmp->hash_next;
87: 		}
88: 	}
89: 
90: 	return tmp;
91: }
92:

94: void cpuinit(void) {
95: 	memset(cpus, 0, sizeof(cpus));
96: }

110: // Create a user page table for a given process,
111: // with no user memory, but with trampoline pages.
112: static pagetable_t
113: proc_pagetable(struct proc *p)
114: {
115: 	pagetable_t pagetable;
116: 
117: 	// An empty page table.
118: 	pagetable = kvmcreate();
119: 	if(pagetable == 0)
120: 		return NULL;
121: 
122: 	return pagetable;
123: }

135: // Free most components of proc, excepting kernel stack, 
136: // kernel mapping of pagetable and proc itself. 
137: static void freeproc(struct proc *p) {
138: 	// file system fields were freed in exit() 
139: 
140: 	// free pagetable 
141: 	proc_freepagetable(p->pagetable, p->segment);
142: 
143: 	// free trapframe 
144: 	kfree(p->trapframe);
145: 
146: 	freepage(p->kstack);
147: 
148: 	// parenting is handled in exit() and wait4()
149: 
150: 	// free signal 
151: 	sigaction_free(p->sig_act);
152: 
153: 	// free the list of sig_frame 
154: 	sigframefree(p->sig_frame);
155: 
156: 	// remove this proc from pid_hash
157: 	__enter_hash_cs
158: 	hash_remove_no_lock(p);
159: 	__leave_hash_cs
160: 
161: 	kfree(p);
162: }
163:

167: 		__debug_warn("allocproc", "fail to alloc\n");

匹配分数 1.000 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": true, "exact_token_fp": true, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 1.0}

159: 	__leave_hash_cs

163: 
164: static struct proc *allocproc(void) {
165: 	struct proc *p = kmalloc(sizeof(struct proc));
166: 	if (NULL == p) {
167: 		__debug_warn("allocproc", "fail to alloc\n");
168: 		return NULL;
169: 	}
170: 
171: 	// times for process performance 
172: 	p->proc_tms.utime = 0;
173: 	p->proc_tms.stime = 0;
174: 	p->proc_tms.cutime = 0;
175: 	p->proc_tms.cstime = 0;
176: 	p->vswtch = p->ivswtch = 0;
177: 
178: 	// parenting 
179: 	initlock(&p->lk, "p->lk");
180: 	p->child = NULL;
181: 
182: 	// memory 
183: 	p->kstack = (uint64)allocpage();
184: 	if (NULL == p->kstack) {
185: 		__debug_warn("allocproc", "fail to set kstack\n");
186: 		kfree(p);
187: 		return NULL;
188: 	}
189: 	p->trapframe = kmalloc(sizeof(struct trapframe));
190: 	if (NULL == p->trapframe) {
191: 		__debug_warn("allocproc", "fail to set trapframe\n");
192: 		freepage((void*)(p->kstack));
193: 		kfree(p);
194: 		return NULL;
195: 	}
196: 	p->segment = NULL;
197: 	if (NULL == (p->pagetable = proc_pagetable(p))) {
198: 		__debug_warn("allocproc", "fail to set pagetable\n");
199: 		kfree((void*)p->trapframe);
200: 		freepage((void*)p->kstack);
201: 		kfree(p);
202: 		return NULL;
203: 	}
204: 
205: 	// scheduling 
206: 	p->chan = NULL;
207: 	p->sched_next = NULL;
208: 	p->sched_pprev = NULL;
209: 	p->context.ra = (uint64)forkret;
210: 	p->context.sp = p->kstack + PGSIZE;
211: 
212: 	// signal 
213: 	p->sig_act = NULL;
214: 	p->sig_frame = NULL;
215: 	for (int i = 0; i < SIGSET_LEN; i ++) {
216: 		p->sig_pending.__val[i] = 0;
217: 	}
218: 	p->killed = 0;
219: 
220: 	// file system 
221: 	memset(&p->fds, 0, sizeof(p->fds));
222: 	p->cwd = p->elf = NULL;
223: 
224: 	// debug information left for initialization 
225: 
226: 	// pid management 
227: 	__enter_hash_cs
228: 	p->pid = __pid ++;
229: 	hash_insert_no_lock(p);
230: 	__leave_hash_cs
231: 
232: 	return p;
233: }
234: 
235: 
236: // lists for scheduling 
237: #define TIMER_IRQ				5
238: #define TIMER_NORMAL			10

xv6-k210@final / 来源候选源码

160: 	__leave_hash_cs
161: 
162: 	kfree(p);
163: }
164: 
165: static struct proc *allocproc(void) {
166: 	struct proc *p = kmalloc(sizeof(struct proc));
167: 	if (NULL == p) {
168: 		__debug_warn("allocproc", "fail to alloc\n");
169: 		return NULL;
170: 	}
171: 
172: 	// times for process performance 
173: 	p->proc_tms.utime = 0;
174: 	p->proc_tms.stime = 0;
175: 	p->proc_tms.cutime = 0;
176: 	p->proc_tms.cstime = 0;
177: 	p->vswtch = p->ivswtch = 0;
178: 
179: 	// parenting 
180: 	initlock(&p->lk, "p->lk");
181: 	p->child = NULL;
182: 
183: 	// memory 
184: 	p->kstack = (uint64)allocpage();
185: 	if (NULL == p->kstack) {
186: 		__debug_warn("allocproc", "fail to set kstack\n");
187: 		kfree(p);
188: 		return NULL;
189: 	}
190: 	p->trapframe = kmalloc(sizeof(struct trapframe));
191: 	if (NULL == p->trapframe) {
192: 		__debug_warn("allocproc", "fail to set trapframe\n");
193: 		freepage((void*)(p->kstack));
194: 		kfree(p);
195: 		return NULL;
196: 	}
197: 	p->segment = NULL;
198: 	if (NULL == (p->pagetable = proc_pagetable(p))) {
199: 		__debug_warn("allocproc", "fail to set pagetable\n");
200: 		kfree((void*)p->trapframe);
201: 		freepage((void*)p->kstack);
202: 		kfree(p);
203: 		return NULL;
204: 	}
205: 
206: 	// scheduling 
207: 	p->chan = NULL;
208: 	p->sched_next = NULL;
209: 	p->sched_pprev = NULL;
210: 	p->context.ra = (uint64)forkret;
211: 	p->context.sp = p->kstack + PGSIZE;
212: 
213: 	// signal 
214: 	p->sig_act = NULL;
215: 	p->sig_frame = NULL;
216: 	for (int i = 0; i < SIGSET_LEN; i ++) {
217: 		p->sig_pending.__val[i] = 0;
218: 	}
219: 	p->killed = 0;
220: 
221: 	// file system 
222: 	memset(&p->fds, 0, sizeof(p->fds));
223: 	p->cwd = p->elf = NULL;
224: 
225: 	// debug information left for initialization 
226: 
227: 	// pid management 
228: 	__enter_hash_cs
229: 	p->pid = __pid ++;
230: 	hash_insert_no_lock(p);
231: 	__leave_hash_cs
232: 
233: 	return p;
234: }
235: 
236: 
237: // lists for scheduling 
238: #define TIMER_IRQ				5
239: #define TIMER_NORMAL			10

250: #define __enter_proc_cs \
251: 	acquire(&proc_lock);
252: #define __leave_proc_cs \
253: 	release(&proc_lock);

255: static void __proc_list_insert_no_lock(struct proc **head, struct proc *p) {
256: 	__debug_assert("proc_list_insert", NULL != p, "insert NULL into list\n");
257: 
258: 	struct proc *tmp = *head;
259: 	while (NULL != tmp) {
260: 		head = &(tmp->sched_next);
261: 		tmp = tmp->sched_next;
262: 	}
263: 	*head = p;
264: 	p->sched_pprev = head;
265: 	p->sched_next = NULL;
266: }

268: 	__debug_assert("proc_list_remove", NULL != p, "remove NULL from list\n");
269: 
270: 	*(p->sched_pprev) = p->sched_next;
271: 	if (NULL != p->sched_next)

288: 
289: int clone(uint64 flag, uint64 stack) {
290: 	struct proc *p = myproc();
291: 	struct proc *np;
292: 
293: 	__debug_info("clone", "%d enter\n", p->pid);
294: 
295: 	np = allocproc();
296: 	if (NULL == np) {
297: 		__debug_warn("fork", "fail to alloc\n");
298: 		return -1;
299: 	}
300: 
301: 	// copy parent's memory layout 
302: 	np->segment = copysegs(p->pagetable, p->segment, np->pagetable);
303: 	if (NULL == np->segment) {
304: 		__debug_warn("clone", "fail to copy segments\n");
305: 		freeproc(np);
306: 		return -1;
307: 	}
308: 	np->pbrk = p->pbrk;
309: 
310: 	// copy parent's signal fields 
311: 	if (0 != sigaction_copy(&np->sig_act, p->sig_act)) {
312: 		__debug_warn("clone", "fail to copy sigaction\n");
313: 		freeproc(np);
314: 		return -1;
315: 	}
316: 	for (int i = 0; i < SIGSET_LEN; i ++) {
317: 		np->sig_set.__val[i] = p->sig_set.__val[i];
318: 	}
319: 
320: 	// these parts may be improved later 
321: 	// filesystem 
322: 	if (copyfdtable(&p->fds, &np->fds) < 0) {
323: 		__debug_warn("clone", "fail to copy fdt\n");
324: 		freeproc(np);
325: 		return -1;
326: 	}
327: 	np->cwd = idup(p->cwd);
328: 	np->elf = p->elf ? idup(p->elf) : NULL;
329: 
330: 	// copy parent's trapframe 
331: 	if (r_sstatus_fs() == SSTATUS_FS_DIRTY) {
332: 		floatstore(p->trapframe);
333: 		w_sstatus_fs(SSTATUS_FS_CLEAN);
334: 	}
335: 	*(np->trapframe) = *(p->trapframe);
336: 	np->trapframe->a0 = 0;
337: 
338: 	if (NULL != stack) {
339: 		np->trapframe->sp = stack;
340: 	}
341: 
342: 	// parenting 
343: 	acquire(&p->lk);
344: 	np->parent = p;
345: 	np->sibling_pprev = &(p->child);
346: 	np->sibling_next = p->child;
347: 	if (NULL != p->child) {
348: 		p->child->sibling_pprev = &(np->sibling_next);
349: 	}
350: 	p->child = np;
351: 	release(&p->lk);
352: 
353: 	// copy debug info 
354: 	safestrcpy(np->name, p->name, sizeof(p->name));
355: 	np->tmask = p->tmask;
356: 
357: 	int pid = np->pid;
358: 
359: 	__debug_info("clone", "hart %d: %d -> %d\n", cpuid(), p->pid, np->pid);
360: 	__debug_info("clone", "hart %d new proc name %s\n", cpuid(), np->name);
361: 
362: 	__enter_proc_cs 
363: 	np->timer = TIMER_NORMAL;
364: 	__insert_runnable(PRIORITY_NORMAL, np);
365: 	__leave_proc_cs 
366: 
367: 	return pid;
368: }

372: 	struct proc *p = proc_sleep;

289: 
290: int clone(uint64 flag, uint64 stack) {
291: 	struct proc *p = myproc();
292: 	struct proc *np;
293: 
294: 	__debug_info("clone", "%d enter\n", p->pid);
295: 
296: 	np = allocproc();
297: 	if (NULL == np) {
298: 		__debug_warn("fork", "fail to alloc\n");
299: 		return -1;
300: 	}
301: 
302: 	// copy parent's memory layout 
303: 	np->segment = copysegs(p->pagetable, p->segment, np->pagetable);
304: 	if (NULL == np->segment) {
305: 		__debug_warn("clone", "fail to copy segments\n");
306: 		freeproc(np);
307: 		return -1;
308: 	}
309: 	np->pbrk = p->pbrk;
310: 
311: 	// copy parent's signal fields 
312: 	if (0 != sigaction_copy(&np->sig_act, p->sig_act)) {
313: 		__debug_warn("clone", "fail to copy sigaction\n");
314: 		freeproc(np);
315: 		return -1;
316: 	}
317: 	for (int i = 0; i < SIGSET_LEN; i ++) {
318: 		np->sig_set.__val[i] = p->sig_set.__val[i];
319: 	}
320: 
321: 	// these parts may be improved later 
322: 	// filesystem 
323: 	if (copyfdtable(&p->fds, &np->fds) < 0) {
324: 		__debug_warn("clone", "fail to copy fdt\n");
325: 		freeproc(np);
326: 		return -1;
327: 	}
328: 	np->cwd = idup(p->cwd);
329: 	np->elf = p->elf ? idup(p->elf) : NULL;
330: 
331: 	// copy parent's trapframe 
332: 	if (r_sstatus_fs() == SSTATUS_FS_DIRTY) {
333: 		((floattrap)floatstore)(p->trapframe);
334: 		w_sstatus_fs(SSTATUS_FS_CLEAN);
335: 	}
336: 	*(np->trapframe) = *(p->trapframe);
337: 	np->trapframe->a0 = 0;
338: 
339: 	if (NULL != stack) {
340: 		np->trapframe->sp = stack;
341: 	}
342: 
343: 	// parenting 
344: 	acquire(&p->lk);
345: 	np->parent = p;
346: 	np->sibling_pprev = &(p->child);
347: 	np->sibling_next = p->child;
348: 	if (NULL != p->child) {
349: 		p->child->sibling_pprev = &(np->sibling_next);
350: 	}
351: 	p->child = np;
352: 	release(&p->lk);
353: 
354: 	// copy debug info 
355: 	safestrcpy(np->name, p->name, sizeof(p->name));
356: 	np->tmask = p->tmask;
357: 
358: 	int pid = np->pid;
359: 
360: 	__debug_info("clone", "hart %d: %d -> %d\n", cpuid(), p->pid, np->pid);
361: 	__debug_info("clone", "hart %d new proc name %s\n", cpuid(), np->name);
362: 
363: 	__enter_proc_cs 
364: 	np->timer = TIMER_NORMAL;
365: 	__insert_runnable(PRIORITY_NORMAL, np);
366: 	__leave_proc_cs 
367: 
368: 	return pid;
369: }

370: // search the hash map to wake up procs 
371: static void __wakeup_no_lock(void *chan) {
372: 	struct proc *p = proc_sleep;
373: 	while (NULL != p) {
374: 		struct proc *next = p->sched_next;
375: 		if ((uint64)chan == (uint64)p->chan) {
376: 			__debug_info("__wakeup_no_lock", "wakeup %d\n", p->pid);
377: 			__remove(p);
378: 			p->timer = TIMER_IRQ;
379: 			p->chan = NULL;
380: 			__insert_runnable(PRIORITY_IRQ, p);
381: 		}
382: 		p = next;
383: 	}
384: }

391: 
392: void exit(int xstate) {
393: 	struct proc *p = myproc();
394: 
395: 	extern struct proc *__initproc;
396: 	__debug_assert("exit", __initproc != p, "init exiting\n");
397: 
398: 	__debug_info("exit", "hart %d pid %d\n", cpuid(), p->pid);
399: 	delsegs(p->pagetable, p->segment);
400: 	p->segment = NULL;
401: 	uvmfree(p->pagetable);
402: 
403: 	// close all open files 
404: 	dropfdtable(&p->fds);
405: 	iput(p->cwd);
406: 	iput(p->elf);
407: 
408: 	p->xstate = xstate;
409: 
410: 	// re-parent all its children to `__initproc`
411: 	acquire(&p->lk);
412: 	if (NULL != p->child) {
413: 		struct proc *first, *last;
414: 		first = last = p->child;
415: 
416: 		while (NULL != last->sibling_next) {
417: 			last->parent = __initproc;
418: 			last = last->sibling_next;
419: 		}
420: 		last->parent = __initproc;
421: 
422: 		acquire(&__initproc->lk);
423: 		first->sibling_pprev = &(__initproc->child);
424: 		last->sibling_next = __initproc->child;
425: 		if (NULL != __initproc->child) {
426: 			__initproc->child->sibling_pprev = &(last->sibling_next);
427: 		}
428: 		__initproc->child = first;
429: 		release(&__initproc->lk);
430: 	}
431: 	release(&p->lk);
432: 
433: 	__debug_info("exit", "%d exit %d\n", p->pid, xstate);
434: 	p->parent->sig_pending.__val[0] |= 1ul << SIGCHLD;
435: 	if (0 == p->parent->killed || SIGCHLD < p->parent->killed) {
436: 		p->parent->killed = SIGCHLD;
437: 	}
438: 	// by holding parent's lock, parent cannot re-parent child
439: 	acquire(&p->parent->lk);
440: 
441: 	// acquire proc_lock after parent's lock, to avoid deadlock 
442: 	// with parent calling sleep(p, &p->lk) in wait4(). 
443: 	__enter_proc_cs
444: 
445: 	p->state = ZOMBIE;
446: 	__remove(p); 
447: 	__wakeup_no_lock(__initproc);
448: 	__wakeup_no_lock(p->parent);
449: 
450: 	// As we don't release p->parent->lk, p->parent is not able 
451: 	// to start searching for zombie child. 
452: 	// As there's no "kernel stack" and kernel pagetable after booting, 
453: 	// parent can free child's proc block only after child has switched out 
454: 	// so release parent's lock in scheduler. 
455: 
456: 	sched();
457: 
458: 	panic("panic! living dead!\n");
459: }
460: 
461: #define WAIT_OPTIONS_WNOHANG 		1
462: #define WAIT_OPTIONS_WUNTRACED 		2
463: #define WAIT_OPTIONS_WCONTINUED 	8
464: int wait4(int pid, uint64 status, uint64 options) {

393: void exit(int xstate) {
394: 	struct proc *p = myproc();
395: 
396: 	extern struct proc *__initproc;
397: 	__debug_assert("exit", __initproc != p, "init exiting\n");
398: 
399: 	__debug_info("exit", "hart %d pid %d\n", cpuid(), p->pid);
400: 	delsegs(p->pagetable, p->segment);
401: 	p->segment = NULL;
402: 	uvmfree(p->pagetable);
403: 
404: 	// close all open files 
405: 	dropfdtable(&p->fds);
406: 	iput(p->cwd);
407: 	iput(p->elf);
408: 
409: 	p->xstate = xstate;
410: 
411: 	// re-parent all its children to `__initproc`
412: 	acquire(&p->lk);
413: 	if (NULL != p->child) {
414: 		struct proc *first, *last;
415: 		first = last = p->child;
416: 
417: 		while (NULL != last->sibling_next) {
418: 			last->parent = __initproc;
419: 			last = last->sibling_next;
420: 		}
421: 		last->parent = __initproc;
422: 
423: 		acquire(&__initproc->lk);
424: 		first->sibling_pprev = &(__initproc->child);
425: 		last->sibling_next = __initproc->child;
426: 		if (NULL != __initproc->child) {
427: 			__initproc->child->sibling_pprev = &(last->sibling_next);
428: 		}
429: 		__initproc->child = first;
430: 		release(&__initproc->lk);
431: 	}
432: 	release(&p->lk);
433: 
434: 	__debug_info("exit", "%d exit %d\n", p->pid, xstate);
435: 	p->parent->sig_pending.__val[0] |= 1ul << SIGCHLD;
436: 	if (0 == p->parent->killed || SIGCHLD < p->parent->killed) {
437: 		p->parent->killed = SIGCHLD;
438: 	}
439: 	// by holding parent's lock, parent cannot re-parent child
440: 	acquire(&p->parent->lk);
441: 
442: 	// acquire proc_lock after parent's lock, to avoid deadlock 
443: 	// with parent calling sleep(p, &p->lk) in wait4(). 
444: 	__enter_proc_cs
445: 
446: 	p->state = ZOMBIE;
447: 	__remove(p); 
448: 	__wakeup_no_lock(__initproc);
449: 	__wakeup_no_lock(p->parent);
450: 
451: 	// As we don't release p->parent->lk, p->parent is not able 
452: 	// to start searching for zombie child. 
453: 	// As there's no "kernel stack" and kernel pagetable after booting, 
454: 	// parent can free child's proc block only after child has switched out 
455: 	// so release parent's lock in scheduler. 
456: 
457: 	sched();
458: 
459: 	panic("panic! living dead!\n");
460: }

463: #define WAIT_OPTIONS_WUNTRACED 		2
464: #define WAIT_OPTIONS_WCONTINUED 	8

460: 
461: #define WAIT_OPTIONS_WNOHANG 		1
462: #define WAIT_OPTIONS_WUNTRACED 		2
463: #define WAIT_OPTIONS_WCONTINUED 	8
464: int wait4(int pid, uint64 status, uint64 options) {
465: 	struct proc *np;
466: 	struct proc *p = myproc();
467: 
468: 	__debug_info("wait4", "hart %d pid %d\n", cpuid(), p->pid);
469: 
470: 	acquire(&p->lk);	// lock must be held to avoid missing wakeup from child's exit()
471: 	while (1) {
472: 		np = p->child;
473: 		while (NULL != np) {
474: 			if (ZOMBIE == np->state && (-1 == pid || pid == np->pid)) {
475: 				// the child is ready to free
476: 				__debug_info("wait4", "%d buries %d\n", p->pid, np->pid);
477: 
478: 				int child_pid = np->pid;
479: 				// remove the child from child list 
480: 				*(np->sibling_pprev) = np->sibling_next;
481: 				if (NULL != np->sibling_next) {
482: 					np->sibling_next->sibling_pprev = np->sibling_pprev;
483: 				}
484: 
485: 				// it's okay to remove lk now, as we find one proc to free 
486: 				release(&p->lk);
487: 
488: 				// add child's tms time 
489: 				p->proc_tms.cstime += np->proc_tms.stime + np->proc_tms.cstime;
490: 				p->proc_tms.cutime += np->proc_tms.utime + np->proc_tms.cutime;
491: 
492: 				// copyout child's xstate 
493: 				np->xstate <<= 8;
494: 				if (status && copyout2(status, (char*)&(np->xstate), sizeof(np->xstate))) {
495: 					__debug_error("wait4", "fail to copy out xstate\n");
496: 					panic("panic!\n");
497: 				}
498: 
499: 				freeproc(np);
500: 				return child_pid;
501: 			}
502: 			else {
503: 				np = np->sibling_next;
504: 			}
505: 		}
506: 
507: 		// if not found, maybe child proc is still runnable or sleeping?
508: 		if (NULL != p->child) {
509: 			if (options & WAIT_OPTIONS_WNOHANG) {
510: 				release(&p->lk);
511: 				return 0;
512: 			}
513: 			else {
514: 				sleep(p, &p->lk);
515: 			}
516: 		}
517: 		else {
518: 			release(&p->lk);
519: 			return -1;
520: 		}
521: 	}
522: }
523: 
524: 
525: // Well, isn'kill a function for signal? We can't place 
526: // it in sched/signal.c because we need to search for the target 
527: // proc with pid, that requires hash_lock.

461: 
462: #define WAIT_OPTIONS_WNOHANG 		1
463: #define WAIT_OPTIONS_WUNTRACED 		2
464: #define WAIT_OPTIONS_WCONTINUED 	8
465: int wait4(int pid, uint64 status, uint64 options) {
466: 	struct proc *np;
467: 	struct proc *p = myproc();
468: 
469: 	__debug_info("wait4", "hart %d pid %d\n", cpuid(), p->pid);
470: 
471: 	acquire(&p->lk);	// lock must be held to avoid missing wakeup from child's exit()
472: 	while (1) {
473: 		np = p->child;
474: 		while (NULL != np) {
475: 			if (ZOMBIE == np->state && (-1 == pid || pid == np->pid)) {
476: 				// the child is ready to free
477: 				__debug_info("wait4", "%d buries %d\n", p->pid, np->pid);
478: 
479: 				int child_pid = np->pid;
480: 				// remove the child from child list 
481: 				*(np->sibling_pprev) = np->sibling_next;
482: 				if (NULL != np->sibling_next) {
483: 					np->sibling_next->sibling_pprev = np->sibling_pprev;
484: 				}
485: 
486: 				// it's okay to remove lk now, as we find one proc to free 
487: 				release(&p->lk);
488: 
489: 				// add child's tms time 
490: 				p->proc_tms.cstime += np->proc_tms.stime + np->proc_tms.cstime;
491: 				p->proc_tms.cutime += np->proc_tms.utime + np->proc_tms.cutime;
492: 
493: 				// copyout child's xstate 
494: 				np->xstate <<= 8;
495: 				if (status && copyout2(status, (char*)&(np->xstate), sizeof(np->xstate))) {
496: 					__debug_error("wait4", "fail to copy out xstate\n");
497: 					panic("panic!\n");
498: 				}
499: 
500: 				freeproc(np);
501: 				return child_pid;
502: 			}
503: 			else {
504: 				np = np->sibling_next;
505: 			}
506: 		}
507: 
508: 		// if not found, maybe child proc is still runnable or sleeping?
509: 		if (NULL != p->child) {
510: 			if (options & WAIT_OPTIONS_WNOHANG) {
511: 				release(&p->lk);
512: 				return 0;
513: 			}
514: 			else {
515: 				sleep(p, &p->lk);
516: 			}
517: 		}
518: 		else {
519: 			release(&p->lk);
520: 			return -1;
521: 		}
522: 	}
523: }
524:

528: // proc with pid, that requires hash_lock.

524: 
525: // Well, isn'kill a function for signal? We can't place 
526: // it in sched/signal.c because we need to search for the target 
527: // proc with pid, that requires hash_lock. 
528: int kill(int pid, int sig) {
529: 	struct proc *tmp;
530: 
531: 	__enter_hash_cs 
532: 	// search for the wanted proc 
533: 	tmp = hash_search_no_lock(pid);
534: 	if (NULL == tmp) {	// if not found 
535: 		__leave_hash_cs 
536: 		return -ESRCH;
537: 	}
538: 
539: 	// we must hold hash_lock here, in case tmp is freed by its parent
540: 	// I mean calling freeproc 
541: 
542: 	__debug_info("kill", "%d send sig %d to %d\n", myproc()->pid, sig, pid);
543: 
544: 	int const len = sizeof(unsigned long) * 8;
545: 	int bit = sig % len;
546: 	int i = sig / len;
547: 
548: 	__assert("kill", i < SIGSET_LEN, "signal too large %d\n", sig);
549: 	tmp->sig_pending.__val[i] |= 1ul << bit;
550: 	if (0 == tmp->killed || sig < tmp->killed) {
551: 		tmp->killed = sig;
552: 	}
553: 
554: 	// printf("pid %d pending_num = %d\n", pid, tmp->killed);
555: 
556: 	__enter_proc_cs 
557: 	if (SLEEPING == tmp->state) {
558: 		__remove(tmp);
559: 		tmp->timer = TIMER_IRQ;
560: 		tmp->chan = NULL;
561: 		__insert_runnable(PRIORITY_IRQ, tmp);
562: 	}
563: 	__leave_proc_cs 
564: 
565: 	__leave_hash_cs 
566: 	return 0;
567: }
568: 
569: void sleep(void *chan, struct spinlock *lk) {
570: 	struct proc *p = myproc();
571: 
572: 	__debug_assert("sleep", NULL != p, "p == NULL\n");

526: // Well, isn'kill a function for signal? We can't place 
527: // it in sched/signal.c because we need to search for the target 
528: // proc with pid, that requires hash_lock. 
529: int kill(int pid, int sig) {
530: 	struct proc *tmp;
531: 
532: 	__enter_hash_cs 
533: 	// search for the wanted proc 
534: 	tmp = hash_search_no_lock(pid);
535: 	if (NULL == tmp) {	// if not found 
536: 		__leave_hash_cs 
537: 		return -ESRCH;
538: 	}
539: 
540: 	// we must hold hash_lock here, in case tmp is freed by its parent
541: 	// I mean calling freeproc 
542: 
543: 	__debug_info("kill", "%d send sig %d to %d\n", myproc()->pid, sig, pid);
544: 
545: 	int const len = sizeof(unsigned long) * 8;
546: 	int bit = sig % len;
547: 	int i = sig / len;
548: 
549: 	__assert("kill", i < SIGSET_LEN, "signal too large %d\n", sig);
550: 	tmp->sig_pending.__val[i] |= 1ul << bit;
551: 	if (0 == tmp->killed || sig < tmp->killed) {
552: 		tmp->killed = sig;
553: 	}
554: 
555: 	// printf("pid %d pending_num = %d\n", pid, tmp->killed);
556: 
557: 	__enter_proc_cs 
558: 	if (SLEEPING == tmp->state) {
559: 		__remove(tmp);
560: 		tmp->timer = TIMER_IRQ;
561: 		tmp->chan = NULL;
562: 		__insert_runnable(PRIORITY_IRQ, tmp);
563: 	}
564: 	__leave_proc_cs 
565: 
566: 	__leave_hash_cs 
567: 	return 0;
568: }

568: 
569: void sleep(void *chan, struct spinlock *lk) {
570: 	struct proc *p = myproc();
571: 
572: 	__debug_assert("sleep", NULL != p, "p == NULL\n");
573: 	__debug_assert("sleep", NULL != lk, "lk == NULL\n");
574: 
575: 	// Either proc_lock or lk must be held, 
576: 	// so that proc would sleep atomically
577: 	if (&proc_lock != lk) {
578: 		__enter_proc_cs 
579: 		release(lk);
580: 	}
581: 
582: 	p->vswtch += 1;
583: 	p->chan = chan;
584: 	__remove(p);		// remove p from runnable 
585: 	__insert_sleep(p);
586: 
587: 	sched();
588: 	p->chan = NULL;
589: 
590: 	// release proc_lock first to avoid deadlock
591: 	// in case another call to sleep() with the same lk 
592: 	__leave_proc_cs 
593: 	acquire(lk);
594: }

匹配分数 1.000 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": true, "exact_token_fp": true, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 1.0}

595: 
596: static struct proc *__get_runnable_no_lock(void) {
597: 	struct proc const *tmp;
598: 
599: 	for (int i = 0; i < PRIORITY_NUMBER; i ++) {
600: 		tmp = proc_runnable[i];
601: 		while (NULL != tmp) {
602: 			if (RUNNABLE == tmp->state) {
603: 				__debug_info("__get_runnable_no_lock", 
604: 						"hart %d get runnable %d\n", 
605: 						cpuid(), tmp->pid);
606: 				return (struct proc*)tmp;
607: 			}
608: 			tmp = tmp->sched_next;
609: 		}
610: 	}
611: 
612: 	return NULL;
613: }

616: int yield(void) {
617: 	struct proc *p = myproc();

633: 	sched();
634: 	__leave_proc_cs

639: 
640: // a fork child's very first sheduling by scheduler() will swtch to forkret 
641: void forkret(uint64 kstack) {
642: 	extern int fs_first;
643: 
644: 	__leave_proc_cs 
645: 
646: 	w_sstatus_fs(SSTATUS_FS_CLEAN);
647: 
648: 	if (fs_first) {
649: 		fs_first = 0;
650: 		rootfs_init();
651: 		myproc()->cwd = namei("/");
652: 	}
653: 
654: 	myproc()->ikstmp = readtime();
655: 	usertrapret();
656: }
657:

657: 
658: void scheduler(void) {
659: 	struct proc *tmp;
660: 	struct cpu *c = mycpu();
661: 
662: 	// it should never returns!
663: 	while (1) {
664: 		int found = 0;
665: 		intr_on();		// make sure interrupts are available 
666: 		__enter_proc_cs 
667: 		tmp = __get_runnable_no_lock();
668: 		if (NULL != tmp) {	// if found one 
669: 			tmp->state = RUNNING;
670: 			c->proc = tmp;
671: 
672: 			// switch to user kernel pagetable 
673: 			// will switch to user's pagetable at usertrapret 
674: 			w_satp(MAKE_SATP(tmp->pagetable));
675: 			sfence_vma();
676: 			// swtch context 
677: 			swtch(&c->context, &tmp->context);
678: 			// switch back to kernel pagetable 
679: 			w_satp(MAKE_SATP(kernel_pagetable));
680: 			sfence_vma();
681: 
682: 			// if tmp is zombie, we need to release the lock of it's parent 
683: 			if (ZOMBIE == tmp->state) {
684: 				release(&(tmp->parent->lk));
685: 			}
686: 			found = 1;
687: 		}
688: 		c->proc = NULL;
689: 		__leave_proc_cs
690: 		if (!found) {
691: 			intr_on();
692: 			asm volatile("wfi");
693: 		}
694: 	} 
695: 
696: 	__debug_error("scheduler", "scheduler returns!\n");
697: 	panic("panic!\n");
698: }
699: 
700: // sched() is the only way back to scheduler() 
701: void sched(void) {
702: 	int intena;
703: 	struct proc *p = myproc();

699: 
700: // sched() is the only way back to scheduler() 
701: void sched(void) {
702: 	int intena;
703: 	struct proc *p = myproc();
704: 
705: 	__debug_assert("sched", NULL != p, "NULL == p\n");
706: 	// __debug_info("sched", "p %s\n", p->name);
707: 	if (!holding(&proc_lock)) 
708: 		panic("sched proc_lock\n");
709: 	if (1 != mycpu()->noff && !(ZOMBIE == p->state && 2 == mycpu()->noff)) 
710: 		panic("sched locks\n");
711: 	if (RUNNING == p->state) 
712: 		panic("sched running\n");
713: 	if (intr_get()) 
714: 		panic("sched interruptible\n");
715: 
716: 	// save floating-point registers
717: 	if (r_sstatus_fs() == SSTATUS_FS_DIRTY) {
718: 		// printf(__INFO("sched")"floatstore, pid=%d, name=%s\n", p->pid, p->name);
719: 		floatstore(p->trapframe);
720: 		w_sstatus_fs(SSTATUS_FS_CLEAN);
721: 	}
722: 	
723: 	p->proc_tms.stime += readtime() - p->ikstmp;
724: 
725: 	intena = mycpu()->intena;
726: 	swtch(&p->context, &mycpu()->context);
727: 	mycpu()->intena = intena;
728: 
729: 	p->ikstmp = readtime();
730: 
731: 	// On k210, sstatus.fs seems to be hardwired to dirty(11b).
732: 	// if (r_sstatus_fs() == SSTATUS_FS_DIRTY)
733: 	// 	panic("sched out with dirty floating-point registers");
734: 
735: 	floatload(p->trapframe);
736: 	w_sstatus_fs(SSTATUS_FS_CLEAN);
737: }
738:

741: 	__enter_proc_cs

720: 		((floattrap)floatstore)(p->trapframe);

735:

739: 
740: void proc_tick(void) {
741: 	__enter_proc_cs 
742: 
743: 	// runnable 
744: 	struct proc *p;
745: 	for (int i = PRIORITY_IRQ; i < PRIORITY_NUMBER; i ++) {
746: 		p = proc_runnable[i];
747: 		while (NULL != p) {
748: 			struct proc *next = p->sched_next;
749: 			if (RUNNING != p->state) {
750: 				p->timer = p->timer - 1;
751: 				if (0 == p->timer) {	// timeout 
752: 					__remove(p);
753: 					__insert_runnable(PRIORITY_TIMEOUT, p);
754: 				}
755: 			}
756: 			p = next;
757: 		}
758: 	}
759: 
760: 	// sleep 
761: 	uint64 now = readtime();
762: 	p = proc_sleep;
763: 	while (NULL != p) {
764: 		struct proc *next = p->sched_next;
765: 		if (p->sleep_expire && now >= p->sleep_expire) {
766: 			p->sleep_expire = 0;
767: 			__remove(p);
768: 			__insert_runnable(PRIORITY_TIMEOUT, p);
769: 		}
770: 		p = next;
771: 	}
772: 
773: 	__leave_proc_cs
774: }
775: 
776:

778: // Return 0 on success, -1 on failure. 
779: int growproc(uint64 newbrk) {

742: 	__enter_proc_cs 
743: 
744: 	// runnable

771: 		p = next;

匹配分数 1.000 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": true, "exact_token_fp": true, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 1.0}

oskernel2023-zmz@recover 源码

774: }
775: 
776: 
777: // Grow or shrink user memory by n bytes. 
778: // Return 0 on success, -1 on failure. 
779: int growproc(uint64 newbrk) {
780: 	struct proc *p = myproc();
781: 	struct seg *heap = getseg(p->segment, HEAP);
782: 
783: 	while (heap && p->pbrk != heap->addr + heap->sz) {
784: 		heap = getseg(heap->next, HEAP);
785: 	}
786: 
787: 	if (!heap) {
788: 		heap = locateseg(p->segment, p->pbrk - 1);
789: 	}
790: 	if (!heap || HEAP != heap->type || newbrk < heap->addr) {	// mmap or munmap ruined this 
791: 		return -1;
792: 	}
793: 
794: 	uint64 boundary = NULL == heap->next ? 
795: 			MAXUVA : PGROUNDDOWN(heap->next->addr) - PGSIZE;
796: 	if (newbrk > boundary) {	// the newbrk outbreach the boundary
797: 		return -1;
798: 	}
799: 
800: 	// to shrink
801: 	if (newbrk < p->pbrk) {
802: 		uvmdealloc(p->pagetable, newbrk, p->pbrk);
803: 		sfence_vma();
804: 	}
805: 
806: 	// update heap size and pbrk
807: 	int64 diff = newbrk - p->pbrk;
808: 	heap->sz += diff;
809: 	p->pbrk = newbrk;
810: 
811: 	return 0;
812: }
813:

817: // Returns 0 on success, -1 on error.

778: // Grow or shrink user memory by n bytes. 
779: // Return 0 on success, -1 on failure. 
780: int growproc(uint64 newbrk) {
781: 	struct proc *p = myproc();
782: 	struct seg *heap = getseg(p->segment, HEAP);
783: 
784: 	while (heap && p->pbrk != heap->addr + heap->sz) {
785: 		heap = getseg(heap->next, HEAP);
786: 	}
787: 
788: 	if (!heap) {
789: 		heap = locateseg(p->segment, p->pbrk - 1);
790: 	}
791: 	if (!heap || HEAP != heap->type || newbrk < heap->addr) {	// mmap or munmap ruined this 
792: 		return -1;
793: 	}
794: 
795: 	uint64 boundary = NULL == heap->next ? 
796: 			MAXUVA : PGROUNDDOWN(heap->next->addr) - PGSIZE;
797: 	if (newbrk > boundary) {	// the newbrk outbreach the boundary
798: 		return -1;
799: 	}
800: 
801: 	// to shrink
802: 	if (newbrk < p->pbrk) {
803: 		uvmdealloc(p->pagetable, newbrk, p->pbrk);
804: 		sfence_vma();
805: 	}
806: 
807: 	// update heap size and pbrk
808: 	int64 diff = newbrk - p->pbrk;
809: 	heap->sz += diff;
810: 	p->pbrk = newbrk;
811: 
812: 	return 0;
813: }

858: 
859: static void __print_proc_no_lock(struct proc *list) {
860: 	extern struct proc *__initproc;
861: 
862: 	while (list) {
863: 		// calculate proc's load and heap memory size 
864: 		uint64 load = 0, heap = 0;
865: 		for (struct seg const *s = list->segment; NULL != s; s = s->next) {
866: 			if (LOAD == s->type) {
867: 				load += s->sz;
868: 			}
869: 			else if (HEAP == s->type) {
870: 				heap = s->sz;
871: 			}
872: 		}
873: 
874: 		printf("%d\t%d\t%s\t%d\t%s\t%d\t%d\t%p\n", 
875: 			list->pid, 
876: 			__initproc == list ? 0 : list->parent->pid, 
877: 			__state_to_str(list->state),
878: 			list->killed, 
879: 			list->name, 
880: 			load, heap, 
881: 			list->chan
882: 		);
883: 		list = list->hash_next;
884: 	}
885: }
886:

888: void procdump(void) {
889: 	printf("\nepc = %p\n", r_sepc());

856: 	default: 			return "\e[31;1m???\e[0m";
857: 	}
858: }

860: static void __print_proc_no_lock(struct proc *list) {
861: 	extern struct proc *__initproc;
862: 
863: 	while (list) {
864: 		// calculate proc's load and heap memory size 
865: 		uint64 load = 0, heap = 0;
866: 		for (struct seg const *s = list->segment; NULL != s; s = s->next) {
867: 			if (LOAD == s->type) {
868: 				load += s->sz;
869: 			}
870: 			else if (HEAP == s->type) {
871: 				heap = s->sz;
872: 			}
873: 		}
874: 
875: 		printf("%d\t%d\t%s\t%d\t%s\t%d\t%d\t%p\n", 
876: 			list->pid, 
877: 			__initproc == list ? 0 : list->parent->pid, 
878: 			__state_to_str(list->state),
879: 			list->killed, 
880: 			list->name, 
881: 			load, heap, 
882: 			list->chan
883: 		);
884: 		list = list->hash_next;
885: 	}
886: }

971: struct proc *__initproc = NULL;
972: extern int newinit[];
973: extern int newinit_size;
974: 
975: // init first proc `__initproc`
976: void userinit(void) {
977: 	fs_first = 1;
978: 
979: 	struct proc *p = allocproc();
980: 	__initproc = p;
981: 
982: 	p->parent = NULL;
983: 	p->sibling_pprev = NULL;
984: 	p->sibling_next = NULL;
985: 
986: 	// allocate one user page and copy initcode's instruction 
987: 	// and data into it 
988: 	// uvminit(p->pagetable, newinit, newinit_size);
989: 	uvminit(p->pagetable, initcode, sizeof(initcode));
990: 
991: 
992: 	struct seg *s = kmalloc(sizeof(struct seg));
993: 	s->addr = 0;
994: 	s->sz = PGSIZE;
995: 	s->next = NULL;
996: 	s->type = LOAD;
997: 	p->segment = s;
998: 
999: 	// prepare for the very first "return from kernel"
1000: 	p->trapframe->epc = 0x0;
1001: 	p->trapframe->sp = PGSIZE;
1002: 
1003: 	//safestrcpy(p->name, "newinit", sizeof(p->name));
1004: 	safestrcpy(p->name, "initcode", sizeof(p->name));
1005: 
1006: 	p->tmask = 0;
1007: 	p->elf = NULL;
1008: 
1009: 	for (int i = 0; i < SIGSET_LEN; i ++) {
1010: 		p->sig_set.__val[i] = ~0;
1011: 	}
1012: 
1013: 	__enter_proc_cs 
1014: 	__insert_runnable(PRIORITY_NORMAL, p);
1015: 	__leave_proc_cs 
1016: 
1017: 	__debug_info("userinit", "init\n");
1018: }

xv6-k210@final / 来源候选源码

970: // initiailization 
971: int fs_first;
972: struct proc *__initproc = NULL;
973: 
974: // init first proc `__initproc`
975: void userinit(void) {
976: 	fs_first = 1;
977: 
978: 	struct proc *p = allocproc();
979: 	__initproc = p;
980: 
981: 	p->parent = NULL;
982: 	p->sibling_pprev = NULL;
983: 	p->sibling_next = NULL;
984: 
985: 	// allocate one user page and copy initcode's instruction 
986: 	// and data into it 
987: 	uvminit(p->pagetable, initcode, sizeof(initcode));
988: 
989: 	struct seg *s = kmalloc(sizeof(struct seg));
990: 	s->addr = 0;
991: 	s->sz = PGSIZE;
992: 	s->next = NULL;
993: 	s->type = LOAD;
994: 	p->segment = s;
995: 
996: 	// prepare for the very first "return from kernel"
997: 	p->trapframe->epc = 0x0;
998: 	p->trapframe->sp = PGSIZE;
999: 
1000: 	safestrcpy(p->name, "initcode", sizeof(p->name));
1001: 
1002: 	p->tmask = 0;
1003: 	// p->tmask = 1;
1004: 	p->elf = NULL;
1005: 
1006: 	for (int i = 0; i < SIGSET_LEN; i ++) {
1007: 		p->sig_set.__val[i] = ~0;
1008: 	}
1009: 
1010: 	__enter_proc_cs 
1011: 	__insert_runnable(PRIORITY_NORMAL, p);
1012: 	__leave_proc_cs 
1013: 
1014: 	__debug_info("userinit", "init\n");
1015: }
1016:

1018: 	// init pid_hash 
1019: 	__pid = 1;
1020: 	for (int i = 0; i < HASH_SIZE; i ++) {

1017: void procinit(void) {
1018: 	// init pid_hash 
1019: 	__pid = 1;
1020: 	for (int i = 0; i < HASH_SIZE; i ++) {
1021: 		pid_hash[i] = NULL;
1022: 	}
1023: 	initlock(&hash_lock, "hash_lock");
1024: 
1025: 	// init list 
1026: 	for (int i = 0; i < PRIORITY_NUMBER; i ++) {
1027: 		proc_runnable[i] = NULL;
1028: 	}
1029: 	proc_sleep = NULL;
1030: 	initlock(&proc_lock, "proc_lock");
1031: 
1032: 	__debug_info("procinit", "init\n");
1033: }

28: static ksigaction_t *__search_sig(struct proc *p, int signum) {
29: 	__debug_assert("__search_sig", NULL != p, "p == NULL\n");
30: 
31: 	ksigaction_t const* tmp = p->sig_act;
32: 
33: 	while (NULL != tmp) {
34: 		if (tmp->signum == signum) {
35: 			break;
36: 		}
37: 		tmp = tmp->next;
38: 	}
39: 
40: 	return (ksigaction_t*)tmp;
41: }

43: int set_sigaction(
44: 	int signum, 
45: 	struct sigaction const *act, 
46: 	struct sigaction *oldact, 
47: 	int len
48: ) {
49: 	struct proc *p = myproc();
50: 
51: 	ksigaction_t *tmp = __search_sig(p, signum);
52: 	// printf("pid %d search %d find %p\n", p->pid, signum, tmp);
53: 
54: 	if (NULL != oldact && NULL != tmp) {
55: 		oldact->__sigaction_handler = tmp->sigact.__sigaction_handler;
56: 		oldact->sa_flags = tmp->sigact.sa_flags;

58: 			oldact->sa_mask.__val[i] = tmp->sigact.sa_mask.__val[i];
59: 		}
60: 	}
61: 
62: 	if (NULL != act) {
63: 		if (NULL == tmp) {
64: 			// insert a new action into the proc 
65: 			ksigaction_t *new = kmalloc(sizeof(ksigaction_t));
66: 			__debug_assert("set_sigaction", NULL != new, "alloc error\n");
67: 			__insert_sig(p, new);
68: 			tmp = new;
69: 		}
70: 
71: 		tmp->sigact.sa_flags = act->sa_flags;
72: 		tmp->sigact.__sigaction_handler = act->__sigaction_handler;

74: 			tmp->sigact.sa_mask.__val[i] = act->sa_mask.__val[i];
75: 		}
76: 		tmp->signum = signum;
77: 	}

89: 
90: int sigprocmask(
91: 	int how, 
92: 	__sigset_t *set, 
93: 	__sigset_t *oldset, 
94: 	int len
95: ) {
96: 	struct proc *p = myproc();
97: 
98: 	__debug_assert("sigprocmask", NULL != p, "p == NULL\n");
99: 
100: 	for (int i = 0; i < len; i ++) {
101: 		if (NULL != oldset) {
102: 			oldset->__val[i] = p->sig_set.__val[i];
103: 		}
104: 
105: 		switch (how) {
106: 			case SIG_BLOCK: 
107: 				p->sig_set.__val[i] |= set->__val[i];
108: 				break;
109: 			case SIG_UNBLOCK: 
110: 				p->sig_set.__val[i] &= ~(set->__val[i]);
111: 				break;
112: 			case SIG_SETMASK: 
113: 				p->sig_set.__val[i] = set->__val[i];
114: 				break;
115: 			default: 
116: 				panic("invalid how\n");
117: 		}
118: 	}
119: 
120: 	// SIGTERM cannot be masked 
121: 	p->sig_set.__val[0] &= 1ul << SIGTERM;
122: 
123: 	return 0;
124: }
125:

129: 		__debug_assert("sigframefree", next != head, "loop!\n");

146: int sigaction_copy(ksigaction_t **pdst, ksigaction_t const *src) {
147: 	ksigaction_t *tmp = NULL;
148: 
149: 	*pdst = NULL;
150: 	if (NULL == src) {
151: 		return 0;
152: 	}
153: 
154: 	while (NULL != src) {
155: 		tmp = kmalloc(sizeof(ksigaction_t));
156: 		if (NULL == tmp) {
157: 			__debug_warn("sigaction_copy", "fail to alloc\n");
158: 			sigaction_free(*pdst);
159: 			*pdst = NULL;
160: 			return -1;
161: 		}
162: 
163: 		*tmp = *src;
164: 		tmp->next = *pdst;
165: 		*pdst = tmp;
166: 
167: 		src = src->next;
168: 	}
169: 
170: 	return 0;
171: }

150: 	if (NULL == src) {
151: 		return 0;
152: 	}
153: 
154: 	while (NULL != src) {
155: 		tmp = kmalloc(sizeof(ksigaction_t));
156: 		if (NULL == tmp) {
157: 			__debug_warn("sigaction_copy", "fail to alloc\n");
158: 			sigaction_free(*pdst);
159: 			*pdst = NULL;
160: 			return -1;
161: 		}
162: 
163: 		*tmp = *src;
164: 		tmp->next = *pdst;
165: 		*pdst = tmp;
166: 
167: 		src = src->next;
168: 	}
169: 
170: 	return 0;
171: }

匹配分数 0.709 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": false, "exact_token_fp": false, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 0.695}

oskernel2023-zmz@recover 源码

172: 
173: extern char sig_trampoline[];
174: extern char sig_handler[];
175: extern char default_sigaction[];
176: 
177: void sighandle(void) {
178: 	struct proc *p = myproc();
179: 
180: 	__debug_assert("sigdetect", NULL != p, "p == NULL\n");
181: 
182: 	__debug_info("sighandle", "killed = %d\n", p->killed);
183: 	int signum = 0;
184: 	if (p->killed) {
185: 		signum = p->killed;
186: 
187: 		const int len = sizeof(unsigned long) * 8;
188: 		int i = (unsigned long)(p->killed) / len;
189: 		int bit = (unsigned long)(p->killed) % len;
190: 		p->sig_pending.__val[i] &= ~(1ul << bit++);
191: 		p->killed = 0;
192: 
193: 		for (; i < SIGSET_LEN; i ++) {
194: 			while (bit < len) {
195: 				if (p->sig_pending.__val[i] & (1ul << bit)) {
196: 					p->killed = i * len + bit;
197: 					goto start_handle;
198: 				}
199: 				bit ++;
200: 			}
201: 		}
202: 
203: 		__debug_info("sighandler", "pid %d update pending_num %d -> %d\n", 
204: 				p->pid, signum, p->killed);
205: 	}
206: 	else {
207: 		// no signal to handle
208: 		return ;
209: 	}
210: 
211: 	struct sig_frame *frame;
212: 	struct trapframe *tf;
213: 	ksigaction_t *sigact;
214: start_handle: 
215: 	// search for signal handler 
216: 	sigact = __search_sig(p, signum);
217: 
218: 	// fast skip 
219: 	// if (NULL == sigact && SIGCHLD == signum) {
220: 	// 	return ;
221: 	// }
222: 	if (SIGCHLD == signum && 
223: 		(NULL == sigact || NULL == sigact->sigact.__sigaction_handler.sa_handler)) {
224: 			return;
225: 	}
226: 
227: 	frame = kmalloc(sizeof(struct sig_frame));
228: 	__assert("sigdetect", NULL != frame, "alloc frame failed\n");
229: 
230: 	tf = kmalloc(sizeof(struct trapframe));
231: 	__assert("sigdetect", NULL != tf, "alloc tf failed\n");
232: 
233: 	// copy mask 
234: 	for (int i = 0; i < SIGSET_LEN; i ++) {
235: 		frame->mask.__val[i] = p->sig_set.__val[i];
236: 		if (NULL == sigact) {
237: 			p->sig_set.__val[i] = 0;
238: 		}
239: 		else {
240: 			p->sig_set.__val[i] &= sigact->sigact.sa_mask.__val[i];
241: 		}
242: 	}
243: 
244: 	// store proc's trapframe 
245: 	frame->tf = p->trapframe;
246: 	tf->epc = (uint64)(SIG_TRAMPOLINE + ((uint64)sig_handler - (uint64)sig_trampoline));
247: 	tf->sp = p->trapframe->sp;
248: 	tf->a0 = signum;
249: 	if (NULL != sigact && sigact->sigact.__sigaction_handler.sa_handler) {
250: 		tf->a1 = (uint64)(sigact->sigact.__sigaction_handler.sa_handler);
251: 	}
252: 	else {
253: 		// use the default handler 
254: 		tf->a1 = (uint64)(SIG_TRAMPOLINE + ((uint64)default_sigaction - (uint64)sig_trampoline));
255: 	}
256: 	p->trapframe = tf;
257: 
258: 	// insert sig_frame into proc's sig_frame list 
259: 	frame->next = p->sig_frame;
260: 	p->sig_frame = frame;
261: }
262: 
263: void sigreturn(void) {
264: 	struct proc *p = myproc();
265: 
266: 	__debug_assert("sigreturn", NULL != p, "no proc\n");

xv6-k210@final / 来源候选源码

173: extern char sig_trampoline[];
174: extern char sig_handler[];
175: extern char default_sigaction[];
176: 
177: void sighandle(void) {
178: 	struct proc *p = myproc();
179: 
180: 	__debug_assert("sigdetect", NULL != p, "p == NULL\n");
181: 
182: 	__debug_info("sighandle", "killed = %d\n", p->killed);
183: 	int signum = 0;
184: 	if (p->killed) {
185: 		signum = p->killed;
186: 
187: 		const int len = sizeof(unsigned long) * 8;
188: 		int i = (unsigned long)(p->killed) / len;
189: 		int bit = (unsigned long)(p->killed) % len;
190: 		p->sig_pending.__val[i] &= ~(1ul << bit++);
191: 		p->killed = 0;
192: 
193: 		for (; i < SIGSET_LEN; i ++) {
194: 			while (bit < len) {
195: 				if (p->sig_pending.__val[i] & (1ul << bit)) {
196: 					p->killed = i * len + bit;
197: 					goto start_handle;
198: 				}
199: 				bit ++;
200: 			}
201: 		}
202: 
203: 		__debug_info("sighandler", "pid %d update pending_num %d -> %d\n", 
204: 				p->pid, signum, p->killed);
205: 	}
206: 	else {
207: 		// no signal to handle
208: 		return ;
209: 	}
210: 
211: 	struct sig_frame *frame;
212: 	struct trapframe *tf;
213: 	ksigaction_t *sigact;
214: start_handle: 
215: 	// search for signal handler 
216: 	sigact = __search_sig(p, signum);
217: 
218: 	// fast skip 
219: 	// if (NULL == sigact && SIGCHLD == signum) {
220: 	// 	return ;
221: 	// }
222: 	if (SIGCHLD == signum && 
223: 		(NULL == sigact || NULL == sigact->sigact.__sigaction_handler.sa_handler)) {
224: 			return;
225: 	}
226: 
227: 	frame = kmalloc(sizeof(struct sig_frame));
228: 	__assert("sigdetect", NULL != frame, "alloc frame failed\n");
229: 
230: 	tf = kmalloc(sizeof(struct trapframe));
231: 	__assert("sigdetect", NULL != tf, "alloc tf failed\n");
232: 
233: 	// copy mask 
234: 	// for (int i = 0; i < SIGSET_LEN; i ++) {
235: 	// 	frame->mask.__val[i] = p->sig_set.__val[i];
236: 	// 	if (NULL == sigact) {
237: 	// 		p->sig_set.__val[i] = 0;
238: 	// 	}
239: 	// 	else {
240: 	// 		p->sig_set.__val[i] &= sigact->sigact.sa_mask.__val[i];
241: 	// 	}
242: 	// }
243: 
244: 	// store proc's trapframe 
245: 	frame->tf = p->trapframe;
246: 	tf->epc = (uint64)(SIG_TRAMPOLINE + ((uint64)sig_handler - (uint64)sig_trampoline));
247: 	tf->sp = p->trapframe->sp;
248: 	tf->a0 = signum;
249: 	if (NULL != sigact && sigact->sigact.__sigaction_handler.sa_handler) {
250: 		tf->a1 = (uint64)(sigact->sigact.__sigaction_handler.sa_handler);
251: 	}
252: 	else {
253: 		// use the default handler 
254: 		tf->a1 = (uint64)(SIG_TRAMPOLINE + ((uint64)default_sigaction - (uint64)sig_trampoline));
255: 	}
256: 	p->trapframe = tf;
257: 
258: 	// insert sig_frame into proc's sig_frame list 
259: 	frame->next = p->sig_frame;
260: 	p->sig_frame = frame;
261: }

匹配分数 0.641 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": false, "exact_token_fp": false, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 0.469}

262: 
263: void sigreturn(void) {
264: 	struct proc *p = myproc();
265: 
266: 	__debug_assert("sigreturn", NULL != p, "no proc\n");
267: 	if (NULL == p->sig_frame) {	// it's not in a sighandler!
268: 		exit(-1);
269: 	}
270: 
271: 	__debug_info("sigreturn", "pid %d sigreturn\n", p->pid);
272: 
273: 	struct sig_frame *frame = p->sig_frame;
274: 	for (int i = 0; i < SIGSET_LEN; i ++) {
275: 		p->sig_set.__val[i] = frame->mask.__val[i];
276: 	}
277: 	kfree(p->trapframe);
278: 	p->trapframe = frame->tf;
279: 
280: 	// remove this frame from list 
281: 	p->sig_frame = frame->next;
282: 	kfree(frame);
283: }

xv6-k210@final / 来源候选源码

258: 	// insert sig_frame into proc's sig_frame list 
259: 	frame->next = p->sig_frame;
260: 	p->sig_frame = frame;
261: }
262: 
263: void sigreturn(void) {
264: 	struct proc *p = myproc();
265: 
266: 	__debug_assert("sigreturn", NULL != p, "no proc\n");
267: 	if (NULL == p->sig_frame) {	// it's not in a sighandler!
268: 		exit(-1);
269: 	}
270: 
271: 	__debug_info("sigreturn", "pid %d sigreturn\n", p->pid);
272: 
273: 	struct sig_frame *frame = p->sig_frame;
274: 	// for (int i = 0; i < SIGSET_LEN; i ++) {
275: 	// 	p->sig_set.__val[i] = frame->mask.__val[i];
276: 	// }
277: 	kfree(p->trapframe);
278: 	p->trapframe = frame->tf;
279: 
280: 	// remove this frame from list 
281: 	p->sig_frame = frame->next;
282: 	kfree(frame);
283: }

29: static char* 
30: sprintint(char* d, int bufLength, int xx, int base, int sign)
31: {
32: 	char buf[16];
33: 	int i;
34: 	uint x;
35: 
36: 	if(sign && (sign = xx < 0))
37: 		x = -xx;
38: 	else
39: 		x = xx;
40: 
41: 	i = 0;
42: 	do {
43: 		buf[i++] = digits[x % base];
44: 	} while((x /= base) != 0);
45: 
46: 	if(sign)
47: 		buf[i++] = '-';
48: 
49: 	int count = 0;
50: 	while (--i >= 0){
51: 		*d++ = buf[i];
52: 		// consputc(buf[i]);
53: 		if (++count >= bufLength) {
54: 			return NULL;
55: 		}
56: 	}
57: 	return d;
58: }
59: 
60: 
61: static char* 
62: sprintptr(char* d, int bufLength, uint64 x)
63: {

61: static char* 
62: sprintptr(char* d, int bufLength, uint64 x)
63: {
64: 	int i;
65: 	int count = 0;
66: 	// consputc('0');
67: 	*d++ =  '0';
68: 	if (++count >= bufLength) {
69: 		return NULL;
70: 	}
71: 	// consputc('x');
72: 	*d++ = 'x';
73: 	if (++count >= bufLength) {
74: 		return NULL;
75: 	}
76: 	for (i = 0; i < (sizeof(uint64) * 2); i++, x <<= 4){
77: 		// consputc(digits[x >> (sizeof(uint64) * 8 - 4)]);
78: 		*d++ = digits[x >> (sizeof(uint64) * 8 -4)];
79: 		if (++count >= bufLength) {
80: 			return NULL;
81: 		}
82: 	}
83: 	return d;
84: }
