匹配分数 0.648 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": false, "exact_token_fp": false, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 0.492}

oskernel2023-zmz@recover 源码

85: 
86: /*
87:  * Description: print to the string. only understands %d, %x, %p, %s.
88:  * Return: number of characters written, -1 at overflow.
89:  */
90: int
91: sprintf(char *s, unsigned long bufLength, char *fmt, ...)
92: {
93: 	va_list ap;
94: 	int i, c;
95: 	char *source;
96: 	char *start = s;
97: 	char *current = s;
98: 	char *end = &s[bufLength];
99: 
100: 	if (fmt == 0)
101: 		panic("null fmt");
102: 
103: 	va_start(ap, fmt);
104: 	for(i = 0; (c = fmt[i] & 0xff) != 0; i++){
105: 		if(c != '%'){
106: 			// consputc(c);
107: 			*current++ = c;
108: 			if (current == end) {
109: 				return -1;
110: 			}
111: 			continue;
112: 		}
113: 		c = fmt[++i] & 0xff;
114: 		if(c == 0)
115: 			break;
116: 		switch(c){
117: 		case 'd':
118: 			current = sprintint(current, bufLength-(current-start), va_arg(ap, int), 10, 1);
119: 			if (!current) {
120: 				return -1;
121: 			}
122: 			break;
123: 		case 'x':
124: 			current = sprintint(current, bufLength-(current-start), va_arg(ap, int), 16, 1);
125: 			if (!current) {
126: 				return -1;
127: 			}
128: 			break;
129: 		case 'p':
130: 			current = sprintptr(current, bufLength-(current-start), va_arg(ap, uint64));
131: 			if (!current) {
132: 				return -1;
133: 			}
134: 			break;
135: 		case 's':
136: 			if((source = va_arg(ap, char*)) == 0)
137: 				source = "(null)";
138: 			for(; *source; source++) {
139: 				// consputc(*source);
140: 				*current++ = *source;
141: 				if (current == end) {
142: 					return -1; 
143: 				}
144: 			}
145: 			break;
146: 		case '%':
147: 			// consputc('%');
148: 			*current++ = '%';
149: 			if (current == end) {
150: 				return -1;
151: 			}
152: 			break;
153: 		default:
154: 			// Print unknown % sequence to draw attention.
155: 			// consputc('%');
156: 			*current++ = '%';
157: 			if (current == end) {
158: 				return -1;
159: 			}
160: 			// consputc(c);
161: 			*current++ = c;
162: 			if (current == end) {
163: 				return -1;
164: 			}
165: 			break;
166: 		}
167: 	}
168: 	*current++ = '\0';
169: 	return current - start;
170: }

xv6-k210@final / 来源候选源码

85: 
86: /*
87:  * Description: print to the string. only understands %d, %x, %p, %s.
88:  * Return: number of characters written, -1 at overflow.
89:  */
90: int
91: sprintf(char *s, unsigned long bufLength, char *fmt, ...)
92: {
93: 	va_list ap;
94: 	int i, c;
95: 	char *source;
96: 	char *start = s;
97: 	char *current = s;
98: 	char *end = &s[bufLength];
99: 
100: 	// if (fmt == 0)
101: 	// 	panic("null fmt");
102: 
103: 	va_start(ap, fmt);
104: 	for(i = 0; (c = fmt[i] & 0xff) != 0; i++){
105: 		if(c != '%'){
106: 			// consputc(c);
107: 			*current++ = c;
108: 			if (current == end) {
109: 				return -1;
110: 			}
111: 			continue;
112: 		}
113: 		c = fmt[++i] & 0xff;
114: 		if(c == 0)
115: 			break;
116: 		switch(c){
117: 		case 'd':
118: 			current = sprintint(current, bufLength-(current-start), va_arg(ap, int), 10, 1);
119: 			if (!current) {
120: 				return -1;
121: 			}
122: 			break;
123: 		case 'x':
124: 			current = sprintint(current, bufLength-(current-start), va_arg(ap, int), 16, 1);
125: 			if (!current) {
126: 				return -1;
127: 			}
128: 			break;
129: 		case 'p':
130: 			current = sprintptr(current, bufLength-(current-start), va_arg(ap, uint64));
131: 			if (!current) {
132: 				return -1;
133: 			}
134: 			break;
135: 		case 's':
136: 			if((source = va_arg(ap, char*)) == 0)
137: 				source = "(null)";
138: 			for(; *source; source++) {
139: 				// consputc(*source);
140: 				*current++ = *source;
141: 				if (current == end) {
142: 					return -1; 
143: 				}
144: 			}
145: 			break;
146: 		case '%':
147: 			// consputc('%');
148: 			*current++ = '%';
149: 			if (current == end) {
150: 				return -1;
151: 			}
152: 			break;
153: 		default:
154: 			// Print unknown % sequence to draw attention.
155: 			// consputc('%');
156: 			*current++ = '%';
157: 			if (current == end) {
158: 				return -1;
159: 			}
160: 			// consputc(c);
161: 			*current++ = c;
162: 			if (current == end) {
163: 				return -1;
164: 			}
165: 			break;
166: 		}
167: 	}
168: 	*current++ = '\0';
169: 	return current - start;
170: }

21: // Acquire the lock.
22: // Loops (spins) until the lock is acquired.
23: void
24: acquire(struct spinlock *lk)
25: {
26: 	push_off(); // disable interrupts to avoid deadlock.
27: 	if(holding(lk))
28: 		panic("acquire");
29: 
30: 	// On RISC-V, sync_lock_test_and_set turns into an atomic swap:
31: 	//   a5 = 1
32: 	//   s1 = &lk->locked
33: 	//   amoswap.w.aq a5, a5, (s1)
34: 	while(__sync_lock_test_and_set(&lk->locked, 1) != 0)
35: 		;
36: 
37: 	// Tell the C compiler and the processor to not move loads or stores
38: 	// past this point, to ensure that the critical section's memory
39: 	// references happen strictly after the lock is acquired.
40: 	// On RISC-V, this emits a fence instruction.
41: 	__sync_synchronize();
42: 
43: 	// Record info about lock acquisition for holding() and debugging.
44: 	lk->cpu = mycpu();
45: }
46: 
47: // Release the lock.
48: void
49: release(struct spinlock *lk)
50: {

xv6-k210@final / 来源候选源码

18: 	lk->cpu = 0;
19: }

21: // Acquire the lock.
22: // Loops (spins) until the lock is acquired.
23: void
24: acquire(struct spinlock *lk)
25: {
26: 	push_off(); // disable interrupts to avoid deadlock.
27: 	// if(holding(lk))
28: 	// 	panic("acquire");

30: 	// On RISC-V, sync_lock_test_and_set turns into an atomic swap:

35: 		;

38: 	// past this point, to ensure that the critical section's memory
39: 	// references happen strictly after the lock is acquired.
40: 	// On RISC-V, this emits a fence instruction.
41: 	__sync_synchronize();
42:

xv6-k210/kernel/sync/spinlock.c::release
匹配分数 0.523 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 0.922, "exact_ast_shape": false, "exact_token_fp": false, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 0.102}

oskernel2023-zmz@recover 源码

43: 	// Record info about lock acquisition for holding() and debugging.
44: 	lk->cpu = mycpu();
45: }
46: 
47: // Release the lock.
48: void
49: release(struct spinlock *lk)
50: {
51: 	if(!holding(lk))
52: 		panic("release");
53: 
54: 	lk->cpu = 0;
55: 
56: 	// Tell the C compiler and the CPU to not move loads or stores
57: 	// past this point, to ensure that all the stores in the critical
58: 	// section are visible to other CPUs before the lock is released,
59: 	// and that loads in the critical section occur strictly before
60: 	// the lock is released.
61: 	// On RISC-V, this emits a fence instruction.
62: 	__sync_synchronize();
63: 
64: 	// Release the lock, equivalent to lk->locked = 0.
65: 	// This code doesn't use a C assignment, since the C standard
66: 	// implies that an assignment might be implemented with
67: 	// multiple store instructions.
68: 	// On RISC-V, sync_lock_release turns into an atomic swap:
69: 	//   s1 = &lk->locked
70: 	//   amoswap.w zero, zero, (s1)
71: 	__sync_lock_release(&lk->locked);
72: 
73: 	pop_off();
74: }
75: 
76: // Check whether this cpu is holding the lock.
77: // Interrupts must be off.
78: int
79: holding(struct spinlock *lk)

53: 
54: 	lk->cpu = 0;
55: 
56: 	// Tell the C compiler and the CPU to not move loads or stores
57: 	// past this point, to ensure that all the stores in the critical
58: 	// section are visible to other CPUs before the lock is released,
59: 	// and that loads in the critical section occur strictly before
60: 	// the lock is released.
61: 	// On RISC-V, this emits a fence instruction.
62: 	__sync_synchronize();
63: 
64: 	// Release the lock, equivalent to lk->locked = 0.
65: 	// This code doesn't use a C assignment, since the C standard
66: 	// implies that an assignment might be implemented with
67: 	// multiple store instructions.
68: 	// On RISC-V, sync_lock_release turns into an atomic swap:
69: 	//   s1 = &lk->locked
70: 	//   amoswap.w zero, zero, (s1)
71: 	__sync_lock_release(&lk->locked);
72: 
73: 	pop_off();
74: }

106: // Fetch the nth word-sized system call argument as a null-terminated string.
107: // Copies into buf, at most max.
108: // Returns string length if OK (including nul), -1 if error.
109: int
110: argstr(int n, char *buf, int max)
111: {
112: 	uint64 addr;
113: 	argaddr(n, &addr);
114: 	int ret = fetchstr(addr, buf, max);
115: 	struct proc *p = myproc();
116: 	if (ret >= 0 && (p->tmask/* & (1 << (p->trapframe->a7 - 1))*/)) {
117: 		printf("=\"%s\"", buf);
118: 	}
119: 	return ret;
120: }

342: 	[SYS_msync]			"msync",
343: 	[SYS_rt_sigtimedwait] "rt_sigtimedwait",
344: 	[SYS_copy_file_range] "copy_file_range",
345: };
346: 
347: void
348: syscall(void)
349: {
350: 	uint64 num;
351: 	struct proc *p = myproc();
352: 
353: 	__debug_info("syscall", "enter syscall\n");
354: 	__debug_assert("syscall", p != NULL, "p == NULL\n");
355: 	num = p->trapframe->a7;
356: 	__debug_info("syscall", "num = %d\n", num);
357: 	if (SYS_rt_sigreturn == num) {
358: 		extern void sigreturn(void);
359: 
360: 		// SYS_rt_sigreturn is very different, as it needs to restore previous 
361: 		// trapframe, we shouldn't save any value to trapframe
362: 		sigreturn();
363: 	}
364: 	else if (num < NELEM(syscalls) && syscalls[num]) {
365: 		// trace
366: 		int trace = p->tmask;// & (1 << (num - 1));
367: 		if (trace) {
368: 			printf("pid %d: %s(", p->pid, sysnames[num]);
369: 		}
370: 		p->trapframe->a0 = syscalls[num]();
371: 		if (trace) {
372: 			printf(") -> %d\n", p->trapframe->a0);
373: 		}
374: 		
375: 	} else {
376: 		// printf(__ERROR("pid %d %s: unknown syscall %d")"\n", p->pid, p->name, num);
377: 		p->trapframe->a0 = -1;
378: 	}
379: }
380:

382: sys_test_proc(void) {
383: 	int n;
384: 	argint(0, &n);

333: syscall(void)
334: {
335: 	uint64 num;
336: 	struct proc *p = myproc();
337: 
338: 	__debug_info("syscall", "enter syscall\n");
339: 	__debug_assert("syscall", p != NULL, "p == NULL\n");
340: 	num = p->trapframe->a7;
341: 	__debug_info("syscall", "num = %d\n", num);
342: 	if (SYS_rt_sigreturn == num) {
343: 		extern void sigreturn(void);
344: 
345: 		// SYS_rt_sigreturn is very different, as it needs to restore previous 
346: 		// trapframe, we shouldn't save any value to trapframe
347: 		sigreturn();
348: 	}
349: 	else if (num < NELEM(syscalls) && syscalls[num]) {
350: 		// trace

353: 			printf("pid %d: %s(", p->pid, sysnames[num]);

357: 			printf(") -> %d\n", p->trapframe->a0);
358: 		}
359: 		
360: 	} else {
361: 		// printf(__ERROR("pid %d %s: unknown syscall %d")"\n", p->pid, p->name, num);
362: 		p->trapframe->a0 = -1;
363: 	}
364: }

34: extern struct inode *proc_INT;
35: 
36: // Fetch the nth word-sized system call argument as a file descriptor
37: // and return both the descriptor and the corresponding struct file.
38: // static 
39: int
40: argfd(int n, int *pfd, struct file **pf)
41: {
42: 	int fd;
43: 	struct file *f;
44: 
45: 	argint(n, &fd);
46: 	if (pfd)
47: 		*pfd = fd;
48: 	if (fd < 0)
49: 		return -1;
50: 	if ((f = fd2file(fd, 0)) == NULL)
51: 		return -1;
52: 	if (pf)
53: 		*pf = f;
54: 	return 0;
55: }
56:

58: // Takes over file reference from caller on success.

60: // fdalloc(struct file *f)

88: uint64
89: sys_dup3(void)
90: {
91: 	struct file *f;
92: 	int old, new, flag;
93: 
94: 	if (argfd(0, &old, &f) < 0 || argint(1, &new) < 0 || argint(2, &flag) < 0)
95: 		return -1;
96: 	if (old == new || new < 0)
97: 		return -1;
98: 
99: 	if (fdalloc3(f, new, flag) < 0)
100: 		return -1;
101: 	
102: 	filedup(f);
103: 	return new;
104: }

90: 
91: 	if (argfd(0, &old, &f) < 0 || argint(1, &new) < 0 || argint(2, &flag) < 0)
92: 		return -1;
93: 	if (old == new || new < 0)
94: 		return -1;
95: 
96: 	if (fdalloc3(f, new, flag) < 0)
97: 		return -1;
98: 	
99: 	filedup(f);
100: 	return new;
101: }

134: 
135: 
136: uint64 sys_copy_file_range(void)
137: {
138: 	struct file *fp_in, *fp_out;
139: 
140: 	uint64 p_offIn,p_offOut;
141: 
142: 	uint32 max_len, flags, real_len;
143: 
144: 	argfd(0,0,&fp_in);
145: 	argint(1, &p_offIn);
146: 	argfd(2,0,&fp_out);
147: 	argint(3,&p_offOut);
148: 	argint(4, &max_len);
149: 	argint(5, &flags);
150: 
151: 	real_len = max_len;
152: 
153: 	int32 off_in, off_out; // 无符号比较容易出错
154: 	if(!p_offIn){
155: 		off_in = fp_in->off;
156: 	}
157: 	else{
158: 		off_in = copyin2(&off_in, p_offIn , sizeof(int)); // AAA1 WWW:注意获取用户地址内容的方法, 以及int是多少位
159: 	}
160: 
161: 	if(!p_offOut){
162: 		off_out = fp_out->off;
163: 	}
164: 	else{
165: 		off_out = copyin2(&off_out, p_offOut , sizeof(int)); // AAA1 WWW:注意获取用户地址内容的方法, 以及int是多少位
166: 	}
167: 
168: 	if(!fp_in || !fp_out){
169: 		return -1;
170: 	}
171: 	
172: 	int tmp;
173: 
174: 	if(real_len  > (tmp = fp_in->ip->size - off_in)){
175: 		real_len = tmp;
176: 	}
177: 
178: 	if(real_len > (tmp = fp_out->ip->size - off_out)){
179: 		real_len = tmp;
180: 	}
181: 
182: 	if(real_len < 0 ) {
183: 		return -1;
184: 	}
185: 
186: 	return copy_file_range(fp_in,off_in,fp_out,off_out,real_len,flags);	
187: 
188: }
189:

215: uint64
216: sys_fstatat(void)
217: {
218: 	int fd;
219: 	struct file *f;
220: 	struct inode *ip, *dp;
221: 	char path[MAXPATH];
222: 	uint64 staddr; // user pointer to struct stat
223: 	int flag;
224: 
225: 	if (argfd(0, &fd, &f) < 0) {
226: 		if (fd != AT_FDCWD)
227: 			return -EBADF;
228: 		dp = myproc()->cwd;
229: 	} else {
230: 		dp = f->ip;
231: 	}
232: 
233: 	if (argstr(1, path, MAXPATH) < 0)
234: 		return -ENAMETOOLONG;
235: 	argaddr(2, &staddr);
236: 	argint(3, &flag);
237: 
238: 	if ((ip = nameifrom(dp, path)) == NULL)
239: 		return -ENOENT;
240: 
241: 	struct kstat st;
242: 	ilock(ip);
243: 	ip->op->getattr(ip, &st);
244: 	iunlockput(ip);
245: 	
246: 	if (copyout2(staddr, (char *)&st, sizeof(st)) < 0)
247: 		return -EFAULT;
248: 
249: 	return 0;
250: }
251:

251: 
252: uint64
253: sys_openat(void)
254: {
255: 	char path[MAXPATH];
256: 	int dirfd, fd, omode, fmode;
257: 	struct file *f = NULL;
258: 	struct inode *dp = NULL, *ip;
259: 
260: 	int check = 0;
261: 
262: 	if (argfd(0, &dirfd, &f) < 0) {
263: 		if (dirfd != AT_FDCWD) {
264: 			return -EBADF;
265: 		}
266: 		dp = myproc()->cwd;
267: 	} else {
268: 		dp = f->ip;
269: 	}
270: 	if (argstr(1, path, MAXPATH) < 0)
271: 		return -ENAMETOOLONG;
272: 	
273: 	argint(2, &omode);
274: 	argint(3, &fmode);
275: 
276: 
277: 
278: 	if(omode & O_CREATE){
279: 		//if() AAA1
280: 		ip = create(dp, path, (fmode & ~S_IFMT) | S_IFREG);
281: 		if(ip == NULL){
282: 			return -1;
283: 		}
284: 	} else {
285: 		if ((ip = nameifrom(dp, path)) == NULL) {
286: 			__debug_warn("sys_openat", "can find %s\n", path);
287: 			return -ENOENT;
288: 		}
289: 		if(ip == proc_INT){
290: 			//AAA1
291: 			check = 1;
292: 			if((omode & (O_WRONLY|O_RDWR)) || (omode & (O_APPEND) || (omode & O_TRUNC)) ){ // AAA1具体是什么权限
293: 				printf("WARN: Could not write on proc/interrupts\n");
294: 				return -1; //AAA1
295: 			}	
296: 		}
297: 		ilock(ip);
298: 		if (S_ISDIR(ip->mode) && (omode & (O_WRONLY|O_RDWR))) {
299: 			iunlockput(ip);
300: 			__debug_warn("sys_openat", "illegel mode 0x%x\n", omode);
301: 			return -EISDIR;
302: 		}
303: 		if ((omode & O_DIRECTORY) && !S_ISDIR(ip->mode)) {
304: 			iunlockput(ip);
305: 			__debug_warn("sys_openat", "o_dir\n");
306: 			return -ENOTDIR;
307: 		}
308: 	}
309: 
310: 	if((f = filealloc()) == NULL || (fd = fdalloc(f, omode & O_CLOEXEC)) < 0){
311: 		if (f) {
312: 			fileclose(f);
313: 		}
314: 		iunlockput(ip);
315: 		return -ENOMEM;
316: 	}
317: 
318: 	__debug_info("sys_openat", "[%s] ip->mode=0x%x\n", path, ip->mode);
319: 
320: 	if (!S_ISDIR(ip->mode) && (omode & O_TRUNC) && (omode & (O_WRONLY|O_RDWR))) {
321: 		ip->op->truncate(ip);
322: 	}
323: 
324: 	if (!S_ISREG(ip->mode) && !S_ISDIR(ip->mode)) {
325: 		f->type = FD_DEVICE;
326: 	} else {
327: 		f->type = FD_INODE;
328: 		f->off = (omode & O_APPEND) ? ip->size : 0;
329: 	}
330: 
331: 	f->ip = ip;
332: 	f->readable = !(omode & O_WRONLY);
333: 	f->writable = (omode & O_WRONLY) || (omode & O_RDWR);
334: 
335: 	iunlock(ip);
336: 
337: 	return fd;
338: }
339:

341: sys_mkdirat(void)
342: {
343: 	char path[MAXPATH];

190: 
191: 	return 0;
192: }

194: uint64
195: sys_openat(void)
196: {
197: 	char path[MAXPATH];
198: 	int dirfd, fd, omode, fmode;
199: 	struct file *f = NULL;
200: 	struct inode *dp = NULL, *ip;
201: 
202: 	if (argfd(0, &dirfd, &f) < 0) {
203: 		if (dirfd != AT_FDCWD) {
204: 			return -EBADF;
205: 		}
206: 		dp = myproc()->cwd;
207: 	} else {
208: 		dp = f->ip;
209: 	}
210: 	if (argstr(1, path, MAXPATH) < 0)
211: 		return -ENAMETOOLONG;
212: 	
213: 	argint(2, &omode);
214: 	argint(3, &fmode);
215: 
216: 	if(omode & O_CREATE){
217: 		ip = create(dp, path, (fmode & ~S_IFMT) | S_IFREG);
218: 		if(ip == NULL){
219: 			return -1;
220: 		}
221: 	} else {
222: 		if ((ip = nameifrom(dp, path)) == NULL) {
223: 			__debug_warn("sys_openat", "can find %s\n", path);
224: 			return -ENOENT;
225: 		}
226: 		ilock(ip);
227: 		if (S_ISDIR(ip->mode) && (omode & (O_WRONLY|O_RDWR))) {
228: 			iunlockput(ip);
229: 			__debug_warn("sys_openat", "illegel mode 0x%x\n", omode);
230: 			return -EISDIR;
231: 		}
232: 		if ((omode & O_DIRECTORY) && !S_ISDIR(ip->mode)) {
233: 			iunlockput(ip);
234: 			__debug_warn("sys_openat", "o_dir\n");
235: 			return -ENOTDIR;
236: 		}
237: 	}
238: 
239: 	if((f = filealloc()) == NULL || (fd = fdalloc(f, omode & O_CLOEXEC)) < 0){
240: 		if (f) {
241: 			fileclose(f);
242: 		}
243: 		iunlockput(ip);
244: 		return -ENOMEM;
245: 	}
246: 
247: 	__debug_info("sys_openat", "[%s] ip->mode=0x%x\n", path, ip->mode);
248: 
249: 	if (!S_ISDIR(ip->mode) && (omode & O_TRUNC) && (omode & (O_WRONLY|O_RDWR))) {
250: 		ip->op->truncate(ip);
251: 	}
252: 
253: 	if (!S_ISREG(ip->mode) && !S_ISDIR(ip->mode)) {
254: 		f->type = FD_DEVICE;
255: 	} else {
256: 		f->type = FD_INODE;
257: 		f->off = (omode & O_APPEND) ? ip->size : 0;
258: 	}
259: 
260: 	f->ip = ip;
261: 	f->readable = !(omode & O_WRONLY);
262: 	f->writable = (omode & O_WRONLY) || (omode & O_RDWR);
263: 
264: 	iunlock(ip);
265: 
266: 	return fd;
267: }
268:

270: sys_mkdirat(void)
271: {
272: 	char path[MAXPATH];

340: uint64
341: sys_mkdirat(void)
342: {
343: 	char path[MAXPATH];
344: 	int dirfd, mode;
345: 	struct file *f = NULL;
346: 	struct inode *dp = NULL, *ip;
347: 
348: 	if (argfd(0, &dirfd, &f) < 0) {
349: 		if (dirfd != AT_FDCWD) {
350: 			return -EBADF;
351: 		}
352: 		dp = myproc()->cwd;
353: 	} else {
354: 		dp = f->ip;
355: 	}
356: 	if (argstr(1, path, MAXPATH) < 0)
357: 		return -ENAMETOOLONG;
358: 	argint(2, &mode);
359: 
360: 	__debug_info("mkdirat", "create dir %s\n", path);
361: 	if ((ip = create(dp, path, (mode & ~S_IFMT) | S_IFDIR)) == NULL) {
362: 		__debug_warn("mkdirat", "create fail\n");
363: 		return -1;
364: 	}
365: 	iunlockput(ip);
366: 	return 0;

269: uint64
270: sys_mkdirat(void)
271: {
272: 	char path[MAXPATH];
273: 	int dirfd, mode;
274: 	struct file *f = NULL;
275: 	struct inode *dp = NULL, *ip;
276: 
277: 	if (argfd(0, &dirfd, &f) < 0) {
278: 		if (dirfd != AT_FDCWD) {
279: 			return -EBADF;
280: 		}
281: 		dp = myproc()->cwd;
282: 	} else {
283: 		dp = f->ip;
284: 	}
285: 	if (argstr(1, path, MAXPATH) < 0)
286: 		return -ENAMETOOLONG;
287: 	argint(2, &mode);
288: 
289: 	__debug_info("mkdirat", "create dir %s\n", path);
290: 	if ((ip = create(dp, path, (mode & ~S_IFMT) | S_IFDIR)) == NULL) {
291: 		__debug_warn("mkdirat", "create fail\n");
292: 		return -1;
293: 	}
294: 	iunlockput(ip);
295: 	return 0;
296: }

370: sys_chdir(void)
371: {
372: 	char path[MAXPATH];
373: 	struct inode *ip;

375:

379: 	if (!S_ISDIR(ip->mode)) {
380: 		iput(ip);
381: 		return -1;
382: 	}
383: 	iput(p->cwd);
384: 	p->cwd = ip;
385: 	return 0;
386: }

388: uint64
389: sys_pipe(void)
390: {
391: 	uint64 fdarray; // user pointer to array of two integers
392: 	struct file *rf, *wf;
393: 	int fd0, fd1;
394: 	int flags;
395: 	// struct proc *p = myproc();
396: 
397: 	if(argaddr(0, &fdarray) < 0)
398: 		return -1;
399: 	if (argint(1, &flags) < 0)
400: 		return -1;
401: 	if(pipealloc(&rf, &wf) < 0)
402: 		return -ENOMEM;
403: 	fd0 = -1;
404: 	if ((fd0 = fdalloc(rf, 0)) < 0 || (fd1 = fdalloc(wf, 0)) < 0) {
405: 		if(fd0 >= 0)
406: 			fd2file(fd0, 1);
407: 		fileclose(rf);
408: 		fileclose(wf);
409: 		return -ENOMEM;
410: 	}
411: 	// if(copyout(p->pagetable, fdarray, (char*)&fd0, sizeof(fd0)) < 0 ||
412: 	//    copyout(p->pagetable, fdarray+sizeof(fd0), (char *)&fd1, sizeof(fd1)) < 0){
413: 	if (copyout2(fdarray, (char*)&fd0, sizeof(fd0)) < 0 ||
414: 		copyout2(fdarray+sizeof(fd0), (char *)&fd1, sizeof(fd1)) < 0)
415: 	{
416: 		fd2file(fd0, 1);
417: 		fd2file(fd1, 1);
418: 		fileclose(rf);
419: 		fileclose(wf);
420: 		return -EFAULT;
421: 	}
422: 	return 0;
423: }

437: // get absolute cwd string
438: uint64
439: sys_getcwd(void)
440: {
441: 	uint64 addr;
442: 	int size;
443: 	if (argaddr(0, &addr) < 0 || argint(1, (int*)&size) < 0)
444: 		return -1;
445: 
446: 	if (size < 2)
447: 		return -1;
448: 
449: 	char buf[MAXPATH];
450: 
451: 	int max = MAXPATH < size ? MAXPATH : size;
452: 	if ((size = namepath(myproc()->cwd, buf, max)) < 0)
453: 		return -1;
454: 
455: 	if (copyout2(addr, buf, size) < 0)
456: 		return -1;
457: 
458: 	return size;
459: }

461: uint64
462: sys_unlinkat(void)
463: {
464: 	char path[MAXPATH];
465: 	int dirfd, mode;
466: 	struct file *f = NULL;
467: 	struct inode *dp = NULL, *ip;
468: 
469: 	if (argfd(0, &dirfd, &f) < 0) {
470: 		if (dirfd != AT_FDCWD) {
471: 			return -EBADF;
472: 		}
473: 		dp = myproc()->cwd;
474: 	} else {
475: 		dp = f->ip;
476: 		if (!S_ISDIR(dp->mode)) {
477: 			return -ENOTDIR;
478: 		}
479: 	}
480: 
481: 	int len;
482: 	if((len = argstr(1, path, MAXPATH)) <= 0 || argint(2, &mode) < 0)
483: 		return -ENAMETOOLONG;
484: 
485: 	char *s = path + len - 1;
486: 	while (s >= path && *s == '/') {
487: 		s--;
488: 	}
489: 	if (s >= path && *s == '.' && (s == path || *--s == '/')) {
490: 		__debug_warn("sys_unlinkat", "illegel path %s\n", path);
491: 		return -EACCES;
492: 	}
493: 	
494: 	if ((ip = nameifrom(dp, path)) == NULL) {
495: 		__debug_warn("sys_unlinkat", "can namei %s\n", path);
496: 		return -ENOENT;
497: 	}
498: 
499: 	if(ip == proc_INT){
500: 		//A1
501: 		return -1;
502: 	}
503: 
504: 	int isdir = S_ISDIR(ip->mode);
505: 	if (isdir && mode != AT_REMOVEDIR) {
506: 		iput(ip);
507: 		__debug_warn("sys_unlinkat", "illegel mode 0x%x against 0x%x\n", mode, ip->mode);
508: 		return -EISDIR;
509: 	} else if (!isdir && mode == AT_REMOVEDIR) {
510: 		iput(ip);
511: 		return -ENOTDIR;
512: 	}
513: 	ilock(ip);
514: 	if (isdir && isdirempty(ip) != 1) {
515: 		iunlockput(ip);
516: 		__debug_warn("sys_unlinkat", "dir isn't empty\n");
517: 		return -ENOTEMPTY;
518: 	}
519: 	int ret = unlink(ip);
520: 	iunlockput(ip);
521: 
522: 	return ret;
523: }
524: 
525: // Must hold too many locks at a time! It's possible to raise a deadlock.
526: // Because this op takes some steps, we can't promise

525: // Must hold too many locks at a time! It's possible to raise a deadlock.

527: uint64
528: sys_renameat(void)
529: {
530: 	int fd1, fd2, flags;
531: 	struct file *f1, *f2;
532: 	char name1[MAXPATH], name2[MAXPATH];
533: 	struct inode *ip1, *ip2;
534: 	struct proc *p = myproc();
535: 
536: 	if (argfd(0, &fd1, &f1) < 0) {
537: 		if (fd1 != AT_FDCWD) return -EBADF;
538: 		ip1 = p->cwd;
539: 	} else
540: 		ip1 = f1->ip;
541: 	if (argfd(2, &fd2, &f2) < 0) {
542: 		if (fd2 != AT_FDCWD) return -EBADF;
543: 		ip2 = p->cwd;
544: 	} else
545: 		ip2 = f2->ip;
546: 
547: 	if (!S_ISDIR(ip1->mode) || !S_ISDIR(ip2->mode))
548: 		return -ENOTDIR;
549: 
550: 	if (argstr(1, name1, MAXPATH) < 0 || argstr(3, name2, MAXPATH) < 0 || argint(4, &flags))
551: 		return -ENAMETOOLONG;
552: 
553: 	int ret = 0;
554: 	if ((ip1 = nameifrom(ip1, name1)) == NULL ||
555: 		(ip2 = nameiparentfrom(ip2, name2, name1)) == NULL) // name1 stores the new name
556: 	{
557: 		if (ip1)
558: 			iput(ip1);
559: 		return -ENOENT;
560: 	}
561: 	
562: 	if(ip1 == proc_INT){
563: 		return -1;
564: 	}
565: 
566: 	ret = do_rename(ip1, ip2, name1);
567: 
568: 	iput(ip1);
569: 	iput(ip2);
570: 	return ret;
571: }

573: uint64
574: sys_mount(void)
575: {
576: 	char buf[MAXPATH];
577: 	struct inode *dev = NULL, *imnt = NULL;
578: 	uint64 flag;
579: 
580: 	if (argstr(0, buf, MAXPATH) < 0 || (dev = namei(buf)) == NULL) {
581: 		return -1;
582: 	}
583: 	if (argstr(1, buf, MAXPATH) < 0 || (imnt = namei(buf)) == NULL) {
584: 		goto fail;
585: 	}
586: 	// Type of fs to mount and flag.
587: 	if (argstr(2, buf, MAXPATH) < 0 || argint(3, (int*)&flag) < 0) {
588: 		goto fail;
589: 	}
590: 
591: 	// We don't plan to support this
592: 	// char data[512];
593: 	// if (argstr(4, data, sizeof(data)) < 0) {
594: 	//   goto fail;
595: 	// }
596: 
597: 	ilock(dev);
598: 	ilock(imnt);
599: 	if (do_mount(dev, imnt, buf, flag, NULL) < 0) {
600: 		iunlock(imnt);
601: 		iunlock(dev);
602: 		goto fail;
603: 	}
604: 	iunlockput(imnt);
605: 	iunlockput(dev);
606: 
607: 	return 0;
608: 
609: fail:
610: 	if (dev) {
611: 		iput(dev);
612: 	}
613: 	if (imnt) {
614: 		iput(imnt);
615: 	}
616: 
617: 	return -1;
618: }

492: uint64
493: sys_mount(void)
494: {
495: 	char buf[MAXPATH];
496: 	struct inode *dev = NULL, *imnt = NULL;
497: 	uint64 flag;
498: 
499: 	if (argstr(0, buf, MAXPATH) < 0 || (dev = namei(buf)) == NULL) {
500: 		return -1;
501: 	}
502: 	if (argstr(1, buf, MAXPATH) < 0 || (imnt = namei(buf)) == NULL) {
503: 		goto fail;
504: 	}
505: 	// Type of fs to mount and flag.
506: 	if (argstr(2, buf, MAXPATH) < 0 || argint(3, (int*)&flag) < 0) {
507: 		goto fail;
508: 	}
509: 
510: 	// We don't plan to support this
511: 	// char data[512];
512: 	// if (argstr(4, data, sizeof(data)) < 0) {
513: 	//   goto fail;
514: 	// }
515: 
516: 	ilock(dev);
517: 	ilock(imnt);
518: 	if (do_mount(dev, imnt, buf, flag, NULL) < 0) {
519: 		iunlock(imnt);
520: 		iunlock(dev);
521: 		goto fail;
522: 	}
523: 	iunlockput(imnt);
524: 	iunlockput(dev);
525: 
526: 	return 0;
527: 
528: fail:
529: 	if (dev) {
530: 		iput(dev);
531: 	}
532: 	if (imnt) {
533: 		iput(imnt);
534: 	}
535: 
536: 	return -1;
537: }
538:

620: uint64
621: sys_umount(void)
622: {
623: 	char buf[MAXPATH];
624: 	struct inode *mnt = NULL;
625: 	uint64 flag;
626: 
627: 	if (argstr(0, buf, MAXPATH) < 0 || (mnt = namei(buf)) == NULL) {
628: 		return -1;
629: 	}
630: 	if (argint(1, (int*)&flag) < 0 ||
631: 			mnt->ref > 1) // Is there anyone else holding this inode?
632: 	{                 // If a syscall try to umount the same mntpoint, he won't pass this.
633: 		iput(mnt);
634: 		return -1;
635: 	}
636: 
637: 	ilock(mnt);
638: 	if (do_umount(mnt, flag) < 0) {
639: 		iunlockput(mnt);
640: 		return -1;
641: 	}
642: 
643: 	// If umount successfully, mnt is no longer available,
644: 	// we shouldn't unlock it, or put it.
645: 
646: 	return 0;
647: }
648:

650: sys_readv(void)
651: {

649: uint64
650: sys_readv(void)
651: {
652: 	int fd, count;
653: 	uint64 iovp;
654: 	struct file *f = NULL;
655: 	if (argfd(0, &fd, &f) < 0 || argaddr(1, &iovp) < 0 || argint(2, &count) < 0)
656: 		return -EBADF;
657: 
658: 	struct iovec *ioarr = kmalloc(count * sizeof(struct iovec));
659: 	if (ioarr == NULL)
660: 		return -ENOMEM;
661: 
662: 	if (copyin2((char *)ioarr, iovp, count * sizeof(struct iovec)) < 0) {
663: 		kfree(ioarr);
664: 		return -EFAULT;
665: 	}
666: 
667: 	int ret = filereadv(f, ioarr, count);
668: 	
669: 	kfree(ioarr);
670: 	return ret;
671: }
672:

697: uint64
698: sys_readlinkat(void)
699: {
700: 	int dirfd;
701: 	uint64 ubuf, size;
702: 	char path[MAXPATH];
703: 	struct file *f;
704: 	struct inode *dp, *ip;
705: 
706: 	if (argfd(0, &dirfd, &f) < 0) {
707: 		if (dirfd != AT_FDCWD) {
708: 			return -EBADF;
709: 		}
710: 		dp = myproc()->cwd;
711: 	} else {
712: 		dp = f->ip;
713: 	}
714: 
715: 	if (argstr(1, path, MAXPATH) < 0 || argaddr(2, &ubuf) < 0 || argaddr(3, &size) < 0)
716: 		return -ENAMETOOLONG;
717: 
718: 	__debug_info("sys_readlinkat", "path=%s\n", path);
719: 
720: 	ip = nameifrom(dp, path);
721: 	if (ip == NULL)
722: 		return -ENOENT;
723: 
724: 	int len;
725: 	len = namepath(ip, path, MAXPATH);
726: 	iput(ip);
727: 
728: 	if (len > size)
729: 		len = size;
730: 	if (copyout2(ubuf, path, len) < 0)
731: 		return -EFAULT;
732: 
733: 	return len;
734: }
735:

737: sys_fcntl(void)
738: {
739: 	int fd, cmd;

735: 
736: uint64
737: sys_fcntl(void)
738: {
739: 	int fd, cmd;
740: 	struct file *f;
741: 	uint64 arg;
742: 	if (argfd(0, &fd, &f) < 0 || argint(1, &cmd) < 0 || argaddr(2, &arg) < 0)
743: 		return -1;
744: 
745: 	if (cmd == F_DUPFD || cmd == F_DUPFD_CLOEXEC) {
746: 		int minfd = (int) arg;
747: 		if (minfd < 0)
748: 			return -1;
749: 		minfd = fcntldup(f, minfd, cmd == F_DUPFD_CLOEXEC);
750: 		if (minfd >= 0)
751: 			filedup(f);
752: 		return minfd;
753: 	}
754: 
755: 	__debug_warn("sys_fcntl", "unsupported cmd %d\n", cmd);
756: 
757: 	return -1;
758: }
759:

761: sys_pselect(void)

760: uint64
761: sys_pselect(void)
762: {
763: 	int nfds;
764: 	uint64 urfds, uwfds, uexfds;
765: 	uint64 utoaddr, usmaddr;
766: 	
767: 	argint(0, &nfds);
768: 	argaddr(1, &urfds);
769: 	argaddr(2, &uwfds);
770: 	argaddr(3, &uexfds);
771: 	argaddr(4, &utoaddr);
772: 	argaddr(5, &usmaddr);
773: 	
774: 	if (nfds <= 0 || nfds > FDSET_SIZE)
775: 		return -EINVAL;
776: 	if (!(urfds || uwfds || uexfds))
777: 		return -EINVAL;
778: 
779: 	struct fdset rfds, wfds, exfds;
780: 	struct timespec timeout;
781: 	__sigset_t sigmask;
782: 
783: 	if (urfds && copyin2((char *)&rfds, urfds, sizeof(struct fdset)) < 0)
784: 		return -EFAULT;
785: 	if (uwfds && copyin2((char *)&wfds, uwfds, sizeof(struct fdset)) < 0)
786: 		return -EFAULT;
787: 	if (uexfds && copyin2((char *)&exfds, uexfds, sizeof(struct fdset)) < 0)
788: 		return -EFAULT;
789: 	if (utoaddr && copyin2((char *)&timeout, utoaddr, sizeof(timeout)) < 0)
790: 		return -EFAULT;
791: 	if (usmaddr && copyin2((char *)&sigmask, usmaddr, sizeof(sigmask)) < 0)
792: 		return -EFAULT;
793: 
794: 	struct proc *p = myproc();
795: 	if (p->tmask) {
796: 		printf(") ...\n");
797: 	}
798: 
799: 	int ret = pselect(nfds,
800: 				urfds ? &rfds: NULL,
801: 				uwfds ? &wfds: NULL,
802: 				uexfds ? &exfds: NULL,
803: 				utoaddr ? &timeout : NULL,
804: 				usmaddr ? &sigmask : NULL
805: 			);
806: 
807: 	if (urfds)
808: 		copyout2(urfds, (char *)&rfds, sizeof(struct fdset));
809: 	if (uwfds)
810: 		copyout2(uwfds, (char *)&wfds, sizeof(struct fdset));
811: 	if (uexfds)
812: 		copyout2(uexfds, (char *)&exfds, sizeof(struct fdset));
813: 
814: 	if (p->tmask) {
815: 		printf("pid %d: return from pselect(", p->pid);
816: 	}
817: 	return ret;
818: }
819:

821: sys_ppoll(void)
822: {
823: 	uint64 addr;

680: sys_pselect(void)
681: {
682: 	int nfds;
683: 	uint64 urfds, uwfds, uexfds;
684: 	uint64 utoaddr, usmaddr;
685: 	
686: 	argint(0, &nfds);
687: 	argaddr(1, &urfds);
688: 	argaddr(2, &uwfds);
689: 	argaddr(3, &uexfds);
690: 	argaddr(4, &utoaddr);
691: 	argaddr(5, &usmaddr);
692: 	
693: 	if (nfds <= 0 || nfds > FDSET_SIZE)
694: 		return -EINVAL;
695: 	if (!(urfds || uwfds || uexfds))
696: 		return -EINVAL;
697: 
698: 	struct fdset rfds, wfds, exfds;
699: 	struct timespec timeout;
700: 	__sigset_t sigmask;
701: 
702: 	if (urfds && copyin2((char *)&rfds, urfds, sizeof(struct fdset)) < 0)
703: 		return -EFAULT;
704: 	if (uwfds && copyin2((char *)&wfds, uwfds, sizeof(struct fdset)) < 0)
705: 		return -EFAULT;
706: 	if (uexfds && copyin2((char *)&exfds, uexfds, sizeof(struct fdset)) < 0)
707: 		return -EFAULT;
708: 	if (utoaddr && copyin2((char *)&timeout, utoaddr, sizeof(timeout)) < 0)
709: 		return -EFAULT;
710: 	if (usmaddr && copyin2((char *)&sigmask, usmaddr, sizeof(sigmask)) < 0)
711: 		return -EFAULT;
712: 
713: 	struct proc *p = myproc();
714: 	if (p->tmask) {
715: 		printf(") ...\n");
716: 	}
717: 
718: 	int ret = pselect(nfds,
719: 				urfds ? &rfds: NULL,
720: 				uwfds ? &wfds: NULL,
721: 				uexfds ? &exfds: NULL,
722: 				utoaddr ? &timeout : NULL,
723: 				usmaddr ? &sigmask : NULL
724: 			);
725: 
726: 	if (urfds)
727: 		copyout2(urfds, (char *)&rfds, sizeof(struct fdset));
728: 	if (uwfds)
729: 		copyout2(uwfds, (char *)&wfds, sizeof(struct fdset));
730: 	if (uexfds)
731: 		copyout2(uexfds, (char *)&exfds, sizeof(struct fdset));
732: 
733: 	if (p->tmask) {
734: 		printf("pid %d: return from pselect(", p->pid);
735: 	}
736: 	return ret;
737: }

820: uint64
821: sys_ppoll(void)
822: {
823: 	uint64 addr;
824: 	uint32 nfds;
825: 	uint64 timeoutaddr;
826: 	uint64 sigmaskaddr;
827: 	if (argaddr(0, &addr) < 0 || argint(1, (int*)&nfds) < 0 || argaddr(2, &timeoutaddr) < 0 || argaddr(3, &sigmaskaddr) < 0)
828: 		return -1;
829: 
830: 	// if (timeoutaddr || sigmaskaddr) {
831: 	// 	__debug_error("sys_ppoll", "not supported yet!\n");
832: 	// 	return -1;
833: 	// }
834: 	if (nfds == 0)
835: 		return -EINVAL;
836: 
837: 	struct pollfd *pfds = kmalloc(nfds * sizeof(struct pollfd));
838: 	if (pfds == NULL)
839: 		return -ENOMEM;
840: 
841: 	for (int i = 0; i < nfds; i++) {
842: 		if (copyin2((char *)(pfds + i), addr + i * sizeof(struct pollfd), sizeof(struct pollfd)) < 0) {
843: 			kfree(pfds);
844: 			return -EFAULT;
845: 		}
846: 	}
847: 	
848: 	struct timespec timeout;
849: 	__sigset_t sigmask;
850: 	if (timeoutaddr && copyin2((char *)&timeout, timeoutaddr, sizeof(timeout)) < 0)
851: 		return -EFAULT;
852: 	if (sigmaskaddr && copyin2((char *)&sigmask, sigmaskaddr, sizeof(sigmask)) < 0)
853: 		return -EFAULT;
854: 
855: 	int ret = ppoll(pfds, nfds,
856: 					timeoutaddr ? &timeout : NULL,
857: 					sigmaskaddr ? &sigmask : NULL);
858: 
859: 	copyout2(addr, (char *)pfds, sizeof(struct pollfd) * nfds);
860: 	kfree(pfds);
861: 	return ret;
862: }
863:

865: #define W_OK	2		/* Test for write permission.  */
866: #define X_OK	1		/* Test for execute permission.  */
867: #define F_OK	0		/* Test for existence.  */

779: 	kfree(pfds);
780: 	return ret;

864: #define R_OK	4		/* Test for read permission.  */
865: #define W_OK	2		/* Test for write permission.  */
866: #define X_OK	1		/* Test for execute permission.  */
867: #define F_OK	0		/* Test for existence.  */              
868: 
869: uint64
870: sys_faccessat(void)
871: {
872: 	char path[MAXPATH];
873: 	int dirfd, mode, flags;
874: 	struct file *f = NULL;
875: 	struct inode *dp, *ip;
876: 
877: 	if (argfd(0, &dirfd, &f) < 0) {
878: 		if (dirfd != AT_FDCWD) {
879: 			return -1;
880: 		}
881: 		dp = myproc()->cwd;
882: 	} else {
883: 		dp = f->ip;
884: 	}
885: 	if (argstr(1, path, MAXPATH) < 0 || argint(2, &mode) < 0 || argint(3, &flags) < 0)
886: 		return -1;
887: 
888: 	ip = nameifrom(dp, path);
889: 	if (ip != NULL) {
890: 		if (mode == F_OK) {
891: 			iput(ip);
892: 			return 0;
893: 		}
894: 	} else return -1;
895: 
896: 	// assume user as root
897: 	int imode = (ip->mode >> 6) & 0x7;
898: 	iput(ip);
899: 
900: 	if ((imode & mode) != mode)
901: 		return -1;
902: 
903: 	return 0;
904: }
905: 
906: #define SEEK_SET	0	/* Seek from beginning of file.  */                                                                                      
907: #define SEEK_CUR	1	/* Seek from current position.  */
908: #define SEEK_END	2	/* Seek from end of file.  */
909:

793: 	struct file *f = NULL;
794: 	struct inode *dp, *ip;
795: 
796: 	if (argfd(0, &dirfd, &f) < 0) {
797: 		if (dirfd != AT_FDCWD) {
798: 			return -1;
799: 		}

匹配分数 1.000 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": true, "exact_token_fp": true, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 1.0}

oskernel2023-zmz@recover 源码

905: 
906: #define SEEK_SET	0	/* Seek from beginning of file.  */                                                                                      
907: #define SEEK_CUR	1	/* Seek from current position.  */
908: #define SEEK_END	2	/* Seek from end of file.  */
909: 
910: uint64
911: sys_lseek(void)
912: {
913: 	int fd;
914: 	struct file *f;
915: 	int64 off;
916: 	int whence;
917: 
918: 	if (argfd(0, &fd, &f) < 0)
919: 		return -EBADF;
920: 	argaddr(1, (uint64 *)&off);
921: 	argint(2, &whence);
922: 
923: 	if (f->type != FD_INODE)
924: 		return -ESPIPE;
925: 
926: 	uint64 cur = f->off;	
927: 	uint64 size = f->ip->size;
928: 	switch (whence)
929: 	{
930: 		case SEEK_SET:
931: 			cur = off;
932: 			break;
933: 		case SEEK_CUR:
934: 			cur += off;
935: 			break;
936: 		case SEEK_END:
937: 			cur = size + off;
938: 			break;
939: 		default:
940: 			return -EINVAL;
941: 	}
942: 
943: 	// Doesn't support offset out of size
944: 	if (cur > 0xffffffff)
945: 		return -EFBIG;
946: 
947: 	acquire(&f->lock);
948: 	f->off = cur;
949: 	release(&f->lock);
950: 
951: 	return cur;
952: }
953:

955: sys_utimensat(void)
956: {
957: 	char path[MAXPATH];

828: 
829: uint64
830: sys_lseek(void)
831: {
832: 	int fd;
833: 	struct file *f;
834: 	int64 off;
835: 	int whence;
836: 
837: 	if (argfd(0, &fd, &f) < 0)
838: 		return -EBADF;
839: 	argaddr(1, (uint64 *)&off);
840: 	argint(2, &whence);
841: 
842: 	if (f->type != FD_INODE)
843: 		return -ESPIPE;
844: 
845: 	uint64 cur = f->off;	
846: 	uint64 size = f->ip->size;
847: 	switch (whence)
848: 	{
849: 		case SEEK_SET:
850: 			cur = off;
851: 			break;
852: 		case SEEK_CUR:
853: 			cur += off;
854: 			break;
855: 		case SEEK_END:
856: 			cur = size + off;
857: 			break;
858: 		default:
859: 			return -EINVAL;
860: 	}
861: 
862: 	// Doesn't support offset out of size
863: 	if (cur > 0xffffffff)
864: 		return -EFBIG;
865: 
866: 	acquire(&f->lock);
867: 	f->off = cur;
868: 	release(&f->lock);
869: 
870: 	return cur;
871: }

954: uint64
955: sys_utimensat(void)
956: {
957: 	char path[MAXPATH];
958: 	int dirfd, flags;
959: 	uint64 addr;
960: 	struct file *f = NULL;
961: 	struct inode *ip;
962: 	struct timespec times[2];
963: 
964: 	if (argfd(0, &dirfd, &f) < 0) {
965: 		if (dirfd != AT_FDCWD) {
966: 			return -EBADF;
967: 		}
968: 		ip = myproc()->cwd;
969: 	} else {
970: 		ip = f->ip;
971: 	}
972: 	if (argstr(1, path, MAXPATH) < 0 || argaddr(2, &addr) < 0 || argint(3, &flags) < 0)
973: 		return -ENAMETOOLONG;
974: 
975: 	if (addr != NULL && copyin2((char *)times, addr, sizeof(times)) < 0)
976: 		return -EFAULT;
977: 	else if (addr == NULL) {
978: 		// memset(times, 0, sizeof(times));
979: 	
980: 	}
981: 
982: 	ip = nameifrom(ip, path);
983: 	if (ip == NULL)
984: 		return -ENOENT;
985: 
986: 	int ret = 0;
987: 	struct kstat st;
988: 	memset(&st, 0, sizeof(st));
989: 
990: 	ilock(ip);
991: 	ip->op->getattr(ip, &st);
992: 	st.atime_sec = times[0].sec;
993: 	st.atime_nsec = times[0].nsec;
994: 	st.mtime_sec = times[1].sec;
995: 	st.mtime_nsec = times[1].nsec;
996: 	if (!ip->op->setattr || ip->op->setattr(ip, &st) < 0) {
997: 		ret = -EACCES;
998: 	}
999: 	iunlockput(ip);
1000: 
1001: 	return ret;
1002: }

874: sys_utimensat(void)
875: {
876: 	char path[MAXPATH];

878: 	uint64 addr;
879: 	struct file *f = NULL;
880: 	struct inode *ip;
881: 	struct timespec times[2];
882:

884: 		if (dirfd != AT_FDCWD) {
885: 			return -EBADF;
886: 		}
887: 		ip = myproc()->cwd;
888: 	} else {
889: 		ip = f->ip;
890: 	}
891: 	if (argstr(1, path, MAXPATH) < 0 || argaddr(2, &addr) < 0 || argint(3, &flags) < 0)
892: 		return -ENAMETOOLONG;

894: 	if (addr != NULL && copyin2((char *)times, addr, sizeof(times)) < 0)
895: 		return -EFAULT;
896: 	else if (addr == NULL) {
897: 		// memset(times, 0, sizeof(times));

899: 	}

902: 	if (ip == NULL)

916: 		ret = -EACCES;
917: 	}
918: 	iunlockput(ip);
919: 
920: 	return ret;
921: }

1004: // Temporarily
1005: uint64
1006: sys_ioctl(void)
1007: {
1008: 	#define TIOCGWINSZ	0x5413
1009: 	#define TCGETS		0x5401
1010: 
1011: 	struct winsize {
1012: 		uint16 ws_row;		/* rows， in character */
1013: 		uint16 ws_col; 		/* columns, in characters */
1014: 		uint16 ws_xpixel;	/* horizontal size, pixels (unused) */
1015: 		uint16 ws_ypixel;	/* vertical size, pixels (unused) */
1016: 	};
1017: 
1018: 	#define ICRNL		0000400
1019: 	#define OPOST		0000001
1020: 	#define ONLCR		0000004
1021: 	#define ICANON		0000002
1022: 	#define ECHO		0000010
1023: 
1024: 
1025: 	struct termios {
1026: 		uint16 c_iflag; /* 输入模式标志*/
1027: 		uint16 c_oflag; /* 输出模式标志*/
1028: 		uint16 c_cflag; /* 控制模式标志*/
1029: 		uint16 c_lflag; /*区域模式标志或本地模式标志或局部模式*/
1030: 		uint8 c_line; /*行控制line discipline */
1031: 		uint8 c_cc[8]; /* 控制字符特性*/
1032: 	};
1033: 
1034: 	int fd;
1035: 	struct file *f;
1036: 	uint64 request;
1037: 	uint64 argp;
1038: 
1039: 	if (argfd(0, &fd, &f) < 0 || argaddr(1, &request) < 0 || argaddr(2, &argp) < 0)
1040: 		return -EBADF;
1041: 	
1042: 	if (f->type != FD_DEVICE)
1043: 		return -EPERM;
1044: 
1045: 	switch (request) {
1046: 	case TIOCGWINSZ: {
1047: 		struct winsize win = {
1048: 			.ws_row = 24,
1049: 			.ws_col = 80,
1050: 		};
1051: 		if (copyout2(argp, (char*)&win, sizeof(win)) < 0)
1052: 			return -EFAULT;
1053: 		break;
1054: 	}
1055: 	case TCGETS: {
1056: 		struct termios terminfo = {
1057: 			.c_iflag = ICRNL,
1058: 			.c_oflag = OPOST|ONLCR,
1059: 			.c_cflag = 0,
1060: 			.c_lflag = ICANON|ECHO,
1061: 			.c_line = 0,
1062: 			.c_cc = {0},
1063: 		};
1064: 		if (copyout2(argp, (char*)&terminfo, sizeof(terminfo)) < 0)
1065: 			return -EFAULT;
1066: 		break;
1067: 	}
1068: 	default:
1069: 		return -EPERM;
1070: 	}
1071: 
1072: 	return 0;
1073: }
1074:

1077: {
1078: 	char path[MAXPATH];

921: }

924: uint64
925: sys_ioctl(void)
926: {
927: 	#define TIOCGWINSZ	0x5413
928: 	#define TCGETS		0x5401
929: 
930: 	struct winsize {
931: 		uint16 ws_row;		/* rows， in character */
932: 		uint16 ws_col; 		/* columns, in characters */
933: 		uint16 ws_xpixel;	/* horizontal size, pixels (unused) */
934: 		uint16 ws_ypixel;	/* vertical size, pixels (unused) */
935: 	};
936: 
937: 	#define ICRNL		0000400
938: 	#define OPOST		0000001
939: 	#define ONLCR		0000004
940: 	#define ICANON		0000002
941: 	#define ECHO		0000010
942: 
943: 
944: 	struct termios {
945: 		uint16 c_iflag; /* 输入模式标志*/
946: 		uint16 c_oflag; /* 输出模式标志*/
947: 		uint16 c_cflag; /* 控制模式标志*/
948: 		uint16 c_lflag; /*区域模式标志或本地模式标志或局部模式*/
949: 		uint8 c_line; /*行控制line discipline */
950: 		uint8 c_cc[8]; /* 控制字符特性*/
951: 	};
952: 
953: 	int fd;
954: 	struct file *f;
955: 	uint64 request;
956: 	uint64 argp;
957: 
958: 	if (argfd(0, &fd, &f) < 0 || argaddr(1, &request) < 0 || argaddr(2, &argp) < 0)
959: 		return -EBADF;
960: 	
961: 	if (f->type != FD_DEVICE)
962: 		return -EPERM;
963: 
964: 	switch (request) {
965: 	case TIOCGWINSZ: {
966: 		struct winsize win = {
967: 			.ws_row = 24,
968: 			.ws_col = 80,
969: 		};
970: 		if (copyout2(argp, (char*)&win, sizeof(win)) < 0)
971: 			return -EFAULT;
972: 		break;
973: 	}
974: 	case TCGETS: {
975: 		struct termios terminfo = {
976: 			.c_iflag = ICRNL,
977: 			.c_oflag = OPOST|ONLCR,
978: 			.c_cflag = 0,
979: 			.c_lflag = ICANON|ECHO,
980: 			.c_line = 0,
981: 			.c_cc = {0},
982: 		};
983: 		if (copyout2(argp, (char*)&terminfo, sizeof(terminfo)) < 0)
984: 			return -EFAULT;
985: 		break;
986: 	}
987: 	default:
988: 		return -EPERM;
989: 	}
990: 
991: 	return 0;
992: }

1075: uint64
1076: sys_statfs(void)
1077: {
1078: 	char path[MAXPATH];
1079: 	uint64 addr;
1080: 	if (argstr(0, path, MAXPATH) < 0 || argaddr(1, &addr) < 0)
1081: 		return -ENAMETOOLONG;
1082: 
1083: 	struct inode *ip = namei(path);
1084: 	if (ip == NULL)
1085: 		return -ENOENT;
1086: 	
1087: 	struct superblock *sb = ip->sb;
1088: 	iput(ip);
1089: 	
1090: 	struct statfs stat;
1091: 	if (!sb->op.statfs)
1092: 		return -EPERM;
1093: 	if (sb->op.statfs(sb, &stat) < 0)
1094: 		return -EIO;
1095: 	if (copyout2(addr, (char *)&stat, sizeof(stat)) < 0)
1096: 		return -EFAULT;
1097: 	
1098: 	return 0;
1099: }
1100: 
1101:

21: sys_sbrk(void)
22: {
23: 	int n;
24: 	if(argint(0, &n) < 0)
25: 		return -1;
26: 	
27: 	struct proc *p = myproc();
28: 	uint64 addr = p->pbrk;
29: 
30: 	if (growproc(addr + n) < 0)
31: 		return -1;
32: 
33: 	return addr;

54: uint64
55: sys_mprotect(void)
56: {
57: 	uint64 addr, len;
58: 	int prot;
59: 
60: 	argaddr(0, &addr);
61: 	argaddr(1, &len);
62: 	argint(2, &prot);
63: 	if (addr % PGSIZE || (prot & ~PROT_ALL))
64: 		return -EINVAL;
65: 
66: 	prot = (prot << 1) & (PTE_X|PTE_W|PTE_R); // convert to PTE_attr
67: 	struct proc *p = myproc();
68: 	struct seg *s = partofseg(p->segment, addr, addr + len);
69: 	if (s == NULL ||
70: 		(s->type == MMAP && !(s->flag & PTE_W) && (prot & PTE_W))
71: 	)
72: 	return -EINVAL;
73: 
74: 	return uvmprotect(p->pagetable, addr, len, prot);
75: }
76:

匹配分数 0.754 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": true, "exact_token_fp": false, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 0.18}

74: 	return uvmprotect(p->pagetable, addr, len, prot);

77: extern int argfd(int n, int *pfd, struct file **pf);
78: 
79: uint64
80: sys_mmap(void)
81: {
82: 	uint64 start, len;
83: 	int prot, flags, fd;
84: 	int64 off;
85: 	struct file *f = NULL;
86: 
87: 	argaddr(0, &start);
88: 	argaddr(1, &len);
89: 	argint(2, &prot);
90: 	argint(3, &flags);
91: 	argfd(4, &fd, &f);
92: 	argaddr(5, (uint64*)&off);
93: 	
94: 	if (off % PGSIZE || len == 0)
95: 		return -EINVAL;
96: 	
97: 	if ((fd < 0 || f == NULL) && !(flags & MAP_ANONYMOUS)) {
98: 		return -EBADF;
99: 	} else if (flags & MAP_ANONYMOUS) {
100: 		if (off != 0)
101: 			return -EINVAL;
102: 		f = NULL;
103: 	} else if (f->type != FD_INODE) {
104: 		return -EPERM;
105: 	}
106: 
107: 	// Must provide one of them.
108: 	if (!(flags & (MAP_SHARED|MAP_PRIVATE))) {
109: 		return -EINVAL;
110: 	}
111: 
112: 	return do_mmap(start, len, prot, flags, f, off);
113: }
114:

117: {
118: 	uint64 start, len;

81: {
82: 	uint64 start, len;
83: 	int prot, flags, fd;
84: 	int64 off;
85: 	struct file *f = NULL;
86: 
87: 	argaddr(0, &start);
88: 	argaddr(1, &len);
89: 	argint(2, &prot);
90: 	argint(3, &flags);
91: 	argfd(4, &fd, &f);
92: 	argaddr(5, (uint64*)&off);
93: 	
94: 	if (off % PGSIZE || len == 0)
95: 		return -EINVAL;
96: 	
97: 	if ((fd < 0 || f == NULL) && !(flags & MAP_ANONYMOUS)) {
98: 		return -EBADF;
99: 	} else if (flags & MAP_ANONYMOUS) {
100: 		if (off != 0)
101: 			return -EINVAL;
102: 		f = NULL;
103: 	} else if (f->type != FD_INODE) {
104: 		return -EPERM;
105: 	}
106: 
107: 	// Must provide one of them.
108: 	if (!(flags & (MAP_SHARED|MAP_PRIVATE))) {
109: 		return -EINVAL;
110: 	}
111: 
112: 	return do_mmap(start, len, prot, flags, f, off);
113: }

40: sys_execve(void)
41: {
42: 	char path[MAXPATH];

101: uint64
102: sys_wait(void)
103: {
104: 	uint64 p;
105: 	if(argaddr(0, &p) < 0)
106: 		return -1;
107: 	// since wait suspends the proc, we print the left trace-info here
108: 	// and when coming back, we re-print the leading trace-info for a clear view
109: 	struct proc *pr = myproc();
110: 	int mask = pr->tmask;// & (1 << (SYS_wait - 1));
111: 	if (mask) {
112: 		printf(") ...\n");
113: 	}
114: 	__debug_info("sys_wait", "p = %p\n", p);
115: 	int ret = wait4(-1, p, 0);
116: 	if (mask) {
117: 		printf("pid %d: return from wait(0x%x", pr->pid, p);
118: 	}
119: 	return ret;
120: }
121:

122: uint64 sys_wait4(void) {
123: 	int pid;
124: 	uint64 status, options;
125: 
126: 	// extract syscall args 
127: 	if (argint(0, &pid) < 0) 
128: 		return -1;
129: 	if (argaddr(1, &status) < 0) 
130: 		return -1;
131: 	if (argaddr(2, &options) < 0) 
132: 		return -1;
133: 
134: 	struct proc *pr = myproc();
135: 	int mask = pr->tmask;
136: 	if (mask) {
137: 		printf(") ...\n");
138: 	}
139: 	int ret = wait4(pid, status, options);
140: 	if (mask) {
141: 		printf("pid %d: return from wait4(%p", pr->pid, status);
142: 	}
143: 
144: 	return ret;
145: }

154: uint64
155: sys_sleep(void)
156: {
157: 	int n;
158: 	int ret = 0;
159: 	uint64 now, expire;
160: 	argint(0, &n);
161: 
162: 	struct proc *p = myproc();
163: 	int mask = p->tmask;
164: 	if (mask) {
165: 		printf(") ...\n");
166: 	}
167: 
168: 	now = readtime();
169: 	// ont tick is 0.1s in xv6
170: 	expire = now + n * (CLK_FREQ / 10);
171: 
172: 	if (expire > now) {
173: 		acquire(&p->lk);
174: 		p->sleep_expire = expire;
175: 		do {
176: 			sleep(&p->sleep_expire, &p->lk);
177: 			if (SIGTERM == p->killed) {
178: 				ret = -EINTR;
179: 				break;
180: 			}
181: 		} while (p->sleep_expire != 0);
182: 		release(&p->lk);
183: 	}
184: 
185: 	if (mask) {
186: 		printf("pid %d: return from sleep(%d", p->pid, n);
187: 	}
188: 	return ret;
189: }
190:

192: 	uint64 ureq, urem;
193: 	struct timespec req, rem;

155: sys_sleep(void)

164: 	if (mask) {
165: 		printf(") ...\n");
166: 	}
167: 
168: 	now = readtime();
169: 	// ont tick is 0.1s in xv6
170: 	expire = now + n * (CLK_FREQ / 10);
171: 
172: 	if (expire > now) {
173: 		acquire(&p->lk);
174: 		p->sleep_expire = expire;
175: 		do {
176: 			sleep(&p->sleep_expire, &p->lk);
177: 			if (SIGTERM == p->killed) {
178: 				ret = -EINTR;
179: 				break;
180: 			}
181: 		} while (p->sleep_expire != 0);
182: 		release(&p->lk);
183: 	}
184: 
185: 	if (mask) {
186: 		printf("pid %d: return from sleep(%d", p->pid, n);
187: 	}
188: 	return ret;
189: }

匹配分数 1.000 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": true, "exact_token_fp": true, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 1.0}

191: uint64 sys_nanosleep(void) {
192: 	uint64 ureq, urem;
193: 	struct timespec req, rem;
194: 
195: 	argaddr(0, &ureq);
196: 	argaddr(1, &urem);
197: 	if (copyin2((char*)&req, ureq, sizeof(struct timespec)) < 0) 
198: 		return -EFAULT;
199: 	if (req.nsec > 999999999L)
200: 		return -EINVAL;
201: 
202: 	int ret = 0;
203: 	struct proc *p = myproc();
204: 	int mask = p->tmask;
205: 	if (mask) {
206: 		printf(") ...\n");
207: 	}
208: 	
209: 	uint64 now, expire;
210: 	now = readtime();
211: 	expire = now + convert_from_timespec(&req);
212: 
213: 	if (expire > now) {
214: 		acquire(&p->lk);
215: 		p->sleep_expire = expire;
216: 		do {
217: 			sleep(&p->sleep_expire, &p->lk);
218: 			if (SIGTERM == p->killed) {	// or signal pending with -EINTR
219: 				ret = -EINTR;
220: 				break;
221: 			}
222: 		} while (p->sleep_expire != 0);
223: 		release(&p->lk);
224: 	}
225: 
226: 	if (mask) {
227: 		printf("pid %d: return from nanosleep(", p->pid);
228: 	}
229: 
230: 	if (ret < 0 && urem) {
231: 		now = readtime();
232: 		if (now < expire) {
233: 			convert_to_timespec(expire - now, &rem);
234: 			copyout2(urem, (char *)&rem, sizeof(struct timespec));
235: 		}
236: 	}
237: 
238: 	return ret;
239: }
240: 
241: // return how many clock tick interrupts have occurred
242: // since start.
243: uint64
244: sys_uptime(void)

xv6-k210@final / 来源候选源码

186: 		printf("pid %d: return from sleep(%d", p->pid, n);
187: 	}
188: 	return ret;
189: }
190: 
191: uint64 sys_nanosleep(void) {
192: 	uint64 ureq, urem;
193: 	struct timespec req, rem;
194: 
195: 	argaddr(0, &ureq);
196: 	argaddr(1, &urem);
197: 	if (copyin2((char*)&req, ureq, sizeof(struct timespec)) < 0) 
198: 		return -EFAULT;
199: 	if (req.nsec > 999999999L)
200: 		return -EINVAL;
201: 
202: 	int ret = 0;
203: 	struct proc *p = myproc();
204: 	int mask = p->tmask;
205: 	if (mask) {
206: 		printf(") ...\n");
207: 	}
208: 	
209: 	uint64 now, expire;
210: 	now = readtime();
211: 	expire = now + convert_from_timespec(&req);
212: 
213: 	if (expire > now) {
214: 		acquire(&p->lk);
215: 		p->sleep_expire = expire;
216: 		do {
217: 			sleep(&p->sleep_expire, &p->lk);
218: 			if (SIGTERM == p->killed) {	// or signal pending with -EINTR
219: 				ret = -EINTR;
220: 				break;
221: 			}
222: 		} while (p->sleep_expire != 0);
223: 		release(&p->lk);
224: 	}
225: 
226: 	if (mask) {
227: 		printf("pid %d: return from nanosleep(", p->pid);
228: 	}
229: 
230: 	if (ret < 0 && urem) {
231: 		now = readtime();
232: 		if (now < expire) {
233: 			convert_to_timespec(expire - now, &rem);
234: 			copyout2(urem, (char *)&rem, sizeof(struct timespec));
235: 		}
236: 	}
237: 
238: 	return ret;

xv6-k210/kernel/syscall/sysproc.c::sys_getrusage
匹配分数 0.765 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": false, "exact_token_fp": false, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 0.883}

oskernel2023-zmz@recover 源码

274: 	// for now it's not very necessary to implement this syscall

277: }
278: 
279: uint64
280: sys_getrusage(void)
281: {
282: 	int who;
283: 	uint64 addr;
284: 	struct rusage r;
285: 	struct proc *p = myproc();
286: 
287: 	if (argint(0, &who) < 0 || argaddr(1, &addr) < 0)
288: 		return -1;
289: 
290: 	memset(&r, 0, sizeof(r));
291: 	switch (who)
292: 	{
293: 		case RUSAGE_SELF:
294: 		case RUSAGE_THREAD:
295: 			convert_to_timeval(p->proc_tms.utime, &r.ru_utime);
296: 			convert_to_timeval(p->proc_tms.stime, &r.ru_stime);
297: 			r.ru_nvcsw = p->vswtch;
298: 			r.ru_nivcsw = p->ivswtch;
299: 			__debug_info("sys_getrusage", "{u: %ds %dus | s: %ds %dus}, nvcsw=%d, nivcsw=%d\n", 
300: 						r.ru_utime.sec, r.ru_utime.usec, r.ru_stime.sec, r.ru_stime.usec, p->vswtch, p->ivswtch);
301: 			break;
302: 		case RUSAGE_CHILDREN:
303: 			convert_to_timeval(p->proc_tms.cutime, &r.ru_utime);
304: 			convert_to_timeval(p->proc_tms.cstime, &r.ru_stime);
305: 			break;
306: 		default:
307: 			return -EINVAL;
308: 	}
309: 
310: 	if (copyout2(addr, (char *)&r, sizeof(r)) < 0)
311: 		return -EFAULT;
312: 
313: 	return 0;
314: }

xv6-k210@final / 来源候选源码

274: 	// for now it's not very necessary to implement this syscall 
275: 	// may be implemented later 
276: 	return 0;
277: }
278: 
279: uint64
280: sys_getrusage(void)
281: {
282: 	int who;
283: 	uint64 addr;
284: 	struct rusage r;
285: 	struct proc *p = myproc();
286: 
287: 	// if (argint(0, &who) < 0 || argaddr(1, &addr) < 0)
288: 	// 	return -1;
289: 	argint(0, &who);
290: 	argaddr(1, &addr);
291: 
292: 	memset(&r, 0, sizeof(r));
293: 	switch (who)
294: 	{
295: 		case RUSAGE_SELF:
296: 		case RUSAGE_THREAD:
297: 			convert_to_timeval(p->proc_tms.utime, &r.ru_utime);
298: 			convert_to_timeval(p->proc_tms.stime, &r.ru_stime);
299: 			r.ru_nvcsw = p->vswtch;
300: 			r.ru_nivcsw = p->ivswtch;
301: 			__debug_info("sys_getrusage", "{u: %ds %dus | s: %ds %dus}, nvcsw=%d, nivcsw=%d\n", 
302: 						r.ru_utime.sec, r.ru_utime.usec, r.ru_stime.sec, r.ru_stime.usec, p->vswtch, p->ivswtch);
303: 			break;
304: 		case RUSAGE_CHILDREN:
305: 			convert_to_timeval(p->proc_tms.cutime, &r.ru_utime);
306: 			convert_to_timeval(p->proc_tms.cstime, &r.ru_stime);
307: 			break;
308: 		default:
309: 			return -EINVAL;
310: 	}
311: 
312: 	if (copyout2(addr, (char *)&r, sizeof(r)) < 0)
313: 		return -EFAULT;
314: 
315: 	return 0;
316: }

12: #include "mm/vm.h"
13: #include "errno.h"
14: 
15: #include "utils/debug.h"
16: 
17: uint64 sys_rt_sigaction(void) {
18: 	int signum;
19: 	uint64 uptr_act;		// struct sigaction const *act
20: 	uint64 uptr_oldact;		// struct sigaction *oldact
21: 	int size;
22: 
23: 	// extract user args
24: 	// if (argint(0, &signum) < 0) {
25: 	// 	return -EINVAL;
26: 	// }
27: 	// if (argaddr(1, &uptr_act) < 0) {
28: 	// 	return -EINVAL;
29: 	// }
30: 	// if (argaddr(2, &uptr_oldact) < 0) {
31: 	// 	return -EINVAL;
32: 	// }
33: 	argint(0, &signum);
34: 	argaddr(1, &uptr_act);
35: 	argaddr(2, &uptr_oldact);
36: 	argint(3, &size);
37: 
38: 	// copy struct sigaction from user space 
39: 	struct sigaction act;
40: 	struct sigaction oldact;
41: 	// if (uptr_act && copyin2((char*)&act, uptr_act, sizeof(struct sigaction)) < 0) {
42: 	// 	return -EFAULT;
43: 	// }
44: 	if (size > 16 * 8) {
45: 		size = 16 * 8;
46: 	}
47: 	else if (size < 8) {
48: 		size = 8;
49: 	}
50: 
51: 	if (uptr_act) {
52: 		if (
53: 			copyin2((char*)&(act.__sigaction_handler), uptr_act, sizeof(__sighandler_t)) < 0 || 
54: 			copyin2((char*)&(act.sa_mask), uptr_act + sizeof(__sighandler_t), size) < 0 || 
55: 			copyin2((char*)&(act.sa_flags), uptr_act + sizeof(__sighandler_t) + size, sizeof(int)) < 0
56: 		) {
57: 			return -EFAULT;
58: 		}
59: 	}
60: 
61: 	if (set_sigaction(
62: 		signum, 
63: 		uptr_act ? &act : NULL, 
64: 		uptr_oldact ? &oldact : NULL, 
65: 		size / 8
66: 	) < 0) {
67: 		return -EINVAL;
68: 	}
69: 
70: 	// copyout old struct sigaction 
71: 	// if (uptr_oldact && copyout2(uptr_oldact, (char*)&oldact, sizeof(struct sigaction)) < 0) {
72: 	// 	return -EFAULT;
73: 	// }
74: 	if (uptr_oldact) {
75: 		if (
76: 			copyout2(uptr_oldact, (char*)&(act.__sigaction_handler), sizeof(__sighandler_t)) < 0 || 
77: 			copyout2(uptr_oldact + sizeof(__sighandler_t), (char*)&(act.sa_mask), size) < 0 || 
78: 			copyout2(uptr_oldact + sizeof(__sighandler_t) + size, (char*)&(act.sa_flags), sizeof(int)) < 0
79: 		) {
80: 			return -EFAULT;
81: 		}
82: 	}
83: 
84: 	return 0;
85: }
86: 
87: uint64 sys_rt_sigprocmask(void) {
88: 	int how;
89: 	uint64 uptr_set, uptr_oldset;
90:

92: 	int size;
93: 
94: 	// extract user arguments
95: 	// if (argint(0, &how) < 0) 
96: 	// 	return -EINVAL;

116: 	}
117: 
118: 	if (sigprocmask(
119: 		how, 
120: 		&set, 
121: 		uptr_oldset ? &oldset : NULL,

123: 	)) {
124: 		return -EINVAL;

30: void
31: set_next_timeout() {
32: 	// There is a very strange bug,
33: 	// if comment the `printf` line below
34: 	// the timer will not work.
35: 
36: 	// this bug seems to disappear automatically
37: 	// printf("");
38: 	// sbi_set_timer(r_time() + INTERVAL);
39: 	// struct sbiret result = sbi_xv6_get_timer();
40: 	// __debug_assert("set_next_timeout", SBI_SUCCESS == result.error, "SBI call failed");
41: 	struct sbiret result;
42: 	result.value = readtime();	// don't need to trap for time
43: 	result = sbi_set_timer(result.value + INTERVAL);
44: 	__debug_assert("set_next_timeout", SBI_SUCCESS == result.error, "SBI call failed");
45: }
46:

49: 		ticks++;

30: void
31: set_next_timeout() {
32: 	// There is a very strange bug,
33: 	// if comment the `printf` line below
34: 	// the timer will not work.
35: 
36: 	// this bug seems to disappear automatically
37: 	// printf("");
38: 	// sbi_set_timer(r_time() + INTERVAL);
39: 	// struct sbiret result = sbi_xv6_get_timer();
40: 	// __debug_assert("set_next_timeout", SBI_SUCCESS == result.error, "SBI call failed");
41: 	uint64 now = readtime();
42: 	static uint64 last = 0;
43: 	static int64 const MAX_TIME = CLK_FREQ * 200;
44: 	if (now < MAX_TIME) {
45: 		if (cpuid() == 0) {	
46: 			if (now - last >= CLK_FREQ * 5) {
47: 				last = now;
48: 				// printf("%d s\n", now / CLK_FREQ);
49: 				printf(" ");
50: 			}
51: 		}
52: 		else if (now - last >= CLK_FREQ * 8) {
53: 			last = now;
54: 			// printf("%d s\n", now / CLK_FREQ);
55: 			printf(" ");
56: 		}
57: 	}
58: 	// struct sbiret result;
59: 	// result.value = readtime();	// don't need to trap for time
60: 	sbi_set_timer(now + INTERVAL);
61: 	// __debug_assert("set_next_timeout", SBI_SUCCESS == result.error, "SBI call failed");
62: }
63: 
64: void timer_tick() {
65: 	set_next_timeout();
66: }

1: # 
2: # The floating-point registers context.
3: # 
4: # When switching process, call here to save/restore the floating-point registers.
5: # We don't bother to save those register during trap, since the kernel doesn't
6: # touch any of them. But technically speaking, this should be trap's work.

1: # 
2: # The floating-point registers context.
3: # 
4: # When switching process, call here to save/restore the floating-point registers.
5: # We don't bother to save those register during trap, since the kernel doesn't
6: # touch any of them. But technically speaking, this should be trap's work.

58: uint64 CountInteruptInfo[INTER_COUNT];
59: extern struct inode *proc_INT;
60: 
61: 
62: // set up to take exceptions and traps while in the kernel.
63: void
64: trapinithart(void)
65: {
66: 	w_stvec((uint64)kernelvec);
67: 	w_sstatus(r_sstatus() | SSTATUS_SIE);
68: 	// enable supervisor-mode timer interrupts.
69: 	w_sie(r_sie() | SIE_SEIE | SIE_SSIE | SIE_STIE);
70: 	set_next_timeout();
71: 
72: 	__debug_info("trapinithart", "init\n");
73: }
74: #include "mm/pm.h"
75: //
76: // handle an interrupt, exception, or system call from user space.
77: // called from trampoline.S
78: //

xv6-k210/kernel/trap/trap.c::usertrap
匹配分数 0.764 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 0.898, "exact_ast_shape": false, "exact_token_fp": false, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 0.914}

oskernel2023-zmz@recover 源码

74: #include "mm/pm.h"
75: //
76: // handle an interrupt, exception, or system call from user space.
77: // called from trampoline.S
78: //
79: void
80: usertrap(void)
81: {
82: 	__debug_assert("usertrap", 0 == (r_sstatus() & SSTATUS_SPP), 
83: 			"not from user mode\n");
84: 
85: 	// send interrupts and exceptions to kerneltrap(),
86: 	// since we're now in the kernel.
87: 	w_stvec((uint64)kernelvec);
88: 
89: 	protect_usr_mem();  // since we turned on this when leaving S-mode
90: 
91: 	struct proc *p = myproc();
92: 
93: 	uint64 timestamp = readtime();
94: 	p->ikstmp = timestamp;
95: 	p->proc_tms.utime += timestamp - p->okstmp;
96: 
97: 	// save user program counter.
98: 	p->trapframe->epc = r_sepc();
99: 
100: 	uint64 cause = r_scause();
101: 	__debug_info("usertrap", "cause %d\n", cause);
102: 	__debug_info("usertrap", "epc = %p\n", r_sepc());
103: 	__debug_info("usertrap", "p->killed = %d\n", p->killed);
104: 	if (cause == EXCP_ENV_CALL) {
105: 		// system call
106: 		if(SIGTERM == p->killed) {
107: 			exit(-1);
108: 		}
109: 		// sepc points to the ecall instruction,
110: 		// but we want to return to the next instruction.
111: 		p->trapframe->epc += 4;
112: 		// an interrupt will change sstatus &c registers,
113: 		// so don't enable until done with those registers.
114: 		intr_on();
115: 		syscall();
116: 	} 
117: 	else if (0 == handle_intr(cause)) { // 在这里处理中断
118: 		// handle interrupt 
119: 		if (yield()) {
120: 			p->ivswtch += 1;
121: 		}
122: 	}
123: 	else if (0 == handle_excp(cause)) {
124: 		// handle exception 
125: 	}
126: 	else {
127: 		printf("\nusertrap(): unexpected scause %p pid=%d %s\n", cause, p->pid, p->name);
128: 		printf("            sepc=%p stval=%p\n", r_sepc(), r_stval());
129: 		trapframedump(p->trapframe);
130: 		printf("idlepages %d\n", idlepages());
131: 		p->killed = SIGTERM;
132: 	}
133:    
134: 	if (p->killed) {
135: 		if (SIGTERM == p->killed)
136: 			exit(-1);
137: 		__debug_info("usertrap", "enter handler\n");
138: 		sighandle();
139: 	}
140: 
141: 	__debug_info("usertrap", "%d: enter usertrapret\n", cause);
142: 	usertrapret();
143: } 
144:    
145: // 
146: // return to user space
147: //
148: void

xv6-k210@final / 来源候选源码

69: #include "mm/pm.h"
70: //
71: // handle an interrupt, exception, or system call from user space.
72: // called from trampoline.S
73: //
74: void
75: usertrap(void)
76: {
77: 	__debug_assert("usertrap", 0 == (r_sstatus() & SSTATUS_SPP), 
78: 			"not from user mode\n");
79: 
80: 	// send interrupts and exceptions to kerneltrap(),
81: 	// since we're now in the kernel.
82: 	w_stvec((uint64)kernelvec);
83: 
84: 	protect_usr_mem();  // since we turned on this when leaving S-mode
85: 
86: 	struct proc *p = myproc();
87: 
88: 	uint64 timestamp = readtime();
89: 	p->ikstmp = timestamp;
90: 	p->proc_tms.utime += timestamp - p->okstmp;
91: 
92: 	// save user program counter.
93: 	p->trapframe->epc = r_sepc();
94: 
95: 	uint64 cause = r_scause();
96: 	__debug_info("usertrap", "cause %d\n", cause);
97: 	__debug_info("usertrap", "epc = %p\n", r_sepc());
98: 	__debug_info("usertrap", "p->killed = %d\n", p->killed);
99: 	if (cause == EXCP_ENV_CALL) {
100: 		// system call
101: 		if(SIGTERM == p->killed) {
102: 			exit(-1);
103: 		}
104: 		// sepc points to the ecall instruction,
105: 		// but we want to return to the next instruction.
106: 		p->trapframe->epc += 4;
107: 		// an interrupt will change sstatus &c registers,
108: 		// so don't enable until done with those registers.
109: 		intr_on();
110: 		syscall();
111: 	} 
112: 	else if (0 == handle_intr(cause)) {
113: 		// handle interrupt 
114: 		if (yield()) {
115: 			p->ivswtch += 1;
116: 		}
117: 	}
118: 	else if (0 == handle_excp(cause)) {
119: 		// handle exception 
120: 	}
121: 	else {
122: 		printf("\nusertrap(): unexpected scause %p pid=%d %s\n", cause, p->pid, p->name);
123: 		printf("            sepc=%p stval=%p\n", r_sepc(), r_stval());
124: 		// trapframedump(p->trapframe);
125: 		// printf("idlepages %d\n", idlepages());
126: 		p->killed = SIGTERM;
127: 
128: 		// sync files, so progresses won't be lost even if the panic happens
129: 		syncfs();
130: 	}
131:    
132: 	if (p->killed) {
133: 		if (SIGTERM == p->killed)
134: 			exit(-1);
135: 		__debug_info("usertrap", "enter handler\n");
136: 		sighandle();
137: 	}
138: 
139: 	__debug_info("usertrap", "%d: enter usertrapret\n", cause);
140: 	usertrapret();
141: } 
142:    
143: // 
144: // return to user space
145: //
146: void

xv6-k210/kernel/trap/trap.c::usertrapret
匹配分数 1.000 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": true, "exact_token_fp": true, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 1.0}

oskernel2023-zmz@recover 源码

143: } 
144:    
145: // 
146: // return to user space
147: //
148: void
149: usertrapret(void) {
150: 	struct proc *p = myproc();
151: 
152: 	// we're about to switch the destination of traps from
153: 	// kerneltrap() to usertrap(), so turn off interrupts until
154: 	// we're back in user space, where usertrap() is correct.
155: 	intr_off();
156: 
157: 	uint64 timestamp = readtime();
158: 	p->okstmp = timestamp;
159: 	p->proc_tms.stime += timestamp - p->ikstmp;
160: 
161: 	// send syscalls, interrupts, and exceptions to trampoline.S
162: 	w_stvec(TRAMPOLINE + (uservec - trampoline));
163: 
164: 	// set up trapframe values that uservec will need when
165: 	// the process next re-enters the kernel.
166: 	p->trapframe->kernel_satp = r_satp();         // kernel page table
167: 	p->trapframe->kernel_sp = p->kstack + PGSIZE;	// process's kernel stack 
168: 	p->trapframe->kernel_trap = (uint64)usertrap;
169: 	p->trapframe->kernel_hartid = r_tp();         // hartid for cpuid()
170: 
171: 	// set up the registers that trampoline.S's sret will use
172: 	// to get to user space.
173: 	 
174: 	// set S Previous Privilege mode to User.
175: 	unsigned long x = r_sstatus();
176: 	x &= ~SSTATUS_SPP; // clear SPP to 0 for user mode
177: 	x |= SSTATUS_SPIE; // enable interrupts in user mode
178: 	w_sstatus(x);
179: 	 
180: 	// set S Exception Program Counter to the saved user pc.
181: 	w_sepc(p->trapframe->epc);
182: 	 
183: 	// tell trampoline.S the user page table to switch to.
184: 	// printf("[usertrapret]p->pagetable: %p\n", p->pagetable);
185: 	uint64 satp = MAKE_SATP(p->pagetable);
186: 	 
187: 	permit_usr_mem(); // it's odd that without this the hart will stuck in u-mode on k210
188: 
189: 	// jump to trampoline.S at the top of memory, which 
190: 	// switches to the user page table, restores user registers,
191: 	// and switches to user mode with sret.
192: 	uint64 fn = TRAMPOLINE + (userret - trampoline);
193: 	// ((void (*)(uint64,uint64))fn)(TRAPFRAME, satp);
194: 	((void (*)(uint64, uint64))fn)((uint64)(p->trapframe), satp);
195: }
196: 
197: // interrupts and exceptions from kernel code go here via kernelvec,
198: // on whatever the current kernel stack is.
199: void 
200: kerneltrap() {

xv6-k210@final / 来源候选源码

141: } 
142:    
143: // 
144: // return to user space
145: //
146: void
147: usertrapret(void) {
148: 	struct proc *p = myproc();
149: 
150: 	// we're about to switch the destination of traps from
151: 	// kerneltrap() to usertrap(), so turn off interrupts until
152: 	// we're back in user space, where usertrap() is correct.
153: 	intr_off();
154: 
155: 	uint64 timestamp = readtime();
156: 	p->okstmp = timestamp;
157: 	p->proc_tms.stime += timestamp - p->ikstmp;
158: 
159: 	// send syscalls, interrupts, and exceptions to trampoline.S
160: 	w_stvec(TRAMPOLINE + (uservec - trampoline));
161: 
162: 	// set up trapframe values that uservec will need when
163: 	// the process next re-enters the kernel.
164: 	p->trapframe->kernel_satp = r_satp();         // kernel page table
165: 	p->trapframe->kernel_sp = p->kstack + PGSIZE;	// process's kernel stack 
166: 	p->trapframe->kernel_trap = (uint64)usertrap;
167: 	p->trapframe->kernel_hartid = r_tp();         // hartid for cpuid()
168: 
169: 	// set up the registers that trampoline.S's sret will use
170: 	// to get to user space.
171: 	 
172: 	// set S Previous Privilege mode to User.
173: 	unsigned long x = r_sstatus();
174: 	x &= ~SSTATUS_SPP; // clear SPP to 0 for user mode
175: 	x |= SSTATUS_SPIE; // enable interrupts in user mode
176: 	w_sstatus(x);
177: 	 
178: 	// set S Exception Program Counter to the saved user pc.
179: 	w_sepc(p->trapframe->epc);
180: 	 
181: 	// tell trampoline.S the user page table to switch to.
182: 	// printf("[usertrapret]p->pagetable: %p\n", p->pagetable);
183: 	uint64 satp = MAKE_SATP(p->pagetable);
184: 	 
185: 	permit_usr_mem(); // it's odd that without this the hart will stuck in u-mode on k210
186: 
187: 	// jump to trampoline.S at the top of memory, which 
188: 	// switches to the user page table, restores user registers,
189: 	// and switches to user mode with sret.
190: 	uint64 fn = TRAMPOLINE + (userret - trampoline);
191: 	// ((void (*)(uint64,uint64))fn)(TRAPFRAME, satp);
192: 	((void (*)(uint64, uint64))fn)((uint64)(p->trapframe), satp);
193: }
194: 
195: // interrupts and exceptions from kernel code go here via kernelvec,
196: // on whatever the current kernel stack is.
197: void 
198: kerneltrap() {

oskernel2023-zmz@recover 第 199 行 · 主要确定性来源：xv6-k210/kernel/trap/trap.c::kerneltrap

候选来源与匹配信号

xv6-k210/kernel/trap/trap.c::kerneltrap
匹配分数 1.000 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": true, "exact_token_fp": true, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 1.0}

oskernel2023-zmz@recover 源码

194: 	((void (*)(uint64, uint64))fn)((uint64)(p->trapframe), satp);
195: }
196: 
197: // interrupts and exceptions from kernel code go here via kernelvec,
198: // on whatever the current kernel stack is.
199: void 
200: kerneltrap() {
201: 	uint64 sepc = r_sepc();
202: 	uint64 sstatus = r_sstatus();
203: 	uint64 scause = r_scause();
204: 
205: 	struct proc *p = myproc();
206: 
207: 	// the kernel might be accessing user space when this trap was happening
208: 	// the pre-state is stored in the var 'sstatus' as above, which will be restored before leaving
209: 	protect_usr_mem();
210: 
211: 	__debug_assert("kerneltrap", (0 != (sstatus & SSTATUS_SPP)), 
212: 			"not from supervisor mode\n");
213: 	__debug_assert("kerneltrap", 0 == intr_get(), 
214: 			"interrupts enable\n");
215: 
216: 	//__debug_info("kerneltrap", "enter %d\n", scause);
217: 	if (0 == handle_intr(scause)) {
218: 		// handle interrupt
219: 	}
220: 	else if (0 == handle_excp(scause)) {
221: 		// handle exception 
222: 	}
223: 	else if (p && is_page_fault(scause) && PGSIZE <= r_stval() && r_stval() < MAXUVA) {
224: 		// This case may happen when kernel is accessing user's lazy-allocated page.
225: 		// The handler should allocate a real one, but failed for lack of memory.
226: 		//p->killed = 1;
227: 		sepc = kern_pgfault_escape(r_stval());
228: 		__debug_warn("kerneltrap", "sepc=%p stval=%p escape=%p pid=%d\n", 
229: 				r_sepc(), r_stval(), sepc, p->pid);
230: 	}
231: 	else {
232: 		// unsupported trap 
233: 		printf("\nscause %p\n", scause);
234: 		printf("sepc=%p stval=%p hart=%d\n", r_sepc(), r_stval(), r_tp());
235: 		if (p != NULL) {
236: 			printf("pid: %d, name: %s\n", p->pid, p->name);
237: 		}
238: 		panic("kerneltrap");
239: 	}
240: 	// the yield() may have caused some traps to occur,
241: 	// so restore trap registers for use by kernelvec.S's sepc instruction.
242: 	w_sepc(sepc);
243: 	w_sstatus(sstatus);
244: }
245: 
246: // check if it's an interrupt, and handle it 
247: // returns 
248: static char digits[] = "0123456789abcdef";
249: #define LEN_STORE_BUF 32

xv6-k210@final / 来源候选源码

192: 	((void (*)(uint64, uint64))fn)((uint64)(p->trapframe), satp);
193: }
194: 
195: // interrupts and exceptions from kernel code go here via kernelvec,
196: // on whatever the current kernel stack is.
197: void 
198: kerneltrap() {
199: 	uint64 sepc = r_sepc();
200: 	uint64 sstatus = r_sstatus();
201: 	uint64 scause = r_scause();
202: 
203: 	struct proc *p = myproc();
204: 
205: 	// the kernel might be accessing user space when this trap was happening
206: 	// the pre-state is stored in the var 'sstatus' as above, which will be restored before leaving
207: 	protect_usr_mem();
208: 
209: 	__debug_assert("kerneltrap", (0 != (sstatus & SSTATUS_SPP)), 
210: 			"not from supervisor mode\n");
211: 	__debug_assert("kerneltrap", 0 == intr_get(), 
212: 			"interrupts enable\n");
213: 
214: 	//__debug_info("kerneltrap", "enter %d\n", scause);
215: 	if (0 == handle_intr(scause)) {
216: 		// handle interrupt
217: 	}
218: 	else if (0 == handle_excp(scause)) {
219: 		// handle exception 
220: 	}
221: 	else if (p && is_page_fault(scause) && PGSIZE <= r_stval() && r_stval() < MAXUVA) {
222: 		// This case may happen when kernel is accessing user's lazy-allocated page.
223: 		// The handler should allocate a real one, but failed for lack of memory.
224: 		//p->killed = 1;
225: 		sepc = kern_pgfault_escape(r_stval());
226: 		__debug_warn("kerneltrap", "sepc=%p stval=%p escape=%p pid=%d\n", 
227: 				r_sepc(), r_stval(), sepc, p->pid);
228: 	}
229: 	else {
230: 		// unsupported trap 
231: 		printf("\nscause %p\n", scause);
232: 		printf("sepc=%p stval=%p hart=%d\n", r_sepc(), r_stval(), r_tp());
233: 		if (p != NULL) {
234: 			printf("pid: %d, name: %s\n", p->pid, p->name);
235: 		}
236: 		panic("kerneltrap");
237: 	}
238: 	// the yield() may have caused some traps to occur,
239: 	// so restore trap registers for use by kernelvec.S's sepc instruction.
240: 	w_sepc(sepc);
241: 	w_sstatus(sstatus);
242: }
243: 
244: // check if it's an interrupt, and handle it 
245: // returns 
246: int handle_intr(uint64 scause) {
247: 	#ifdef DEBUG
Int2Stringzmz 中新增的函数候选
oskernel2023-zmz@recover 第 253 行 · 主要确定性来源：无确定性来源

248: static char digits[] = "0123456789abcdef";
249: #define LEN_STORE_BUF 32
250: char ArrStore[LEN_STORE_BUF];
251: 
252: 
253: int
254: Int2String(int xx, int base, int sign, char *DestStore)
255: {
256: 	char buf[LEN_STORE_BUF];
257: 	int i;
258: 	uint x;
259: 	int j;
260: 	int tot;
261: 
262: 	if(sign && (sign = xx < 0))
263: 		x = -xx;
264: 	else
265: 		x = xx;
266: 
267: 	i = 0;
268: 	do {
269: 		buf[i++] = digits[x % base];
270: 	} while((x /= base) != 0);
271: 
272: 	if(sign)
273: 		buf[i++] = '-';
274: 
275: 	tot = i;
276: 	j = 0;
277: 	while( i ){
278: 		//ArrStore[j++] = buf[--i];
279: 		DestStore[j++] = buf[--i];
280: 	}
281: 	return tot;
282: }

284: // 1----更新全局计数
285: 
286: // 2----及时写入文件
287: int handle_intr(uint64 scause) {
288: 	#ifdef DEBUG 
289: 	static int cnt = 0;
290: 	#endif 
291: 	int x1,c1;
292: 	int Lx1,Lc1;
293: 	int i;
294: 
295: 	x1 = scause & (~INTERRUPT_FLAG);
296: 	c1 = ++CountInteruptInfo[x1];
297: 	// 2----及时写入文件
298: 	//char left[LEN_STORE_BUF];
299: 	//char right[LEN_STORE_BUF];
300: 	//Lx1 = Int2String(x1,10,0,left);
301: 	//Lc1 = Int2String(c1,10,0,right);
302: 	int off = 0;
303: 	if (INTR_TIMER == scause) {
304: 		#ifdef DEBUG 
305: 		if (100 == cnt) {
306: 			__debug_info("handle_intr", "tick\n");
307: 			cnt = 0;
308: 		}
309: 		else {
310: 			cnt ++;
311: 		}
312: 		#endif 
313: 		// 1----更新全局计数
314: 		//INTERRUPT_FLAG
315: 		//proc_INT->fop->write(proc_INT,0,)
316: 		timer_tick();
317: 		proc_tick();
318: 		return 0;
319: 	}
320: 	#ifdef QEMU 
321: 	else if (INTR_EXTERNAL == scause) 
322: 	#else 
323: 	/* I'm afraid that we couldn't use stval as a token,
324: 	because when an intr is pending during critical section
325: 	where the intr is disabled, an exception may occur and
326: 	overwrite the stval register. So is the same on the
327: 	other way, when an S-exception is happening, an M-intr
328: 	setting the stval might cover the real original stval. */
329: 	// else if (INTR_SOFTWARE == scause && 9 == r_stval()) 
330: 	else if (INTR_SOFTWARE == scause && sbi_xv6_is_ext().value) 
331: 	#endif 
332: 	{
333: 		int irq = plic_claim();
334: 		int c;
335: 		switch (irq) {
336: 		case UART_IRQ: 
337: 			// keyboard input 
338: 			c = sbi_console_getchar();
339: 			if (-1 != c) 
340: 				consoleintr(c);
341: 			break;
342: 		case DISK_IRQ: 
343: 			// disk read/write complete 
344: 			__debug_info("handle_intr", "disk\n");
345: 			disk_intr();
346: 			break;
347: 		default: 
348: 			__debug_warn("handle_intr", "unexpected interrupt irq = %d\n", irq);
349: 			break;
350: 		}
351: 
352: 		if (irq) plic_complete(irq);
353: 
354: 		#ifndef QEMU 
355: 		w_sip(r_sip() & ~2);	// clear pending bit 
356: 		{
357: 			if (SBI_SUCCESS != sbi_xv6_set_ext().error) {
358: 				panic("sbi_xv6_set_ext() failed\n");
359: 			}
360: 		}
361: 		#endif 
362: 
363: 		// send software interrupts to other harts to inform them 
364: 		// for (int i = 0; i < NCPU; i ++) {
365: 		// 	if (cpuid() != i) {
366: 		// 		sbi_send_ipi(1 << i, 0);
367: 		// 	}
368: 		// }
369: 
370: 		return 0;
371: 	}
372: 	else if (INTR_SOFTWARE == scause) {		// the real software interrupt	
373: 		sbi_clear_ipi();
374: 
375: 		return 0;
376: 	}
377: 	else {
378: 		// to be honest, on k210 software interrupts may be used for IPI, 
379: 		// but as it is not yet supported, handle this as an unsupported one 
380: 		return -1;
381: 	}
382: 	// AAA1
383: 	/*
384: 	for(i=0;i<32;i++){
385: 		if(c1 = CountInteruptInfo[i]){
386: 			x1 = i;
387: 			c1 = CountInteruptInfo[x1];
388: 			// 2----及时写入文件
389: 			char left[LEN_STORE_BUF];
390: 			char right[LEN_STORE_BUF];
391: 			char format[] = ":    ";
392: 			Lx1 = Int2String(x1,10,0,left);
393: 			Lc1 = Int2String(c1,10,0,right);
394: 			right[Lc1++] = '\n';
395: 			proc_INT->fop->write(proc_INT,0,left,off,Lx1);
396: 			off += Lx1;
397: 			proc_INT->fop->write(proc_INT,0,format,off,5);
398: 			off += 5;
399: 			proc_INT->fop->write(proc_INT,0,right,off,Lc1);
400: 		}
401: 	}*/
402: }
