244: // check if it's an interrupt, and handle it 
245: // returns 
246: int handle_intr(uint64 scause) {
247: 	#ifdef DEBUG 
248: 	static int cnt = 0;
249: 	#endif 
250: 
251: 	if (INTR_TIMER == scause) {
252: 		#ifdef DEBUG 
253: 		if (100 == cnt) {
254: 			__debug_info("handle_intr", "tick\n");
255: 			cnt = 0;
256: 		}
257: 		else {
258: 			cnt ++;
259: 		}
260: 		#endif 
261: 		timer_tick();
262: 		proc_tick();
263: 		return 0;
264: 	}
265: 	#ifdef QEMU 
266: 	else if (INTR_EXTERNAL == scause) 
267: 	#else 
268: 	/* I'm afraid that we couldn't use stval as a token,
269: 	because when an intr is pending during critical section
270: 	where the intr is disabled, an exception may occur and
271: 	overwrite the stval register. So is the same on the
272: 	other way, when an S-exception is happening, an M-intr
273: 	setting the stval might cover the real original stval. */
274: 	// else if (INTR_SOFTWARE == scause && 9 == r_stval()) 
275: 	else if (INTR_SOFTWARE == scause && sbi_xv6_is_ext().value) 
276: 	#endif 
277: 	{
278: 		int irq = plic_claim();
279: 		int c;
280: 		switch (irq) {
281: 		case UART_IRQ: 
282: 			// keyboard input 
283: 			c = sbi_console_getchar();
284: 			if (-1 != c) 
285: 				consoleintr(c);
286: 			break;
287: 		case DISK_IRQ: 
288: 			// disk read/write complete 
289: 			__debug_info("handle_intr", "disk\n");
290: 			disk_intr();
291: 			break;
292: 		default: 
293: 			__debug_warn("handle_intr", "unexpected interrupt irq = %d\n", irq);
294: 			break;
295: 		}
296: 
297: 		if (irq) plic_complete(irq);
298: 
299: 		#ifndef QEMU 
300: 		w_sip(r_sip() & ~2);	// clear pending bit 
301: 		{
302: 			sbi_xv6_set_ext();
303: 		}
304: 		#endif 
305: 
306: 		// send software interrupts to other harts to inform them 
307: 		// for (int i = 0; i < NCPU; i ++) {
308: 		// 	if (cpuid() != i) {
309: 		// 		sbi_send_ipi(1 << i, 0);
310: 		// 	}
311: 		// }
312: 
313: 		return 0;
314: 	}
315: 	else if (INTR_SOFTWARE == scause) {		// the real software interrupt
316: 		sbi_clear_ipi();
317: 
318: 		return 0;
319: 	}
320: 	else {
321: 		// to be honest, on k210 software interrupts may be used for IPI, 
322: 		// but as it is not yet supported, handle this as an unsupported one 
323: 		return -1;
324: 	}
325: }

329: 	// later implementation may handle more cases, such as lazy allocation, mmap, etc.
330: 	switch (scause) {

404: // be noticed that syscall is not handled here 
405: int handle_excp(uint64 scause) {
406: 	// later implementation may handle more cases, such as lazy allocation, mmap, etc.
407: 	switch (scause) {
408: 	case EXCP_STORE_PAGE: 
409: 	#ifndef QEMU 
410: 	case EXCP_STORE_ACCESS: 
411: 	#endif 
412: 		return handle_page_fault(1, r_stval());
413: 	case EXCP_LOAD_PAGE: 
414: 	#ifndef QEMU 
415: 	case EXCP_LOAD_ACCESS: 
416: 	#endif 
417: 		return handle_page_fault(0, r_stval());
418: 	case EXCP_INST_PAGE:
419: 	#ifndef QEMU
420: 	case EXCP_INST_ACCESS:
421: 	#endif
422: 		return handle_page_fault(2, r_stval());
423: 	// unsupported exception 
424: 	default: return -1;
425: 	}
426: }

27: int uname_copyout(uint64 addr) {
28: 	struct utsname *user_uts = (struct utsname*)addr;
29: 
30: 	if (copyout2((uint64)user_uts->sysname, 
31: 			(void*)UNAME_SYSNAME, sizeof(UNAME_SYSNAME)) < 0) 
32: 	{
33: 		__debug_warn("uname_copyout", "fail to copy sysname\n");
34: 		return -1;
35: 	}
36: 	if (copyout2((uint64)user_uts->nodename, 
37: 			(void*)UNAME_NODENAME, sizeof(UNAME_NODENAME)) < 0) 
38: 	{
39: 		__debug_warn("uname_copyout", "fail to copy nodename\n");
40: 		return -1;
41: 	}
42: 	if (copyout2((uint64)user_uts->release, 
43: 			(void*)UNAME_RELEASE, sizeof(UNAME_RELEASE)) < 0) 
44: 	{
45: 		__debug_warn("uname_copyout", "fail to copy release\n");
46: 		return -1;
47: 	}
48: 	if (copyout2((uint64)user_uts->version, 
49: 			(void*)UNAME_VERSION, sizeof(UNAME_VERSION)) < 0) 
50: 	{
51: 		__debug_warn("uname_copyout", "fail to copy version\n");
52: 		return -1;
53: 	}

30: 	if (copyout2((uint64)user_uts->sysname, 
31: 			(void*)UNAME_SYSNAME, sizeof(UNAME_SYSNAME)) < 0)

40: 		return -1;

46: 		return -1;
47: 	}
48: 	if (copyout2((uint64)user_uts->version, 
49: 			(void*)UNAME_VERSION, sizeof(UNAME_VERSION)) < 0)

52: 		return -1;

54: 	if (copyout2((uint64)user_uts->machine, 
55: 			(void*)UNAME_MACHINE, sizeof(UNAME_MACHINE)) < 0) 
56: 	{
57: 		__debug_warn("uname_copyout", "fail to copy machine\n");
58: 		return -1;
59: 	}
60: 	if (copyout2((uint64)user_uts->domainname, 
61: 			(void*)UNAME_DOMAINNAME, sizeof(UNAME_DOMAINNAME)) < 0) 
62: 	{
63: 		__debug_warn("uname_copyout", "fail to copy domainname\n");
64: 		return -1;
65: 	}
66: 
67: 	return 0;
68: }

18: list_node_t *_list_push_back(list_node_t **phead, list_node_t *node) {

35: static void __rb_rotate_left(struct rb_node *node, struct rb_root *root)
36: {
37: 	struct rb_node *right = node->rb_right;
38: 	struct rb_node *parent = rb_parent(node);
39: 
40: 	if ((node->rb_right = right->rb_left))
41: 		rb_set_parent(right->rb_left, node);
42: 	right->rb_left = node;
43: 
44: 	rb_set_parent(right, parent);
45: 
46: 	if (parent)
47: 	{
48: 		if (node == parent->rb_left)
49: 			parent->rb_left = right;
50: 		else
51: 			parent->rb_right = right;
52: 	}
53: 	else
54: 		root->rb_node = right;
55: 	rb_set_parent(node, right);
56: }
57:

59: {
60: 	struct rb_node *left = node->rb_left;

匹配分数 1.000 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": true, "exact_token_fp": true, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 1.0}

80: 
81: void rb_insert_color(struct rb_node *node, struct rb_root *root)
82: {
83: 	struct rb_node *parent, *gparent;
84: 
85: 	while ((parent = rb_parent(node)) && rb_is_red(parent))
86: 	{
87: 		gparent = rb_parent(parent);
88: 
89: 		if (parent == gparent->rb_left)
90: 		{
91: 			{
92: 				struct rb_node *uncle = gparent->rb_right;
93: 				if (uncle && rb_is_red(uncle))
94: 				{
95: 					rb_set_black(uncle);
96: 					rb_set_black(parent);
97: 					rb_set_red(gparent);
98: 					node = gparent;
99: 					continue;
100: 				}
101: 			}
102: 
103: 			if (parent->rb_right == node)
104: 			{
105: 				struct rb_node *tmp;
106: 				__rb_rotate_left(parent, root);
107: 				tmp = parent;
108: 				parent = node;
109: 				node = tmp;
110: 			}
111: 
112: 			rb_set_black(parent);
113: 			rb_set_red(gparent);
114: 			__rb_rotate_right(gparent, root);
115: 		} else {
116: 			{
117: 				struct rb_node *uncle = gparent->rb_left;
118: 				if (uncle && rb_is_red(uncle))
119: 				{
120: 					rb_set_black(uncle);
121: 					rb_set_black(parent);
122: 					rb_set_red(gparent);
123: 					node = gparent;
124: 					continue;
125: 				}
126: 			}
127: 
128: 			if (parent->rb_left == node)
129: 			{
130: 				struct rb_node *tmp;
131: 				__rb_rotate_right(parent, root);
132: 				tmp = parent;
133: 				parent = node;
134: 				node = tmp;
135: 			}
136: 
137: 			rb_set_black(parent);
138: 			rb_set_red(gparent);
139: 			__rb_rotate_left(gparent, root);
140: 		}
141: 	}
142: 
143: 	rb_set_black(root->rb_node);
144: }
145: 
146: static void __rb_erase_color(struct rb_node *node, struct rb_node *parent,
147: 			     struct rb_root *root)
148: {

81: void rb_insert_color(struct rb_node *node, struct rb_root *root)
82: {
83: 	struct rb_node *parent, *gparent;
84: 
85: 	while ((parent = rb_parent(node)) && rb_is_red(parent))
86: 	{
87: 		gparent = rb_parent(parent);
88: 
89: 		if (parent == gparent->rb_left)
90: 		{
91: 			{
92: 				struct rb_node *uncle = gparent->rb_right;
93: 				if (uncle && rb_is_red(uncle))
94: 				{
95: 					rb_set_black(uncle);
96: 					rb_set_black(parent);
97: 					rb_set_red(gparent);
98: 					node = gparent;
99: 					continue;
100: 				}
101: 			}
102: 
103: 			if (parent->rb_right == node)
104: 			{
105: 				struct rb_node *tmp;
106: 				__rb_rotate_left(parent, root);
107: 				tmp = parent;
108: 				parent = node;
109: 				node = tmp;
110: 			}
111: 
112: 			rb_set_black(parent);
113: 			rb_set_red(gparent);
114: 			__rb_rotate_right(gparent, root);
115: 		} else {
116: 			{
117: 				struct rb_node *uncle = gparent->rb_left;
118: 				if (uncle && rb_is_red(uncle))
119: 				{
120: 					rb_set_black(uncle);
121: 					rb_set_black(parent);
122: 					rb_set_red(gparent);
123: 					node = gparent;
124: 					continue;
125: 				}
126: 			}
127: 
128: 			if (parent->rb_left == node)
129: 			{
130: 				struct rb_node *tmp;
131: 				__rb_rotate_right(parent, root);
132: 				tmp = parent;
133: 				parent = node;
134: 				node = tmp;
135: 			}
136: 
137: 			rb_set_black(parent);
138: 			rb_set_red(gparent);
139: 			__rb_rotate_left(gparent, root);
140: 		}
141: 	}
142: 
143: 	rb_set_black(root->rb_node);
144: }

146: static void __rb_erase_color(struct rb_node *node, struct rb_node *parent,
147: 			     struct rb_root *root)
148: {
149: 	struct rb_node *other;

164: 			    (!other->rb_right || rb_is_black(other->rb_right)))

168: 				parent = rb_parent(node);
169: 			}
170: 			else

172: 				if (!other->rb_right || rb_is_black(other->rb_right))
173: 				{
174: 					rb_set_black(other->rb_left);
175: 					rb_set_red(other);
176: 					__rb_rotate_right(other, root);
177: 					other = parent->rb_right;
178: 				}
179: 				rb_set_color(other, rb_color(parent));
180: 				rb_set_black(parent);
181: 				rb_set_black(other->rb_right);
182: 				__rb_rotate_left(parent, root);
183: 				node = root->rb_node;
184: 				break;
185: 			}
186: 		}
187: 		else
188: 		{
189: 			other = parent->rb_left;
190: 			if (rb_is_red(other))
191: 			{
192: 				rb_set_black(other);
193: 				rb_set_red(parent);

226: void rb_erase(struct rb_node *node, struct rb_root *root)
227: {
228: 	struct rb_node *child, *parent;
229: 	int color;
230: 
231: 	if (!node->rb_left)
232: 		child = node->rb_right;
233: 	else if (!node->rb_right)
234: 		child = node->rb_left;
235: 	else
236: 	{
237: 		struct rb_node *old = node, *left;
238: 
239: 		node = node->rb_right;
240: 		while ((left = node->rb_left) != NULL)
241: 			node = left;
242: 
243: 		if (rb_parent(old)) {
244: 			if (rb_parent(old)->rb_left == old)
245: 				rb_parent(old)->rb_left = node;
246: 			else
247: 				rb_parent(old)->rb_right = node;
248: 		} else
249: 			root->rb_node = node;
250: 
251: 		child = node->rb_right;
252: 		parent = rb_parent(node);
253: 		color = rb_color(node);
254: 
255: 		if (parent == old) {
256: 			parent = node;
257: 		} else {
258: 			if (child)
259: 				rb_set_parent(child, parent);
260: 			parent->rb_left = child;
261: 
262: 			node->rb_right = old->rb_right;
263: 			rb_set_parent(old->rb_right, node);
264: 		}
265: 
266: 		node->rb_parent_color = old->rb_parent_color;
267: 		node->rb_left = old->rb_left;
268: 		rb_set_parent(old->rb_left, node);
269: 
270: 		goto color;
271: 	}
272: 
273: 	parent = rb_parent(node);
274: 	color = rb_color(node);
275: 
276: 	if (child)
277: 		rb_set_parent(child, parent);
278: 	if (parent)
279: 	{
280: 		if (parent->rb_left == node)
281: 			parent->rb_left = child;
282: 		else
283: 			parent->rb_right = child;
284: 	}
285: 	else
286: 		root->rb_node = child;
287: 
288:  color:
289: 	if (color == RB_BLACK)
290: 		__rb_erase_color(child, parent, root);
291: }

293: static void rb_augment_path(struct rb_node *node, rb_augment_f func, void *data)
294: {
295: 	struct rb_node *parent;
296: 
297: up:
298: 	func(node, data);
299: 	parent = rb_parent(node);
300: 	if (!parent)
301: 		return;
302: 
303: 	if (node == parent->rb_left && parent->rb_right)
304: 		func(parent->rb_right, data);
305: 	else if (parent->rb_left)
306: 		func(parent->rb_left, data);
307: 
308: 	node = parent;
309: 	goto up;
310: }
311: 
312: /*
313:  * after inserting @node into the tree, update the tree to account for
314:  * both the new entry and any damage done by rebalance

312: /*
313:  * after inserting @node into the tree, update the tree to account for
314:  * both the new entry and any damage done by rebalance
315:  */
316: void rb_augment_insert(struct rb_node *node, rb_augment_f func, void *data)
317: {
318: 	if (node->rb_left)
319: 		node = node->rb_left;
320: 	else if (node->rb_right)
321: 		node = node->rb_right;
322: 
323: 	rb_augment_path(node, func, data);
324: }
325: 
326: /*
327:  * before removing the node, find the deepest node on the rebalance path
328:  * that will still be there after @node gets removed
329:  */

325: 
326: /*
327:  * before removing the node, find the deepest node on the rebalance path
328:  * that will still be there after @node gets removed
329:  */
330: struct rb_node *rb_augment_erase_begin(struct rb_node *node)
331: {
332: 	struct rb_node *deepest;
333: 
334: 	if (!node->rb_right && !node->rb_left)
335: 		deepest = rb_parent(node);
336: 	else if (!node->rb_right)
337: 		deepest = node->rb_left;
338: 	else if (!node->rb_left)
339: 		deepest = node->rb_right;
340: 	else {
341: 		deepest = rb_next(node);
342: 		if (deepest->rb_right)
343: 			deepest = deepest->rb_right;
344: 		else if (rb_parent(deepest) != node)
345: 			deepest = rb_parent(deepest);
346: 	}
347: 
348: 	return deepest;
349: }
350: 
351: /*
352:  * after removal, update the tree to account for the removed entry
353:  * and any rebalance damage.
354:  */

xv6-k210@final / 来源候选源码

355: void rb_augment_erase_end(struct rb_node *node, rb_augment_f func, void *data)
356: {
357: 	if (node)
358: 		rb_augment_path(node, func, data);
359: }

383: 	while (n->rb_right)

387: 
388: struct rb_node *rb_next(const struct rb_node *node)
389: {
390: 	struct rb_node *parent;
391: 
392: 	if (rb_parent(node) == node)
393: 		return NULL;
394: 
395: 	/* If we have a right-hand child, go down and then left as far
396: 	   as we can. */
397: 	if (node->rb_right) {
398: 		node = node->rb_right; 
399: 		while (node->rb_left)
400: 			node=node->rb_left;
401: 		return (struct rb_node *)node;
402: 	}
403: 
404: 	/* No right-hand children.  Everything down and left is
405: 	   smaller than us, so any 'next' node must be in the general
406: 	   direction of our parent. Go up the tree; any time the
407: 	   ancestor is a right-hand child of its parent, keep going
408: 	   up. First time it's a left-hand child of its parent, said
409: 	   parent is our 'next' node. */
410: 	while ((parent = rb_parent(node)) && node == parent->rb_right)
411: 		node = parent;
412: 
413: 	return parent;
414: }
415: 
416: struct rb_node *rb_prev(const struct rb_node *node)
417: {
418: 	struct rb_node *parent;
419:

416: struct rb_node *rb_prev(const struct rb_node *node)
417: {
418: 	struct rb_node *parent;
419: 
420: 	if (rb_parent(node) == node)

422: 
423: 	/* If we have a left-hand child, go down and then right as far

匹配分数 0.735 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": true, "exact_token_fp": false, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 0.117}

435: 		node = parent;

439: 
440: void rb_replace_node(struct rb_node *victim, struct rb_node *new,
441: 		     struct rb_root *root)
442: {
443: 	struct rb_node *parent = rb_parent(victim);
444: 
445: 	/* Set the surrounding nodes to point to the replacement */
446: 	if (parent) {
447: 		if (victim == parent->rb_left)
448: 			parent->rb_left = new;
449: 		else
450: 			parent->rb_right = new;
451: 	} else {
452: 		root->rb_node = new;
453: 	}
454: 	if (victim->rb_left)
455: 		rb_set_parent(victim->rb_left, new);
456: 	if (victim->rb_right)
457: 		rb_set_parent(victim->rb_right, new);
458: 
459: 	/* Copy the pointers/colour from the victim to the replacement */
460: 	*new = *victim;
461: }

xv6-k210@final / 来源候选源码

435: 		node = parent;
436: 
437: 	return parent;
438: }
439: 
440: void rb_replace_node(struct rb_node *victim, struct rb_node *new,
441: 		     struct rb_root *root)
442: {
443: 	struct rb_node *parent = rb_parent(victim);
444: 
445: 	/* Set the surrounding nodes to point to the replacement */
446: 	if (parent) {
447: 		if (victim == parent->rb_left)
448: 			parent->rb_left = new;
449: 		else
450: 			parent->rb_right = new;
451: 	} else {
452: 		root->rb_node = new;
453: 	}
454: 	if (victim->rb_left)
455: 		rb_set_parent(victim->rb_left, new);
456: 	if (victim->rb_right)
457: 		rb_set_parent(victim->rb_right, new);
458: 
459: 	/* Copy the pointers/colour from the victim to the replacement */
460: 	*new = *victim;
461: }

42: int
43: memcmp(const void *v1, const void *v2, uint n)
44: {
45: 	const uchar *s1, *s2;

58: void*
59: memmove(void *dst, const void *src, uint n)
60: {
61: 	const char *s;
62: 	char *d;
63: 
64: 	s = src;
65: 	d = dst;
66: 	if(s < d && s + n > d){
67: 		s += n;
68: 		d += n;
69: 		while(n-- > 0)
70: 			*--d = *--s;
71: 	} else
72: 		while(n-- > 0)
73: 			*d++ = *s++;
74: 
75: 	return dst;
76: }
77: 
78: // memcpy exists to placate GCC.  Use memmove.

7: void
8: cat(int fd)
9: {
10:   int n;
11:

16:     }
17:   }
18:   if(n < 0){
19:     fprintf(2, "cat: read error\n");

匹配分数 1.000 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": true, "exact_token_fp": true, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 1.0}

oskernel2023-zmz@recover 源码

6: #include "user.h"
7: 
8: // allocate more than half of physical memory,
9: // then fork. this will fail in the default
10: // kernel, which does not support copy-on-write.
11: void
12: simpletest()
13: {
14:   // uint64 phys_size = PHYSTOP - KERNBASE;
15:   struct sysinfo info;
16:   sysinfo(&info);
17:   
18:   int sz = (info.freeram / 3) * 2;
19: 
20:   printf("simple: ");
21:   
22:   char *p = sbrk(sz);
23:   if(p == (char*)0xffffffffffffffffL){
24:     printf("sbrk(%d) failed\n", sz);
25:     exit(-1);
26:   }
27: 
28:   for(char *q = p; q < p + sz; q += 4096){
29:     *(int*)q = getpid();
30:   }
31: 
32:   int pid = fork();
33:   if(pid < 0){
34:     printf("fork() failed\n");
35:     exit(-1);
36:   }
37: 
38:   if(pid == 0)
39:     exit(0);
40: 
41:   wait(0);
42: 
43:   if(sbrk(-sz) == (char*)0xffffffffffffffffL){
44:     printf("sbrk(-%d) failed\n", sz);
45:     exit(-1);
46:   }
47: 
48:   printf("ok\n");
49: }
50: 
51: // three processes all write COW memory.
52: // this causes more than half of physical memory
53: // to be allocated, so it also checks whether
54: // copied pages are freed.

xv6-k210@final / 来源候选源码

6: #include "user.h"
7: 
8: // allocate more than half of physical memory,
9: // then fork. this will fail in the default
10: // kernel, which does not support copy-on-write.
11: void
12: simpletest()
13: {
14:   // uint64 phys_size = PHYSTOP - KERNBASE;
15:   struct sysinfo info;
16:   sysinfo(&info);
17:   
18:   int sz = (info.freeram / 3) * 2;
19: 
20:   printf("simple: ");
21:   
22:   char *p = sbrk(sz);
23:   if(p == (char*)0xffffffffffffffffL){
24:     printf("sbrk(%d) failed\n", sz);
25:     exit(-1);
26:   }
27: 
28:   for(char *q = p; q < p + sz; q += 4096){
29:     *(int*)q = getpid();
30:   }
31: 
32:   int pid = fork();
33:   if(pid < 0){
34:     printf("fork() failed\n");
35:     exit(-1);
36:   }
37: 
38:   if(pid == 0)
39:     exit(0);
40: 
41:   wait(0);
42: 
43:   if(sbrk(-sz) == (char*)0xffffffffffffffffL){
44:     printf("sbrk(-%d) failed\n", sz);
45:     exit(-1);
46:   }
47: 
48:   printf("ok\n");
49: }
50: 
51: // three processes all write COW memory.
52: // this causes more than half of physical memory
53: // to be allocated, so it also checks whether
54: // copied pages are freed.

oskernel2023-zmz@recover 源码

50: 
51: // three processes all write COW memory.
52: // this causes more than half of physical memory
53: // to be allocated, so it also checks whether
54: // copied pages are freed.
55: void
56: threetest()
57: {
58:   uint64 phys_size = PHYSTOP - KERNBASE;
59:   int sz = phys_size / 4;
60:   int pid1, pid2;
61: 
62:   printf("three: ");
63:   
64:   char *p = sbrk(sz);
65:   if(p == (char*)0xffffffffffffffffL){
66:     printf("sbrk(%d) failed\n", sz);
67:     exit(-1);
68:   }
69: 
70:   pid1 = fork();
71:   if(pid1 < 0){
72:     printf("fork failed\n");
73:     exit(-1);
74:   }
75:   if(pid1 == 0){
76:     pid2 = fork();
77:     if(pid2 < 0){
78:       printf("fork failed");
79:       exit(-1);
80:     }
81:     if(pid2 == 0){
82:       for(char *q = p; q < p + (sz/5)*4; q += 4096){
83:         *(int*)q = getpid();
84:       }
85:       for(char *q = p; q < p + (sz/5)*4; q += 4096){
86:         if(*(int*)q != getpid()){
87:           printf("wrong content\n");
88:           exit(-1);
89:         }
90:       }
91:       exit(-1);
92:     }
93:     for(char *q = p; q < p + (sz/2); q += 4096){
94:       *(int*)q = 9999;
95:     }
96:     exit(0);
97:   }
98: 
99:   for(char *q = p; q < p + sz; q += 4096){
100:     *(int*)q = getpid();
101:   }
102: 
103:   wait(0);
104: 
105:   sleep(1);
106: 
107:   for(char *q = p; q < p + sz; q += 4096){
108:     if(*(int*)q != getpid()){
109:       printf("wrong content\n");
110:       exit(-1);
111:     }
112:   }
113: 
114:   if(sbrk(-sz) == (char*)0xffffffffffffffffL){
115:     printf("sbrk(-%d) failed\n", sz);
116:     exit(-1);
117:   }
118: 
119:   printf("ok\n");
120: }
121: 
122: char junk1[4096];
123: int fds[2];
124: char junk2[4096];
125: char buf[4096];

xv6-k210@final / 来源候选源码

124: char junk2[4096];
125: char buf[4096];
126: char junk3[4096];
127: 
128: // test whether copyout() simulates COW faults.
129: void
130: filetest()
131: {
132:   printf("file: ");
133:   
134:   buf[0] = 99;
135: 
136:   for(int i = 0; i < 4; i++){
137:     if(pipe(fds) != 0){
138:       printf("pipe() failed\n");
139:       exit(-1);
140:     }
141:     int pid = fork();
142:     if(pid < 0){
143:       printf("fork failed\n");
144:       exit(-1);
145:     }
146:     if(pid == 0){
147:       sleep(1);
148:       if(read(fds[0], buf, sizeof(i)) != sizeof(i)){
149:         printf("error: read failed\n");
150:         exit(1);
151:       }
152:       sleep(1);
153:       int j = *(int*)buf;
154:       if(j != i){
155:         printf("error: read the wrong value\n");
156:         exit(1);
157:       }
158:       exit(0);
159:     }
160:     if(write(fds[1], &i, sizeof(i)) != sizeof(i)){
161:       printf("error: write failed\n");
162:       exit(-1);
163:     }
164:   }
165: 
166:   int xstatus = 0;
167:   for(int i = 0; i < 4; i++) {
168:     wait(&xstatus);
169:     if(xstatus != 0) {
170:       exit(1);
171:     }
172:   }
173: 
174:   if(buf[0] != 99){
175:     printf("error: child overwrote parent\n");
176:     exit(1);
177:   }
178: 
179:   printf("ok\n");
180: }
181: 
182: int
183: main(int argc, char *argv[])
184: {
185:   simpletest();

oskernel2023-zmz@recover 源码

1: #include "user.h"
2: 
3: int main(int argc, char *argv[])
4: {
5:     int fd;
6:     
7:     if (argc <= 1) {
8:         // fd = dup3(1, 100, 0);
9:         fd = dup3(1, 100, FD_CLOEXEC);
10:         if (fd < 0) {
11:             fprintf(2, "dup3 fail!\n");
12:             exit(1);
13:         }
14:         if (write(fd, "dup3 success!\n", 15) < 0) {
15:             fprintf(2, "dup3 write to fd 100 fail!\n");
16:             exit(1);
17:         }
18:         fprintf(fd, "write to fd 100, get %d\n", fd);
19:         fprintf(fd, "now test cloexe\n");
20: 
21:         char *argvs[] = { "./bin/dup3", "100" };
22:         exec("./bin/dup3", argvs);
23:         fprintf(2, "exec fail!\n");
24:         exit(1);
25:     }
26:     else {
27:         fd = atoi(argv[1]);
28:         printf("get fd %d\n", fd);
29:         if (write(fd, "is this fd still exists?\n", 26) >= 0) {
30:             fprintf(2, "fd %d still exists!\n", fd);
31:         }
32:         else {
33:             printf("fail to write, OK\n");
34:         }
35:     }
36:     exit(0);
37: }

xv6-k210@final / 来源候选源码

1: #include "types.h"
2: #include "fs/stat.h"
3: #include "user.h"
4: 
5: int
6: main(int argc, char *argv[])
7: {
8:   int i;
9: 
10:   for(i = 1; i < argc; i++){
11:     write(1, argv[i], strlen(argv[i]));
12:     if(i + 1 < argc){
13:       write(1, " ", 1);
14:     } else {
15:       write(1, "\n", 1);
16:     }
17:   }
18:   exit(0);
19: }

3: #include "fs/fcntl.h"
4: #include "user.h"
5: 
6: static char path[512];
7: 
8: void find(char *filename)
9: {
10:     int fd;
11:     struct kstat st;
12:     if ((fd = open(path, O_RDONLY)) < 0) {
13:         fprintf(2, "find: cannot open %s\n", path);
14:         return;
15:     }
16:     if (fstat(fd, &st) < 0) {
17:         fprintf(2, "find: cannot stat %s\n", path);
18:         close(fd);
19:         return;
20:     }
21:     if (!(st.mode & ST_MODE_DIR)) {
22:         close(fd);
23:         return;
24:     }
25:     int len = strlen(path);
26:     if (len + 255 + 2 > sizeof(path)) {
27:         fprintf(2, "find: path too long\n");
28:         close(fd);
29:         return;
30:     }
31:     char *p = path + len - 1;
32:     if (*p != '/') {
33:         *++p = '/';
34:     }
35:     p++;
36:     char buf[128];
37:     int ret;
38:     while ((ret = getdents(fd, (struct dirent *)buf, sizeof(buf))) > 0) {
39:         struct dirent *entry;
40:         for (int i = 0; i < ret; i += entry->reclen) {
41:             entry = (struct dirent *)(buf + i);
42:             strcpy(p, entry->name);
43:             if (strcmp(p, ".") == 0 || strcmp(p, "..") == 0) {
44:                 continue;
45:             }
46:             if (strcmp(p, filename) == 0) {
47:                 fprintf(1, "%s\n", path);
48:             }
49:             if (entry->type == T_DIR) {
50:                 find(filename);
51:             }
52:         }
53:     }
54:     close(fd);
55:     return;
56: }
57: 
58: 
59: int main(int argc, char *argv[])
60: {
61:     if (argc < 3) {

16: void
17: forktest(void)
18: {
19:   int n, pid;
20: 
21:   print("fork test\n");
22: 
23:   for(n=0; n<N; n++){
24:     pid = fork();
25:     if(pid < 0)
26:       break;
27:     if(pid == 0)
28:       exit(0);
29:   }
30: 
31:   if(n == N){
32:     print("fork claimed to work N times!\n");
33:     exit(1);
34:   }
35: 
36:   for(; n > 0; n--){
37:     if(wait(0) < 0){
38:       print("wait stopped early\n");
39:       exit(1);
40:     }
41:   }
42: 
43:   if(wait(0) != -1){
44:     print("wait got too many\n");
45:     exit(1);
46:   }
47: 
48:   print("fork test OK\n");
49: }
50: 
51: int
52: main(void)
53: {
54:   forktest();

15: 
16: void
17: forktest(void)
18: {
19:   int n, pid;
20: 
21:   print("fork test\n");
22: 
23:   for(n=0; n<N; n++){
24:     pid = fork();
25:     if(pid < 0)
26:       break;
27:     if(pid == 0)
28:       exit(0);
29:   }
30: 
31:   if(n == N){
32:     print("fork claimed to work N times!\n");
33:     exit(1);
34:   }
35: 
36:   for(; n > 0; n--){
37:     if(wait(0) < 0){
38:       print("wait stopped early\n");
39:       exit(1);
40:     }
41:   }
42: 
43:   if(wait(0) != -1){
44:     print("wait got too many\n");
45:     exit(1);
46:   }
47: 
48:   print("fork test OK\n");
49: }
50: 
51: int
52: main(void)

10: void
11: grep(char *pattern, int fd)
12: {
13:   int n, m;
14:   char *p, *q;
15: 
16:   m = 0;
17:   while((n = read(fd, buf+m, sizeof(buf)-m-1)) > 0){
18:     m += n;
19:     buf[m] = '\0';
20:     p = buf;
21:     while((q = strchr(p, '\n')) != 0){
22:       *q = 0;
23:       if(match(pattern, p)){
24:         *q = '\n';
25:         write(1, p, q+1 - p);
26:       }
27:       p = q+1;
28:     }
29:     if(m > 0){
30:       m -= p - buf;
31:       memmove(buf, p, m);
32:     }
33:   }
34: }

82: // matchhere: search for re at beginning of text
83: int matchhere(char *re, char *text)
84: {
85:   if(re[0] == '\0')
86:     return 1;
87:   if(re[1] == '*')
88:     return matchstar(re[0], re+2, text);
89:   if(re[0] == '$' && re[1] == '\0')
90:     return *text == '\0';
91:   if(*text!='\0' && (re[0]=='.' || re[0]==*text))
92:     return matchhere(re+1, text+1);
93:   return 0;
94: }
95: 
96: // matchstar: search for c*re at beginning of text
97: int matchstar(int c, char *re, char *text)
98: {
99:   do{  // a * matches zero or more instances

匹配分数 1.000 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": true, "exact_token_fp": true, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 1.0}

oskernel2023-zmz@recover 源码

10: #include "syscall.h"
11: #include "memlayout.h"
12: #include "hal/riscv.h"
13: 
14: // from FreeBSD.
15: int
16: do_rand(unsigned long *ctx)
17: {
18: /*
19:  * Compute x = (7^5 * x) mod (2^31 - 1)
20:  * without overflowing 31 bits:
21:  *      (2^31 - 1) = 127773 * (7^5) + 2836
22:  * From "Random number generators: good ones are hard to find",
23:  * Park and Miller, Communications of the ACM, vol. 31, no. 10,
24:  * October 1988, p. 1195.
25:  */
26:     long hi, lo, x;
27: 
28:     /* Transform to [1, 0x7ffffffe] range. */
29:     x = (*ctx % 0x7ffffffe) + 1;
30:     hi = x / 127773;
31:     lo = x % 127773;
32:     x = 16807 * lo - 2836 * hi;
33:     if (x < 0)
34:         x += 0x7fffffff;
35:     /* Transform to [0, 0x7ffffffd] range. */
36:     x--;
37:     *ctx = x;
38:     return (x);
39: }
40: 
41: unsigned long rand_next = 1;
42: 
43: int
44: rand(void)

xv6-k210@final / 来源候选源码

10: #include "syscall.h"
11: #include "memlayout.h"
12: #include "hal/riscv.h"
13: 
14: // from FreeBSD.
15: int
16: do_rand(unsigned long *ctx)
17: {
18: /*
19:  * Compute x = (7^5 * x) mod (2^31 - 1)
20:  * without overflowing 31 bits:
21:  *      (2^31 - 1) = 127773 * (7^5) + 2836
22:  * From "Random number generators: good ones are hard to find",
23:  * Park and Miller, Communications of the ACM, vol. 31, no. 10,
24:  * October 1988, p. 1195.
25:  */
26:     long hi, lo, x;
27: 
28:     /* Transform to [1, 0x7ffffffe] range. */
29:     x = (*ctx % 0x7ffffffe) + 1;
30:     hi = x / 127773;
31:     lo = x % 127773;
32:     x = 16807 * lo - 2836 * hi;
33:     if (x < 0)
34:         x += 0x7fffffff;
35:     /* Transform to [0, 0x7ffffffd] range. */
36:     x--;
37:     *ctx = x;
38:     return (x);
39: }
40: 
41: unsigned long rand_next = 1;
42: 
43: int
44: rand(void)

48: 
49: void
50: go(int which_child)
51: {
52:   int fd = -1;
53:   static char buf[999];
54:   char *break0 = sbrk(0);
55:   uint64 iters = 0;
56: 
57:   mkdir("grindir");
58:   if(chdir("grindir") != 0){
59:     printf("chdir grindir failed\n");
60:     exit(1);
61:   }
62:   chdir("/");
63:   
64:   while(1){
65:     iters++;
66:     if((iters % 500) == 0)
67:       write(1, which_child?"B":"A", 1);
68:     int what = rand() % 23;
69:     if(what == 1){
70:       close(open("grindir/../a", O_CREATE|O_RDWR));
71:     } else if(what == 2){
72:       close(open("grindir/../grindir/../b", O_CREATE|O_RDWR));
73:     } else if(what == 3){
74:       // unlink("grindir/../a");
75:       remove("grindir/../a");
76:     } else if(what == 4){
77:       if(chdir("grindir") != 0){
78:         printf("chdir grindir failed\n");
79:         exit(1);
80:       }
81:       // unlink("../b");
82:       remove("../b");
83:       chdir("/");
84:     } else if(what == 5){
85:       close(fd);
86:       fd = open("/grindir/../a", O_CREATE|O_RDWR);
87:     } else if(what == 6){
88:       close(fd);
89:       fd = open("/./grindir/./../b", O_CREATE|O_RDWR);
90:     } else if(what == 7){
91:       write(fd, buf, sizeof(buf));
92:     } else if(what == 8){
93:       read(fd, buf, sizeof(buf));
94:     } else if(what == 9){
95:       mkdir("grindir/../a");
96:       close(open("a/../a/./a", O_CREATE|O_RDWR));
97:       // unlink("a/a");
98:       remove("a/a");
99:     } else if(what == 10){
100:       mkdir("/../b");
101:       close(open("grindir/../b/b", O_CREATE|O_RDWR));
102:       // unlink("b/b");
103:       remove("b/b");
104:     } else if(what == 11){
105:       // unlink("b");
106:       // link("../grindir/./../a", "../b");
107:     } else if(what == 12){
108:       // unlink("../grindir/../a");
109:       // link(".././b", "/grindir/../a");
110:     } else if(what == 13){
111:       int pid = fork();
112:       if(pid == 0){
113:         exit(0);
114:       } else if(pid < 0){
115:         printf("grind: fork failed\n");
116:         exit(1);
117:       }
118:       wait(0);
119:     } else if(what == 14){
120:       int pid = fork();
121:       if(pid == 0){
122:         fork();
123:         fork();
124:         exit(0);
125:       } else if(pid < 0){
126:         printf("grind: fork failed\n");
127:         exit(1);
128:       }
129:       wait(0);
130:     } else if(what == 15){
131:       sbrk(6011);
132:     } else if(what == 16){
133:       if(sbrk(0) > break0)
134:         sbrk(-(sbrk(0) - break0));
135:     } else if(what == 17){
136:       int pid = fork();
137:       if(pid == 0){
138:         close(open("a", O_CREATE|O_RDWR));
139:         exit(0);
140:       } else if(pid < 0){
141:         printf("grind: fork failed\n");
142:         exit(1);
143:       }
144:       if(chdir("../grindir/..") != 0){
145:         printf("chdir failed\n");
146:         exit(1);
147:       }
148:       kill(pid, SIGTERM);
149:       wait(0);
150:     } else if(what == 18){
151:       int pid = fork();
152:       if(pid == 0){
153:         kill(getpid(), SIGTERM);
154:         exit(0);
155:       } else if(pid < 0){
156:         printf("grind: fork failed\n");
157:         exit(1);
158:       }
159:       wait(0);
160:     } else if(what == 19){
161:       int fds[2];
162:       if(pipe(fds) < 0){
163:         printf("grind: pipe failed\n");
164:         exit(1);
165:       }
166:       int pid = fork();
167:       if(pid == 0){
168:         fork();
169:         fork();
170:         if(write(fds[1], "x", 1) != 1)
171:           printf("grind: pipe write failed\n");
172:         char c;
173:         if(read(fds[0], &c, 1) != 1)
174:           printf("grind: pipe read failed\n");
175:         exit(0);
176:       } else if(pid < 0){
177:         printf("grind: fork failed\n");
178:         exit(1);
179:       }
180:       close(fds[0]);
181:       close(fds[1]);
182:       wait(0);
183:     } else if(what == 20){
184:       int pid = fork();
185:       if(pid == 0){
186:         // unlink("a");
187:         remove("a");
188:         mkdir("a");
189:         chdir("a");
190:         // unlink("../a");
191:         remove("../a");
192:         fd = open("x", O_CREATE|O_RDWR);
193:         // unlink("x");
194:         remove("x");
195:         exit(0);
196:       } else if(pid < 0){
197:         printf("fork failed\n");
198:         exit(1);
199:       }
200:       wait(0);
201:     } else if(what == 21){
202:       // unlink("c");
203:       remove("c");
204:       // should always succeed. check that there are free i-nodes,
205:       // file descriptors, blocks.
206:       int fd1 = open("c", O_CREATE|O_RDWR);
207:       if(fd1 < 0){
208:         printf("create c failed\n");
209:         exit(1);
210:       }
211:       if(write(fd1, "x", 1) != 1){
212:         printf("write c failed\n");
213:         exit(1);
214:       }
215:       struct kstat st;
216:       if(fstat(fd1, &st) != 0){
217:         printf("fstat failed\n");
218:         exit(1);
219:       }
220:       if(st.size != 1){
221:         printf("fstat reports wrong size %d\n", (int)st.size);
222:         exit(1);
223:       }
224:       // if(st.ino > 200){
225:       //   printf("fstat reports crazy i-number %d\n", st.ino);
226:       //   exit(1);
227:       // }
228:       close(fd1);
229:       // unlink("c");
230:       remove("c");
231:     } else if(what == 22){
232:       // echo hi | cat
233:       int aa[2], bb[2];
234:       if(pipe(aa) < 0){
235:         fprintf(2, "pipe failed\n");
236:         exit(1);
237:       }
238:       if(pipe(bb) < 0){
239:         fprintf(2, "pipe failed\n");
240:         exit(1);
241:       }
242:       int pid1 = fork();
243:       if(pid1 == 0){
244:         close(bb[0]);
245:         close(bb[1]);
246:         close(aa[0]);
247:         close(1);
248:         if(dup(aa[1]) != 1){
249:           fprintf(2, "dup failed\n");
250:           exit(1);
251:         }
252:         close(aa[1]);
253:         char *args[3] = { "echo", "hi", 0 };
254:         exec("grindir/../echo", args);
255:         fprintf(2, "echo: not found\n");
256:         exit(2);
257:       } else if(pid1 < 0){
258:         fprintf(2, "fork failed\n");
259:         exit(3);
260:       }
261:       int pid2 = fork();
262:       if(pid2 == 0){
263:         close(aa[1]);
264:         close(bb[0]);
265:         close(0);
266:         if(dup(aa[0]) != 0){
267:           fprintf(2, "dup failed\n");
268:           exit(4);
269:         }
270:         close(aa[0]);
271:         close(1);
272:         if(dup(bb[1]) != 1){
273:           fprintf(2, "dup failed\n");
274:           exit(5);
275:         }
276:         close(bb[1]);
277:         char *args[2] = { "cat", 0 };
278:         exec("/cat", args);
279:         fprintf(2, "cat: not found\n");
280:         exit(6);
281:       } else if(pid2 < 0){
282:         fprintf(2, "fork failed\n");
283:         exit(7);
284:       }
285:       close(aa[0]);
286:       close(aa[1]);
287:       close(bb[1]);
288:       char buf[3] = { 0, 0, 0 };
289:       read(bb[0], buf+0, 1);
290:       read(bb[0], buf+1, 1);
291:       close(bb[0]);
292:       int st1, st2;
293:       wait(&st1);
294:       wait(&st2);
295:       if(st1 != 0 || st2 != 0 || strcmp(buf, "hi") != 0){
296:         printf("exec pipeline failed %d %d \"%s\"\n", st1, st2, buf);
297:         exit(1);
298:       }
299:     }
300:   }
301: }

49: void
50: go(int which_child)
51: {
52:   int fd = -1;
53:   static char buf[999];
54:   char *break0 = sbrk(0);
55:   uint64 iters = 0;
56: 
57:   mkdir("grindir");
58:   if(chdir("grindir") != 0){
59:     printf("chdir grindir failed\n");
60:     exit(1);
61:   }
62:   chdir("/");
63:   
64:   while(1){
65:     iters++;
66:     if((iters % 500) == 0)
67:       write(1, which_child?"B":"A", 1);
68:     int what = rand() % 23;
69:     if(what == 1){
70:       close(open("grindir/../a", O_CREATE|O_RDWR));
71:     } else if(what == 2){
72:       close(open("grindir/../grindir/../b", O_CREATE|O_RDWR));
73:     } else if(what == 3){
74:       // unlink("grindir/../a");
75:       remove("grindir/../a");
76:     } else if(what == 4){
77:       if(chdir("grindir") != 0){
78:         printf("chdir grindir failed\n");
79:         exit(1);
80:       }
81:       // unlink("../b");
82:       remove("../b");
83:       chdir("/");
84:     } else if(what == 5){
85:       close(fd);
86:       fd = open("/grindir/../a", O_CREATE|O_RDWR);
87:     } else if(what == 6){
88:       close(fd);
89:       fd = open("/./grindir/./../b", O_CREATE|O_RDWR);
90:     } else if(what == 7){
91:       write(fd, buf, sizeof(buf));
92:     } else if(what == 8){
93:       read(fd, buf, sizeof(buf));
94:     } else if(what == 9){
95:       mkdir("grindir/../a");
96:       close(open("a/../a/./a", O_CREATE|O_RDWR));
97:       // unlink("a/a");
98:       remove("a/a");
99:     } else if(what == 10){
100:       mkdir("/../b");
101:       close(open("grindir/../b/b", O_CREATE|O_RDWR));
102:       // unlink("b/b");
103:       remove("b/b");
104:     } else if(what == 11){
105:       // unlink("b");
106:       // link("../grindir/./../a", "../b");
107:     } else if(what == 12){
108:       // unlink("../grindir/../a");
109:       // link(".././b", "/grindir/../a");
110:     } else if(what == 13){
111:       int pid = fork();
112:       if(pid == 0){
113:         exit(0);
114:       } else if(pid < 0){
115:         printf("grind: fork failed\n");
116:         exit(1);
117:       }
118:       wait(0);
119:     } else if(what == 14){
120:       int pid = fork();
121:       if(pid == 0){
122:         fork();
123:         fork();
124:         exit(0);
125:       } else if(pid < 0){
126:         printf("grind: fork failed\n");
127:         exit(1);
128:       }
129:       wait(0);
130:     } else if(what == 15){
131:       sbrk(6011);
132:     } else if(what == 16){
133:       if(sbrk(0) > break0)
134:         sbrk(-(sbrk(0) - break0));
135:     } else if(what == 17){
136:       int pid = fork();
137:       if(pid == 0){
138:         close(open("a", O_CREATE|O_RDWR));
139:         exit(0);
140:       } else if(pid < 0){
141:         printf("grind: fork failed\n");
142:         exit(1);
143:       }
144:       if(chdir("../grindir/..") != 0){
145:         printf("chdir failed\n");
146:         exit(1);
147:       }
148:       kill(pid, SIGTERM);
149:       wait(0);
150:     } else if(what == 18){
151:       int pid = fork();
152:       if(pid == 0){
153:         kill(getpid(), SIGTERM);
154:         exit(0);
155:       } else if(pid < 0){
156:         printf("grind: fork failed\n");
157:         exit(1);
158:       }
159:       wait(0);
160:     } else if(what == 19){
161:       int fds[2];
162:       if(pipe(fds) < 0){
163:         printf("grind: pipe failed\n");
164:         exit(1);
165:       }
166:       int pid = fork();
167:       if(pid == 0){
168:         fork();
169:         fork();
170:         if(write(fds[1], "x", 1) != 1)
171:           printf("grind: pipe write failed\n");
172:         char c;
173:         if(read(fds[0], &c, 1) != 1)
174:           printf("grind: pipe read failed\n");
175:         exit(0);
176:       } else if(pid < 0){
177:         printf("grind: fork failed\n");
178:         exit(1);
179:       }
180:       close(fds[0]);
181:       close(fds[1]);
182:       wait(0);
183:     } else if(what == 20){
184:       int pid = fork();
185:       if(pid == 0){
186:         // unlink("a");
187:         remove("a");
188:         mkdir("a");
189:         chdir("a");
190:         // unlink("../a");
191:         remove("../a");
192:         fd = open("x", O_CREATE|O_RDWR);
193:         // unlink("x");
194:         remove("x");
195:         exit(0);
196:       } else if(pid < 0){
197:         printf("fork failed\n");
198:         exit(1);
199:       }
200:       wait(0);
201:     } else if(what == 21){
202:       // unlink("c");
203:       remove("c");
204:       // should always succeed. check that there are free i-nodes,
205:       // file descriptors, blocks.
206:       int fd1 = open("c", O_CREATE|O_RDWR);
207:       if(fd1 < 0){
208:         printf("create c failed\n");
209:         exit(1);
210:       }
211:       if(write(fd1, "x", 1) != 1){
212:         printf("write c failed\n");
213:         exit(1);
214:       }
215:       struct kstat st;
216:       if(fstat(fd1, &st) != 0){
217:         printf("fstat failed\n");
218:         exit(1);
219:       }
220:       if(st.size != 1){
221:         printf("fstat reports wrong size %d\n", (int)st.size);
222:         exit(1);
223:       }
224:       // if(st.ino > 200){
225:       //   printf("fstat reports crazy i-number %d\n", st.ino);
226:       //   exit(1);
227:       // }
228:       close(fd1);
229:       // unlink("c");
230:       remove("c");
231:     } else if(what == 22){
232:       // echo hi | cat
233:       int aa[2], bb[2];
234:       if(pipe(aa) < 0){
235:         fprintf(2, "pipe failed\n");
236:         exit(1);
237:       }
238:       if(pipe(bb) < 0){
239:         fprintf(2, "pipe failed\n");
240:         exit(1);
241:       }
242:       int pid1 = fork();
243:       if(pid1 == 0){
244:         close(bb[0]);
245:         close(bb[1]);
246:         close(aa[0]);
247:         close(1);
248:         if(dup(aa[1]) != 1){
249:           fprintf(2, "dup failed\n");
250:           exit(1);
251:         }
252:         close(aa[1]);
253:         char *args[3] = { "echo", "hi", 0 };
254:         exec("grindir/../echo", args);
255:         fprintf(2, "echo: not found\n");
256:         exit(2);
257:       } else if(pid1 < 0){
258:         fprintf(2, "fork failed\n");
259:         exit(3);
260:       }
261:       int pid2 = fork();
262:       if(pid2 == 0){
263:         close(aa[1]);
264:         close(bb[0]);
265:         close(0);
266:         if(dup(aa[0]) != 0){
267:           fprintf(2, "dup failed\n");
268:           exit(4);
269:         }
270:         close(aa[0]);
271:         close(1);
272:         if(dup(bb[1]) != 1){
273:           fprintf(2, "dup failed\n");
274:           exit(5);
275:         }
276:         close(bb[1]);
277:         char *args[2] = { "cat", 0 };
278:         exec("/cat", args);
279:         fprintf(2, "cat: not found\n");
280:         exit(6);
281:       } else if(pid2 < 0){
282:         fprintf(2, "fork failed\n");
283:         exit(7);
284:       }
285:       close(aa[0]);
286:       close(aa[1]);
287:       close(bb[1]);
288:       char buf[3] = { 0, 0, 0 };
289:       read(bb[0], buf+0, 1);
290:       read(bb[0], buf+1, 1);
291:       close(bb[0]);
292:       int st1, st2;
293:       wait(&st1);
294:       wait(&st2);
295:       if(st1 != 0 || st2 != 0 || strcmp(buf, "hi") != 0){
296:         printf("exec pipeline failed %d %d \"%s\"\n", st1, st2, buf);
297:         exit(1);
298:       }
299:     }
300:   }

11: int
12: main(void)
13: {
14:   int pid, wpid;
15: 
16:   // if(open("console", O_RDWR) < 0){
17:   //   mknod("console", CONSOLE, 0);
18:   //   open("console", O_RDWR);
19:   // }
20:   // dev(O_RDWR, CONSOLE, 0);
21:   open("/dev/console", O_RDWR);
22:   dup(0);  // stdout

24: 
25:   for(;;){
26:     printf("init: starting sh\n");
27:     pid = fork();

29:       printf("init: fork failed\n");
30:       exit(1);
31:     }
32:     if(pid == 0){
33:       exec("sh", argv);
34:       printf("init: exec sh failed\n");
35:       exit(1);
36:     }
37: 
38:     for(;;){
39:       // this call to wait() returns if the shell exits,
40:       // or if a parentless process exits.
41:       wpid = wait((int *) 0);
42:       if(wpid == pid){
43:         // the shell exited; restart it.
44:         break;
45:       } else if(wpid < 0){
46:         printf("init: wait returned an error\n");
47:         exit(1);
48:       } else {
49:         // it was a parentless process; do nothing.
50:       }
51:     }
52:   }
53: }

8: #include "memlayout.h"
9: #include "hal/riscv.h"
10: 
11: #define REGION_SZ (1024 * 1024 * 1024)
12: 
13: void
14: sparse_memory(char *s)
15: {
16:   char *i, *prev_end, *new_end;
17:   
18:   prev_end = sbrk(REGION_SZ);
19:   if (prev_end == (char*)0xffffffffffffffffL) {
20:     printf("sbrk() failed\n");
21:     exit(1);
22:   }
23:   new_end = prev_end + REGION_SZ;
24: 
25:   for (i = prev_end + PGSIZE; i < new_end; i += 1024 * PGSIZE)
26:     *(char **)i = i;
27: 
28:   for (i = prev_end + PGSIZE; i < new_end; i += 1024 * PGSIZE) {
29:     if (*(char **)i != i) {
30:       printf("failed to read value from memory\n");
31:       exit(1);
32:     }
33:   }
34: 
35:   exit(0);
36: }
37: 
38: void
39: sparse_memory_unmap(char *s)
40: {
41:   int pid;

33:   }

38: void
39: sparse_memory_unmap(char *s)
40: {
41:   int pid;
42:   char *i, *prev_end, *new_end;
43: 
44:   prev_end = sbrk(REGION_SZ);
45:   if (prev_end == (char*)0xffffffffffffffffL) {
46:     printf("sbrk() failed\n");
47:     exit(1);
48:   }
49:   new_end = prev_end + REGION_SZ;
50: 
51:   for (i = prev_end + PGSIZE; i < new_end; i += PGSIZE * PGSIZE)
52:     *(char **)i = i;
53: 
54:   for (i = prev_end + PGSIZE; i < new_end; i += PGSIZE * PGSIZE) {
55:     pid = fork();
56:     if (pid < 0) {
57:       printf("error forking\n");
58:       exit(1);
59:     } else if (pid == 0) {
60:       sbrk(-1L * REGION_SZ);
61:       *(char **)i = i;
62:       exit(0);
63:     } else {
64:       int status;
65:       wait(&status);
66:       if (status == 0) {
67:         printf("memory not unmapped\n");
68:         exit(1);
69:       }
70:     }
71:   }
72: 
73:   exit(0);
74: }
75: 
76: void
77: oom(char *s)
78: {
79:   void *m1, *m2;

60:       sbrk(-1L * REGION_SZ);

62:       exit(0);
63:     } else {
64:       int status;
65:       wait(&status);
66:       if (status == 0) {
67:         printf("memory not unmapped\n");

69:       }
70:     }
71:   }

96: // run each test in its own process. run returns 1 if child's exit()
97: // indicates success.
98: int
99: run(void f(char *), char *s) {
100:   int pid;
101:   int xstatus;
102:   
103:   printf("running test %s\n", s);
104:   if((pid = fork()) < 0) {
105:     printf("runtest: fork error\n");
106:     exit(1);
107:   }
108:   if(pid == 0) {
109:     f(s);
110:     exit(0);
111:   } else {
112:     wait(&xstatus);
113:     if(xstatus != 0) 
114:       printf("test %s: FAILED\n", s);
115:     else
116:       printf("test %s: OK\n", s);
117:     return xstatus == 0;
118:   }
119: }
120: 
121: int
122: main(int argc, char *argv[])
123: {
124:   char *n = 0;

121: int
122: main(int argc, char *argv[])
123: {
124:   char *n = 0;
125:   if(argc > 1) {
126:     n = argv[1];
127:   }
128:   
129:   struct test {
130:     void (*f)(char *);
131:     char *s;
132:   } tests[] = {
133:     { sparse_memory, "lazy alloc"},
134:     { sparse_memory_unmap, "lazy unmap"},
135:     { oom, "out of memory"},
136:     { 0, 0},
137:   };
138:     
139:   printf("lazytests starting\n");
140: 
141:   int fail = 0;
142:   for (struct test *t = tests; t->s != 0; t++) {
143:     if((n == 0) || strcmp(t->s, n) == 0) {
144:       if(!run(t->f, t->s))
145:         fail = 1;
146:     }
147:   }
148:   if(!fail)
149:     printf("ALL TESTS PASSED\n");
150:   else
151:     printf("SOME TESTS FAILED\n");
152:   exit(1);   // not reached.
153: }

1: #include "types.h"
2: #include "fs/stat.h"
3: #include "user.h"
4: 
5: char *getname(char *path)
6: {
7: 	char *p = path + strlen(path) - 1;
8: 	while (p > path && *p == '/')
9: 		p--;
10: 
11: 	p[1] = '\0';
12: 	while (p >= path && *p != '/')
13: 		p--;
14: 
15: 	return p + 1;
16: }
17:

匹配分数 1.000 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": true, "exact_token_fp": true, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 1.0}

13: 		p--;

17: 
18: void
19: ls(char *path)
20: {
21: 	int fd;
22: 	struct kstat st;
23: 	char *types[] = {
24: 		[T_DIR]     "DIR ",
25: 		[T_FILE]    "FILE",
26: 		[T_DEVICE]  "DEV ",
27: 	};
28: 	char sizes[] = "BKMGT";
29: 
30: 	if((fd = open(path, 0)) < 0){
31: 		fprintf(2, "ls: cannot open %s\n", path);
32: 		return;
33: 	}
34: 
35: 	if(fstat(fd, &st) < 0){
36: 		fprintf(2, "ls: cannot stat %s\n", path);
37: 		close(fd);
38: 		return;
39: 	}
40: 
41: 	char buf[260 + 255 + 2];
42: 	if (st.mode & ST_MODE_DIR){
43: 		int len = strlen(path);
44: 		if (len >= 260) {
45: 			fprintf(2, "ls: path \"%s\" overflow\n", path);
46: 			close(fd);
47: 			return;
48: 		}
49: 		strcpy(buf, path);
50: 		buf[len] = '/';
51: 		char *p = buf + len + 1;
52: 		
53: 		char dents[512];
54: 		int ret = 0;
55: 		while ((ret = getdents(fd, (struct dirent *)dents, sizeof(dents))) > 0) {
56: 			for (int i = 0; i < ret;) {
57: 				struct dirent *d = (struct dirent *)(dents + i);
58: 				strcpy(p, d->name);
59: 				if (stat(buf, &st) < 0) {
60: 					fprintf(2, "ls: cannot stat %s\n", d->name);
61: 				} else {
62: 					int szno = 0;
63: 					while (st.size >= 1024) {
64: 						st.size /= 1024;
65: 						szno++;
66: 					}
67: 					printf("%x\t%d\t%s\t%l%c\t%s\n", st.dev, st.ino, types[d->type], st.size, sizes[szno], d->name);
68: 				}
69: 				i += d->reclen;
70: 			}
71: 		}
72: 	} else {
73: 		int szno = 0;
74: 		while (st.size >= 1024) {
75: 			st.size /= 1024;
76: 			szno++;
77: 		}
78: 		int t = st.rdev ? T_DEVICE : T_FILE;
79: 		printf("%x\t%d\t%s\t%l%c\t%s\n",
80: 				st.dev, st.ino, types[t], st.size, sizes[szno], getname(path));
81: 	}
82: 	close(fd);
83: }
84: 
85: int
86: main(int argc, char *argv[])
87: {
88: 	int i;

14: 
15: 	return p + 1;
16: }

19: ls(char *path)

36: 		fprintf(2, "ls: cannot stat %s\n", path);
37: 		close(fd);
38: 		return;
39: 	}
40: 
41: 	char buf[260 + 255 + 2];
42: 	if (st.mode & ST_MODE_DIR){
43: 		int len = strlen(path);
44: 		if (len >= 260) {
45: 			fprintf(2, "ls: path \"%s\" overflow\n", path);
46: 			close(fd);
47: 			return;
48: 		}
49: 		strcpy(buf, path);
50: 		buf[len] = '/';
51: 		char *p = buf + len + 1;

54: 		int ret = 0;
55: 		while ((ret = getdents(fd, (struct dirent *)dents, sizeof(dents))) > 0) {
56: 			for (int i = 0; i < ret;) {
57: 				struct dirent *d = (struct dirent *)(dents + i);
58: 				strcpy(p, d->name);
59: 				if (stat(buf, &st) < 0) {
60: 					fprintf(2, "ls: cannot stat %s\n", d->name);
61: 				} else {
62: 					int szno = 0;
63: 					while (st.size >= 1024) {
64: 						st.size /= 1024;
65: 						szno++;
66: 					}
67: 					printf("%x\t%d\t%s\t%l%c\t%s\n", st.dev, st.ino, types[d->type], st.size, sizes[szno], d->name);
68: 				}
69: 				i += d->reclen;
70: 			}
71: 		}
72: 	} else {
73: 		int szno = 0;
74: 		while (st.size >= 1024) {
75: 			st.size /= 1024;
76: 			szno++;
77: 		}
78: 		int t = st.rdev ? T_DEVICE : T_FILE;
79: 		printf("%x\t%d\t%s\t%l%c\t%s\n",
80: 				st.dev, st.ino, types[t], st.size, sizes[szno], getname(path));
81: 	}
82: 	close(fd);
83: }
84:

86: main(int argc, char *argv[])
87: {
88: 	int i;

oskernel2023-zmz@recover 源码

1: #include "user.h"
2: 
3: int
4: countfree()
5: {
6:     int fds[2];
7:     if (pipe(fds) < 0) {
8:         printf("pipe() failed in countfree()\n");
9:         exit(1);
10:     }
11:     int pid = fork();
12:     if (pid < 0) {
13:         printf("fork failed in countfree()\n");
14:         exit(1);
15:     }
16:     if (pid == 0) {
17:         close(fds[0]);
18:         while (1) {
19:             uint64 a = (uint64) sbrk(4096);
20:             if (a == 0xffffffffffffffff)
21:                 break;
22: 
23:             // modify the memory to make sure it's really allocated.
24:             *(char *)(a + 4096 - 1) = 1;
25: 
26:             // report back one more page.
27:             if (write(fds[1], "x", 1) != 1) {
28:                 printf("write() failed in countfree()\n");
29:                 exit(1);
30:             }
31:         }
32:         exit(0);
33:     }
34: 
35:     close(fds[1]);
36:     int n = 0;
37:     while (1) {
38:         char c;
39:         int cc = read(fds[0], &c, 1);
40:         if (cc < 0) {
41:             printf("read() failed in countfree()\n");
42:             exit(1);
43:         }
44:         if (cc == 0)
45:             break;
46:         n += 1;
47:     }
48: 
49:     close(fds[0]);
50:     wait((int*)0);
51:     return n;
52: }
53: 
54: 
55: char *openmmap(char *file, uint64 *len)
56: {
57:     int fd;

55: char *openmmap(char *file, uint64 *len)
56: {
57:     int fd;
58:     struct kstat stat;
59:     char *start;
60: 
61:     if ((fd = open(file, O_RDWR)) < 0) {
62:         return NULL;
63:     }
64:     if (fstat(fd, &stat) < 0) {
65:         close(fd);
66:         return NULL;
67:     }
68:     *len = stat.size;
69:     start = mmap(NULL, stat.size, PROT_READ|PROT_WRITE, MAP_SHARED, fd, 0);
70:     close(fd);
71:     if ((int64)start < 0) {
72:         return NULL;
73:     }
74:     return start;
75: }

77: void sharewirte(char *testname)
78: {
79:     int pid;
80:     char *start;
81:     uint64 len;
82: 
83:     pid = fork();
84:     if (pid < 0) {
85:         fprintf(2, "fork fail\n");
86:         exit(1);
87:     }
88:     else if (pid == 0) {
89:         start = openmmap("README", &len);
90:         if (start == NULL) {
91:             fprintf(2, "mmap fail\n");
92:             exit(1);
93:         }
94:         for (int i = 5; i < 10; i++) {
95:             start[i] = '0' + i;
96:         }
97:         munmap(start, len);
98:     } else {
99:         start = openmmap("README", &len);
100:         if (start == NULL) {
101:             fprintf(2, "mmap fail\n");
102:             exit(1);

111:             fprintf(2, "open README fail\n");
112:             exit(1);
113:         }
114:         char buf[11];
115:         buf[10] = 0;
116:         if (read(fd, buf, 10) != 10) {
117:             fprintf(2, "read README fail\n");
118:             exit(1);
119:         }
120:         close(fd);
121:         if (strcmp(buf, "0123456789") != 0) {
122:             fprintf(2, "read unexpected: %s\n", buf);
123:             exit(1);
124:         }
125:     }
126:     exit(0);
127: }

129: void outoflen(char *testname)
130: {
131:     int fd;
132:     struct kstat stat;
133:     if ((fd = open("README", O_RDWR)) < 0) {
134:         fprintf(2, "open README fail\n");
135:         exit(1);
136:     }
137:     if (fstat(fd, &stat) < 0) {
138:         fprintf(2, "fs/stat fd %d fail\n", fd);
139:         exit(1);
140:     }
141:     char *addr;
142:     unsigned len = stat.size + 4096;
143:     addr = mmap(NULL, len, PROT_READ|PROT_WRITE, MAP_SHARED, fd, 0);
144:     if ((int64)addr < 0) {
145:         fprintf(2, "mmap out of file size fail!\n");
146:         exit(1);
147:     }
148:     if (fork() == 0) {
149:         int data = addr[len];
150:         printf("get %d\n", data);
151:         exit(0);
152:     }
153:     int xstatus;
154:     wait(&xstatus);
155:     if (WEXITSTATUS(xstatus) != (char)-1) {
156:         fprintf(2, "access mmap page out of file size success!\n");
157:         exit(1);
158:     }
159:     exit(0);
160: }
161:

162: 
163: void sharedrw(char *testname)
164: {
165:     int pid;
166:     char *start;
167:     uint64 len;
168:     char ch = '@';
169: 
170:     pid = fork();
171:     if (pid < 0) {
172:         fprintf(2, "fork fail\n");
173:         exit(1);
174:     }
175:     else if (pid == 0) {
176:         start = openmmap("README", &len);
177:         if (start == NULL) {
178:             fprintf(2, "mmap fail\n");
179:             exit(1);
180:         }
181:         start[0] = ch;
182:         munmap(start, len);
183:     } else {
184:         start = openmmap("README", &len);
185:         if (start == NULL) {
186:             fprintf(2, "mmap fail\n");
187:             exit(1);
188:         }
189:         int count = 10;
190:         while (count-- && start[0] != ch) {
191:             sleep(3);
192:         }
193:         // printf("get %c\n", start[0]);
194:         munmap(start, len);
195:     }
196:     exit(0);
197: }
198: 
199: 
200: void wrongprot(char *testname)

163: void sharedrw(char *testname)
164: {
165:     int pid;
166:     char *start;
167:     uint64 len;
168:     char ch = '@';
169: 
170:     pid = fork();
171:     if (pid < 0) {
172:         fprintf(2, "fork fail\n");
173:         exit(1);
174:     }
175:     else if (pid == 0) {
176:         start = openmmap("README", &len);
177:         if (start == NULL) {
178:             fprintf(2, "mmap fail\n");
179:             exit(1);
180:         }
181:         start[0] = ch;
182:         munmap(start, len);
183:     } else {
184:         start = openmmap("README", &len);
185:         if (start == NULL) {
186:             fprintf(2, "mmap fail\n");
187:             exit(1);
188:         }
189:         int count = 10;
190:         while (count-- && start[0] != ch) {
191:             sleep(3);
192:         }
193:         // printf("get %c\n", start[0]);
194:         munmap(start, len);
195:     }
196:     exit(0);
197: }

200: void wrongprot(char *testname)
201: {
202:     int fd;
203:     if ((fd = open("README", O_RDONLY)) < 0) {
204:         fprintf(2, "open README fail\n");
205:         exit(1);
206:     }
207:     char *addr;
208:     addr = mmap(NULL, 1, PROT_READ|PROT_WRITE, MAP_SHARED, fd, 0);
209:     if ((int64)addr >= 0) {
210:         fprintf(2, "writable-map on read-only fd success!\n");
211:         exit(1);
212:     }
213:     addr = mmap(NULL, 1, PROT_READ, MAP_SHARED, fd, 0);
214:     if ((int64)addr < 0) {
215:         fprintf(2, "mmap fail\n");
216:         exit(1);
217:     }
218:     if (fork() == 0) {
219:         addr[0] = 'x'; // this should raise a page fault
220:         exit(0);
221:     }
222:     int xstatus;
223:     wait(&xstatus);
224:     if (WEXITSTATUS(xstatus) != (char)-1) {
225:         fprintf(2, "write to read-only mmap success!\n");
226:         exit(1);
227:     }
228:     exit(0);
229: }

231: void anonsharedrw(char *testname)
232: {
233:     int pid, i;
234:     char *addr;
235:     addr = mmap(NULL, 0x2000, PROT_READ|PROT_WRITE, MAP_SHARED|MAP_ANONYMOUS, -1, 0);
236:     if ((int64)addr < 0) {
237:         fprintf(2, "mmap fail\n");
238:         exit(1);
239:     }
240:     pid = fork();
241:     if (pid < 0) {
242:         fprintf(2, "%s: fork fail!\n", testname);
243:         exit(1);
244:     }
245:     if (pid == 0) {
246:         /**
247:          * The child writes to the second page, where the parent
248:          * did not touch (not mapped). Then the child exit()s,
249:          * to see if the page still remains to let the parent read.
250:          */
251:         for (i = 0; i < 128; i++)
252:             addr[i + 0x1000] = i;
253:         
254:         sleep(5);
255:         for (i = 0; i < 128; i++)
256:             if (addr[i] != i) {
257:                 fprintf(2, "can't read what the parent wrote!\n");
258:                 exit(1);
259:             }
260:         
261:         munmap(addr, 0x2000);   // to see if this affect the parent
262:         exit(0);
263:     } else {
264:         for (i = 0; i < 128; i++)
265:             addr[i] = i;
266:         wait(NULL);
267:         for (i = 0; i < 128; i++)
268:             if (addr[i + 0x1000] != i) {
269:                 fprintf(2, "can't read what the child wrote!\n");
270:                 exit(1);
271:             }
272:     }
273: }
274:

276: {
277:     int pid, fd, i;
278:     char *addr, ch;

227:     }
228:     exit(0);
229: }

275: void shareprivate(char *testname)
276: {
277:     int pid, fd, i;
278:     char *addr, ch;
279: 
280:     fd = open("README", O_RDWR);
281:     if (fd < 0) {
282:         fprintf(2, "%s: can't open file\n", testname);
283:         exit(1);
284:     }
285: 
286:     pid = fork();
287:     if (pid < 0) {
288:         fprintf(2, "%s: fork fail!\n", testname);
289:         exit(1);
290:     }
291: 
292:     if (pid == 0) {
293:         pid = fork();
294:         if (pid < 0) {
295:             fprintf(2, "%s: fork fail!\n", testname);
296:             exit(1);
297:         }
298: 
299:         addr = mmap(NULL, 0x1000, PROT_WRITE|PROT_READ, MAP_SHARED, fd, 0);
300:         if ((int64)addr < 0) {
301:             fprintf(2, "mmap fail\n");
302:             exit(1);
303:         }
304: 
305:         if (pid == 0) {
306:             i = 0;
307:             ch = '0';
308:         } else {
309:             i = 1;
310:             ch = '1';
311:         }
312:         for (; i < 0x1000; i += 2)      // write data
313:             addr[i] = ch;
314: 
315:         sleep(10);
316: 
317:         for (i = 0; i < 0x1000; i++) {  // check what all the procs wrote
318:             if (addr[i] != (i % 2) + '0') {
319:                 fprintf(2, "%s: read wrong data\n", testname);
320:                 exit(1);
321:             }
322:         }
323:         munmap(addr, 0x1000);
324:         if (pid > 0)
325:             wait(NULL);
326:     } else {
327:         // to see if we can steal some data from the shared mapping (we should)
328:         addr = mmap(NULL, 0x1000, PROT_WRITE|PROT_READ, MAP_PRIVATE, fd, 0);
329:         if ((int64)addr < 0) {
330:             fprintf(2, "mmap fail\n");
331:             exit(1);
332:         }
333:         sleep(5);
334:         for (i = 0; i < 0x1000; i++) {
335:             if (addr[i] != (i % 2) + '0') {
336:                 fprintf(2, "%s: can't read the right data\n", testname);
337:                 exit(1);
338:             }
339:         }
340:         // now see if we can mess up the shared mapping (we shouldn't)
341:         for (i = 0; i < 0x1000; i++)
342:             addr[i] = 'x';
343: 
344:         wait(NULL);
345: 
346:         // now the children's work should be carried to the file, try reading
347:         i = read(fd, addr, 0x1000);
348:         if (i < 0) {
349:             fprintf(2, "%s: can't read from file\n", testname);
350:             exit(1);
351:         }
352:         for (i-- ; i >= 0; i--) {
353:             if (addr[i] != (i % 2) + '0') {
354:                 fprintf(2, "%s: can't read the right data from file\n", testname);
355:                 exit(1);
356:             }
357:         }
358:     }
359:     close(fd);
360:     exit(0);
361: }
362:

364: {
365:     char *brkpoint, *addr, *old;
366:     old = brk(NULL);

271:             }
272:     }
273: }

362: 
363: void ruinheap(char *testname)
364: {
365:     char *brkpoint, *addr, *old;
366:     old = brk(NULL);
367: 
368:     addr = mmap(old, 0x1000, PROT_NONE, MAP_ANONYMOUS|MAP_PRIVATE|MAP_FIXED, -1, 0);
369:     if ((int64)addr < 0) {
370:         fprintf(2, "%s: mmap fail\n", testname);
371:         exit(1);
372:     }
373:     if (addr != old) {
374:         fprintf(2, "%s: mmap not fixed! %p against %p\n", testname, addr, old);
375:         exit(1);
376:     }
377: 
378:     // this shall fail
379:     brkpoint = brk(old + 0x1000);
380:     if (brkpoint != old) {
381:         fprintf(2, "%s: brk success! break the mmap region!\n", testname);
382:         exit(1);
383:     }
384:     
385:     int pid = fork();
386:     if (pid < 0) {
387:         fprintf(2, "%s: fork fail\n", testname);
388:         exit(1);
389:     }
390:     if (pid == 0) {
391:         printf("%s: read PROT_NONE mmap success! get %d\n", testname, addr[0]);
392:         exit(0);
393:     }
394:     int status;
395:     wait(&status);
396:     if (WEXITSTATUS(status) == 0) {
397:         fprintf(2, "%s: the child survived\n", testname);
398:         exit(1);
399:     }
400: 
401:     // take away the obstacle
402:     munmap(addr, 0x1000);
403:     addr = old + 0x1000;
404:     brkpoint = brk(addr);
405:     if (brkpoint != addr) {
406:         fprintf(2, "%s: brk fail!\n", testname);
407:         exit(1);
408:     }
409:     old[0] = 0; // can we survive?
410: 
411:     addr = mmap(old, 0x2000, PROT_NONE, MAP_ANONYMOUS|MAP_PRIVATE|MAP_FIXED, -1, 0);
412:     if ((int64)addr < 0) {
413:         fprintf(2, "%s: mmap fail\n", testname);
414:         exit(1);
415:     }
416:     if (addr != old) {
417:         fprintf(2, "%s: mmap not fixed! %p against %p\n", testname, addr, old);
418:         exit(1);
419:     }
420: 
421:     if (brk(brkpoint - 0x1000) != brkpoint ||
422:         brk(brkpoint + 0x2000) != brkpoint)
423:     {
424:         fprintf(2, "%s: heap is still alive!!\n", testname);
425:         exit(1);
426:     }
427:     exit(0);
428: }
429:

430: void mmaploop(char *testname)
431: {
432:     int fd = open("mmap_junk", O_CREATE|O_TRUNC|O_RDWR);
433:     if (fd < 0) {
434:         fprintf(2, "%s: open fail!\n", testname);
435:         exit(1);
436:     }
437: 
438:     char *addr = mmap(NULL, 0x1000, PROT_READ|PROT_WRITE, MAP_ANONYMOUS|MAP_PRIVATE, -1, 0);
439:     if ((int64)addr < 0) {
440:         fprintf(2, "%s: mmap buf fail with %d!\n", testname, (int64)addr);
441:         exit(1);
442:     }
443:     for (int i = 0; i < 128; i++) {
444:         if (write(fd, addr, 0x1000) != 0x1000) {

453:         for (int j = 0; j < 128 * 0x1000; j += 0x1000)
454:             *(addr + j) = '0';
455:         munmap(addr, 128 * 0x1000);
456:     }
457:     close(fd);
458:     unlink("mmap_junk");
459:     exit(0);
460: }

462: void mmapswap(char *testname)
463: {
464:     int fd = open("mmap_swap", O_CREATE|O_TRUNC|O_RDWR);
465:     if (fd < 0) {
466:         fprintf(2, "%s: open fail!\n", testname);
467:         exit(1);
468:     }
469: 
470:     uint64 const filesz = 8 * 1024 * 1024;    // 8M
471:     char *addr = mmap(NULL, 0x1000, PROT_READ|PROT_WRITE, MAP_ANONYMOUS|MAP_PRIVATE, -1, 0);
472:     if ((int64)addr < 0) {
473:         fprintf(2, "%s: mmap buf fail with %d!\n", testname, (int64)addr);
474:         exit(1);
475:     }
476:     for (int i = 0; i < filesz / 0x1000; i++) {
477:         if (write(fd, addr, 0x1000) != 0x1000) {
478:             fprintf(2, "%s: write fail!\n", testname);
479:             exit(1);

489:             fprintf(2, "%s: wrong data at offset %p\n", testname, j);
490:             exit(1);
491:         }
492:     munmap(addr, filesz);
493:     close(fd);
494:     unlink("mmap_swap");
495:     exit(0);

497: 
498: int run(void f(char *), char *s) {
499:     int pid;
500:     int xstatus;
501: 
502:     if ((pid = fork()) < 0) {
503:         printf("runtest: fork error\n");
504:         exit(1);
505:     }
506:     if (pid == 0) {
507:         f(s);
508:         exit(0);
509:     }
510:     printf("test %s: ", s);
511:     wait(&xstatus);
512:     if(WEXITSTATUS(xstatus) != 0) 
513:         printf("FAILED\n", s);
514:     else
515:         printf("OK\n", s);
516: 
517:     return WEXITSTATUS(xstatus) == 0;
518: }
519: 
520: 
521: int main(int argc, char *argv[]){
522:     // trace(1);
523:     char *testname = NULL;

521: int main(int argc, char *argv[]){
522:     // trace(1);
523:     char *testname = NULL;
524:     if (argc > 1)
525:         testname = argv[1];
526: 
527:     struct test {
528:         void (*f)(char *);
529:         char *s;
530:     } tests[] = {
531:         { sharewirte, "sharewirte" },
532:         { outoflen, "outoflen" },
533:         { wrongprot, "wrongprot" },
534:         { sharedrw, "sharedrw" },
535:         { anonsharedrw, "anonsharedrw" },
536:         { shareprivate, "shareprivate" },
537:         { ruinheap, "ruinheap" },
538:         { mmaploop, "mmaploop" },
539:         { mmapswap, "mmapswap" },
540:         { 0, 0 },
541:     };
542: 
543:     printf("mmaptests starting\n");
544:     int free0 = countfree();
545:     int free1 = 0;
546:     int fail = 0;
547:     for (struct test *t = tests; t->s != 0; t++) {
548:         if ((testname == 0) || strcmp(t->s, testname) == 0) {
549:             if (!run(t->f, t->s))
550:                 fail = 1;
551:         }
552:     }
553:     if (fail) {
554:         printf("SOME TESTS FAILED\n");
555:         exit(1);
556:     } else if ((free1 = countfree()) < free0) {
557:         printf("FAILED -- lost some free pages %d (out of %d)\n", free1, free0);
558:         exit(1);
559:     } else {
560:         printf("ALL TESTS PASSED\n");
561:     }
562:     exit(0);
563: }

2: #include "fs/stat.h"
3: #include "fs/fcntl.h"
4: #include "param.h"
5: #include "user.h"
6: 
7: int main(int argc, char *argv[])
8: {
9:     if (argc < 3) {
10:         fprintf(2, "Usage: mv old_name new_name\n");
11:         exit(1);
12:     }
13: 
14:     char src[MAXPATH];
15:     char dst[MAXPATH];
16:     strcpy(src, argv[1]);
17:     strcpy(dst, argv[2]);
18:     struct kstat st;
19:     if (stat(dst, &st) == 0) {
20:         if (st.mode & ST_MODE_DIR) {
21:             char *ps, *pd;
22:             for (ps = src + strlen(src) - 1; ps >= src; ps--) { // trim '/' in tail
23:                 if (*ps != '/') {
24:                     *(ps + 1) = '\0';
25:                     break;
26:                 }
27:             }
28:             for (; ps >= src && *ps != '/'; ps--);
29:             ps++;
30:             pd = dst + strlen(dst);
31:             *pd++ = '/';
32:             while (*ps) {
33:                 *pd++ = *ps++;
34:                 if (pd >= dst + MAXPATH) {
35:                     fprintf(2, "mv: fail! final dst path too long (exceed MAX=%d)!\n", MAXPATH);
36:                     exit(-1);
37:                 }
38:             }
39:         } else {
40:             fprintf(2, "mv: fail! %s exists!\n", dst);
41:             exit(-1);
42:         }
43:     }
44:     // printf("moving [%s] to [%s]\n", src, dst);
45:     if (rename(src, dst) < 0) {
46:         fprintf(2, "mv: fail to rename %s to %s!\n", src, dst);
47:         exit(-1);
48:     }
49:     exit(0);
50: }

xv6-k210@final / 来源候选源码

53: void _entry(void) {
54: 	openat(AT_FDCWD, cons, O_RDWR, 0666);
55: 	dup(0);  // stdout
56: 	dup(0);  // stderr
57: 
58: 	for (int i = 0; i < test_file_num; i ++) {
59: 		if (fork() == 0) {
60: 			exec(test_files[i], NULL);
61: 		}
62: 		else {
63: 			wait(NULL);
64: 		}
65: 	}
66: 	for (;;)
67: 		;
68: 	
69: 	exit(0);
70: }

53: void _entry(void) {
54: 	openat(AT_FDCWD, cons, O_RDWR, 0666);
55: 	dup(0);  // stdout
56: 	dup(0);  // stderr
57: 
58: 	for (int i = 0; i < test_file_num; i ++) {

50:   char buf[16];

52:   uint x;

59:     x = xx;

62:   i = 0;

67:     buf[i++] = '-';
68: 
69:   while(--i >= 0)
70:     outchar(fd, buf[i]);
71: }

73: static void
74: printptr(int fd, uint64 x) {
75:   int i;
76:   outchar(fd, '0');

79:     outchar(fd, digits[x >> (sizeof(uint64) * 8 - 4)]);
80: }

81: 
82: // Print to the given fd. Only understands %d, %x, %p, %s.
83: void
84: vprintf(int fd, const char *fmt, va_list ap)
85: {
86:   char *s;
87:   int c, i, state;
88: 
89:   state = 0;
90:   for(i = 0; fmt[i]; i++){
91:     c = fmt[i] & 0xff;
92:     if(state == 0){
93:       if(c == '%'){
94:         state = '%';
95:       } else {
96:         outchar(fd, c);
97:       }
98:     } else if(state == '%'){
99:       if(c == 'd'){
100:         printint(fd, va_arg(ap, int), 10, 1);
101:       } else if(c == 'l') {
102:         printint(fd, va_arg(ap, uint64), 10, 0);
103:       } else if(c == 'x') {
104:         printint(fd, va_arg(ap, int), 16, 0);
105:       } else if(c == 'p') {
106:         printptr(fd, va_arg(ap, uint64));
107:       } else if(c == 's'){
108:         s = va_arg(ap, char*);
109:         if(s == 0)
110:           s = "(null)";
111:         while(*s != 0){
112:           outchar(fd, *s);
113:           s++;
114:         }
115:       } else if(c == 'c'){
116:         outchar(fd, va_arg(ap, uint));
117:       } else if(c == '%'){
118:         outchar(fd, c);
119:       } else {
120:         // Unknown % sequence.  Print it to draw attention.
121:         outchar(fd, '%');
122:         outchar(fd, c);
123:       }
124:       state = 0;
125:     }
126:   }
127:   fflush(fd);
128: }
129: 
130: void
131: fprintf(int fd, const char *fmt, ...)
132: {
133:   va_list ap;

oskernel2023-zmz@recover 源码

1: #include "types.h"
2: #include "fs/stat.h"
3: #include "fs/fcntl.h"
4: #include "user.h"
5: 
6: int
7: main(int argc, char *argv[])
8: {
9:   int i;
10: 
11:   if(argc < 2){
12:     fprintf(2, "Usage: rm files...\n");
13:     exit(1);
14:   }
15: 
16:   for(i = 1; i < argc; i++){
17:     if(remove(argv[i]) < 0){
18:       fprintf(2, "rm: %s failed to delete\n", argv[i]);
19:     }
20:   }
21: 
22:   exit(0);
23: }

86: int
87: export(char *argv)
88: {
89:   if(!strcmp(argv, "-p"))
90:   { // print all the env vars
91:     if(!nenv)
92:     {
93:       printf("NO env var exported\n");
94:       return 0;
95:     }
96:     for(int i=0; i<nenv; i++)
97:       printf("export %s\n", envsc[i]);
98:     return 0;
99:   }
100:   else if(nenv == NENVS)
101:   {
102:     fprintf(2, "too many env vars\n");
103:     return -1;
104:   }
105:   char name[32], value[96];
106:   char *s = argv, *t = name;
107:   envsc[nenv] = (char*)malloc(strlen(argv)+1);
108:   strcpy(envsc[nenv], s);
109: 
110:   for(s=argv, t=name; (*t=*s++)!='='; t++)
111:     ;
112:   *t = 0;
113: 
114:   if(checkenvname(name) != ((s - argv) - 1))
115:   {
116:     fprintf(2, "Invalid NAME!\n");
117:     return -1;
118:   }
119:   for(t=value; (*t=*s); s++, t++)
120:     ;
121:   if(*--t == '/')
122:     *t = 0;
123:   
124:   strcpy(envs[nenv].name, name);
125:   strcpy(envs[nenv].value, value);
126:   nenv++;
127:   return 0;
128: }

130: int
131: replace(char *buf)
132: {
133:   char raw[100], name[32], *s, *t, *tmp;
134:   int n = 0;
135:   strcpy(raw, buf);
136:   for(s=raw, t=buf; (*t=*s); t++)
137:   {
138:     if(*s++ == '$'){
139:       tmp = name;
140:       if((*s >= 'A' && *s <= 'Z') || (*s >= 'a' && *s <= 'z') || *s == '_')
141:       {
142:         *tmp++ = *s++;
143:         while((*s >= 'A' && *s <= 'Z') || (*s >= 'a' && *s <= 'z') || (*s >= '0' && *s <= '9') || *s == '_')
144:           *tmp++ = *s++;
145:         *tmp = 0;
146:         for(int i=0; i<nenv; i++)
147:           if(!strcmp(name, envs[i].name))
148:             for(tmp=envs[i].value; (*t=*tmp); t++, tmp++)
149:               ;
150:         t--;
151:       }
152:       n++;
153:     }
154:   }
155:   return n;
156: }

匹配分数 1.000 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": true, "exact_token_fp": true, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 1.0}

154:   }

157: 
158: // Execute cmd.  Never returns.
159: void
160: runcmd(struct cmd *cmd)
161: {
162:   int p[2];
163:   struct backcmd *bcmd;
164:   struct execcmd *ecmd;
165:   struct listcmd *lcmd;
166:   struct pipecmd *pcmd;
167:   struct redircmd *rcmd;
168: 
169:   if(cmd == 0)
170:     exit(1);
171: 
172:   switch(cmd->type){
173:   default:
174:     panic("runcmd");
175: 
176:   case EXEC:
177:     ecmd = (struct execcmd*)cmd;
178:     if(ecmd->argv[0] == 0)
179:       exit(1);
180:     
181:     execve(ecmd->argv[0], ecmd->argv, envsc);
182: 
183:     int i;
184:     char env_cmd[64];
185:     for(i=0; i<nenv; i++)
186:     {
187:       char *s_tmp = env_cmd;
188:       char *d_tmp = envs[i].value;
189:       while((*s_tmp = *d_tmp++))
190:         s_tmp++;
191:       *s_tmp++ = '/';
192:       d_tmp = ecmd->argv[0];
193:       while((*s_tmp++ = *d_tmp++))
194:         ;
195: 
196:       execve(env_cmd, ecmd->argv, envsc);
197:     }
198:     fprintf(2, "exec %s failed\n", ecmd->argv[0]);
199:     break;
200: 
201:   case REDIR:
202:     rcmd = (struct redircmd*)cmd;
203:     close(rcmd->fd);
204:     if(open(rcmd->file, rcmd->mode) < 0){
205:       fprintf(2, "open %s failed\n", rcmd->file);
206:       exit(1);
207:     }
208:     runcmd(rcmd->cmd);
209:     break;
210: 
211:   case LIST:
212:     lcmd = (struct listcmd*)cmd;
213:     if(fork1() == 0)
214:       runcmd(lcmd->left);
215:     wait(0);
216:     runcmd(lcmd->right);
217:     break;
218: 
219:   case PIPE:
220:     pcmd = (struct pipecmd*)cmd;
221:     if(pipe(p) < 0)
222:       panic("pipe");
223:     if(fork1() == 0){
224:       close(1);
225:       dup(p[1]);
226:       close(p[0]);
227:       close(p[1]);
228:       runcmd(pcmd->left);
229:     }
230:     if(fork1() == 0){
231:       close(0);
232:       dup(p[0]);
233:       close(p[0]);
234:       close(p[1]);
235:       runcmd(pcmd->right);
236:     }
237:     close(p[0]);
238:     close(p[1]);
239:     wait(0);
240:     wait(0);
241:     break;
242: 
243:   case BACK:
244:     bcmd = (struct backcmd*)cmd;
245:     if(fork1() == 0)
246:       runcmd(bcmd->cmd);
247:     break;
248:   }
249:   exit(0);
250: }
251: 
252: int
253: getcmd(char *buf, int nbuf)
254: {
255:   fprintf(2, "-> %s $ ", mycwd);

xv6-k210@final / 来源候选源码

154:   }
155:   return n;
156: }
157: 
158: // Execute cmd.  Never returns.
159: void
160: runcmd(struct cmd *cmd)
161: {
162:   int p[2];
163:   struct backcmd *bcmd;
164:   struct execcmd *ecmd;
165:   struct listcmd *lcmd;
166:   struct pipecmd *pcmd;
167:   struct redircmd *rcmd;
168: 
169:   if(cmd == 0)
170:     exit(1);
171: 
172:   switch(cmd->type){
173:   default:
174:     panic("runcmd");
175: 
176:   case EXEC:
177:     ecmd = (struct execcmd*)cmd;
178:     if(ecmd->argv[0] == 0)
179:       exit(1);
180:     
181:     execve(ecmd->argv[0], ecmd->argv, envsc);

184:     char env_cmd[64];

187:       char *s_tmp = env_cmd;

189:       while((*s_tmp = *d_tmp++))
190:         s_tmp++;

194:         ;

200: 
201:   case REDIR:
202:     rcmd = (struct redircmd*)cmd;
203:     close(rcmd->fd);
204:     if(open(rcmd->file, rcmd->mode) < 0){
205:       fprintf(2, "open %s failed\n", rcmd->file);
206:       exit(1);
207:     }
208:     runcmd(rcmd->cmd);
209:     break;
210: 
211:   case LIST:
212:     lcmd = (struct listcmd*)cmd;
213:     if(fork1() == 0)
214:       runcmd(lcmd->left);
215:     wait(0);
216:     runcmd(lcmd->right);
217:     break;
218: 
219:   case PIPE:
220:     pcmd = (struct pipecmd*)cmd;
221:     if(pipe(p) < 0)
222:       panic("pipe");
223:     if(fork1() == 0){
224:       close(1);
225:       dup(p[1]);
226:       close(p[0]);
227:       close(p[1]);
228:       runcmd(pcmd->left);
229:     }
230:     if(fork1() == 0){
231:       close(0);
232:       dup(p[0]);
233:       close(p[0]);
234:       close(p[1]);
235:       runcmd(pcmd->right);
236:     }
237:     close(p[0]);
238:     close(p[1]);
239:     wait(0);
240:     wait(0);
241:     break;
242: 
243:   case BACK:
244:     bcmd = (struct backcmd*)cmd;
245:     if(fork1() == 0)
246:       runcmd(bcmd->cmd);
247:     break;
248:   }
249:   exit(0);
250: }
