279: int
280: main(void)
281: {
282:   static char buf[128];
283:   int fd;
284: 
285:   // Ensure that three file descriptors are open.
286:   while((fd = open("/dev/console", O_RDWR)) >= 0){
287:     if(fd >= 3){
288:       close(fd);
289:       break;
290:     }
291:   }
292: 
293:   // Add an embedded env var(for basic commands in shell)
294:   fd = open("shrc", O_RDONLY);
295:   if(fd < 0){
296:     fprintf(2, "sh: cannot open shrc\n");
297:   } else{
298:     while(readline(fd, buf))
299:       export(buf);
300:     close(fd);
301:   }
302: 
303:   getcwd(mycwd, sizeof(mycwd));
304:   // Read and run input commands.
305:   while(getcmd(buf, sizeof(buf)) >= 0){
306:     replace(buf);
307:     if(buf[0] == 'c' && buf[1] == 'd' && buf[2] == ' '){
308:       // Chdir must be called by the parent, not the child.
309:       buf[strlen(buf)-1] = 0;  // chop \n
310:       if(chdir(buf+3) < 0)
311:         fprintf(2, "cannot cd %s\n", buf+3);
312:       getcwd(mycwd, sizeof(mycwd));
313:     }
314:     else{
315:       struct cmd *cmd = parsecmd(buf);
316:       struct execcmd *ecmd;
317: 
318:       ecmd = (struct execcmd*)cmd;
319:       if(ecmd->argv[0] == 0) {
320:         free(cmd);
321:         continue;
322:       }
323:       else if(!strcmp(ecmd->argv[0], "export"))
324:       {
325:         // Export must be called by the parent, not the child.
326:         if(ecmd->argv[1] == NULL)
327:           fprintf(2, "Usage: export [-p] [NAME=VALUE]\n");
328:         else if(export(ecmd->argv[1]) < 0)
329:           fprintf(2, "export failed\n");
330:         free(cmd);
331:         continue;
332:       }
333:       else if(fork1() == 0) 
334:         runcmd(cmd);
335:       wait(0);
336:       free(cmd);
337:     }
338:   }
339:   exit(0);
340: }
341:

匹配分数 1.000 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": true, "exact_token_fp": true, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 1.0}

428: // Parsing
429: 
430: char whitespace[] = " \t\r\n\v";
431: char symbols[] = "<|>&;()";
432: 
433: int
434: gettoken(char **ps, char *es, char **q, char **eq)
435: {
436:   char *s;
437:   int ret;
438: 
439:   s = *ps;
440:   while(s < es && strchr(whitespace, *s))
441:     s++;
442:   if(q)
443:     *q = s;
444:   ret = *s;
445:   switch(*s){
446:   case 0:
447:     break;
448:   case '|':
449:   case '(':
450:   case ')':
451:   case ';':
452:   case '&':
453:   case '<':
454:     s++;
455:     break;
456:   case '>':
457:     s++;
458:     if(*s == '>'){
459:       ret = '+';
460:       s++;
461:     }
462:     break;
463:   default:
464:     ret = 'a';
465:     while(s < es && !strchr(whitespace, *s) && !strchr(symbols, *s))
466:       s++;
467:     break;
468:   }
469:   if(eq)
470:     *eq = s;
471: 
472:   while(s < es && strchr(whitespace, *s))
473:     s++;
474:   *ps = s;
475:   return ret;
476: }

478: int
479: peek(char **ps, char *es, char *toks)

xv6-k210@final / 来源候选源码

428: // Parsing
429: 
430: char whitespace[] = " \t\r\n\v";
431: char symbols[] = "<|>&;()";
432: 
433: int
434: gettoken(char **ps, char *es, char **q, char **eq)
435: {
436:   char *s;
437:   int ret;
438: 
439:   s = *ps;
440:   while(s < es && strchr(whitespace, *s))
441:     s++;
442:   if(q)
443:     *q = s;
444:   ret = *s;
445:   switch(*s){
446:   case 0:
447:     break;
448:   case '|':
449:   case '(':
450:   case ')':
451:   case ';':
452:   case '&':
453:   case '<':
454:     s++;
455:     break;
456:   case '>':
457:     s++;
458:     if(*s == '>'){
459:       ret = '+';
460:       s++;
461:     }
462:     break;
463:   default:
464:     ret = 'a';
465:     while(s < es && !strchr(whitespace, *s) && !strchr(symbols, *s))
466:       s++;
467:     break;
468:   }
469:   if(eq)
470:     *eq = s;
471: 
472:   while(s < es && strchr(whitespace, *s))
473:     s++;
474:   *ps = s;
475:   return ret;
476: }
477:

496: parsecmd(char *s)

505:     fprintf(2, "leftovers: %s\n", s);

512: struct cmd*
513: parseline(char **ps, char *es)
514: {
515:   struct cmd *cmd;
516: 
517:   cmd = parsepipe(ps, es);
518:   while(peek(ps, es, "&")){
519:     gettoken(ps, es, 0, 0);
520:     cmd = backcmd(cmd);
521:   }
522:   if(peek(ps, es, ";")){
523:     gettoken(ps, es, 0, 0);
524:     cmd = listcmd(cmd, parseline(ps, es));
525:   }
526:   return cmd;
527: }

542: struct cmd*
543: parseredirs(struct cmd *cmd, char **ps, char *es)
544: {
545:   int tok;
546:   char *q, *eq;
547: 
548:   while(peek(ps, es, "<>")){
549:     tok = gettoken(ps, es, 0, 0);
550:     if(gettoken(ps, es, &q, &eq) != 'a')
551:       panic("missing file for redirection");
552:     switch(tok){
553:     case '<':
554:       cmd = redircmd(cmd, q, eq, O_RDONLY, 0);
555:       break;
556:     case '>':
557:       cmd = redircmd(cmd, q, eq, O_WRONLY|O_CREATE|O_TRUNC, 1);
558:       break;
559:     case '+':  // >>
560:       cmd = redircmd(cmd, q, eq, O_WRONLY|O_CREATE|O_APPEND, 1);
561:       break;
562:     }
563:   }
564:   return cmd;
565: }

582: 
583: struct cmd*
584: parseexec(char **ps, char *es)
585: {
586:   char *q, *eq;
587:   int tok, argc;
588:   struct execcmd *cmd;
589:   struct cmd *ret;
590: 
591:   if(peek(ps, es, "("))
592:     return parseblock(ps, es);
593: 
594:   ret = execcmd();
595:   cmd = (struct execcmd*)ret;
596: 
597:   argc = 0;
598:   ret = parseredirs(ret, ps, es);
599:   while(!peek(ps, es, "|)&;")){
600:     if((tok=gettoken(ps, es, &q, &eq)) == 0)
601:       break;
602:     if(tok != 'a')
603:       panic("syntax");
604:     cmd->argv[argc] = q;
605:     cmd->eargv[argc] = eq;
606:     argc++;
607:     if(argc >= MAXARGS)
608:       panic("too many args");
609:     ret = parseredirs(ret, ps, es);
610:   }
611:   cmd->argv[argc] = 0;
612:   cmd->eargv[argc] = 0;
613:   return ret;
614: }
615:

618: nulterminate(struct cmd *cmd)

615: 
616: // NUL-terminate all the counted strings.
617: struct cmd*
618: nulterminate(struct cmd *cmd)
619: {
620:   int i;
621:   struct backcmd *bcmd;
622:   struct execcmd *ecmd;
623:   struct listcmd *lcmd;
624:   struct pipecmd *pcmd;
625:   struct redircmd *rcmd;
626: 
627:   if(cmd == 0)
628:     return 0;
629: 
630:   switch(cmd->type){
631:   case EXEC:
632:     ecmd = (struct execcmd*)cmd;
633:     for(i=0; ecmd->argv[i]; i++)
634:       *ecmd->eargv[i] = 0;
635:     break;
636: 
637:   case REDIR:
638:     rcmd = (struct redircmd*)cmd;
639:     nulterminate(rcmd->cmd);
640:     *rcmd->efile = 0;
641:     break;
642: 
643:   case PIPE:
644:     pcmd = (struct pipecmd*)cmd;
645:     nulterminate(pcmd->left);
646:     nulterminate(pcmd->right);
647:     break;
648: 
649:   case LIST:
650:     lcmd = (struct listcmd*)cmd;
651:     nulterminate(lcmd->left);
652:     nulterminate(lcmd->right);
653:     break;
654: 
655:   case BACK:
656:     bcmd = (struct backcmd*)cmd;
657:     nulterminate(bcmd->cmd);
658:     break;
659:   }
660:   return cmd;
661: }

613:   return ret;
614: }

616: // NUL-terminate all the counted strings.
617: struct cmd*
618: nulterminate(struct cmd *cmd)
619: {
620:   int i;
621:   struct backcmd *bcmd;
622:   struct execcmd *ecmd;
623:   struct listcmd *lcmd;
624:   struct pipecmd *pcmd;
625:   struct redircmd *rcmd;
626: 
627:   if(cmd == 0)
628:     return 0;
629: 
630:   switch(cmd->type){
631:   case EXEC:
632:     ecmd = (struct execcmd*)cmd;
633:     for(i=0; ecmd->argv[i]; i++)
634:       *ecmd->eargv[i] = 0;
635:     break;
636: 
637:   case REDIR:
638:     rcmd = (struct redircmd*)cmd;
639:     nulterminate(rcmd->cmd);
640:     *rcmd->efile = 0;
641:     break;
642: 
643:   case PIPE:
644:     pcmd = (struct pipecmd*)cmd;
645:     nulterminate(pcmd->left);
646:     nulterminate(pcmd->right);
647:     break;
648: 
649:   case LIST:
650:     lcmd = (struct listcmd*)cmd;
651:     nulterminate(lcmd->left);
652:     nulterminate(lcmd->right);
653:     break;
654: 
655:   case BACK:
656:     bcmd = (struct backcmd*)cmd;
657:     nulterminate(bcmd->cmd);
658:     break;
659:   }
660:   return cmd;
661: }

23: int
24: main(int argc, char *argv[], char *envp[])
25: {
26:   int i;
27:   char *nargv[MAXARG];
28: 
29:   if(argc < 2){
30:     fprintf(2, "usage: %s COMMAND\n", argv[0]);
31:     exit(1);
32:   }
33: 
34:   if (trace() < 0) {
35:     fprintf(2, "%s: strace failed\n", argv[0]);
36:     exit(1);
37:   }
38:   
39:   for(i = 1; i < argc && i < MAXARG; i++){
40:     nargv[i-1] = argv[i];
41:   }
42:   execve(nargv[0], nargv, envp);
43:   char buf[128];
44:   char *env = getenv(envp, "PATH");
45:   if (env != NULL) {
46:     while (*env) {
47:       int i;
48:       for (i = 0; i < 128; i++) {
49:         buf[i] = *env++;
50:         if (buf[i] == ';' || buf[i] == '\0') {
51:           buf[i] = '/';
52:           break;
53:         }
54:       }
55:       char *argp = argv[1];
56:       for (i++; i < 128; i++) {
57:         if ((buf[i] = *argp++) == '\0') {
58:           break;
59:         }
60:       }
61:       if (i < 128) {
62:         execve(buf, nargv, envp);
63:       }
64:     }
65:   }
66:   printf("strace: exec %s fail\n", nargv[0]);
67:   exit(0);
68: }

10: #include "types.h"
11: #include "fs/stat.h"
12: #include "user.h"
13: #include "fs/fcntl.h"
14: 
15: int
16: main(int argc, char *argv[])
17: {
18:   int fd, i;
19:   char path[] = "stressfs0";
20:   char data[512];
21: 
22:   printf("stressfs starting\n");
23:   memset(data, 'a', sizeof(data));
24: 
25:   for(i = 0; i < 4; i++)
26:     if(fork() > 0)
27:       break;
28: 
29:   printf("write %d\n", i);
30: 
31:   path[8] += i;
32:   fd = open(path, O_CREATE | O_RDWR);
33:   for(i = 0; i < 20; i++)
34: //    printf(fd, "%d\n", i);
35:     write(fd, data, sizeof(data));
36:   close(fd);
37: 
38:   printf("read\n");
39: 
40:   fd = open(path, O_RDONLY);
41:   for (i = 0; i < 20; i++)
42:     read(fd, data, sizeof(data));
43:   close(fd);
44: 
45:   wait(0);
46: 
47:   exit(0);
48: }

114: void*
115: memmove(void *vdst, const void *vsrc, int n)
116: {
117:   char *dst;
118:   const char *src;
119: 
120:   dst = vdst;
121:   src = vsrc;
122:   if (src > dst) {
123:     while(n-- > 0)
124:       *dst++ = *src++;
125:   } else {
126:     dst += n;
127:     src += n;
128:     while(n-- > 0)
129:       *--dst = *--src;
130:   }
131:   return vdst;
132: }

47: morecore(uint nu)

59:   free((void*)(hp + 1));

62: 
63: void*
64: malloc(uint nbytes)
65: {
66:   Header *p, *prevp;
67:   uint nunits;
68: 
69:   nunits = (nbytes + sizeof(Header) - 1)/sizeof(Header) + 1;
70:   if((prevp = freep) == 0){
71:     base.s.ptr = freep = prevp = &base;
72:     base.s.size = 0;
73:   }
74:   for(p = prevp->s.ptr; ; prevp = p, p = p->s.ptr){
75:     if(p->s.size >= nunits){
76:       if(p->s.size == nunits)
77:         prevp->s.ptr = p->s.ptr;
78:       else {
79:         p->s.size -= nunits;
80:         p += p->s.size;
81:         p->s.size = nunits;
82:       }
83:       freep = prevp;
84:       return (void*)(p + 1);
85:     }
86:     if(p == freep)
87:       if((p = morecore(nunits)) == 0)
88:         return 0;
89:   }
90: }

65: {
66:   Header *p, *prevp;
67:   uint nunits;
68: 
69:   nunits = (nbytes + sizeof(Header) - 1)/sizeof(Header) + 1;
70:   if((prevp = freep) == 0){
71:     base.s.ptr = freep = prevp = &base;
72:     base.s.size = 0;
73:   }
74:   for(p = prevp->s.ptr; ; prevp = p, p = p->s.ptr){

79:         p->s.size -= nunits;
80:         p += p->s.size;
81:         p->s.size = nunits;
82:       }
83:       freep = prevp;
84:       return (void*)(p + 1);
85:     }
86:     if(p == freep)

xv6-k210/xv6-user/usertests.c::copyin
匹配分数 1.000 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": true, "exact_token_fp": true, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 1.0}

oskernel2023-zmz@recover 源码

23: char buf[BUFSZ];
24: char name[3];
25: 
26: // what if you pass ridiculous pointers to system calls
27: // that read user memory with copyin?
28: void
29: copyin(char *s)
30: {
31:   uint64 addrs[] = { 0x80000000LL, 0xffffffffffffffff };
32: 
33:   for(int ai = 0; ai < 2; ai++){
34:     uint64 addr = addrs[ai];
35:     
36:     int fd = open("copyin1", O_CREATE|O_WRONLY);
37:     if(fd < 0){
38:       printf("open(copyin1) failed\n");
39:       exit(1);
40:     }
41:     int n = write(fd, (void*)addr, 8192);
42:     if(n >= 0){
43:       printf("write(fd, %p, 8192) returned %d, not -1\n", addr, n);
44:       exit(1);
45:     }
46:     close(fd);
47:     remove("copyin1");
48:     
49:     n = write(1, (char*)addr, 8192);
50:     if(n > 0){
51:       printf("write(1, %p, 8192) returned %d, not -1 or 0\n", addr, n);
52:       exit(1);
53:     }
54:     
55:     int fds[2];
56:     if(pipe(fds) < 0){
57:       printf("pipe() failed\n");
58:       exit(1);
59:     }
60:     n = write(fds[1], (char*)addr, 8192);
61:     if(n > 0){
62:       printf("write(pipe, %p, 8192) returned %d, not -1 or 0\n", addr, n);
63:       exit(1);
64:     }
65:     close(fds[0]);
66:     close(fds[1]);
67:   }
68: }

129: // what if a string system call argument is exactly the size
130: // of the kernel buffer it is copied into, so that the null
131: // would fall just beyond the end of the kernel buffer?
132: void
133: copyinstr2(char *s)
134: {
135:   char b[MAXPATH+1];
136: 
137:   for(int i = 0; i < MAXPATH; i++)
138:     b[i] = 'x';
139:   b[MAXPATH] = '\0';
140:   
141:   int ret = remove(b);
142:   if(ret >= 0){
143:     printf("remove(%s) returned %d, not -1\n", b, ret);
144:     exit(1);
145:   }
146: 
147:   int fd = open(b, O_CREATE | O_WRONLY);
148:   if(fd >= 0){
149:     printf("open(%s) returned %d, not -1\n", b, fd);
150:     exit(1);
151:   }
152: 
153:   // ret = link(b, b);
154:   // if(ret != -1){
155:   //   printf("link(%s, %s) returned %d, not -1\n", b, b, ret);
156:   //   exit(1);
157:   // }
158: 
159:   char *args[] = { "xx", 0 };
160:   ret = exec(b, args);
161:   if(ret >= 0){
162:     printf("exec(%s) returned %d, not negative\n", b, fd);
163:     exit(1);
164:   }
165: 
166:   int pid = fork();
167:   if(pid < 0){
168:     printf("fork failed\n");
169:     exit(1);
170:   }
171:   if(pid == 0){
172:     static char big[PGSIZE+1];
173:     for(int i = 0; i < PGSIZE; i++)
174:       big[i] = 'x';
175:     big[PGSIZE] = '\0';
176:     char *args2[] = { big, big, big, 0 };
177:     ret = exec("echo", args2);
178:     if(ret >= 0){
179:       printf("exec(echo, BIG) returned %d, not negative\n", fd);
180:       exit(1);
181:     }
182:     exit(47); // OK
183:   }
184: 
185:   int st = 0;
186:   wait(&st);
187:   if(WEXITSTATUS(st) != 47){
188:     printf("exec(echo, BIG) succeeded, should have failed\n");
189:     exit(1);
190:   }
191: }
192:

195: copyinstr3(char *s)

193: // what if a string argument crosses over the end of last user page?
194: void
195: copyinstr3(char *s)
196: {
197:   sbrk(8192);
198:   uint64 top = (uint64) sbrk(0);
199:   if((top % PGSIZE) != 0){
200:     sbrk(PGSIZE - (top % PGSIZE));
201:   }
202:   top = (uint64) sbrk(0);
203:   if(top % PGSIZE){
204:     printf("oops\n");
205:     exit(1);
206:   }
207: 
208:   char *b = (char *) (top - 1);
209:   *b = 'x';
210: 
211:   int ret = remove(b);
212:   if(ret>= 0){
213:     printf("remove(%s) returned %d, not -1\n", b, ret);
214:     exit(1);
215:   }
216: 
217:   int fd = open(b, O_CREATE | O_WRONLY);
218:   if(fd>= 0){
219:     printf("open(%s) returned %d, not -1\n", b, fd);
220:     exit(1);
221:   }
222: 
223:   // ret = link(b, b);
224:   // if(ret != -1){
225:   //   printf("link(%s, %s) returned %d, not -1\n", b, b, ret);
226:   //   exit(1);
227:   // }
228: 
229:   char *args[] = { "xx", 0 };
230:   ret = exec(b, args);
231:   if(ret != -1){
232:     printf("exec(%s) returned %d, not -1\n", b, fd);
233:     exit(1);
234:   }
235: }

237: // // test O_TRUNC.
238: void
239: truncate1(char *s)
240: {
241:   char buf[32];
242:   
243:   remove("truncfile");
244:   int fd1 = open("truncfile", O_CREATE|O_WRONLY|O_TRUNC);
245:   write(fd1, "abcd", 4);
246:   close(fd1);
247: 
248:   int fd2 = open("truncfile", O_RDONLY);
249:   int n = read(fd2, buf, sizeof(buf));
250:   if(n != 4){
251:     printf("%s: read %d bytes, wanted 4\n", s, n);
252:     exit(1);
253:   }
254: 
255:   fd1 = open("truncfile", O_WRONLY|O_TRUNC);
256: 
257:   int fd3 = open("truncfile", O_RDONLY);
258:   n = read(fd3, buf, sizeof(buf));
259:   if(n != 0){
260:     printf("aaa fd3=%d\n", fd3);
261:     printf("%s: read %d bytes, wanted 0\n", s, n);
262:     exit(1);
263:   }
264: 
265:   n = read(fd2, buf, sizeof(buf));
266:   if(n != 0){
267:     printf("bbb fd2=%d\n", fd2);
268:     printf("%s: read %d bytes, wanted 0\n", s, n);
269:     exit(1);
270:   }
271:   
272:   write(fd1, "abcdef", 6);
273: 
274:   n = read(fd3, buf, sizeof(buf));
275:   if(n != 6){
276:     printf("%s: read %d bytes, wanted 6\n", s, n);
277:     exit(1);
278:   }
279: 
280:   n = read(fd2, buf, sizeof(buf));
281:   if(n != 2){
282:     printf("%s: read %d bytes, wanted 2\n", s, n);
283:     exit(1);
284:   }
285: 
286:   remove("truncfile");
287: 
288:   close(fd1);
289:   close(fd2);
290:   close(fd3);
291: }
292: 
293: // write to an open FD whose file has just been truncated.
294: // this causes a write at an offset beyond the end of the file.
295: // such writes fail on xv6 (unlike POSIX) but at least
296: // they don't crash.

xv6-k210@final / 来源候选源码

233:     exit(1);
234:   }
235: }

238: void
239: truncate1(char *s)
240: {
241:   char buf[32];
242:   
243:   remove("truncfile");
244:   int fd1 = open("truncfile", O_CREATE|O_WRONLY|O_TRUNC);
245:   write(fd1, "abcd", 4);
246:   close(fd1);
247: 
248:   int fd2 = open("truncfile", O_RDONLY);
249:   int n = read(fd2, buf, sizeof(buf));
250:   if(n != 4){
251:     printf("%s: read %d bytes, wanted 4\n", s, n);
252:     exit(1);
253:   }
254: 
255:   fd1 = open("truncfile", O_WRONLY|O_TRUNC);
256: 
257:   int fd3 = open("truncfile", O_RDONLY);
258:   n = read(fd3, buf, sizeof(buf));
259:   if(n != 0){
260:     printf("aaa fd3=%d\n", fd3);
261:     printf("%s: read %d bytes, wanted 0\n", s, n);
262:     exit(1);
263:   }
264: 
265:   n = read(fd2, buf, sizeof(buf));
266:   if(n != 0){
267:     printf("bbb fd2=%d\n", fd2);
268:     printf("%s: read %d bytes, wanted 0\n", s, n);
269:     exit(1);
270:   }
271:   
272:   write(fd1, "abcdef", 6);
273:

275:   if(n != 6){
276:     printf("%s: read %d bytes, wanted 6\n", s, n);
277:     exit(1);
278:   }
279: 
280:   n = read(fd2, buf, sizeof(buf));
281:   if(n != 2){
282:     printf("%s: read %d bytes, wanted 2\n", s, n);
283:     exit(1);
284:   }
285: 
286:   remove("truncfile");
287: 
288:   close(fd1);
289:   close(fd2);
290:   close(fd3);
291: }
292: 
293: // write to an open FD whose file has just been truncated.
294: // this causes a write at an offset beyond the end of the file.
295: // such writes fail on xv6 (unlike POSIX) but at least
296: // they don't crash.

294: // this causes a write at an offset beyond the end of the file.
295: // such writes fail on xv6 (unlike POSIX) but at least
296: // they don't crash.
297: 
298: // NOW it's like POSIX!
299: void
300: truncate2(char *s)
301: {
302:   remove("truncfile");
303: 
304:   int fd1 = open("truncfile", O_CREATE|O_TRUNC|O_WRONLY);
305:   write(fd1, "abcd", 4);
306: 
307:   int fd2 = open("truncfile", O_TRUNC|O_WRONLY);
308: 
309:   int n = write(fd1, "x", 1);

320: void
321: truncate3(char *s)
322: {
323:   int pid, xstatus;
324: 
325:   close(open("truncfile", O_CREATE|O_TRUNC|O_WRONLY));
326:   
327:   pid = fork();
328:   if(pid < 0){
329:     printf("%s: fork failed\n", s);
330:     exit(1);
331:   }
332: 
333:   if(pid == 0){
334:     for(int i = 0; i < 100; i++){
335:       char buf[32];
336:       int fd = open("truncfile", O_WRONLY);
337:       if(fd < 0){
338:         printf("%s: open failed\n", s);
339:         exit(1);
340:       }
341:       int n = write(fd, "1234567890", 10);
342:       if(n != 10){
343:         printf("%s: write got %d, expected 10\n", s, n);
344:         exit(1);
345:       }
346:       close(fd);
347:       fd = open("truncfile", O_RDONLY);
348:       read(fd, buf, sizeof(buf));
349:       close(fd);
350:     }
351:     exit(0);
352:   }
353: 
354:   for(int i = 0; i < 150; i++){
355:     int fd = open("truncfile", O_CREATE|O_WRONLY|O_TRUNC);
356:     if(fd < 0){
357:       printf("%s: open failed\n", s);
358:       exit(1);
359:     }
360:     int n = write(fd, "xxx", 3);
361:     if(n != 3){
362:       printf("%s: write got %d, expected 3\n", s, n);
363:       exit(1);
364:     }
365:     close(fd);
366:   }
367: 
368:   wait(&xstatus);
369:   remove("truncfile");
370:   exit(WEXITSTATUS(xstatus));
371: }

375: void
376: iputtest(char *s)
377: {
378:   if(mkdir("iputdir") < 0){
379:     printf("%s: mkdir failed\n", s);
380:     exit(1);
381:   }
382:   if(chdir("iputdir") < 0){
383:     printf("%s: chdir iputdir failed\n", s);
384:     exit(1);

386:   if(rmdir("../iputdir") < 0){
387:     printf("%s: remove ../iputdir failed\n", s);

389:   }
390:   if(chdir("/") < 0){
391:     printf("%s: chdir / failed\n", s);
392:     exit(1);
393:   }
394: }

437: void
438: openiputtest(char *s)
439: {
440:   int pid, xstatus;
441: 
442:   if(mkdir("oidir") < 0){
443:     printf("%s: mkdir oidir failed\n", s);

452:     int fd = open("oidir", O_RDWR);

454:       printf("%s: open directory for write succeeded\n", s);
455:       exit(1);
456:     }
457:     exit(0);
458:   }
459:   sleep(1);
460:   if(rmdir("oidir") != 0){
461:     printf("%s: remove failed\n", s);
462:     exit(1);
463:   }
464:   wait(&xstatus);
465:   exit(WEXITSTATUS(xstatus));
466: }

488: void
489: writetest(char *s)
490: {
491:   int fd;
492:   int i;
493:   enum { N=100, SZ=10 };
494:   
495:   fd = open("small", O_CREATE|O_RDWR);
496:   if(fd < 0){
497:     printf("%s: error: creat small failed!\n", s);
498:     exit(1);
499:   }
500:   for(i = 0; i < N; i++){
501:     if(write(fd, "aaaaaaaaaa", SZ) != SZ){
502:       printf("%s: error: write aa %d new file failed\n", i);
503:       exit(1);
504:     }
505:     if(write(fd, "bbbbbbbbbb", SZ) != SZ){
506:       printf("%s: error: write bb %d new file failed\n", i);
507:       exit(1);
508:     }
509:   }
510:   close(fd);
511:   fd = open("small", O_RDONLY);
512:   if(fd < 0){
513:     printf("%s: error: open small failed!\n", s);
514:     exit(1);
515:   }
516:   i = read(fd, buf, N*SZ*2);
517:   if(i != N*SZ*2){
518:     printf("%s: read failed\n", s);
519:     exit(1);
520:   }
521:   close(fd);
522: 
523:   if(remove("small") < 0){
524:     printf("%s: remove small failed\n", s);
525:     exit(1);
526:   }
527: }

529: void
530: writebig(char *s)
531: {
532:   int i, fd, n;
533: 
534:   fd = open("big", O_CREATE|O_RDWR);
535:   if(fd < 0){
536:     printf("%s: error: creat big failed!\n", s);
537:     exit(1);
538:   }
539: 
540:   for(i = 0; i < MAXFILE; i++){
541:     ((int*)buf)[0] = i;
542:     if(write(fd, buf, BSIZE) != BSIZE){
543:       printf("%s: error: write big file failed\n", s);
544:       exit(1);
545:     }
546:   }
547: 
548:   close(fd);
549: 
550:   fd = open("big", O_RDONLY);
551:   if(fd < 0){
552:     printf("%s: error: open big failed!\n", s);
553:     exit(1);
554:   }
555: 
556:   n = 0;
557:   for(;;){
558:     i = read(fd, buf, BSIZE);
559:     if(i == 0){
560:       if(n < MAXFILE){
561:         printf("%s: read only %d blocks from big", s, n);
562:         exit(1);
563:       }
564:       break;
565:     } else if(i != BSIZE){
566:       printf("%s: read failed %d\n", s, i);
567:       exit(1);
568:     }
569:     if(((int*)buf)[0] != n){
570:       printf("%s: read content of block %d is %d\n",
571:              s, n, ((int*)buf)[0]);
572:       exit(1);
573:     }
574:     n++;
575:   }
576:   close(fd);
577:   if(remove("big") < 0){
578:     printf("%s: remove big failed\n", s);
579:     exit(1);
580:   }
581: }

563:       }

565:     } else if(i != BSIZE){
566:       printf("%s: read failed %d\n", s, i);
567:       exit(1);
568:     }
569:     if(((int*)buf)[0] != n){
570:       printf("%s: read content of block %d is %d\n",
571:              s, n, ((int*)buf)[0]);
572:       exit(1);
573:     }
574:     n++;
575:   }
576:   close(fd);
577:   if(remove("big") < 0){
578:     printf("%s: remove big failed\n", s);
579:     exit(1);
580:   }
581: }

605: void dirtest(char *s)
606: {
607:   printf("mkdir test\n");
608: 
609:   if(mkdir("dir0") < 0){
610:     printf("%s: mkdir failed\n", s);
611:     exit(1);
612:   }
613: 
614:   if(chdir("dir0") < 0){
615:     printf("%s: chdir dir0 failed\n", s);
616:     exit(1);
617:   }
618: 
619:   if(chdir("..") < 0){
620:     printf("%s: chdir .. failed\n", s);
621:     exit(1);
622:   }
623: 
624:   if(remove("dir0") < 0){
625:     printf("%s: remove dir0 failed\n", s);
626:     exit(1);
627:   }
628:   printf("%s: mkdir test ok\n");
629: }

631: void
632: exectest(char *s)
633: {
634:   int fd, xstatus, pid;
635:   char *echoargv[] = { "echo", "OK", 0 };
636:   char buf[3];
637: 
638:   remove("echo-ok");
639:   pid = fork();
640:   if(pid < 0) {
641:      printf("%s: fork failed\n", s);
642:      exit(1);
643:   }
644:   if(pid == 0) {
645:     close(1);
646:     fd = open("echo-ok", O_CREATE|O_WRONLY);
647:     if(fd < 0) {
648:       printf("%s: create failed\n", s);
649:       exit(1);
650:     }
651:     if(fd != 1) {
652:       printf("%s: wrong fd\n", s);
653:       exit(1);
654:     }
655:     if(exec("echo", echoargv) < 0){
656:       printf("%s: exec echo failed\n", s);
657:       exit(1);
658:     }
659:     // won't get to here
660:   }
661:   if (wait(&xstatus) != pid) {
662:     printf("%s: wait failed!\n", s);
663:   }
664:   if(WEXITSTATUS(xstatus) != 0)
665:     exit(WEXITSTATUS(xstatus));
666: 
667:   fd = open("echo-ok", O_RDONLY);
668:   if(fd < 0) {
669:     printf("%s: open failed\n", s);
670:     exit(1);
671:   }
672:   if (read(fd, buf, 2) != 2) {
673:     printf("%s: read failed\n", s);
674:     exit(1);
675:   }
676:   remove("echo-ok");
677:   if(buf[0] == 'O' && buf[1] == 'K')
678:     exit(0);
679:   else {
680:     printf("%s: wrong output\n", s);
681:     exit(1);
682:   }
683: 
684: }

匹配分数 1.000 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": true, "exact_token_fp": true, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 1.0}

685: 
686: // simple fork and pipe read/write
687: 
688: void
689: pipe1(char *s)
690: {
691:   int fds[2], pid, xstatus;
692:   int seq, i, n, cc, total;
693:   enum { N=5, SZ=1033 };
694:   
695:   if(pipe(fds) != 0){
696:     printf("%s: pipe() failed\n", s);
697:     exit(1);
698:   }
699:   pid = fork();
700:   seq = 0;
701:   if(pid == 0){
702:     close(fds[0]);
703:     for(n = 0; n < N; n++){
704:       for(i = 0; i < SZ; i++)
705:         buf[i] = seq++;
706:       if(write(fds[1], buf, SZ) != SZ){
707:         printf("%s: pipe1 oops 1\n", s);
708:         exit(1);
709:       }
710:     }
711:     exit(0);
712:   } else if(pid > 0){
713:     close(fds[1]);
714:     total = 0;
715:     cc = 1;
716:     while((n = read(fds[0], buf, cc)) > 0){
717:       for(i = 0; i < n; i++){
718:         if((buf[i] & 0xff) != (seq++ & 0xff)){
719:           printf("%s: pipe1 oops 2\n", s);
720:           return;
721:         }
722:       }
723:       total += n;
724:       cc = cc * 2;
725:       if(cc > sizeof(buf))
726:         cc = sizeof(buf);
727:     }
728:     if(total != N * SZ){
729:       printf("%s: pipe1 oops 3 total %d\n", total);
730:       exit(1);
731:     }
732:     close(fds[0]);
733:     wait(&xstatus);
734:     exit(WEXITSTATUS(xstatus));
735:   } else {
736:     printf("%s: fork() failed\n", s);
737:     exit(1);
738:   }
739: }
740:

743: preempt(char *s)

692:   int seq, i, n, cc, total;
693:   enum { N=5, SZ=1033 };
694:   
695:   if(pipe(fds) != 0){
696:     printf("%s: pipe() failed\n", s);
697:     exit(1);
698:   }
699:   pid = fork();
700:   seq = 0;
701:   if(pid == 0){
702:     close(fds[0]);
703:     for(n = 0; n < N; n++){
704:       for(i = 0; i < SZ; i++)
705:         buf[i] = seq++;
706:       if(write(fds[1], buf, SZ) != SZ){
707:         printf("%s: pipe1 oops 1\n", s);
708:         exit(1);
709:       }
710:     }
711:     exit(0);
712:   } else if(pid > 0){
713:     close(fds[1]);
714:     total = 0;
715:     cc = 1;
716:     while((n = read(fds[0], buf, cc)) > 0){
717:       for(i = 0; i < n; i++){

729:       printf("%s: pipe1 oops 3 total %d\n", total);
730:       exit(1);
731:     }
732:     close(fds[0]);
733:     wait(&xstatus);
734:     exit(WEXITSTATUS(xstatus));
735:   } else {

737:     exit(1);

741: // meant to be run w/ at most two CPUs
742: void
743: preempt(char *s)
744: {
745:   int pid1, pid2, pid3;
746:   int pfds[2];
747: 
748:   pid1 = fork();
749:   if(pid1 < 0) {
750:     printf("%s: fork failed");
751:     exit(1);
752:   }
753:   if(pid1 == 0)
754:     for(;;)
755:       ;
756: 
757:   pid2 = fork();
758:   if(pid2 < 0) {
759:     printf("%s: fork failed\n", s);
760:     exit(1);
761:   }
762:   if(pid2 == 0)
763:     for(;;)
764:       ;
765: 
766:   pipe(pfds);
767:   pid3 = fork();
768:   if(pid3 < 0) {
769:      printf("%s: fork failed\n", s);
770:      exit(1);
771:   }
772:   if(pid3 == 0){
773:     close(pfds[0]);
774:     if(write(pfds[1], "x", 1) != 1)
775:       printf("%s: preempt write error");
776:     close(pfds[1]);
777:     for(;;)
778:       ;
779:   }
780: 
781:   close(pfds[1]);
782:   if(read(pfds[0], buf, sizeof(buf)) != 1){
783:     printf("%s: preempt read error");
784:     return;
785:   }
786:   close(pfds[0]);
787:   printf("kill... ");
788:   kill(pid1, SIGTERM);
789:   kill(pid2, SIGTERM);
790:   kill(pid3, SIGTERM);
791:   printf("wait... ");
792:   wait(0);
793:   wait(0);
794:   wait(0);
795: }

828: void
829: reparent(char *s)
830: {
831:   int master_pid = getpid();
832:   for(int i = 0; i < 200; i++){
833:     int pid = fork();

835:       printf("%s: fork failed\n", s);

844:       int pid2 = fork();
845:       if(pid2 < 0){
846:         kill(master_pid, SIGTERM);
847:         exit(1);
848:       }
849:       exit(0);
850:     }
851:   }
852:   exit(0);
853: }

834:     if(pid < 0){
835:       printf("%s: fork failed\n", s);
836:       exit(1);
837:     }
838:     if(pid){
839:       if(wait(0) != pid){
840:         printf("%s: wait wrong pid\n", s);
841:         exit(1);
842:       }
843:     } else {
844:       int pid2 = fork();
845:       if(pid2 < 0){
846:         kill(master_pid, SIGTERM);
847:         exit(1);
848:       }
849:       exit(0);
850:     }
851:   }
852:   exit(0);
853: }

884: void
885: forkfork(char *s)
886: {
887:   enum { N=2 };
888:   
889:   for(int i = 0; i < N; i++){
890:     int pid = fork();
891:     if(pid < 0){
892:       printf("%s: fork failed", s);

894:     }

899:           exit(1);
900:         }
901:         if(pid1 == 0){
902:           exit(0);
903:         }
904:         wait(0);
905:       }
906:       exit(0);
907:     }
908:   }
909: 
910:   int xstatus;
911:   for(int i = 0; i < N; i++){
912:     wait(&xstatus);
913:     if(WEXITSTATUS(xstatus) != 0) {
914:       printf("%s: fork in child failed", s);
915:       exit(1);
916:     }
917:   }
918: }

920: void
921: forkforkfork(char *s)
922: {
923:   remove("stopforking");
924: 
925:   int pid = fork();
926:   if(pid < 0){
927:     printf("%s: fork failed", s);
928:     exit(1);
929:   }
930:   if(pid == 0){
931:     while(1){
932:       int fd = open("stopforking", 0);
933:       if(fd >= 0){
934:         exit(0);
935:       }
936:       if(fork() < 0){
937:         close(open("stopforking", O_CREATE|O_RDWR));
938:       }
939:     }
940: 
941:     exit(0);
942:   }
943: 
944:   sleep(20); // two seconds
945:   close(open("stopforking", O_CREATE|O_RDWR));
946:   wait(0);
947:   sleep(10); // one second
948:   remove("stopforking");
949: }

951: // regression test. does reparent() violate the parent-then-child
952: // locking order when giving away a child to init, so that exit()
953: // deadlocks against init's wait()? also used to trigger a "panic:
954: // release" due to exit() releasing a different p->parent->lock than
955: // it acquired.
956: void
957: reparent2(char *s)
958: {
959:   for(int i = 0; i < 800; i++){
960:     int pid1 = fork();
961:     if(pid1 < 0){
962:       printf("fork failed\n");
963:       exit(1);
964:     }
965:     if(pid1 == 0){
966:       fork();
967:       fork();
968:       exit(0);
969:     }
970:     wait(0);
971:   }
972: 
973:   exit(0);
974: }

976: // allocate all mem, free it, and allocate again
977: void
978: mem(char *s)
979: {
980:   void *m1, *m2;
981:   int pid;
982: 
983:   if((pid = fork()) == 0){
984:     m1 = 0;
985:     while((m2 = malloc(10001)) != 0){
986:       *(char**)m2 = m1;
987:       m1 = m2;
988:     }
989:     while(m1){
990:       m2 = *(char**)m1;
991:       free(m1);
992:       m1 = m2;
993:     }
994:     m1 = malloc(1024*20);
995:     if(m1 == 0){
996:       printf("couldn't allocate mem?!!\n", s);
997:       exit(1);
998:     }
999:     free(m1);
1000:     exit(0);
1001:   } else {
1002:     int xstatus;
1003:     wait(&xstatus);
1004:     if(WEXITSTATUS(xstatus) == (char)-1){
1005:       // probably page fault, so might be lazy lab,
1006:       // so OK.
1007:       exit(0);
1008:     }
1009:     exit(WEXITSTATUS(xstatus));
1010:   }
1011: }
1012:

1012: 
1013: // More file system tests
1014: 
1015: // two processes write to the same file descriptor
1016: // is the offset shared? does inode locking work?
1017: void
1018: sharedfd(char *s)
1019: {
1020:   int fd, pid, i, n, nc, np;
1021:   enum { N = 1000, SZ=10};
1022:   char buf[SZ];
1023: 
1024:   remove("sharedfd");
1025:   fd = open("sharedfd", O_CREATE|O_RDWR);
1026:   if(fd < 0){
1027:     printf("%s: cannot open sharedfd for writing", s);
1028:     exit(1);
1029:   }
1030:   pid = fork();
1031:   memset(buf, pid==0?'c':'p', sizeof(buf));
1032:   for(i = 0; i < N; i++){
1033:     if(write(fd, buf, sizeof(buf)) != sizeof(buf)){
1034:       printf("%s: write sharedfd failed\n", s);
1035:       exit(1);
1036:     }
1037:   }
1038:   if(pid == 0) {
1039:     exit(0);
1040:   } else {
1041:     int xstatus;
1042:     wait(&xstatus);
1043:     if(WEXITSTATUS(xstatus) != 0)
1044:       exit(WEXITSTATUS(xstatus));
1045:   }
1046:   
1047:   close(fd);
1048:   fd = open("sharedfd", 0);
1049:   if(fd < 0){
1050:     printf("%s: cannot open sharedfd for reading\n", s);
1051:     exit(1);
1052:   }
1053:   nc = np = 0;
1054:   while((n = read(fd, buf, sizeof(buf))) > 0){
1055:     for(i = 0; i < sizeof(buf); i++){
1056:       if(buf[i] == 'c')
1057:         nc++;
1058:       if(buf[i] == 'p')
1059:         np++;
1060:     }
1061:   }
1062:   close(fd);
1063:   remove("sharedfd");
1064:   if(nc == N*SZ && np == N*SZ){
1065:     exit(0);
1066:   } else {
1067:     printf("%s: nc/np test fails\n", s);
1068:     exit(1);
1069:   }
1070: }
1071: 
1072: // four processes write different files at the same
1073: // time, to test block allocation.
1074: void
1075: fourfiles(char *s)

xv6-k210@final / 来源候选源码

1017: void
1018: sharedfd(char *s)
1019: {
1020:   int fd, pid, i, n, nc, np;
1021:   enum { N = 1000, SZ=10};
1022:   char buf[SZ];
1023: 
1024:   remove("sharedfd");
1025:   fd = open("sharedfd", O_CREATE|O_RDWR);
1026:   if(fd < 0){
1027:     printf("%s: cannot open sharedfd for writing", s);
1028:     exit(1);
1029:   }
1030:   pid = fork();
1031:   memset(buf, pid==0?'c':'p', sizeof(buf));
1032:   for(i = 0; i < N; i++){
1033:     if(write(fd, buf, sizeof(buf)) != sizeof(buf)){
1034:       printf("%s: write sharedfd failed\n", s);
1035:       exit(1);
1036:     }
1037:   }
1038:   if(pid == 0) {
1039:     exit(0);
1040:   } else {
1041:     int xstatus;
1042:     wait(&xstatus);
1043:     if(WEXITSTATUS(xstatus) != 0)
1044:       exit(WEXITSTATUS(xstatus));
1045:   }
1046:   
1047:   close(fd);
1048:   fd = open("sharedfd", 0);
1049:   if(fd < 0){
1050:     printf("%s: cannot open sharedfd for reading\n", s);
1051:     exit(1);
1052:   }
1053:   nc = np = 0;
1054:   while((n = read(fd, buf, sizeof(buf))) > 0){
1055:     for(i = 0; i < sizeof(buf); i++){
1056:       if(buf[i] == 'c')
1057:         nc++;
1058:       if(buf[i] == 'p')
1059:         np++;
1060:     }
1061:   }
1062:   close(fd);
1063:   remove("sharedfd");
1064:   if(nc == N*SZ && np == N*SZ){
1065:     exit(0);
1066:   } else {
1067:     printf("%s: nc/np test fails\n", s);
1068:     exit(1);
1069:   }
1070: }

1071: 
1072: // four processes write different files at the same
1073: // time, to test block allocation.
1074: void
1075: fourfiles(char *s)
1076: {
1077:   int fd, pid, i, j, n, total, pi;
1078:   char *names[] = { "f0", "f1", "f2", "f3" };
1079:   char *fname;
1080:   enum { N=12, NCHILD=4, SZ=500 };
1081:   
1082:   for(pi = 0; pi < NCHILD; pi++){
1083:     fname = names[pi];
1084:     remove(fname);
1085: 
1086:     pid = fork();
1087:     if(pid < 0){
1088:       printf("fork failed\n", s);
1089:       exit(1);
1090:     }
1091: 
1092:     if(pid == 0){
1093:       fd = open(fname, O_CREATE | O_RDWR);
1094:       if(fd < 0){
1095:         printf("create failed\n", s);
1096:         exit(1);
1097:       }
1098: 
1099:       memset(buf, '0'+pi, SZ);
1100:       for(i = 0; i < N; i++){
1101:         if((n = write(fd, buf, SZ)) != SZ){
1102:           printf("write failed %d\n", n);
1103:           exit(1);
1104:         }
1105:       }
1106:       exit(0);
1107:     }
1108:   }
1109: 
1110:   int xstatus;
1111:   for(pi = 0; pi < NCHILD; pi++){
1112:     wait(&xstatus);
1113:     if(WEXITSTATUS(xstatus) != 0)
1114:       exit(WEXITSTATUS(xstatus));
1115:   }
1116: 
1117:   for(i = 0; i < NCHILD; i++){
1118:     fname = names[i];
1119:     fd = open(fname, 0);
1120:     total = 0;
1121:     while((n = read(fd, buf, sizeof(buf))) > 0){
1122:       for(j = 0; j < n; j++){
1123:         if(buf[j] != '0'+i){
1124:           printf("wrong char\n", s);
1125:           exit(1);
1126:         }
1127:       }
1128:       total += n;
1129:     }
1130:     close(fd);
1131:     if(total != N*SZ){
1132:       printf("wrong length %d\n", total);
1133:       exit(1);
1134:     }
1135:     remove(fname);
1136:   }
1137: }
1138: 
1139: // four processes create and delete different files in same directory
1140: void
1141: createdelete(char *s)
1142: {

1070: }

1078:   char *names[] = { "f0", "f1", "f2", "f3" };
1079:   char *fname;
1080:   enum { N=12, NCHILD=4, SZ=500 };
1081:   
1082:   for(pi = 0; pi < NCHILD; pi++){
1083:     fname = names[pi];
1084:     remove(fname);
1085: 
1086:     pid = fork();
1087:     if(pid < 0){
1088:       printf("fork failed\n", s);
1089:       exit(1);
1090:     }
1091: 
1092:     if(pid == 0){
1093:       fd = open(fname, O_CREATE | O_RDWR);
1094:       if(fd < 0){
1095:         printf("create failed\n", s);
1096:         exit(1);
1097:       }
1098: 
1099:       memset(buf, '0'+pi, SZ);
1100:       for(i = 0; i < N; i++){
1101:         if((n = write(fd, buf, SZ)) != SZ){
1102:           printf("write failed %d\n", n);
1103:           exit(1);
1104:         }
1105:       }
1106:       exit(0);
1107:     }
1108:   }
1109: 
1110:   int xstatus;
1111:   for(pi = 0; pi < NCHILD; pi++){
1112:     wait(&xstatus);
1113:     if(WEXITSTATUS(xstatus) != 0)
1114:       exit(WEXITSTATUS(xstatus));
1115:   }
1116: 
1117:   for(i = 0; i < NCHILD; i++){
1118:     fname = names[i];
1119:     fd = open(fname, 0);
1120:     total = 0;
1121:     while((n = read(fd, buf, sizeof(buf))) > 0){
1122:       for(j = 0; j < n; j++){
1123:         if(buf[j] != '0'+i){
1124:           printf("wrong char\n", s);
1125:           exit(1);
1126:         }
1127:       }
1128:       total += n;
1129:     }
1130:     close(fd);
1131:     if(total != N*SZ){
1132:       printf("wrong length %d\n", total);
1133:       exit(1);
1134:     }
1135:     remove(fname);
1136:   }
1137: }

1138: 
1139: // four processes create and delete different files in same directory
1140: void
1141: createdelete(char *s)
1142: {
1143:   enum { N = 20, NCHILD=4 };
1144:   int pid, i, fd, pi;
1145:   char name[32];
1146:   char illegal[] = { '\"', '*', '/', ':', '<', '>', '?', '\\', '|', 0 };
1147:   for(pi = 0; pi < NCHILD; pi++){
1148:     pid = fork();
1149:     if(pid < 0){
1150:       printf("fork failed\n", s);
1151:       exit(1);
1152:     }
1153: 
1154:     if(pid == 0){
1155:       name[0] = 'p' + pi;
1156:       name[2] = '\0';
1157:       for(i = 0; i < N; i++){
1158:         name[1] = '0' + i;
1159:         if (strchr(illegal, name[1]) == 0) {
1160:           fd = open(name, O_CREATE | O_RDWR);
1161:           if(fd < 0){
1162:             printf("%s: create %s failed\n", s, name);
1163:             exit(1);
1164:           }
1165:           close(fd);
1166:         }
1167:         if(i > 0 && (i % 2 ) == 0){
1168:           name[1] = '0' + (i / 2);
1169:           if (strchr(illegal, name[1]) == 0) {
1170:             if(remove(name) < 0){
1171:               printf("%s: remove failed\n", s);
1172:               exit(1);
1173:             }
1174:           }
1175:         }
1176:       }
1177:       exit(0);
1178:     }
1179:   }
1180: 
1181:   int xstatus;
1182:   for(pi = 0; pi < NCHILD; pi++){
1183:     wait(&xstatus);
1184:     if(WEXITSTATUS(xstatus) != 0)
1185:       exit(1);
1186:   }
1187: 
1188:   name[0] = name[1] = name[2] = 0;
1189:   for(i = 0; i < N; i++){
1190:     for(pi = 0; pi < NCHILD; pi++){
1191:       name[0] = 'p' + pi;
1192:       name[1] = '0' + i;
1193:       if (strchr(illegal, name[1]) != 0) { continue; }
1194:       fd = open(name, 0);
1195:       if((i == 0 || i >= N/2) && fd < 0){
1196:         printf("%s: oops createdelete %s didn't exist\n", s, name);
1197:         exit(1);
1198:       } else if((i >= 1 && i < N/2) && fd >= 0){
1199:         printf("%s: oops createdelete %s did exist\n", s, name);
1200:         exit(1);
1201:       }
1202:       if(fd >= 0)
1203:         close(fd);
1204:     }
1205:   }
1206: 
1207:   for(i = 0; i < N; i++){
1208:     for(pi = 0; pi < NCHILD; pi++){
1209:       name[0] = 'p' + pi;
1210:       name[1] = '0' + i;
1211:       remove(name);
1212:     }
1213:   }
1214: }
1215:

1217: void
1218: removeread(char *s)
1219: {

1136:   }
1137: }

1140: void
1141: createdelete(char *s)
1142: {
1143:   enum { N = 20, NCHILD=4 };
1144:   int pid, i, fd, pi;
1145:   char name[32];
1146:   char illegal[] = { '\"', '*', '/', ':', '<', '>', '?', '\\', '|', 0 };
1147:   for(pi = 0; pi < NCHILD; pi++){
1148:     pid = fork();
1149:     if(pid < 0){
1150:       printf("fork failed\n", s);
1151:       exit(1);
1152:     }
1153: 
1154:     if(pid == 0){
1155:       name[0] = 'p' + pi;
1156:       name[2] = '\0';
1157:       for(i = 0; i < N; i++){
1158:         name[1] = '0' + i;
1159:         if (strchr(illegal, name[1]) == 0) {
1160:           fd = open(name, O_CREATE | O_RDWR);
1161:           if(fd < 0){
1162:             printf("%s: create %s failed\n", s, name);
1163:             exit(1);
1164:           }
1165:           close(fd);
1166:         }
1167:         if(i > 0 && (i % 2 ) == 0){
1168:           name[1] = '0' + (i / 2);
1169:           if (strchr(illegal, name[1]) == 0) {
1170:             if(remove(name) < 0){
1171:               printf("%s: remove failed\n", s);
1172:               exit(1);
1173:             }
1174:           }
1175:         }
1176:       }
1177:       exit(0);
1178:     }
1179:   }
1180: 
1181:   int xstatus;
1182:   for(pi = 0; pi < NCHILD; pi++){
1183:     wait(&xstatus);
1184:     if(WEXITSTATUS(xstatus) != 0)
1185:       exit(1);
1186:   }
1187: 
1188:   name[0] = name[1] = name[2] = 0;
1189:   for(i = 0; i < N; i++){
1190:     for(pi = 0; pi < NCHILD; pi++){
1191:       name[0] = 'p' + pi;
1192:       name[1] = '0' + i;
1193:       if (strchr(illegal, name[1]) != 0) { continue; }
1194:       fd = open(name, 0);
1195:       if((i == 0 || i >= N/2) && fd < 0){
1196:         printf("%s: oops createdelete %s didn't exist\n", s, name);
1197:         exit(1);
1198:       } else if((i >= 1 && i < N/2) && fd >= 0){
1199:         printf("%s: oops createdelete %s did exist\n", s, name);
1200:         exit(1);
1201:       }
1202:       if(fd >= 0)
1203:         close(fd);
1204:     }
1205:   }
1206: 
1207:   for(i = 0; i < N; i++){
1208:     for(pi = 0; pi < NCHILD; pi++){
1209:       name[0] = 'p' + pi;
1210:       name[1] = '0' + i;
1211:       remove(name);
1212:     }
1213:   }
1214: }

1216: // can I remove a file and still read it?
1217: void
1218: removeread(char *s)
1219: {
1220:   enum { SZ = 5 };
1221:   int fd, fd1;
1222: 
1223:   fd = open("removeread", O_CREATE | O_RDWR);
1224:   if(fd < 0){
1225:     printf("%s: create removeread failed\n", s);
1226:     exit(1);
1227:   }
1228:   write(fd, "hello", SZ);
1229:   close(fd);
1230: 
1231:   fd = open("removeread", O_RDWR);
1232:   if(fd < 0){
1233:     printf("%s: open removeread failed\n", s);
1234:     exit(1);
1235:   }
1236:   if(remove("removeread") != 0){
1237:     printf("%s: remove removeread failed\n", s);
1238:     exit(1);
1239:   }
1240: 
1241:   fd1 = open("removeread", O_CREATE | O_RDWR);
1242:   write(fd1, "yyy", 3);
1243:   close(fd1);
1244: 
1245:   if(read(fd, buf, sizeof(buf)) != SZ){
1246:     printf("%s: removeread read failed", s);
1247:     exit(1);
1248:   }
1249:   if(buf[0] != 'h'){
1250:     printf("%s: removeread wrong data\n", s);
1251:     exit(1);
1252:   }
1253:   if(write(fd, buf, 10) != 10){
1254:     printf("%s: removeread write failed\n", s);
1255:     exit(1);
1256:   }
1257:   close(fd);
1258:   remove("removeread");
1259: }
1260:

1491: void
1492: subdir(char *s)
1493: {
1494:   int fd, cc;
1495: 
1496:   remove("ff");
1497:   if(mkdir("dd") != 0){
1498:     printf("%s: mkdir dd failed\n", s);
1499:     exit(1);
1500:   }
1501: 
1502:   fd = open("dd/ff", O_CREATE | O_RDWR);
1503:   if(fd < 0){
1504:     printf("%s: create dd/ff failed\n", s);
1505:     exit(1);
1506:   }
1507:   write(fd, "ff", 2);
1508:   close(fd);
1509: 
1510:   if(rmdir("dd") >= 0){
1511:     printf("%s: remove dd (non-empty dir) succeeded!\n", s);
1512:     exit(1);
1513:   }
1514: 
1515:   if(mkdir("dd/dd") != 0){
1516:     printf("subdir mkdir dd/dd failed\n", s);
1517:     exit(1);
1518:   }
1519: 
1520:   fd = open("dd/dd/ff", O_CREATE | O_RDWR);
1521:   if(fd < 0){
1522:     printf("%s: create dd/dd/ff failed\n", s);
1523:     exit(1);
1524:   }
1525:   write(fd, "FF", 2);
1526:   close(fd);
1527: 
1528:   fd = open("dd/dd/../ff", 0);
1529:   if(fd < 0){
1530:     printf("%s: open dd/dd/../ff failed\n", s);
1531:     exit(1);
1532:   }
1533:   cc = read(fd, buf, sizeof(buf));
1534:   if(cc != 2 || buf[0] != 'f'){
1535:     printf("%s: dd/dd/../ff wrong content\n", s);
1536:     exit(1);
1537:   }
1538:   close(fd);
1539: 
1540:   // if(link("dd/dd/ff", "dd/dd/ffff") != 0){
1541:   //   printf("link dd/dd/ff dd/dd/ffff failed\n", s);
1542:   //   exit(1);
1543:   // }
1544: 
1545:   if(remove("dd/dd/ff") != 0){
1546:     printf("%s: remove dd/dd/ff failed\n", s);
1547:     exit(1);
1548:   }
1549:   if(open("dd/dd/ff", O_RDONLY) >= 0){
1550:     printf("%s: open (removeed) dd/dd/ff succeeded\n", s);
1551:     exit(1);
1552:   }
1553:

1555:     printf("%s: chdir dd failed\n", s);
1556:     exit(1);

1568:     exit(1);
1569:   }
1570:

1670: // test writes that are larger than the log.
1671: void
1672: bigwrite(char *s)
1673: {
1674:   int fd, sz;
1675: 
1676:   remove("bigwrite");

1682:     }
1683:     int i;

1685:       int cc = write(fd, buf, sz);

1687:         printf("%s: write(%d) ret %d\n", s, sz, cc);
1688:         exit(1);
1689:       }
1690:     }
1691:     close(fd);
1692:     remove("bigwrite");
1693:   }
1694: }

1696: void
1697: bigfile(char *s)
1698: {
1699:   enum { N = 20, SZ=600 };
1700:   int fd, i, total, cc;
1701: 
1702:   remove("bigfile.dat");
1703:   fd = open("bigfile.dat", O_CREATE | O_RDWR);
1704:   if(fd < 0){
1705:     printf("%s: cannot create bigfile", s);
1706:     exit(1);
1707:   }
1708:   for(i = 0; i < N; i++){
1709:     memset(buf, i, SZ);
1710:     if(write(fd, buf, SZ) != SZ){
1711:       printf("%s: write bigfile failed\n", s);
1712:       exit(1);
1713:     }
1714:   }
1715:   close(fd);
1716: 
1717:   fd = open("bigfile.dat", 0);
1718:   if(fd < 0){
1719:     printf("%s: cannot open bigfile\n", s);
1720:     exit(1);
1721:   }
1722:   total = 0;
1723:   for(i = 0; ; i++){
1724:     cc = read(fd, buf, SZ/2);
1725:     if(cc < 0){
1726:       printf("%s: read bigfile failed\n", s);
1727:       exit(1);
1728:     }
1729:     if(cc == 0)
1730:       break;
1731:     if(cc != SZ/2){
1732:       printf("%s: short read bigfile\n", s);
1733:       exit(1);
1734:     }
1735:     if(buf[0] != i/2 || buf[SZ/2-1] != i/2){
1736:       printf("%s: read bigfile wrong data\n", s);
1737:       exit(1);
1738:     }
1739:     total += cc;
1740:   }
1741:   close(fd);
1742:   if(total != N*SZ){
1743:     printf("%s: read bigfile wrong total\n", s);
1744:     exit(1);
1745:   }
1746:   remove("bigfile.dat");
1747: }
1748:

1870:     printf("%s: remove dirfile failed!\n", s);
1871:     exit(1);
1872:   }
1873: 
1874:   fd = open(".", O_RDWR);
1875:   if(fd >= 0){
1876:     printf("%s: open . for writing succeeded!\n", s);
1877:     exit(1);
1878:   }
1879:   fd = open(".", 0);
1880:   if(write(fd, "x", 1) > 0){
1881:     printf("%s: write . succeeded!\n", s);
1882:     exit(1);
1883:   }
1884:   close(fd);
1885: }

1832: void
1833: dirfile(char *s)
1834: {
1835:   int fd;
1836: 
1837:   fd = open("dirfile", O_CREATE);
1838:   if(fd < 0){
1839:     printf("%s: create dirfile failed\n", s);
1840:     exit(1);
1841:   }
1842:   close(fd);
1843:   if(chdir("dirfile") == 0){
1844:     printf("%s: chdir dirfile succeeded!\n", s);
1845:     exit(1);
1846:   }
1847:   fd = open("dirfile/xx", 0);
1848:   if(fd >= 0){
1849:     printf("%s: create dirfile/xx succeeded!\n", s);
1850:     exit(1);
1851:   }
1852:   fd = open("dirfile/xx", O_CREATE);
1853:   if(fd >= 0){
1854:     printf("%s: create dirfile/xx succeeded!\n", s);
1855:     exit(1);
1856:   }
1857:   if(mkdir("dirfile/xx") == 0){
1858:     printf("%s: mkdir dirfile/xx succeeded!\n", s);
1859:     exit(1);
1860:   }
1861:   if(remove("dirfile/xx") == 0){
1862:     printf("%s: remove dirfile/xx succeeded!\n", s);
1863:     exit(1);
1864:   }
1865:   // if(link("README", "dirfile/xx") == 0){
1866:   //   printf("%s: link to dirfile/xx succeeded!\n", s);
1867:   //   exit(1);
1868:   // }
1869:   if(remove("dirfile") != 0){
1870:     printf("%s: remove dirfile failed!\n", s);
1871:     exit(1);
1872:   }
1873: 
1874:   fd = open(".", O_RDWR);
1875:   if(fd >= 0){
1876:     printf("%s: open . for writing succeeded!\n", s);
1877:     exit(1);
1878:   }
1879:   fd = open(".", 0);
1880:   if(write(fd, "x", 1) > 0){
1881:     printf("%s: write . succeeded!\n", s);
1882:     exit(1);
1883:   }
1884:   close(fd);
1885: }

1889: void
1890: iref(char *s)
1891: {
1892:   int i, fd;

1895:     if(mkdir("irefd") != 0){
1896:       printf("%s: mkdir irefd failed\n", s);
1897:       exit(1);

1914:

1917:     chdir("..");
1918:     rmdir("irefd");
1919:   }
1920:

1922: }

1922: }
1923: 
1924: // test that fork fails gracefully
1925: // the forktest binary also does this, but it runs out of proc entries first.
1926: // inside the bigger usertests binary, we run out of memory first.
1927: void
1928: forktest(char *s)
1929: {
1930:   enum{ N = 1000 };
1931:   int n, pid;
1932: 
1933:   for(n=0; n<N; n++){
1934:     pid = fork();
1935:     if(pid < 0)
1936:       break;
1937:     if(pid == 0)
1938:       exit(0);
1939:   }
1940: 
1941:   if (n == 0) {
1942:     printf("%s: no fork at all!\n", s);
1943:     exit(1);
1944:   }
1945: 
1946:   if(n == N){
1947:     printf("%s: fork claimed to work 1000 times!\n", s);
1948:     exit(1);
1949:   }
1950: 
1951:   for(; n > 0; n--){
1952:     if(wait(0) < 0){
1953:       printf("%s: wait stopped early\n", s);
1954:       exit(1);
1955:     }
1956:   }
1957: 
1958:   if(wait(0) != -1){
1959:     printf("%s: wait got too many\n", s);
1960:     exit(1);
1961:   }
1962: }
1963: 
1964: void
1965: sbrkbasic(char *s)
1966: {
1967:   enum { TOOMUCH=1024*1024*1024};

1927: void
1928: forktest(char *s)
1929: {
1930:   enum{ N = 1000 };
1931:   int n, pid;
1932: 
1933:   for(n=0; n<N; n++){
1934:     pid = fork();
1935:     if(pid < 0)
1936:       break;

1938:       exit(0);
1939:   }
1940: 
1941:   if (n == 0) {
1942:     printf("%s: no fork at all!\n", s);
1943:     exit(1);
1944:   }
1945: 
1946:   if(n == N){
1947:     printf("%s: fork claimed to work 1000 times!\n", s);
1948:     exit(1);
1949:   }
1950: 
1951:   for(; n > 0; n--){
1952:     if(wait(0) < 0){
1953:       printf("%s: wait stopped early\n", s);
1954:       exit(1);
1955:     }
1956:   }
1957: 
1958:   if(wait(0) != -1){
1959:     printf("%s: wait got too many\n", s);
1960:     exit(1);
1961:   }
1962: }

1959:     printf("%s: wait got too many\n", s);

1963: 
1964: void
1965: sbrkbasic(char *s)
1966: {
1967:   enum { TOOMUCH=1024*1024*1024};
1968:   int i, pid, xstatus;
1969:   char *c, *a, *b;
1970: 
1971:   // does sbrk() return the expected failure value?
1972:   pid = fork();
1973:   if(pid < 0){
1974:     printf("fork failed in sbrkbasic\n");
1975:     exit(1);
1976:   }
1977:   if(pid == 0){
1978:     a = sbrk(TOOMUCH);
1979:     if(a == (char*)0xffffffffffffffffL){
1980:       // it's OK if this fails.
1981:       exit(0);
1982:     }
1983:     
1984:     for(b = a; b < a+TOOMUCH; b += 4096){
1985:       *b = 99;
1986:     }
1987:     
1988:     // we should not get here! either sbrk(TOOMUCH)
1989:     // should have failed, or (with lazy allocation)
1990:     // a pagefault should have killed this process.
1991:     exit(1);
1992:   }
1993: 
1994:   // We implement both COW and lazy, this op will lead to a write on a COW page,
1995:   // so a real page will be allocated. If we don't do so, the child will eat up all
1996:   // memory, which causes the failure of wait(&xstatus), since there is no page for
1997:   // a COW allocation.
1998:   //      --AtomHeartCoder
1999:   xstatus = pid;
2000: 
2001:   wait(&xstatus);
2002:   if(WEXITSTATUS(xstatus) == 1){
2003:     printf("%s: too much memory allocated!\n", s);
2004:     exit(1);
2005:   }
2006: 
2007:   // can one sbrk() less than a page?
2008:   a = sbrk(0);
2009:   for(i = 0; i < 5000; i++){
2010:     b = sbrk(1);
2011:     if(b != a){
2012:       printf("%s: sbrk test failed %d %x %x\n", s, i, a, b);
2013:       exit(1);
2014:     }
2015:     *b = 1;
2016:     a = b + 1;
2017:   }
2018:   pid = fork();
2019:   if(pid < 0){
2020:     printf("%s: sbrk test fork failed\n", s);
2021:     exit(1);
2022:   }
2023:   c = sbrk(1);
2024:   c = sbrk(1);
2025:   if(c != a + 1){
2026:     printf("%s: sbrk test failed post-fork\n", s);
2027:     exit(1);
2028:   }
2029:   if(pid == 0)
2030:     exit(0);
2031:   wait(&xstatus);
2032:   exit(WEXITSTATUS(xstatus));
2033: }
2034: 
2035: void
2036: sbrkmuch(char *s)
2037: {
2038:   enum { BIG=3*1024*1024 };

xv6-k210@final / 来源候选源码

1959:     printf("%s: wait got too many\n", s);
1960:     exit(1);
1961:   }
1962: }

1968:   int i, pid, xstatus;
1969:   char *c, *a, *b;
1970: 
1971:   // does sbrk() return the expected failure value?

1973:   if(pid < 0){
1974:     printf("fork failed in sbrkbasic\n");

1976:   }

1989:     // should have failed, or (with lazy allocation)
1990:     // a pagefault should have killed this process.
1991:     exit(1);
1992:   }
1993: 
1994:   // We implement both COW and lazy, this op will lead to a write on a COW page,
1995:   // so a real page will be allocated. If we don't do so, the child will eat up all
1996:   // memory, which causes the failure of wait(&xstatus), since there is no page for
1997:   // a COW allocation.
1998:   //      --AtomHeartCoder
1999:   xstatus = pid;
2000: 
2001:   wait(&xstatus);
2002:   if(WEXITSTATUS(xstatus) == 1){
2003:     printf("%s: too much memory allocated!\n", s);
2004:     exit(1);
2005:   }
2006: 
2007:   // can one sbrk() less than a page?
2008:   a = sbrk(0);
2009:   for(i = 0; i < 5000; i++){
2010:     b = sbrk(1);
2011:     if(b != a){

2022:   }
2023:   c = sbrk(1);
2024:   c = sbrk(1);
2025:   if(c != a + 1){
2026:     printf("%s: sbrk test failed post-fork\n", s);
2027:     exit(1);
2028:   }
2029:   if(pid == 0)
2030:     exit(0);
2031:   wait(&xstatus);
2032:   exit(WEXITSTATUS(xstatus));
2033: }

匹配分数 1.000 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": true, "exact_token_fp": true, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 1.0}

2030:     exit(0);

2034: 
2035: void
2036: sbrkmuch(char *s)
2037: {
2038:   enum { BIG=3*1024*1024 };
2039:   char *c, *oldbrk, *a, *lastaddr, *p;
2040:   uint64 amt;
2041: 
2042:   oldbrk = sbrk(0);
2043: 
2044:   // can one grow address space to something big?
2045:   a = sbrk(0);
2046:   amt = BIG - (uint64)a;
2047:   p = sbrk(amt);
2048:   if (p != a) {
2049:     printf("%s: sbrk test failed to grow big address space; enough phys mem?\n", s);
2050:     exit(1);
2051:   }
2052: 
2053:   // touch each page to make sure it exists.
2054:   char *eee = sbrk(0);
2055:   for(char *pp = a; pp < eee; pp += 4096)
2056:     *pp = 1;
2057: 
2058:   lastaddr = (char*) (BIG-1);
2059:   *lastaddr = 99;
2060: 
2061:   // can one de-allocate?
2062:   a = sbrk(0);
2063:   c = sbrk(-PGSIZE);
2064:   if(c == (char*)0xffffffffffffffffL){
2065:     printf("%s: sbrk could not deallocate\n", s);
2066:     exit(1);
2067:   }
2068:   c = sbrk(0);
2069:   if(c != a - PGSIZE){
2070:     printf("%s: sbrk deallocation produced wrong address, a %x c %x\n", a, c);
2071:     exit(1);
2072:   }
2073: 
2074:   // can one re-allocate that page?
2075:   a = sbrk(0);
2076:   c = sbrk(PGSIZE);
2077:   if(c != a || sbrk(0) != a + PGSIZE){
2078:     printf("%s: sbrk re-allocation failed, a %x c %x\n", a, c);
2079:     exit(1);
2080:   }
2081:   if(*lastaddr == 99){
2082:     // should be zero
2083:     printf("%s: sbrk de-allocation didn't really deallocate\n", s);
2084:     exit(1);
2085:   }
2086: 
2087:   a = sbrk(0);
2088:   c = sbrk(-(sbrk(0) - oldbrk));
2089:   if(c != a){
2090:     printf("%s: sbrk downsize failed, a %x c %x\n", a, c);
2091:     exit(1);
2092:   }
2093: }
2094:

2097: kernmem(char *s)
2098: {

2031:   wait(&xstatus);

2038:   enum { BIG=3*1024*1024 };
2039:   char *c, *oldbrk, *a, *lastaddr, *p;

2043: 
2044:   // can one grow address space to something big?

2046:   amt = BIG - (uint64)a;
2047:   p = sbrk(amt);

2049:     printf("%s: sbrk test failed to grow big address space; enough phys mem?\n", s);
2050:     exit(1);
2051:   }
2052: 
2053:   // touch each page to make sure it exists.
2054:   char *eee = sbrk(0);
2055:   for(char *pp = a; pp < eee; pp += 4096)
2056:     *pp = 1;
2057: 
2058:   lastaddr = (char*) (BIG-1);
2059:   *lastaddr = 99;
2060: 
2061:   // can one de-allocate?
2062:   a = sbrk(0);
2063:   c = sbrk(-PGSIZE);
2064:   if(c == (char*)0xffffffffffffffffL){
2065:     printf("%s: sbrk could not deallocate\n", s);
2066:     exit(1);
2067:   }
2068:   c = sbrk(0);
2069:   if(c != a - PGSIZE){
2070:     printf("%s: sbrk deallocation produced wrong address, a %x c %x\n", a, c);
2071:     exit(1);
2072:   }
2073: 
2074:   // can one re-allocate that page?
2075:   a = sbrk(0);
2076:   c = sbrk(PGSIZE);
2077:   if(c != a || sbrk(0) != a + PGSIZE){
2078:     printf("%s: sbrk re-allocation failed, a %x c %x\n", a, c);
2079:     exit(1);
2080:   }
2081:   if(*lastaddr == 99){
2082:     // should be zero
2083:     printf("%s: sbrk de-allocation didn't really deallocate\n", s);
2084:     exit(1);
2085:   }
2086: 
2087:   a = sbrk(0);
2088:   c = sbrk(-(sbrk(0) - oldbrk));
2089:   if(c != a){
2090:     printf("%s: sbrk downsize failed, a %x c %x\n", a, c);
2091:     exit(1);
2092:   }
2093: }

2095: // can we read the kernel's memory?
2096: void
2097: kernmem(char *s)
2098: {
2099:   char *a;
2100:   int pid;
2101: 
2102:   for(a = (char*)(KERNBASE); a < (char*) (KERNBASE+2000000); a += 50000){

2104:     if(pid < 0){

2109:       printf("%s: oops could read %x = %x\n", a, *a);
2110:       exit(1);

2113:     wait(&xstatus);
2114:     if(WEXITSTATUS(xstatus) != (char)-1)  // did kernel kill child?
2115:       exit(1);
2116:   }
2117: }

2095: // can we read the kernel's memory?
2096: void
2097: kernmem(char *s)
2098: {
2099:   char *a;
2100:   int pid;
2101: 
2102:   for(a = (char*)(KERNBASE); a < (char*) (KERNBASE+2000000); a += 50000){
2103:     pid = fork();
2104:     if(pid < 0){
2105:       printf("%s: fork failed\n", s);
2106:       exit(1);
2107:     }
2108:     if(pid == 0){
2109:       printf("%s: oops could read %x = %x\n", a, *a);
2110:       exit(1);
2111:     }
2112:     int xstatus;
2113:     wait(&xstatus);
2114:     if(WEXITSTATUS(xstatus) != (char)-1)  // did kernel kill child?
2115:       exit(1);
2116:   }
2117: }

2119: // if we run the system out of memory, does it clean up the last
2120: // failed allocation?
2121: void
2122: sbrkfail(char *s)
2123: {
2124:   enum { BIG=100*1024*1024 };
2125:   int i, xstatus;
2126:   int fds[2];
2127:   char scratch;
2128:   char *c, *a;
2129:   int pids[10];
2130:   int pid;
2131:  
2132:   if(pipe(fds) != 0){
2133:     printf("%s: pipe() failed\n", s);
2134:     exit(1);
2135:   }
2136:   for(i = 0; i < sizeof(pids)/sizeof(pids[0]); i++){
2137:     if((pids[i] = fork()) == 0){
2138:       // allocate a lot of memory
2139:       sbrk(BIG - (uint64)sbrk(0));
2140:       write(fds[1], "x", 1);
2141:       // sit around until killed
2142:       for(;;) sleep(1000);
2143:     }
2144:     if(pids[i] != -1)
2145:       read(fds[0], &scratch, 1);
2146:   }
2147: 
2148:   // if those failed allocations freed up the pages they did allocate,
2149:   // we'll be able to allocate here
2150:   c = sbrk(PGSIZE);
2151:   for(i = 0; i < sizeof(pids)/sizeof(pids[0]); i++){
2152:     if(pids[i] == -1)
2153:       continue;
2154:     kill(pids[i], SIGTERM);
2155:     wait(0);
2156:   }
2157:   if(c == (char*)0xffffffffffffffffL){
2158:     printf("%s: failed sbrk leaked memory\n", s);
2159:     exit(1);
2160:   }
2161: 
2162:   // test running fork with the above allocated page 
2163:   pid = fork();
2164:   xstatus = pid; // nonsense, just to cause a page fault to get a COW page
2165:   if(pid < 0){
2166:     printf("%s: fork failed\n", s);
2167:     exit(1);
2168:   }
2169:   if(pid == 0){
2170:     // allocate a lot of memory.
2171:     // this should produce a page fault,
2172:     // and thus not complete.
2173:     a = sbrk(0);
2174:     sbrk(10*BIG);
2175:     int n = 0;
2176:     for (i = 0; i < 10*BIG; i += PGSIZE) {
2177:       n += *(a+i);
2178:     }
2179:     // print n so the compiler doesn't optimize away
2180:     // the for loop.
2181:     printf("%s: allocate a lot of memory succeeded %d\n", n);
2182:     exit(1);
2183:   }
2184:   wait(&xstatus);
2185:   if(WEXITSTATUS(xstatus) != (char)-1 && WEXITSTATUS(xstatus) != 2)
2186:     exit(1);
2187: }
2188: 
2189:   
2190: // test reads/writes from/to allocated memory
2191: void
2192: sbrkarg(char *s)

2117: }

2192: sbrkarg(char *s)
2193: {
2194:   char *a;

2198:   fd = open("sbrk", O_CREATE|O_WRONLY);

2202:     exit(1);

2209:

2257: 
2258:   remove("bigarg-ok");

2260:   if(pid == 0){
2261:     static char *args[MAXARG];
2262:     int i;
2263:     for(i = 0; i < MAXARG-1; i++)
2264:       args[i] = "bigargs test: failed\n                                                                                                                                                                                                       ";
2265:     args[MAXARG-1] = 0;
2266:     exec("echo", args);
2267:     fd = open("bigarg-ok", O_CREATE);
2268:     close(fd);

2272:     exit(1);

2280:     printf("%s: bigarg test failed!\n", s);
2281:     exit(1);
2282:   }
2283:   close(fd);
2284:   remove("bigarg-ok");
2285: }

2286: 
2287: // what happens when the file system runs out of blocks?
2288: // answer: balloc panics, so this test is not useful.
2289: void
2290: fsfull()
2291: {
2292:   int nfiles;
2293:   int fsblocks = 0;
2294: 
2295:   printf("fsfull test\n");
2296: 
2297:   for(nfiles = 0; ; nfiles++){
2298:     char name[64];
2299:     name[0] = 'f';
2300:     name[1] = '0' + nfiles / 1000;
2301:     name[2] = '0' + (nfiles % 1000) / 100;
2302:     name[3] = '0' + (nfiles % 100) / 10;
2303:     name[4] = '0' + (nfiles % 10);
2304:     name[5] = '\0';
2305:     printf("%s: writing %s\n", name);
2306:     int fd = open(name, O_CREATE|O_RDWR);
2307:     if(fd < 0){
2308:       printf("%s: open %s failed\n", name);
2309:       break;
2310:     }
2311:     int total = 0;
2312:     while(1){
2313:       int cc = write(fd, buf, BSIZE);
2314:       if(cc < BSIZE)
2315:         break;
2316:       total += cc;
2317:       fsblocks++;
2318:     }
2319:     printf("%s: wrote %d bytes\n", total);
2320:     close(fd);
2321:     if(total == 0)
2322:       break;
2323:   }
2324: 
2325:   while(nfiles >= 0){
2326:     char name[64];
2327:     name[0] = 'f';
2328:     name[1] = '0' + nfiles / 1000;
2329:     name[2] = '0' + (nfiles % 1000) / 100;
2330:     name[3] = '0' + (nfiles % 100) / 10;
2331:     name[4] = '0' + (nfiles % 10);
2332:     name[5] = '\0';
2333:     remove(name);
2334:     nfiles--;
2335:   }
2336: 
2337:   printf("fsfull test finished\n");
2338: }
2339:

2342:   int fd;
2343:   fd = open("init", O_RDONLY);

2360: // check that there's an invalid page beneath
2361: // the user stack, to catch stack overflow.
2362: void
2363: stacktest(char *s)
2364: {
2365:   int pid;
2366:   int xstatus;
2367:   
2368:   pid = fork();
2369:   if(pid == 0) {
2370:     char *sp = (char *) r_sp();
2371:     sp -= PGSIZE;
2372:     // the *sp should cause a trap.
2373:     printf("%s: stacktest: read below stack %p\n", *sp);
2374:     exit(1);
2375:   } else if(pid < 0){
2376:     printf("%s: fork failed\n", s);
2377:     exit(1);
2378:   }
2379:   wait(&xstatus);
2380:   if(WEXITSTATUS(xstatus) == (char)-1)  // kernel killed child?
2381:     exit(0);
2382:   else
2383:     exit(WEXITSTATUS(xstatus));
2384: }
2385: 
2386: // regression test. copyin(), copyout(), and copyinstr() used to cast
2387: // the virtual page address to uint, which (with certain wild system
2388: // call arguments) resulted in a kernel page faults.
2389: void

2401: // regression test. does the kernel panic if a process sbrk()s its
2402: // size to be less than a page, or zero, or reduces the break by an
2403: // amount too small to cause a page to be freed?
2404: void
2405: sbrkbugs(char *s)
2406: {
2407:   int pid = fork();
2408:   if(pid < 0){
2409:     printf("fork failed\n");
2410:     exit(1);
2411:   }
2412:   if(pid == 0){
2413:     int sz = (uint64) sbrk(0);
2414:     // free all user memory; there used to be a bug that
2415:     // would not adjust p->sz correctly in this case,
2416:     // causing exit() to panic.
2417:     sbrk(-sz);
2418:     // user page fault here.
2419:     exit(0);
2420:   }
2421:   wait(0);
2422: 
2423:   pid = fork();
2424:   if(pid < 0){
2425:     printf("fork failed\n");
2426:     exit(1);
2427:   }
2428:   if(pid == 0){
2429:     int sz = (uint64) sbrk(0);
2430:     // set the break to somewhere in the very first
2431:     // page; there used to be a bug that would incorrectly
2432:     // free the first page.
2433:     sbrk(-(sz - 3500));
2434:     exit(0);
2435:   }
2436:   wait(0);
2437: 
2438:   pid = fork();
2439:   if(pid < 0){
2440:     printf("fork failed\n");
2441:     exit(1);
2442:   }
2443:   if(pid == 0){
2444:     // set the break in the middle of a page.
2445:     sbrk((10*4096 + 2048) - (uint64)sbrk(0));
2446: 
2447:     // reduce the break a bit, but not enough to
2448:     // cause a page to be freed. this used to cause
2449:     // a panic.
2450:     sbrk(-10);
2451: 
2452:     exit(0);
2453:   }
2454:   wait(0);
2455: 
2456:   exit(0);
2457: }
2458:

2461: // file is deleted? if the kernel has this bug, it will panic: balloc:
2462: // out of blocks. assumed_free may need to be raised to be more than

匹配分数 1.000 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": true, "exact_token_fp": true, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 1.0}

oskernel2023-zmz@recover 源码

2459: // regression test. does write() with an invalid buffer pointer cause
2460: // a block to be allocated for a file that is then not freed when the
2461: // file is deleted? if the kernel has this bug, it will panic: balloc:
2462: // out of blocks. assumed_free may need to be raised to be more than
2463: // the number of free blocks. this test takes a long time.
2464: void
2465: badwrite(char *s)
2466: {
2467:   int assumed_free = 600;
2468:   
2469:   remove("junk");
2470:   for(int i = 0; i < assumed_free; i++){
2471:     int fd = open("junk", O_CREATE|O_WRONLY);
2472:     if(fd < 0){
2473:       printf("open junk failed\n");
2474:       exit(1);
2475:     }
2476:     write(fd, (char*)0xffffffffffL, 1);
2477:     close(fd);
2478:     remove("junk");
2479:   }
2480: 
2481:   int fd = open("junk", O_CREATE|O_WRONLY);
2482:   if(fd < 0){
2483:     printf("open junk failed\n");
2484:     exit(1);
2485:   }
2486:   if(write(fd, "x", 1) != 1){
2487:     printf("write failed\n");
2488:     exit(1);
2489:   }
2490:   close(fd);
2491:   remove("junk");
2492: 
2493:   exit(0);
2494: }
2495:

2499: badarg(char *s)

2510: 
2511: // test the exec() code that cleans up if it runs out
2512: // of memory. it's really a test that such a condition
2513: // doesn't cause a panic.
2514: void
2515: execout(char *s)
2516: {
2517:   for(int avail = 0; avail < 15; avail++){
2518:     int pid = fork();
2519:     if(pid < 0){
2520:       printf("fork failed\n");
2521:       exit(1);
2522:     } else if(pid == 0){
2523:       // allocate all of memory.
2524:       while(1){
2525:         uint64 a = (uint64) sbrk(4096);
2526:         if(a == 0xffffffffffffffffLL)
2527:           break;
2528:         *(char*)(a + 4096 - 1) = 1;
2529:       }
2530: 
2531:       // free a few pages, in order to let exec() make some
2532:       // progress.
2533:       for(int i = 0; i < avail; i++)
2534:         sbrk(-4096);
2535:       
2536:       close(1);
2537:       char *args[] = { "echo", "x", 0 };
2538:       exec("echo", args);
2539:       exit(0);
2540:     } else {
2541:       wait((int*)0);
2542:     }
2543:   }
2544: 
2545:   exit(0);
2546: }
2547: 
2548: //
2549: // use sbrk() to count how many free physical memory pages there are.
2550: // touches the pages to force allocation.
2551: // because out of memory with lazy allocation results in the process

2509: }

2514: void
2515: execout(char *s)
2516: {

2518:     int pid = fork();
2519:     if(pid < 0){
2520:       printf("fork failed\n");
2521:       exit(1);
2522:     } else if(pid == 0){
2523:       // allocate all of memory.
2524:       while(1){
2525:         uint64 a = (uint64) sbrk(4096);
2526:         if(a == 0xffffffffffffffffLL)
2527:           break;
2528:         *(char*)(a + 4096 - 1) = 1;
2529:       }
2530: 
2531:       // free a few pages, in order to let exec() make some
2532:       // progress.
2533:       for(int i = 0; i < avail; i++)
2534:         sbrk(-4096);
2535:       
2536:       close(1);
2537:       char *args[] = { "echo", "x", 0 };
2538:       exec("echo", args);
2539:       exit(0);
2540:     } else {
2541:       wait((int*)0);
2542:     }
2543:   }
2544: 
2545:   exit(0);
2546: }

oskernel2023-zmz@recover 源码

2549: // use sbrk() to count how many free physical memory pages there are.
2550: // touches the pages to force allocation.
2551: // because out of memory with lazy allocation results in the process
2552: // taking a fault and being killed, fork and report back.
2553: //
2554: int
2555: countfree()
2556: {
2557:   int fds[2];
2558: 
2559:   if(pipe(fds) < 0){
2560:     printf("pipe() failed in countfree()\n");
2561:     exit(1);
2562:   }
2563:   
2564:   int pid = fork();
2565: 
2566:   if(pid < 0){
2567:     printf("fork failed in countfree()\n");
2568:     exit(1);
2569:   }
2570: 
2571:   if(pid == 0){
2572:     close(fds[0]);
2573:     
2574:     while(1){
2575:       uint64 a = (uint64) sbrk(4096);
2576:       if(a == 0xffffffffffffffff){
2577:         break;
2578:       }
2579: 
2580:       // modify the memory to make sure it's really allocated.
2581:       *(char *)(a + 4096 - 1) = 1;
2582: 
2583:       // report back one more page.
2584:       if(write(fds[1], "x", 1) != 1){
2585:         printf("write() failed in countfree()\n");
2586:         exit(1);
2587:       }
2588:     }
2589: 
2590:     exit(0);
2591:   }
2592: 
2593:   close(fds[1]);
2594: 
2595:   int n = 0;
2596:   while(1){
2597:     char c;
2598:     int cc = read(fds[0], &c, 1);
2599:     if(cc < 0){
2600:       printf("read() failed in countfree()\n");
2601:       exit(1);
2602:     }
2603:     if(cc == 0)
2604:       break;
2605:     n += 1;
2606:   }
2607: 
2608:   close(fds[0]);
2609:   wait((int*)0);
2610:   
2611:   return n;
2612: }
2613: 
2614: // run each test in its own process. run returns 1 if child's exit()
2615: // indicates success.
2616: int
2617: run(void f(char *), char *s) {

xv6-k210@final / 来源候选源码

2549: // use sbrk() to count how many free physical memory pages there are.
2550: // touches the pages to force allocation.
2551: // because out of memory with lazy allocation results in the process
2552: // taking a fault and being killed, fork and report back.
2553: //
2554: int
2555: countfree()
2556: {
2557:   int fds[2];
2558: 
2559:   if(pipe(fds) < 0){
2560:     printf("pipe() failed in countfree()\n");
2561:     exit(1);
2562:   }
2563:   
2564:   int pid = fork();
2565: 
2566:   if(pid < 0){
2567:     printf("fork failed in countfree()\n");
2568:     exit(1);
2569:   }
2570: 
2571:   if(pid == 0){
2572:     close(fds[0]);
2573:     
2574:     while(1){
2575:       uint64 a = (uint64) sbrk(4096);
2576:       if(a == 0xffffffffffffffff){
2577:         break;
2578:       }
2579: 
2580:       // modify the memory to make sure it's really allocated.
2581:       *(char *)(a + 4096 - 1) = 1;
2582: 
2583:       // report back one more page.
2584:       if(write(fds[1], "x", 1) != 1){
2585:         printf("write() failed in countfree()\n");
2586:         exit(1);
2587:       }
2588:     }
2589: 
2590:     exit(0);
2591:   }
2592: 
2593:   close(fds[1]);
2594: 
2595:   int n = 0;
2596:   while(1){
2597:     char c;
2598:     int cc = read(fds[0], &c, 1);
2599:     if(cc < 0){
2600:       printf("read() failed in countfree()\n");
2601:       exit(1);
2602:     }
2603:     if(cc == 0)
2604:       break;
2605:     n += 1;
2606:   }
2607: 
2608:   close(fds[0]);
2609:   wait((int*)0);
2610:   
2611:   return n;
2612: }
2613: 
2614: // run each test in its own process. run returns 1 if child's exit()
2615: // indicates success.
2616: int
2617: run(void f(char *), char *s) {

2614: // run each test in its own process. run returns 1 if child's exit()

2616: int
2617: run(void f(char *), char *s) {
2618:   int pid;
2619:   int xstatus;
2620: 
2621:   printf("test %s: ", s);
2622:   if((pid = fork()) < 0) {
2623:     printf("runtest: fork error\n");
2624:     exit(1);
2625:   }
2626:   if(pid == 0) {
2627:     f(s);
2628:     exit(0);
2629:   } else {
2630:     wait(&xstatus);
2631:     if(WEXITSTATUS(xstatus) != 0) 
2632:       printf("FAILED\n");
2633:     else
2634:       printf("OK\n");
2635:     return WEXITSTATUS(xstatus) == 0;
2636:   }
2637: }
2638:

2641: {

2657:     void (*f)(char *);
2658:     char *s;
2659:   } tests[] = {
2660:     {execout, "execout"},
2661:     {copyin, "copyin"},
2662:     {copyout, "copyout"},
2663:     {copyinstr1, "copyinstr1"},
2664:     {copyinstr2, "copyinstr2"},

2666:     {truncate1, "truncate1"},
2667:     {truncate2, "truncate2"},

2671:     {sbrkbugs, "sbrkbugs" },

2674:     {reparent, "reparent" },
2675:     {twochildren, "twochildren"},
2676:     {forkfork, "forkfork"},
2677:     {forkforkfork, "forkforkfork"},
2678:     {argptest, "argptest"},
2679:     {createdelete, "createdelete"},
2680:               // {linkremove, "linkremove"},
2681:               // {linktest, "linktest"},
2682:     {removeread, "removeread"},
2683:               // {concreate, "concreate"},
2684:     {subdir, "subdir"},
2685:     {fourfiles, "fourfiles"},
2686:     {sharedfd, "sharedfd"},
2687:     {exectest, "exectest"},
2688:     {bigargtest, "bigargtest"},
2689:     {bigwrite, "bigwrite"},
2690:     {bsstest, "bsstest"},
2691:     {sbrkbasic, "sbrkbasic"},
2692:     {sbrkmuch, "sbrkmuch"},
2693:     {kernmem, "kernmem"},
2694:     {sbrkfail, "sbrkfail"},
2695:     {sbrkarg, "sbrkarg"},
2696:     {validatetest, "validatetest"},
2697:               // {stacktest, "stacktest"},
2698:     {opentest, "opentest"},
2699:     {writetest, "writetest"},
2700:     {writebig, "writebig"},
2701:     {createtest, "createtest"},
2702:     {openiputtest, "openiput"},
2703:     {exitiputtest, "exitiput"},
2704:     {iputtest, "iput"},
2705:     {mem, "mem"},
2706:     {pipe1, "pipe1"},
2707:     {preempt, "preempt"},
2708:     {exitwait, "exitwait"},
2709:     {rmdot, "rmdot"},
2710:               // {fourteen, "fourteen"},
2711:     {bigfile, "bigfile"},
2712:     {dirfile, "dirfile"},
2713:     {iref, "iref"},
2714:     {forktest, "forktest"},
2715:               // {bigdir, "bigdir"}, // slow
2716:     { 0, 0},
2717:   };
2718: 
2719:   if(continuous){
2720:     printf("continuous usertests starting\n");
2721:     while(1){
2722:       int fail = 0;
2723:       int free0 = countfree();
2724:       for (struct test *t = tests; t->s != 0; t++) {
2725:         if(!run(t->f, t->s)){
2726:           fail = 1;
2727:           break;

2729:       }
2730:       if(fail){
2731:         printf("SOME TESTS FAILED\n");
2732:         if(continuous != 2)
2733:           exit(1);
2734:       }
2735:       int free1 = countfree();
2736:       if(free1 < free0){
2737:         printf("FAILED -- lost %d free pages\n", free0 - free1);

2751:         fail = 1;
2752:     }
2753:   }
2754: 
2755:   if(fail){
2756:     printf("SOME TESTS FAILED\n");
2757:     exit(1);
2758:   } else if((free1 = countfree()) < free0){
2759:     printf("FAILED -- lost some free pages %d (out of %d)\n", free1, free0);
2760:     exit(1);
2761:   } else {
2762:     printf("ALL TESTS PASSED\n");
2763:     exit(0);
2764:   }
2765: }

2: #include "fs/stat.h"
3: #include "user.h"
4: 
5: char buf[512];
6: 
7: void
8: wc(int fd, char *name)
9: {
10:   int i, n;
11:   int l, w, c, inword;
12: 
13:   l = w = c = 0;
14:   inword = 0;
15:   while((n = read(fd, buf, sizeof(buf))) > 0){
16:     for(i=0; i<n; i++){
17:       c++;
18:       if(buf[i] == '\n')
19:         l++;
20:       if(strchr(" \r\t\n\v", buf[i]))
21:         inword = 0;
22:       else if(!inword){
23:         w++;
24:         inword = 1;
25:       }
26:     }
27:   }
28:   if(n < 0){
29:     printf("wc: read error\n");
30:     exit(1);
31:   }
32:   printf("%d\t%d\t%d\t%s\n", l, w, c, name);
33: }
34:

36: main(int argc, char *argv[])
37: {

xv6-k210/xv6-user/xargs.c::readline
匹配分数 1.000 · {"call_neighbor_similarity": 1.0, "exact_ast_shape": true, "exact_token_fp": true, "path_role_similarity": 1.0, "same_name": true, "token_similarity": 1.0}

oskernel2023-zmz@recover 源码

4: 
5: /**
6:  * len:    include the 0 in the end.
7:  * return: the number of bytes that read successfully (0 in the end is not included)
8:  */
9: int readline(int fd, char *buf, int len)
10: {
11:     char *p = buf;
12:     while (read(fd, p, 1) != 0 && p < buf + len) {
13:         if (*p == '\n') {
14:             if (p == buf) {     // ignore empty line
15:                 continue;
16:             }
17:             *p = '\0';
18:             break;
19:         }
20:         p++;
21:     }
22:     if (p == buf) {
23:         return 0;
24:     }
25:     return p - buf;
26: }
27: 
28: int main(int argc, char *argv[])
29: {
30:     if (argc < 2) {
31:         fprintf(2, "Usage: xargs COMMAND [INITIAL-ARGS]...\n");

28: int main(int argc, char *argv[])
29: {
30:     if (argc < 2) {
31:         fprintf(2, "Usage: xargs COMMAND [INITIAL-ARGS]...\n");
32:         exit(-1);
33:     }
34:     char *argvs[MAXARG];
35:     char buf[128];
36:     int i;
37:     for (i = 1; i < argc; i++) {
38:         argvs[i - 1] = argv[i];         // argvs[0] = COMMAND
39:     }
40:     i--;
41:     if (readline(0, buf, 128) == 0) {   // if there is no input
42:         argvs[i] = 0;
43:         if (fork() == 0) {
44:             exec(argv[1], argvs);
45:             printf("xargs: exec %s fail\n", argv[1]);
46:             exit(0);
47:         }
48:         wait(0);
49:     } else {
50:         argvs[i] = buf;
51:         argvs[i + 1] = 0;
52:         do {
53:             if (fork() == 0) {
54:                 exec(argv[1], argvs);
55:                 printf("xargs: exec %s fail\n", argv[1]);
56:                 exit(0);
57:             }
58:             wait(0);
59:         } while (readline(0, buf, 128) != 0);
60:     }
61:     exit(0);
62: }
